PN CN107686192-A
TI Phosphorus and ammonia nitrogen wastewater treatment includes adding magnesium phosphate on phosphorus and ammonia nitrogen wastewater to remove ammonia, filtering, adding lime, introducing filtrate through carbon dioxide and collecting.
AU XU Y
   ZHENG Y
AE UNIV LIAONING TECHNICAL (ULNT-C)
GA 201814986G
AB    NOVELTY - Phosphorus and ammonia nitrogen wastewater treatment comprises adding magnesium phosphate on phosphorus and ammonia nitrogen wastewater to remove ammonia, filtering, collecting magnesium phosphate precipitate and filtrate, adding lime on filtrate to remove phosphorus, filtering suspension of magnesium hydroxide and calcium hydroxide, collecting precipitate and filtrate, introducing filtrate through carbon dioxide to remove residual calcium ions, filtering, collecting calcium carbonate and filtrate and removing phosphorus and ammonia.
   USE - Method of phosphorus and ammonia nitrogen wastewater treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes harmful substances in waste water, drops phosphorus content below 1 mg/L and ammonia nitrogen content below 25 mg/L and has better treated water quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The mole ratio of calcium in lime and phosphorus in phosphorus and ammonia nitrogen wastewater is 2-4:1.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN112316985-A
TI Preparation of catalytic material used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol includes performing contact reaction between UiO-66 and copper acetate in the presence of solvent.
AU HONG X
   LIU G
   LIU T
AE WUHAN KELIKAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 202118043B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalytic material comprises performing first contact reaction between UiO-66 and copper acetate in the presence of solvent to obtain solution I and performing second contact reaction between solution I and sodium borohydride.
   USE - The preparation of catalytic material is used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases stability of copper species and catalytic dissociation of hydrogen and activation of carbon dioxide and is conducive to carbon dioxide reduction reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of catalytic material further comprises mixing zirconium source, water, formic acid and terephthalic acid in the presence of solvent, performing hydrothermal reaction and drying solid product. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of copper acetate based on copper element to amount of UiO-66 based on zirconium element is 0.3-0.6:1. The molar ratio of sodium borohydride to copper acetate in terms of copper element is 4-5:1. The molar ratio of zirconium source to water, formic acid and terephthalic acid in terms of zirconium element is 1:3-3.5:100-110:0.9-1.1. The solvent is N,N'-dimethylformamide.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN110776426-A; CN110776426-B
TI New 2-perfluoroepoxy oligo-based-tetrafluoropropionic acid p-alkylphenyl ester useful in supercritical carbon dioxide system.
AU YANG L
   CHEN J
   LIU Z
   ZHANG Q
   LI R
   SHEN S
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
   UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 202015705U
AB    NOVELTY - 2-perfluoroepoxy oligo-based-tetrafluoropropionic acid p-alkylphenyl ester compounds (I) are new.
   USE - (I) are useful in supercritical carbon dioxide system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): have simple preparation method, mild reaction conditions, high yield, simple post-processing, excellent solubility in supercritical carbon dioxide, mild radical polymerization conditions and have excellent homogeneity in supercritical carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - 2-perfluoroepoxy oligo-based-tetrafluoropropionic acid p-alkylphenyl ester compounds of formula (I) are new.
   R = -CH2CH3 or -CH=CH2; and
   n = 1-14.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises adding p-ethylphenol or p-hydroxystyrene compounds of formula (II) and (S)-2,3,3,3-tetrafluoro-2-(perfluoropropoxy)propanal compounds of formula (III) into solvent, then adding catalyst and acid binding agent and stirring and reacting at room temperature to 60 degrees C for 1-5 hours under argon atmosphere, isolating and purifying to obtain final product, where the catalyst is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide or 4-dimethylaminopyridine, and acid-binding agent is triethylamine, pyridine or N,N-dimethylaniline.X = F or Cl.Preferred Components: The molar ratio between (II) and (III) is 1:1-1.5. The catalyst is N,N-dimethylformamide. The addition amount of catalyst is 0.03-0.1% based on molar amount of (II). The acid binding agent is triethylamine. The addition amount of acid binding agent is 0.07-0.2% based on molar amount of (II). The reaction solvent is dichloromethane, dichloroethane, chloroform or trichloroethane.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

GA 2019470319

   USE - Carbon dioxide enrichment agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide enrichment agent has non-toxic and harmless, green and environmentally friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The composite bacteria comprises 35-40 wt.% Bacillus polymyxa, 30-35 wt.% Bacillus thuringiensis and 25-35 wt.% Bifidobacterium. The carbon dioxide-enriching agent further comprises 1-3 pts. wt. purple bacteria.

The earth conditioner comprises 70-75 wt.% calcium phosphate and 25-30 wt.% sodium percarbonate. Preferred Components: The yeast sugar is seaweed yeast sugar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complexing agent is diethylenetriamine pentacarboxylate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The water absorbing agent comprises 40-50 wt.% polyethylene oxide and 50-60 wt.% coal gangue.

PT P
PN CN109294992-A
TI Inducing apoptotic bodies of tumor cell by carbon dioxide shock method useful for inducing tumor cell apoptotic bodies in anti-tumor vaccine, comprises obtaining apoptotic bodies by placing tumor cells in a carbon dioxide-free incubator.
AU LIU A
   LV S
AE QINGYUNTANG BIOTECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2019146525
AB    NOVELTY - Inducing apoptotic bodies of tumor cell by carbon dioxide shock method comprises obtaining apoptotic bodies by placing tumor cells in a carbon dioxide-free incubator to cause apoptosis of tumor cells.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing tumor cell apoptotic bodies in anti-tumor medicine and inducing tumor cell apoptotic bodies in anti-tumor vaccine (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures strong anti-tumor effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) incubating the tumor cells in a logarithmic growth phase and placing in a carbon dioxide-free incubator to induce tumor cell apoptosis; (ii) collecting the culture supernatant of the cells and centrifuging twice at room temperature; (iii) moving the supernatant into a sterile syringe, installing a membrane filtration device, obtaining slight pressure, and passing the supernatant through 5 mm filter; (iv) collecting the filtrate by a centrifuge tube, placing in a high-speed centrifuge at 1-6 degrees C, centrifuging 16,000-18000 g for 18-23 minutes; and (v) re-suspending the precipitated gray lake and washing with phosphate buffer saline, and centrifuging at 16000-8000 g for 30-35 minutes, and purifying the bottom of the tube and obtaining purified apoptotic cell of the tumor cell. The prepared tumor cell apoptotic bodies are further purified by flow cytometry or differential centrifugation.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN108745340-A; CN108745340-B
TI Preparing carbon-supported bismuth nanoparticle catalyst, comprises adopting aqueous chemical reduction method.
AU QIAO J
   ZHANG X
   CAI Y
   WANG M
   XU N
   LIU J
   PENG L
   ZHOU Y
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 201888710F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon-supported bismuth nanoparticle catalyst, comprises adopting aqueous chemical reduction method.
   USE - The carbon supported ruthenium nanoparticle catalyst is useful in carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good dispersibility, high selectivity and increases the current density, which greatly improves the electrochemical surface area of the catalyst, improves the exposure of the carbon dioxide reduction active site, reduces the electron mass transfer resistance, increases the current density of carbon dioxide reduction, improves the carbon dioxide utilization rate and conversion rate; has simple preparation method, large output, and is especially suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises dissolving bismuth nitrate and nitric acid in deionized water, stirring until dissolved, adding trisodium citrate to obtain a catalyst precursor solution; then adding carbon material, stirring, adding sodium borohydride reduction solution for reduction reaction; washing the obtained solid with deionized water and suction filtering, and vacuum drying to obtain a carbon-supported ruthenium nanoparticle catalyst. Preferred Components: The bismuth nanoparticles have a particle diameter of 2-5 nm. The concentration of bismuth nitrate in the catalyst precursor solution is 0.2-0.5 M. The molar ratio of trisodium citrate to bismuth nitrate in the catalyst precursor solution is not more than 8:1. The carbon material is a multi-walled carbon nanotube functionalized with graphene or carboxy group. The mass ratio of the carbon material added to the catalytically precursor solution and bismuth is not more than 3:1. The reaction temperature of the reduction reaction is 0-60 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6-12 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AE HAIR ORIGHT INT CORP (HAIR-Non-standard)

PN CN114672529-A
TI Combined preparation of seabuckthorn            oligosaccharide and oligopeptide involves crushing            seabuckthorn seeds, performing supercritical carbon            dioxide extraction, performing enzymolysis,            centrifuging, and vacuum-concentrating enzymolysis            liquid.
AU YAO Y
   WANG J
   ZHANG Z
AE INNER MONGOLIA YUHANGREN HI TECH IND CO (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 202289063E
AB    NOVELTY - Combined preparation method of seabuckthorn                oligosaccharide and oligopeptide involves drying                seabuckthorn seeds, crushing the seabuckthorn seeds                by 20 mesh sieve, performing supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction, crushing the seabuckthorn seed                meal after supercritical carbon dioxide extraction,                obtaining seabuckthorn seed meal powder, heating                the powder, cooling the mixture, adding cellulase                enzyme, performing enzymolysis in water bath,                centrifuging and obtaining the supernatant liquid,                collecting the filtrate to obtain oligosaccharide                extract, performing enzymolysis, adding protease,                performing heat preservation, inactivating enzyme,                centrifuging and removing residue, centrifuging the                supernatant, desalting oligosaccharide and                oligopeptide extract by anion and cation exchange                resin, mixing the oligopeptide enzymolysis liquid                and oligosaccharide enzymolysis liquid and                vacuum-concentrating the enzymolysis liquid.
   USE - The combined method is useful for preparation                of seabuckthorn oligosaccharide and                oligopeptide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method turns industrial wastes into                treasures and increases the added value of                seabuckthorn.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Combined preparation method of seabuckthorn                oligosaccharide and oligopeptide involves drying                seabuckthorn seeds, peeling until moisture content                reaches 12-20%, crushing the seabuckthorn seeds by                20 mesh sieve, performing supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction at 20-30 MPa and                30-40&#176;C, crushing the seabuckthorn seed                meal after supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                to obtain 80 mesh, where the material liquid mass                ratio of seabuckthorn seed meal and purified water                is 1:10-1:12 (g/ml), obtaining seabuckthorn seed                meal powder, heating the powder at                70-85&#176;C, stirring at 300-450 revolutions                per minute (rpm) for 20-30 minutes, cooling the                mixture, adjusting the pH of the mixture to 4-7                using acid, adding 5500-6900 U/g cellulase enzyme,                performing enzymolysis in water bath of                40-60&#176;C for 2-5 hours, centrifuging and                obtaining the supernatant liquid, where the                intercepting molecular weight film is &#8814; 3                kDd, collecting the filtrate to obtain                oligosaccharide extract, performing enzymolysis to                the centrifugally separated residue and the trapped                liquid, adjusting the pH to 7-11 using alkali,                adding 7500-9000 U/g protease, performing heat                preservation at 50-60&#176;C for 4-8 hours,                inactivating enzyme for 10 minutes, centrifuging                and removing residue at 4000 rpm, centrifuging the                supernatant with film to intercept molecular weight                of 1 kDd oligopeptide extract after the two-stage                enzymolysis to obtain oligopeptide extract,                desalting oligosaccharide and oligopeptide extract                by anion and cation exchange resin, mixing the                oligopeptide enzymolysis liquid and oligosaccharide                enzymolysis liquid, and vacuum-concentrating the                enzymolysis liquid to 10-15%, where the air inlet                temperature is 180-220&#176;C, the air outlet                temperature is 80-90&#176;C, the sampling speed                is 30-55 ml/minute, revolution is 11000-15000 rpm,                powder yield is more than 95% and moisture content                is less than 5%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The enzyme comprises                  2200-2800 U/g cellulase, 1300-1600 U/g pectinase                  and 2000-2500 U/g xylanase. The protease                  comprises alkaline protease, neutral protease and                  papain. 3600-4300 U/g alkaline protease,                  2200-2600 U/g neutral protease and 1700-2100 U/g                  pawpaw protease. The extraction rate of                  seabuckthorn oligosaccharide is 70-75%, the                  content is 30-35%, and the extraction rate of                  seabuckthorn oligopeptide is 50-60%, content is                  30-40%, total flavonoids (calculated as rutin)                  content is 2-3.5%.Preferred Method: The method involves adding                  enzyme into first section of enzymolysis,                  performing second section of enzymolysis by                  adding alkaline protease for 2-4 hours, and                  adding neutral protease and papain for 2-5                  hours.

   USE - Heat-volatile plant deodorant.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat-volatile plant deodorant has                characteristics of natural extract, controllable                volatile rate and controllability volatile                concentration, and is suitable for various hotels,                hotels, public toilets, and rubbish kinds of public                places and places where air peculiar smell is                needed to be treated. In the public service, the                environmental protection field has wide                application, installation and maintenance are                simple and easy to popularize.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A heat-volatile plant deodorant comprises                deodorant stock solution and speed-regulating stock                solution.    INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a                deodorizing bottle of heat-volatile plant                deodorant; and (2) a using method of heat-volatile                plant deodorant.

PN CN111700259-A
TI Preparing instant dry dish dip by removing            impurities from spice, soybeans, peanuts and oats,            stir-frying, crushing, mixing with crushed sesame seeds            and seasoning, adding carbon dioxide, ultrasonically            processing and packaging in bags.
AU LI J
   YE D
   WANG C
   WANG Q
   LIU P
   YIN H
AE SICHUAN TIANWEI FOOD GROUP CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202096929X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing instant dry dish dip                involves (a) removing impurity by removing                impurities from spice, soybeans, peanuts and oats,                (b) performing raw material maturation by                stir-frying the raw materials after removing                impurities, (c) crushing by crushing the spices,                sieving through 60-80 mesh sieve to obtain material                I, then mixing soybeans, peanuts, oats and sesame                seeds, pulverizing, sieving through 60-100 mesh                sieve to obtain material II and then crushing the                seasoning to obtain material III, (d) mixing the                material I, material II and material III to obtain                material IV, (e) placing the material IV in a                closed environment, adding carbon dioxide at a                pressure of 15-50 MPa and ultrasonically processing                and (f) then packaging the obtained materials in                separate bags.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing instant dry                dish dip.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses high-pressure carbon dioxide                and ultrasonic waves to sterilize the materials to                kill Salmonella, Escherichia coli and other                microorganisms and reduces the probability of                food-borne diseases occurring in the instant dry                dish dip, where agitation is performed to make the                dip fully contact with carbon dioxide which solves                the disadvantage of insufficient sterilization in                most non-thermal sterilization technologies when                sterilizing powdered materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing instant dry dish dip                involves (a) removing impurity by removing                impurities from spice, soybeans, peanuts and oats,                (b) performing raw material maturation by                stir-frying the raw materials after removing                impurities, by stir-frying the spices at 40-60                degrees C for 10-20 minutes, frying the peanuts and                peeling for 10-20 minutes, frying the soybeans for                15-20 minutes, frying the oats for 5-10 minutes and                frying the sesame for 5-10 minutes, (c) crushing by                after crushing the spices, sieving through 60-80                mesh sieve to obtain material I, then mixing                soybeans, peanuts, oats and sesame seeds in a                weight ratio of 10-15:5-10:10-15:1-8, pulverizing,                sieving through 60-100 mesh sieve to obtain                material II and then crushing the seasoning to                obtain material III, (d) mixing the material I,                material II and material III at a weight ratio of                8-10:6-8:2-5 to obtain material IV, (e) placing the                material IV in a closed environment, adding carbon                dioxide at a pressure of 15-50 MPa and                ultrasonically processing for 3-5 times under the                conditions of 30-40 degrees C, 100-300                revolutions/minute (rpm) and 300-600 W, where each                time for 30-40 minutes, and (f) then packaging the                obtained materials in separate bags.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The spice comprises                  20-30 pts. wt. Erjingtiao pepper, 1-5 pts. wt.                  Hanyuan pepper, 0.5-2 pts. wt. star anise, 0.1-1                  pt. wt. fennel, 0.1-1 pt. wt. cinnamon and                  0.1-0.5 pt. wt. bay leaves. The seasoning                  comprises at least two of sugar, monosodium                  glutamate and salt. Preferred Method: The spices                  are crushed and passed through a 60-mesh sieve.                  The mixture of soybeans, peanuts, oats and sesame                  are crushed and passed through a 100-mesh sieve.                  The soybeans, peanuts, oats and sesame seeds are                  mixed in a weight ratio of 15:10:10:6. The                  material I, material II and material III are                  mixed in a weight ratio of 8-9:6-8:2-3. The                  pressure of the carbon dioxide is 20-30 MPa. The                  ultrasonic evaluation rate is 400-600 W, the                  temperature is 32-35 degrees C and the rotation                  speed is 150-200 rpm. During the ultrasonic                  processing step, the interval between each                  treatment is 20 minutes.

PN CN111533150-A; CN111533150-B
TI Preparing ultrafine nano calcium carbonate            involves adding water to calcium oxide to prepare a            crude calcium hydroxide slurry, adding calcium ion            dispersant, carbonizing, and filtering and drying crude            calcium carbonate slurry.
AU HE J
   WU X
   XIE B
AE ANHUI EAST CALCIUM IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202080726V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultrafine nano calcium carbonate                involves adding water to calcium oxide to prepare a                crude calcium hydroxide slurry, adjusting the water                content in the calcium hydroxide coarse slurry to                prepare a calcium hydroxide slurry with a mass                concentration of 10-14%, adding calcium ion                dispersant to the calcium hydroxide slurry, and                mixing uniformly to obtain a calcium hydroxide                suspension, where the amount of calcium ion                dispersant added is 2-4% of the mass of calcium                hydroxide in the calcium hydroxide slurry. A mixed                gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen is poured into                the calcium hydroxide suspension while stirring the                calcium hydroxide suspension at a stirring rate of                100-120 revolutions per minute. The inflow rate of                the mixed gas is 1-1.3 cubic meters per second, the                mass fraction of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is                30-46%, and the carbonization is performed at 40-45                degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing ultrafine nano calcium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables uniform dispersion of                calcium hydroxide molecules in the calcium                hydroxide slurry, that results in promoting the                reaction between the calcium hydroxide molecules                and the carbon dioxide molecules, and improving the                nucleation rate of calcium carbonate crystal                nuclei.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ultrafine nano calcium carbonate                involves adding water to calcium oxide to prepare a                crude calcium hydroxide slurry, adjusting the water                content in the calcium hydroxide coarse slurry to                prepare a calcium hydroxide slurry with a mass                concentration of 10-14%, adding calcium ion                dispersant to the calcium hydroxide slurry, and                mixing uniformly to obtain a calcium hydroxide                suspension, where the amount of calcium ion                dispersant added is 2-4% of the mass of calcium                hydroxide in the calcium hydroxide slurry. A mixed                gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen is poured into                the calcium hydroxide suspension while stirring the                calcium hydroxide suspension at a stirring rate of                100-120 revolutions per minute. The inflow rate of                the mixed gas is 1-1.3 cubic meters per second, the                mass fraction of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is                30-46%, and the carbonization is performed at 40-45                degrees C. After carbonization to the pH value of                8.5-9.2, the crystal form regulator is added, where                the addition amount of the crystal form regulator                is 0.8-1.2% of the calcium hydroxide mass in the                calcium hydroxide slurry. Carbonization is                continued to a pH of 6.5-7 to obtain a crude                calcium carbonate slurry. The crude calcium                carbonate slurry is filtered that involves using                50% ethanol solution to wash 3-4 times, and dried                at 95-105 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The calcium ion                  dispersant comprises 15-26 pts. wt. carboxymethyl                  chitosan, 10-19 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol,                  11-24 pts. wt. fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene                  ether. Preferred Components: The crystal form                  regulator is selected from sodium sulfate, sodium                  acetate and sodium citrate. Preferred Conditions:                  The carbonization step involves pouring mixed gas                  of carbon dioxide and nitrogen into the calcium                  hydroxide suspension while stirring the calcium                  hydroxide suspension at a stirring rate of                  100-120 revolutions per minute. The inlet rate of                  the mixed gas is 1-1.3 cubic meters per second.                  The carbonization pressure is controlled at 3-3.4                  megaPascal.

PN CN110270193-A
TI Method for purifying volatile organic compounds waste gas, involves contacting volatile organic compounds exhaust gas and adsorbent in adsorption device, followed by converting into carbon dioxide and water and discharging to atmosphere.
AU GAO L
   ZHANG J
   XU Z
AE SUZHOU MOEVE ENVIRONMENTAL ENG CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019825990
AB    NOVELTY - A volatile organic compounds waste gas purifying method involves contacting the volatile organic compounds exhaust gas and the adsorbent in first adsorption device to obtain a low concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas, causing the low-concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas to enter a purification reaction device, and converting into carbon dioxide and water in the purification reaction device, and discharging to an atmosphere, opening an adsorption control mechanism of second adsorption device, and closing the regeneration control mechanism of second adsorption device, causing the high-concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas generated, entering the low-concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas discharged from a part of the exhaust gas and second adsorption device to the purification reaction device, converting into carbon dioxide and water in the purification reaction device, and discharging to the atmosphere.
   USE - Method for purifying volatile organic compounds waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables purifying volatile organic compounds waste gas by effectively process a tissue or tissue-free volatile organic compounds waste gas discharged by industry, and processing effect meets the national or local predetermined related volatile organic compounds waste gas emission standard.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A volatile organic compounds waste gas purifying method involves contacting the volatile organic compounds exhaust gas and the adsorbent in first adsorption device to make a portion of the volatile organic compounds organic matter in the volatile organic compounds exhaust gas is adsorbed by the adsorbent to obtain a low concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas, causing the low-concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas to enter a normal temperature deep purification reaction device, and converting into carbon dioxide and water in the normal temperature deep purification reaction device, and discharging to an atmosphere, after first adsorption device is saturated, closing the adsorption control mechanism of first adsorption device and opening the regeneration control mechanism of first adsorption device to make first adsorption device is in a regeneration state, opening an adsorption control mechanism of second adsorption device, and closing the regeneration control mechanism of second adsorption device to make second adsorption device is in an adsorption state, causing the high-concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas generated by the regeneration of first adsorption device to enter a low-temperature catalytic oxidation reaction device for purification treatment to make the purified exhaust gas enters first adsorption device through the desorption device, entering the low-concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas discharged from a part of the exhaust gas and second adsorption device to the normal temperature deep purification reaction device, converting into carbon dioxide and water in the normal temperature deep purification reaction device, and discharging to the atmosphere. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a volatile organic compounds waste gas deep purifying system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The purifying method further involves contacting the volatile organic compounds exhaust gas and the adsorbent at one third adsorption device to adsorb a portion of the volatile organic compounds organic matter in the volatile organic compounds exhaust gas by the adsorbent, obtaining low-concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas, causing the low-concentration volatile organic compounds exhaust gas to enter a normal temperature deep purification reaction device, and converting into carbon dioxide and water in the normal temperature deep purification reaction device, and discharging to the atmosphere, or using fresh air to supplement fresh air into the air duct of a desorption device, discharging the carbon dioxide and water discharged from the normal temperature deep purification reaction device into the atmosphere by using an air guiding device, when the concentration of volatile organic compounds exhausted by first adsorption device or second adsorption device or third adsorption device is higher than 150 mg/m3, closing the adsorption control mechanism of first adsorption device or second adsorption device or third adsorption device, opening the regeneration control mechanism of first adsorption device or second adsorption device or third adsorption device, where first adsorption device or second adsorption device or third adsorption device is in a regenerative state. Preferred Components: The adsorbent comprises a regenerable adsorbent, where the regenerated adsorbent comprises activated carbon and/or molecular sieve. The molecular sieve comprises silicon dioxide to aluminum oxide in the ratio of 1.8-2.3. The low-temperature catalytic oxidation reaction device comprises a platinum and/or palladium catalyst supported on aluminum oxide.

PT P
PN CN109354050-A; CN109354050-B
TI Preparing calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide used in architectural coating, involves calcining dolomite powder, evaporating ammonia, filtering, and grinding, and passing ammonia gas into magnesium chloride-rich solution and filtering.
AU FAN T
   LI L
AE SHENYANG CHENXING CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENYANG CHENXING CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201920677D
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide involves (1) crushing dolomite ore and grinding to obtain dolomite powder, (2) calcining dolomite powder to obtain calcined powder, and recycling carbon dioxide gas, (3) subjecting calcined powder and ammonium chloride solution to ammonia evaporating reaction, and filtering to obtain filtrate and filter residue, and recovering ammonia gas, (4) introducing ammonia gas into calcium chloride-rich solution, saturating ammonia, introducing carbon dioxide, filtering, drying, and grinding to obtain high-purity calcium carbonate powder product, (5) adding filter residue to filtrate, performing ammonia evaporating reaction, and filtering to obtain filtrate and filter residue, distilling ammonia gas and recycling, and (6) passing ammonia gas into magnesium chloride-rich solution, and filtering to obtain filtrate and filter residue, washing residue, drying and grinding to obtain high-purity magnesium hydroxide powder product.
   USE - Preparation method of calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide used in architectural coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide with high purity, excellent dispersibility, high value utilization of carbon dioxide byproducts, small amount of waste, high heat utilization rate, and low energy consumption, and which recycles the process material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide involves (1) crushing dolomite ore, and grinding to obtain dolomite powder of more than 200 mesh, (2) placing the dolomite powder in a calcining furnace, controlling the temperature at 950-1000 degrees C, and calcining for 1-2 hours to obtain calcined powder, and recycling the carbon dioxide gas generated in the process of calcining, (3) subjecting the calcined powder and ammonium chloride solution to ammonia evaporating reaction until no precipitate is precipitated, and filtering to obtain filtrate and filter residue, where the filtrate is calcium chloride solution, and recovering the ammonia gas, (4) introducing ammonia gas into calcium chloride-rich solution, making the ammonia to saturation state in the solution, introducing carbon dioxide into the solution until no precipitate occurs, filtering, where the filtrate is circulating mother liquid (A), washing the filter residue 3 times, filtering, drying, and grinding to obtain high-purity calcium carbonate powder product, (5) adding filter residue to circulating mother liquor (A), performing ammonia evaporating reaction until no precipitate is separated out, and filtering to obtain filtrate and filter residue, where the filtrate is magnesium chloride solution, and the filter residue is solid ammonium chloride undissolved substance, distilling ammonia gas, and recycling, and (6) passing ammonia gas obtained in the step (3) and step (5) into magnesium chloride-rich solution until no precipitate is separated out, and filtering to obtain filtrate and filter residue, where the filtrate is circulating mother liquid (B), washing the filter residue, filtering, drying, and grinding to obtain high-purity magnesium hydroxide powder product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The circulating mother liquid (A) is an ammonium chloride-containing solution. The circulating mother liquid (B) is an ammonium chloride-containing solution. The filter residue produced in the step (6) is an inactive, water-insoluble ammonium chloride. Preferred Composition: The concentration of ammonium chloride in the circulating mother liquid (A) is 0.5-2 mol/L. The mass ratio of ammonium chloride to magnesium in calcined powder is 2-3. In the circulating mother liquid (B), the concentration of ammonium chloride is 0.5-2 mol/L. The mass ratio of ammonium chloride to calcium in calcined powder is 1.7-2.5. Preferred Conditions: In the step (4), the reaction temperature is 20-25 degrees C, the ammonia gas passage time is 20-40 minutes, the carbon dioxide passage time is 30-90 minutes, and the total reaction time is 1.5-3 hours. In step (5), the reaction temperature of the ammonia evaporating reaction is 150-300 degrees C, and the reaction time is 3-4 hours. In step (6), the reaction temperature is 20-25 degrees C, the ammonia passage time is 20-40 minutes, and the reaction time is 2-4 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In the step (3), the ammonia evaporating reaction temperature is 95-100 degrees C, and the reaction time is 2-4 hours.

PN CN108822580-A
TI Preparing low-viscosity active nano-calcium carbonate comprises e.g. introducing kiln gas with carbon dioxide into calcium hydroxide suspension, carbonizing, surface modifying, dewatering, drying, pulverizing and sieving.
AU YANG J
AE ANHUI YANGLIUQING CALCIUM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019124263
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-viscosity active nano-calcium carbonate comprises e.g. (i) introducing kiln gas with carbon dioxide into a calcium hydroxide suspension, carbonizing, and adding calcium hydroxide 0.5-5% of crystal regulator, (ii) allowing to stand the prepared nano-calcium carbonate slurry until the pH is stable, (iii) introducing kiln gas having a carbon dioxide into the stabilized nano-calcium carbonate slurry, carrying out the surface modification, and stirring, (iv) dewatering, drying and pulverizing the surface-modified nano-calcium carbonate slurry by pressure filtration, (v) heating the pulverized nano-calcium carbonate and composite modifier in a ratio of 6:(1-3) to 200-300 degrees C and transferring into a ball mill for 45-60 minutes, and (vi) mixing the ground mixed powder with deionized water, stirring and adding polyoxyethylene ether as an emulsifier, and ultrasonically shaking for 30-45 minutes, at the end of the reaction, dehydrating, drying, pulverizing and sieving.
   USE - The method s useful for preparing low-viscosity active nano-calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-calcium carbonate has reduces viscosity, small average particle size, narrow particle size distribution and good compatibility and stability with aqueous system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing low-viscosity active nano-calcium carbonate comprises (i) introducing kiln gas with carbon dioxide into a calcium hydroxide suspension having a concentration of 20%, controlling the temperature at 25-45 degrees C for carbonization, at the same time, and adding calcium hydroxide 0.5-5% of crystal regulator until the pH of the slurry is 7 to obtain a nano-calcium carbonate slurry, (ii) allowing to stand the prepared nano-calcium carbonate slurry until the pH of the nano-calcium carbonate slurry is stable, (iii) introducing kiln gas having a carbon dioxide temperature of 50-70 degrees C into the stabilized nano-calcium carbonate slurry, at the same time, carrying out the surface modification of the nanometer calcium carbonate by adding fatty acid or a fatty acid derivative modifier having a calcium hydroxide mass of 5-10% to the nano-calcium carbonate slurry, and stirring at 500-800 rotations/minute for 1-3 hours, (iv) dewatering, drying and pulverizing the surface-modified nano-calcium carbonate slurry by pressure filtration, (v) heating the pulverized nano-calcium carbonate and composite modifier in a ratio of 6:(1-3) to 200-300 degrees C and transferring into a ball mill for 45-60 minutes, and (vi) mixing the ground mixed powder with deionized water, stirring and adding polyoxyethylene ether as an emulsifier, and ultrasonically shaking for 30-45 minutes, at the end of the reaction, dehydrating, drying, pulverizing and sieving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the crystal modifier is a mixture of sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate and polyvinylpyrrolidone. The mass ratio of the sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate to polyvinylpyrrolidone is 1: (50-100). In the step (iii), the fatty acid or fatty acid derivative has a carbon chain length of 8-18 carbon atoms. The fatty acid is at least one of linoleic acid, palmitic acid and linolenic acid. The mass of the talc powder is 0.5-2% of the mass of the powder after drying. Preferred Method: In the step (v), the preparation of composite modifier is mixing sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, polyacrylamide, vinyl bisstearamide, and organic bentonite in a mass ratio (25-35):(2-5):(10-15): (2-8) evenly, gelatinizing with hydrogenated castor oil, treating with high temperature and high pressure steam for 30-45 minutes, finally drying, adding talc powder, and grinding for 30 minutes. The water vapor pressure is not less than 8.7 MPa, and the temperature is not less than 300 degrees C. In the step (vi), the ultrasonic frequency used for ultrasonic oscillation is 100-200 kHz.

PT P
PN US2018002248-A1; US10087121-B2
TI Production of hydrocarbon fuel containing five to twelve carbons from organic material applying heat source to heat organic feedstock and oxygen, partially combusting, conditioning and further cleaning, and collecting dimethyl ether.
AU KELLY K S
   MELNICHUK L J
AE KELLY K S (KELL-Individual)
   MELNICHUK L J (MELN-Individual)
   KELLY K S (KELL-Individual)
   MELNICHUK L J (MELN-Individual)
GA 2018009905
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrocarbon fuel containing 5-12 carbons (5-12C) is produced from organic material by applying heat source to heat organic feedstock (12) and oxygen, partially combusting, substantially removing unwanted solid and liquid matter, compressing 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream, conditioning and further cleaning 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream, selectively removing carbon dioxide from 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream, catalytically treating, collecting dimethyl ether from 1st mixture and catalytically reacting to produce 2nd mixture containing alkanes, and selectively obtaining 5-12C alkanes.
   USE - Production of 5-12 carbon-containing (5-12C) hydrocarbon fuel from organic material.
   ADVANTAGE - Unused combustible matter, such as light oils, olefins, and tars, is recycled, increasing the amount of synthesis gas that can be made per unit of organic feedstock input and reducing usable waste, and as a result, increasing the efficiency of the overall reaction process with respect to the production of 5-12C hydrocarbons produced per unit of organic feedstock input.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrocarbon fuel containing 5-12 carbons (5-12C) is produced from organic material by (a) applying heat source to heat organic feedstock (12) and oxygen at substoichiometric conditions to a temperature sufficient for partial combustion of organic feedstock to occur and ceasing application of heat source once partial combustion has commenced; (b) partially combusting organic feedstock to produce synthesis gas stream containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen; (c) substantially removing unwanted solid and liquid matter comprising oxides, ash, and greater than or equal to 10C hydrocarbons from synthesis gas stream to produce 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen; (d) compressing 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream and substantially removing water; (e) conditioning and further cleaning 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream by substantially removing inorganic elements and inorganic compounds from 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream to provide 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen; (f) selectively removing carbon dioxide from 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream; (g) catalytically treating 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream to produce 1st mixture containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and dimethyl ether; (h) collecting dimethyl ether from 1st mixture; (i) catalytically reacting dimethyl ether to produce 2nd mixture containing alkanes; and (j) selectively obtaining 5-12C alkanes from 2nd mixture. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of 5-12C hydrocarbon fuel from organic material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of the production method starting from solid or semi-solid organic feedstock.
   Production of 5-12C hydrocarbon fuel from organic material (10)
   Organic feedstock (12)
   Particle size reduction step (14)
   Drying process (16)
   Surge bin (18)
   Gasifier (20)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Base metal catalyst is nickel catalyst, copper catalyst, zinc catalyst, or iron catalyst. Zeolite catalyst has silica:alumina ratio of 298:1-2000:1. Hydrogen:carbon monoxide ratio is 1:1-1:2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Hydrocarbons having carbon skeleton of greater than or equal to 10 are light oils, tar, or olefins. The 2nd mixture further contains alkenes, alkynes, aromatic compounds, or naphthalenes. Organic feedstock is a solid, a gas, or a liquid. Preferred Processes: Step (e) further includes removing a portion of alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes contained in 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream and reacting removed alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes in a reformer to produce carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen. Reformer is an autothermal reformer, where alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes are reacted in the reformer at 950-1100 degrees C and less than or equal to 9790 kPa. Carbon dioxide is selectively removed from the 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream by acid gas/carbon dioxide removal process. The 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream is catalytically treated to produce 1st mixture comprising carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and dimethyl ether using base metal catalyst. The 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream is catalytically treated to produce 1st mixture containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and dimethyl ether using base metal catalyst at 225-300 degrees C and 2.5-7.5 MPa. Dimethyl ether in 1st mixture is catalytically treated to produce 2nd mixture containing alkanes using zeolite catalyst. Dimethyl ether in the 1st mixture is catalytically treated at 350-450 degrees C and 140-350 kPa. The 5-12C alkanes are selectively obtained from the 2nd mixture by distillation. Alkenes, alkynes, aromatic compounds, or naphthalenes are selectively obtained from the 2nd mixture by distillation. Step (f) further includes using a water/gas shift reaction. Water/gas shift reaction increases the ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide. Production of 5-12C hydrocarbon fuel from organic material comprises, e.g.: (a) applying heat source to heat organic feedstock and oxygen at substoichiometric conditions up to 800 degrees C, ceasing application of heat source once partial combustion in exothermic reaction has commenced, partially combusting organic feedstock without continuous application of external heat source to produce synthesis gas stream containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen, substantially removing unwanted solid and liquid matter containing oxides, ash, and greater than or equal to 10C hydrocarbons from synthesis gas stream to produce 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen, compressing 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream and substantially removing water, removing a portion of any alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes from 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream, reacting removed alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes with catalyst to produce supplemental gas stream containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen, removing inorganic elements and inorganic compounds from 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream to provide 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen, merging 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream with supplemental gas stream, selectively removing carbon dioxide from 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream merged with supplemental gas stream, catalytically treating 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream merged with supplemental gas stream to produce 1st mixture containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and dimethyl ether, collecting dimethyl ether from 1st mixture, catalytically reacting dimethyl ether to produce 2nd mixture containing alkanes, and selectively obtaining 5-12C alkanes from 2nd mixture; or (b) forming 1st synthesis gas stream by (i) applying heat source to heat organic feedstock and oxygen at substoichiometric conditions up to 800 degrees C and ceasing application of heat source once partial combustion in an exothermic reaction has commenced, (ii) partially combusting organic feedstock without continuous application of heat source to produce synthesis gas stream containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen, (iii) substantially removing unwanted solid and liquid matter comprising oxides, ash, and greater than or equal to 10C hydrocarbons from synthesis gas stream to produce 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen, (iv) recycling and enjoining greater than or equal to 10C hydrocarbons to step (ii) for partial combustion, (v) compressing 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream and substantially removing water, and (vi) removing a portion of any alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes from 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream; forming 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream by (i) reacting removed alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes of step (vi) with a catalyst to produce carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen and selectively removing carbon dioxide from 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream; merging 1st cleaned synthesis gas stream and 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream to provide a merged cleaned synthesis gas stream; catalytically treating merged synthesis gas stream to produce 1st mixture containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and dimethyl ether; collecting dimethyl ether from 1st mixture and recycling carbon monoxide back into 2nd cleaned synthesis gas stream for additional catalytic treatment; catalytically reacting dimethyl ether to produce 2nd mixture containing alkanes; and selectively obtaining 5-12C alkanes from 2nd mixture.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PN WO2021182391-A1; JP2022507176-X
TI Water sterilizer has discharge pipe for            discharging sterilizing water containing hypochlorous            acid or chlorous acid from sterilizing water outlet            that is connected to outlet of static mixer pipe of            stirring tank.
AU TOSHIHISA T
AE MATSUBARA CO LTD (MATS-Non-standard)
GA 2021A7427D
AB    NOVELTY - The water sterilizer has a carbon dioxide gas                injection piping (4) with a check valve (3) between                a raw material water inlet (1) and a sterilizing                water outlet (2). Piping (6) for injecting sodium                hypochlorite aqueous solution or sodium chlorite                aqueous solution with check valve (5) is provided.                A stirring tank (7) is provided with a static mixer                (8) with stirring blades inserted into the tube.                The piping for injecting sodium hypochlorite                aqueous solution or sodium chlorite aqueous                solution is connected on the upper side. A main                pipe (9) is connected to the inlet of the stirring                tank. A hole serves as an inlet (13) for raw water                to which carbon dioxide gas (16) and sodium                hypochlorite aqueous solution (17) or sodium                chlorite aqueous solution are added at the bottom                of the static mixer tube. A discharge pipe (18) for                discharging sterilizing water (15) containing                hypochlorous acid or chlorous acid from the                sterilizing water outlet is connected to the outlet                of the static mixer pipe.
   USE - Water sterilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The sterilized water containing hypochlorous                acid and chlorine dioxide is obtained from aqueous                solution of sodium hypochlorite or aqueous solution                of sodium chlorite by a stable potential of                hydrogen (pH) adjusting action of carbon dioxide                gas. The water sterilizer is effective in                stabilizing the pH of the sterilizing water                generated by mixing carbon dioxide gas with the raw                material water in the piping and the aqueous                solution of sodium hypochlorite or sodium chlorite,                and stirring with a static mixer in the stirring                tank after mixing. The sterilizing water produced                by the apparatus maintains a predetermined range of                pH in which hypochlorous acid stably exists for six                months or more.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The main pipe from the raw material water                inlet to the stirring tank inlet, which has a                pumping unit for sending the raw material water                (14) upward, is connected to the carbon dioxide gas                injection piping and the piping for injecting                sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution or sodium                chlorite aqueous solution. The sodium hypochlorite                aqueous solution or sodium-chlorite aqueous                solution is conveyed by tube pump (12). The static                mixer is resin-made. A tee joint (10) for injecting                carbon dioxide gas is connected to the main pipe. A                sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution or a sodium                chlorite aqueous solution injection tee joint (11)                is connected immediately after the carbon dioxide                injection tee joint. The carbon dioxide injection                tee joint is connected to the carbon dioxide                injection pipe. The tee joint for injecting sodium                hypochlorite aqueous solution or sodium chlorite                aqueous solution is connected to the piping for                injecting sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution or                sodium chlorite aqueous solution. Reynolds number                of particle is equal to ratio of multiplication of                flow rate and carbon dioxide bubble diameter to                multiplication of hydraulic viscosity and pipe                cross-sectional area, where the Reynolds number of                particles for a carbon dioxide bubble having a                representative length of 0.3 mm is set to 500 or                less. The flow rate of water in the flow path from                the raw material water inlet to the sterilizing                water outlet through the stirring tank is 500 L or                less per hour. The diameter of the main pipe, the                pipe of the static mixer and the discharge pipe is                11 mm or more, and 1 to 8 holes having a diameter                of 4 mm or more are provided in the pipe of the                static mixer. The static mixer in the stirring tank                constitutes a hydrocyclone that separates the                carbon dioxide gas bubbles of the raw material                water to which the carbon dioxide gas and the                sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution or the sodium                chlorite aqueous solution are added into coarse                bubbles and fine bubbles. The water sterilizer has                an element that separates excess carbon dioxide gas                and sterilizing water that did not dissolve in the                stirring tank and releases the excess carbon                dioxide gas to the atmosphere. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for a manufacturing method of the                sterile water involves using a sterilized water                production device equipped with the stirring tank                having the static mixer with stirring blades                inserted into the tube. Carbon dioxide gas is                injected from the carbon dioxide gas injection                piping into the main pipe from the raw material                water inlet to the stirring tank inlet, which has a                pumping unit for sending the raw material water                upward. The sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution or                the sodium chlorite aqueous solution is injected                from the pipe for injecting the sodium hypochlorite                aqueous solution or the sodium chlorite aqueous                solution. Raw material water to which carbon                dioxide gas and an aqueous solution of sodium                hypochlorite or an aqueous solution of sodium                chlorite are added is supplied from the main pipe                to the stirring tank. The sterilized water                containing hypochlorous acid or chlorous acid is                produced by flowing the raw material water from the                inlet provided at the lower portion of the pipe of                the static mixer in the stirring tank and stirring                with the static mixer. Disinfecting water                containing hypochlorous acid or chlorous acid is                discharged from the sterilizing water outlet of the                discharge pipe connected to the outlet of the                static mixer pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                water sterilizer.
   Raw material water inlet (1)
   Sterilizing water outlet (2)
   Check valve of carbon dioxide gas injection                piping (3)
   Carbon dioxide gas injection piping                (4)
   Check valve of piping for injecting sodium                hypochlorite aqueous solution or sodium chlorite                aqueous solution (5)
   Piping for injecting sodium hypochlorite                aqueous solution or sodium chlorite aqueous                solution (6)
   Stirring tank (7)
   Static mixer (8)
   Main pipe (9)
   Tee joint for injecting carbon dioxide gas                (10)
   Sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution or                sodium chlorite aqueous solution injection tee                joint (11)
   Tube pump (12)
   Inlet for raw water (13)
   Raw material water (14)
   Sterilizing water (15)
   Carbon dioxide gas (16)
   Sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution                (17)

PT P
PN CN113060749-A
TI Preparing superfine calcium carbonate comprises driving high-purity carbon dioxide into reaction tank, boiling calcium carbonate particles, and processing in electrostatic precipitator.
AU YAN Z
AE YAN Z (YANZ-Individual)
GA 2021805002
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing superfine calcium carbonate comprises driving high-purity carbon dioxide into reaction tank, equipping bottom of the buffer tank with a rotating knife row, heating buffer tank by an infrared heating device to 280-350 degrees C, cutting boiling calcium carbonate particles, turning on induced draft fan, opening discharge valve of buffer tank, passing mixture in the buffer tank through cyclone dust collector, baghouse, collecting using electrostatic precipitator by ultra-fine calcium carbonate products, cyclone dust collector, bag dust collector, heating electric precipitator by infrared heating device, and ensuring insulation of cyclone dust collector.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing superfine calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has a simple preparation process, high production efficiency, stable product quality, safety, environmental protection and pollution-free features, and can be combined with ultrasonic vibration to improve stability of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing superfine calcium carbonate comprises (i) driving high-purity carbon dioxide using gas compressor into reaction tank, cutting off pipeline valve, stop injecting carbon dioxide gas, heating up reaction tank using infrared heating device, heating at rate of 5-10 degrees C/minutes, stopping heating and warming when the temperature in the tank reaches 80-95 degrees C, (ii) electrically heating refined calcium hydroxide cooked pulp with 5-8% in the storage tank to 80-90 degrees C, pressurizing using centrifugal pump and calcium hydroxide cooked slurry to 0.8 MPa and driving it into the reaction tank through a spray head, and controlling spray time for 1-1.2 hours, reacting quickly high-purity carbon dioxide gas with calcium hydroxide cooked slurry to produce calcium carbonate and water, the chemical reaction formula is as follows: calcium hydroxide and carbon dioxide giving raises calcium carbonate and water, a small amount of carbon dioxide, calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide, water reacting to obtain calcium bicarbonate under the condition of excessive carbon dioxide gas in the reaction tank, opening discharge valve of reaction tank, introducing reaction mixture using buffer tank; (iii) equipping bottom of the buffer tank with a rotating knife row, heating buffer tank by an infrared heating device to 280-350 degrees C, heating calcium bicarbonate entering the buffer tank and decomposing into calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide, water at high temperature. The chemical reaction formula: calcium bicarbonate and calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide, and water, buffer tank at 280-350 degrees C, the water in the buffer tank generates water vapor at high temperature, reacting mixture in the buffer tank for 2-2.5 hours, turning on rotating knife row and rotating laser, cutting and boiling calcium carbonate particles at high speed by rotating knife row, cutting boiling calcium carbonate particles at high speed by a rotating laser twice for 5-6 hours. The particle size of calcium carbonate particles after cutting 30-80 nm, and (iv) turning on induced draft fan, opening discharge valve of buffer tank, passing mixture in the buffer tank through cyclone dust collector, baghouse, collecting using electrostatic precipitator by ultra-fine calcium carbonate products, cyclone dust collector, bag dust collector, heating electric precipitator by infrared heating device, ensuring insulation of cyclone dust collector, where the temperature in the insulation layer of the bag filter and the insulation layer of the electrostatic precipitator is more than 270 degrees C, the water vapor and carbon dioxide after dust removal are introduced into the water tank by the induced draft fan, water vapor becomes liquid water when cold, the liquid water can be used as a cooked slurry equipped with calcium hydroxide. The chemical reaction: calcium hydroxide and hydrogen give raising calcium hydroxide, where the purified carbon dioxide gas can be injected into the carbon dioxide gas tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The buffer tank comprises a tank body, a buffer tank feed port, a buffer tank discharge port, a laser head, knife teeth, and a rotary knife row drive motor, motor and laser assembly, rotating laser, rotating knife row. The upper part of the tank body is provided with a buffer tank inlet. The lower side is provided with a buffer tank outlet. The rotary knife row is provided at the bottom of the tank. The rotary knife row is driven to rotate by the rotary knife row drive motor. The outer side of the rotary knife row is provided multiple of cutter teeth. The rotating cutter row is equipped with a rotating laser. The top of the rotating laser is provided with a multiple of laser heads. The bottom passes through the motor and laser assembly. The boiling calcium carbonate particles are cut at a high speed by the rotating laser for a second time. The diameter of the calcium carbonate particles after cutting is between 30-80 nm.

PT P
PN CN109470826-A
TI Calculating field habitat plant metabolic water utilization amount and actual water demand comprises selecting leaves of second unfolding leaf, calculating data of net photosynthetic rate and transpiration rate and actual water demand.
AU WU Y
   RAO S
   FANG L
   SU Y
   LIANG X
AE INST GEOCHEMISTRY CHINESE ACAD SCI (GEOC-Non-standard)
GA 201926453X
AB    NOVELTY - Calculating field habitat plant metabolic water utilization amount and actual water demand comprises (i) selecting leaves of second unfolding leaf on other branch of plant grown in environment under investigation, measuring photosynthetic; (ii) calculating data of net photosynthetic rate and transpiration rate; (iii) calculating data of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration and intercellular carbon dioxide concentration; (iv) calculating average value of stable carbon isotope composition of atmospheric carbon dioxide; (v) calculating stable carbon isotope fractionation value of assimilated carbon dioxide of plant leaves grown; (vi) calculating plant bicarbonate utilization share; (vii) calculating plant metabolic water utilization share according to plant bicarbonate utilization share; (viii) calculating actual water use efficiency of plant under investigation grown in environment; and (ix) calculating actual water demand of plants being inspected grown in environment.
   USE - The method is useful for calculating field habitat plant metabolic water utilization amount and actual water demand.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides scientific basis for screening of karst fitted plants and scientific support for water-saving irrigation and sustainable use of water resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Calculating field habitat plant metabolic water utilization amount and actual water demand comprises (i) selecting leaves of second unfolding leaf on branch of plant grown in environment under investigation, measuring photosynthetic parameters of morning 9:00-10:00 by portable photosynthetic apparatus in sunny weather; (ii) calculating data of net photosynthetic rate (Pn) and transpiration rate (Tr) from portable photosynthetic apparatus; (iii) calculating data of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration (Ca) and intercellular carbon dioxide concentration (Ci) from portable photosynthetic apparatus subsequently, calculating ratio (f) of intercellular carbon dioxide concentration of plant leaves and extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration; (iv) calculating average value ( delta a) of stable carbon isotope composition ( delta 13C) of atmospheric carbon dioxide in investigated environment and value of stable carbon isotope composition ( delta 13C) of rhizosphere soil bicarbonate of plant grown under environment ( delta b), selecting leaves of first unfolded leaves on other branches of plants grown in environment under investigation simultaneously, determining stable carbon isotope composition value ( delta T) of plant leaves by standard methods; (v) calculating stable carbon isotope fractionation value ( Delta a) of assimilated carbon dioxide of plant leaves grown in environment under investigation and stable carbon isotope fractionation value ( Delta b) of assimilated bicarbonate of plant leaf grown in investigated environment according to ratio of intercellular carbon dioxide concentration i of plant leaves and extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration; (vi) calculating plant bicarbonate utilization share (fb), according to value of stable carbon isotope composition of plant leaves grown in environment under investigation, average value of stable carbon isotope composition of ambient atmospheric carbon dioxide under investigation, stable carbon isotope composition of rhizosphere soil of plant grown in environment under investigation value, stable carbon isotope fractionation value of assimilated carbon dioxide in plant leaves grown in environment under investigation and stable carbon isotope fractionation value of assimilated bicarbonate of plant leaves grown in environment under investigation; (vii) calculating plant metabolic water utilization share (fw) according to plant bicarbonate utilization share; (viii) calculating actual water use efficiency (WUEa) of plant under investigation grown in environment according to plant bicarbonate utilization share grown in environment under investigation ad net photosynthetic rate and transpiration rate data; and (ix) calculating actual water demand (Q) of plants being inspected grown in environment under investigation based on plant metabolic water utilization share grown in environment under investigation and net photosynthetic rate and transpiration rate data.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), second unfolded leaf blade on branch of plant is based on principle of going from up to down, leaf that has just developed and fully expanded on branch is first fully developed leaf and second fully expanded and third fully expanded leaves. In the step (vii), plant metabolic water utilization share fw: fw is fb. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), ratio (f) of intercellular carbon dioxide concentration of plant leaves and extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration is calculated according to the formula as given in the specification. In the step (v), stable carbon isotope fractionation value Delta a assimilated carbon dioxide of plant leaves grown in environment under investigation is calculated according to the formula as given in the specification. In the step (vi), plant bicarbonate utilization fraction fb is calculated according to the formula as given in the specification. In the step (viii), actual water use efficiency (WUEa) of plant under investigation grown in environment under investigation is calculated according to the formula as given in the specification. In the step (ix), actual water demand of plants being inspected grown in environment under investigation (Q), is calculated according to the formula as given in the specification.

PT P
PN CN109569242-A; CN109569242-B
TI Enhancing the absorption of carbon dioxide by gas-liquid-liquid three-phase system comprises passing carbon dioxide-containing gas into a supergravity machine from a gas inlet, reacting with aqueous phase, organic phase and promoter.
AU SHAO L
   LIU Z
   WANG L
   WANG Y
   YE J
   FENG Q
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201933626R
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing the absorption of carbon dioxide by gas-liquid-liquid three-phase system comprises passing carbon dioxide-containing gas into a supergravity machine from a gas inlet, passing the aqueous phase and the organic phase into the supergravity machine from a liquid inlet, passing a promoter into the supergravity machine through the liquid distributor, where the aqueous phase and the organic phase flow from the inner edge of the promoter to the outer edge, and gas-liquid-liquid three-phase comes in contact in the promoter, the carbon dioxide is absorbed by the absorbent in the liquid phase, the liquid after absorbing carbon dioxide leaves the super gravity machine from the liquid outlet, and the decarburized gas leaves the super gravity machine from the gas outlet; the contact between the gas phase and the liquid phase in the supergravity machine is countercurrent contact or co-current contact.
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing the absorption of carbon dioxide by gas-liquid-liquid three-phase system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high efficiency absorption of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enhancing the absorption of carbon dioxide by gas-liquid-liquid three-phase system comprises passing carbon dioxide-containing gas into a supergravity machine from a gas inlet, passing the aqueous phase and the organic phase into the supergravity machine from a liquid inlet, passing a promoter into the supergravity machine through the liquid distributor, where the aqueous phase and the organic phase flow from the inner edge of the promoter to the outer edge, and gas-liquid-liquid three-phase comes in contact in the promoter, the carbon dioxide is absorbed by the absorbent in the liquid phase, the liquid after absorbing carbon dioxide leaves the super gravity machine from the liquid outlet, and the decarburized gas leaves the super gravity machine from the gas outlet; the contact between the gas phase and the liquid phase in the supergravity machine is countercurrent contact or co-current contact; the temperature at which the reaction is absorbed at 10-90 degrees C, the gas-liquid volume flow ratio is 1-1000:1, the speed of the supergravity rotating bed rotor is 200-3000 rotations/minute; the aqueous phase includes pure water, dipotassium carbonate solution, dipotassium carbonate/potassium bicarbonate solution, ethanolamine, diethanolamine, N-methyldiethanolamine, potassium glycinate and/or piperazine; the organic phase includes n-octanol, dimethyl carbonate, cyclohexane, n-hexane, n-heptane and/or benzene; the promoter includes sodium hypochlorite, sodium meta arsenite, ACT-1, ammonium borate, ammonium glycol and/or diethanolamine; and the emulsifier includes Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate-20), Tween 40 (RTM: Polysorbate 40), Tween-60 (RTM: Polysorbate-60), Tween-80 (RTM: Polysorbate-80), Span-20 (RTM: Sorbitan fatty acid esters), SPAN 40 (RTM: sorbitan monopalmitate), Span 60 (RTM: Sorbitan monostearate 60), Span 80 (RTM: Sorbitan oleate), nonylphenol ethoxylate, octylphenol oxyethylene vinyl ether and/or fatty acid methyl ester polyoxyethylene ether.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The supergravity rotating bed reactor is rotating packed bed, constant-rotor reactor or baffling rotating bed. The organic phase is: 1-80 wt.% n-octanol or dimethyl carbonate; 1-20 wt.% cyclohexane or n-hexane; or 0.1-10 wt.% n-heptane or benzene. The aqueous phase is 0-3 mol/l dipotassium carbonate or 0-1 mol/l dipotassium carbonate/potassium bicarbonate solution. The concentration of the promoter is 0-0.5 mol/l. The concentration of the emulsifier is 0-20 ml/l. The mixed liquid phase of the aqueous phase and the organic phase can be recycled or used in a non-circulating single use.

PN CN114856689-A
TI Fly ash, carbon dioxide mineralized sealing and            gob filling integrated method, involves determining            effective flow radius of carbon dioxide by established            carbon dioxide seepage model, and setting vertical            drilling step distance.
AU GUO W
   LIU W
   GE Z
   ZHOU L
   TANG J
   ZHANG X
   ZHOU J
   PENG J
   XIA B
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2022A48302
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting an area for                sequering carbon dioxide. A mining coverage                pressure calculation model is established in the                selected area. A key layer of a stope is                determined. A limit collapsing step distance of a                covered rock layer is calculated. A coal bed is                mining the stope. A gangue in the coal layer is                filled to a goaf produced by the mining coal bed. A                bulk fly ash is fully stirred through a stirring                device to form a bulk fly air. The bulk air is                output to the goaf to fill. An effective flow                radius of the carbon dioxide is determined by the                established carbon dioxide seepage model. A                vertical drilling step distance is reasonably set                according to the effective flow rate of the CO2.                The CO2 in a carbon dioxide storage tank is                sequentially sequentially entered into the gob                area. The gangue and the bulk air from the vertical                drilling well are sequentially extracted from the                GOF through the vertical drill well.
   USE - Fly ash, carbon dioxide mineralized sealing                and gob filling integrated method for use in                coal-fired power plant. Can also be used in land                occupation, polluted soil water resources, harmful                environment and resource waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a good environment for                stacking waste fly ash and sealing CO2. The method                can carry out secondary mineralization to the CO2                in the process of making the bulk fly ash from the                bulk flying ash to the gob, which can realize the                long-term safe sealing of the CO 2, greatly reduce                the emission of the carbon dioxide, and effectively                reduce the greenhouse effect. The CO2 sequered                region is at least satisfy the following                conditions: the storage space has large capacity,                good sealing property, stable formation, large                capacity capacity, and high sealing property.
