AB    NOVELTY - Use of carbon dioxide is claimed for preventing and reducing allergic reactions to food and medicine and for simultaneously reducing bacterial count on food and medicine.
   USE - Carbon dioxide used for preventing and reducing allergic reactions to food and medicine, and for simultaneously reducing bacterial count on food and medicine, where allergic reactions are caused by immunoglobulin E-reactive allergens which are of animal origin derived from milk protein, egg, crab, fish, meat, meat product and mite cheese, plant origin derived from beans, nuts, pome fruit, stone fruit, berry fruit, peel fruit, wild fruit, exotic fruit, grain, rice or soybean, or medicinal origin derived from medicine and/or galenic additive of medicine, and bacterial count is reduced by killing and/or stopping increasing ability of bacteria (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing an allergen and bacterial count reducing food and medicine, which involves placing gaseous, liquid and/or solid carbon dioxide in a closed container for affecting food or medicine;
   (2) a food and medicine having reduced or no allergic reactions, which has a reduced bacterial count as compared to untreated food and medicine that is produced by action of gaseous, liquid and/or solid carbon dioxide on food and medicine; and
   (3) a stackable gas-tight container for permanent storage of allergen-reduced and bacteria-reduced food and medicine under carbon dioxide, where gas-tight container is sealed by lid, and the container are standardized to distance in refrigerator, deep freezer and freezer and/or exhaust valve and inlet valve are mounted on side for carbon dioxide or laterally-mounted valve which serves both as an inlet and outlet valve for carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide is in gaseous, liquid and/or solid form. The carbon dioxide is allowed to affect food or medicine at a temperature of -80 degrees C to 50 degrees C and at a pressure of 1-400 bar for 30 seconds to 1000 hours. The food is sprayed with water before action of the carbon dioxide. The food has a sweet and/or sour taste. The stackable container is transparent, and is rinsed with carbon dioxide when lid is removed and then closed again, and container is colored differently for purpose of identification and/or are marked, where valve is recessed in one side panel and/or exhibits perforated intermediate bottom.

PT P
PN CN107991409-A; CN107991409-B
TI Simultaneous determination of sulfonamides e.g. sulfadiazine in plasma, involves adding acetonitrile to plasma, filtering and performing high-performance converging chromatography using supercritical carbon dioxide-methanol mixture.
AU ZHANG Y
   LI G
   YAN J
   LIU M
   SHEN X
   LIU J
   ZHOU H
AE CANCER HOSPITAL CHINESE ACAD MEDICINE SC (MSUM-C)
GA 2018376106
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneous determination of sulfonamides in plasma involves adding acetonitrile to plasma sample, mixing, centrifuging, filtering, and subjecting the filtrate to high-performance converging chromatography using a mixture of supercritical carbon dioxide and methanol. The gradient elution procedure is as follows: The volume fraction of supercritical carbon dioxide is reduced from 95% to 85% (in 0-5 minutes) and 85% to 80% (in 5.0-5.5 minutes), increased from 80% to 95% (in 5.5-6 minutes), and remained unchanged at 95% (in 6-7 minutes).
   USE - Simultaneous determination of sulfonamides e.g. sulfamethoxazole, sulfadimethoxine, sulfacetamide, sulfamethazine, sulfadiazine, sulfathiazole, sulfapyridine, N4-acesulfamethoxazole, N4-acesulfamethylpyrimidine, N4-acefazine, N4-acesulfathiazole, and N4-acesulfapyridine in plasma (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and enables simple and determination of sulfonamides in plasma with minimum quantitative limit of 0.5 mu l, recovery rate of 80.53-103.33%, and high accuracy in short period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Simultaneous determination of sulfonamides in plasma involves adding acetonitrile to plasma sample, mixing, centrifuging, filtering with 0.22 mu m-membrane, and subjecting the filtrate to high-performance converging chromatography with Acquity UPC2 (RTM: ethylene-bridged hybrid column) at 35-45 degrees C and pressure of 1800-2700 psi using a mixture of supercritical carbon dioxide and methanol. The column flow rate is 1.55-1.65 mL/minute. The gradient elution procedure is as follows: The volume fraction of supercritical carbon dioxide is reduced from 95% to 85% (in 0-5 minutes), the volume fraction of supercritical carbon dioxide is reduced from 85% to 80% (in 5.0-5.5 minutes), the volume fraction of supercritical carbon dioxide increased from 80% to 95% (in 5.5-6 minutes), and the volume fraction of supercritical carbon dioxide remained unchanged at 95% (in 6-7 minutes).

PN CN114965079-A
TI Method for measuring fracture parameter of I-type            coal rock mass under action of ultra-critical carbon            dioxide, involves measuring opening displacement and            opening displacement change of preset crack tip of test            piece by crack displacement monitoring device during            testing process.

   MA C
   LIU Z
   REN Y
   YANG L
   YANG J
AE UNIV XIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYXS-C)
GA 2022B51849
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pre-setting a crack from a                bottom unit of a coal rock sample. A three-shaft                pressure kettle is placed on an external loading                platform. A test piece is placed on two support                columns. An axis of the test piece, a loading                middle line of an axial loading device and an axis                of a locating base are located on a same vertical                plane. Carbon dioxide fluid is injected into a                cavity of the three-shaft pressure kettle through                an axial flow pump according to preset pressure.                Three-point bending test process is performed.                Opening displacement and opening displacement                change of a preset crack tip of the test piece is                measured by a crack displacement monitoring device                during testing process.
   USE - Method for measuring fracture parameter of                I-type coal rock mass under action of                ultra-critical carbon dioxide for use in technical                field of carbon dioxide geological sealing and                non-conventional natural gas high-efficient mining                construction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables controlling the pressure                and temperature of the carbon dioxide fluid, so as                to simulate the influence of supercritical carbon                dioxide on the coal rock body I-type fracture                action under the high ground stress environment,                thus realizing three-point bending loading test of                the test piece according to the different                temperature, the pressure super critical carbon                dioxide and the fracture and characteristics. The                method enables accurately measuring preset crack                tip opening degree of the test piece of the coal                rock sample in an efficient manner.

PN KR2300742-B1
TI System for separating and reducing harmful substances for removing particulate matter and carbon dioxide from exhaust gas of plant, has compressor for compressing exhaust gas to high pressure, where vortex tube separates high-temperature gas.
AU YEON I
   YU S S
AE UNIV IND & ACADEMIC COOP IN CHUNGNAM NAT (UCHU-C)
GA 2021A43751
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor (120) configured for compressing an exhaust gas to high pressure. A vortex tube (200) is configured for separating high-temperature gas and low temperature gas from the high-pressure exhaust gas. A chamber (300) is connected to the vortex tube to separate particulate matter and liquefied carbon dioxide from the introduced gas. The compressor compresses the high temperature gas separated from the vortex pipe at high pressure. A sub-compressor compresses a gas exhausted from the chamber. A filter is arranged on a discharge pipe to filter particulate matter from liquefied carbon dioxide. The mixed gaseous particulate matter is separated and the mixed gas is cooled to discharge liquefied carbon dioxide.
   USE - System for separating and reducing harmful substances for removing particulate matter and carbon dioxide from exhaust gas of plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces a large amount of greenhouse gases in a short time as the low-temperature gas separated from the vortex tube is put into the chamber, and separates the liquefied carbon dioxide from the chamber. The first filter and the second filter are installed in the discharge pipe and the drain pipe, so that efficiency is higher as the particulate matter, liquefaction carbon dioxide, and the gas with reduced harmful substances are separated. The system can divide and mix reduction of hazardous substances into multiple processes through multiple vortex tubes, which can lead to the advantage of increasing the efficiency while reducing the size of the facility.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the system for separating and reducing harmful substances for removing particulate matter and carbon dioxide from exhaust gas of plant. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN113101789-A; CN113101789-B
TI Method for sealing carbon dioxide by open pit mine pit, involves paving anti-seepage film at bottom of open pit mine pit, constructing clay layer on anti-seepage film, and injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into mixed material layer.
AU DI S
   ZHOU Y
   WU X
   CHEN M
   HOU Z
   HUANG Y
AE LUOJIA HUANXUN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (LUOJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021837790
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves paving (S101) an anti-seepage film at a bottom portion of an open pit mine pit. A clay layer is constructed (S102) on the anti-seepage film. A guide groove is formed around the clay layer. A fluid collecting tank is constructed at a bottom portion of the open pit mine. The fluid collecting tank is connected with a water pump through a pipeline. A side slope anti-seepage film is paved on a side slope of the open pit mine. The supercritical carbon dioxide is injected (S106) into the mixed material layer of the previous layer by an injection tube, and the injection tube is lifted away from the filling hole of the previous layer, so that the filling hole of the previous layer is one-way closed by the one-way valve according to sealing requirement.
   USE - Method for sealing carbon dioxide by open pit mine pit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a synergistic treatment of multiple industrial solid waste, realizes the waste reduction, resource utilization while greatly improving the utilization value of the mine pit, and realizing a solid carbon engineering technology in order to realize the sealing of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an open pit mining method; and
   (2) a structure for sealing carbon dioxide by using open pit mine pit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method of sealing carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step of paving anti-seepage film (S101)
   Step of constructing clay layer (S102)
   Step of crushing solid waste (S103)
   Step of forming slope structure (S104)
   Step of injecting supercritical carbon dioxide (S106)

TI Carbon dioxide resource treatment system, includes methane synthesis device, hydrogen inlet, reaction product outlet, methane separation equipment, thermal cracking device, hydrogen outlet, and solid carbon outlet.
AU MAO Z
AE WEICHAI POWER CO LTD (WEIC-C)
   MAO Z (MAOZ-Individual)
GA 2021811772
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide resource treatment system includes methane synthesis device (3) which is provided hydrogen and carbon dioxide (CO2) as reaction raw materials to catalyze production of methane, comprises CO2 inlet, hydrogen inlet, reaction product outlet, and catalyst is filled inside; methane separation equipment (4) provided to separate reaction products delivered from methane synthesis device to obtain methane, water and unreacted raw materials respectively, and provided with feed inlet, methane outlet, water outlet and unreacted raw material outlet; feed inlet of methane separation equipment is connected to reaction product outlet of methane synthesis device; thermal cracking device (5) is provided for catalytically cracking methane to produce hydrogen and solid carbon materials, provided with feed inlet, hydrogen outlet and solid carbon outlet, and inside is filled with catalyst. The feed port of thermal cracking device is connected to methane outlet of methane separation device.
   USE - Carbon dioxide resource treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is converted CO2 into solid carbon materials, realizes resource utilization of CO2, and fundamentally reduces carbon dioxide content in atmospheric environment, and has both good environmental and economic benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide resource treatment method, which involves:
   (A) heating hydrogen, and CO2 in presence of catalyst to react to obtain mixture containing methane;
   (B) separating components of methane-containing mixture to obtain methane, water, and unreacted raw materials respectively; and
   (C) providing catalytically cracking of methane to obtain hydrogen, and solid carbon materials.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of carbon dioxide resource treatment system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Carbon dioxide purification device (1)
   Water electrolysis hydrogen production device (2)
   Methane synthesis device (3)
   Methane separation equipment (4)
   Thermal cracking device (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide resource treatment method involves obtaining hydrogen by hydrogen production by water electrolysis, providing electric energy for hydrogen production by water electrolysis by renewable energy power generation; obtaining CO2 by purifying and removing impurities from CO2 raw material gas; returning unreacted raw material to first step to continue reaction; separating obtained water to produce hydrogen by water electrolysis; returning all or portion of obtained hydrogen to first step as reaction raw material.

PN US10909547-B1
TI Eliminating and disposing plastic waste comprises            combusting mixed content solid waste, recording and            controlling rate of plastic waste contained in mixed            content solid waste, separating carbon dioxide gas and            sequestering.
AU GUYER E C
AE YANKEE SCI INC (YANK-Non-standard)
GA 202116427U
AB    NOVELTY - Eliminating and disposing plastic waste                comprises combusting and sequestering to minimize a                length of conduit carrying the carbon dioxide to                produce combustion flue gas that contains carbon                dioxide; recording and controlling a rate of                plastic waste contained in the parts. Mixed content                solid waste that is provided to the combustion                process; separating carbon dioxide gas contained in                the combustion flue gas from other gaseous                constituents of the combustion flue gas at a rate,                or in an amount, approximately equal to, or                proportional to, an amount of carbon dioxide gas                produced by the combustion of plastic material                contained in the mixed plastic waste; and                sequestering the separated carbon dioxide in                geologic formations, so as to provide for                elimination and disposal of plastic waste contained                in the mixed content solid waste, in one                consolidated process and free of the need for                separation of the plastic material from other                non-plastic solid waste.
   USE - The method is useful for eliminating and                disposing plastic waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is performed at a facility that                captures carbon dioxide gas from the combustion                flue gas at a rate or in an amount that is less                than, or greater than, an amount produced by the                combustion of the plastic material; provides a                versatile, efficient and robust process for                permanent elimination of undesirable plastic waste                that can optimize power generation and also                minimize excess carbon dioxide as appropriate to                the circumstances; and affords both users and the                facility operator a number of fee-generating and/or                income sources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows block diagram showing a                system and method for plastic waste                disposal.
   System arrangement (100)
   Collection unit (101)
   Flue gas cleanup unit (104)
   Conventional exhaust stack arrangement                (106)
   Flue gases (123)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of combusting                  includes inputting air that is enriched in oxygen                  up to 100%. The carbon dioxide produced by the                  combusting of the plastic material is removed                  from the combustion flue gas in a process unit by                  contact with a solid or liquid medium that                  absorbs or adsorbs the carbon dioxide, with the                  absorbed or adsorbed carbon dioxide subsequently                  is separate from the recyclable medium in a                  desorption unit to produce a stream of carbon                  dioxide gas of high purity.

PN CN111677486-A
TI Method for performing tight oil carbon dioxide            huff and puff simulation, involves determining            covariance matrix of production dynamic parameter            simulation value, and adjusting initial inversion            parameter eigenvalue to obtain new eigenvalue.
AU XUE L
   ZHANG D
   LIU Y
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 202093622Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S101) reservoir                geological parameters and initial inversion                parameter characteristic values of tight oil                reservoirs. A gridded embedded component discrete                fracture prior model is established (S102) based on                the reservoir geological parameters. A numerical                simulation of the carbon dioxide huff and puff                production history data is performed (S103) on the                embedded component discrete fracture prior model to                obtain the production dynamic parameter simulation                value. A covariance matrix of the production                dynamic parameter simulation value is determined                (S104). An initial inversion parameter eigenvalue                is adjusted (S105) according to the Kalman                iterative gain to obtain a new inversion parameter                eigenvalue. A currently obtained inversion                parameter characteristic value is used (S106) as                the reservoir characteristic parameter fitting                result of the tight oil reservoir, when the                numerical simulation satisfies the iteration                termination condition.
   USE - Method for performing tight oil carbon dioxide                huff and puff simulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the precision and                efficiency of the dense oil carbon dioxide                throughput simulation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a device for performing tight oil carbon                dioxide huff and puff simulation; and
   (2) a computer storage medium storing program                for performing tight oil carbon dioxide huff and                puff simulation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for performing tight oil carbon dioxide                huff and puff simulation. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Step for obtaining reservoir geological                parameter (S101)
   Step for establishing gridded embedded                component discrete fracture prior model                (S102)
   Step for performing numerical simulation of                carbon dioxide huff and puff production history                data (S103)
   Step for determining covariance matrix of                production dynamic parameter simulation value                (S104)
   Step for adjusting initial inversion                parameter eigenvalue (S105)
   Step for using currently obtained inversion                parameter characteristic value as reservoir                characteristic parameter fitting result                (S106)

PT P
PN CN111495384-A
TI Carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation catalyst is composed of metal oxide carrier, active metal nickel and metal promoter.
AU XIAO R
   YANG F
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2020773126
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation catalyst is composed of a metal oxide carrier, an active metal nickel and a metal promoter. The metal oxide carrier is 75-85 wt.%. The active metal nickel is 10-20 wt.%. The metal auxiliary agent is 2-5 wt.%.
   USE - Carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation catalyst has large specific surface area, stable structure and low price as the carrier of the carbon dioxide hydromethanation catalyst, has low temperature activity, good methane selectivity, good heat resistance and long service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for following:
   (1) a method for preparing a carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation catalyst, which involves weighing gama-alumina according to the above weight percentage, placing it in a muffle furnace, and calcining at 700 degrees C for 8 hours, calculating the weights of nickel nitrate hexahydrate, manganese nitrate and cerium nitrate hexahydrate in the form of nitrates according to the above weight, weighing the corresponding weights of nitrates and dissolving them in deionized water, and magnetically stirring for 1 hour at room temperature to obtain a mixed nitrate solution, immersing gama-alumina in the mixed nitrate solution under stirring conditions, where immersion temperature is 50-70 degrees C, and the immersion time is 12 hours, drying gama-alumina and immersing in the mixed nitrate solution at a temperature of 100-120 degrees C for 12 hours and putting gama-alumina into a muffle furnace for calcination, heating up to 550 degrees C at a heating rate of 1 degrees C/min, and keeping it for 3 hours to prepare the carbon dioxide hydromethanation catalyst; and
   (2) a method for using carbon dioxide hydromethanation catalyst, which involves using conditions, such as reaction temperature is 200-400 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 1-10 atmospheres, and the space velocity of raw material gas is 2000-12000ml per gram per hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal additive is a mixture of manganese and cerium. Preferred Conditions: The metal oxide support is spherical gama-alumina, with a specific surface area of 98 m2/g, an average pore diameter of 62 nm, and a particle size of 100 mu m.

TI Device for passive collection of atmospheric            carbon dioxide, has tiles that are sufficiently            enclosed inside release chamber that sorbent            regeneration system can operate on tiles to release            captured carbon dioxide from sorbent material.
AU LACKNER K
   KEDIA S
   CHOODAMANI V
   PAGE R
   PATCH R
AE ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   ARIZONA (UYAZ-C)
   UNIV ARIZONA STATE (UYAS-C)
GA 2020376467
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a release chamber (104)                that comprises an opening and a sorbent                regeneration system. A capture structure (106) is                coupled to the release chamber and comprises a                collapsible support and multiple tiles (108)                coupled to and spaced along the collapsible                support, each tile comprises a sorbent material. A                lid (114) covers the opening of the release chamber                when the capture structure is in the release                configuration. A product outlet is in fluid                communication with the inside of the release                chamber and configured to receive a product stream                of enriched gas. The release configuration                comprises the collapsible support of the capture                structure is collapsed. The tiles are sufficiently                enclosed inside the release chamber that the                sorbent regeneration system can operate on the                tiles to release captured carbon dioxide from the                sorbent material and form an enriched gas within                the release chamber.
   USE - Device for passive collection of atmospheric                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide (C0 2) losses can be                minimized during evacuation or other preparation                steps, because the evacuation or these other steps                occur before heat and moisture. The use of a water                saturated sweep gas such as steam (which at low                partial pressure is referred to as water vapor)                will keep moisture-swing sorbents from releasing                water, which can increase the overall efficiency of                the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for passive collection of                atmospheric carbon dioxide; and
   (2) a system for passive collection of                atmospheric carbon dioxide.

   Release chamber (104)
   Capture structure (106)
   Tiles (108)

PN CN110057431-A; CN110057431-B
TI Overturning platform mixed gas filling method, involves connecting nitrogen pipeline with nitrogen bottle, closing cylinder valve assembly when bottle required weight is reached such that exhaust valve is opened to empty gas in pipelines.
AU ZHAO S
   MENG L
   LI W
   YANG H
   FENG B
AE LIAONING METAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 201966909K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves opening knob of an overturning platform of a fixing cylinder. A carbon dioxide pipeline is connected with a carbon dioxide bottle. Weight of a bottle valve assembly is recorded using an electronic scale. A charging pipeline is connected with a charging valve. The charging valve is opened to open a valve of the carbon dioxide bottle. A cylinder valve assembly is placed on the electronic scale. A connecting valve is slowly opened when carbon dioxide gas is passed into the bottle valve assembly. The connection valve and the bottle valve are closed when preset quality is filled. A carbon dioxide exhaust valve of a working platform is opened to exhaust. A nitrogen pipeline is connected with a nitrogen bottle. The cylinder valve assembly is closed when required weight of a gas bottle is reached such that the exhaust valve is opened for emptying gas in the pipelines.
   USE - Overturning platform mixed gas filling method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adopting a transducer to control turning of carbon dioxide gas to save time and labor intensity, integrating two input ports and an output interface in a system filling device for filling nitrogen gas in the carbon dioxide bottle connected to an input pipe filling device to avoid repeated detaching of input pipes on a panel control valve to realize mixing and filling of carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas and avoids need of closing a valve of the cylinder valve assembly and improving gas bottle charging accuracy using a weighting mode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of an overturning platform mixed gas filling device. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PT P
PN EP3398970-A1; EP3398970-B1
TI Synthesizing cellulose carbonate, comprises mixing solution of dimethyl sulfoxide and cellulose with organic base in presence of carbon dioxide to form cellulose solution, contacting cellulose solution with organochloride and adding alcohol.
AU LUO H
   LEE A M X
   HUANG S
AE AGENCY SCI TECHNOLOGY & RES (SGST-C)
GA 201888230Q
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing cellulose carbonate, comprises: mixing a solution comprising dimethyl sulfoxide and cellulose with an organic base in the presence of carbon dioxide to form a cellulose solution comprising cellulose carbonate anions, where the organic base is substituted amine containing compounds (I); contacting the cellulose solution with at least one organochloride in the presence of carbon dioxide to form the cellulose carbonate; and adding an alcohol or water to precipitate the cellulose carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing cellulose carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is beneficial to the environment as it utilizes carbon dioxide as a feed stock, which is an abundant greenhouse gas, and converting the greenhouse gas carbon dioxide into cellulose carbonate; serves as an economic route for synthesizing cellulose carbonates as it utilizes common and inexpensive feed stocks including carbon doxide and organochlorides, instead of expensive and toxic materials including chloroformates; is versatile in that it is not limited to a specific type of organochlorides but various types of organochlorides can be utilized for synthesizing cellulose carbonates; and allows for synthesis of cellulose carbonate using mild reaction conditions i.e. room temperatures of 20-40 degrees C and 1 atm carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing cellulose carbonate, comprises: mixing a solution comprising dimethyl sulfoxide and cellulose with an organic base in the presence of carbon dioxide to form a cellulose solution comprising cellulose carbonate anions, where the organic base is substituted amine containing compounds of formula (I); contacting the cellulose solution with at least one organochloride in the presence of carbon dioxide to form the cellulose carbonate; and adding an alcohol or water to precipitate the cellulose carbonate.
   R1-R3 = 1-20C alkyl; and
   R4, R5 = H or 1-20C alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide in the mixing step and the contacting step has a pressure of at least 1 atm, preferably 1-20 atm. The water comprises deionized water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of mixing is: carried out at a temperature of 20-40 degrees C; and carried out based on a molar ratio of organic base to cellulose-hydroxy of 0.5-3. The step of contacting is carried out at a temperature of 20-40 degrees C for 1-72 hours. Preferred Components: The organic base comprises 2-tert-butyl-1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine, 1,2,3-triisopropylguanidine, 2-butyl-1,3-diisopropylguanidine. The protonated form of the organic base comprises (2-tert-butyl-1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine-hydrogen)+. The organochloride includes benzyl chloride, 4-(trifluoromethyl) benzyl chloride, 4-methylbenzyl chloride, 4-(tert-butyl)benzyl chloride, 4-methoxybenzyl chloride, 3,4-dichlorobenzyl chloride, 2,4-dichlorobenzyl chloride, N-(4-(chloromethyl)phenyl)-4-(trifluoromethyl)benzamide, N-(4-(chloromethyl)phenyl)benzamide, 4-chloro-N-(4-(chloromethyl)phenyl)benzamide, or ethyl chloroacetate. The alcohol comprises methanol or ethanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The cellulose comprises microcrystalline cellulose, amorphous cellulose or cotton. The cellulose solution further comprises cellulose-oxide anions and/or a protonated form of the organic base. The cellulose carbonate comprises substituted hydrocarbon containing repeating units of formula (II).R = e.g. benzyl, 4-(trifluoromethyl)benzyl, 4-methylbenzyl, 4-methoxybenzyl and 3,4-dichlorobenzyl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).)

PT P
PN CN107941838-A; CN107941838-B
TI Method for quantitatively evaluating influence of            asphaltene precipitation on distribution of pore throat            during carbon dioxide flooding, involves selecting            artificially cemented sandstone core followed by            injecting carbon dioxide into core.
AU ZHAO J
   AN X
   FU M
   BAO H
AE UNIV XIAN SHIYOU (UXSY-C)
GA 201832629P
AB    NOVELTY - An influence of asphaltene precipitation                quantitative evaluation method involves selecting                artificially cemented sandstone cores, preparing                simulated aqueous solution containing manganese                ions and preparing simulated crude oil. The cores                are saturated with manganese ions followed by                measuring porosity, calculating pore volume,                utilizing simulated crude oil to displace the core,                measuring NMR spectrum (T2), injecting carbon                dioxide into core for flooding experiments and                evaluating influence of asphaltene precipitation on                distribution of pore throat during carbon dioxide                flooding.
   USE - Method for quantitatively evaluating influence                of asphaltene precipitation on distribution of pore                throat during carbon dioxide flooding.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quantitatively evaluating                influence of asphaltene precipitation on                distribution of pore throat during carbon dioxide                flooding with high accuracy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An influence of asphaltene precipitation                quantitative evaluation method involves selecting                artificially cemented sandstone cores, according to                actual study of reservoir permeability, preparing                simulated aqueous solution containing manganese                ions, preparing simulated crude oil using aviation                paraffin and degassed crude oil and making it                viscous at reservoir temperature consistent with                viscosity of crude oil in formation. The cores are                saturated with manganese ions to simulate formation                water followed by measuring porosity, calculating                pore volume, utilizing simulated crude oil to                displace the core, until the core exports 100% oil,                measuring NMR spectrum (T2), injecting carbon                dioxide into core for flooding experiments, until                the core exports 100% water and measuring NMR                spectrum (T2). The core is placed in centrifugal                device for centrifugal degreasing followed by                measuring NMR spectrum (T2). The measured NMR                spectrum is converted into pore throat radial                distribution curve followed by quantitatively                evaluating influence of asphaltene precipitation on                distribution of pore throat during carbon dioxide                flooding.

AB    NOVELTY - Pyridinium-modified UiO-66 type compound,                comprises linear dicarboxylic acid ligands                containing pyridinium substituents and metal oxygen                clusters zirconium 1,4-dicarboxybenzene.
   USE - The pyridinium-modified UiO-66 type compound                is useful in catalyzing the reaction of carbon                dioxide and epoxide to synthesize cyclic carbonate                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound has high stability, high specific                surface area and significant porous                characteristics, can effectively catalyze carbon                dioxide and epoxide to synthesize cyclic carbonate                under no cocatalyst, no solvent and mild                conditions, and can be recycled and is easy to                separate and purify.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing pyridinium-modified UiO-66 type compound,                comprising (i) placing methyl isonicotinate,                acetone, sodium metal and anhydrous ether in a                three-necked flask, stirring at room temperature                for 3 hours under nitrogen protection, and                filtering to obtain solid                1-(pyridin-4-yl)-1,3-butanedione sodium salt,                washing and drying, where the dosage ratio of the                methyl isonicotinate, acetone, sodium metal and                anhydrous ether is 0.1 mol:0.1 mol:0.1 mol:50 ml;                (b) placing 1-(pyridin-4-yl)-1,3-butanedione sodium                salt, carbon disulfide, anhydrous potassium                carbonate and N,N-dimethylformamide in a                three-necked flask and stirring at room temperature                for 1 hour, adding ethyl bromoacetate and reacting                at room temperature for 24 hours, heating to                80&#8451; (d) placing                3-methyl-4-(pyridin-4-yl)-[2,3-b]thiophene                dicarboxylic acid, benzoic acid, zirconium                chloride, and N,N-dimethylformamide in the reaction                kettle, heating and reacting at 120&#8451; for                36 hours, filtering, soaking the obtained                precipitate in dichloromethane and activating by                heating to obtain a solid i.e. Pyridyl-MOF-1, where                the dosage ratio of                3-methyl-4-(pyridin-4-yl)-[2,3-b]thiophene                dicarboxylic acid, benzoic acid, zirconium chloride                and N,N-dimethylformamide is: 0.3 mmol: 9 mmol: 0.3                mmol: 10 ml; and (e) placing pyridyl-MOF-1 in                iodomethane solvent and heating for 24 hours,                filtering, placing the obtained solid in potassium                bromide aqueous solution and soaking for 24 hours,                and separating.

PN IN202011027265-A
TI Producing porous particles-fibers carbon composite            material useful for supercapacitor electrode material            for high voltage supercapacitor comprises taking            activated carbon particles, and performing activationin            tubular furnace.
AU KARTHIK M
   RAVULA V
   RAO T N
AE INT ADVANCED RES CENT POWDER METALLURGY (ITAD-Non-standard)
GA 202220512B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing porous particles-fibers carbon                composite material by single step carbon dioxide                activation comprises (a) taking activated carbon                particles having surface area of 600-1300 m2/g, and                carbonized carbon fibers in the ratio of                90:10-10:90 and mixing thoroughly, (b) subjecting                the resultant mixture to single step activation                process in tubular furnace by heating at a                temperature of 700-1100 degrees C in inert                atmosphere of nitrogen or argon, passing carbon                dioxide gas at flow rate of 25 ml/minute to 25                liters/minute for 1-24 hours, (c) cooling down the                tubular furnace to room temperature under inert                atmosphere of nitrogen/argon and (d) collecting the                resultant porous particles-fibers carbon composite                material called as activated composite material                (ACM).
   USE - The porous particles-fibers carbon composite                material is useful for as supercapacitor electrode                materialfor high voltage supercapacitor for energy                storage applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous particles-fibers carbon composite                material: has a mixture of micropores, mesopores                and macropores combination which helps to enhance                the electrolyte diffusion and electrolyte ion                transport inside the supercapacitor electrodes and                electrochemical double-layer capacitors, when used                as electrode material, has high surfacearea of                1300-2200 m2/g with average pore volume of 0.6-1.8                cm3/g and pore size of 0.6-100 nm; and exhibits                excelled electrochemical performance, high specific                capacitance and long-term durability for electrical                energy storageapplications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) porous particles-fibers carbon composite                material prepared by single step carbon dioxide                activation process, where majority of the                micropores formed in the carbon particles are in                the range of 0.8-2 nm and at the same time, the                carbon fibers are having the pore size in the wide                range of 0.6-10 nm and 10-100 nm; and
   (2) producing supercapacitor (EDLCs)                electrodes for electrical energy storage                applications comprising (a) incorporating porous                particles-fibers carbon composite material in the                mixture of conductive carbon and binder materials,                preferably polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) or                polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), (b) addition of                N-methyl-2-pyrolidone (NMP) in the carbon mixture                for getting the uniform slurry in the case of PVDF                binder, addition of PTFE (60 wt.% suspension in                water) in the carbon mixture in the case of PTFE                binder so that the composition of the final mixture                will have ACM and conductive carbon: PVDF/PTFE is                in the range of ratio 70-90:5-20:5-20 wt.%,                preferably 90:10:10 wt.%, (c) coating the slurry on                the surface of carbon coated aluminum foil by                Doctor Blade or continuous coating technique in the                case of PVDF binder and the preparationof long                strip self-standing electrodes in the case of PTFE                binder and (d) drying the resultant coated aluminum                foil at 100-150 degrees C for 10-24 hours,                preferably 120 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain                supercapacitor (EDLCs) electrodes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The surface area of the                  activated carbon materials is 1100 m2/g, the                  temperature of heating of the activated carbon                  particles and carbonized carbon fibers mixture in                  step (b) is 900 degrees C and flow rate of carbon                  dioxide gas is1 liter/min at 900 degrees C for 7                  hours. The evolution of carbon monoxide gas is                  monitored carefully by using carbon monoxide                  sensor and simultaneous burnt off or gainfully                  utilized as input material for by-product for                  industrial applications.The addition of the                  carbon fibers can act as an active material as                  well as interconnected filler material which                  enhance the electrical conductivity and ionic                  mobility of the electrolytes. During carbon                  dioxide activation, the majority of the                  micropores is 0.8-2 nm are formed in the carbon                  particles as confirmed by BET analysis (2D-NLDFT                  method), and at the same time the pore size in                  the wide range of 0.6-10 nm are formed in the                  carbon fiber as confirmed by BET analysis                  (2D-NLDFT method), and the pore size in the wide                  range of 10-100 nm was clearly observed from SEM                  analysis.After carbon dioxide activation, the                  micropores are created both in the carbon                  particles as well as carbon fibers and provide                  very good pathway not only for the electrolyte                  diffusion and also electrolyte ion transfers when                  used as electrode material in the supercapacitor                  or electrochemical double layer capacitors                  (EDLCs). Preferred Components: The                  carbonizedcarbon fibers is of industrial waste                  cotton fibers origin and activated carbon                  materials is of coconut shell origin. The ratio                  ofcarbon particle to fiber in the composite                  material is 90:10, the specific surface area and                  micropores volume of immaterial is 1560 m2/g and                  .2 cm3/g. The porousparticles-fibers carbon                  composite material also called as activated                  composite material (ACM) which is mixed with                  conductive carbon alongwith PVDF/PTFE as binder                  material, so as to form the electrodematerial for                  high voltage supercapacitor for energy storage                  applications.The mixture for electrode material                  contains porous particles-fibers carbon                  compositematerial (activated composite material                  (ACM)), conductive carbon, polyvinylidene                  fluoride (PVDF)/polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE)                  inthe ratio of 70-90:5-20:5-10 wt.%. The                  resultantmixture of activated composite material                  (ACM), and conductivecarbon along with PVDF/PTFE                  is further added with N-methyl-2-pyrolidone (NMP)                  to obtain the uniform slurry which is coated on                  thesurface of the carbon coated aluminum foil by                  Doctor Blade or othercoating technique followed                  by drying at a temperature in the range of100-150                  degrees C for 10-20hours, preferably at 120                  degrees C for 12 hours to form anelectrode of the                  supercapacitor. The mixture for electrode                  material contains activated composite material                  (ACM), conductive carbon, PVDF/PTFE in the ratio                  of 80:10:10 (wt.%).
   EXAMPLE - High surface area (1100 m2/g) carbon particles                and carbon fibers were taken in the mass ratios of                90:10-10:90 and mixed thoroughly in mechanical                mixture. 350 g carbon particles and 150 g carbon                fibers were taken in the mass ratio of 70:30. Then                above composition of materials was subjected                tophysical activation in tubular furnace by using                carbon dioxide gas as activation agentat 800- 900                degrees C in an inert atmosphere for 1 hour. Then                the final activatedporous particles-fibers carbon                composite was collected.

PN CN113289645-A; CN113289645-B
TI Preparing one-dimensional self-assembled composite photocatalyst used in photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction, involves dissolving zinc selenide nanosheets in a water-organic amine binary mixed solvent and adding cadmium source.
AU DAI K
   LI X
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV HUAIBEI NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021A0543B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing one-dimensional self-assembled ZnxCd1-xSe/Cu2O/Cu composite photocatalyst involves fully dissolving zinc (Zn) source and sodium selenite (Na2SeO3) in the water-organic amine-hydrazine hydrate ternary mixed solvent and stirring uniformly. The obtained mixture is transferred to the oven and heated up to 145-155 degrees C. The reaction system is cooled down after the reaction. The white precipitate is obtained after centrifugation, then washed and dried through a freeze dryer to obtain zinc selenide nanosheets. The obtained zinc selenide nanosheets are dissolved in a water-organic amine binary mixed solvent and stirred uniformly. A certain molar ratio of cadmium (Cd) source is added to the mixed solution, stirred for 1-3 hours, then transferred to an oven, and heated up to 145-155 degrees C for 6-10 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing one-dimensional self-assembled ZnxCd1-xSe/Cu2O-Cu composite photocatalyst used in the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to operate, cost effective and reaction conditions are mild. The obtained composite photocatalyst has good dispersibility, good stability and high performance of photocatalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing one-dimensional self-assembled ZnxCd1-xSe/Cu2O/Cu composite photocatalyst involves fully dissolving zinc (Zn) source and sodium selenite (Na2SeO3) in the water-organic amine-hydrazine hydrate ternary mixed solvent and stirring uniformly. The obtained mixture is transferred to the oven and heated up to 145-155 degrees C. The reaction system is cooled down after the reaction. The white precipitate is obtained after centrifugation, then washed and dried through a freeze dryer to obtain zinc selenide nanosheets. The obtained zinc selenide nanosheets are dissolved in a water-organic amine binary mixed solvent and stirred uniformly. A certain molar ratio of cadmium (Cd) source is added to the mixed solution, stirred for 1-3 hours, then transferred to an oven, and heated up to 145-155 degrees C for 6-10 hours. The reaction system is cooled down to room temperature after the reaction to obtain a dark red precipitate by centrifugation which is washed and dried through a freeze dryer to obtain nanostructures of selenium-cadmium-zinc. Cuprous oxide (Cu2O)/coppper (Cu) is dispersed in water ultrasonically followed by the addition of selenide-cadmium-zinc nanostructure and stirred after ultrasonication. The precipitate obtained is washed and dried in an oven to obtain one-dimensional self-assembled ZnxCd1-xSe/Cu2O/Cu composite photocatalyst. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a one-dimensional self-assembled ZnxCd1-xSe/Cu2O/Cu composite photocatalyst; and
   (2) an application method for one-dimensional self-assembled ZnxCd1-xSe/Cu2O/Cu composite photocatalyst, which involves pouring one-dimensional self-assembled ZnxCd1-xSe/Cu2O/Cu composite photocatalyst into the reactor, adding secondary water, subjecting to ultrasonic treatment, then drying the catalyst, placing the catalyst in a reactor that releases carbon dioxide, and irradiating the reactor with a xenon lamp with a filter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for preparing Cu2O/Cu involves dissolving anhydrous copper sulfate and polyethylene glycol in the secondary water, stirring vigorously, mixing uniformly, and adding ascorbic acid and sodium hydroxide. Sodium borohydride is dissolved in secondary water, and slowly added dropwise to the above solution. The solution is then stirred and centrifuged to obtain the dark green precipitate which is washed and dried through a freeze dryer to obtained Cu2O/Cu. Preferred Components: The zinc source is zinc acetate or zinc chloride. The cadmium source is cadmium acetate, cadmium chloride or cadmium nitrate. The organic amines are selected from diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine and tetraethylenepentamine, preferably diethylenetriamine. Preferred Conditions: The value of x in ZnxCd1-xSe/Cu2O/Cu is 0-1. The mass ratio of the selenium cadmium zinc nano structure and Cu2O/Cu is 10:1-4, more preferably 10:1.5-2.5. The reaction time of heat preservation reaction is 10-14 hours, preferably 12 hours. The water-organic amine-hydrazine hydrate ternary mixed solvent contains water, organic amine and hydrazine hydrate in volume ratio of 16:14:5. The reaction time of the heat preservation reaction is 6-10 hours, preferably, 8 hours. The volume ratio of water and organic amine in the binary mixed solvent of water and organic amine is 14:6. The molar ratio of zinc selenide to cadmium source is 1:0.3-0.7.

PN CN113244349-A
TI Compound essential oil useful in compound essential oil nanocapsules for preparing compound essential oil nanocapsule hydrogel, comprises combination of amomum tsaoko essential oil, basil essential oil and Cymbidium essential oil.
AU GONG Y
   SUN R
   WANG H
   ZHANG M
   TIAN Y
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 2021968937
AB    NOVELTY - Compound essential oil comprises combination of amomum tsaoko essential oil, basil essential oil and Cymbidium essential oil in a mass ratio of (1-4):(3-6):(2-5), where the amomum tsaoko essential oil is prepared by crushing amomum tsaoko by plant crusher, placing crushed product in distillation bottle of Clevenger volatile oil measuring device, adding distilled water for water vapor distillation, and removing moisture, the basil essential oil is prepared by crushing basil leaves, adding distilled water for soaking, setting microwave power, performing microwave radiation, and collecting distilled essential oil, and the Cymbidium essential oil is prepared by extracting Cymbidium petals, drying, crushing, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, extracting obtained extract with diethyl ether, and drying, and uniformly mixing amomum tsao-ko essential oil, basil essential oil and Cymbidium essential oil.
   USE - Compound essential oil useful in compound essential oil nanocapsules for preparing compound essential oil nanocapsule hydrogel used for healing mild to moderate wound injuries (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound essential oil adopts natural plants, is economical, easy to obtain and prepared by simple process, enriches effective active ingredients, and plays long-term and stable effect without toxic and side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Compound essential oil comprises combination of amomum tsaoko essential oil, basil essential oil and Cymbidium essential oil in a mass ratio of (1-4):(3-6):(2-5), where the amomum tsaoko essential oil is prepared by crushing amomum tsaoko by plant crusher, placing crushed product in distillation bottle of Clevenger volatile oil measuring device, adding 7 times distilled water for water vapor distillation, collecting the distillate after condensing for 6 hours, collecting the distillate after condensation, after separating upper layer of essential oil, analyzing water by leaving still overnight at-10 degrees C, and removing moisture to obtain amomum tsaoko essential oil, the basil essential oil is prepared by crushing basil leaves, adding 20 times distilled water for soaking for 3 hours, setting microwave power to 560 W, performing microwave radiation for 50 minutes, connecting volatile oil separator on microwave extraction device, and collecting distilled essential oil to obtain basil essential oil, and the Cymbidium essential oil is prepared by extracting Cymbidium petals, drying at low temperature, crushing, placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle at 55 degrees C under extraction pressure of 16 MPa at carbon dioxide flow rate of 30 l/hour for 80 minutes, extracting obtained extract with diethyl ether for three times, combining ether extract, drying at 3500 rpm for 15 minutes, and taking the supernatant to obtain Cymbidium essential oil, and uniformly mixing amomum tsao-ko essential oil, basil essential oil and Cymbidium essential oil to obtain compound essential oil. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a compound essential oil nanocapsules, comprising preparing compound essential oil to nanocapsules containing 20 wt.% compound essential oil using reverse emulsification method; and
   (2) a compound essential oil nanocapsule hydrogel, comprising adding compound essential oil nanocapsules to hydrogel substrate to obtain hydrogel containing 15-20 wt.% compound essential oil nanocapsules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of amomum tsaoko essential oil, basil essential oil and Cymbidium essential oil is 2:5:3. Preferred Method: The compound essential oil nanocapsules are prepared by adding 0.3 g chitosan to 30 ml deionized water to obtain solution, taking 10 ml compound essential oil, performing ultrasonic emulsification, adding obtained mixture to beaker, adding 10 g sodium alginate, dripping 0.1 mol/l sodium hydroxide for adjusting the pH value to 7.5 to obtain sodium alginate nanocapsules, washing and centrifuging the sodium alginate nanocapsules, adding sodium alginate nanocapsule to acetyl chitosan solution, and magnetically stirring for 3 hours to obtain compound essential oil nanocapsules. The compound essential oil nano-capsule hydrogel is prepared by uniformly mixing 940 g carbomer, 50 g glycerol and 80 g polysorbate in beaker, adding 50 ml deionized water, and uniformly stirring for completely dispersing carbomer, dissolving another solution of 4 g sodium hydroxide in 50 ml deionized water, gradually adding to carbomer solution, dissolving 1 g hydroxyethyl acrylate in 50 g ethanol, gradually adding 38.3-54.3 g compound essential oil nanocapsules, and uniformly stirring.
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary.

TI Industrial waste water advanced oxidation treatment system has electromagnetic and control system emitting high frequency electromagnetic energy, electromagnetic oxidation system, advanced treatment system and solid-liquid separation system.
AU SONG J
   YU T
   ZHAO F

AB    NOVELTY - An industrial waste water advanced oxidation treatment system has electromagnetic and control system (10) that emits high frequency electromagnetic energy to waste water of electromagnetic oxidation system (20) and carries out primary oxidation reaction, so that the long chain, stable, and difficult-to-oxidize organic chemical bond in the wastewater are distorted and weakened, and oxidation is improved. The waste water after the primary oxidation reaction is introduced into advanced treatment system (30). The advanced treatment is carried out under the action of catalytic medium, fully oxidizing the unstable organic matter in the waste water to generate water and carbon dioxide. The waste water after advanced treatment is introduced into solid-liquid separation system (40).
   USE - The system is for advanced oxidation treatment system of industrial wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The system solves the problem that industrial wastewater advanced treatment process cannot meet the discharge requirement in the prior art.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The industrial waste water advanced oxidation treatment system has electromagnetic oxidation system (20) containing the waste water to be processed and composite medicine. Electromagnetic and control system (10) emits high frequency electromagnetic energy to the waste water of the electromagnetic oxidation system and carries out primary oxidation reaction, so that the long chain, stable, and difficult-to-oxidize organic chemical bond in the wastewater are distorted and weakened, and oxidation is improved. Advanced treatment system (30) is connected with the electromagnetic oxidation system. The waste water after the primary oxidation reaction is introduced into the advanced treatment system. The advanced treatment is carried out under the action of catalytic medium in the advanced treatment system, fully oxidizing the unstable organic matter in the waste water after the primary oxidation reaction to generate water and carbon dioxide. Solid-liquid separation system (40) is connected with the depth processing system. The waste water after advanced treatment is introduced into the solid-liquid separation system. The electromagnetic and control system, the electromagnetic oxidation system, the depth processing system and the solid-liquid separation system are fixedly mounted on integrated combination system of the device, and connected and controlled by the integrated combination system of the device. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for deep oxidation treatment method for industrial waste water comprising subjecting the waste water to multi-stage, continuous, strong oscillating radiation action, performing the primary oxidation reaction; entering into the advanced treatment system, under the stirring of the stirring device attached with the catalytic medium, contacting the catalytic medium and the waste water, under the action of catalytic oxidation, subjecting the waste water to deep oxidation reaction; fully oxidizing to generate water and carbon dioxide; entering into the solid-liquid separation system, and separating to obtain supernatant water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic view illustrating the industrial waste water advanced oxidation treatment system.

   Electromagnetic oxidation system (20)
   Advanced treatment system (30)
   Solid-liquid separation system (40)

PN CN112800665-A
TI Performing self-feedback learning and cultivating            for plant growth based on artificial intelligence            involves configuring a cultivation system with multiple            independent plant incubators.
AU GU Y
   ZHU J
   WEI J
   YU Z
AE DIANKAI CHANGZHOU NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (DIAN-Non-standard)
   YU Z (YUZZ-Individual)
GA 202156697T
AB    NOVELTY - Performing self-feedback learning and                cultivating for plant growth based on artificial                intelligence involves configuring a cultivation                system with multiple independent plant incubators                and collecting existing preferred growth parameter                data of the plant to be cultivated. The preferred                growth parameters include the light intensity,                light spectrum, ambient temperature, environmental                humidity, wind flow, carbon dioxide concentration,                nutrient solution ratio, nutrient solution spray                cycle. The image data sample sets are collected for                each growth cycle of plants, and self-feedback                algorithm based on the image data sample set is                trained to obtain a plant growth model. A random                parameter range is set according to the preferred                growth parameter data, and set the growth                parameters of each plant growth stage of each plant                incubator.
   USE - Method for performing self-feedback learning                and cultivating for plant growth based on                artificial intelligence.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform self-feedback                learning and cultivating for plant growth based on                artificial intelligence, which conducts real-time                statistics and analysis of plants from the initial                seedling stage to flowering results, and                automatically conducts plant growth research in a                self-feedback manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing self-feedback learning and                cultivating for plant growth based on artificial                intelligence involves configuring a cultivation                system with multiple independent plant incubators                and collecting existing preferred growth parameter                data of the plant to be cultivated. The preferred                growth parameters include the light intensity,                light spectrum, ambient temperature, environmental                humidity, wind flow, carbon dioxide concentration,                nutrient solution ratio, nutrient solution spray                cycle. The image data sample sets are collected for                each growth cycle of plants, and self-feedback                algorithm based on the image data sample set is                trained to obtain a plant growth model. A random                parameter range is set according to the preferred                growth parameter data, and set the growth                parameters of each plant growth stage of each plant                incubator. The current plant growth stage is                determined according to the current plant growth                status data so as to configure the growth                environment at each stage of plant growth in each                plant incubator, periodically collect                two-dimensional and three-dimensional images of the                plants in each plant incubator at each growth                stage, and respectively mark the shooting time of                the images. The collected two-dimensional and                three-dimensional images are inputted into the                plant growth model in real time, optimizing the                plant growth model, and obtaining current plant                growth status data and predicted plant growth data.                The plant growth environment data is collected                periodically, including illuminance, light                spectrum, environmental temperature, environmental                humidity, wind flow, carbon dioxide concentration,                nutrient solution pH value, nutrient solution EC                value, nutrient solution spray cycle, and mark the                collection time. The current plant growth status                data and predicted plant growth data are used,                evaluating the plant growth environment data before                the current plant growth status data time sequence,                thus obtaining a production parameter tuning model,                and outputting the tuning growth parameter as the                existing preferred growth parameter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: According to the type                  of plant to be cultivated and the structure of                  the plant incubator, single plant monitoring area                  is selected, and two-dimensional camera and a                  three-dimensional camera are set up in each                  incubator to capture the overall plant growth                  status in the plant growth area, and set up a                  two-dimensional camera and a three-dimensional                  camera for shooting the growth state of the plant                  in the monitoring area of a single plant. The                  two-dimensional and three-dimensional images                  collected include an image of the overall growth                  state of the plant and the growth state of a                  single plant in the single plant monitoring area.                  The plant growth status data includes plant                  growth stage, overall size, height, leaf                  structure area, plant biomass data and the leaf                  area, leaf circumference, color depth, plant                  height, root length, area of a single plant,                  contour, seed setting rate, fruit color, fruit                  size, contour area and perimeter data. The                  classification algorithm includes a convolutional                  neural network. The crop pests and diseases data                  sets are collected, and training classification                  algorithms based on the crop pests and diseases                  data sets to obtain plant pests and diseases                  model. The collected two-dimensional and                  three-dimensional images of the leaves and plants                  are input into the plant disease and insect pest                  model, the plant disease and insect pest model is                  optimized, and the current plant growth disease                  and pest status data is obtained. A near-infrared                  spectroscopy nondestructive tester is arranged in                  each incubator. The infrared light detection data                  is collected in plant petals and fruit pulp by                  the near-infrared spectroscopy non-destructive                  tester and input the collected infrared light                  detection data into the BP neural network to                  establish the fruit and vegetable sugar content                  model. The nutrient solution spraying period                  includes a root spraying period and a leaf                  surface ultrasonic atomizing spraying                  period.

PT P
PN CN111073941-A; CN111073941-B
TI Preparation sandalwood polypeptide by pretreating            sandalwood fruit to remove impurities or dust, drying,            crushing, mixing sandalwood fruit powder with wheat            bran, and extracting sandalwood seed oil by            supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU LI H
   HUANG M X
   CHEN T
AE TIANJIN YUNSEE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
   TIANJIN YUNSEE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020389086
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation sandalwood polypeptide includes                pretreating sandalwood fruit to remove impurities                or dust, drying and crushing; mixing the sandalwood                fruit powder with wheat bran, extracting sandalwood                seed oil by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                of the mixed raw materials, and obtaining residue                A; adding acetone to the residue A containing wheat                bran, performing Soxhlet extraction, degreasing,                drying to remove the solvent, and obtaining residue                B; adding the residue B containing wheat bran with                distilled water, soaking, carrying out ultrasonic                oscillation at ultrasonic temperature, and                obtaining mixture A; centrifuging mixture A,                removing the insoluble impurities, and obtaining                polypeptide crude extract A; adjusting the pH of                crude polypeptide extract A with hydrochloric acid                solution to obtain acid precipitate turbid solution                A; centrifuging to obtain precipitate A; and                washing the precipitate A until neutral with                distilled water, and drying to obtain protein                powder.
   USE - The method is for preparation sandalwood                polypeptide.
   ADVANTAGE - The sandalwood polypeptide has more stable                properties and higher yield by synergistic                enzymolysis of neutral protease and plant protease.                The method is simple, has high yield and improved                residue utilization rate, and solves the problem of                protein waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation sandalwood polypeptide comprises                pretreating sandalwood fruit to remove impurities                or dust, drying and crushing to 2-20-mesh                sandalwood fruit powder; mixing the sandalwood                fruit powder with wheat bran at 10:1-1:3 mass                ratio, extracting sandalwood seed oil by                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the                mixed raw materials, and obtaining residue A;                adding acetone to the residue A containing wheat                bran at 1:1-1:3 volume ratio, performing Soxhlet                extraction for 6 hours, degreasing, drying at 60                degrees C for 5-7 hours to remove the solvent, and                obtaining residue B; adding the residue B                containing wheat bran with distilled water at                1:2-1:5 volume ratio, soaking, carrying out                ultrasonic oscillation for 12 hours at ultrasonic                temperature of 30 degrees C, and obtaining mixture                A; centrifuging mixture A for 20 minutes, removing                the insoluble impurities, and obtaining polypeptide                crude extract A; adjusting the pH of crude                polypeptide extract A at 3.8-4.2 with 3 mass%                hydrochloric acid solution to obtain acid                precipitate turbid solution A; centrifuging for 20                minutes to obtain precipitate A; washing the                precipitate A until neutral with distilled water,                and drying to obtain protein powder; adding the                protein powder with distilled water at 1:10-1:15                mass ratio, heating at 50-60 degrees C, stirring,                adding pH regulator to adjust the pH to neutral,                adding 8-12 mass% (based on the mass of protein                powder) protease, fully stirring, ensuring full                contact between enzyme and substrate, shaking at 45                degrees C using constant temperature water bath                pot, carrying out enzymolysis for 5 hours, and                obtaining enzymolysis hydrolysate A; and putting in                an oil bath at 120 degrees C for 10 minutes to                inactivate the enzyme, taking the supernatant as                polypeptide hydrolysate, and drying to obtain                sandalwood polypeptide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The drying in seventh and                  ninth steps is freeze-drying at - 40 to - 50                  degrees C and 20-30 Pa or microwave drying at                  0.04-0.08 mPa and 55-65 degrees C. The                  centrifuging in fifth and seventh steps is                  conducted at 4000 revolutions/minute. The                  protease is neutral protease and plant protease                  at 1:1 mass ratio. The total amount of neutral                  protease and plant protease is 5000                  units/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pH regulator is                  hydrochloric acid or sodium hydroxide. In the                  enzymolysis of step nine, the pH regulator is                  required to adjust the pH to neutral all the                  time.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The sandalwood polypeptide                  is a small molecular protein peptide having                  molecular weight of 1000-3000 Da. The content of                  sandalwood polypeptide is 4.8-8.6 g/kg.
