PN CN110862842-A
TI Preparing liquid natural gas from syngas involves preparing low-rank coal by gasification reduction process to prepare upgraded coal and rich gas, using upgraded coal and low-rank coal to make coal water slurry, and preparing syngases.
AU WANG J
   YU T
   XUE A
   WANG Z
   ZHENG S
   LV B
AE ZHEJIANG TIANLU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202020907Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing liquid natural gas (LNG) from syngas involves: (1) preparing a low-rank coal by gasification reduction process to prepare upgraded coal and rich gas using the upgraded coal and the low-rank coal to make a coal water slurry, preparing a syngas (A) from the upgraded coal, preparing a syngas (B) from the coal water slurry, preparing a syngas (C) from the rich gas, subjecting the syngases (A)-(C) to pre-desulfurization process, hydrogenation process, desulfurization process, conversion process and decarbonization process to get purified gas, passing the purified gas into a reactor (A), and preheating at 150-500 degrees C to get a preheated purified gas; and (2) introducing the preheated purified gas into a reactor (B) in which carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of the preheated purified gas are reacted in the presence of a high-temperature resistant methane catalyst (A) to synthesize methane to obtain a methane stream (A) to obtain a methane product stream (A).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing LNG from syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of LNG from syngas with high quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing liquid natural gas (LNG) from syngas involves: (1) preparing a low-rank coal by gasification reduction process to prepare upgraded coal and rich gas using the upgraded coal and the low-rank coal to make a coal water slurry, preparing a syngas (A) from the upgraded coal, preparing a syngas (B) from the coal water slurry, preparing a syngas (C) from the rich gas, subjecting the syngases (A)-(C) to pre-desulfurization process, hydrogenation process, desulfurization process, conversion process and decarbonization process to get purified gas, passing the purified gas into a reactor (A), and preheating at 150-500 degrees C to get a preheated purified gas; (2) introducing the preheated purified gas into a reactor (B) in which carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of the preheated purified gas are reacted in the presence of a high-temperature resistant methane catalyst (A) to synthesize methane to obtain a methane stream (A) to obtain a methane product stream (A); (3) passing the methane product stream (A) obtained in step (2) through a gas-water separation device (A) to obtain a methane product stream (B); (4) introducing the methane product stream (B) into a reactor (C) in which carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of the methane product stream (B) are reacted in the presence of a high-temperature resistant methane catalyst (B) to synthesize methane to obtain a methane stream (B) to obtain a methane product stream (C); (5) passing the methane product stream (C) obtained in step (4) through a gas-water separation device (B) to separate original LNG stream; and (6) passing the original LNG stream into a liquefaction process, where the cryogenic liquefaction process produces methane with a volume percentage of not less than 90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The methane stream (A) is passed to a reactor (D) in which carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of the methane stream (A) are reacted in the presence of a high-temperature resistant methane catalyst (C) to synthesize methane to obtain a methane stream (C) to obtain the methane product stream (A). The methane stream (C) is passed to a reactor (E) in which carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of the methane stream (C) are reacted in the presence of a high-temperature resistant methane catalyst (D) to synthesize methane to obtain a methane stream (D) to obtain the methane product stream (A). The methane stream (B) is passed to a reactor (F) in which carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of the methane stream (B) are reacted in the presence of a high-temperature resistant methane catalyst (E) to synthesize methane to yield a methane stream (D), such that the volume percentage of carbon monoxide in the methane stream (D) is less than 1%, resulting in the methane product stream (C). The syngas (A) passes through the pre-desulfurization process, the hydrogenation process, the desulfurization process, the conservation process and the decarburization process to obtain a purified gas (A). The syngas gas (B) is passed through the pre-desulfurization process, the hydrogenation process, the desulfurization process, the conversion process, and the decarbonization process to obtain a purified gas (B). The syngas gas (C) is passed through the pre-desulfurization process, the hydrogenation process, the desulfurization process, and the decarbonization process to obtain a purified gas (C). The purified gas includes one of the purified gas (A), the purified gas (B) and the purified gas (C). The purified gas (C) is passed to the reactor (B), the reactor (C) and/or the reactor (D), so that when synthesizing methane, the reaction temperature is 200-500 degrees C. A portion of the methane stream (B) is recycled back to the reactor (B), so that when synthesizing methane, the reaction temperature is 200-500 degrees C. A portion of the methane stream (C) is recycled back to the reactor (B) and/or the reactor (D), so that when synthesizing methane, the reaction temperature is 200-500 degrees C. A portion of the methane stream (D) is recycled back to the reactor (B), the reactor (D) and/or the reactor (E), so that when synthesizing methane, the reaction temperature is 200-500 degrees C. The carbon dioxide-rich gas is introduced into the reactor (B), the reactor (D) and/or the reactor (E). The hydrogen-carbon ratio of the carbon dioxide-rich gas is (2.9-3.5):1. In the carbon dioxide-rich gas, the volume percentage of carbon dioxide is not less than 5%, and the reaction temperature is 200-500 degrees C.

AU XU M
   CAO H
   LUO D
   ZHENG S
   LI Y
   GU P
   SONG H
AE DONGGUAN HUAIPU PLANT ESSENTIAL OIL CO (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201976831K
AB    NOVELTY - A freckle-removing compound essential oil comprises 20-25 wt.%, preferably 25 wt.% rose essential oil, 10-15 wt.%, preferably 15 wt.% lemon essential oil, 10-15 wt.%, preferably 15 wt.% neroli essential oil, 2-5 wt.%, preferably 5 wt.% Angelica sinensis essential oil, 1-3 wt.%, preferably 1 wt.% rhizoma ligustici wallichii essential oil and 37-57 wt.%, preferably 39 wt.% base oil.
   USE - Freckle-removing compound essential oil used for removing facial spot.
   ADVANTAGE - The freckle-removing compound essential oil has high scientific, safe and non-toxic side effects, high whitening effect and high skin care effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of freckle-removing compound essential oil, which involves (1) adding fresh rose flower and dried A.sinensis and rhizoma ligustici wallichii, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide, refining by molecular distillation to obtain A.sinensis essential oil, rhizoma ligustici wallichii oil and rose essential oil, where supercritical carbon dioxide extraction temperature is 45-48 degrees C, the pressure is 13.70-17.80 MPa, time is 50-100 minutes, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 25-35 kg/hour, the molecular distillation method feed temperature is 40-45 degrees C, injection speed is 480-530 mL/hour, evaporation temperature is 110-115 degrees C, two-stage evaporation temperature is 60-65 degrees C, (2) crushing fresh lemon and pressing in a press, filtering and collecting the oil, and purifying by molecular distillation to obtain lemon essential oil, where molecular distillation feed temperature is 40-45 degrees C, injection speed is 480-530 mL/hour, temperature for evaporation (I) is 110-115 degrees C, and temperature for evaporation (II) is 60-65 degrees C, (3) extracting fresh orange flower by steam distillation to obtain neroli essential oil, where extraction temperature of steam distillation process is 100-120 degrees C, and the extraction time is 5-7 hours, (4) crushing raw material, pressing in a press, filtering and collecting the oil, purifying by molecular distillation to obtain base oil, where raw material is rose hip, coconut, jojoba seed or evening primrose seed, the base oil is rosehip oil, coconut oil, jojoba oil or evening primrose oil, molecular distillation feed temperature is 40-45 degrees C, an injection speed is 480-530 mL/hour, temperature for evaporation (I) is 110-115 degrees C, temperature for evaporation (II) is 60-65 degrees C, (5) uniformly mixing A.sinensis essential oil, rhizoma ligustici wallichii essential oil, rose essential oil, lemon essential oil, neroli essential oil and base oil to obtain compound essential oil, where component comprises 20-25 wt.% rose essential oil, 10-15 wt.% lemon essential oil, 10-15 wt.% neroli essential oil, 2-5 wt.% angelica essential oil, 1-3 wt.% rhizoma ligustici wallichii essential oil and 37-57 wt.% base oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The base oil is composed of rosehip oil, evening primrose oil, jojoba oil, and/or coconut oil.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

PN CN109999397-A
TI Preparing storage stability flame foam material comprises e.g. taking camphor leaves, washing and drying, crushing, placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extracting device, static extraction, collecting extract, and filtering.
AU WANG C
AE WANG C (WANG-Individual)
GA 2019628788
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing storage stability flame foam material comprises e.g. (i) taking camphor leaves and washing and drying, crushing and placing in the supercritical carbon dioxide extracting device, the static extraction, collecting the extract and filtering, collecting the filtrate to obtain modified essential oil, taking modified oil, adding ethanol solution with sulfuric acid solution in the beaker, stirring, mixing for preserving temperature and reacting, allowing to stand, cooling and placing in a separatory funnel, allowing to stand and layering and collecting the upper layer oil phase, adjusting the pH value to 7, washing treatment, distilling and separating the oil phase, and collecting to obtain stable modified solution, and (ii) taking rubber particles and vacuum freezing and drying, grinding and dispersing and collecting the dispersed powder to obtain rubber powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing storage stability flame foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the displacement resistance of the desulfurized rubber powder particles; improves the structural properties of the material and storage stability of the material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing storage stability flame foam material comprises (i) taking camphor leaves and washing and drying, crushing and placing in the supercritical carbon dioxide extracting device, the static extraction, collecting the extract and filtering, collecting the filtrate to obtain the modified essential oil, respectively taking the modified oil, adding the mass fraction of 85% ethanol solution with mass fraction of 30% sulfuric acid solution in the beaker, stirring and mixing for preserving temperature and reacting, allowing to stand and cooling to room temperature to obtain reaction liquid and placing in a separatory funnel, allowing to stand and layering and collecting the upper layer oil phase, adjusting the pH value to 7, washing treatment, distilling and separating the oil phase, and collecting to obtain stable modified solution, (ii) taking rubber particles and vacuum freezing and drying, grinding and dispersing and collecting the dispersed powder to obtain rubber powder, taking bacteria culture, Tween-80 (RTM: Non-ionic surfactant), glucose in the beaker, stirring and mixing and sterilizing treatment, allowing to stand and cooling to room temperature to obtain modified substrate, (iii) adding yeast powder according to the inoculum size is 1:50 into the modified matrix, adjusting the pH value to 5.5, shaking and culturing for 6-8 days, ultraviolet sterilizing and filtering, collecting the filter cake and grinding, sieving to obtain modified particles, (iv) adding 45-50 pts. wt. glycol, 10-15 pts. wt. modified liquid stable, 25-30 pts. wt. propylene glycol, 10-15 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, 3-5 pts. wt. dodecyl betaine, 6-8 pts. wt. ammonium chloride, 8-10 pts. wt. urea in the beaker, stirring and mixing and allowing to stand to obtain the mixed solution, and (v) adding the modified particles into the mixed solution, ultrasonically dispersing, continuously stirring and mixing, and allowing to stand.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The static extraction described in the step (i) is such that the extraction pressure is 25-28 MPa, the extraction temperature is 45-50 degrees C, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 1.7-2 l/minutes, the separation temperature is 52 degrees C, and the static extraction is 45-50 minutes. The ratio of the modified essential oil, the mass fraction 85% ethanol solution and the mass fraction 30% sulfuric acid solution in the step (i) is determined by weight, and 40-45 pts. wt. modified essential oil, 25-30 pts. wt. 85% ethanol solution and 6-8 pts. wt. 30% sulfuric acid solution. The pH adjustment as described in the step (i) is carried out using a saturated sodium hydrogencarbonate solution at room temperature. The washing treatment described in the step (i) is to rinse with deionized water for 3-5 times. The bacterial culture medium described in the step (ii) is divided into 45-50 pts. wt. peptone, 25-30 pts. wt. deionized water, 10-15 pts. wt. malt-impregnated powder, 1-2 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 0.5-1 pts. wt. dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. potassium dihydrogen phosphate and 1-2 pts. wt. zinc sulfide are prepared by stirring and mixing and sterilizing. The bacterial culture medium, Tween-80 (RTM: Non-ionic surfactant), and glucose described in the step (ii). The shaking temperature of the shaker described in the step (iii) is 30-32 degrees C. The modified particles described in the step (iii) have a particle diameter of 200 meshes. The ratio between the modified particles and the mixed liquid described in the step (v) is 1:5.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); K01 (Fire fighting, fire-extinguishing compositions - excluding fire engines, sprinkler systems, hose reels and protective clothing (A62D with K2).); P35 (Life-saving, fire-fighting (A62).)

TI Production of reactor product gas used for            producing e.g. ethanol, involves reacting carbonaceous            material with steam, oxidizing product gas to obtain            another product gas, mixing product gases, and reacting            combined product gas.
AU CHANDRAN R
   NEWPORT D G
   BURCIAGA D A
   LEO D M
   MILLER J K
   HARRINGTON K E
   ATTWOOD B C
   NEWPORT D
   BURCIAGA D
AE THERMOCHEM RECOVERY INT INC (THER-Non-standard)
   THERMOCHEM RECOVERY INT INC (THER-Non-standard)
GA 201936745H
AB    NOVELTY - Production of a reactor product gas (C)                involves providing a source of carbonaceous                material (2C-01) comprising one or more materials                chosen from agricultural residues, agro-industrial                residues, animal waste, biomass, cardboard, coal,                coke, energy crops, farm slurries, fishery waste,                food waste, fruit processing waste, lignite,                municipal solid waste, paper, paper mill residues,                paper mill sludge, paper mill-spent liquors,                plastic, refuse-derived fuel, sewage sludge, tires,                urban waste, wood products and/or wood wastes,                reacting the carbonaceous material with steam to                produce a reactor product gas (A),                sub-stoichiometrically oxidizing at least a portion                of the reactor product gas (A) to form a reactor                product gas (B), mixing the reactor product gas (A)                and reactor product gas (B) to form a combined                product gas, and reacting the combined product gas                with an oxygen-containing gas to produce the                reactor product gas (C).
   USE - Production of reactor product gas used for                manufacturing synthesize product e.g. ethanol,                mixed alcohol, methanol, dimethyl ether and                Fischer-Tropsch products (all claimed) e.g. diesel,                jet fuel and naphtha.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical manufacture of                synthesize products with high efficiency and carbon                conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of a reactor product gas (C)                involves providing a source of carbonaceous                material comprising one or more materials chosen                from agricultural residues, agro-industrial                residues, animal waste, biomass, cardboard, coal,                coke, energy crops, farm slurries, fishery waste,                food waste, fruit processing waste, lignite,                municipal solid waste, paper, paper mill residues,                paper mill sludge, paper mill-spent liquors,                plastic, refuse-derived fuel, sewage sludge, tires,                urban waste, wood products and/or wood wastes,                reacting the carbonaceous material with steam to                produce a reactor product gas (A) having a hydrogen                to carbon monoxide ratio (h1) and a carbon monoxide                to carbon dioxide ratio (c1),                sub-stoichiometrically oxidizing at least a portion                of the reactor product gas (A) to form a reactor                product gas (B) having a hydrogen to carbon                monoxide ratio (h2) and a carbon monoxide to carbon                dioxide ratio (c2), mixing the reactor product gas                (A) and reactor product gas (B) to form a combined                product gas, and reacting the combined product gas                with an oxygen-containing gas to produce the                reactor product gas (C) having a hydrogen to carbon                monoxide ratio (h3) and a carbon monoxide to carbon                dioxide ratio (c3). The ratio (h1) is more than the                ratio (h2), the ratio (h3) is lower than the ratio                (c1), and the ratio (c3) is more than both the                ratio (c1) and (c2). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for production of synthesize                products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of mass                flow regulation subsystem or sequence step of                feedstock delivery system.
   Mass flow regulation subsystem (2C)
   Carbonaceous material (2C-01)
   Air (2C-03)
   Feeder input (2C-05)
   Mass flow regulation volume (CV-2C)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The reaction of the                  carbonaceous material with steam involves steam                  reforming the carbonaceous material at a                  superficial fluidization velocity of 0.6-25                  ft/second to produce carbon monoxide and                  subjecting the carbon monoxide to a water-gas                  shift reaction to produce carbon dioxide. The                  combined gas product is reacted with a                  hydrocarbon stream chosen from Fischer Tropsch                  tail gas, natural gas, naphtha, product gas                  and/or landfill gas. Preferred Components: The                  product gas (A) further comprises semi-volatile                  organic compounds and volatile organic                  compounds.

PN CN109432377-A
TI Preparation of day onion stalk extract by preparing day onion stalk extract, preparing enzymatic hydrolysate, concentrating, mixing with ginger brown sugar extract, centrifuging, vacuum concentrating, and adding day onion stalk extract.
AU KE Y
   YANG J
   TIAN W
AE SHENLONGJIA CHINESE MEDICINE HOSPITAL (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201925034R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of day onion stalk extract includes taking day onion stalk, crushing into powder, freezing, drying, and subjecting to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to obtain day onion stalk extract; adding ginger slices to pure water, boiling, filtering, collecting filtrate, adding residue with brown sugar, mixing, pulping, steaming, drying, grinding to powder, adding ethanol, reflux extracting for 3 times while stirring, and combining extracts to obtain ginger brown sugar extract; mixing sancha bitter wood root, pine knot, papaya and loofah, crushing, adding to collected filtrate, adding enzyme comprising cellulase, pectinase and papain, performing ultrasonic enzymatic hydrolysis, inactivating enzyme, collecting enzymatic hydrolysate, concentrating, mixing with ginger brown sugar extract, centrifuging, and vacuum concentrating supernatant to obtain thick paste; and adding day onion stalk extract, stirring into paste, and adding to sticker.
   USE - Method for preparation of day onion stalk extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The extract is suitable for patients who are intolerant of oral medications, can stimulate meridian-acupoint, and has good clinical effect and little bad reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of day onion stalk extract comprises:
   (A) taking 7 pts. wt. day onion stalk, crushing into powder, freezing to - 5 degrees C for 12 hours, drying, and subjecting to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction at 40 degrees C and at pressure of 22 MPa and at carbon dioxide flow rate of 10 kg/hour for 2.5 hours to obtain day onion stalk extract;
   (B) adding 2 pts. wt. ginger slices to 5 pts. wt. pure water, boiling for 5 minutes, filtering, collecting filtrate, adding residue with 3 pts. wt. brown sugar, mixing, pulping, steaming, drying, grinding to powder, adding ethanol at ratio of 1:30, reflux extracting for 3 times at 50 degrees C for 1.5 hours, 75 degrees C for 1 hour and at 90 degrees C for 45 minutes respectively while stirring for 5 minutes every 25 minutes interval, and combining extracts to obtain ginger brown sugar extract;
   (C) mixing 3 pts. wt. sancha bitter wood root, 1 pt. wt. pine knot, 1.5 pts. wt. papaya and 2.2 pts. wt. loofah, crushing, adding to collected filtrate, adding enzyme comprising cellulase, pectinase and papain, performing ultrasonic enzymatic hydrolysis at 40 degrees C for 55 minutes, inactivating enzyme, collecting enzymatic hydrolysate, concentrating until solid content is 12%, mixing with ginger brown sugar extract, centrifuging, and vacuum concentrating supernatant to obtain thick paste having relative density of 1.13; and
   (D) adding day onion stalk extract, stirring into paste, and adding to sticker.
   The sticker comprises drug layer, adhesive layer, and two layers of release paper. The drug layer is made of spunlace nonwoven wrapped in target plaster, ans has circular area in the center and ventilated area filled with breathable cotton. The adhesive layer comprises three adhesive areas. The two layers of release paper covers the drug layer and adhesive layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of cellulase, pectinase and papain is 2:2:1. The concentration of cellulase is 0.07 g/L. The concentration of pectinase is 0.3 g/L. The concentration of papain is 0.15 g/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The vacuum concentration is done at 60 degrees C, steam pressure of 0.2 MPa and at vacuum degree of - 0.03 MPa. The centrifuging is done twice at speed of 2300 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes and at 4100 revolutions/minute for 8 minutes. Preferred Components: The sticker is medical tape.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-A08; B04-A10; B04-A98; B04-L01; B04-L05B; B04-L05C; B14-S18; D05-A02
IP A61K-036/9068; A61K-009/70; A61P-009/04; A61P-009/10
PD CN109432377-A   08 Mar 2019   A61K-036/9068   201945   Pages: 7   Chinese
AD CN109432377-A    CN11609260    27 Dec 2018
PI CN11609260    27 Dec 2018
DN 90355-0-0-0-K M; 103220-0-0-0-K M; 526203-0-0-0-K M
CI RA023Q-K M; RA2Z3M-K M; RA078P-K M; RA6R6I-K M

PT P
PN CN109321596-A; CN109321596-B
TI Preparing exosome of carrier protein useful in            delivery system for gene, by constructing recombinant            expression vector containing gene fragment of target            protein, transfecting expression vector into cells,            culturing and isolating exosomes.
AU XIE Q
   JI Y
   XIONG S
   CHEN J
   LU J
AE UNIV JINAN GUANGDONG (UJIN-C)
   UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
GA 201918556P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing exosome of carrier                protein involves (a) constructing a recombinant                expression vector containing a gene fragment of a                target exogenous protein, (b) transfecting the                recombinant expression vector into cells for                expression, culturing the transfected cells and                allowing the cells to secrete exosomes carrying the                exogenous target protein and (c) isolating the                exosomes in the cell culture supernatant.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing exosome of                carrier protein which is useful in a delivery                system for gene, protein or drug (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require the addition of                serum, hence eliminates the contamination of serum                vesicles during exosome extraction, produces large                quantities of exosomes loaded with biologically                active antibodies and is suitable for industrial                production. The exosome contains active proteins,                is easily permeated through tissues and cell                membranes and blood-brain barriers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing exosome of carrier                protein involves (a) constructing a recombinant                expression vector containing a gene fragment of a                target exogenous protein, (b) transfecting the                recombinant expression vector into cells for                expression, culturing the transfected cells and                allowing the cells to secrete exosomes carrying the                exogenous target protein, where (i) when the                expression is transient, (A) inoculating the cells                cultured to logarithmic growth phase into the                medium at a concentration of 4-6x 105 cells/ml and                culturing at 36-38 degrees C and 160-200                revolutions per minute (rpm) under 3-7% carbon                dioxide in a Tubespin (RTM: Not defined) tube for                60-84 hours and maintaining cell viability in the 1                l culture shake flask at 90% and (B) transfecting                the recombinant expression vector into cells and                culturing the transfected cells in suspension at                36-38 degrees C and 160-200 rpm under 4-8% carbon                dioxide until the cell viability is less than 30%                or (ii) when the expression is stable (A)                transfecting the recombinant expression vector into                the cells, where the cell viability was greater                than 90% on the day of culture, culturing in the                cloning medium, determining the protein expression                by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) on the                12-14th day of culture and selecting and amplifying                the clone with high expression in 12-well plate and                (B) inoculating the cells cultured to logarithmic                growth phase into the medium at a final                concentration of 4-6x 105 cells/ml, culturing at                36-38 degrees C and 160-200 rpm under 3-7% carbon                dioxide in a Tubespin (RTM: Not defined) tube for                60-84 hours, maintaining cell viability in the 1 l                culture shake flask at 90%, adding the amplified                cells into 1 l culture shake flask, culturing for                60-84 hours, transferring to 5 l fermenter,                culturing for 60-84 hours, transferring to a 20 l                fermenter, culturing for 60-84 hours, transferring                to a 200 l fermenter and culturing for 60-84 hours                and (c) isolating the exosomes in the cell culture                supernatant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Cell: The cell is at least one of                  CHO cells and HEK 293F cells, where when the cell                  is a CHO cell, the medium is a ProCHO5 medium of                  Lonza Corporation and when the cells were HEK                  293F cells, the medium was 293 serum-free medium                  of Sigma. Preferred Method: The step (c) involves                  isolating the exosomes in the cell culture                  supernatant by an ultracentrifugation method or                  an exosomal extraction kit. The                  ultracentrifugation method involves (a)                  centrifuging cell culture supernatant at 300 g                  for 10 minutes, then centrifuging the cell                  culture supernatant at 2000 g for 10-20 minutes,                  collecting the first supernatant and removing the                  cells, (ii) centrifuging the first supernatant                  10000 g for 30 minutes, discarding the                  precipitate and leaving the second supernatant to                  remove subcellular components, (iii) centrifuging                  the second supernatant at 100000 g for 70-90                  minutes, discarding the supernatant and retaining                  the first precipitate, (iv) resuspending the                  first precipitate obtained in the step (iii) in a                  phosphate buffered saline (PBS) solution, mixing                  and then centrifuging at 100000 g for 70-90                  minutes to wash away the soluble protein and                  washing the precipitate to obtain the exosomes,                  where steps (a)-(d) are all carried out at 4                  degrees C. The method involves performing                  large-scale culture of the stably expressed cells                  having a high expression amount for 15-18 days                  until cell viability is 80% and the cell density                  is 1-3x 107 cells/ml. Preferred Biomolecule: The                  expression vector is pcDNATM3.4. The exogenous                  target protein is a human epidermal growth factor                  receptor 2 (HER2) antibody or recombinant fusion                  protein TNF receptor (p75) fragment                  crystallizable region (TNFR-Fc).

PN CN109107557-A; CN109107557-B
TI Photocatalytic graphene/silicon composite film            useful for reducing carbon dioxide and water to form            methane, carbon monoxide and oxygen, comprises silicon            nanoparticles are supported on surface of graphene film            to form silicon nano film.
AU GAO C
   PENG L
   LIU Y
   GUO Y
AE HANGZHOU GAOXI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201904044D
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic graphene/silicon composite film                comprises silicon nanoparticles which are supported                on the surface of the graphene film to form a                silicon nano film, where graphene film is                cross-linked between layers with 1-5% of cross                linking, the thickness of the graphene film is                10-100 nm and the defect density is ID/IG less than                or equal to 0.01.
   USE - The composite film is useful for reducing                carbon dioxide and water to form methane, carbon                monoxide and oxygen (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing photocatalytic graphene/silicon                composite film, comprising (i) preparing graphene                oxide to 0.5-10 mu g/ml graphene oxide aqueous                solution and filtering to form a film, (ii) placing                the graphene oxide film attached to the suction                filter substrate in a closed container and                fumigating from the bottom upwards at 80-100                degrees C HI for 0.1-1 hours, (iii) coating                uniformly melted solid transfer agent on the                surface of the reduced graphene oxide film and                cooling at room temperature until the film and AAO                film substrate are separated, (iv) subjecting the                reduced graphene oxide film for heating, so that                the solid transfer agent is sublimated or                volatilized, (v) heating reduced graphene oxide                film at 300 degrees C at 1 degrees C/minutes (slow                heating and increasing the surface of the graphene                film wrinkles to expand the area of the graphene                film in the unit space), heating to 2000 degrees C                at 10 degrees C/minute for 6-12 hours to remove                most of the atomic defects inside the graphene but                restoring internal stacking structure of the                graphene, (vi) spraying a layer of metal                nanoparticles on the surface of the graphene film                treated by means of magnetron sputtering, where                metal nanoparticles comprises titanium, tungsten,                iron, magnesium, and molybdenum, molar amount of                the sputtered metal nanoparticles is not greater                than 30% of molar amount of carbon atoms in the                graphene film, subjecting sputtered graphene film                with the metal nanoparticles to chlorination                treatment at 800-1200 degrees C so that dispersing                metal nanoparticles in the form of chloride, (vii)                treating the chlorinated graphene film at 2000                degrees C to obtain an interlayer cross linked                graphene film, and (viii) spraying the surface of                the cross-linked graphene film with a layer of                silicon nanoparticles having a thickness of 5-40                nm; and
   (2) application of photocatalytic                graphene/silicon composite film, comprising                separating electrons and holes by an electric                field, where separated electrons and holes are                respectively located on two surfaces of the                composite film, one side of the silicon                nanoparticles is coated with electrons to form an                electron layer, and the other side is formed with                holes to form an empty space cave layer and                potential difference is formed between the electron                layer and the hole layer for reducing carbon                dioxide and water to form methane, carbon monoxide                and oxygen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid transfer                  agent comprises paraffin, naphthalene, arsenic                  trioxide, camphor, sulfur, norbornene and rosin,                  which are submerged or volatilized under certain                  conditions, and insoluble in water. Preferred                  Method: The sublimation temperature of the solid                  transfer agent is controlled below 320 degrees C.                  The chlorination treatment comprises heating                  graphene film sputtered with the metal                  nanoparticles in an environment having a chlorine                  content of 0.5-10% for 0.1-4 hours. The step                  (vii) comprises heating at 2000 degrees C                  comprises heating below 1500 degrees C at rate of                  5-20 degrees C/minute and heating above 1500                  degrees C at rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute. The                  photocatalytic graphene/silicon composite film                  forms electrons and holes under                  illumination.

PN CN108315038-A
TI Environmentally friendly recycling waste bamboo chips comprises e.g. washing bamboo chips using alkali liquid, drying, crushing into particle, placing in charcoal kiln, increasing temperature, performing pyrolysis, and collecting distillate.
AU YANG J
AE GUANGXI AISHANGJIA HOME FURNISHING CO LT (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201859616V
AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally friendly recycling waste bamboo chips comprises e.g. washing bamboo chips using alkali liquid, drying until moisture content is 15-20%, crushing into particle, placing particles into the charcoal kiln, increasing temperature at 20-50 degrees C/hours speed to 80-150 degrees C, performing pyrolysis for 1-2 hours, collecting distillate to obtain A crude wood vinegar solution, continuously heating at 300-400 degrees C for 1-2 hours, collecting distilled liquid to obtain B crude wood vinegar, raising temperature to 800-900 degrees C, maintaining temperature for 1-2 hours, collecting distilled liquid to obtain C crude wood vinegar solution, placing A crude wood vinegar solution into the distillation pot, distilling at 20 MPa pressure at 150 degrees C, collecting the condensed liquid to obtain the refined A wood vinegar, placing B crude wood vinegar into the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, and extracting with mixture of diethyl ether, toluene and ethyl acetate to obtain refined B wood vinegar.
   USE - The method is useful for environmentally friendly recycling waste bamboo chips.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high bamboo resource utilization rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Environmentally friendly recycling waste bamboo chips comprises (i) washing bamboo chips using alkali liquid, drying until moisture content is 15-20%, and crushing into particle, (ii) placing particles into the charcoal kiln, increasing temperature at 20-50 degrees C/hours speed to 80-150 degrees C, performing pyrolysis for 1-2 hours, collecting distillate to obtain A crude wood vinegar solution, continuously heating at 300-400 degrees C for 1-2 hours, collecting distilled liquid to obtain B crude wood vinegar, raising temperature to 800-900 degrees C, maintaining temperature for 1-2 hours, and collecting distilled liquid to obtain C crude wood vinegar solution, (iii) placing A crude wood vinegar solution into the distillation pot, distilling at 20 MPa pressure at 150 degrees C, collecting the condensed liquid to obtain the refined A wood vinegar, placing B crude wood vinegar into the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting with a mixture of diethyl ether, toluene and ethyl acetate to obtain refined B wood vinegar, where extraction temperature is 30-35 degrees C, pressure is 60-63 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate is 4 kg/hour, adding activated carbon into the C crude wood vinegar solution for adsorption and removing impurity, where adding amount of activated carbon is 10-15 g/l, treatment time is 30-50 minutes, and treatment temperature is 40-50 degrees C, and (iv) aging refined A wood vinegar, refined B wood vinegar, and refined C wood vinegar for 4-6 months. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) soil modifying agent comprising purified C wood vinegar solution having 10-20% concentration;
   (2) feed additive comprising refined B wood vinegar solution; and
   (3) bactericide comprising refined A wood vinegar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The alkali is lime aqueous solution or 1-5% potassium hydroxide solution. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the ratio of diethyl ether, toluene, and ethyl acetate is 1:24-30:2-7. In the step (iv), aging under infrared irradiation using near-infrared irradiator, where infrared wavelength of irradiation is 0.75-1.3 mu m.

TI Method for controlling sleep mode for air            conditioner, involves detecting indoor environment            temperature after set time, carbon dioxide            concentration, and adjusting preset temperature            according to indoor environment temperature ring and            carbon dioxide concentration.

   ZHANG L
   WANG X
   LIU J
   YI H
   LIU Y
   CHENG J
   CHEN Z
   ZHOU P
AE AUX AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (AUXS-C)
   NINGBO AUX ELECTRIC CO LTD (AUXS-C)
GA 202227965S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves allowing an                air-conditioner to enter into a sleep mode. An                indoor environment temperature ring is detected                after set time. A carbon dioxide concentration is                detected. A preset temperature of the air                conditioner is dynamically adjusted according to                the indoor environment temperature ring and the                carbon dioxide concentration. The indoor                environment temperature ring is provided with a                temperature sensor and a humidity sensor. The                humidity sensor is connected with a carbon dioxide                sensor.
   USE - Method for controlling a sleep mode for an air                conditioner (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables detecting the carbon                dioxide concentration in the sleep mode and                comparing the carbon dioxide concentration with the                threshold value to adjust the set temperature, thus                avoiding over-cooling or overheating risks caused                by the change of the indoor user number, and hence                improving the sleep experience of the user. The                method enables judging the user preferences                according to the indoor environment temperature                when entering into the sleep mode, thus improving                the user experience.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a device for controlling a sleep mode for                an air conditioner; and
   (2) a computer readable storage medium                comprising instructions to perform a method for                controlling a sleep mode for an air                conditioner.

PT P
PN CN112774689-A; CN112774689-B
TI Preparing manganese-doped bismuth ferrite            nano-wire useful in catalytic reduction of carbon            dioxide, comprises mixing and dissolving bismuth salt,            iron salt and manganese salt in organic solvent, adding            water, and continuously stirring.
AU ZHU M
   HE J
   PU C
   WANG X
   LU G
AE UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
GA 2021587156
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing manganese-doped bismuth ferrite                nano-wire, comprises (i) mixing and dissolving                bismuth salt, iron salt and manganese salt in an                organic solvent, adding water and continuously                stirring to obtain mixed solution, and adjusting pH                of the mixed solution to be alkaline to obtain                precipitate; and (ii) dispersing precipitate in                water to obtain suspension, adding strong alkali,                stirring and adding polyethylene glycol,                continuously stirring, and subjecting obtained                mixture to hydro-thermal reaction.
   USE - The nano-wire is useful in catalytic reduction                of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly; has                high repeatability and high value; prepares                nano-wire with significant piezoelectric effect,                catalyst will generate deformation under the action                of ultrasonic, generates potential polarization,                and generates a large amount of electrons which                effectively catalyze carbon dioxide reduction to                generate methane and carbon monoxide. The carbon                dioxide reduction method is cost-effective; has                simple process flow, strong operability and wide                application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) manganese-doped bismuth ferrite nano-wire                prepared by the above method; and
   (2) catalyzing carbon dioxide reduction by                piezoelectric effect, comprising uniformly                dispersing manganese-doped bismuth ferrite                nano-wire in methanol-water mixed solution,                introducing carbon dioxide gas, and performing                catalytic reduction to the carbon dioxide in                reaction system by using the manganese-doped                bismuth ferrite nano-wire under the action of                mechanical force.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the                  bismuth salt, iron salt and manganese salt is                  3-6:3-6:0.25-1.25. The bismuth salt is bismuth                  chloride or bismuth nitrate pentahydrate. The                  iron salt is ferric chloride or ferric nitrate                  nonahydrate. The manganese salt is manganese                  chloride or manganese nitrate tetrahydrate. The                  organic solvent is ethylene glycol or acetone.                  The volume ratio of the organic solvent to water                  in the step (i) is 35-50:150-200. The                  concentration of the bismuth salt in the mixed                  solution is 0.012-0.03 mmol/ml. The concentration                  of the precipitate in the suspension is 12.5-25                  g/l. The concentration of the sodium hydroxide in                  the suspension is 3-7 mol/l. The adding amount                  ratio of water to polyethylene glycol is                  40:3.5-5. The concentration of the                  manganese-doped bismuth ferrite nano-wire in the                  mixed solution is 0.5-2 mg/ml. Preferred Method:                  The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at                  150-300 degrees C for 24-72 hours. The mechanical                  force is specifically generated by ultrasonically                  processing the dispersion liquid containing the                  manganese-doped bismuth ferrite nano-wire. The                  ultrasonic frequency is 20-60 kHz, the ultrasonic                  power is 12-120 W, the ultrasonic time is 60-120                  minutes.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Method for horizontal well supercritical carbon            dioxide fracturing to develop enhanced geothermal,            involves raising coiled tubing to move jet nozzle to            next position to be reformed, pump isolation fluid to            protect fractured well section.
AU DONG Z
   XUE H
   SUN F
   ZHANG F
   LIU R
   CHEN Y
   CHEN S
   FANG C
   ZENG B
   CAO Q
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202078642M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves lowering the obtained                mixture through coiled tubing to the position to be                perforated in the horizontal section of the third                opening of the horizontal well. The pure liquid                carbon dioxide is pumped to the perforation                position through coiled tubing. The resulting                high-pressure supercritical carbon dioxide further                expands the natural fractures and forms artificial                fractures. The artificial fractures communicates                with the natural fractures to form a complex                network of fractures. The proppant and                supercritical carbon dioxide are stirred in the                sand mixer on the ground. The coiled tubing is                raised to move the hydraulic jet nozzle to the next                position to be reformed, pump isolation fluid to                protect the fractured well section.
   USE - Method for horizontal well supercritical                carbon dioxide fracturing to develop enhanced                geothermal.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide is used as a                circulating working fluid for dry hot rock heat                exchange production to achieve repeated heat                generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for horizontal well supercritical carbon dioxide                fracturing to develop enhanced geothermal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                system for horizontal well supercritical CO2                fracturing to develop enhanced geothermal.

PT P
PN CN111234385-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide-based polypropylene foam material comprises polypropylene resin, polydimethylsiloxane, mesoporous silicon dioxide, dispersant, surfactant, and antioxidant.
AU DING Y
   DING X
   LIN Q
   LV F
   YANG W
AE FUJIAN ANDAFU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202051874Y
AB    NOVELTY - A supercritical carbon dioxide-based polypropylene foam material comprises 70-85 pts. wt. polypropylene resin, 10-20 pts. wt. polydimethylsiloxane, 1-12 pts. wt. mesoporous silicon dioxide, 1.5-6.5 pts. wt. dispersant, 0.1-2 pts. wt. surfactant, and 0.1-2 pts. wt. antioxidant.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide-based polypropylene foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide-based polypropylene foam material has excellent tensile strength, cushioning property and mechanical property, high foaming rate, large cell density, small cell size and uniform cell size, and is lightweight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the supercritical carbon dioxide-based polypropylene foam material, which involves: (1) weighing the above-mentioned components; (2) sequentially adding the weighed polypropylene resin, dispersant and surfactant to a high-pressure reaction kettle, stirring and evenly dispersing, gradually adding polydimethylsiloxane, mesoporous silicon dioxide and antioxidant, and uniformly stirring to get a polypropylene base material; and (3) continuously introducing low-pressure carbon dioxide into the high-pressure reaction kettle to completely replace the air in the kettle, then passing high-pressure carbon dioxide to make the pressure in the kettle to reach 5.5-8.5 MPa, heating the high-pressure reaction kettle at 110-120 degrees C, simultaneously turning on a stirring pulp, maintaining the pressure at 15.5-18.5 MPa for 1-3 hours, depressurizing, cooling, then releasing the pressure to atmospheric pressure and cooling to 10-20 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mesoporous silicon dioxide has a pore diameter of 5-15 nm, a specific surface area of 500-800 m2/g and a pore volume of 0.5-1.5 m3/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, sodium dodecyl sulfonate, sodium dodecyl sulfate, cetylpyridinium chloride and/or cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. The dispersant is methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol and/or pentanol aqueous solutions. The mass fraction of alcohol in its aqueous solution is 50-60%. The antioxidant is octadecyl 3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The supercritical carbon dioxide-based polypropylene foam material preferably comprises 75 pts. wt. polypropylene resin, 10 pts. wt. polydimethylsiloxane, 5 pts. wt. mesoporous silicon dioxide, 3 pts. wt. dispersant, 0.8 pts. wt. surfactant and 1 pt. wt. antioxidant. Preferred Components: The polypropylene resin is blended modified polypropylene, and crosslinked or grafted modified polypropylene.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.)

AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of cyclic carbonate by chemical                fixation of carbon dioxide under normal temperature                and pressure conditions of eutectic ionic liquid                comprises using eutectic ionic liquid as catalyst,                adding epoxy compound to carbon dioxide ring to                obtain cyclic carbonate, using aminophenol in                eutectic ionic liquid as hydrogen bond donor                component and taking quaternary phosphonium bromide                (1) as hydrogen bond acceptor component. The                aminophenol is o-aminophenol (2-AP), m-aminophenol                (3-AP) or p-aminophenol (4-AP).
   USE - The method is synthesis of cyclic carbonate by                chemical fixation of carbon dioxide under normal                temperature and pressure conditions of eutectic                ionic liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonate has excellent in catalytic                performance. The method has high-selective                synthesis of cyclic carbonate under normal                temperature and normal pressure conditions, avoids                toxic transition metals, volatile organic solvents                and cocatalysts and easily separates catalyst and                product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of cyclic carbonate by chemical                fixation of carbon dioxide under normal temperature                and pressure conditions of eutectic ionic liquid                comprises using eutectic ionic liquid as catalyst,                adding epoxy compound to carbon dioxide ring to                obtain cyclic carbonate, using aminophenol in                eutectic ionic liquid as hydrogen bond donor                component and taking quaternary phosphonium bromide                of formula (1) as hydrogen bond acceptor component.                The aminophenol is o-aminophenol (2-AP),                m-aminophenol (3-AP) or p-aminophenol (4-AP).
   R1, R2, R3 and R4=phenyl, benzyl or                alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound                  comprises propylene oxide, 1,2-epoxybutane,                  epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin or                  1,2-epoxyhexane.

PT P
PN KR2018047989-A; KR1984561-B1
TI Absorber for carbon dioxide depositing, comprises enhancer and magnesium oxide/aluminum oxide composition including eutectic mixture, and prepared by mixing and pulverizing two or more alkali nitrate salts to prepare eutectic mixture.
AU SEO J G
   VISHWANATH H
   HWANG S H
AE UNIV MYONGJI IND & ACAD COOP FOUND (UYMY-C)
GA 201839086R
AB    NOVELTY - Absorber comprises enhancer and magnesium oxide/aluminum oxide composition including the eutectic mixture.
   USE - Absorber used for carbon dioxide depositing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing absorber, which involves mixing and pulverizing two or more alkali nitrate salts to prepare a eutectic mixture; heating the eutectic mixture to a temperature above the eutectic temperature to homogenizing eutectic mixture; cooling and homogenizing the eutectic mixture to obtain a eutectic mixture powder; mixing the eutectic mixture powder with a magnesium oxide/aluminum oxide composite; and calcining the magnesium oxide / aluminum oxide composite mixed with the eutectic mixture powder to obtain final product; and
   (2) a method for depositing carbon dioxide, which involves inducing the melting of the eutectic mixture by heating the adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide comprising a promoter comprising a eutectic mixture and a magnesium oxide/aluminum oxide complex to a temperature above the eutectic temperature of the eutectic mixture, and introducing a carbon dioxide gas into the adsorbent in which the eutectic mixture is molten; and
   (3) a method of regenerating an adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide, which involves using promoter containing a eutectic mixture and a magnesium oxide / aluminum oxide composite, subjecting adsorbent to carbon dioxide adsorption, heating at 400-550 degrees C for 5-20 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The eutectic mixture is a mixture of two or more kinds of alkali nitrate salts. The eutectic mixture is a eutectic mixture of potassium nitrate (KNO3) and lithium nitrate (LiNO3). The alkali nitrate salt is selected from sodium nitrate (NaNO3), lithium nitrate (LiNO3) and potassium nitrate (KNO3). The eutectic mixture is a mixture of potassium nitrate and lithium nitrate in a molar ratio of 1:1-1:4. Preferred Composition: The amount of eutectic mixture is 20-45 wt.% based on the weight of the total adsorbent. The eutectic mixture is contained in an amount of 20-45 wt.% based on the weight of the total adsorbent. The homogenization is carried out by heating at a temperature higher at 80-150 degrees C than the eutectic temperature of the eutectic mixture for 8-16 hours. The magnesium precursor is selected from magnesium hydroxide, magnesium nitrate and magnesium acetate. The aluminum precursor is selected from aluminum tetrabutoxide, aluminum isopropoxide and aluminum nitrate. The acid is selected from nitric acid, acetic acid, hydrochloric acid, and sulfuric acid. The solvent is selected from ethanol, methanol, and isopropyl alcohol. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of potassium nitrate and lithium nitrate is 1:1-1:4. The molar ratio of magnesium/aluminum is 4.0-5.0. The magnesium oxide/aluminum oxide composite is prepared by adding and stirring a metal precursor including an magnesium precursor and an aluminum precursor and an acid to a solvent, evaporating the solvent to obtain a solid, calcining solid to obtain a magnesium oxide/aluminum oxide composite. The calcination is carried out under nitrogen gas at 300-500 degrees C for 4-8 hours. The magnesium precursor and the aluminum precursor are added so that the molar ratio of magnesium / aluminum is 4.0-5.0. The adsorbent is heated at 250-300 degrees C. The eutectic mixture is a eutectic mixture of potassium nitrate and lithium nitrate. The molar ratio of potassium nitrate and lithium nitrate is 1:1-1:4. The heating is carried out in an atmosphere of nitrogen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN115078623-A
TI Identifying components of burning smoke from tree            branches and leaves, involves collecting camphor tree,            masson pine, birch, ginkgo four typical arbor fresh and            dry branches and leaves, measuring emissions of carbon            dioxide, carbon monoxide during combustion of fresh and            dry branches and leaves.
AU JIANG N
   DAI X
   DONG M
   XIA W
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 2022C3892K
AB    NOVELTY - Identifying components of burning smoke from                tree branches and leaves involves collecting                camphor tree, masson pine, birch, ginkgo four                typical arbor fresh and dry branches and leaves,                filling the dried and wet condition in the sealed                freshness protection bag with specific to different                tree species. 30 g of different samples with an                analytical balance is weighed and put them in a                sealed bag. The fresh branches and leaves of each                tree species are made into 12 samples, and the                dried branches and leaves are made into 12 samples.                Particle sampler is used to sample, and performed                particle analysis. Baseline is calibrated for 15                minutes, and deionized water is injected to remove                impurities and activate the suppressor. The                emissions of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, CxHy,                nitric oxide, NxOyand sulfur dioxide during the                combustion of fresh and dry branches and leaves of                three kinds of trees under different moisture                contents are measured.
   USE - Method for identifying components of burning                smoke from tree branches and leaves.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables deepening the understanding                of forest fire flue gas harm, and can more                pertinently control the flue gases generated by                forest fire, preventing the air pollution caused by                the direct discharge of the smoke, and preventing                the smoke generated by the forest fire from being                aerosol particulate matter smoke.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Identifying components of burning smoke from                tree branches and leaves involves collecting                camphor tree, masson pine, birch, ginkgo four                typical arbor fresh and dry branches and leaves,                filling the dried and wet condition in the sealed                freshness protection bag with specific to different                tree species. 30 g of different samples with an                analytical balance is weighed and put them in a                sealed bag. The fresh branches and leaves of each                tree species are made into 12 samples, and the                dried branches and leaves are made into 12 samples.                Each sample is pulverized with a pulverizer and                passed through a 60-mesh sieve, and each 25 g                portion is weighed with an analytical balance and                put into a sealed bag. The constant weight total                suspended particulate matter sampling membrane is                put into the sampling filter membrane clip of the                particulate matter sampler and fixed in the                sampler. Particle sampler is used to sample, and                performed particle analysis. Baseline is calibrated                for 15 minutes, and deionized water is injected to                remove impurities and activate the suppressor.                Determination of water-soluble inorganic ions are                provided data for source apportionment of forest                fire smoke, and also provided data for                distinguishing the types of inorganic salts it                produces. The emissions of carbon dioxide, carbon                monoxide, CxHy, nitric oxide, NxOyand sulfur                dioxide during the combustion of fresh and dry                branches and leaves of three kinds of trees under                different moisture contents are measured.

PT P
PN CN111233658-A; CN111233658-B
TI Extracting shikimic acid and quinic acid, by extracting Ginkgo biloba leaves, adding polyamide, separating ginkgolide and flavonoids, filtering polysaccharide, separating by bipolar membrane electrodialysis, and adding activated carbon.
AU WANG X
   CHENG S
   XIAO H
   ZHANG Y
AE SHAANXI JIAHE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
   SHAANXI JIAHE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202051891G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting shikimic acid and quinic acid, involves (a) extracting the Ginkgo biloba leaves by carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method to obtain extract and extraction residue, and purifying chlorophyll, (b) extracting the residue with alkaline water, deactivating enzyme, adding ethanol, filtering to obtain supernatant, adding polyamide, filtering to obtain a filtrate (C) and a precipitate, and separating the precipitate by a mixed column to obtain ginkgolide and flavonoids, (c) filtering by an ultra-filtration membrane to obtain the filtrate (C) to obtain concentrated solution (E) and clear solution (F), adding ethanol to the solution (E), and precipitating to obtain polysaccharide, (d) separating by a bipolar membrane electrodialysis device to obtain the liquid (F), to obtain an organic acid solution, and concentrating to obtain crude crystals, and (e) dissolving the crude crystals in a mixed solvent, washing with methanol, and adding activated carbon.
   USE - Method for extracting shikimic acid and quinic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method overcomes the problems of long time consumption, large amount of wastewater treatment, environmental pollution, and complex operations in the existing methods. The method strives to maximize the development of various active ingredients in G.biloba leaves, except shikimic acid and quinic acid, under the premise of commercial value. The method extracts chlorophyll, flavonoids, lactone and polysaccharides, and finally separates shikimic acid and quinic acid products with the content of greater than 98%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting shikimic acid and quinic acid from Ginkgo leaves, involves (a) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by using organic solvent as an entraining agent, extracting the fresh raw materials of Ginkgo biloba leaves by carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method to obtain extract (A) and extraction residue (B), and purifying chlorophyll in the extract (A), (b) extracting the residue (B) with alkaline water, deactivating the enzyme, adding ethanol solution for extraction, concentrating the extract, adjusting to neutral and filtering to obtain supernatant, adding polyamide to the supernatant, stirring and filtering to obtain a filtrate (C) and a precipitate (D), and separating the precipitate (D) by a mixed column to obtain ginkgolide and Ginkgo flavonoids, (c) performing membrane filtration by using the ultra-filtration membrane to filter the filtrate (C) to obtain concentrated solution (E) and clear solution (F), adding ethanol to the solution (E), precipitating, filtering, precipitating and drying to obtain Ginkgo polysaccharide, (d) performing electrodialysis by a bipolar membrane electrodialysis device to separate the liquid (F), to obtain an organic acid solution in the acid chamber, concentrating it, and placing it to obtain crude crystals, and (e) recrystallizing by dissolving the crude crystals in a mixed solvent and filtering to obtain the precipitate and filtrate, washing the precipitate with methanol and drying to obtain white crystals, which are quinic acid and the content is greater than 98%, decolorizing the filtrate by adding activated carbon, concentrating the decolorizing solution to obtain white crystals, which are shikimic acid, and the content is greater than 98%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The process parameters of the carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method in step (a) are extraction kettle pressure of 28-32 MPa, extraction temperature of 50-55 degrees C, extraction time of 1-2 hours, No.1 separation kettle pressure of 8 MPa, extraction temperature of 40-45 degrees C, and No.2 separation kettle pressure of 6 MPa, and the extraction temperature of 35-40 degrees C. In step (c), the amount of ethanol used is 5-8 times the volume of the solution (E), and the ultra-filtration membrane is 5000-8000 D. The operating voltage in step (d) is 15-25 V, and the specific gravity after concentration is 1.25. Preferred Components: The organic solvent in step (a) is a weakly alkaline acetone solution having a pH of 8-9. Preferred Composition: The mixed solvent in step (e) is ethanol and ethyl acetate, where the volume ratio of ethanol to ethyl acetate is (8:2)-(9:1), and the amount of the mixed solvent is 2.5-3.5 times the amount of crude crystals. The methanol in step (e) is 95% methanol, where the specific gravity of the decolorized solution after concentration is 1.28. The mass ratio of supernatant and polyamide in step (b) is 3:1. In step (b), the alkaline water is 5 times the amount of sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution, where the stewing temperature is 100 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - Semi-conducting biogenic hybrid catalyst                comprises electroactive microorganism selected from                the group consisting of Enterobacter aerogenes                preserved in microbial type culture collection                (MTCC: 25016), Serratia MTCC 25017, Shewanella sp.    USE - Semi-conducting biogenic hybrid                catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for bio-assisting conversion of                carbon dioxide to fuel precursors employing the                semiconducting biogenic hybridcatalyst, which                involves adding the semi-conducting biogenic hybrid                catalyst to culture medium in a transparent,                sparging carbon dioxide through the culture medium                and irradiating the transparent reactor with a                light source having wavelength not greater than                400nm, and recovering the fuel precursors from the                culture medium, where the fuel precursors are                selected from the group consisting of methanol,                ethanol, acetic acid, butanol, isopropanol, butyric                acid, and caproic acid; and

PT P
PN CN109939152-A
TI Preparing granules used for e.g. relieving cold, by supercritical extracting using e.g. honeysuckle flower with ethanol, microwave extracting using e.g. radix platycodi with ethanol, decocting remaining herbs with water and microfiltering.
AU ZHANG Z
   FU Z
   ZHOU W
   CHEN Y
   TANG X
   LI Z
AE GUANGDONG HENGCHENG PHARM CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201958511M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing granules, involves (a) using radix bupleuri, honeysuckle flower, radix puerariae, herba artemisiae apiaceae, fructus forsythiae, radix scutellariae, radix platycodi, bitter almonds and menthol as raw materials, (b) extracting honeysuckle flower, radix puerariae and radix scutellariae with ethanol in a carbon dioxide supercritical extractor to obtain supercritical extract, (c) crushing herba artemisiae apiaceae, fructus forsythiae and radix platycodi, microwave extracting using ethanol, combining the extract, concentrating, eluting, collecting eluent, recovering ethanol under reduced pressure, concentrating, and drying to obtain microwave extract, (d) decocting the remaining herbs with water, combining the decoction, concentrating, and filtering, and (e) mixing the supercritical extract, microwave extract and filtrate, adding sucrose and dextrin to prepare granules, drying, spraying dissolved menthol and ethanol into the granules, and mixing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing granules used for relieving cold and cough and preparing medicine for inhibiting proliferation of human lymphoma cell U-937 cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the content of active ingredients in the granules.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing granules, involves (a) using 100 g radix bupleuri, 75 g honeysuckle flower, 100 g radix puerariae, 75 g herba artemisiae apiaceae, 75 g fructus forsythiae, 75 g radix scutellariae, 50 g radix platycodi, 50 g bitter almonds and 0.15 g menthol as raw materials, (b) adding honeysuckle flower, radix puerariae and radix scutellariae to carbon dioxide supercritical extractor, adding 3-5 vol.% ethanol as entrainer, carrying out isothermal forward pressure extraction, initially extracting under a pressure of 10-20 MPa for 45-75 minutes, extracting under a pressure of 20-30 MPa for 45-75 minutes, and finally extracting at 40-50 degrees C under a pressure of 40-50 MPa for 30-60 minutes at a carbon dioxide flow rate of 1-3 m1/g crude drug-minute to obtain supercritical extract, (c) taking herba artemisiae apiaceae, fructus forsythiae and radix platycodi, crushing, adding 2 l 70% ethanol, microwave extracting in a microwave extraction device under a power of 500 W, extracting for 2 times, each time for 10-15 minutes, combining the extract, concentrating, adding to D101 macroporous adsorption resin column, eluting with 50% ethanol, collecting 8 times column volume eluent, recovering ethanol under reduced pressure, concentrating, and drying to obtain microwave extract, (d) adding 6-8 times water to the remaining herbs, decocting twice, each time for 2 hours, combining the decoction, concentrating under vacuum, reducing to a relative density of 1.05-1.10 at 50 degrees C, and filtering using a 0.22-1 mu m microfiltration membrane to obtain a filtrate, and (e) mixing the supercritical extract, microwave extract and filtrate, adding sucrose and dextrin to prepare granules, drying, spraying dissolved menthol and ethanol into the granules, and mixing to make up to 1000 g to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves adding 4 vol.% entrainer, initially extracting under the pressure of 15 MPa for 60 minutes, extracting under the pressure of 25 MPa for 60 minutes, and finally extracting at 45 degrees C under the pressure of 45 MPa for 45 minutes at the carbon dioxide flow rate of 2 m1/g crude drug-minute to the obtain supercritical extract. The step (c) involves microwave extracting under the power of 500 W, extracting each time for 10 minutes. The step (d) involves (d) adding 2 times water to the remaining herbs, decocting twice, and reducing to the relative density of 1.10 at 50 degrees C, and filtering using the 0.45 mu m microfiltration membrane to obtain the filtrate.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide; Antitussive; Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Human lymphoma cell U-937 cells proliferation inhibitor.
