PT P
PN CN109735405-A
TI Preparing craft citrus maxima beer comprises            selecting fresh and mature citrus maxima, processing            epidermal layer and citrus maxima epithecial layer,            adding petrifying water, filtering, sprinkling,            packaging and inflating carbon dioxide.
AU WEN L
   JIANG B
   ZHANG W
   LIN S
   LIU K
   ZHONG L
   WEN Z
AE MEIZHOU JUMBO GOLDEN POMELO IND CO LTD (MEIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201944847K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing craft citrus maxima beer comprises                (i) selecting fresh and mature citrus maxima,                cleaning, and drying; (ii) peeling the citrus                maxima; (iii) processing epidermal layer and citrus                maxima epithecial layer, adding petrifying water                for petrifying process and centrifuging to obtain                filler; (iv) crushing the malt, adding purified                water, adding filler, and fermenting for; (v)                crushing the malt, boiling in boiling pot, adding                hops and adding pure juice; (vi) filtering,                cooling, placing into the fermentation tank to                performing separately liquid fermentation and                adding yeast to ferment; (vii) shifting the bottom                sprinkling liquid into the liquid fermentation tank                and continuously fermenting; (viii) sprinkling the                liquid fermenting material in the liquid                fermentation tank once a day; (ix) ending the                liquid fermentation; and (x) filling the beer into                a clean packaging container and simultaneously                inflating carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing craft                citrus maxima beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures the beer clarity. The beer                has good taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing craft citrus maxima beer comprises                (i) selecting fresh and mature citrus maxima,                cleaning, and drying; (ii) peeling the citrus                maxima to obtain pulp, processing by thresher to                obtain the citrus maxima pulp granules, squeezing                the citrus maxima pulp granules without water to                obtain the pure juice, where the citrus maxima peel                is divided as the epidermal layer and the citrus                maxima epithecial layer; (iii) processing the                epidermal layer and the citrus maxima epithecial                layer into 0.3-0.5 centimeter epidermal and the                epithecial dices, adding 3-8 times petrifying water                for petrifying process for 20-30 minutes and                centrifuging to obtain filler; (iv) crushing the                malt, adding purified water, heating and boiling by                steam for 20-30 minutes, adding filler, uniformly                stirring, cooling to 25-30?Oc, inoculating 3-10%                yeast and fermenting for 24-36 hours to obtain the                mixed fermentation product; (v) crushing the malt,                boiling in boiling pot for 1-2 hours, adding                0.1-0.5% hops and adding pure juice before 10                minutes of ending the saccharification; (vi)                shifting the saccharifying malt into sedimentation                tank for filtering, cooling, placing into the                fermentation tank to performing separately liquid                fermentation and adding yeast to ferment, where the                initial yeast number is (12-15)x 106 ml; (vii)                conveying the separate liquid fermented material in                step (vi) to the top of the solid fermentation                tank, sprinkling on the mixed fermentation product                by the distributor, shifting the bottom sprinkling                liquid into the liquid fermentation tank and                continuously fermenting; (viii) sprinkling the                liquid fermenting material in the liquid                fermentation tank once a day until only leaving the                fermentation filler on the solid fermentation tank,                closing the valve between the liquid tank and the                solid tank and continuously performing liquid                fermentation; (ix) ending the liquid fermentation,                filtering according to conventional beer brewing                process, diluting in beer buffer tank and shifting                into the packaging workshop; (x) filling the beer                into a clean packaging container and simultaneously                inflating carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The petrifying water                  comprises 0.5-2% calcium chloride, 0.3-1.5 %                  magnesium chloride, 0.5-1.5% gluconolactone. The                  ratio of epidermis and pomelo bursa is 1:1-1:5.                  In the step (iv), the addition amount of malt is                  20-50%. The moisture content of the solid                  fermentation is 40-50%. In the step (v), the                  addition ratio of the pure juice is 10-20% of the                  saccharified malt liquid. In the step (viii), the                  washing is carried out for 3-5 days and the                  liquid is fermented for 15-20 days. In the step                  (x), the filling process is filled with 1-3%                  carbon dioxide.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN114643074-A
TI Nitrogen-doped composite carbon material useful            for removing carbon dioxide prepared by e.g. adding            copper acetate, nickel acetylacetonate, acrylonitrile,            3-aminophenol and zinc chloride to ethanol, heating            intermediate product A in inert gas atmosphere, rinsing            with nitric acid and post-processing.
AU HUA L
AE YANGZHOU POLYTECHNIC INST (YZPI-C)
GA 202285482K
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-doped composite carbon material is                claimed. The carbon material is obtained by (i)                adding copper acetate, nickel acetylacetonate,                acrylonitrile, 3-aminophenol and zinc chloride to                ethanol, stirring, ultrasonically dispersed, vacuum                drying to obtain solid mixture and grinding to                powder to obtain intermediate product A, (ii)                heating intermediate product A in inert gas                atmosphere and carbonizing, and (iii) rinsing the                product obtained in step (ii) with acid, then                soaking in acid and finally washing with deionized                water until neutral and drying.
   USE - The nitrogen-doped composite carbon material                is useful for removing carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-doped composite carbon material                has photocatalytic conversion effect and can                achieve efficient removal of carbon dioxide through                dual functions unlike traditional materials that                only remove carbon dioxide by adsorption and                improves the upper limit of adsorption rate of                carbon dioxide by nitrogen-doped composite carbon                materials, which provides new idea for removal of                carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), adding copper                  acetate, nickel acetylacetonate, acrylonitrile,                  3-aminophenol and zinc chloride to ethanol,                  stirring at 500-1000 revolution per minutes for                  3-10 minutes, ultrasonically dispersing for 5-30                  minutes, vacuum-drying at 60-95 &#8451; for                  10-30 hours to obtain solid mixture and grinding                  to powder to obtain intermediate product A.                  Preferably, adding copper acetate, nickel                  acetylacetonate, acrylonitrile, 3-aminophenol and                  zinc chloride into ethanol, stirring at 600                  revolution per minutes for 5 minutes,                  ultrasonically dispersing for 15 minutes and                  vacuum drying at 70 &#8451; for 15 hours to                  obtain solid mixture and grinding to powder to                  obtain intermediate A. In step(ii), placing                  intermediate product A in quartz boat, heating to                  200-250 &#8451; in inert gas atmosphere and                  then maintaining constant temperature in air for                  1-3 hours, then heating to 500-800 &#8451; in                  inert gas atmosphere at constant temperature for                  2-6 hours, cooling to room temperature to obtain                  intermediate product B. Then mixing the                  intermediate product B with activator, then                  grinding uniformly, heating to 500-700                  &#8451; and activating for 0.5-3 hours.                  Preferably, placing intermediate product A in                  quartz boat, heating to 230 &#8451; in inert                  gas atmosphere and then maintaining at constant                  temperature in air for 1.5 hours, then heating to                  750 &#8451; in inert gas atmosphere at                  constant temperature for 3 hours and cooling to                  room temperature to obtain intermediate product                  B, mixing intermediate product B with activator,                  then grinding evenly, heating to 650 &#8451;                  and activating for 1 hour. In step (iii), rinsing                  the product obtained in step (ii) with nitric                  acid for many times, then soaking in nitric acid                  for 1-2 hours, and finally washing with deionized                  water until neutral and vacuum drying at 50-75                  &#8451; for 2-8 hours, to obtain final                  product. Further, the method includes for                  preparing nitrogen-doped composite carbon                  material, comprises (i) adding 0.2-2 g copper                  acetate, 0.5-5 g nickel acetylacetonate, 4-12 g                  acrylonitrile, 2-8 g 3-aminophenol and 0.1-0.5 g                  zinc chloride into 30-80 g ethanol, stirring at                  600 revolution per minutes for 5 minutes,                  ultrasonically dispersed for 15 minutes, and                  vacuum-drying at 70 &#8451; for 15 hours to                  obtain solid mixture and grinding to powder to                  obtain intermediate product A, (ii) placing                  intermediate product A in quartz boat, heating to                  230 &#8451; in inert gas atmosphere, and then                  maintaining constant temperature in air for 1.5                  hours, then heating to 750 &#8451; in inert                  gas atmosphere at constant temperature for 3                  hours and cooling to room temperature to obtain                  intermediate product B, mixing intermediate                  product B with KOH at 2:1 according to mass                  ratio, then grinding uniformly, heating to 650                  &#8451; and activating for 1 hour, and (iii)                  rinsing the product obtained in step (ii) with                  nitric acid for 3 times, then soaking in nitric                  acid for 1 hour, and finally washing with                  deionized water until neutral, and vacuum drying                  at 60 &#8451; for 4 hours to obtain final                  product.Preferred Components: The activator is                  potassium hydroxide (KOH).

PN US2018222752-A1; US10611634-B2
TI Production of hydrogen, comprises supplying hydrocarbon or carbonaceous feedstock to torrefaction or pyrolysis process, supplying resultant to first reactor, supplying resultant to second reactor, and supplying resultant to first condenser.
AU MERRITT J K
AE GRANNUS LLC (GRAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018661433
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrogen, comprises: supplying a hydrocarbon or carbonaceous feedstock to a torrefaction or pyrolysis process to produce a synthesis gas, where the synthesis gas comprises carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen; supplying the synthesis gas to a first reactor comprising a catalyst and configured to convert at least a portion of the carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide and produce a modified synthesis gas; supplying the modified synthesis gas to a second reactor comprising a catalyst and configured to convert remaining carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide to produce a carbon dioxide-rich synthesis gas; and supplying the carbon dioxide-rich synthesis gas from the second reactor to a first condenser to remove water and produce a gas stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for production of hydrogen which is useful in various industrial processes for production of ammonia and/or nitrogen based fertilizers or synthetic fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides the high production of recoverable hydrogen and salable power and limited emissions; and reduces and repurposes industrial greenhouse gases produced as a product of power generation into nitrogen containing chemical compounds e.g. fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: supplying the hydrogen and carbon dioxide stream to a pressure swing adsorption process to produce a pure hydrogen stream and pressure swing adsorption tail gas; extracting exothermic heat for the production of power, heating and cooling of the process, or export of steam; supplying the pure hydrogen stream and nitrogen gas to a fourth reactor comprising a catalyst and configured to produce an ammonia product stream; supplying the pure hydrogen stream as a feedstock to a Fischer Tropsch process; supplying the synthesis gas or modified synthesis gas to a soot removal process to remove soot; supplying at least one of the hydrocarbon or carbonaceous feedstock, the synthesis gas, or the modified synthesis gas to a desulfurization process; supplying the gas stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide to an amine separator unit to produce a residual pure carbon dioxide stream and amine treated high hydrogen content stream; supplying the gas stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide from the first condenser to a vacuum pressure swing adsorption process to produce a pure carbon dioxide stream and a high hydrogen content stream; supplying the pure hydrogen stream to a third reactor comprising a catalyst and configured for the production of methane from carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide present in the hydrogen stream, where the third reactor produces a methane product stream and a high purity hydrogen stream; utilizing a hydrogen generator, where the hydrogen generator produces hydrogen and oxygen from a water feedstock and the hydrogen is supplied to the fourth reactor and the oxygen is supplied to an oxygen supplied partial oxidation process; supplying the pure hydrogen stream to a process comprising a process for the production of urea, ammonium nitrate, nitric acids, diesel exhaust fluids, mixed alcohols, production of aldehyde alcohols, ethanol, methanol, methyl tert-butyl ether, acetic acid, olefins, synthetic diesel, gasoline, gasoline additives and dimethyl ether; supplying the gas stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide to a demineralized water wash system to decrease concentration of ammonia and hydrogen cyanide in the gas stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide to below 5 parts per million by volume; and recycling the water removed by the first condenser. The first and second reactors are replaced by 1-3 reactors. At least one gas or tail gas is purged to atmosphere, a fuel cell, or a thermal oxidizer, duct burner or flare. The amine separator unit is a multi-pressure system with at least one carbon dioxide output and at least one amine regeneration process. Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon or carbonaceous feedstock comprises methane, or digestate from biogas production. The pure hydrogen stream has a purity of at least 98 mol.%. The composition of the gas stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide comprises 65-80% of hydrogen, 20-35% of carbon dioxide and 0-8% of carbon monoxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   LEE S
   SUN S
   CHAESOOCHUN
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 202251811M
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of carbonate involves (A) adjusting a pH of a concentrated seawater to obtain a filtrate containing a filtrate and magnesium hydroxide, (B) after adjusting the pH of the filtrate in the step (A), obtaining a carbonate containing a filtrate and calcium carbonate by reacting the metal cations with the carbon dioxide microbubbles in the filtrate in step (A) by introducing carbon dioxide microbubbles, and (C) mixing the filtrate in the step (B) and the filtrate in the step (A), after adjusting the pH value, adding carbon dioxide microbubbles to hydromagnesite and magnesite and pressurizing the carbon dioxide microbubbles. The carbon dioxide microbubbles have an average bubble size of 100 mu m or less. The steps (A)-(C) are reacted under stirring conditions.
   USE - Manufacture of carbonate from concentrated seawater from industry e.g. power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, and environmentally-friendly, enables manufacture of carbonate from concentrated seawater capable of safely treating without harming the environment, and reducing the harm that concentrated seawater discharged from the seawater desalination plant directly affects the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentrated seawater is seawater desalination wastewater, sea salt, or salt production wastewater. The carbonate comprises magnesium hydroxide, calcium carbonate, hydromagnesite, lithium carbonate, strontium carbonate, and magnesite. Preferred Composition: The seawater comprises more than 6 wt.% salt. The carbon dioxide microbubbles comprise 15% carbon dioxide gas discharged from an industrial facility. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide microbubbles are pressurized at a pressure of 5 bar or less. The method further involves producing carbonate by using a semi-batch reactor, and installing the semi-batch reactor which is a stirrer for stirring during the reaction.

GA 2021B5991V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing carbon dioxide from a carbonic acid-containing diamino alkane solution involves passing the carbonic acid-containing diamino alkane solution through a separation membrane module.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon dioxide from a carbonic acid-containing diamino alkane solution and producing diamino alkane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is efficient in an energy-efficient manner at a relatively low temperature through the primary decarboxylation process by combining the primary decarboxylation process using a membrane module prior to the secondary decarboxylation process of removing carbon dioxide through distillation, reduces burden in the secondary decarboxylation process, solves the problems such as poor mass transfer performance due to low gas-liquid contact area generated in the carbon dioxide removal process by simple distillation and consequently large-scale equipment, corrosion of equipment, solvent loss, overflow, foam, drift and entrainment, adopts the separation membrane module does not use an additive, does not require an additional process for separating and improves economic efficiency and efficiency of the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing diamino alkane involving separating diamino alkane from a solution from which carbon dioxide has been removed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbonic acid-containing diamino alkane solution is prepared through fermentation of microorganisms and/or biological conversion. The method involves passing of the solution through the separation membrane module by injecting the solution at a flow rate of 10-60 cm/s. The method involves passing the solution through the separation membrane module at a pressure difference of 1-3 bar. The method involves passing the solution through the separation membrane module by applying a vacuum of 1x 103 to 1x 102 torr to the lower portion of the separation membrane module. The method involves passing the solution through the separation membrane module at 80-110 degrees C for 30 minutes to 10 hours. The method involves increasing pH of the solution to greater than or equal to 10 after passing the solution through the separation membrane module. In the method, the step of passing the solution through the separation membrane module further involves adding water in the middle of performing, where the separation membrane module is provided with two or more separation membranes connected in parallel to each other. The method involves distilling the solution to remove residual carbonic acid and/or removing impurities in the solution after passing the solution through the separation membrane module. Preferred Components: The diaminoalkane is 1,4-diaminobutane, 1,5-diaminopentane or 1,6-diaminohexane. The separator module is provided with a hollow fiber membrane made of polysulfone, polyvinylidene fluoride or polypropylene material.

   ADVANTAGE - The system avoids problem of insufficient refrigeration performance of transcritical carbon dioxide, promotes process of green refrigerant carbon dioxide to enter practical application process and achieves huge contribution to environmental protection and energy saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a trans-critical carbon dioxide electric vehicle thermal management system control method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a trans-critical carbon dioxide electric vehicle thermal management system.
   Compressor (1)
   Gas cooler (2)
   Electronic expansion valves (3,5)
   Accumulator (4)
   Evaporator (6)

PN CN110906494-A
TI Ventilation control method for a new air conditioner fan involves detecting the room Carbon dioxide concentration, the new fan is controlled to maintain the current speed of the first fan, and second fan is operated to compensate for speed.
AU CHEN R
   LIU X
   ZHANG J
   CHEN J
   YANG W
AE QINGDAO HAIER AIR-CONDITIONER CO LTD (HIER-C)
GA 202024815E
AB    NOVELTY - The ventilation control method involves detecting the room Carbon dioxide concentration. After the first preset time of the new fan, then determine whether the carbon dioxide concentration is less than the target concentration. When the carbon dioxide concentration is less than the target concentration, then control the new fan to maintain the current first fan. When the carbon dioxide concentration is greater than the target concentration. The new fan is controlled to maintain the current speed of the first fan. The second fan of the air conditioning body is operated to compensate for the speed. After the new fan runs for a second preset time. It returns to perform indoor carbon dioxide concentration detection.
   USE - Ventilation control method for a new air conditioner fan.
   ADVANTAGE - Ventilation control method for a new air conditioner fan can increase the wind speed of the new fan and improve the efficiency of replacing the new wind.

AU GOLDBACH A

   XU H
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2018474255
AB    NOVELTY - Palladium-ceramic-palladium dual-layer composite film material comprises hollow tubular or flat porous ceramics as carrier, prepared palladium film on the inner and outer surfaces of the hollow tubular carrier, or prepared palladium film on the upper and lower surfaces of the flat porous ceramic carrier, forming palladium film - porous ceramic carrier - palladium film double metal film composite system, where the porous ceramic carrier is used as the skeleton of the membrane module, two sides of the framework are palladium-gold film, palladium film hydrogen provided as a separated body of the active hydrogen atom, the two are matched to form the palladium composite membrane structure.
   USE - The material is useful in separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen, or capture of greenhouse methane and carbon dioxide gas, fossil, preparation of hydrogen in biochemicals, hydrogen separating or purifying hydrogen (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of palladium-ceramic-palladium dual-layer composite film material in separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen, or capture of greenhouse methane and carbon dioxide gas, fossil, preparation of hydrogen in biochemicals, hydrogen separating or purifying hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The matrix carrier material is the porous ceramic gamma -aluminum oxide, porosity is 40-55 wt.%, pore distribution is 70-100 mm, where 70-80 mm of the pore volume ratio is 70-85%, 80-100 mm of the pore volume is 15-30 %, the sides of the carrier is compact metal palladium film. The thickness of the plating layer is 0.1-100 mu m or not, doped in the composite film doped with silver, copper, gold, platinum, nickel, rhodium, ruthenium, iron, or cobalt metal, and the doping quantity is 5-50%, the mechanical property of the film material. The prepared composite membrane is activated for 30-50 hours at 200-400 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere before use. The double-layer film material, under the condition of keeping relatively high hydrogen permeability, but nitrogen it is substantially zero, so the hydrogen/nitrgen selectivity can reach more than 10000. Preferred Method: The preparing method of dense palladium metal film can be obtained by electroplating or chemical plating method, the plating liquid is not doped or can be used in composite film doped with silver, copper, gold, platinum, nickel, rhodium, ruthenium, iron, or cobalt metal, metal in soluble salt or acid, and improving the mechanical property of the film material. In the electroplating process, using water as the solvent, the main components of the palladium plating bath are: diamine dichloro platinum complex, co-salt: ammonium chloride, potassium chloride, complexing agent: mass fraction of 20-40% ammonia, the mass concentration of diamine dichloro platinum complex is 2-10 g/l, the mass concentration of ammonium chloride is 2-10 g/l, and the mass concentration of potassium chloride is 0.1-5 g/l. The amount of the complexing agent is 10-20 ml/l, the pH of the palladium plating bath is 7-10. The chemical plating palladium sensitization process and palladium plating process, the electroless plating process, using water as solvent, the components of the palladium solution mainly, salt palladium sulfate, removing sodium tetraborate decahydrate, reducing agent, borane dimethyl palladium plating liquid ingredient is mainly, salt palladium chloride, removing sodium formate, complexing agent ethylenediamine palladium solution, the mass concentration of palladium sulfate is 0.1-10 g/l, mass concentration of sodium tetraborate decahydrate is 0.1-10 g/l, the mass concentration of dimethyl borane is 0.1-5 g/l, in the palladium plating solution, the mass concentration of palladium chloride is 0.1-10 g/l, the mass concentration of the sodium formate is 5-0.1 g/l, the concentration of ethylenediamine is 0.05-2 g/l, pH of the palladium plating liquid is 4-10. The electroplating process at 20-60 degrees C, normal pressure, stirring rate is 10-60 revolutions/second, chemical plating palladium process condition: 10-60 degrees C, normal pressure and stirring, chemical plating palladium process condition: 20-80 degrees C, normal pressure and stirring rate is 25-80 revolutions/second.

GA 2018A3551S
AB    NOVELTY - Kit comprises NADH analog recycling system and carbon dioxide measuring reagent. The NADH analog recycling system comprises 6-phosphate-fructose dehydrogenase, hexokinase, fructose, and adenosine triphosphate disodium salt. The carbon dioxide determination reagent comprises tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane (Tris)-hydrochloric acid (pH of 8), stabilizer, oxaloacetate, phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, phosphoenolpyruvate potassium, low temperature malate dehydrogenase, and Triton X-100 (RTM: t-octyl phenoxy polyethoxy ethanol). The 6-phosphate-fructose dehydrogenase is derived from Candida glycerol. The hexokinase is derived from Lactobacillus brevis. The phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase is derived from marine low-temperature Corynebacterium glutamicum. The low temperature malate dehydrogenase is derived from Bacillus mucilaginosus and Serratia marcescens.
   USE - The kit is useful for detecting concentration of carbon dioxide in a high salt sample at lower temperatures using cyclic enzymatic method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The NADH analogue recycling system comprises 5-50 U/l, preferably 10 U/l 6-phosphate-fructose dehydrogenase, 50-500 U/l, preferably 100 U/l hexokinase, 1-5g/l, preferably 1 g/l fructose, and 0.5-2.5 g /l, preferably 0.5 g/l adenine nucleoside triphosphate disodium salt. The carbon dioxide determination reagent comprises 0.1 mol/l tris-hydrochloric acid in pH=8, 1 g/l stabilizer, 0.3 g/l oxaloacetate, 0.1 g/l phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, 0.6 g/l phosphoenol-type potassium pyruvate, 0.05g /l low temperature malate dehydrogenase and 1 g/l Triton X-100 (RTM: t-octyl phenoxy polyethoxy ethanol). Preferred Component: The stabilizer is at least one of sorbitol, mannitol, and glycerol. Preferred Method: The specific screening method for low temperature malate dehydrogenase involves collecting samples from deep sea mud from seawater, and screening for strain with strong acidity by preliminary screening of beef extract peptone solid medium containing 0.1-0.2 g/l bromocresol green, detecting motility of the strain by using beef extract protein solid medium containing MTT0.015 g/l, and the second screening is performed to select the enzyme with strong kinetic activity to measure the enzyme activity, and accurately screening low temperature strain of high-yield malate dehydrogenases (MDHs) by double screening process.

TI Preparation of pre-coated powder for electrochemical cell, involves combining core particles containing electrochemical material and/or ionically conductive material, with supercritical carbon dioxide and binder, and removing carbon dioxide.
AU PHARES D
AE DRAGONFLY ENERGY CORP (DRAG-Non-standard)
   DRAGONFLY ENERGY CORP (DRAG-Non-standard)
   DRAGONFLY ENERGY CORP (DRAG-Non-standard)
   DRAGONFLY ENERGY CORP (DRAG-Non-standard)
GA 201865813F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of pre-coated powder involves combining core particles (30) containing an electrochemical material and/or an ionically conductive material, with supercritical carbon dioxide and a binder (32) containing a thermoplastic polymer to form a mixture, dissolving the binder in the supercritical carbon dioxide, and removing the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent from the mixture to form coating of the binder on core particles that partially encapsulate the core particles.
   USE - Preparation of pre-coated powder for forming electrode e.g. anode of electrochemical cell (claimed) e.g. battery, super capacitor and fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The pre-coated powder can be formed stably by the deposition process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the encapsulated core particles.
   Core particles (30)
   Binder material (32)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mixture is agitated. Alternately, the core particles are combined with a monomer, a polymerization initiator, and a solvent containing supercritical carbon dioxide to form a mixture. The monomer and polymerization initiator are reacted to form thermoplastic polymer coatings on the core particles. The mixture is sprayed by aerosolization, and the solvent is evaporated to form coatings of the binder on the core particles. The separate encapsulated particles are not bonded to one another. A charge is applied to the spray of particles, and a substrate is heated. The charged spray of particles is applied to the heated substrate to form a film of the particles on the substrate. The substrate is heated by radiating, convecting and/or conducting heat to the substrate, or passing an electrical current through the substrate. Preferred Composition: The electrochemical material consists of lithium-cobalt oxide, lithium-nickel-manganese-cobalt oxide, lithium-manganese-cobalt oxide, lithium-iron phosphate, lithium-manganese-iron phosphate, lithium-nickel-cobalt-aluminum oxide, lithium titanate and/or graphite. The ionically conductive material comprises an ionically conductive metal oxide and/or an ionically conductive glass. The core material contains aluminum oxide, silica, titanium oxide, magnesium oxide, zinc oxide, zirconium oxide, copper oxide, cadmium oxide and/or lithium oxide. Alternately, the core particles comprise lithium sulfide, phosphorus sulfide (P2S5), complex lithium-phosphorus sulfide (xLi2S-(l-x)P2S5), and glass electrolytes comprising lithium compound (Li3-xHxOCl).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The thermoplastic polymer comprises polyvinylidene fluoride, polytetrafluoroethylene, styrene-butadiene, polyethylene oxide and/or polyethylene oxide with a dissolved lithium salt.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A81 (Adhesives and binders - including chipboard.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded.    USE - Method for gaseous separation.
   ADVANTAGE - Method for gaseous separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gaseous separation involves utilizing gas separation membrane containing a polyimide having a repeating unit of formula (I). The carbon dioxide is separated from a gas containing at least carbon dioxide and methane at a temperature of 35 degrees C or more and 70 degrees C or less
   R1=a divalent organic group containing 2-hydroxy 1, 1, 1, 3, 3,3-hexafluoro isopropyl group; and
   R2=a tetravalent organic group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The divalent organic groups are represented by the group of formulae (III-VI).. The divalent organic group is an organic group represented by formula (VII). The gas containing at least carbon dioxide and methane is natural gas or biogas. The gas separation membrane is a hollow fiber membrane. The gas permeation membrane is a composite film. Preferred Conditions: The polyimide having a repeating unit represented by the formula (I) is a polyimide having a weight average molecular weight of 20000 or more and 500000 or less. The gas separation membrane is a gas separation membrane heat-treated at 100 degrees C or more and 400 degrees C or less. The temperature of 50 degrees C of the gas permeation membrane and the permeability coefficient of the carbon dioxide in the pressure of 150 kpa are 20 or more Barrer.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1=a divalent organic group represented with formula (II);
   R3=single bond, an oxygen atom, a sulfur atom, CO, CH2, CH2 CH2, SO, SO2, C(CH3)2, C(CH3)(CH2CH3), NHCO, C(CF3)-2, an aromatic ring, or an alicyclic ring;
   R4 and R5=a hydrogen atom, an alkyl group, or a fluoroalkyl group each independently; and
   A and b=an integer of 0-2 each independently.

TI Production of aromatic hydrocarbon involves            directly converting mixed gas containing hydrogen and            carbon dioxide using composite catalyst containing            catalyst for hydrogenation of iron-based carbon dioxide            to lower olefin, into product.
AU GE Q
   WEI J
   XU H
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201822519X
AB    NOVELTY - Production of aromatic hydrocarbon involves                directly converting (i) a mixed gas containing                hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a molar ratio of                0.5-8 as a raw material under catalytic action of a                composite catalyst into an aromatic hydrocarbon.                The composite catalyst is obtained by mixing a                catalyst (a) for hydrogenation of iron-based carbon                dioxide to lower olefin, and a catalyst (b) which                is optionally metal modified molecular sieve(s) for                olefin aromatization in a mass ratio of 1:10-10:1,                preferably 1:3-3:1.
   USE - Production of aromatic hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of aromatic                hydrocarbon having a methane content of less than                8% and 5C or more hydrocarbon content of more than                65%, with high selectivity of 80% or more and                carbon dioxide conversion rate of 33% or                more.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst (a)                  comprises a main active component comprising                  iron, iron(II) oxide, iron(III) oxide and/or                  iron(II,III) oxide having excellent water gas                  shift function, carbon monoxide hydrogenation                  function and olefinization function, preferably                  iron(II,III) oxide, and 0-20 %mass, preferably                  0.5-10 %mass auxiliary agent. The auxiliary agent                  is chosen from potassium oxide, sodium oxide,                  copper oxide, manganese oxide, vanadium oxide,                  zirconium oxide, zinc oxide and cerium oxide. The                  molecular sieve is a molecular sieve containing                  10-membered ring pore structure, preferably                  ZSM-5, ZSM-22, ZSM-23 and/or MCM-22 molecular                  sieves, preferably ZSM-5 and/or ZSM-22 molecular                  sieves, in which the molar ratio of silica with                  respect to alumina is 20-200. The catalyst (b)                  comprises 0.1-20 %mass, preferably 0.5-10 %mass                  metal(s) chosen from molybdenum, gallium,                  chromium, lanthanum, copper and zinc. The carbon                  dioxide is a gas containing carbon dioxide,                  industrial waste gas containing carbon dioxide,                  automobile exhaust gas, coal-fired exhaust gas,                  and carbon dioxide absorbed in seawater. The                  molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is                  0.5-8.0. Preferred Process: The catalyst (a) is                  prepared by mixing a soluble iron(II) salt and                  0.02-0.8 mol/l soluble iron(III) salt in                  auxiliary agent to obtain a salt solution, in                  which the molar ratio of soluble iron(III) salt                  and soluble iron(II) salt is 2:0.8-2.8, adjusting                  pH of the solution to 0-3 by adding a                  hydrochloride solution with a concentration of                  3-12.1 mol/l, adding 0.1-8 mol/l alkaline                  solution chosen from sodium hydroxide, potassium                  hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate,                  potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate,                  sodium oxalate, potassium oxalate, carboxylate of                  formula: RCOONa or RCOOK, and aqueous ammonia for                  adjusting pH of the solution from 0-3 to 9-12,                  aging for 1-10 hours, separating the deposited                  product by magnetic adsorption, performing                  centrifugation or suction filtration, washing the                  deposited product with deionized water, drying,                  and baking at 200-600 degrees C for 2-10 hours.                  The soluble iron(II) salt and the soluble                  iron(III) salt are water-soluble salt, preferably                  chloride salt, nitrate salt and/or acetate salt.                  The composite catalyst is prepared by separately                  weighing an iron-based catalyst powder and                  molecular sieve catalyst powder, mixing,                  compression-molding the obtained mixture, and                  forming composite catalyst. The composite                  catalyst optionally comprises 0.01-10 %mass inert                  material barrier layer.R=1-20C alkyl, 1-20C alkenyl, or 6-20C aryl,                  preferably methyl, ethyl, or phenyl.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (i) is                  performed at a reaction temperature of 250-450                  degrees C, a reaction pressure of 0.01-10.0 MPa,                  and a feed gas space velocity of 500-50000                  mL/(hour.gcat).

PT P
PN CN114231518-A
TI Immobilized carbonic anhydrase useful for preparing carbon dioxide absorbent, is prepared by taking carbonic anhydrase, zeolite imidazolate skeleton material, and deionized water, reacting, centrifuging, washing precipitate with deionized water and vacuum drying immobilized carbonic anhydrase.
AU YE J
   SHAO P
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2022491749
AB    NOVELTY - Immobilized carbonic anhydrase is prepared by taking carbonic anhydrase, as an active component, zeolite imidazolate skeleton material, as a carrier and deionized water, as a medium, stirring and reacting at 25-30degrees Celsius for 1-4 hours, then centrifuging, washing the precipitate with deionized water and vacuum drying to obtain the immobilized carbonic anhydrase.
   USE - The immobilized carbonic anhydrase is useful for preparing carbon dioxide absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The immobilized carbonic anhydrase has improved stability and increases rate of carbon dioxide absorption by organic amines by 2.2 times compared with the carbon dioxide absorption rate of pure 1 mol/l organic amine (MDEA).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the immobilized carbonic anhydrase in preparation of carbon dioxide absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The zeolite imidazolate framework material and carbonic anhydrase are in a mass ratio of 1:0.01-0.5. Preferred Method: The zeolite imidazolate framework material is prepared by dissolving organic ligand in 10-150 ml/g solvent, then adding zinc nitrate hexahydrate, reacting at 25-100degrees Celsius for 0.5-18 hours, then centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain the zeolite imidazolate framework material, where the organic ligand is 2-methylimidazole, benzimidazole or imidazole-2-carbaldehyde, the solvent is a mixture of deionized water, ethanol, N,N-dimethylformamide or toluene and 25-28% aqueous ammonia and the organic ligand and zinc nitrate hexahydrate are in a ratio of 1:0.001-1. The zeolite imidazolate framework material is prepared by (a) dissolving 2-methylimidazole in 10.5 ml/g deionized water, then adding a solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate dissolved in deionized water, mixing, stirring at room temperature for 30 minutes, centrifuging and washing 3 times with deionized water, drying at 80degrees Celsius to obtain zeolite imidazolate framework material i.e. ZIF-8, where the 2-methylimidazole and zinc nitrate hexahydrate are in a ratio of 1:0.019, (b) dissolving the benzimidazole in ethanol, then adding toluene and 25-28% aqueous ammonia while stirring at room temperature, then adding zinc nitrate hexahydrate, stirring at room temperature for 3 hours, stirring the mixture at 40degrees Celsius for 2 hours, centrifuging the product, washing 3 times with ethanol and deionized water in turn, and drying to obtain the zeolite imidazolate framework material i.e. ZIF-11, where the benzimidazole and hexahydrate zinc nitrate material are in a ratio of 1:0.5, the total volume consumption of ethanol, toluene and aqueous ammonia is 116.7 ml/g in terms of benzimidazole quality and the ethanol, toluene and aqueous ammonia are in a volume ratio of 1:0.62:0.007 and (c) heating and dissolving imidazole-2-carbaldehyde and zinc nitrate hexahydrate in 48 ml/g N,N-dimethylformamide, incubating at 100degrees Celsius for 18 hours, centrifuging the product, washing 3 times with N,N-dimethylformamide and deionized water and drying to obtain the zeolite imidazolate framework material i.e. ZIF-90, where the imidazole-2-formaldehyde and zinc nitrate hexahydrate are in a ratio of 1:0.7. The immobilized carbonic anhydrase is prepared by uniformly mixing 5-10 mg/ml zeolite imidazolate framework material aqueous solution with 2-5 mg/ml carbonic anhydrase aqueous solution. The absorbent is prepared by adding 0.1-1 mg/ml immobilized carbonic anhydrase to 1 mol/l organic amine aqueous solution, evenly dispersing at 200 revolutions per minute (rpm) and absorbing carbon dioxide at 40degrees Celsius, where the carbon dioxide amount is maintained at 15 kPa in the reaction system, where the organic amine is N-ethyl-1-(3,4-methylenedioxyphenyl)-2-propylamine or N,N-dihydroxyethyl-2-aminopropionic acid.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC D05-A01C2; D05-A01B4; D05-A01A5; J01-E02B
IP B01D-053/14; C12N-011/089; C12N-009/88

PT P
PN CN114028447-A
TI Extracting Entada phaseoloides by supercritical carbon dioxide comprises e.g. drying peeled Entada phaseoloides powder, extracting raw material with supercritical carbon dioxide as solvent and carrying out two-stage decompression separation.
AU LIU L
   HE C
   JIANG P
   JIN J
   XIONG S
AE HUNAN HANHUA JINGDIAN CLEAN ENERGY TECHN (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202233962L
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Entada phaseoloides by supercritical carbon dioxide comprises (i) drying peeled Entada phaseoloides powder at less than or equal to65degrees Celsius for 6-12 hours, (ii) extracting raw material with supercritical carbon dioxide as solvent, under pressure of 20-35 MPa, and temperature of 35-65degrees Celsius, and (iii) carrying out two-stage decompression separation to obtain extract of Entada phaseoloides.
   USE - The method is useful for Extracting Entada phaseoloides by supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low extraction temperature, fast extraction speed and high extraction rate, which retains original properties of substances; has no organic solvent residue, high selectivity, high yield, low toxicity and other effects, and no adverse effects on people; and is environmental friendly; and can prevent problem of oxidative degradation in production process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of Entada phaseoloides powder is 0.1-1.5 mm.Preferred Method: In the step (1), the drying temperature is 50-65degrees Celsius. In the step (ii), specifically comprises adding Entada phaseoloides powder into supercritical extraction tank, using supercritical carbon dioxide as solvent, anhydrous ethanol or 60-95% ethanol as cosolvent, and extracting for 3-6 hours under conditions of pressure of 20-35 MPa and temperature of 35-65degrees Celsius, where The cosolvent is methanol, ethanol or its aqueous solutions. The cosolvent and carbon dioxide mass ratio in the step (ii) is 5:100-35:100. In the step (iii), the one-stage separation pressure of two-stage decompression separation is 8-10 MPa, and the temperature is 35-65degrees Celsius. In the step (ii), the two-stage separation pressure of two-stage decompression separation is 4-6 MPa, and the temperature is 30-50degrees Celsius.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)
MC B04-A08G2; B11-B03C; B04-A10

PN JP2022007403-A
TI Polyethylene polyamine derivative for carbon-dioxide absorption diffusion agent comprises a derivative of polyethylene polyamine having primary amino group in which atoms are substituted with hydroxyalkyl group.
AU SAKODA K
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 202239434N
AB    NOVELTY - A polyethylene polyamine derivative comprises a derivative of (i) a polyethylene polyamine having a nitrogen atom number of greater than or equal to 4, and (ii) a primary amino group (-NH2) of a polyethylene polyamine having a nitrogen atom number of 4 or more which is a precursor, in which both the atoms of 20-100% primary amino group (-NH2) are substituted with hydroxyalkyl group having 1-4 carbon atoms.
   USE - A polyethylene polyamine derivative for carbon-dioxide absorption diffusion agent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyethylene polyamine derivative provides carbon-dioxide absorption diffusion agent excellent in absorption rate and diffusion rate of carbon dioxide. The separation and recovery of the carbon dioxide can be carried out by energy saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for amine composition comprising polyethylene polyamine derivative and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The derivative is obtained by substituting 1-4C hydroxyalkyl group of both or atoms of 40-95% primary amino group of polyethylene polyamine having a nitrogen atom number of greater than or equal to 4 as a precursor. Preferred Components: The polyethylene polyamine having a nitrogen atom number of greater than or equal to 4 or more is a polyethylene polyamine having a nitrogen atom number of 6-9. The 1-4C hydroxyalkyl group is a group selected from 2-hydroxypropyl group, 2-hydroxy-1-methylethyl group and 2-hydroxyethyl group. The content of polyethylene polyamine derivative in amine composition is 5-70 wt.%. Carbon dioxide absorption diffusion agent consists of silica, alumina, magnesia, porous glass, activated carbon, polymethylmethacrylate-based porous resin, or fiber deposited with polyethylene polyamine derivative.

PN CN113105273-A
TI Slow-release of carbon dioxide in solar greenhouse, involves subjecting agricultural and forestry wastes to composting and fermenting at high and medium temperature stages, stop fermenting, mixing initial substrate material with urea and/or swamp fertilizer, and fermenting in solar greenhouse.
AU QU J
   ZHANG L
   ZHU Q
   HUANG L
   LU J
   WANG Z
AE NINGXIA ACAD AGRIC & FORESTRY SCI HORTIC (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202185410A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for slow-release of carbon dioxide in a solar greenhouse, involves subjecting agricultural and forestry wastes to composting and fermenting at a high temperature stage and a medium temperature stage, stop fermenting to obtain an initial substrate raw material, mixing the initial substrate material with urea and/or swamp fertilizer, and performing low-temperature mineralization fermentation in a solar greenhouse, and accompanying by production of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method useful for slow-release of carbon dioxide in solar greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The method slows down the humification process of the substrate, improves the mineralization degree of the substrate, and increases the production of carbon dioxide gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The agricultural and forestry waste comprises Caragana korshinskii twig powder, wolfberry twig powder, and/or grape twig powder. Preferred Conditions: The fermentation in the high temperature stage is carried out at 55-75 degrees C for 15-25 days. The fermentation in the middle temperature stage is carried out at 40-54 degrees C for 20-30 days. The measures for stopping fermentation involve removing the cover of the fermentation stack, and turning the stack as a whole to make the temperature quickly reach the air temperature, where the water content drop below 40%. The time for the low-temperature mineralization fermentation is 35-90 days. Preferred Composition: The water content during the fermentation is 55-65%. The added amount of urea is 0.1-1 kg per m3 of the initial base material. The addition amount of the biogas slurry fertilizer is 3-10 kg per m3 of the initial substrate material. The volume ratio of the low-temperature mineralized fermentation material and the solar greenhouse is 1:(200-600).
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

The membrane composite is arranged between the cathode and the anode, and a gap (4) between the anode and the membrane composite is arranged for carrying an electrolyte.
   USE - The electrolysis cell is useful for electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide (claimed).

PN KR2021045649-A; KR2290528-B1
TI Preparing polysilazane using carbon dioxide as a solvent involves adding ammonium carbamate to the second reactor, and adding silane compound to the first reactor, maintaining the first reactor at room temperature and heating second reactor.
AU PARK I
   HWANG H
   LEE J Y
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 2021433849
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polysilazane using carbon dioxide as a solvent involves adding ammonium carbamate to the second reactor, and adding silane compound to the first reactor, maintaining the first reactor at room temperature and heating the second reactor, injecting carbon dioxide into the first and second reactors to pressurize, pressurizing the pressurized pressure of the first reactor higher than the pressure of the second reactor to transfer the silane compound of the first reactor to the second reactor to induce a supercritical reaction, recovering a product obtained by a supercritical reaction from the second reactor.
   USE - Method for preparing polysilazane using carbon dioxide as a solvent.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The silane compound is dichlorosilane. Preferred Conditions: The heating temperature of the second reactor is 65 degrees C. The ammonium carbamate in the second reactor is decomposed to produce ammonia. The pressure of the first reactor after the injection of carbon dioxide in the second system is 130-150 bar, and the pressure of the second reactor is lower than the pressure of the first reactor. The first reactor and the second reactor are connected by a transfer pipe provided with an on-off valve. The carbon dioxide is injected at a rate of 10-30 mL/minutes. The multiple reaction devices including the first reactor and the second reactor are provided.

TI Method of determining oxygen isotope composition of aqueous sample used for stable isotope geochemistry, involves equilibrating aqueous sample with carbon dioxide gas, and measuring oxygen isotope composition of carbon dioxide equilibrium.
AU KIM S
   RANDAZZO N
   KNYF M
AE UNIV MCMASTER (MCST-C)
   UNIV MCMASTER (MCST-C)
GA 2020360237
AB    NOVELTY - A method of determining oxygen isotope composition of aqueous sample involves equilibrating aqueous sample with carbon dioxide gas. The aqueous sample comprises effective amount of carbonic anhydrase (CA) enzyme, and measuring oxygen isotope composition of carbon dioxide at equilibrium. The oxygen isotope composition of carbon dioxide corresponds to oxygen isotope composition of aqueous sample.
   USE - Method of determining oxygen isotope composition of aqueous sample used for stable isotope geochemistry and earth sciences e.g. geology, biology and hydrology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for improving analytical efficiency and cost effectiveness of oxygen isotope analysis, efficiency of well-established technique and essentially saving entire day of analysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for kit for determining oxygen isotope composition of aqueous sample.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The effective amount of carbonic anhydrase (CA) enzyme is 2-10 mu molal, preferably 4 mu molal. Preferred Component: The oxygen isotope composition of carbon dioxide at equilibrium of aqueous sample comprising effective amount of carbonic anhydrase (CA) enzyme is substantially same as oxygen isotope composition carbon dioxide of at equilibrium of aqueous without CA.

AB    NOVELTY - Use of magnetic ionic liquid-supported catalyst in carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction, is claimed. The magnetic ionic liquid-supporting catalyst is characterized in that 1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate is immobilized in a cavity of a magnetic MOF composite.    USE - The catalyst is useful in carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the magnetic ionic liquid-supporting catalyst, comprising immobilizing 1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate in a cavity of a magnetic MOF composite.

The concentration of the 1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate solution is 1.5-2 mg/ml. Preferred Method: The magnetic ionic liquid-supporting catalyst comprises (i) coating a HKUST-1 film on the surface of iron oxide by immersion stirring method to obtain a magnetic MOF composite, (ii) dispersing the magnetic MOF complex in a solution of 1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, which is combined with 1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, the weight ratio of 0.8-1.2:0.3-0.5 gives a magnetic ionic liquid-supported catalyst. The magnetic MOF complex is dispersed in a solution of 1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate and stirring at 45-55 degrees C for 12-24 hours. The magnetic MOF complex dispersing in a solution of 1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, stirred at 45-55 degrees C for 12-24 hours, washing, and magnetically collected to obtain a magnetic ionic liquid-supported catalyst.

The cooling time is 10 minutes.

The volume concentration of carbon dioxide introduced into the cathode chamber is 10-100%. The concentration of formate produced by bipolar membrane electrodialysis reactor is 1-30%. The reaction pressure of the electrochemical reactor is 0.05-1 MPaG, and the reaction temperature is 10-180 degrees C. The reaction pressure of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis reactor is 0.05-1 MPaG, and the reaction temperature is 10-180 degrees C.

TI Catalyst used for methanation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrogen, comprises alumina, nickel active component and iron, and has preset mass ratio of nickel and iron.
AU KOEHLER K
   THOMYS O
   HINRICHSEN K
   KOSCHANY F
   BURGER T
AE CLARIANT PROD DEUT GMBH (CLRN-C)
   WACKER CHEM AG (WACK-C)
   UNIV TECH MUENCHEN (MUEN-C)
GA 201861432B
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises alumina, a nickel active component and iron. The mass ratio of nickel and iron is 4-25, preferably 5-6.
   USE - Catalyst is used for methanation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrogen (claimed), for electricity generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is economical, and has excellent catalytic activity, methane selectivity, uniformity and stability, and enables methanation of carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide at low temperature with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of catalyst, which involves performing co-precipitation of a solution (s1) comprising aluminum, nickel and iron in dissolved form, separating the precipitate, drying the precipitate and calcining; and
   (2) methanation of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, which involves contacting gas comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide with the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The nickel active component has diameter of less than 20 nm, preferably less than 10 nm. The catalyst has carbon dioxide uptake capacity at 35 degrees C of more than 200 mu mol/g catalyst, preferably 200-300 mu mol/g catalyst. The oxidation state of iron in solution (s1) is II or III. Preferred Composition: Solution (s1) is an aqueous solution. Solution (s1) comprises aluminum, nickel and iron in dissolved form, as ionic compounds including an anion chosen from nitrate, sulfate, halide, chloride and acetate. Preferred Conditions: The calcination is performed at 300-600 degrees C. The methanation reaction is carried out at more than 200 degrees C. Preferred Process: The precipitate in solution (s1) is aged for 30 minutes or more. The separated precipitate is washed before drying.

TI Preparing specific hydrocarbons, involves            introducing feed stream comprising hydrogen gas and            carbon-containing gas chosen from carbon monoxide, and            carbon dioxide into reaction zone of reactor, and            converting feed stream into product stream comprising            specific hydrocarbons in reaction zone.
AU POLLEFEYT G
   DU F
   TOCHA E
   KIRILIN A
   HO C
   YANCEY D F
   NIESKENS D L S
   MALEK A
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 2022B2472G
AB    NOVELTY - Process for preparing 2-4C hydrocarbons,                involves (a) introducing a feed stream comprising                hydrogen gas and a carbon-containing gas chosen                from carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide into a                reaction zone of a reactor, and (b) converting the                feed stream into a product stream comprising 2-4C                hydrocarbons in the reaction zone in the presence                of a formed hybrid catalyst, where the formed                hybrid catalyst comprises a metal oxide catalyst                component comprising gallium oxide and zirconia, a                microporous catalyst component which is a molecular                sieve having 8-membered ring pore openings, and a                binder comprising alumina and/or zirconia.
   USE - The process is used for preparing 2-4C                hydrocarbons for producing hybrid catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is substantially free of silica,                achieves high conversion of synthesis gas feeds                resulting in good conversion of carbon and high                yield of desired products, and allows lower                reaction temperatures to be used for providing high                conversion, selectivity, yield, and low oxygenate                selectivity over time on stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process                for preparing a formed hybrid catalyst, involving                (i) mixing a metal oxide catalyst component and a                microporous catalyst component, where the metal                oxide catalyst component comprises gallium oxide                and zirconia, and the microporous catalyst                component comprises a molecular sieve having                8-member ring pore openings, (ii) adding a binder                to the mixture of the metal oxide catalyst                component and the microporous catalyst component to                form a paste, where the binder is a colloidal                solution, suspension, or gel of a binder precursor                comprising oxides or hydroxides of aluminum, oxides                or hydroxides of zirconium and their mixtures, and                (iii) extruding the paste to obtain the                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The metal oxide                  catalyst component has a particle size of less                  than 150 &#956; m. The formed hybrid catalyst                  has a particle size of 0.5-6 mm, preferably                  1.5-3.0 mm. Preferred Components: The metal oxide                  catalyst component further comprises lanthanum.                  The microporous catalyst component comprises                  silicoaluminophosphate-34 (SAPO-34), preferably                  uncalcined SAPO-34. The binder comprises pure                  alumina and/or pure zirconia. Preferred                  Composition: The metal oxide catalyst component                  comprises 0.1-30 g gallium oxide per 100 g of                  zirconia. The metal oxide catalyst component                  comprises 40-80 wt.% formed hybrid catalyst. The                  process has 2-3C olefin selectivity/paraffin                  selectivity ratio of 2-20. Preferred Process: The                  metal oxide catalyst component is formed by an                  impregnation method. Preferred Condition: The                  temperature within the reaction zone during the                  converting is 350-480&#176; C.
   EXAMPLE - The example section describes the preparation                of hybrid catalyst that does not support the                claims.

PN JP2022085652-A
TI New gas storage/release compound used in material for storing/releasing gases e.g. hydrogen and/or carbon dioxide, comprising trivalent aliphatic hydrocarbon structural unit and divalent aromatic hydrocarbon structural unit bound by amide bond.
AU ARIMURA T
AE TAISEI CORP (TISE-C)
GA 202278089Q
AB    NOVELTY - A gas storage/release compound comprising a trivalent aliphatic hydrocarbon structural unit (I) and a divalent aromatic hydrocarbon structural unit (II), is new. Structural units (I) and (II) are bound by an amide bond.
   USE - The gas storage/release compound is used in material in the form of particles, fibers, film, nonwoven fabric, woven fabric, porous component, or molded component for storing/releasing one or more gases such as hydrogen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, noble gas, and hydrocarbon gas (all claimed), preferably hydrogen used for hydrogen fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas storage/release compound is economical and highly versatile, has excellent storage/release characteristics for various gases, can store a large amount of hydrogen under mild conditions and low energy utilization, and has low density than the hydrogen storage alloy. The gas storage/release compound can safely store hydrogen at room temperature under atmospheric pressure for a long period of time, and can release the stored hydrogen by simple process, can be easily processed into an arbitrary shape, and is lightweight, so that it can be easily transported and stored.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gas storage/release compound comprising a trivalent aliphatic hydrocarbon structural unit of formula (I) and a divalent aromatic hydrocarbon structural unit of formula (II), is new. Structural units (I) and (II) are bound by an amide bond.
   R1 = trivalent aliphatic hydrocarbyl;
   R2-R7 = divalent aliphatic hydrocarbyl;
   p,q,r = 1-500;and
   R8 = divalent aryl.
   (i) The structural units of the same formula are not directly bound. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) production of compound (I); and
   (2) a gas storage/release material, which comprises the gas storage/release compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) is obtained by reacting an amino-group-terminated polyoxyalkylene-added triol compound of formula (III) with an aromatic tricarboxylic acid of formula (IV).X,Y',Z = OH, halo, OR9, or NHR10;andR9,R10 = 1-6C alkyl.Preferred Property: The gas storage/release compound has a network structure. Preferred Process: The storage and/or release of gas is carried out by pressurization, depressurization, temperature increase, temperature decrease, application of electric potential, and/or irradiation of energy waves.
   EXAMPLE - In 200 ml round bottom flask, 2.65 g 1,3,5-benzenetricarbonyltrichloride, 4.4 g amine-terminated trimethylolpropane tris(poly(propylene glycol)) having a number average molecular weight of 440, and 15 ml N,N-dimethylformamide were added, and the mixture was stirred at 25 degrees C for 2 hours using a magnetic stirrer. 5 ml Methanol was added to the reaction solution form a precipitate. The precipitate was filtered through 0.45 mu m pore membrane filter to separate the precipitate and filtrate. The precipitate was air-dried and vacuum-dried to obtain a gas storage/release compound having a weight average molecular weight of 178000.

TI Empty soft drink syrup container sensing system for use in soda pop dispensing arrangement, has signal indicator electrically connected with optic sensor for providing signal indicating when transmission line is devoid of syrup.
AU RIDDLE M
   CANDLER K M
AE RIDDLE M (RIDD-Individual)
   CANDLER K M (CAND-Individual)
GA 202216824P
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a mixing station (42) for mixing water and carbon dioxide with soft drink syrup to make a soda pop mixture. The pump (26) pumps the syrup to the station and beyond to be dispensed. The optic sensor (24) is located in a housing and spliced in a tubing transmission line for sensing when the line is devoid of the syrup. The unit of the line extending between the sensor and the pump is lower relative to a horizontal than an outlet end of the housing. The control unit is electrically connected to the sensor, and a signal indicator provides a voltage from a direct current power supply (30). Multiple of soft drink syrup containers, pumps, and optic sensors in a manifold system so that a variety of soda pops may be dispensed from the soda pop dispenser.
   USE - System for sensing empty soft drink syrup containers in a soda pop dispensing arrangement that is utilized in a restaurant, bar, and convenient store industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The signal indicator electrically connected with the optic sensor provides the signal indicating when the tubing transmission line is devoid of syrup, thus providing operators of a dispensing unit with a notification that a soft drink syrup container has run empty before the transmission line runs completely dry, and hence reducing the time and cost associated with replacing the carbon dioxide supply.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for sensing empty soft drink syrup containers in a soda pop dispensing arrangement.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system for sensing empty soft drink syrup containers in a soda pop dispensing arrangement.

PN CN113732550-A; CN113732550-B
TI Low-carbon micro-alloy steel welding evaluation            method based on filament gas shielded welding, involves            obtaining welded joint, and observing and detecting            crack tendency of welded joint to evaluate process            weld-ability of material.
AU LIU S
AE BAOSHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 2021E5418Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting the test plate                (1) as a low-carbon micro-alloy steel plate. A gas                shielded solid core welding wire is selected with a                diameter of 1.2mm. The pair of test plates is butt                welded at the flat welding position. The                single-sided welding and double-sided forming root                welding is performed on the test plate by using the                pulse short-circuit transition mode. The welding                shielding gas is a mixture of argon and carbon                dioxide. The welding shielding gas is a mixture of                argon and carbon dioxide. The welding heat input                value matches the welding heat input value. The                swing width of the welding torch is within the                range of 1 to 3 mm from the edge of the groove. A                welded joint is obtained, the crack tendency of the                welded joint is observed and detected to evaluate                the process weld-ability of the material, and the                mechanical properties of the welded joint are                tested to evaluate the use weld-ability of the                material after the welding is completed.
   USE - Low-carbon micro-alloy steel welding                evaluation method based on filament gas shielded                welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The process weld-ability and the use                weld-ability evaluation requirement under a given                thermal input condition can be met at the same                time, implementation is convenient, operation is                flexible, requirements on hardware equipment are                low, implementation cost is low, and                reproducibility is good.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating the low-carbon micro-alloy steel                weld-ability evaluation method based on filament                gas shielded welding.
   Test plate (1)

PN EP3913310-A1; EP3913310-A4
TI Method for separating air by cryogenic distillation using column system, involves sending gas enriched in argon from second column to third column, and withdrawing fluid rich argon at top of third column.
AU TRANIER J
   DUBETTIER-GRENIER R
   ROZIERES M
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2021D5020M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a cryogenic distillation using a column system consisting of two columns operating at different pressures. The head of the first column is thermally connected to the bottom of the second column in which a first airflow constituting seventy-five to ninety-eight percent of the air sent to the column system that is compressed to a third pressure above the first pressure. The purified water and carbon dioxide of the first purified flow are sent to the first column and optionally to the second column. The second airflow is used to constitute ten to twenty-five percent of total air. The air is cooled in the first cooling tower (4) to a temperature above thirty degrees Celsius.
   USE - Method for separating air by cryogenic distillation using a column system for producing pure oxygen. Can also be used for separating water and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process separates air by cryogenic distillation using a column system comprising one column operating at a lower pressure than the other. It provides a strong injection of low-pressure air directly into the low-pressure column of the column system. The pressure used is significantly greater than those used according to the prior art. It uses an argon separation column and with the production of pure oxygen surprisingly for those skilled in the art. The process produces oxygen at relatively low purities and does not produce argon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for separating air by cryogenic distillation using a column system that has a first column and a second column.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the method.
   Compressor (2)
   First cooling tower (4)
   Booster (20)
   Turbine (27)
   Exchanger (80)

TI Device for determining carbon dioxide partial            pressure value on blood side of membrane oxygenator,            has control unit for deducing partial pressure values            of gas side and blood side when calculated change rate            equals or falls within range.

AE ABIOMED EURO GMBH (ABIO-C)
   ABIOMED EURO GMBH (ABIO-C)
GA 2021B1627A
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has an oxygenator (4) with a                blood side (2), a gas side (6) and a semipermeable                membrane (8). A first sensor (10) is configured to                measure CO2 partial pressure values of the gas                side. A control unit (12) is configured to process                the measured CO2 partial pressure values of the gas                side and to determine a CO2 partial pressure value                on the blood side based on the measured CO2 partial                pressure values of the gas side. The CO2 partial                pressure value is deducted on the blood side from                the CO2 partial pressure values of the gas side                which are measured when or after the calculated                change rate equals the predetermined change rate                value or falls within the predetermined change rate                range at the predetermined measuring                distance.
   USE - Device for determination of CO2 partial                pressure value on blood side of oxygenator used in                cardiopulmonary bypass (CPB) system or                extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO)                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The device allows a fast, easy and more                reliable or accurate determination of the partial                pressure of carbon dioxide in the patient's                blood.

   (1) an extracorporeal membrane oxygenation                system; and
   (2) a method for determining a CO2 partial                pressure value on a blood side of an oxygenator of                a device.
