PN EP3882506-A1; FR3108385-A1; FR3108385-B1
TI Vaporization equipment for liquid cryogenic fluids            e.g. carbon dioxide, has injection unit that injects            liquid gas into gas produced at outlet of vaporization            exchanger, in order to lower temperature of gas            produced within given range.
AU TAMMAR M
   REYMOND C
   MOEZ T
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE FRANCE IND SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE FRANCE IND SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2021B00232
AB    NOVELTY - The equipment comprises a main heat exchanger                (4) that allows heat exchange between a liquid                cryogenic fluid (1,2) and water or any other                exchange fluid, such as oil or water glycol, in                order to achieve vaporization. An injection unit                (5) injects a liquid gas into the gas produced at                the outlet of the vaporization exchanger, in order                to lower the temperature of the gas produced within                a given range. The exchange fluid is a source of                water vapor, and the water vapor is injected                directly into the main heat exchanger to achieve                vaporization.
   USE - Equipment for vaporizing liquid cryogenic                fluids used in industrial applications e.g.                enrichment of greenhouses with carbon dioxide,                anesthesia of poultry and pigs, carbonation of                drinks, and water treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The equipment regulates the temperature of the                gas obtained at the outlet of the vaporization                equipment to account for the effects of expansion,                of the ambient temperature, with a measurement of                the temperature at the point of use. It regulates                the final temperature of gas obtained when leaving                the equipment. It can be done automatically, e.g.                by a progressive valve on the gas supply gas                liquid, to ensure a good dosage. It is done                manually, for example by a calibrated orifice on                gas inlet. The regulation is all the finer, because                there is a natural self-adaptation, the more flow                there is in the exchanger, the greater the quantity                of liquid that can be injected at the gas                outlet.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                process for vaporizing liquid cryogenic                fluids.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of                the vaporization equipment.
   Liquid cryogenic fluid (1,2)
   Main heat exchanger (4)
   Injection unit (5)

PN WO2021075634-A1; KR2021046212-A; US2022235955-A1
TI Air purifier for reducing concentration of odor in            air by using hybrid filter, comprises first fan for            inhaling air, odor sensor detects concentration of odor            in air, hybrid filter contains activated carbon and            photocatalyst.
AU PARK H
   KIM S
   KIM J
   JEONG Y
   PARK H J
   KIM G
   SAEMI K
   JEONG Y W
AE SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
   SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 202141708X
AB    NOVELTY - The air purifier (100), comprises a first fan                for inhaling air, an odor sensor that detects the                concentration of odor in the air. A hybrid filter                contains activated carbon and a photocatalyst. An                LED (Light Emitting Diode) arranged in a region in                which the photocatalyst included in the hybrid                filter is located to emit light. The LED identifies                whether the concentration of the odor in the air                detected through the odor sensor exceeds a first                threshold value. The first fan is driven to inhale                air in order to reduce the odor concentration in                the air, and the odor concentration in the air                sensed through the odor sensor after the first fan                is driven is a threshold value greater than or                equal to the first threshold. When not reduced to                the threshold value, the processor is used for                controlling the LED to emit light to the                photocatalyst based on the concentration of carbon                dioxide in the air.
   USE - Air purifier for controlling the display and                reducing the concentration of odor in the air by                using a hybrid filter.
   ADVANTAGE - The air purifier can prevent pressure loss                from increasing and the thickness of the filter is                increased, the activated carbon and the                photocatalyst of the hybrid filter may be                implemented in the form of beads. The user may be                provided with a more comfortable indoor                environment, by reducing the concentration of odors                in the air through the air purifier, and                regenerating the filter based on the concentration                of carbon dioxide in the air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for controlling air purifier.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                air purifier.
   Air purifier (100)

PN KR2231451-B1; WO2022080588-A1
TI Greenhouse gas emission reduction device for ship, has main portion that is comprised with ammonia regeneration unit which is configured for regenerating and returning ammonia to absorption tower to be reused as absorption liquid.
AU NAMBYUNGTAK
   NAM B T
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
   DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE (DEWO-C)
GA 202130872U
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an exhaust gas cooling unit (110) that is configured to cool exhaust gas discharged from a ship engine. An absorbent liquid manufacturing unit (120) is configured to manufacture and supply an absorbent liquid. The carbon dioxide is reacted with the exhaust gas cooled by the exhaust gas cooling unit and the absorbent liquid from the absorbent liquid manufacturing unit is converted into an aqueous ammonium salt solution to remove carbon dioxide. An absorbent liquid concentration control unit (140) is configured for adjusting a concentration of absorbent liquid supplied from absorbent liquid preparation unit to absorption tower and reacting the aqueous ammonium salt solution discharged from the absorption tower with an aqueous divalent metal hydroxide solution to obtain ammonia. An ammonia regeneration unit is configured for regenerating and returning ammonia to the absorption tower to be reused as an absorption liquid.
   USE - Greenhouse gas emission reduction device for ship (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The size of the capacity of the filter is reduced, and the ammonia regeneration cost is reduced. The concentration of absorbent liquid is prevented from decreasing by cooling the exhaust gas with fresh water of in-ship cooling system by heat exchange method, and is possible to reduce concentration of absorbent solution, to prevent the reduction of greenhouse gas absorption performance by maintaining constant concentration of absorbent solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the apparatus for reducing greenhouse gas emissions of ship. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Exhaust gas cooling unit (110)
   Absorbent liquid manufacturing unit (120)
   Absorption unit (131)
   Absorbent liquid concentration control unit (140)
   Steam generating unit (160)

PT P
PN CN112341351-A; CN112341351-B
TI Preparation of aminobenzamide derivative involves passing 1-bromo-2,3-difluorobenzene with butyllithium to form 4-bromo-2,3-difluorobenzoic acid, nitrating, reacting with acid chloride, aminating, reducing and performing Sandmeyer reaction.
AU ZHANG G
AE WUXI SHUANGQI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202119291B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aminobenzamide derivative (I) involves (1) passing 1-bromo-2,3-difluorobenzene (II) under action of butyllithium through carbon dioxide under condition of removing hydrogen to obtain 4-bromo-2,3-difluorobenzoic acid (III), (2) nitrating compound (III) with concentrated sulfuric acid and nitric acid to obtain 4-bromo-2,3-difluoro-5-nitrobenzoic acid (IV), (3) reacting compound (IV) with acid chloride to obtain 4-bromo-2,3-difluoro-5-nitrobenzoyl chloride (V), (4) reacting compound (V) with ammonia to obtain amide compound (VI), (5) reducing compound (VI) by iron powder to obtain 5-amino-4-bromo-2,3-difluorobenzamide (VII), (6) subjecting compound (VII) to Sandmeyer reaction to obtain 4-bromo-2,3-difluoro-5-iodobenzamide (VIII) and (7) reacting compound (VIII) with ammonia gas at high pressure.
   USE - Preparation of aminobenzamide derivative.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method produces product with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of aminobenzamide derivative of formula (I) involves (1) passing 1-bromo-2,3-difluorobenzene of formula (II) under action of butyllithium through carbon dioxide under condition of removing hydrogen to obtain 4-bromo-2,3-difluorobenzoic acid of formula (III), (2) nitrating compound (III) with concentrated sulfuric acid and nitric acid to obtain 4-bromo-2,3-difluoro-5-nitrobenzoic acid of formula (IV), (3) reacting compound (IV) with acid chloride to obtain 4-bromo-2,3-difluoro-5-nitrobenzoyl chloride of formula (V), (4) reacting compound (V) with ammonia to obtain amide compound of formula (VI), (5) reducing compound (VI) by iron powder to obtain 5-amino-4-bromo-2,3-difluorobenzamide of formula (VII), (6) subjecting compound (VII) to Sandmeyer reaction to obtain 4-bromo-2,3-difluoro-5-iodobenzamide of formula (VIII) and (7) reacting compound (VIII) with ammonia gas at high pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The ammonia is ammonia gas or aqueous ammonia.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (6) involves subjecting compound (VII) to diazotization reaction with sodium nitrite and replacing formed diazonium salt by halogen under the catalysis of cuprous iodide or potassium iodide as iodination reagent to carry out Sandmeyer reaction, using water or N,N-dimethylformamide as solvent. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of compound (II) and butyllithium is 1:1.0-1.25. The molar ratio of compound (III) and nitric acid is 1:1.0-1.5. The weight-volume ratio of the compound (III) to concentrated sulfuric acid is 1 g:5 ml. The nitrating mixture comprises 98 %mass concentrated sulfuric acid and 68 %mass nitric acid. The molar ratio of compound (VII) and acid chloride is 1:1.0-5.0. The molar ratio of compound (V) and 28% concentrated aqueous ammonia is 1:1.0-5.0. The molar ratio of compound (VI) and iron powder is 1:1.0-5.0. The molar ratio of the compound (VII), sodium nitrite and potassium iodide is 1:1.0-1.5:4.0-6.0. Preferred Components: The solvent used in step (2) is concentrated sulfuric acid. The solvent used in step (3) is one or more of toluene, dichloromethane and chloroform. The acid chloride is thionyl chloride or oxalyl chloride. The solvent used in step (5) is mixed solvent of acetic acid, ethanol and water. The solvent used in step (7) is one or more of methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, N,N-dimethylformamide and dimethyl sulfoxide. Preferred Condition: The reaction in step (1) is carried out at -78 degrees C for 1-2 hours, step (2) is carried out at 0-30 degrees C for 2-6 hours, step (3) is carried out at 60-110 degrees C for 3-10 hours, step (4) is carried out at -10 degrees C to 20 degrees C in presence of 28% concentrated aqueous ammonia for 1-2 hours and step (5) is carried out at 80-100 degrees C for 6-10 hours. The diazotization reaction is carried out at -10 degrees C to 0 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours. The Sandmeyer reaction is carried out at 60-80 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours. The step (7) is carried out at 30-120 degrees C under pressure of 0.1-2 MPa for 1-10 hours.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)
MC B10-D03; N01-A01; N02-D; N07-D09

AB    NOVELTY - Emergency breathing apparatus with air cleaning function comprises a main body including an air inlet (1), an air outlet (2), and an upstream side communicating with the air inlet and a downstream side communicating with the air outlet to allow air to flow therein. At least one air cleaning filter (4) is connected to the main passage in the main body and purifying the flowing air passing through the main passage. A first respiratory passage (5) is connected to one side of the main passage. A second respiratory passage (6) is connected to the other side of the main passage. An exhalation air bag is connected to the first respiratory tract. At least one facial oxygen mask is connected to an inhalation air bag and the exhalation air bag and accommodating a respirator formed when the user inhales and exhales. PN US2021024438-A1
TI Production of hydrocarbon compound e.g. olefin, involves performing oxidative coupling of methane and separating effluent stream to obtain stream containing compound having preset number of carbon atoms with preset carbon efficiency.
AU RADAELLI G
   RAFIQUE H A
   VUDDAGIRI S
   SCHER E C
   MCCORMICK J
   CIZERON J
   PATEL B
   LAKHAPATRI S
AE LUMMUS TECHNOLOGY LLC (CBRI-C)
GA 2021115265
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrocarbon compound including two or more carbon atoms (2C or more compound) involves performing oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) reaction in a reactor to produce an effluent stream comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, 2C or more compound(s) including ethane and ethylene, and methane, directing the effluent stream to a heat-recovery steam generator (HRSG) system, transferring heat from the effluent stream to a water stream with the HRSG system to produce a steam, separating the effluent stream into a stream (S1) comprising at least some of 2C or more compound(s) and a stream (S2) comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and methane with a carbon efficiency of at least about 50%.
   USE - Production of hydrocarbon compound e.g. olefin, preferably ethylene, propylene (or propene), and butylene (or butene).
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically produces hydrocarbon compound with improved carbon efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for OCM system (100), which has an OCM subsystem (101) having the OCM reactor which takes as input a feed stream comprising methane and a feed stream comprising an oxidizing agent, and generates a product stream comprising 2C or more compounds and non-2C or more impurities, heat recovery steam generator system fluidly and/or thermally coupled to the OCM subsystem, and a separation subsystem fluidly coupled to the OCM subsystem and heat recovery steam generator system which is configured to separate the product stream into streams (S1) and (S2).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block flow diagram of the oxidative coupling of methane system.
   Oxidative coupling of methane system (100)
   Oxidative coupling of methane subsystem (101)
   Oxygen stream (103)
   Gas compressor (107)
   Methanation unit (111)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The OCM subsystem has at least one post-bed cracking unit within the OCM reactor or downstream of the OCM reactor. The post-bed cracking unit is configured to convert at least one portion of alkanes in the product stream to alkenes. The OCM system further has an air separation unit housing a gas turbine combined cycle unit fluidly coupled to the separation subsystem and to the OCM subsystem, and configured to accept at least one portion of stream (S2) to burn as fuel for driving a compressor which provides compressed air to the air separation unit, and a heat exchanger in fluid communication with the OCM subsystem and methanation subsystem, and configured to transfer heat from the product stream to an effluent stream comprising methane from the methanation subsystem, and a sulfur removal system in fluid communication with the OCM subsystem, and configured to remove sulfur-containing compounds from the feed stream comprising methane. The air separation unit is configured to separate the compressed air into an oxygen stream (103) and a nitrogen stream and to direct the oxygen stream to the OCM subsystem. The OCM system consumes less than 150 MMBtu of energy per ton of ethylene or 3C or more compounds enriched. The separation subsystem has an acetylene conversion unit configured to convert acetylene in stream (S1) to ethane and/or ethylene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Production of hydrocarbon compound further involves directing the OCM effluent stream to a cracking unit which is a portion of the OCM reactor before directing to the heat recovery steam generator system, directing a stream comprising ethane to the cracking unit, directing at least one portion of stream (S2) to a methanation unit (111) to react hydrogen with carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide to form a methane stream, directing at least one portion of the methane stream to the OCM reactor, and directing at least one portion of the methane stream into a natural gas pipeline, directing a portion (P1) of stream (S2) and an air stream to a gas compressor (107), burning portion (P1) of stream (S2) to compress the air stream to produce a compressed air stream, separating the compressed air stream in an air separation unit into a stream (S3) comprising oxygen and a stream (S4) comprising nitrogen, and directing stream (S3) to the OCM reactor. The stream comprising ethane is external to the OCM reactor. Preferred Condition: The inlet temperature of the OCM reactor is at most 600 degrees C. Preferred Components: The methanation unit comprises a methanation catalyst which converts carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide into methane at a selectivity for formation of methane which is at least 10-fold greater than a selectivity of the methanation catalyst for formation of coke from carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide.

PN CN112191665-A; CN213559101-U
TI System for preparing biological natural gas, has            discharge port that is connected with solid-liquid            separator after fermentation of fermentation system,            where biogas residue and biogas liquid are sent to            solid and liquid organic fertilizers.
AU XIN Q
   LUO X
   LI W
   GAO L
   WANG M
   CHAI B
   YANG J
   ZHANG H
   DONG Y
   RAN L
   HAN X
   KOU X
   XIONG D
   NIU T
AE POWERCHINA GROUP NORTHWEST ENG CO LTD (CNPW-C)
GA 202106852Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a material pretreatment system                that is provided with a food waste pretreatment                system and a mixing system mixed with livestock                manur. A biogas purification system (9) includes a                steam-water separation system, biogas                desulfurization system e.g. two-stage                desulfurization system and carbon dioxide removal                system. A booster device is provided between the                steam-water separation system and biogas                desulfurization system. The biogas desulfurization                system and the carbon dioxide removal system are                equipped with a voltage stabilizing device. A                digestion liquid discharge port is connected with a                solid-liquid separator (10) after a fermentation of                anaerobic fermentation system. Biogas residue and                biogas liquid are sent to a preparation place of                solid organic fertilizer and liquid organic                fertilizer after solid-liquid separation. A                biological sulfur sludge desulfurized by the biogas                desulfurization system is transported as an                additive to solid organic fertilizer preparation                place.
   USE - System for preparing biological natural gas by                co-processing kitchen waste and livestock and                poultry manure.
   ADVANTAGE - The system for preparing biological natural                gas can co-process kitchen waste and livestock and                poultry manure to produce bio-natural gas. The                anaerobic fermentation of biogas slurry and biogas                residue is effectively used to produce organic                fertilizer. PN CN112175675-A
TI Operation method of pressurized coal-fired heating system, involves stopping feeding of compressed air, when oxygen carrier in first reactor is completely oxidized and closing compressed air mass flow controller and back pressure valve.
AU GUO Q
   GUO X
   PAN X
   HU X
   LI Y
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 202106382C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding the oxygen carrier in the oxidation state to the first reactor and the second reactor respectively. The inert carrier gas is sent into the coal bunker. The coal gasification product and the oxygen carrier undergo a reduction reaction to release reaction heat to continuously supply heat for the coal gasification process. The high-pressure flue gas that has fallen to room temperature is recovered by the gas-liquid separator tank to collect high-pressure and high-concentration carbon dioxide. Oxygen in the air is configured to undergo an oxidation reaction with reduced oxygen carrier. The feeding of compressed air is stopped, when oxygen carrier in the first reactor is completely oxidized, compressed air mass flow controller and back pressure valve are closed, and gasification agent mass flow controller and back pressure valve are opened. The first reactor and the second reactor alternately operate and continuously supply heat according to above steps.
   USE - Operation method of pressurized coal-fired heating system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heat load of the heat supply system and the operation efficiency of the pressurizing device are improved. The system is convenient for storing and downstream processing for directly collecting carbon dioxide with high pressure and high concentration. The oxygen-carrying body oxygen decoupling endothermic reaction is converted into oxygen-carrying body reducing exothermic reaction without external heat energy supply, thus realizing energy-saving and consumption-reducing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a pressurized coal-fired heating system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the reduction reaction process. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN111718282-A; CN111718282-B
TI Preparation of isocyanate with low content of            chlorinated impurities involves reacting amine stream            with hydrogen chloride and/or carbon dioxide, passing            phosgene to react to obtain photochemical reaction            liquid, refining and separating.
AU LI T
   WANG J
   SUN S
   CUI X
   HAO C
   ZHANG Y
   HE W
   GUO Y
   FANG T
   SHANG Y
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM NINGBO CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 202098973U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of isocyanate with low content of                chlorinated impurities involves (1) reacting amine                stream with hydrogen chloride and/or carbon dioxide                to obtain amine hydrochloride and/or amine                carbonate slurry, (2) passing phosgene into the                amine hydrochloride and/or amine carbonate slurry                to react to obtain a photochemical reaction liquid,                (3) removing the phosgene of the photochemical                reaction liquid, refining and separating to obtain                isocyanate products. In the amine hydrochloride                and/or amine carbonate slurry obtained in step (1),                the proportion of the particle size of the amine                hydrochloride and/or amine carbonate particles                within the range of the average particle size plus                minus 30% accounts for more than 70% of the total                particle size distribution, preferably 80% or more.                Before starting the phosgenation reaction of step                (2), the average residence time of amine                hydrochloride and/or amine carbonate particles                without stirring is less than 60 minutes,                preferably less than 10 minutes.
   USE - Preparation of isocyanate with low content of                chlorinated impurities (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for low                chlorinated impurity content isocyanate prepared by                the method. The isocyanate is an aliphatic or                aromatic isocyanate of formula: R(NCO)n, preferably                chosen from one or more of phenyl isocyanate,                cyclohexyl isocyanate, 1,4-butane diisocyanate,                1,5-naphthalene diisocyanate, 1,3-dimethyl                isocyanate cyclohexane, 1,6-hexamethylene                diisocyanate, 1, 4-cyclohexane diisocyanate,                isophorone diisocyanate, 4,4'-dicyclohexylmethane                diisocyanate, p-phenylene diisocyanate, m-xylylene                diisocyanate, 2,4-toluene diisocyanate, 2,6-toluene                diisocyanate,                1,8-diisocyanate-4-(isocyanatomethyl)octane and                nonane triisocyanate.
   R = 4-15C aliphatic or aromatic                hydrocarbyl;and
   n = 1-10.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The amine in step (1)                  is added as a separate stream, or form an amine                  solution or suspension with an inert organic                  solvent. When step (1) uses an amine with a                  melting point not higher than 60 degrees C, the                  salt formation reaction temperature is 20-80                  degrees C, preferably 0-50 degrees C. When using                  amines with a melting point higher than 60                  degrees C, the salt formation reaction                  temperature is 40-150 degrees C, preferably                  60-130 degrees C. In step (1), the reaction                  pressure is 0.03-0.50 MPa (absolute pressure),                  preferably 0.07-0.3 MPa (absolute pressure). In                  step (1), the concentration of the reaction                  solution is controlled at 5%-40% according to the                  mass of amine. The amount of hydrogen chloride                  and/or carbon dioxide stream is 1.05-5 times the                  theoretical amount, preferably 1.5-3 times. In                  step (2), the reaction is carried out at a                  temperature of 90-180 degrees C, preferably                  100-150 degrees C and pressure is 0.05-0.80 MPa                  (absolute pressure), preferably 0.08-0.50 MPa                  (absolute pressure) for 1-8 hours, preferably 2-4                  hours. Preferred Components: The inert solvent is                  used in step (1) and step (2) chosen from one or                  more of chlorinated aromatic hydrocarbon, dialkyl                  terephthalate, diethyl phthalate, toluene and                  xylene, preferably one or more of chlorobenzene,                  dichlorobenzene, toluene and xylene. The                  chlorinated impurity in the isocyanate is a                  structure formed by replacing one or more                  isocyanate groups in the isocyanate of formula:                  R(NCO)n structure with chlorine atoms. The amine                  in step (1) is of formula: R(NH2)n, preferably                  one or more of aniline, cyclohexylamine,                  1,4-butanediamine, 1,5-naphthalenediamine,                  1,3-cyclohexanedimethylamine, 1,6-hexanediamine,                  1,4-diaminocyclohexane,                  1-amino-3,3,5-trimethyl-5-aminomethylcyclohexane,                  4,4'-diaminodicyclohexylmethanediamine,                  p-phenylenediamine, m-xylylenediamine,                  2,4-toluenediamine, 2,6-toluenediamine,                  1,8-diamino-4-(aminomethyl)octane and                  triaminononane. Preferred Composition: The                  phosgene is added in an amount of 1.1-6 times,                  preferably 1.5-4 times the theoretical                  dosage.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)

PT P
PN US2020225149-A1; WO2020150304-A1; US10732101-B1
TI Gas analyzer for determining non-destructively concentration of gas in pressurized transparent or semitransparent container, comprises sensor unit including surface and sensor region between the first and second surfaces.
AU SHI Q
   TSENG A S
AE ENOS ANALYTICAL LLC (ENOS-Non-standard)
   ENOS ANALYTICAL LLC (ENOS-Non-standard)
GA 2020671842
AB    NOVELTY - The gas analyzer comprises a sensor unit (40) including a first surface, a second surface, opposite the first surface, and a sensor region between the first and second surfaces. The sensor region including a first end having a stop surface. The second end including an aperture (50) in communication with the sensor region to receive a neck portion (52) of a container in the sensor region until a cap (54) contacts the stop surface. The sensor unit includes sidewall (58) extending from the first surface toward the second surface. An infrared emitter is affixed to the sidewall to transmit infrared radiation across the sensor region. An infrared detector is affixed to the sidewall. The sidewall is opposite the infrared emitter to receive infrared radiation, which is transmitted by the infrared emitter. The sensor region provide an output signal to the received infrared radiation.
   USE - Gas analyzer is used for determining non-destructively concentration of a gas in a pressurized transparent or semitransparent container having a neck portion extending from a shoulder and terminating in an opening sealed with a cap used as containers for beverages in the plastic material to forms a barrier against permeation of the gas, such as a carbon dioxide, which provides the carbonation.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas analyzer reduces the carbon dioxide permeation rate helps to maintain a high level of carbonation in the carbonated beverages, and decrease over time in carbonation level in a sealed container.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method to non-destructively detect the concentration of a gas in a pressurized transparent or semi-transparent container for transmitting an infrared emitter of a pressurized container.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the gas analyzer.
   Sensor unit (40)
   Aperture (50)
   Neck portion (52)
   Cap (54)
   Sidewall (58)

TI Architectural composition structure for ecological city, has air filter module communicated with air supply module to filter air, and intelligent management system for including lighting control system and home energy management system.
AU SHI J
   LIU R
AE GEOMETRY SMART CITY TECHNOLOGY GUANGZHOU CO LTD (GEOM-Non-standard)
GA 202064834N
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a green energy-saving and thermal insulation indoor decoration for including a high-strength thermal insulation integrated decorative panels. An intelligent fresh air control system includes a heat exchanger, an air conditioning module, a humidification module, a heating module, an air supply module, an exhaust module, a control module, an air filter module, a temperature detector, a humidity detector and Carbon dioxide detection installed indoors Device. A temperature detector, humidity detector and Carbon dioxide detector are connected to a signal input end of the control module. The heat exchanger, the air conditioning module, the humidification module, the heating module, the air supply module and the exhaust module are connected to a signal output end of the control module. The air filter module is communicated with the air supply module to filter the air. An intelligent management system includes a lighting control system and a home energy management system.
   USE - Architectural composition structure for ecological city.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure can effectively reduce the harmful gas in the environment of the residential area by setting up an eco-environmental protection of the exterior wall, and adopts a visible light catalyst to prevent the mildew of the exterior wall and intelligent fresh air control system and green energy-saving insulation interior decoration to make the room effects of fireproof, mildew, moisture and heat preservation. The structure has environmentally friendly and energy-saving, and adopts the prefabricated building main structure, which can save labor and shorten the construction period, utilizes intelligent management system to make the building energy-saving and comfortable, and satisfies the requirements of an ecological city.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an architectural composition structure for ecological city.

TI Preparation of lysosomal adenosine triphosphate recognition carbon dot for recognizing adenosine triphosphate in vitro acidic environment, involves mixing citric acid, triethylenetetramine and rhodamine into anhydrous ethanol.
AU LI C
   GENG X
   SUN Y
   YANG R
   QU L
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 2020087850
AB    NOVELTY - A lysosomal adenosine triphosphate recognition carbon dot is prepared by mixing citric acid, triethylenetetramine and rhodamine into anhydrous ethanol, obtaining precursor mixture, placing in microwave tube, microwave reacting at 100-135 degrees C for 0.1-1 hour, cooling, taking reaction product, placing in 1 KDa dialysis membrane, dialyzing in ethanol solution for 48 hours, obtaining purified carbon dot solution, rotary evaporating to obtain carbon dot oily substance, dissolving in distilled water, and freeze drying.
   USE - Method for preparing lysosomal adenosine triphosphate recognition carbon dot for recognizing adenosine triphosphate in vitro acidic environment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product monitors real time changes of adenosine triphosphate concentration in lysosomes induced by drugs including chloroquine, etoposide and/or oligomycin, has great potential in the detection, drug screening and treatment of adenosine triphosphate in lysosomes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the:(A)application of lysosomal adenosine triphosphate recognition carbon dot comprising diluting carbon dot ethanol solution in Tris-hydrochloric buffer to final concentration of 50 mu g/mL, adding different concentrations of adenosine triphosphate solution, reacting to obtain series of mixed solutions, putting into fluorescent cuvette, and using fluorescence spectrophotometer to measure fluorescence intensity; and#(B)lysosomal adenosine triphosphate recognition method comprising taking cells, culturing at 37 degrees C in 5 wt.% carbon dioxide atmosphere and saturated humidity for 12-24 hours to obtain culture solution A, adding lysosomal adenosine triphosphate recognition carbon dot, culturing at 37 degrees C in 5 wt.% carbon dioxide atmosphere and saturated humidity for 0.5-12 hours, adding Lyso Tracker (RTM: not defined) Deep Red, incubating for 10 minutes, washing cells with phosphate buffer solution, and imaging using laser confocal microscope imaging system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rhodamine is rhodamine 6G, rhodamine B, rhodamine 123, rhodamine 101 and/or rhodamine 110. The molar ratio of citric acid, triethylenetetramine and rhodamine is (100-2):(100-2):1. The cell is HeLa cells, HepG-2 cells, RAW264.7 cells, HL-7702 cells and/or 3T3 cells. The concentration after carbon dots are added is 10-100 mu g/mL. Preferred Method: The application of lysosomal adenosine triphosphate recognition carbon dot comprises diluting carbon dot ethanol solution in 20 mM Tris-hydrochloric buffer with pH value of 4-6.5 to final concentration of 50 mu g/mL, adding 0-5 mM different concentrations of adenosine triphosphate solution, reacting for 10-60 minutes to obtain series of mixed solutions, putting into fluorescent cuvette, and using fluorescence spectrophotometer to measure fluorescence intensity at excitation wavelength of 420-530 nm.

PT P
PN CN110314701-A; CN110314701-B
TI Preparing surface cadmium rich cadmium selenium quantum dot photocatalyst useful in reducing carbon dioxide comprises dissolving cadmium salt in water, adding aqueous organic carboxylic acid ligand, reacting, concentrating and centrifuging.
AU WANG F
   XIA W
   WU J
   HU J
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019877167
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing surface cadmium-rich cadmium selenium quantum dot photocatalyst comprises (1) dissolving cadmium salt in water, adding an aqueous organic carboxylic acid ligand containing a mercapto group, adjusting the pH to 9-11, the sulfhydryl group coordinates with the cadmium ion to form a complex of the sulfhydryl containing aqueous organic carboxylic acid and cadmium ion, adding selenosulfate, heating under a protective atmosphere at 110-140 degrees C for 2-4 hours to obtain a surface modified ligand cadmium selenium quantum dot solution, where the ratio of cadmium ions to selenosulfate substance is 2-10:1 and (2) concentrating the cadmium selenium quantum dot solution, adding acid and adjusting the pH to 2-5, reacting the cadmium selenium quantum dot reacts with hydrogen ions to remove a part of the ligand from the surface of the cadmium selenium quantum dot, centrifuging to remove the supernatant.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful in reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst: has simple synthesis method; and has excellent photocatalytic reduction under visible light conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a surface cadmium-rich cadmium selenium quantum dot photocatalyst prepared by the above mentioned method; and
   (2) photocatalytic reducing of carbondioxide comprising (a) adding the surface cadmium-rich cadmium selenium quantum dot photocatalyst and an electron sacrificial body to a solvent, placing the solution in a light-transmissive reaction vessel to obtain a photocatalytic reduction carbondioxide system and (b) sealing the reaction vessel, irradiating the photocatalytic reduction CO2 system with excitation light at 380-650 nm, catalytically reducing the carbondioxide to carbon monoxide by the surface cadmium-rich cadmium selenium quantum dot photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cadmium salt in the step (1) is cadmium chloride, cadmium sulfate and/or cadmium nitrate and the selenosulfate is sodium selenosulfate or potassium selenosulfate. The ligand in the step (1) is mercaptopropionic acid or thioglycolic acid. The acid solution in the step (2) is 10-12 mol/l hydrochloric acid or 12-15 mol/l sulfuric acid. The ratio of the amount of the cadmium atom and the selenium atom on the surface of the cadmium selenium quantum dot is 1.5-8:1. The diameter of the cadmium selenium quantum dots is 1-5 nm. The preparation of surface cadmium rich cadmium selenium quantum dot photocatalyst comprises at least a part of the ligand on the surface of the cadmium selenium quantum dot is detached so that the surface is rich in Cd2+. The ratio of the solvent to the electron sacrificial body is 4:0.1-1.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

GA 201987779Q
AB    NOVELTY - Treating alcohol generated in overall industry, involves directing the vinasse solution into large storage tanks with 3 wt.% average solids content, transferring into a reaction tank called carbonator, adding carbonator with 7-10 wt.% calcium hydroxide and adjusting 10-12 pH, reacting the salt of potassium with carbon dioxide, passing the carbon dioxid into the reactor, forming a double calcium and potassium carbonate, performing Calcium and potassium appreciation simultaneously after absorption of hydroxyls, precipitating in carbonate, obtaining calcium cxarbonate and potassium carbonate, sedimenting, passing the obtained suspension into sedimentation tanks of a tapered shape, where liquid and solid separation occurs, and transferring the sedimented portion for continuous centrifugation, separating liquid phase through the centrifuge, and recovering the centrifuged solid.
   USE - Method used for treating alcohol generated in overall industry (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating alcohol generated in overall industry, involves directing the vinasse solution into large storage tanks with 3 wt.% average solids content, transferring into a reaction tank called carbonator, adding carbonator with 7-10 wt.% calcium hydroxide and adjusting 10-12 pH, reacting the salt of potassium with carbon dioxide, passing the carbon dioxid into the reactor, forming a double calcium and potassium carbonate, performing Calcium and potassium appreciation simultaneously after absorption of hydroxyls, precipitating in carbonate, obtaining calcium cxarbonate and potassium carbonate, sedimenting, passing the obtained suspension into sedimentation tanks of a tapered shape, where liquid and solid separation occurs, and transferring the sedimented portion for continuous centrifugation, separating liquid phase through the centrifuge, and recovering the centrifuged solid, routing the material into a rotary dryer and heating by steam-generated steam at 125-175 degrees C temperature, entering the material to be dried with 30 wt.% solides and 70 wt.% water, and obtaining the materials 70 wt.% solids and 30 wt.% humidity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Conditions: Treating alcohol generated in overall industry, involves using centrifuge as a decanter type. The rotary drier is a drum type dryer. Preferred Components: The product is a dry powder used as agricultural fertilizer containing 20-10 wt.% moisture content, 10-15 wt.% calcium content, 12-18 wt.% potassium content and 5-10 wt.% phosphorus content.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 201977121T

   (A) obtaining a deodorant by mixing its components, then pulverizing and stirring at room temperature using a pulverizing stirrer to get mixture and transferring to a carbon dioxide extraction device, and subjecting to freeze-dehydration at -10 degrees C, followed by carbon dioxide extraction, to get deodorant;
   (B) obtaining filler by mixing its components at high temperature and heating in a high temperature furnace at a temperature of 500-600 degrees C to be melted and then cooling to room temperature to obtain filler; and
   (C) mixing 50-60 pts. wt. polymethyl methacrylate, 10-15 pts. wt. tourmaline, 5-10 pts. wt. medical stone, 1-5 pts. wt. deodorant, 20-30 pts. wt. filler, and stirring in a blender, melt-mixing in a screw extruder to obtain a polymer, and then injecting obtained polymer into the cavity of the mold, and after cooling to get desired product.

TI Red Scaevola beer with bright red color, sour            taste, and saltiness similar to sea water, has certain            alcohol content, pH and carbon dioxide content.
AU SHI H
AE PANJIN HALOUXIANZAI BEER IND CO LTD (PANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019601509
AB    NOVELTY - Red Scaevola beer with bright red color, sour                taste, and saltiness similar to sea water, has                alcohol content of 2.7-6.5 vol.%, pH of 3.3-3.5,                and carbon dioxide content of 0.25-0.40%.
   USE - Red Scaevola beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The red Scaevola beer uses locally grown red                Scaevola that has certain healthcare functions and                enriches beer colors, and capable of meeting needs                of consumers at all levels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                brewing red Scaevola beer by: (a) performing wort                acidification involving using existing beer                production process to mash wort; directly boiling                after filtration without hops; adding lactic acid                bacteria when temperature is cooled at 40 degrees                C; keeping temperature and fermenting for 24-48                hours until pH value drops to 3.3-3.5; stop                fermenting and continue boiling; adding hops; and                saccharifying according to normal production                process; (b) preparing sour beer-based wine by                brewing beer clear wine using existing beer                production process, which is connected to yeast                fermentation with original wort concentration of                8-12 degrees degrees; performing primary                fermentation using Saccharomyces cerevisiae for 3                days; and adding brett wild yeast for fermentation                to obtain sour beer-based wine; (c) preparing red                Scaevola raspberry juice by washing red Scaevola;                crushing; filtering to obtain raw juice; thawing                frozen raspberry fruit at 12 degrees C; crushing to                obtain puree; adding pectinase; clarifying at low                temperature; filtering; and mixing raw juice and                filtrate to obtain red Scaevola raspberry juice;                and (d) subjecting red Scaevola raspberry juice to                secondary fermentation by adding 10-35% red                Scaevola raspberry juice to sour beer-based wine in                tank; adding 600-1200 mg/l sea salt; sealing tank                for 3-5 days; and quickly cooling at - 1 to 0                degrees C under constant pressure storage for 10-30                days to obtain finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The sour beer-based wine                  is prepared using raw wort concentration of 10-12                  degrees . The low temperature clarification is                  performed at 8-10 degrees C for 15-24 hours, and                  filtration is performed through sterile membrane.                  The sea sat is added at 1000 mg/l. The method                  preferably involves adding 5-25% red Scaevola                  raspberry juice to 5-10 vol.% sour beer-based                  wine.

TI Method for testing performance of downhole            equipment in environment, involves entering            experimental waste liquid into waste liquid recovery            tank and connecting pipes to clean water and flushing            pump valve components.
AU WEI N
   LI X
   HU Y
   LIU S
AE INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
GA 201933366S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves installing a rubber                cylinder stress detecting device at the position of                the packer rubber barrel. The low pressure drive                air system (4) is opened. The experimental process                monitoring system is started. The packer central                tube pressure pneumatic control valve (1-7) is                closed. The central pressure of the packer is kept                constant. The brine mixture is injected into the                lower annulus of container, and saline mixed liquid                pressure pump (1-10) is controlled. The pressure of                central tube of packer is kept constant. The packer                central tube pressure relief pneumatic control                valve is opened to remove the central tube                pressure. During pressure relief process, signal                acquisition system is used to test the stress data                of characteristic points on the surface of the                rubber cylinder. The experimental waste liquid is                entered into the waste liquid recovery tank (1-28).                The packer is taken out, all the pipes are                connected to clean water, and all the pump valve                components are flushed.
   USE - Method for testing performance of downhole                equipment in environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate and simple to                operate, and is used for testing the long-term                reliability of downhole equipment in a special                geological environment for carbon dioxide (CO2)                geological storage.

AB    NOVELTY - Rubber softener comprises 4-7 pts. wt. asphalt, 2-5 pts. wt. methyl anthranilate, 8-12 pts. wt. n-butanol, 10-15 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate, 6-10 pts. wt. dihydromyrcenol, 18-25 pts. wt. active substrates and 20-45 pts. wt. complex softening components.
   USE - Used as rubber softener.
   ADVANTAGE - The rubber softener has improved mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing softener comprising (i) mixing (N-amidino)dodecyl acrylamide and polyethylene glycol phosphate in a mass ratio of 3-6:1 at 30-45 degrees C, adding polyethylene glycol phosphate with 20-40% p-dichlorobenzene and polyethylene glycol phosphate with 4-8% ferrocene, stirring at 55-60 degrees C, discharging to obtain base material, taking 30-50 pts. wt. base material, 20-35 pts. wt. oil phase, 3-7 pts. wt. tert-butyl hydroquinone, 10-15 pts. wt. pretreated plant material and 20-30 pts. wt. epoxy resin, mixing and stirring, heat preservation to obtain mixture, taking mixture, carbon dioxide material and auxiliary agent in a mass ratio of 5-8:3:0.1, mixing, cutting and dispersing to obtain a dispersion; and (ii) adding 10-20 pts. wt. starch octenyl succinate, 40-60 pt s. wt. solvent B, 4-8 pts. wt. linoleic acid, 3-7 pts. wt. stearic acid, mixing starch octenyl succinate, stearic acid and solvent B and stirring at 65-70 degrees C room temperature, adding linoleic acid, stirring and mixing to obtain emulsion, mixing and stirring 12-20 pts. wt. emulsion with 8-15 pts. wt. polyethyleneimine, 3-8 pts. wt. silane coupling agent KH-560 and 20-35 pts. wt. dispersion, passing carbon dioxide, stirring and mixing silane coupling agent KH-560 and evaporating in vacuum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of the active substrate, comprises (A) adding cardanol, tetraethylammonium bromide and reagent A in a ratio of 5:1:14-20, mixing and stirring at 25-40 degrees C, heating to 70-80 degrees C, adding 2-3 dibromopropane with 20-35% cardanol, heating to 90-100 degrees C, keeping warm, cooling, and obtaining the reactants, adding reactants to the ether in a mass ratio of 5-8:1, washing organic phase with a washing liquid, and evaporating solvent to obtain an active intermediate, (B) mixing brown coal and the sodium hydroxide solution and stirring in a mass ratio of 1:10-50, filtering, and concentrating the filtrate in a vacuum at 60-80 degrees C to obtain the concentrated liquid, mixing with acrylic acid, acryloxy butyl sulfonic acid and acrylamide in a mass ratio of 7:1-3:0.3-0.7:1, and raising the temperature to 60-75 degrees C, and the mass of the concentrated liquid is 5-10 wt.% potassium persulfate, stirring, obtaining a gelatinous substance, drying, crushing and sieving, and collecting sieved particles to obtain graft copolymerization treatment; and (C) taking an alternate active intermediate having mass ratio is 1:8-15:2, adding dichloromethane, mixing graft copolymerization treatment and stirring at 28-35 degrees C, and allowing the mixture to stand, and then the active intermediate mass is 30-45% chlorosulfonic acid, and mixing the mixture with 3-6 times of methylene chloride, reacting in an ice water bath for 5-7 hours, adjusting pH, and evaporating to dryness at 55-70 degrees C to obtain a dried product, washing the dried product with acetone and drying, mixing the washing liquid obtained in the step (A) with acetic acid, oxalic acid and water in a mass ratio of 4:1-2:25, mixing the reagent A obtained in the step (A) with sodium hydroxide solution and sodium bicarbonate in a mass ratio of 8-14:3. The oil phase in the step (i) is obtained by mixing castor oil, paraffin oil and naphthenic oil in a mass ratio of 5:1:1-3. The pretreated plant material in the step (i) is peach core, the peanut shell and the glycerol ball mill are obtained by mixing in the mass ratio of 4:1-2:0.1, and the ball abrasive is obtained, and the ball abrasive is taken according to the water mass ratio of 20-30:1 steam explosion, obtaining blasting material, taking the blasting material into the ethanol solution according to the mass ratio of 1:12-16, soaking at room temperature, filtering, taking the filter residue to dry. The carbon dioxide feedstock in the step (i) is obtained by mixing isocyanate, sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride in a mass ratio of 4-8:1:0.1. The auxiliary agent in the step (i) is prepared by tetrabutyl titanate and the prenol are mixed in a mass ratio of 1:3-6. The solvent B in the step (ii) is obtained by mixing dopamine hydrochloride and acetic acid solution in a mass ratio of 1:12-18.

TI System for controlling a concentration of gas            dissolved in a beverage comprises saturation tank which            contains beverage supersaturated with gas, brite tank            having lower pressure than saturation tank, and            beverage supply system.
AU OSBORN G S
AE OSBORN G S (OSBO-Individual)
   UNIV ARKANSAS (UYAR-C)
GA 2018A1513S
AB    NOVELTY - A system (20) for controlling a concentration                of gas dissolved in a beverage, comprises                saturation tank (14) which contains beverage                supersaturated with gas; brite tank (10) having                lower pressure than saturation tank; beverage                supply system which is connected to saturation tank                and brite tank and passes beverage from saturation                tank to brite tank and from brite tank to                saturation tank; and source of gas connected with                saturation tank.
   USE - A system for controlling a concentration of                gas dissolved in a beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is provided for carbonating                beverages that improves operational efficiency by                providing a consistent and faster carbonation, and                includes an automatic shut-off and alert once                carbonation has reached the desired level. The                carbonation process may be completely automated for                reduced operator input, improved operational                efficiency, and consistent quality of the final                product. The uses far less carbon dioxide gas than                existing methods, which reduces operating costs and                the amount of carbon dioxide released into the                atmosphere, lowering the carbon footprint of the                user.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                controlling a concentration of gas dissolved in a                beverage which involves providing a saturation tank                (14) containing a beverage; providing a brite tank                (10) containing the beverage where beverage                contained in brite tank is non-saturated with gas;                passing beverage supersaturated with gas from                saturation tank to brite tank where beverage                supersaturated with gas mixes with beverage in                brite tank that is non saturated with gas; and                passing beverage in brite tank from brite tank to                saturation tank until beverage remaining in brite                tank is saturated with gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                apparatus.
   Brite tank (10)
   Pump (12)
   Saturation tank (14)
   System (20)
   Pressure sensor (22)

AU LIU B
   DONG T
   DUAN Z
   KANG Y
   YANG L
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 2018A3665U
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic system comprises ruthenium carbonyl complex, imidazole ionic liquid and inorganic salt accelerant, or ruthenium carbonyl complex and imidazole ion liquid, where the mola ratio of imidazole ionic liquid to carbonyl ruthenium cluster complex is 100-250:1, when the catalytic system includes accelerant, the molar ratio of accelerant to ruthenium carbonyl complex cluster is 1-75:1, and the accelerant is carbonate, lithium halide salt or transition metal chloride salt.
   USE - The catalytic system is useful for preparing methyl propionate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic system: utilizes imidazole-based ionic liquid to consume water produced in the reaction, promotes reaction in the positive direction, and also utilizes inorganic salt promoter to increase catalytic activity; catalyzes the preparation of methyl propionate from ethylene, carbon dioxide and methanol; and shortens reaction time to increase yield of methyl propionate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing methyl propionate, comprising adding material in catalysis system, then adding in a reaction kettle, sealing the reaction kettle, vacuuming at 70 degrees C and stirring for 0.5 hour, then adding methanol and ethylene into the reaction kettle under argon gas atmosphere, then filling carbon dioxide under pressure of 3-5 MPa, heating to 160-200 degrees C and reacting under magnetic stirring for 1.5-6 hours to obtain methyl propionate, where the molar ratio of methanol to ethylene is 25-100:1, and molar ratio of methanol to ruthenium carbonyl cluster complex is 2500-5000:1; and
   (2) imidazole ionic liquid i.e. 1-methyl-3-(3-(trihydroxysilyl)propyl)-1H-imidazol-3-ium salt of formula (I).
   R = CH3, CH3CH2 or COCH3; and
   X = Cl, Br, I, BF4, PF6, SCN, HSO4, HCO3, CF3SO3, CF3C(=O)O or Tf2N.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ruthenium carbonyl complex is triruthenium dodecacarbonyl, H4Ru4(CO)12 , (PPN)(RuCl3(CO)3), Ru3(CO)9(PPh3)3, Ru3(CO)11(PPh2py), Ru(CO)HCl(PPh3)3, RuH2(PPh3)4, Ru3(CO)11H(PPN), Ru(acac)3, Ru(CO)3(PPh3)2, Ru(CO)3(PCy3)2, Et4N(Ru(CO)3I3), Et4N(HRu3(CO)11) or Ru(OAc)2(Ph3P)2. The promoter is carbonate, lithium halide salts or transition metal chloride, e.g. sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, lithium carbonate, potassium carbonate, cesium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, lithium fluoride, lithium chloride, lithium bromide, lithium iodide, sodium chloride, potassium chloride, cesium chloride, cuprous chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride, zinc chloride, nickel chloride, copper chloride, manganese chloride, stannous chloride, ferrous chloride, aluminum chloride or iron chloride.

UT DIIDW:2018A3665U
ER

PT P
PN US2018299443-A1; US10761094-B2
TI System for performing an assay for determining biological condition in a mammalian subject such as blood diseases, leukemia, thrombocytopenia, and immune system disorders, comprise self-contained stationary cartridge for receiving specimen.
AU KASDAN H L
   MEISSONNIER J
   ZUTA Y
   ROSEN M
   HIMMEL Y
   BRODER Y
   DAVIS B
   GOLDMAN B
   GIRON B
   BOTESAZAN Z
   BLASBERG E
   SEMMEL I
   ASCHKENASY J
AE LEUKODX LTD (LEUK-Non-standard)
   ACCELLIX LTD (ACCE-Non-standard)
GA 201880519W
AB    NOVELTY - System (100) comprise self-contained stationary cartridge for receiving a specimen from the mammalian subject, where the cartridge comprises storage element for storing into liquid composition, bellows, which are configured to provide pressure force to the sample composition chamber (104) to fluidly transfer the composition to induce mixing with a portion of the specimen to form a treated sample. The pressure force exerts negative pressure force, cartridge used for handling unit (CHU) for receiving the cartridge, reader optics assembly is adapted to impinge radiation via a dichroic filter and a focusing lens via a first optical path on the treated sample to form multiple spectrally distinct signals, the reader optics assembly further configured to detect multiple spectrally distinct signals using a multiple emission detector through the first optical path, and multiple spectrally distinct signals are associated with reporter functionality unit.
   USE - System for performing an assay for determining biological condition in a mammalian subject such as blood diseases, leukemia, thrombocytopenia, immune system disorders, local infections, urinary tract disorders, autoimmune diseases and sepsis and detects blood chemistry parameter and urine chemistry parameter selected from albumin, total protein, chloride, carbon dioxide, sodium, potassium, glucose, urea, nitrogen, urea nitrogen, creatinine, carbon dioxide content, total bilirubin, calcium, phosphorus, cholesterol, iron, uric acid, creatine kinase, alkaline phosphatase, lactate dehydrogenase, aspartate aminotransferase and alanine aminotransferases, urine pH, urine protein, urine glucose, urine ketone, urine bilirubin, urine blood, Urobilinogen, urine nitrite, urine leukocytes, urine specific gravity, procalcitonin, alpha-fetoprotein receptor (RECAF) protein, D-dimer, and fetal hemoglobin (all claimed).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system
   System (100)
   Sample composition chamber (104)
   Transfer element (105)
   Treatment chamber (112)
   Specimen receiving element (118)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The system is a flow cytometer system. The system further comprises software adapted to run diagnostic process of the assay, user interface touch-screen, adapted to control operation of the reader assembly and to automatically receive and display a result from the processor for determining the biological condition in the mammalian subject. The cartridge further comprise a pre-analytical component configured to receive the specimen. The storage element chamber comprises two storage elements. The specimen is a fluidly transferable bodily sample comprising cells. The self-contained stationary cartridge is a microfluidics cartridge comprising a reading zone configured for passing the cells through the reading zone to provide a flow cytometric assay result to the specimen. The cartridge further comprise one treatment chamber for incubating the specimen from the subject in the cartridge for a predetermined period of time. The system is configured to detect a cell surface marker selected from cluster of differentiation-64 (CD64), CD34 Stem Cell, CD62, CD20, CD52, CD4, human leukocyte antigen (HLA) DR or cytokine. The assay is selected from cell cycle analysis, cell proliferation detection assay or cytokine detection assay. The software is associated for computing CD64 and CD163 indices on leukocytes. The reader optics assembly comprise a laser unit for impinging the radiation and where the multiple emission detector comprise a photomultiplier (PMT) assembly. Preferred Conditions: The sensors configured in the system to detect a position of motors and actuators. The sample is of a volume of less than 200 microliters ( mu L). The multiple spectrally distinct signals associated with at least one reporter functionality are associated with two reporter functionalities and where the spectrally distinct signals of the two reporter functionalities overlap in wavelength.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN108126754-A; CN108126754-B
TI Asymmetric nitrogen-hydrogen-pyridine nickel type metal catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, is hexacoordinate octahedral model structure, and is prepared by dissolving reactant A in tetrahydrofuran.
AU KANG P
   LIU F
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 2018465005
AB    NOVELTY - Asymmetric nitrogen-hydrogen-pyridine nickel type metal catalyst is hexacoordinate octahedral model structure (I).
   USE - Asymmetric nitrogen-hydrogen-pyridine nickel type metal catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Asymmetric nitrogen-hydrogen-pyridine nickel type metal catalyst is hexacoordinate octahedral model structure of formula (I).
   R1 and R2=alkyl, trimethyl silicon group, amidocyanogen, imine, alkoxy, benzyl or halogen substituent, where halogen substituent is fluorine, chlorine, bromine or iodine;
   R3=hydrogen atom, an alkyl group, alkoxy, phenyl, benzyl, amino, pyridyl, oxazolyl or biotin;
   R4 and R5=hydrogen atom, alkyl group, alkoxy group, amino group, nitrile group, aryl group or biotin; and
   L1 and L2=halogen atom, acetonitrile, carboxylate groups, methanol molecules, water molecules, or tetrahydrofuran group.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing asymmetric nitrogen-hydrogen-pyridine nickel type metal catalyst, which involves:
   (A) dissolving reactant A in tetrahydrofuran to obtain mixed solution B, dissolving alkyl magnesium bromide in diethyl ether to obtain mixed solution C, pouring the mixed solution C in mixed solution B, mixing and reacting to obtain mixed solution D, quenching saturated ammonium-containing solution and mixed solution D to obtain reactant E, where structure formula of the reactant A is (II);
   (B) adding reagent E into alcohol to obtain mixed solution, adding mixed solution F and acid to obtain mixed liquid G and mixing mixed solution of aniline compound and reacting to obtain reactant H;
   (C) dissolving reactant in alcohol to obtain mixed liquid, mixing mixed solution I and reducing compound reaction to obtain reactant; and
   (D) adding tetrahydrofuran, reactants and nickel salt, reacting to obtain asymmetric hydrogen-nitrogen pyridine-nickel type metal catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkyl bromide is methyl magnesium bromide, ethyl magnesium bromide, butyl magnesium bromide, propyl magnesium bromide. The alcohol is methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol or glycol. The acid is formic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid or oxalic acid. The aniline compound is 2,6-diisopropylaniline, aniline, 2,6-dinitroaniline or 2,6-dibromoaniline. The reducing compound is sodium borohydride, sodium borohydride or sodium cyanoborohydride. Preferred Composition: The amount of saturated ammonium solution is 0.01-1 mol/L. The concentration of magnesium is 1-2mol/L. The concentration of reactant E in the mixed liquid is 0.01-0.2 mol/L. Preferred Condition: The ratio of the mixed solution B and mixed solution C is 1-10:1. The dropping temperature is 0-10 degrees C. The stirring reaction is (-)15-(-)20 degrees C. The stirring and reacting for 2-4 hours. The normal temperature to stir for 3-4 hours. The ratio of the mixed solution F and acid is 1:0.001-0.01. The ratio of reactant E and aniline compound in the mixed solution G is 0.8-1:1. The heating, refluxing and stirring time is 22-24 hours. The reflowing temperature is 90-110 degrees C. The ratio of the mixed solution F and acid is 1:0.001-0.01. The ratio of reactant E and aniline compound in the mixed solution G is 0.8-1:1. The reaction condition is heating reflux stirring for 22-24 hours. The reflux temperature is 90-110 degrees C. The concentration of reactant in the mixed solution I is 0.01-0.2 mol/L. The ratio of the reactant and the reducing compound in the mixed solution I is 1-1:-10. The reaction condition is normal temperature stirring for 22-24 hours. The ratio of reactant and nickel salt is 1-1.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 and R2=16C alkyl;
   R3=1-10C alkyl; and
   R4 and R5=hydrogen atom, 1-6C aryl or alkyl.

PT P
PN CN108060877-A; CN207879183-U
TI Double-cavity four-elastic sheet structure for e.g. full-wood door, has energy absorbing boards in cavities staggered together, where cavities are filled with carbon dioxide gas with pressure of specific times of atmospheric pressure.
AU ZHENG L
AE ZHEJIANG YADILE WOOD IND CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018417480
AB    NOVELTY - The sheet structure has a door plate (1) fixed with a frame (2) that is formed as a solid wood structure. The door plate is provided with three wood board layers, two sealing cavities and four sound-absorbing boards along thickness direction. The two sealing cavities are respectively provided with two pieces of elastic energy absorbing boards i.e. steel plates. The two pieces of elastic energy absorbing boards in the two sealing cavities are respectively formed with two energy-absorbing plate through-holes along the thickness direction and staggered together. The two sealing cavities are filled with carbon dioxide gas with pressure of about 0.5-0.8 times of standard atmospheric pressure. A concave channel is extended along width direction of the door plate and provided with an energy absorbing shaft. The shaft is provided with a rigid tube. An elastic sleeve is arranged on the rigid tube. The sound-absorbing boards are made of sponge material.
   USE - Double-cavity four-elastic sheet structure for a wooden door e.g. full-wood door, solid wood door and composite wood door.
   ADVANTAGE - The sheet structure increases sound-insulation, heat insulating and anti-seismic effects of the hollow structural wood door. The adjacent elastic sleeves are connected together by rigid rods to make that the energy absorbing rod is extruded by the elastic plate to generate absorption deformation energy so as to drive the energy absorbing rod to generate linkage absorption deformation energy, thus increasing shock energy absorbing effect of the wood door.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of the double-cavity four-elastic sheet structure.
   Door plate (1)
   Frame (2)

PN CN107879913-A; CN107879913-B
TI Preparation of crude benzaldehyde used for tobacco product by vacuum freeze-drying Anacardiaceae raw material, crushing, supercritical extracting, filtering, vacuum-concentrating, processing in water bath and molecularly distilling.
AU XIANG Z
   HOU X
   DENG J
   SUN B
AE YINGTAN HUABAO FLAVOURS & FRAGRANCES CO (YING-Non-standard)
GA 201828014T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of crude benzaldehyde comprises vacuum freeze-drying Anacardiaceae raw material, crushing into granules, adding 100 pts. wt. granules into supercritical extraction device, extracting at 15-25 MPa and 35-55 degrees C for 70-90 minutes at carbon dioxide flow rate of 15-20 kg/hour, filtering extracting liquid, removing precipitate, vacuum-concentrating, processing in water bath at 48-52 degrees C and 0.088-0.092 MPa, molecularly distilling at feeding speed of 1.5-2.5 mL/minute, evaporating at 10-30 Pa and 35-45 degrees C at drawing speed of 300-400 revolutions/minute and cooling to 1-8 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing crude benzaldehyde used for tobacco product.
   ADVANTAGE - The crude benzaldehyde has high purity, pure and sweet aroma, simple operation, high quality and no pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of crude benzaldehyde comprises vacuum freeze-drying Anacardiaceae raw material, crushing into granules, adding 100 pts. wt. granules into supercritical extraction device, extracting at 15-25 MPa and 35-55 degrees C for 70-90 minutes at carbon dioxide flow rate of 15-20 kg/hour at separation pressure of 2-4 MPa and separation temperature of 25-35 degrees C using 8-14 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol as entrainer, filtering extracting liquid, removing precipitate, vacuum-concentrating in rotary evaporator to obtain concentrated solution, processing in water bath at 48-52 degrees C and 0.088-0.092 MPa, molecularly distilling at feeding speed of 1.5-2.5 mL/minute, evaporating at 10-30 Pa and 35-45 degrees C at drawing speed of 300-400 revolutions/minute and cooling to 1-8 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The Anacardiaceae raw material is pretreated by vacuum freezing-drying at - 20 to - 15 degrees C at 30-45Pa, analyzing, and drying at 20-30 degrees C for 8-10 hours. Filtering is done using 300-450 mesh filter screen.

   MA J
   MA W

GA 201821764E

Drynaria rhizome, 4-9 pts. wt. rhizoma nardostachyos, 2-5 pts. wt. giant knotweed, 2-5 pts. wt. Chinese knotweed, 1-3 pts. wt. mint, 1-2 pts. wt.    USE - Rheumatism capsule used for treating rheumatoid arthritis, cervical rib neuralgia, sciatica, and insomnia.
   ADVANTAGE - The rheumatism capsule has short treatment period and no side effects even after taking a long time, does not cause drug-induced liver disease, renal disease, and stomach disease, and is safer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of rheumatism capsule comprising taking Photinia vine, E. indica, Mahonia, B. laciniata, Drynaria rhizome, rhizoma nardostachyos, giant knotweed, Chinese knotweed, mint, and Areca peel, cleaning, drying, respectively grinding into 200-400-mesh powder, mixing the respective powder to obtain mixed powder, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting at 42-46 MPa and 42-55 degrees C, separating at separator pressure of 32-35 Pa and temperature of 65-75 degrees C for 1-1.8 hours, where carbon dioxide flow rate is 32-40 L/hour, obtaining extract liquid, decompressing and drying at 45-55 degrees C to obtain dry extract, adding sodium bicarbonate, mixing, high-energy nano-impact grinding into 300-500 mesh, obtaining the mixed dry paste powder, adding xylitol, maltodextrin and sodium alginate, uniformly mixing, wet granulating with 75% ethanol, drying at 40-50 degrees C, pelletizing and placing in capsule to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises respectively grinding into 250-mesh powder. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is conducted at 45 MPa and 51 degrees C, separating at separator pressure of 33 Pa and temperature of 72 degrees C for 1.6 hours, where carbon dioxide flow rate is 38 L/hour, obtaining extract liquid, decompressing and drying at 52 degrees C to obtain dry extract, adding sodium bicarbonate, mixing, high-energy nano-impact grinding into 400 mesh, obtaining the mixed dry paste powder, adding xylitol, maltodextrin and sodium alginate, uniformly mixing, wet granulating with 75% ethanol, drying at 46 degrees C, pelletizing and placing in capsule to obtain the product.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The rheumatism capsule comprises 12 pts. wt. Photinia vine, 8 pts. wt. E. indica, 7 pts. wt. Mahonia, 5 pts. wt. B. laciniata, 6 pts. wt. Areca peel, 1.5 pts. wt. xylitol, 1.8 pts. wt. maltodextrin, 1.4 pts. wt. sodium alginate, and 0.5 pt. wt. sodium bicarbonate.

AE SHANGHAI BODAN ENVIRONMENT ENG TECHNOLOG (SHAN-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - A graphite loaded tin dioxide electrode is prepared by polishing graphite using sand paper, removing surface inert layer, soaking in concentrated nitric acid or aqua regia, processing, placing into muffle furnace, temperature programming by heat treating under carbon dioxide atmosphere, expanding treatment temperature, heat treating, mixing stannic chloride, manganese chloride, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, deionized water and ethanol, coating on graphite sheet, drying, calcining into muffle furnace, cooling, and repeating drying and calcining.
   USE - Method for preparing graphite loaded tin dioxide electrode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses metal element manganese for improving coating surface structure of tin dioxide. The product has small grain size, compact arrangement, improves electrode performance, keeps graphite electrode stability, and exhibits high oxygen evolution potential and electro-catalysis activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A graphite loaded tin dioxide electrode is prepared by polishing graphite using sand paper, removing surface inert layer, soaking in 65% concentrated nitric acid for 24 hours or aqua regia for 1 hour, processing to obtain graphite electrode, placing into muffle furnace, temperature programming by heat treating under carbon dioxide atmosphere at 1-5 degrees C/minute, expanding treatment temperature to 600-80 degrees C, heat treating for 3-5 hours to obtain graphite sheet, mixing stannic chloride, manganese chloride, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, deionized water and ethanol at molar ratio of 1-1.5:0.05-0.06:0.0053-0.0063:18-30:28-33 to prepare sol-gel pre-deposition liquid, coating on graphite sheet, drying into drying box at 100 degrees C for 12 hours and 200 degrees C for 2 hours, calcining into muffle furnace under air atmosphere at 300-500 degrees C for 1 hour, cooling at speed of 1 degrees C/minute, and repeating drying and calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The graphite sheet has fixed carbon content of more than 99% and polished by 100#, 300# and 500# sand paper. Preferred Method: The surface inert layer is removed by ultrasonically cleaning graphite in distilled water and acetone for 20 minutes, washing by distilled water for 2 times and drying.
