PN KR2022111532-A
TI Preparing catalyst for carbon dioxide reduction            comprises forming bismuth-based metal layer on base            material and immersing it in electrolyte containing            carbonate anion to form nanostructure composed of            bismuth, oxygen, and carbon.
AU LAM L J
   DAE-MYUNG H
   RIM J W
   DONG W
AE POSTECH ACAD-IND FOUND (UYPO-C)
GA 2022A1585A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst for carbon dioxide                reduction comprises forming bismuth-based metal                layer on a base material and immersing it in an                electrolyte containing carbonate anion to form                nanostructure composed of bismuth, oxygen, and                carbon by the reaction between the metal layer and                the electrolyte.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing catalyst                for carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases reduction efficiency of                reducing carbon dioxide to formic acid, is                economical and easy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base material is                  iron, silver, gold, copper, chromium, tungsten,                  aluminum, molybdenum, zinc, nickel, platinum,                  palladium, cobalt, indium, manganese, silicon,                  tantalum, titanium, tin, lead, vanadium,                  ruthenium, iridium, zirconium, rhodium, bismuth                  and/or magnesium. The solution containing the                  carbonate anion is potassium bicarbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate, and/or cesium bicarbonate                  electrolyte, and the concentration of the                  solution is 0.01-1 M. Preferred Method: The                  bismuth-based metal layer is formed through one                  method including electron beam deposition,                  sputter deposition, thermal deposition, and                  chemical vapor deposition, and the metal layer                  has a thickness of 5-100 nm. The catalyst is                  immersed in the electrolyte for 3-60                  minutes.
   EXAMPLE - Substrate constituting a base material was                prepared, metal layer on substrate was formed.                Bismuth carbonate nanostructure was prepared by                supporting metal base material on which the metal                layer is formed in an electrolyte solution for                carbon dioxide reduction.

TI New amide-based ordered porous polyionic liquid            material used in adsorption of carbon dioxide, and            conversion of carbon dioxide.
AU WANG J
   ZHOU Y
   CAO J
   LI G
   ZOU Z
   ZOU C
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201925373G
AB    NOVELTY - An amide-based ordered porous polyionic liquid                material chosen from components (I)-(VI), is                new.
   USE - New amide-based ordered porous polyionic                liquid material is used in adsorption of carbon                dioxide, and conversion of carbon dioxide (all                claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An amide-based ordered porous polyionic liquid                material chosen from components of formulae                (I)-(VI), is new.
   A = group of formula (i);
   B' = group of formula (ib);
   D' = group of formula (id);
   M = group of formula (ie);and
   G = group of formula (if).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of amide-based ordered porous polyionic                liquid material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The epoxy compound is                  propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, phenyl glycidyl                  ether, glycidyl ether, styrene oxide,                  epoxybutane, epoxyhexane, epoxyoctane,                  epoxydecane, and/or epoxycyclohexene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): Preparation of                  ordered porous polyionic liquid material involves                  reacting 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene,                  terephthalaldehyde and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  chloride in an organic solvent by Schiff base                  reaction under the action of acetic acid as                  catalyst to obtain component (I). Preparation of                  ordered porous polyionic liquid material involves                  reacting 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene,                  pyromellitic acid and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  bromide in an organic solvent by Schiff base                  reaction under the action of acetic acid as                  catalyst to obtain component (II). Preparation of                  ordered porous polyionic liquid material involves                  reacting 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene,                  pyromellitic acid and guanidine hydrochloride in                  an organic solvent by Schiff base reaction under                  the action of acetic acid as catalyst to obtain                  component (III). Preparation of ordered porous                  polyionic liquid material involves reacting                  1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene,                  2,4,6-trihydroxybenzene-1,3,5-tricarbaldehyde and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  bromide in an organic solvent by Schiff base                  reaction under the action of acetic acid as                  catalyst to obtain component (IV). Preparation of                  ordered porous polyionic liquid material involves                  reacting p-phenylenediamine,                  2,4,6-trihydroxybenzene-1,3,5-tricarbaldehyde and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  bromide in an organic solvent by Schiff base                  reaction under the action of acetic acid as                  catalyst to obtain component (V). Preparation of                  ordered porous polyionic liquid material involves                  reacting                  2,4,6-trihydroxybenzene-1,3,5-tricarbaldehyde and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  bromide in an organic solvent by Schiff base                  reaction under the action of acetic acid as                  catalyst to obtain component (VI). Preferred                  Composition: The molar ratio of                  1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene,                  terephthalaldehyde and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  chloride is x:0.13:y, where x is 0-0.09, and                  3x+2y is 0.26. The molar ratio of                  1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene, pyromellitic                  acid and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  bromide is x:0.09:y, where x is 0-0.09, and 3x+2y                  is 0.26. The molar ratio of                  1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene, isophthalic                  acid and guanidine hydrochloride is x:0.09:y,                  where x is 0-0.09, and 3x+2y is 0.27. The molar                  ratio of 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene,                  2,4,6-trihydroxybenzene-1,3,5-tricarbaldehyde and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  bromide is x:0.09:y, where x is 0-0.09, and 3x+2y                  is 0.27. The molar ratio of p-phenylenediamine,                  2,4,6-trihydroxybenzene-1,3,5-tricarbaldehyde and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  bromide is x:0.14:y, where x is 0-0.21, and 2x+2y                  is 0.42. The molar ratio of                  2,4,6-trihydroxybenzene-1,3,5-tricarbaldehyde and                  3,7-diamino-2,8-dimethyl-5-phenylphenazinium                  bromide is 0.07:0.11. The organic solvent is                  tetradecane, mesitylene, absolute ethanol,                  deionized water, ortho-dichlorobenzene, and/or                  N-methylpyrrolidone. The concentration of the                  acetic acid is 3-10 M. The usage amount of acetic                  acid is 0.3-3 ml. The reaction is performed at                  70-120 degrees C. The amide-based ordered porous                  polyionic liquid material is placed in a sample                  tank under conditions of 273 K, and the sample                  tank is immersed in a liquid circulation                  thermostat for carbon dioxide adsorption                  measurements, in which all samples were activated                  at 120 degrees C for 12 hours before the start of                  the measurement. In the carbon dioxide adsorption                  measurements, the amide-based ordered porous                  polyionic liquid material is introduced into a                  carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction system, and                  an epoxy compound is used as a substrate in the                  reaction system.

PN KR2018019867-A; KR1958060-B1
TI Solid absorbent useful for treating exhaust gas to absorb carbon dioxide at low temperature, comprises composite particles of tin dioxide and alkali metal carbonate.
AU CHANG K J
   CHOOL L S
   MIN S C
   HO J C
   JOONG B L
   TAE H E
   KIM J C
   LEE S C
AE UNIV KYUNGPOOK NAT IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UKYK-C)
GA 201819052L
AB    NOVELTY - Solid absorbent comprises composite particles of tin dioxide and alkali metal carbonate.
   USE - The absorbent is useful for treating exhaust gas to absorb carbon dioxide at low temperature (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent retains carbon dioxide absorbing ability even in repeated absorption- and regeneration processes, absorbs and regenerates carbon dioxide at low temperature region, and has thermal stability and high absorption power without decreasing the absorption power at high temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for treating exhaust gas using the solid absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorbent has carbon dioxide absorbing performance in the presence of water at less than or equal to 100 degrees C, and carbon dioxide regeneration performance at less than or equal to 150 degrees C under inert atmosphere. The alkali metal includes potassium or sodium. The absorbent has a core-shell structure made of tin dioxide and alkali metal carbonate. The alkali metal carbonate includes potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate and/or sodium carbonate. The alkali metal carbonate has: carbon dioxide absorption performance at 50-90 degrees C, when the alkali metal carbonate is potassium bicarbonate; carbon dioxide absorbing performance at 30-60 degrees C, when the alkali metal carbonate is sodium bicarbonate; carbon dioxide regeneration performance at greater than or equal to 150 degrees C, when the alkali metal carbonate is potassium bicarbonate; and carbon dioxide regeneration performance at less than or equal to 110 degrees C, when the alkali metal carbonate is sodium bicarbonate. The absorbent comprises 40-99 wt.% tin dioxide and 1-60 wt.% alkali metal carbonate; or 50-80 wt.% tin dioxide and 20-50 wt.% alkali metal carbonate. Preferred Method: The absorbent is produced by preparing mixed aqueous solution of alkali metal carbonate aqueous solution and tin dioxide, evaporating and drying the mixed aqueous solution and firing at 100-900 degrees C under inert atmosphere to obtain composite particles of tin dioxide and alkali metal carbonate. The exhaust gas treatment method is carried out in a fixed bed reactor, a moving bed reactor or a fluid bed reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Carbon dioxide phase change directional blasting            device for blasting rock body, has locating sheet whose            inner surface is fixed on outer cylindrical surface of            flexible airbag, where airbag is provided with trigger            connected with excitation lead.
AU ZUO L
   LI H
   YAN C
   QIN Z
   CHEN Z
   YUAN Y
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2022C30451
AB    NOVELTY - The directional blasting device has a                cap-shaped upper plug (1), an upper locating ring                (2), a flexible airbag (3), a positioning sheet                (4), and a lower positioning ring (5). The flexible                airbag is cylindrical flexible sealing bag with an                opening at the upper end. The top plate of the top                plate is provided with an excitation wire and an                air charging hole (8). The cap of the upper plug                circumferential side plate is matched and connected                with the upper position ring and extrudes the                flexible end of the airbag to generate a sealing                effect. The lower position ring is placed at the                bottom of the flexible airbags. The positioning                sheet is provided of multiple arc sheets. The inner                surface of the locating sheet is fixedly fixed on                the outside of the outer surface of a flexible bag.                The flexible airbag is further provided with a                trigger connected with the excitation lead.
   USE - Carbon dioxide phase change directional                blasting device for use in road construction and                mine side slope construction.
   ADVANTAGE - The device combines the flexible airbag and                the locating sheet, so that the pressure stress                generated by the expansion of the gas is acted on                the rock body around the locator sheet, thus                generating tensile stress in the directional                direction, and hence realizing the result of                directional blasting cracking. The device has high                energy utilization rate, and the directional effect                is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                directional blasting method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional structure                schematic diagram of the directional blasting                device.

TI Wood heat treatment method for performing in-situ            repair, involves putting obtained impregnated wood in            salt solution containing carbon-32-ion for vacuum            negative pressure dipping, and drying to obtain needed            modified wood.
AU LIANG D
   YU M
   ZHOU L
   LIU S
   DING S
   CHU D
   YAN Q
AE UNIV ANHUI AGRIC (UYAH-C)
GA 2022B48834
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves subjecting raw material                wood to high-temperature heat treatment to obtain                heat-treated wood (S1). The vacuum negative                pressure of the obtained heat-treating wood is                dipped in a salt solution containing calcium ion to                obtain a wood after drying. The obtained                impregnated wood is placed in the salt solution                with carbon dioxide ion for vacuum-negative                pressure dipping to obtain another wood (S3). The                latter wood is desalted with desalted treatment and                dried to obtain the required modified wood                (S4).
   USE - Wood heat treatment method for performing                in-situ repair and modified wood. Uses include but                are not limited to outdoor floor, outdoor                decorative wallboard, courtyard furniture and                wooden fence.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has scientific and reasonable                design. The prepared modified wood has excellent                surface hardness, bending strength and heat                stability. The calcium carbonate can be effectively                attached to the heat modified material. The effect                of repairing heat treatment wood cell wall in situ,                with important value for wood utilization                processing and wood material diversification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                modified wood.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a wood heat                treatment method for performing in-situ repair and                modified wood. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
   S1Step for subjecting raw material wood to                high-temperature heat treatment to obtain                heat-treated wood
   S2Step for dipping vacuum negative pressure                of the obtained heat-treating wood in a salt                solution containing calcium ion to obtain a wood                after drying
   S3Step for placing the obtained impregnated                wood in the salt solution with carbon dioxide ion                for vacuum-negative pressure dipping to obtain                another wood
   S4Step for desalting the latter wood with                desalted treatment, and drying to obtain the                required modified wood

PN CN114122265-A
TI Method for improving life-span of solar cell involves forming silica thin-film isolation layer by reacting sodium silicate, carbon dioxide and water, between poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene)-polystyrene sulfonate layer and active layer of device to prevent water from reducing life of device.
AU JIN Y
   XU T
   WANG C
   ZHANG Y
   WU Z
   ZHU P
   XU M
AE UNIV NANTONG (UYNT-C)
GA 2022374865
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving life-span of solar cell involves providing a silica (SiO2) thin-film isolation layer between a poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) polystyrene sulfonate (PEDOT:PSS) thin-film layer and an active layer of a device to prevent water from reducing the life of the device, such that the silica thin-film isolation layer ensures that the active layer is not decomposed by water, and the interface layer is not affected by water. The silica thin-film isolation layer is formed by reacting sodium silicate, carbon dioxide and water to heat, preferably formed by dissolving solid sodium silicate in deionized water at a concentration of 1-10 mg/ml, stirring at room temperature for 1 hour in air, spin-coating obtained mixture on the PEDOT:PSS thin-film layer, aging for 30 minutes in air, and annealing at 300degrees Celsius for 10 minutes.
   USE - Method for improving life-span of solar cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adding a layer of isolation layer SiO2 between the PEDOT: PSS and the device active layer, thus preventing moisture from reducing the solar device service life, and hence improving the service life of solar device. The method ensures the active layer is not decomposed by water, and ensures the interface layer not affected by water. The silica layer has good light transmittance, and does not affect the absorption of active layer to the sunlight. The silicon dioxide preparation method is simple, low cost, and can be used for commercial large area production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Property: After 4 months, the lifetime of PEDOT:PSS-based perovskite solar cell is reduced to 50% of the original, and the lifetime of PEDOT:PSS/silica-based perovskite solar cell is reduced to 80% of the original, and the PEDOT:PSS/silica layer has excellent water barrier properties.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. TI Integrated process for hydrotreating renewable feedstock, by hydrotreating renewable feedstock in presence of hydrogen stream in hydrotreating reactor, contacting entire hydrotreated effluent stream with water gas shift catalyst, passing shift reactor effluent stream to cold separator and recycling.
AU JADAUN K P
   BAJPAI H S
   MANI K
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 202202656P
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated process for hydrotreating renewable feedstock, involves hydrotreating renewable feedstock in presence of hydrogen stream in hydrotreating reactor under hydrotreating conditions to obtain hydrotreated effluent stream, contacting entire hydrotreated effluent stream with water gas shift catalyst under water gas shift reaction conditions to obtain shift reactor effluent stream, passing shift reactor effluent stream to cold separator for recovering cold vapor stream and cold liquid stream comprising diesel, and recycling cold vapor stream having reduced concentration of carbon monoxide to hydrotreating zone.
   USE - The integrated process is used for hydrotreating renewable feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The integrated process improves carbon monoxide management by converting carbon monoxide produced during hydrotreating of renewable feedstock to carbon dioxide and hydrogen by water gas shift reaction, minimizes installation and operational cost, reduces the revamp requirement, provides economic advantage with minimal investment, increases the co-processing capabilities of renewable feedstock, and minimizes the carbon monoxide build-up in recycle gas loop by converting carbon monoxide to gas which is easily removed from recycle gas loop.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for hydrotreating renewable feedstock, comprising a hydrotreating reactor operating under hydrotreating conditions configured to produce hydrotreated effluent stream, a hot separator in fluid communication with hydrotreating reactor and configured to vaporize flash stream from hydrotreated effluent stream, a water gas shift catalyst bed located above inlet to hot separator to receive flash stream and configured to produce shift catalyst effluent stream comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen which exits in hot separator overhead line, and a cold separator in fluid communication with hot separator overhead line and configured to recover cold vapor stream and cold liquid stream comprising diesel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The water gas shift reactor is operating at 150-300 degrees C. Preferred Method: The method involves passing shift reactor effluent stream to cold separator by passing shift reactor effluent stream to hot separator for recovering hot vapor stream and passing hot vapor stream to cold separator. The method involves combining petroleum feedstock with renewable feedstock. The method further involves passing cold vapor stream for recycling gas scrubber for removal of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide to produce recycle hydrogen stream. The method involves adding water to water gas shift catalyst bed for reacting with carbon monoxide present in hydrotreated effluent stream and producing shift reactor effluent stream comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen. Preferred Components: The petroleum feedstock comprises diesel, shale oil, kerosene, gas oil and/or cracked feeds. The renewable feedstock comprises vegetable oil and/or animal oil. Preferred Composition: The renewable feedstock comprises 5-95 wt.% feedstock.

PN CN113855744-A
TI Detoxification preparation useful for realizing expelling toxin of human body, improving metabolic ability of human body comprises hawthorn, medlar and dandelion.
AU CHEN Y
AE CHEN Y (CHEN-Individual)
GA 202210724U
AB    NOVELTY - Detoxification preparation comprises 10 pts. wt. hawthorn, 15 pts. wt. medlar and 5 pts. wt. dandelion, where the dandelion is dandelion flower, leaf and stem 1: 5: 10.
   USE - The detoxification preparation is useful for realizing expelling toxin of human body, improving the metabolic ability of human body, improving the efficiency of expelling toxin, and fundamentally improving human metabolism ability (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The detoxification preparation: ensures the vitamin in natural substance, polysaccharide and natural pigment will not be damaged; ensures the best use of the material; ensures the natural property of the preparation; and can ensure the clear indication of allergen; is convenient for consumer to select, and greatly reduces the invisible allergen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing detoxification preparation comprising (1) raw material processing, using sterile water to wash all raw materials, soaking for 20 minutes, using sterile water to wash, using drying device, drying at constant temperature, where the raw material water content is less than 5 %, crushing processing, raw material solid particulate matter sieving and storing, (2) mixing the medlar solid particulate matter sterile water, improving humidity, using supercritical carbon dioxide to extract, dissolving medlar pigment, carbon dioxide releasing pressure, obtaining medlar pigment extract, (3) mixing the hawthorn and dandelion solid particulate matter solvent, centrifuging and stirring, pressurizing and heating curing process, cooling, centrifuging and stirring again, and taking the supernatant, (4) concentrating the supernatant, using macroporous absorption resin column to absorb, using 10 times resin column volume of distilled water to elute, using 5 times resin column volume of 95 % ethanol to elute, collecting the ethanol eluent, removing solvent, obtaining hawthorn and dandelion extract, and (5) mixing hawthorn and dandelion extract with honey, mixing and granulating with medlar pigment extract, packaging after drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), washing the raw material, using 10 litres per minute flow to wash, where the drying temperature is 45degrees Celsius, and the drying time is more than 30 minutes. In the step (2), the carbon dioxide pressure relief pressure is 7 MPa, the pressure release temperature is maintained at 35-40degrees Celsius. In the step (3), the hawthorn and dandelion solid particulate matter with solvent, the solvent is ethanol, the centrifugal stirring condition needs to maintain 35degrees Celsius and 300 revolutions per second. In the step (3), the pressurizing heating curing process, the heating temperature is maintained at 45-55degrees Celsius, the curing time is 35 minutes. In the step (4), concentrating the supernatant and the heating temperature is not greater than 60degrees Celsius, accelerating an ethanol to volatilize and concentrate. In the step (4), the removing solvent adopts fractionating heating mode, promoting obtaining hawthorn and dandelion extract. In the step (5), the granulation adopts medlar extract and dandelion extract and honey wrapping mode.
   ACTIVITY - Metabolic.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.)

TI Use of mineral blend comprising first component of            natural ground calcium carbonate and/or precipitated            calcium carbonate and second component of            surface-reacted calcium carbonate, as cosmetic agent            for wet cosmetic composition.
AU SHARMA L
   BUDDE T
   LANDER S
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2021D93836
AB    NOVELTY - Use of a mineral blend comprising: a first                component comprising natural ground calcium                carbonate and/or precipitated calcium carbonate;                and a second component comprising a surface-reacted                calcium carbonate, which is a reaction product of                natural ground calcium carbonate or precipitated                calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and at least                one hydronium ion donor, and the carbon dioxide is                formed in situ by the hydronium ion donor treatment                and/or is supplied from an external source, as                cosmetic agent for a wet cosmetic                composition.
   USE - The mineral blend is useful as: cosmetic agent                for a wet cosmetic composition which is used as                makeup product, preferably lip make-up product, eye                make-up product or facial make-up product, nail                care product, lip care product, skin care product,                hair care product, hair styling product, hair                coloring product, deodorant, hair remover, make-up                remover, baby care product, sun protection product,                tanner product, feminine hygiene product, bath                product, facial cleaning product, hair cleaning                product, skin cleaning product, soap product, or                oral care product; absorbing fluids and fragrance                booster for decreasing skin friction, modifying the                skin feel, and/or modifying the skin appearance;                and replacement for talc or talc-containing                materials (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mineral blend: improves the sensory                properties of a wet cosmetic composition e.g. the                skin appearance, skin feel, skin smoothness and/or                the applicability of the wet cosmetic composition;                can replace talc or talc-containing material; is                obtained from natural sources and is suitable for                organic cosmetics; and provides wet cosmetic                composition that is less greasy and less sticky,                and exhibits a good spreadability and dries                fast.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                wet cosmetic composition comprising a mineral blend                comprising the first component, and the second                component.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mineral blend                  comprises 1-99 wt.%, preferably 70-90 wt.% the                  first component, and 1-99 wt.%, preferably 10-30                  wt.% the second component. The first component                  is: a natural ground calcium carbonate comprising                  marble, chalk and/or limestone; and/or a                  precipitated calcium carbonate comprising                  precipitated calcium carbonates having an                  aragonitic, vateritic and/or calcitic crystal                  form. The hydronium ion donor comprises                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid,                  phosphoric acid, citric acid, oxalic acid, an                  acidic salt, acetic acid and/or formic acid,                  preferably the hydronium ion donor is                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid,                  phosphoric acid, oxalic acid, phosphoric acid,                  being at least partially neutralized by a cation                  including lithium ion, sodium ion and/or                  potassium ion, hydrogenphosphate, being at least                  partially neutralised by a cation selected from                  lithium ion, sodium ion, potassium ion, magnesium                  ion, and/or calcium ion, preferably the hydronium                  ion donor is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid,                  sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid and/or oxalic                  acid, and preferably, the hydronium ion donor is                  phosphoric acid. In the mineral blend: the first                  component has a volume median particle size d50                  of 0.1-50 mu m, preferably 0.8-8 mu m, and/or a                  specific surface area of 0.5-30 m2/g, preferably                  2-15 m2/g, measured using nitrogen and the                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) method; and/or the                  second component has a volume median particle                  size d50 of 0.5-50 mu m, preferably 3-10 mu m,                  and/or a specific surface area of 15-200 m2/g,                  preferably 30-90 m2/g, measured using nitrogen                  and the BET method. The surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate is associated with at least one active                  agent comprising agents, biologically active                  agents, disinfecting agents, preservatives,                  vitamins, flavoring agents, surfactants, oils                  and/or fragrances. Preferred Composition: The wet                  cosmetic composition comprises at least 15 wt.%,                  preferably at least 30 wt.% a water. The wet                  cosmetic composition is free of talc or a                  talc-containing material. The wet cosmetic                  preparation further comprises at least one                  additive comprising bleaching agents, thickeners,                  stabilizers, chelating agents, preserving agents,                  wetting agents, emulsifiers, emollients,                  fragrances, colorants, flavours, oils, skin                  tanning compounds, antioxidants, minerals,                  pigments, UV-A and/or UV-B filter. The wet                  cosmetic preparation is in form of a paste, an                  ointment, a cream, a gel, a lotion, a solution,                  an emulsion or a solid form. The wet cosmetic                  composition comprises 0.1-50 wt.%, preferably 3-6                  wt.% the mineral blend.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of skin cream                composition and deodorant composition that do not                support the claims.

PN CN113698983-A
TI Method used for reducing plasticizer of Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, involves opening valves to form series-parallel mixed flow operation, and obtaining spore oil product with greatly reduced plasticizer from separation kettle.
AU HUANG S
AE ANHUI JINZHAI XIANZHILING BIOTECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021E2220C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment which includes a mixer whose upstream is mixed by a carbon dioxide pump and a carrier pump. The mixer is connected to the bottom of the first extraction kettle (1) through a pipeline with valve (A1). The mixer is connected to the bottom of the second extraction kettle (2) through a pipeline with valve (A2). The mixer is connected to the bottom of the third extraction kettle (3) through a pipeline with valve (A3). The mixer is connected at the bottom of the fourth extraction kettle (4) through a pipeline with valve (A4). The broken Ganoderma lucidum spore powder is divided into four grades. All valves are opened to form a series-parallel mixed flow operation, the extraction time is 2h, and the spore oil product with greatly reduced plasticizer is obtained from the separation kettle I.
   USE - Method used for reducing plasticizer of Ganoderma lucidum spore oil used in industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for various impurities and spore powder raw materials with high moisture content, has wide adaptability, and avoids the drawbacks that multiple operations such as extraction, transfer, separation are required to obtain the final qualified spore oil product. The circulation and condition control of each extraction kettle and separation kettle are more convenient, and is convenient to switch various states quickly.

PN US2021355369-A1
TI Generated foam used for recovering hydrocarbons from hydrocarbon-containing formation, comprises aqueous wellbore fluid comprising surface active agent package, thickener comprising biopolymer and aqueous base fluid, and gas chosen from carbon dioxide, methane and nitrogen.
AU ALMATOUQ H
   ALABBAD M
   ALYOUSIF Z
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021D0361F
AB    NOVELTY - A generated foam comprises an aqueous wellbore fluid comprising 1-20 gpt (gallons per thousand gallons) surface active agent package comprising an alpha-olefin sulfonate, a terpenoid and isopropyl alcohol, 0.01-0.3 wt.% thickener comprising a biopolymer and an aqueous base fluid, and a gas chosen from one or more of carbon dioxide, methane and nitrogen. The gas is present in an amount such that the generated foam has a quality of 50-99%.
   USE - Generated foam used for recovering hydrocarbons from hydrocarbon-containing formation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The generated foams has an increased viscosity, increased stability under high temperature and pressure conditions, and highly suitable for use in downhole environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) Recovery of hydrocarbons from a hydrocarbon-containing formation, which involves injecting (100) the wellbore fluid comprising the surface active agent package, the thickener and the aqueous base fluid into the hydrocarbon-containing formation, injecting (110) the gas that mixes with the wellbore fluid into the hydrocarbon-containing formation, generating (120) a foam in the formation, introducing (130) a fluid into the hydrocarbon-containing formation, for displacing hydrocarbons from the hydrocarbon-containing formation, and recovering (140) the hydrocarbons,
   (2) Preparation of aqueous wellbore fluid, which involves mixing the surface active agent package, the thickener and the aqueous base fluid,
   (3) Enhancing the recovery of hydrocarbons from a hydrocarbon-containing formation, which involves injecting the wellbore fluid into the hydrocarbon-containing formation, injecting the gas into the hydrocarbon-containing formation, and generating the foam in the formation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart depicting an enhanced oil recovery (EOR) process.
   Injecting wellbore fluid (100)
   Injecting gas (110)
   Generating foam (120)
   Introducing fluid into hydrocarbon-containing formation (130)
   Recovering hydrocarbons (140)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The surface active agent package comprises 20-30 wt.% alpha-olefin sulfonate, 20-30 wt.% isopropyl alcohol, and 10-20 wt.% terpenoid.Preferred Components: The thickener is hydroxyethylcellulose. The terpenoid is citrus terpenes. The wellbore fluid and the gas are co-injected. The gas is injected after the wellbore fluid. The hydrocarbon-containing formation comprises a zone of high permeability and a zone of low permeability.Preferred Properties: The wellbore fluid has viscosity of 30 cP or less at 25degrees Celsius and 20 cP or less at 50degrees Celsius. PN CN113493215-A
TI Preparation of active magnesium hydroxide used for e.g. flame retardant, involves weighing magnesium oxide and distilled water to prepare magnesium oxide solution, placing in water bath, stirring, heating, sequentially adding sodium hydroxide and stearic acid, vacuum-filtering and drying.
AU ZHANG L
   LI Z
   ZHOU B
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 2021C03152
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of active magnesium hydroxide involves (i) respectively weighing magnesium oxide and distilled water and preparing a magnesium oxide solution with a mass fraction of 6.25%, (ii) placing the magnesium oxide solution in a water bath, heating at a temperature of 90degrees Celsius and stirring to obtain a magnesium oxide-magnesium hydroxide mixture slurry, (iii) transferring the mixture slurry to a quartz glass carbonized tube, heating in a water bath at 30degrees Celsius, and passing carbon dioxide into the quartz glass carbonized tube, until the pH of the mixed solution is 7 to obtain a white slurry of magnesium carbonate trihydrate, (iv) heating the white slurry of magnesium carbonate trihydrate at 80degrees Celsius with stirring in a water bath, sequentially adding sodium hydroxide and dispersant, preferably stearic acid to obtain a white active magnesium hydroxide slurry, and (v) vacuum-filtering the active magnesium hydroxide slurry, transferring to an oven and drying at 80degrees Celsius for 24 hours.
   USE - Preparation of active magnesium hydroxide in the form of stacked flakes (claimed) used for flame retardant, analytical reagent, heat insulation coating, wastewater treatment and other applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical preparation of active magnesium hydroxide with high purity, no pollution, no impurity, simple raw material, and high yield, under mild reaction condition. The active magnesium hydroxide has high industrial production value and scientific research prospect. The active magnesium hydroxide has high heat resistance, small grain diameter, large surface area and other special points. The method uses carbon dioxide gas to replace usual industrial production method of ammonia gas, so as to reduce the pollution to the environment and reduce the production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The addition amount of stearic acid is 5 %mass with respect to amount of magnesium oxide. The stearic acid needs to be dissolved in a mixed solution of ethanol with a mass fraction of 22.3% and sodium hydroxide with a mass fraction of 7%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further involves extracting produced by-product sodium carbonate by solid-liquid separation, such as vacuum filtration. The by-product sodium carbonate is used in industrial production of calcium carbonate.Preferred Composition: The addition amount of sodium hydroxide is 200 %mass with respect to amount of magnesium oxide.Preferred Property: The particle size of the active magnesium hydroxide is 0.1-0.3 microm.

PN JP2021147252-A
TI Activated carbon for raw water treatment, has specific pore volume calculated by Barrett, Joyner and Halenda (BJH) method from nitrogen adsorption isotherm, and specific pore volume calculated by density-functional theory (DFT) analysis from carbon dioxide adsorption isotherm.
AU YAMAMOTO T
   NAKADA H
   HITOMI M
   KITATOMI H
   NISHIDA M
AE KURARAY CO LTD (KURS-C)
GA 2021B1366N
AB    NOVELTY - Activated carbon has a ratio ((B)/(A)) of the pore volume (A) calculated by the Barrett, Joyner and Halenda (BJH) method from the nitrogen adsorption isotherm, to the total pore volume (B) calculated by density-functional theory (DFT) analysis from the carbon dioxide adsorption isotherm of 1.6-7.
   USE - The activated carbon is used for raw water treatment, and is used for suppressing a musty odor (all claimed). The activated carbon is preferably powdered activated carbon, and is used for adsorbing a musty odor-causing substance such as 2-methylisoborneol (MIB) even in the presence of humic acid. The activated carbon is useful for water purification applications. The liquid to be treated contains 2-MIB, and includes raw water, tap water, industrial water, wastewater, and domestic drinking water. The activated carbon is used for applying to the raw water treatment in a water purification facility, and is used for the raw water from a water source such as a river or lake water stored in a tank in a wet state, for purification.
   ADVANTAGE - The activated carbon is capable of adsorbing 2-methylisoborneol (MIB) well even in the presence of humic acid. The activated carbon is capable of satisfactorily adsorbing a musty odor-causing substance such as 2-MIB even in the presence of humic acid. The activated carbon, preferably powdered activated carbon, is useful because the musty odor can be satisfactorily suppressed by adding or supplying it to the raw water of the water purification facility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for suppressing a musty odor, involving treating the liquid to be treated with the above-mentioned activated carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The specific surface area (C) of the activated carbon calculated by the Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) method from the carbon dioxide adsorption isotherm is 860-1500 m2/g. The total pore volume (B) of the activated carbon calculated by DFT analysis from the carbon dioxide adsorption isotherm is 0.3 ml/g or more. The activated carbon has an average particle size of 5-15 mu m.
   EXAMPLE - A carbonized coconut shell charcoal as raw material, was put into a fluidized activation furnace to heat to 1000 degrees C. The steam activation was performed under the condition of a water vapor partial pressure of 50% so that the iodine adsorption amount of the coconut shell charcoal after the activation treatment was 1500 mg/g. Then, the activated coconut shell charcoal was pulverized into powder while adjusting using a pulverizer to obtain powdered activated carbon having an average particle size of 12 mu m.

PN CN113234472-A; CN216972439-U
TI Pure oxygen ethylene cracking reaction system has            combustion section connected with oxygen conveying            pipeline and fuel conveying pipeline, and cracking gas            quencher connected to discharging port of cracking            section.
AU LIU Y
   ZHU G
   ZHANG Q
   LI H
   ZHU H
   YU X
   HAN C
   JIN X
   JIN Y
   CHI B
AE SHANGHAI HUANQIU CONTRACTING & ENG CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI YUNYAO THERMAL ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI YUNYAO THERMAL ENERGY TECHNOLOG (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021983927
AB    NOVELTY - System has a cracking furnace having a                combustion section. The feeding port of the                combustion section is connected with an oxygen                conveying pipeline and a fuel conveying pipeline.                The combustion section is used for burning the                introduced oxygen and fuel, and generating high                temperature flue gas comprising carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide, hydrogen and water vapour. The                inlet of the cracking section is connected with the                high temperature flue gas outlet of the raw                material conveying pipeline and the combustion                section. The cracking section is used for mixing                the introduced raw material and high temperature                flue gas to react and generating pyrolysis gas. A                cracking gas quencher is connected to a discharging                port of the cracking section. The cracking gas                quencher is used for quenching and cooling the                pyrolysis gas generated by the reaction. A cracking                gas cooling device and a separating device are                further cooled and separated the cooled cracking                product.
   USE - Used as pure oxygen ethylene cracking reaction                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves the reaction yield,                increases the device processing amount and is                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                cracking pure oxygen ethylene by using the reaction                system comprising (i) introducing the oxygen and                fuel into the combustion section of the cracking                furnace for combustion, generating high temperature                flue gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide, hydrogen and water vapour, (ii) adding raw                material and high temperature flue gas into                cracking furnace cracking section for cracking                reaction, generating pyrolysis gas, and (iii)                quenching and cooling cracking gas by cracking gas                quencher, then further cooling and separating by                cracking gas cooling device and separating device,                separating the gas liquid phase reaction product                respectively to the downstream processing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the pure oxygen ethylene cracking reaction                system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fuel is fuel gas                  or fuel oil. The fuel gas comprises natural gas                  and refinery gas. The fuel oil comprises naphtha,                  hydrogenation tail oil, light diesel oil, heavy                  diesel oil and residual oil. The cracking raw                  material comprises hydrocarbon substance or                  distillate oil. The hydrocarbon substance                  comprises ethane, propane, butane. The distillate                  oil mainly comprises naphtha, raffinate oil,                  hydrogenation tail oil, light diesel oil and                  decompression diesel oil.

PN WO2021155081-A1; CN115038777-A
TI Producing alkyl salicylic acids for lubricating            composition, by contacting alkyl phenol with alkali            metal base compound and carbon dioxide, contacting            product with mineral acid, and removing solvent and            alkali metal salt of mineral acid from product.
AU SAMPLER E P
   DIFLAVIO J L
   WALKER G M
   ROSKI J P
   KIM H
AE LUBRIZOL CORP (LUBR-C)
GA 2021903388
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing alkyl salicylic acids,                involves (a) contacting an alkyl phenol with an                alkali metal base compound and carbon dioxide at a                temperature of 120-180 degrees C to form an alkyl                salicylic acid salt of the alkali metal base                compound, (b) contacting the product of step (a)                with a mineral acid, in the absence of an                additional non-polar hydrocarbon liquid having a                kinematic viscosity of less than 2.5 m2/second at                100 degrees C, where the mineral acid and the                alkali metal base compound form an alkali metal                salt of the mineral acid, and a solvent for the                mineral acid is present in an amount sufficient to                at least partially solubilize the alkali metal salt                of the mineral acid to produce an alkyl salicylic                acid and/or its salt, and (c) removing at least 50                wt.% of the solvent and the alkali metal salt of                the mineral acid from the product of step (b),                based on the total amount of solvent and alkali                metal salt present in the product of step                (b).
   USE - The process is used for producing alkyl                salicylic acids which are used in an overbased                detergent, and in a lubricating composition (all                claimed), which is used for lubricating an internal                combustion engine, preferably a gasoline-fueled                engine, such as a marine diesel cylinder lubricant                (MDCL) or a trunk piston engine oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The process controls temperature during                overbasing process to produce overbased detergents                with superior performance properties when compared                to conventional overbased detergents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a composition comprising at least one                derivative of the alkyl salicylic acid; and
   (2) a lubricating composition comprising the                alkyl salicylic acid, and the composition, or the                overbased detergent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mineral acid is a                  polyprotic mineral acid. The mineral acid                  comprises at least one of sulfuric acid,                  hydrochloric acid, or phosphoric acid. The alkali                  metal base compound is present in an amount of                  0.5-1.5 reactive equivalents based on the amount                  of the alkyl phenol. The mineral acid is present                  in an amount of at least 0.9 equivalents of                  strong acid, based on the amount of alkali metal                  base compound. The alkali metal base comprises                  sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, or lithium                  hydroxide. The alkaline earth metal                  oxide/hydroxide comprises calcium oxide, calcium                  hydroxide, magnesium oxide, or magnesium                  hydroxide. The group II metal oxide/hydroxide                  comprises zinc oxide. The alkaline earth metal                  oxide/hydroxide and/or group II metal                  oxide/hydroxide, or its reactive equivalent, is                  added in an amount of 1.1-8 reactive equivalents                  based on the alkyl salicylic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  involves (d) contacting the product of step (c)                  with an alkaline earth metal oxide/hydroxide                  and/or a group II metal oxide/hydroxide, or its                  reactive equivalent, carbon dioxide, and an                  alcohol, polyol, and/or (poly)ether, to produce                  an overbased detergent having a total base number                  of at least 130 mg potassium hydroxide/g on an                  active basis. The process involves performing                  heating at a temperature of 60-160 degrees C,                  optionally under vacuum. The step (c) involves                  (c1) heating the product of step (b) to a                  temperature to at least partially vaporize the                  solvent in the product of step (b) and forming a                  precipitate of the alkali metal salt of the                  mineral acid, followed by removal of the                  precipitate, optionally where the removal is via                  filtration, and/or (ii) performing liquid-liquid                  phase separation. Preferred Composition: The                  lubricating composition further comprises 30-95                  wt.% oil of lubricating viscosity, and optionally                  0.5-7 wt.% ashless succinimide dispersant, 0.1-2                  wt.% phosphorous-containing anti wear agent,                  0.1-5 wt.% ashless antioxidant, or 0.1-3 wt.%                  polymeric viscosity modifier. Preferred                  Components: The step (a) uses 10-50 wt.% diluent                  oil. The step (b) uses 0.1-70 wt.% solvent. The                  alkyl phenol comprises 4-substituted alkyl                  phenol, 2-substituted alkyl phenol, or                  2,4-disubstituted alkyl phenol. The step (d) uses                  5-30 wt.% alcohol, polyol, and/or (poly)ether.                  The alcohol, polyol, and/or (poly)ether comprises                  ethylene glycol or propylene glycol. The total                  base number of the overbased detergent is 130-600                  mg potassium hydroxide/g on an active basis. The                  overbased detergent has a metal ratio of                  1.5-10.
   EXAMPLE - The process describes the general preparation                of alkyl salicylic acids, so does not support the                claims.
DC D25 (Detergents - other than soap and used for cleaning (C11D)); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H07 (Lubricants and lubrication - this excludes self-lubricating surfaces e.g. PTFE coated surfaces and lubrication systems in general. The section includes lubricants of non-petroleum origin eg silicone oils (C10M).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN113135844-A
TI Extracting taurine from sea cucumber intestine involves cleaning sea cucumber intestine removing silt, adjusting water content, crushing, performing high pressure homogenizing crushed sea cucumber intestine, and adjusting pressure.
AU SHEN H
   SONG S
   CAI D
   CHEN H
AE DALIAN XINYULONG MARINE BIOLOGICAL SEED (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 2021862530
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting taurine from sea cucumber intestine involves cleaning sea cucumber intestine removing silt, adjusting water content is 30-60%, crushing, performing high pressure homogenizing the crushed sea cucumber intestine, adjusting the pressure to make the cell crushing rate reach more than 90% to obtain sea cucumber intestine high pressure homogenate, extracting the sea cucumber intestine oil by supercritical carbon dioxide system, collecting the sea cucumber intestine high pressure homogenate after degreasing, centrifuging the degreased sea cucumber intestine high pressure homogenate, collecting the supernatant, adding equal volume of water in the solid phase, vibrating and mixing to obtain secondary leaching liquor, centrifuging the obtained secondary leaching liquor again, and collecting the supernatant.
   USE - Method for extracting taurine from sea cucumber intestine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has does not require heating, has small water consumption and high extraction efficiency. The extraction method is simple and the taurine crystal purity is high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting taurine from sea cucumber intestine involves cleaning sea cucumber intestine removing silt, adjusting water content is 30-60%, crushing, performing high pressure homogenizing the crushed sea cucumber intestine, adjusting the pressure to make the cell crushing rate reach more than 90% to obtain sea cucumber intestine high pressure homogenate, extracting the sea cucumber intestine oil by supercritical carbon dioxide system, collecting the sea cucumber intestine high pressure homogenate after degreasing, centrifuging the degreased sea cucumber intestine high pressure homogenate, collecting the supernatant, adding equal volume of water in the solid phase, vibrating and mixing to obtain secondary leaching liquor, centrifuging the obtained secondary leaching liquor again, collecting the supernatant, combining the obtained supernatant by two times, adding organic solvent into the supernatant after combining to precipitate, standing at the proper temperature, collecting precipitate, re-dissolving the collected precipitate in water, standing at low temperature to crystallizing and collecting taurine crystal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polar solvent is selected from isopropanol, ethanol and methanol. Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the high-pressure homogenization is 50-150 bar. The supercritical carbon dioxide degreasing temperature is 25-50 degrees C, the pressure is 10-60 megapascal, the static extraction is 0.5-1.5 hours, and the dynamic extraction is 2-3 hours. The first stage separation temperature after supercritical carbon dioxide degreasing is 25-50 degrees C, and the separation pressure is 8-20 megapascal, the secondary separation temperature is 25-40 degrees C, and the separation pressure is 4-8 megapascal. The centrifugal force in the centrifugal separation of degreased sea cucumber intestinehigh pressure homogenate and secondary leaching liquor is 2500-8000 g, the time is 5-30 minutes, and the temperature is 4-5 degrees C. The recrystallization temperature of the taurine is 4-10 degrees C, and the recrystallization of the taurine is allowed to stand for 12-36 hours. The volume fraction of the isopropanol, ethanol and methanol in the solution is 10-60%.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)
MC B04-B04H; B10-A09B; B11-B03D

PT P
PN US2021214756-A1
TI Converting carbon source into lipid by culturing in liquid medium, first organism in presence of carbon source under aerobic condition to oxidize carbon source and culturing second organism in medium in presence of produced carbon dioxide.
AU STEPHANOPOULOS G
AE MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
GA 202192056L
AB    NOVELTY - Converting a carbon source into a lipid involves: (a) culturing in a liquid medium, a first organism in the presence of a carbon source under aerobic conditions suitable for the first organism to oxidize the carbon source where the conditions are oxidizing and conditions, where the first organism produces carbon dioxide (CO_2) as portion of the oxidation process; and (b) culturing in a liquid medium a second organism in the presence of CO_2 produced in (a) under anaerobic conditions suitable for the second organism to reduce the CO_2. The conditions are reducing conditions. The second organism produces a carbon substrate as portion of the reduction process.
   USE - The methods are useful for converting a carbon source into a lipid; and for producing lipids. The carbon substrate is used as a carbon source (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The economic viability of some of the methods depends on the rate of CO_2 fixation and acetate production. While typical reported volumetric rates are low, specific rates of CO_2 fixation by acetogens are reasonable and can be significantly enhanced by applying technologies of metabolic engineering and synthetic biology. Enhanced CO_2 fixation is accomplished by engineering the CO_2 fixation pathway in a CO_2 fixing microorganism, e.g. an acetogenic bacterium, to amplify carbon flux.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for producing lipids which involves providing CO_2 to a first organism in an anaerobic fermentor, where the first organism converts the CO_2 to a carbon substrate; removing the carbon substrate from the anaerobic fermentor; and providing the carbon substrate to a second organism in an aerobic fermentor, where the second organism converts the carbon substrate to a lipid or a precursor and CO_2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Culturing of (a) and of (b) is carried out in separate fermentors. The method further involves contacting the first organism with an oxidizing agent, optionally where the oxidizing agent is oxygen (O_2); contacting the second organism with a reducing agent, optionally where the reducing agent is hydrogen (H_2), carbon monoxide (CO), syngas, or hydrogen sulfide (H_2S); and providing electrons to the second organism of (b) or to the first organism by contacting the second organism of (b) or the first organism with an electric current, optionally where the electrical current is provided by at least one electrode. Producing lipids further involves removing the CO_2 from the aerobic fermentor; and providing the CO_2 to the first organism in the anaerobic fermentor.Preferred Components: The carbon source is a carbohydrate, optionally glucose, fructose, ethanol, butyrate, butanol, acetate, acetic acid, biomass, cellulose, or hemicellulose. The product of the carbon source oxidization process in (a) is a lipid, optionally an edible lipid, or a precursor. The carbon substrate produced in (b) is a biofuel or ethanol. The organism of (a) or the second organism is an oleaginous yeast, optionally Yarrowia lipolytica. The organism of (b) is an acetogenic bacterium, optionally Clostridium sp. bacterium, Clostridium acetobutylicum, Clostridium ljungdahlii, Clostridium carboxydivorans, or Clostridium autoethanogenum, Clostridium thermohydrosulfuricum, Clostridium thermocellum, or Clostridium thermoanaerofacter ethanoliticus. The organisms of (a) and/or (b) are genetically modified, optionally where the organism of (a) overexpresses a stearoyl-coenzyme A (CoA) desaturase gene. The first organism is a CO_2-fixing bacterium, optionally Clostridium sp. bacterium, Clostridium acetobutylicum, Clostridium ljungdahlii, Clostridium carboxydivorans, or Clostridium autoethanogenum, Clostridium thermohydrosulfuricum, Clostridium thermocellum, or Clostridium thermoanaerofacter ethanoliticus.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)
MC D05-C19; E10-G02B2; E11-M; E11-D02; E11-F06; E10-E04E2
IP C12P-007/16; C12P-007/54; C12P-007/10; C12M-001/00; C12P-007/64

PT P
PN JP2021090372-A; WO2021117593-A1; EP4074163-A1
TI Method for cultivating plant, involves spreading            nutrient solution which supplied gas containing carbon            dioxide in nutrient solution as gas and in which            micro-bubble is contained in leaf surface of            plant.
AU ANZAI S
AE ANZAI S (ANZA-Individual)
   ANZAI S (ANZA-Individual)
   ANZAI S (ANZA-Individual)
GA 2021654559
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a nutrient                solution supply apparatus (10) that is configured                to supply a nutrient solution to a plant. A                micro-bubble generation apparatus (20) is adapted                to supply gas as a micro-bubble in nutrient                solution supplied from nutrient solution supply                apparatus. A dispersion apparatus (30) is                configured to atomize the nutrient solution in                which the micro-bubble is contained. A control                apparatus (40) is configured to controls a nutrient                solution supply apparatus, a micro-bubble                generation apparatus, and a dispersion apparatus.                The nutrient solution is made in a mist state to                grow a plant. The nutrient solution which supplied                the gas containing oxygen in the nutrient solution                is spread as gas and in which the micro-bubble is                contained in the leaf surface of plant. The                nutrient solution which supplied gas containing a                carbon dioxide in the nutrient solution is spread                as gas after first process and in which                micro-bubble is contained in leaf surface of                plant.
   USE - Method for cultivating plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The efficient stimulation of growth of a plant                by supplying required gas to leaf surface is                achieved. The quality of the plant is improved. The                atomization of the nutrient solution to the leaf                surface of the plant easily is achieved. PN WO2021099216-A1; IN202247031645-A; CN114727767-A; EP4061203-A1; US2022330827-A1
TI Method for obtaining filtered capnography signal            by suppressing spectral background signal within            optical absorption signal obtained from breathing cycle            of subject, which involves obtaining optical absorption            signal.
AU VAN KESTEREN H W
   VANKESTEREN H W
AE KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
GA 202157275N
AB    NOVELTY - The method (600) involves obtaining (610) an                optical absorption signal from a subject across a                range of wavelengths, where the optical absorption                signal represents a proportion of a light signal                that is absorbed as the light signal passes through                a breathing adapter contains a respiratory air                sample undergoing investigation. The optical                absorption signal has a spectral background signal.                A second harmonic signal is isolated (620) from the                optical absorption signal and identified (630) as a                period of the spectral background signal. Multiple                wavelength components of the second harmonic signal                are sampled (640), where sampled second harmonic                signal has obtained (644) a sample of the second                harmonic signal at a central wavelength of the                second harmonic signal. The central wavelength                represents maximum carbon dioxide absorption.
   USE - Method for obtaining a filtered capnography                signal by suppressing a spectral background signal                within an optical absorption signal obtained from a                breathing cycle of a subject.
   ADVANTAGE - Method enables real-time carbon dioxide                measurements during respiration and improves the                accuracy of the spectral background period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a computer program comprises computer                program code unit which is adapted when the                computer program is run on a computer; and
   (2) a system for obtaining a filtered                capnography signal by suppressing a spectral                background signal within an optical absorption                signal, where the system comprises a processor,                where the processor is adapted to obtain an optical                absorption signal from a subject across a range of                wavelengths.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the                method.
   Method (600)
   Obtaining optical absorption signal                (610)
   Isolating second harmonic signal from the                optical absorption signal (620)
   Identifying the second harmonic signal as a                period of the spectral background signal                (630)
   Obtaining sampled second harmonic signal                (644)

PN CN112494371-A
TI Lotion useful for e.g. gentle moisturizing and soothing obstetric genital and whitening skin care, comprises e.g. polyoxyethylene sorbitol monolaurate, trehalose, malaca fruit oil, Aloe vera, disodium cocoamphoacetate and humectant.
AU SHI P
AE CHANGZHOU WUJIN PEOPLES HOSPITAL (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202128263P
AB    NOVELTY - Lotion comprises 10-15 pts. wt. polyoxyethylene sorbitol monolaurate, 7-10 pts. wt. trehalose, 6-8 pts. wt. malaca fruit oil, 8-10 pts. wt. Aloe vera, 2-4 pts. wt. disodium cocoamphoacetate, 3-5 pts. wt. yeast fermentation product extract, 5-8 pts. wt. humectant, 3-10 pts. wt. rose oil fragrance, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. sodium citrate, 3-5 pts. wt. cucumber viscous liquid, 5-10 pts. wt. deionized water, 3-5 pts. wt. egg gel, and 1-2 pts. wt. preservative.
   USE - The lotion is useful for gentle moisturizing and soothing obstetric genital (claimed) whitening skin, and reducing irritation.
   ADVANTAGE - The lotion: has enhanced functionality, and rose fragrance; is softer; and contains oil, which reduces irritation, and suitable for most skins: and also has improved adaptability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing gentle moisturizing and soothing obstetric obstetric genital lotion, (i) squeezing and filtering rose seeds to obtain rose seed oil, mixing fresh rose petals into rose seed oil after pretreatment, and stirring through stirring mechanism, placing stirred materials in carbon dioxide supercritical extraction equipment for extraction to obtain mixed essential oil, (ii) selecting fresh peeled cucumber, cutring into sections and placing it in blender after high-speed stirring, filtering out residue to obtain viscous liquid, placing viscous mixed liquid in carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device for extraction to obtain extracted viscous liquid, (iii) combining polyoxyethylene sorbitol monolaurate, trehalose, malaca fruit oil, Aloe vera, disodium cocoamphodiacetate, yeast fermentation product extract, humectant, mixing rose oil fragrance, sodium citrate, cucumber viscous liquid, deionized water, egg gel, and preservatives in specific weight ratio, and placing it in high-speed mixer and mixing for 30-60 minutes, and (iv) filling product obtained in step (iii) into packaging bag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The gentle moisturizing and soothing obstetrical genital lotion comprises 12 pts. wt. polyoxyethylene sorbitol monolaurate, 8 pts. wt. trehalose, 6 pts. wt. malaca fruit oil, 9 pts. wt. Aloe vera, 2 pts. wt. disodium cocoyl amphoteric diacetate, 3 pts. wt. yeast fermentation product extract, 5 pts. wt. humectant, 6 pts. wt. rose oil fragrance, 0.2 pts. wt. sodium citrate, 5 pts. wt. cucumber viscous liquid, 8 pts. wt. deionized water, 4 pts. wt. egg gel, and 1 pts. wt. preservative. The preservative includes phenoxyethanol, ethyl p-hydroxybenzoate, and/or propyl p-hydroxybenzoate. Preferred Components: The humectant agent comprises polysorbate 20, PEG-7 glycerol cocoate, lecithin, polyoxyethylene sorbitol monolaurate, polyoxyethylene castor oil, sucrose oleate, decyl glucoside, sodium lauroyl sarcosinate, sodium cocoamphoacetate, sodium cocoyl glutamate, PEG-40 hydrogenated castor oil, and/or PEG-6 caprylic/capric glyceride. The preparation of egg gel comprises select fresh egg whites, and extract free water in egg whites through a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, mix dewatered egg white and sodium hydroxide solution, and obtain egg white gel after being stirred by high-speed mixer. The mixing weight ratio of egg white and sodium hydroxide solution is 3:1. The humectant agent includes polysorbate 20, PEG-7 glycerol cocoate, lecithin, polyoxyethylene sorbitol monolaurate, polyoxyethylene castor oil, sucrose coconut oleate, decyl glucoside, sodium lauroyl sarcosinate, sodium cocoyl amphoteric acetate, sodium cocoyl glutamate, PEG-40 hydrogenated castor oil, and/or PEG-6 caprylic/capric glyceride. Preferred Method: In the step (i), rose petal pre-processing specifically includes (S11) soak fresh rose petals in clean water for 15-30 minutes and remove them, and soak rose petals in 3% salt water for 5-10 minutes and remove, (S12) cut rose petals into small pieces through cutting device, and (S13) mix rose petals cut into blocks with honey of 10% of weight of rose, fully stir and leave for 10-20 minutes, and finally rinse with clean water to obtain pretreated rose petals. In the step (ii), treatment of ciscous liquid specifically comprises (S21) rinse fresh cucumbers with high-pressure water to remove external residual impurities, (S22) place cucumber into hot water at 100 degrees C and soak for 30 seconds, quickly place cucumber into ice water for 30 seconds, and rub outer skin of cucumber by hand, (S23) cut cucumbers into chunks and place them in a blender for stirring and mixing, (S24) filter stirred materials once, and obtain filter residue, after soaking the filter residue in clean water for 10-15 minutes, place filter residue and clean water mixture into a mixer for stirring, and perform secondary filtration to obtain a viscous liquid, and (S25) place viscous mixed liquid into carbon dioxide supercritical extraction equipment for extraction to obtain extracted viscous liquid.
   ACTIVITY - Gynecological.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

TI Reaction system useful for integrated volatile organic compounds adsorption-catalytic combustion, comprises heating layer located around outer side of catalytic combustion chamber, and adsorbent located in adsorption chamber.
AU WANG Y
   SUN D
   DAI Y
   CHEN X
   LIU Y
   ZHAO Q
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202131289X
AB    NOVELTY - Reaction system comprises a catalytic combustion chamber (1), a left side adsorption chamber, a right side adsorption chamber, a heating layer, a flow equalization device (4), an air distribution plate (5), an adsorbent (6), a catalyst (7), a primary catalyst particle collecting device, a secondary catalyst particle collecting device, a vibrating device, a three-stage catalyst particle collecting device, a heat exchanger, a gas-water separating device, a carbon dioxide storage tank and a water storage tank. The heating layer is located around the outer side of the catalytic combustion chamber. The adsorbent is located in the adsorption chamber. The catalyst is located on the air distribution plate in the catalytic combustion chamber. The first-stage catalyst particle collecting device is located at the upper part of the catalytic combustion chamber. A gas-water separating device is respectively connected with a carbon dioxide (CO2) storage tank and a H2O storage tank.
   USE - The system is useful for integrated volatile organic compounds (VOCs) adsorption-catalytic combustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: realizes the VOCs adsorption, desorption, catalytic combustion process; ensures the continuity of the VOCs treatment process; reduces the leakage of VOCs during processing; realizes the fluidized bed catalytic combustion of VOCs by controlling the O2/air intake flow rate; improves the removal efficiency of VOCs; has recycle the heat generated in the process of catalytic combustion; separates and store CO2 and H2O produced in the process of catalytic combustion; saves energy; reduces costs; and reduces environmental pollution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the reaction system for integrated VOCs adsorption-catalytic combustion.
   Catalytic combustion chamber (1)
   Flow equalization device (4)
   Air distribution plate (5)
   Adsorbent (6)
   Catalyst (7)
