PT P
PN US2020197466-A1; US11478520-B2
TI Obtaining extract of Cannabis, used to treat e.g.            nausea, involves contacting Cannabis with carbon            dioxide as sole supercritical fluid solvent, repeating            contacting step, removing supercritical fluid solvent,            and isolating cannabinoids.
AU SPEIER G J
AE CURE PHARM HOLDING CORP (CURE-Non-standard)
GA 202056086G
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining at least one extract of Cannabis,                comprises: (a) contacting Cannabis with carbon                dioxide as the sole supercritical fluid solvent at                a pressure of at least 74 bar and at least 31                degrees C, for up to 8 hours to provide an extract                of Cannabis; (b) repeating step (a) for at least                one time to obtain multiple extracts of Cannabis;                (c) removing the supercritical fluid solvent from                the multiple extracts of Cannabis; and (d)                isolating cannabinoids from one of the extracts of                Cannabis and isolating terpenes from another                extract of Cannabis to yield an extract of Cannabis                enriched with cannabinoids, and an extract of                Cannabis enriched with terpenes.
   USE - The process is useful for obtaining at least                one extract of Cannabis enriched with cannabinoids                or terpenes (all claimed), where the extract is                useful for treating a mammal afflicted with a                disease or disorder including neuroinflammation,                epilepsy, Alzheimer's disease, oxidative injury,                vomiting and nausea, anxiety, anorexia, arthritis,                schizophrenia, multiple sclerosis, joint                inflammation, joint pain, symptoms associated with                AIDS, chronic pain, acute pain, neuropathic pain                and spasticity, inflammatory bowel diseases (e.g.                Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis), tumor                neovascularization, tumor growth, cancer, glaucoma,                muscle spasms, severe pain, severe nausea, and                cachexia or dramatic weight loss or muscle atrophy                (wasting syndrome). No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides the extract of Cannabis,                which: is enriched with cannabinoids that contains                at least 10%, preferably at least 90% higher                concentration of cannabinoids, relative to the                Cannabis; enriched with terpenes that contains at                least 10%, preferably at least 90% higher                concentration of terpenes, relative to the                Cannabis; improves the onset of action, lowers the                dosing, and enhances safety profile; is more                stable, durable and quicker dissolving than many                other conventional dosage forms; and is                synergistic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The step (a) is carried                  out at: at a different temperature and/or a                  different pressure; a different temperature and                  the same pressure; or a different pressure and                  the same temperature. The step of isolating the                  cannabinoids from one of the extracts of Cannabis                  and the isolating the terpenes from another                  extract of the Cannabis is carried out by                  chromatography, adsorption, crystallization,                  distillation, liquid-liquid extraction,                  filtration, fractional distillation,                  precipitation, recrystallization, lyophilization                  (freeze drying) and/or sublimation.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Neuroprotective;                Anticonvulsant; Nootropic; Vulnerary; Antiemetic;                Tranquilizer; Anabolic; Eating-Disorders-Gen;                Antiarthritic; Neuroleptic; Anti-HIV; Analgesic;                Muscle relaxant; Gastrointestinal-Gen; Antiulcer;                Cytostatic; Ophthalmological; Muscular-Gen.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the dosage form is by                transdermal, oral, topical, intravenous,                intramuscular, buccal, sublingual, parenteral,                ophthalmic, or nasal, dermal route, or via                inhalation or infusion. No dosage details                given.

PT P
PN JP2020092681-A; JP6831977-B2
TI Vaporizer used in cultivation room, has two-fluid injection valve that is provided with gas ejection hole to eject gas to be main structure of jet flow, and provided with liquid ejection hole to eject liquid.
AU MATSUMOTO Y
   KOMATSU H
   IZAWA O
AE UNIV KOCHI PREFECTURAL PUBLIC CORP (UYKO-Non-standard)
   TOSA NOKI KK (TOSA-Non-standard)
GA 202054416Y
AB    NOVELTY - The vaporizer (1) has a combustion gas generator (3) that is provided to generate combustion gas. An injection hole injects a jet flow that entrains the generated combustion gas. A two-fluid injection valve (2) comprises gas ejection hole for ejecting a gas to be a main structure of the jet flow (11) and a liquid ejection hole ejects a liquid atomized in the gas. The heat of vaporization of the liquid ejected from the two-fluid injection valve absorbs portion or all of the heat released to the cultivation chamber of the combustion gas generator.
   USE - Vaporizer used in cultivation room for ensuring cultivation environment such as temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The stable room temperature environment is easily obtained, by making the amount of heat released from the combustion gas and the amount of heat of vaporization of the ejected liquid approximately the same. The temperature rise in the cultivation room is reduced by the heat exchange between the heat released from the combustion gas entrained in the jet from the two-fluid injection valve and the heat of vaporization in the process of changing the liquid from fine-grained droplets into steam. The combustion gas is efficiently entrained in the jet from the injection hole to perform the above heat exchange smoothly and efficiently and to diffuse carbon dioxide and water vapor throughout the cultivation room as quickly and uniformly as possible.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the vaporizer.
   Vaporizer (1)
   Two-fluid injection valve (2)
   Combustion gas generator (3)
   Main structure of the jet flow (11)
   Emission surface (24)
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC P13-A01A; P13-A01C; P13-A07

PN KR2020031965-A; KR2101022-B1
TI Microalgae crushing and separation device using ultrasound, has ultrasonic destruction unit that destroys cell walls of microalgae by applying ultrasonic waves to microalgal culture medium flowing from microalgal incubator, and gas supply.
AU JEONG S H
AE UNIV CHOSUN IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (CHOS-C)
GA 202023943J
AB    NOVELTY - Microalgae crushing and separation device using ultrasound comprises ultrasonic destruction unit (10) that destroys cell walls of microalgae by applying ultrasonic waves to microalgal culture medium flowing from microalgal incubator (1). A lipid separating unit (30) is used for separating lipid layer from discharged material of ultrasonic destruction unit. The ultrasonic destruction unit and extraction tank are connected to incubator and connection unit. Ultrasonic generating means are used for generating ultrasonic waves into interior of extraction tank. A pulse electric field portion is used for applying pulse electric field to culture medium moving along connection unit. A gas supply is used for injecting carbon dioxide into culture medium moving along connecting unit. The pulse electric field unit includes electrode installed in connection unit, power unit for supplying power to electrode, and controller for controlling pulses and electric fields generated through electrode.
   USE - Used as microalgae crushing and separation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: separates and recovers lipids through single step extraction instead of conventional multi-step process extraction by extracting lipids through applying ultrasound to microalgae; separates and recovers lipids with low energy, low cost and simple process; provides pure lipids without chemical solvent treatment; extracts lipids from microalgae by separating lipids through destroying cell walls by applying a pulsed electric field and carbon dioxide together with ultrasonic waves; and is capable of automatically constructing a harvesting, destruction and separation system in conjunction with a microalgae incubator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the cell wall destruction and separation device of the microalgae (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Microalgal incubator (1)
   Ultrasonic destruction unit (10)
   Lipid separating unit (30)

PN CN110394027-A
TI Method for performing air separation, involves separating compressed air followed by utilizing radial-pressure swing adsorption tower, separating air using radial-pressure swing adsorption tower and utilizing cryogenic rectification device.
AU TIAN Y
   QIAO Y
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 201992767S
AB    NOVELTY - An air separation performing method involves separating compressed air (which is dried and decarbonized by radial-pretreatment adsorption tower) using radial-pressure swing adsorption tower, such that 30 -85% oxygen-enriched air flows out of the radial-pressure swing adsorption tower and obtaining low-pressure nitrogen as intermediate product, switching radial-pressure swing adsorption tower by program-controlled valve, regenerating radial-pressure swing adsorption tower that reach saturation adsorption by depressurization, separating air using radial-pressure swing adsorption tower, recycling radial-pressure swing adsorption tower followed by utilizing cryogenic rectification device, utilizing low-pressure nitrogen as purge gas (regeneration purge gas) for radial-pretreatment adsorption tower and realizing carbon dioxide-water adsorption and desorption cycles.
   USE - Method for performing air separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing air separation by performing large scale pressure swing adsorption and cryogenic separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An air separation performing method involves separating compressed air (which is dried and decarbonized by radial-pretreatment adsorption tower) using radial-pressure swing adsorption tower, such that 30 -85% oxygen-enriched air flows out of the radial-pressure swing adsorption tower and obtaining low-pressure nitrogen as intermediate product, switching radial-pressure swing adsorption tower by program-controlled valve, regenerating radial-pressure swing adsorption tower that reach saturation adsorption by depressurization, separating air using radial-pressure swing adsorption tower, recycling radial-pressure swing adsorption tower, pressurizing 30-85% oxygen-enriched air at 0.5-0.7 MPa by oxygen-enriched compressor, passing into cryogenic rectification device, after performing heat exchange with low pressure nitrogen, separating liquid oxygen and liquid nitrogen, pressurizing using liquid oxygen pump and liquid nitrogen pump, obtaining high-pressure high-purity oxygen and nitrogen, utilizing low-pressure nitrogen as purge gas (regeneration purge gas) for radial-pretreatment adsorption tower and realizing carbon dioxide-water adsorption and desorption cycles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The radial-pretreatment adsorption tower comprises adsorbents, which are zeolite molecular sieve, activated alumina, activated carbon or silica gel. The radial-pressure swing adsorption tower comprises adsorbents, which are 5A molecular sieve, lithium X zeolite molecular sieve, lithium A zeolite molecular sieve, 13X molecular sieve or alkaline earth metal modified molecular sieve.

PN CN110327969-A
TI Catalyst for preparing low-carbon olefin by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises nitrogen-doped metal oxide, e.g. copper oxide, zirconium oxide and cerium oxide, and molecular sieve, e.g. SAPO-34, H-ZSM-5 or HY molecular sieve.
AU ZHANG L
   LI R
   YANG B
   DENG W
AE QILIHUA WUHAN RESOURCES & ENVIRONMENT (QILI-Non-standard)
GA 201987723P
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst for preparing low-carbon olefin by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (CO2), comprises a nitrogen-doped metal oxide selected from zinc oxide, copper oxide, zirconium oxide and cerium oxide, and a molecular sieve, e.g. SAPO-34, H-ZSM-5 or HY molecular sieve.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing low-carbon olefin by hydrogenation of CO2 on a continuous flow reactor of a pressurized fixed bed; and for carrying out pre-reduction treatment before reaction of CO2 hydrogenation synthesis of low-carbon olefin by using pressurized fixed bed continuous flow reactor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - By using the catalyst, the conversion rate of CO2 can reach greater than or equal to 10%. The selectivity of the low-carbon olefin in a hydrogenation product can reach greater than or equal to 80%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst for preparing low-carbon olefin by hydrogenation of CO2 (CO2), comprises MaN'b.
   M=a nitrogen-doped metal oxide selected from zinc oxide, copper oxide, zirconium oxide and cerium oxide;
   N'=a molecular sieve, e.g. SAPO-34, H-ZSM-5 or HY molecular sieve;
   a=20-70 mass%; and
   b=30-80 mass%.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing catalyst for preparing low-carbon olefin by hydrogenation of CO2, which involves preparing nitrogen doped metal oxide: placing metal oxide into a tubular furnace, introducing ammonia standard gas to carry out nitrogen doping, heating the tubular furnace for nitriding treatment, maintaining a constant time, and naturally cooling to prepare the nitrogen-doped metal oxide; preparing the nitrogen-doped metal oxide and the molecular sieve in a certain mass percentage; and mechanically mixing the nitrogen-doped metal oxide and the molecular sieve to prepare the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: Pre-reduction treatment step involves placing the catalyst in nitrogen and hydrogen atmosphere for reduction, where the pre-reduction treatment temperature is 350-450 degrees C, the pre-reduction treatment time is 0.5-5 hours, and the volume ratio of nitrogen to hydrogen is 80:20. An inert diluent is added in the reaction for carrying out CO2 hydrogenation synthesis of low-carbon olefin, the inert diluent is used for enabling low-carbon olefin synthesis reaction to be stable and beneficial to control, and selectivity is improved; the inert diluent is one of argon, nitrogen or helium, and the volume content of the inert diluent in the reaction system is 0-20 vol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The feeding rate of the ammonia standard gas is 80-400 ml/minute, the heating rate of the tubular furnace is 1-5 degrees C, the nitriding treatment temperature is 400-700 degrees C, and the nitriding treatment time is 3-5 hours. Preferred Conditions: The reaction pressure is 1.0-8.0 MPa, the reaction temperature is 200-400 degrees C, the volume space velocity of the reaction is 6000-40000/hour, and the molar ratio of hydrogen to CO2 is 2: 1-4:1.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E10-J02C3; E11-D02; E34-E02A; E35-A; E35-C04; E35-L; H04-E05; H04-E08; H04-F02E; H04-F05; H04-F06; J04-E01; J04-E04B; J04-E11; N02-D; N03-A02A; N03-B02; N03-F01; N04-A; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-B02

PN US2019290876-A1; WO2020131776-A1; WO2020131776-A8; CA3123864-A1; US2022072257-A1; US11376389-B2
TI Assembly for inserting e.g. catheter, into mouth            of patient for exchanging oxygen through breathing            circuit, has inserting cannula comprising proximal end,            where locking region of Bayonet Neill-Concelman is            configured to change color.
AU FULLER K L
   CARVER L M
   HILL M T
   HOTTA M M
   KRUGER L G
   WASELEWSKI A C
   WASELEWSKL A C
AE SMART BRIDGE MEDICAL INC (SMAR-Non-standard)
   SMART BRIDGE MEDICAL INC (SMAR-Non-standard)
GA 201982129A
AB    NOVELTY - The assembly has an inserting cannula                comprising a proximal end and a distal end, where                the distal end comprises multiple pins (3110)                extending laterally from a sidewall of the distal                end. An elbow adapter (EA) is configured to couple                the inserting cannula with a mechanical ventilator                for providing air to a patient. A pressure release                valve on the EA is configured to mitigate high                airway pressures that cause disconnections or lung                damage. A Bayonet Neill-Concelman (BNC) connector                (3111) is configured to secure the EA to the                inserting cannula, where a locking region of the                BNC is transparent or translucent and configured to                change color when the BNC is engaged with the                inserting cannula.
   USE - Assembly for inserting a tracheal tube e.g.                catheter, endotracheal tube and tracheostomy tube,                into a mouth or a nose of a patient for exchanging                oxygen and carbon dioxide through a breathing                circuit during tracheostomy.
   ADVANTAGE - The assembly realizes emergency disconnections                due to excessive force occur at locations other                than the EA. The assembly allows a user to twist                the pin through a channel as opposed to a friction                fit where twisting motion is not clearly indicated                so as to improve strength and usability of                connection and reduce force into stoma.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for utilizing a tracheal tube within a                patient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of an                assembly for inserting a tracheal tube into a mouth                or a nose of a patient for exchanging oxygen and                carbon dioxide through a breathing circuit during                tracheostomy.
   Outer cannula (3101)
   Inner cannula (3105)
   Extender (3109)
   Pins (3110)
   Connector (3111)

PT P
PN US2019170714-A1; WO2019113148-A1; EP3721193-A1; EP3721193-A4
TI Making color changeable indicator particles used in apparatus involves combining particulate organic polymer with color changeable indicator, adding solvent and forming dissolved solution and evaporating solvent to produce dry powder.
AU HEACOCK G L
   MILLS A
AE SENSOR INT LLC (SENS-Non-standard)
   SENSOR INT LLC (SENS-Non-standard)
   SENSOR INT LLC (SENS-Non-standard)
GA 201949639H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing color changeable indicator particles comprises: providing at least one particulate organic polymer having a melting of 25-200 degrees C; combining the polymer with at least one color changeable indicator; adding a solvent and forming a dissolved solution; and evaporating the solvent to produce a dry powder comprising particles with an organic polymer core.
   USE - The method is useful for producing color changeable indicator particles which are used in apparatus that is a medical device (claimed), for detecting ammonia and carbon dioxide and as food packaging and medical applications comprising disposable catheter, a disposable scalpel, a disposable syringe and/or a disposable ophthalmic lens through which a clinician looks view.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides color changeable indicator particles: that can be incorporated to the polymer materials while maintaining efficacy and stability of the indicator; that are simple, reliable, and cost effective detection means for detecting ammonia and carbon dioxide, enhance the safety, environmental friendliness, and visual effectiveness of the indicator. The color changeable composite is substantially free of particle aggregation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing a color changeable polymeric composite comprising coating organic polymer particles with at least one color changeable indicator to form color changeable indicator particles, combining the color changeable indicator particles with a second organic polymer to form a combined powder and extruding the combined powder;
   (2) the color changeable indicator particles produced by the method;
   (3) mixture comprising the indicator particles where the mixture further comprises a phase transfer agent or indicator particles and a second polymer; and
   (4) apparatus comprising disposable, limited, or restricted use device and color changeable polymeric composite and at least one color changeable indicator substantially homogeneously dispersed within the polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further comprises grinding or milling an organic core polymer to form the particulate organic polymer, adding a base or phase transfer agent to the dissolved solution, extruding the color changeable indicator particles to form an intermediate composite and cutting the intermediate composite to form masterbatch pellets, combining the masterbatch pellets with pellets of a second organic polymer having a melting point of 25-200 degrees C to form a combined mixture of pellets and extruding the combined mixture of pellets. The extrusion produces a first color changeable polymeric composite, further comprises breaking up the first composite to form pellets; and extruding the pellets to form the color changeable polymeric composite. Preferred Components: The particles have an average particle size of greater than 10 microns. The base is tetraoctyl ammonium (TOA), tetrabutyl ammonium (TBA), and/or cetyltrimethyl ammonium (CTMA). The organic polymer particles and the second organic polymer are extrusion-meltable. The organic polymer particles and the second organic polymer are comprises the same polymer. The extrusion occurs at a temperature of 50-200 degrees C. The color changeable indicator particle comprises: particulate organic core comprising a core polymer having a melting point below 200 degrees C; and at least one color changeable indicator coated on the polymer. The core polymer is polyethylene, polystyrene, polymethylmethacrylate, or polypropylene. The core polymer melts between about 120-180 degrees C. The color changeable indicator is a carbon dioxide-based indicator. The color changeable indicator is an ammonia-based indicator. The color changeable polymeric composite comprises at least one organic polymer and at least one color changeable indicator substantially homogeneously dispersed within the polymer where the polymeric composite. The composite substantially lacks particulates. The organic polymer is low density polyethylene (LDPE), polystyrene (PS), polymethylmethacrylate (PMMA), polypropylene (PP) and/or polycaprolactone (PCL). The polymeric composite is comprised of at least two organic polymers and the indicator is substantially homogenously dispersed within the polymers. The organic polymers are a core polymer and a bulk polymer32. The color changeable indicator is an acidic gas-based indicator or an alkaline gas-based indicator. The color changeable indicator is metacresol purple (MCP), thymol blue (TB), phenol red (PR) (all preferred), cresol red (CR), bromocresol green (BCG), bromophenol blue (BPB), chlorophenol red (CPR), and/or methylene blue (MB). The organic polymer is LDPE. The composite is in the form of a film, sheet or tube preferably film having a thickness of 10-250 microns. The composite is incorporated into or disposed on a ring. The composite is incorporated into or disposed upon the apparatus.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); ; E35; S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

   XIA Q

   JIANG Y
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)

GA 2018A0235J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves utilizing air temperature,                air humidity, carbon dioxide, soil temperature,                soil moisture, and time period data as input                variables and a corresponding vegetation coverage                index as an output variable. A sample set is                divided into a training set and a test set. A long                short-term memory (LSTM) network is utilized to                prevent over-fitting phenomenon. The training set                is utilized to train LSTM network to obtain an LSTM                network model. The air temperature, the air                humidity, the carbon dioxide, the soil temperature,                the soil moisture, and the time period of a field                planting environment are collected and stored in                real-time. Current real-time data information is                combined with historical time data information. A                vegetation coverage index is predicted by a user                using the LSTM model.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables predicting a vegetation                coverage rate within a set range by a user to                achieve early forecast effect, so that the user can                predict the vegetation coverage rate in advance and                reflect a difference in vegetation coverage and a                growth condition, thus allowing the user to plan                fertilizing and irrigation according to size of the                vegetation coverage rate, and hence improving plant                growth state operation and planting efficiency and                reducing planting cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                computer readable storage medium for storing a set                of instructions for predicting a field vegetation                coverage index by a processor of a computer                device.

PN DE102017205974-A1; FR3065077-A1; FR3065077-B1
TI Optical sensor device for measuring fluid concentration e.g. carbon dioxide (CO2) gas concentration, has deflection device that deflects measuring beam in way that measuring beam traverse multiple different absorption paths in sample space.
AU KRAUS V
   KOOP P
   PAUL K
   VIKTOR K
AE BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
   BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
GA 2018781912
AB    NOVELTY - The optical sensor device (1) has partially closed sample space (PB) for receiving the fluid to be analyzed. A radiation source (Q) is configured to emit the measurement beam into the sample space. A detector unit (D) is configured to detect the measuring beam after passing through the sample space. A movable controllable beam deflection device (MS) is arranged between the radiation source and the detector device to deflect the measuring beam in such a way that the measuring beam traverse multiple different absorption paths (AS1,AS1') in the sample space.
   USE - Optical sensor device for measuring fluid concentration e.g. carbon dioxide (CO2) gas concentration or liquid concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The optical paths are selected dynamically as required, by use of linear filter. Thus continuous or discrete optical paths are realized and a very compact structure is made possible for the different paths. Since the wavelength-selective filter such as linearly variable filter is used in the detector array, the problem of power distribution over the surface of the linear wavelength-selective filter is solved. Also the surface of the filter which distributes the power selectively as needed, is scanned without applying the same power to the entire surface. Hence the optical parameters of the system are improved and the new options are allowed in the detection of multiple properties of the fluid to be detected.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the schematic cross-sectional views of the optical sensor device.
   Optical sensor device (1)
   Absorption paths (AS1,AS1')
   Detector unit (D)
   Movable controllable beam deflection device (MS)
   Sample space (PB)
   Radiation source (Q)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Mixed gas contains water vapor. Water vapor concentration is 20-70 vol.%, hydrogen concentration is 10-40 vol.% and carbon dioxide concentration is 10-40 vol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Gas containing thermally decomposed product of organic substance discharged from reactor, which is fluidized bed reactor, is cooled to normal temperature or normal temperature vicinity. Portion of thermally decomposed product of organic substance contained in gas is liquefied. Liquid thermally decomposed product is made to adsorb to oil-component adsorption agent. In porositization process, waste plastic is melted and mixed, solidified body is porositized by cooling and solidifying, solidified body is heat-processed, and portion is volatilized. Crushing process of body is carried out before heat processing and/or after heat processing. Preferred Components: Organic substance is chosen from waste plastic, oil-impregnated sludge, waste oil and biomass. Oil-component adsorption agent is waste plastic by which porositization process is carried out.

GA 2018476437
AB    NOVELTY - The photo-bioreactor (10) has a gas supply portion (300) that is adapted for supplying air to a culture container (100). A gas discharging portion (400) is coupled to the culture container to discharge the air passed through the culture solution to the outside. A pump unit is connected to the culture container. A spray portion (600) is adapted for supplying the sprayed liquid to the culture container. A light source portion (200) is positioned on the outer side of the culture container. A circulation control unit (560) is adapted for controlling a pump unit (550), a control valve (511), a spray valve (521), a circulation valve (531) and a main valve (512). A cleaning portion is capable of cleaning the culture container. A cleaning liquid supply unit is adapted for supplying the cleaning liquid to a cleaning line. A cleaning valve is installed in cleaning line. The cleaning liquid supplied to cleaning line to clean the culture container.
   USE - Photo-bioreactor for culturing photosynthetic microorganism e.g. microalgae.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide remaining in the air after the first supply is supplied to the photosynthesis microorganism along with the culture liquid, thus improving the efficiency of the carbon dioxide supply. The function of culturing or cleaning is realized by adjusting the circulation rate and pressure of the fluid in the culture container. The temperature of the culture solution and the various culture modes is provided to improve the culture efficiency of the photosynthesis microorganism.

GA 201843779P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves readjusting (18) a setpoint of amount of nitrogen oxides based on completion of successive running distance periods. The periods are determined by integrating (11) the speed over a time period ending when a predetermined target cumulative amount of carbon dioxide released is reached. An amount of nitrogen oxides output per kilometer traveled is calculated (9) for each period based on cumulative amount of nitrogen oxides measured (8) at output and compared (17) with a target amount of nitrogen oxides per kilometer for calculating a deviation (16) used for readjustment.
   USE - Method for optimizing a removal of nitrogen oxides from gases in an exhaust line of internal combustion engine of car.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables optimizing the removal of nitrogen oxides from gases so as to increase the effectiveness of treatment of nitrogen oxides and to optimize fuel consumptions and reducing agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an assembly of exhaust line and a depolution control unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for optimizing a removal of nitrogen oxides from gases in an exhaust line of internal combustion engine.
   Step for measuring cumulative amount of nitrogen oxides (8)
   Step for calculating amount of nitrogen oxides output per kilometer traveled for each period (9)
   Step for integrating speed over time period ending when predetermined target cumulative amount of carbon dioxide released is reached (11)
   Step for calculating deviation for readjusting setpoint of amount of nitrogen oxides (16)
   Step for comparing amount of nitrogen oxides output per kilometer traveled with target amount of nitrogen oxides per kilometer (17)
   Step for readjusting a setpoint of amount of nitrogen oxides (18)

AB    NOVELTY - A nonaqueous electrolyte secondary battery                (100) consists of a negative electrode (2)                comprising negative electrode compound material                layer, a positive electrode (3) and a nonaqueous                electrolyte, accommodated in an exterior component                provided with a gas open structure. The negative                electrode compound material layer contains a                titanium-containing oxide and manganese. The                negative electrode compound material layer exhibits                X-ray-photoelectron-spectroscopy spectrum                comprising peaks with respect to carbon, peaks with                respect to titanium and peaks with respect to                manganese. The ratio of abundance-ratio (c1) of                carbon comprising carbon trioxide portion and                abundance-ratio of manganese is 3-50. The ratio of                abundance-ratio (c1) with respect to                abundance-ratio of sum of abundance-ratio of carbon                comprising carbon monoxide portion and                abundance-ratio of carbon comprising carbon dioxide                portion of 0.5-5.
   USE - Nonaqueous electrolyte secondary battery is                used for two-wheeled vehicle, four-wheeled vehicle                and portable electronic device.
   ADVANTAGE - The nonaqueous electrolyte secondary battery                is safe, and has excellent input-output                characteristics and deformation resistance, high                capacity retention ratio and low resistance                value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The peaks with respect to carbon comprise                peaks with respect to carbon trioxide portion,                peaks with respect to carbon dioxide portion and/or                peaks with respect to carbon monoxide portion. The                abundance-ratio of manganese with respect to                abundance-ratio of titanium in the                X-ray-photoelectron-spectroscopy spectrum is                0.01-0.2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partially cutaway                perspective view of the nonaqueous electrolyte                secondary battery.
   Negative electrode (2)
   Positive electrode (3)
   Negative electrode terminal (5)
   Positive electrode terminal (6)
   Nonaqueous electrolyte secondary battery                (100)

TI Sulfur-solubilizing agent for sulfur deposition treatment in hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide system, comprises main agent, solvents, and corrosion inhibitor, where main agent comprises Lewis base, dimethyl sulfoxide, and water.
AU NIU X
   ZHOU L
   SONG X
   TENG D
   TENG H
AE CNOOC TIANJIN CHEM DESIGN & RES INST CO (CNOO-C)
   CNOOC ENERGY TECHNOLOGY & SERVICES LTD (CNOO-C)
   CENERTECH TIANJIN CHEM RES & DESIGN INST (CNOO-C)
GA 2018334174
AB    NOVELTY - The sulfur-solubilizing agent comprises 70-80% main agent, 15-25% solvents, and 2.5-7.5% corrosion inhibitor. The main agent comprises 30-40% Lewis base, 50-65% dimethyl sulfoxide, and 3-10% water. The co-solvent is a mixture of N,N-dimethylacetamide and ethanolamine, or a mixture of N,N-dimethylacetamide and hexamethylphosphoramide, or their mixtures. The corrosion inhibitor is an amide imidazoline quaternary ammonium salt compound (I).
   USE - Sulfur-solubilizing agent for sulfur deposition treatment in hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide system.
   ADVANTAGE - The sulfur-solubilizing agent efficiently dissolves sulfur by virtue of synergy between different components, and has wide application temperature range, low toxicity and environmental protection without offensive odor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The sulfur-solubilizing agent comprises 70-80% main agent, 15-25% solvents, and 2.5-7.5% corrosion inhibitor. The main agent comprises 30-40% Lewis base, 50-65% dimethyl sulfoxide, and 3-10% water. The co-solvent is a mixture of N,N-dimethylacetamide and ethanolamine, or a mixture of N,N-dimethylacetamide and hexamethylphosphoramide, or their mixtures. The corrosion inhibitor is an amide imidazoline quaternary ammonium salt compound of general formula (I).

   (A) adding main solvent into reaction vessel with an auxiliary solvent with stirring uniformly; and

PN US2018044639-A1; US10428306-B2
TI Decontaminating bone tissue comprises contacting the bone tissue having contaminants in it with carbon dioxide (CO2) to decontaminate the bone tissue and form CO2 having contaminants and separating the contaminants from the collected CO2.
AU DECARO M
   WEI G
AE WARSAW ORTHOPEDIC INC (MEDT-C)
GA 2018137319
AB    NOVELTY - Decontaminating bone tissue comprises contacting the bone tissue having contaminants in it with carbon dioxide (CO2) to decontaminate the bone tissue and form CO2 having contaminants; collecting the CO2 having contaminants; and separating the contaminants from the collected CO2.
   USE - The methods and system are used for decontaminating bone tissue; purifying contaminated CO2 from contaminated bone tissue; and treating contaminated bone tissue with purified CO2 (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Automated, efficient methods of treating bone tissue are provided without compromising the integrity of desirable biomaterials present in bone tissue and at the same time reduce costs associated with the use of large amounts of CO2, reduce or even eliminate human error, and provide product consistency while improving the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) purifying contaminated CO2 from contaminated bone tissue by collecting contaminated CO2 from CO2 treated contaminated bone tissue; separating contaminants from the collected contaminated CO2 to obtain purified CO2; and
   (2) a system for treating contaminated bone tissue with purified CO2, comprising a bone tissue chamber configured for holding contaminated bone tissue and receiving purified CO2 into the bone tissue chamber and evacuating contaminated CO2 from the bone tissue chamber; a purified CO2 supply configured for supplying purified CO2 to the bone tissue chamber to decontaminate the bone tissue; a collection chamber configured to receive contaminated CO2 from the bone tissue chamber and evacuate contaminated CO2 from the collection chamber; and a purification chamber configured to receive contaminated CO2 from the collection chamber and to remove contaminants from the contaminated CO2 by a purification material to obtain purified CO2, the purification chamber configured to evacuate the purified CO2 and supply it to the bone tissue chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method of decontaminating bone tissue comprises treating the contaminated bone tissue with ethanol gradient dehydration. The method further comprises flushing with liquid CO2 the contaminated bone tissue treated with ethanol gradient dehydration; and subjecting the contaminated bone tissue to critical point dehydration and/or supercritical CO2 treatment; and releasing the CO2 at a controlled rate, where the temperature and pressure of CO2 is raised to or above 31.1 degrees C and 1100 psi, and where supercritical CO2 treatment is carried out at 105 degrees C and 7000 psi. The method further comprises removing the contaminated CO2, purifying the contaminated CO2 with supercritical fluid, liquefying the purified CO2 and using liquid purified CO2 as a cooling fluid. The contaminated CO2 is separated by bubbling through water and/or an organic solvent to remove contaminants comprising lipids, disease causing pathogens, viruses or bacteria. The method further comprises treating the CO2 bubbled through water and/or the organic solvent with acid treatment, filtering and liquefying the CO2, where the liquefied CO2 is purified to 99.9% free of lipids, disease causing pathogens, viruses or bacteria. The method further comprises generating processing charts including vanishing interfacial tension and CO2 pressure. The bone tissue comprises bone fibers, bone chips, bone particles, bone matrices or their mixture. The method further comprises providing a delivery vehicle for the bone tissue having contaminants, the delivery vehicle comprising a carrier or covering. The method of decontaminating the bone tissue is a multiple batch process. In the method for purifying contaminated CO2 from contaminated bone tissue, the contaminated CO2 is separated from the contaminants by bubbling the contaminated CO2 through water and/or an organic solvent to remove the contaminants comprising lipids, disease causing pathogens, viruses or bacteria. The method further comprises treating the CO2 bubbled through water and/or the organic solvent with acid treatment, filtering and liquefying the CO2. The liquefied CO2 is purified to 99.9% free of lipids, disease causing pathogens, viruses or bacteria.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Cellulose acetate asymmetric membrane used in e.g. gas separation, is prepared by forming membrane using casting solution comprising cellulose acetate, solvent, polyamidoamine and additive, washing, drying, and coating with silicone rubber.
AU JI P
   HU Y

AE DALIAN EUROFILM IND CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 2018105996
AB    NOVELTY - A cellulose acetate asymmetric membrane is prepared by uniformly mixing cellulose acetate, a solvent (A), polyamidoamine and additive, obtaining casting solution, performing vacuum degassing for 12-48 hours, and non-solvent phase inversion process, obtaining a wet cellulose acetate asymmetric membrane, washing with running water for 24-48 hours, and with a solvent (B), drying, and coating the resultant dried membrane with a silicone rubber. The silicone rubber is at least one of polydimethylsiloxane, polymethyloctylsiloxane or polymethylphenylsiloxane. The content of additive is 0-40 %mass.
   USE - Cellulose acetate asymmetric membrane is used in deacidification, and desorption or separation of oxygen, hydrogen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide/methane (all claimed) in natural gas, and recovery of hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The cellulose acetate asymmetric membrane has wide application range and high toughness, and excellent carbon dioxide/methane selectivity and permeability, and can be prepared by a simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cellulose acetate asymmetric membrane is prepared by uniformly mixing cellulose acetate, a solvent (A), polyamidoamine and additive, obtaining casting solution, performing vacuum degassing for 12-48 hours, and non-solvent phase inversion process, obtaining a wet cellulose acetate asymmetric membrane, washing with running water for 24-48 hours, and with a solvent (B), drying, and coating the resultant dried membrane with a silicone rubber. The silicone rubber is at least one of polydimethylsiloxane, polymethyloctylsiloxane or polymethylphenylsiloxane. The content of additive is 0-40 %mass with respect to total content of the casting solution. The additive is chosen from magnesium perchlorate, formamide, water, methanol, ethanol, lactic acid, maleic acid, maleic anhydride, acetic acid, acetic anhydride, zinc chloride, lithium chloride, polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyethylene glycol, oxalic acid, and glycerol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solvent (B) is methanol, ethanol, isopropyl alcohol or n-hexane. The content of solvent (A) is 40-85 %mass with respect to total content of the casting solution. The solvent (A) is chosen from 1,4-dioxane, acetone, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, chloroform, dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran and N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The casting solution comprises 10-30 %mass cellulose acetate and 0.1-5 %mass polyamidoamine. The cellulose acetate is chosen from cellulose diacetate or cellulose triacetate.

TI Recovery and purification of nitrous oxide in exhaust gas discharged during e.g. adipic acid production, used in LCD preparation, involves pre-treating nitrous oxide gaseous mixture, adsorbing impurities, compressing, and distilling.
AU KIM J H
   LEE S B
   PARK I J
   HA J
   KANG H C
   CHANG B J
   LEE K W
   YOOK S H
   LEE S G
   PARK B R
   HA J W
   KIM J

   PARK I
   KANG H
   CHANG B
   LEE G
   YOOK S

AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 201807417G
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery and purification (100) of nitrous oxide involves pre-treating (10) gaseous mixture or exhaust gas containing nitrous oxide to remove moisture in fine droplet state, oil in fine droplet state, and/or fine particle impurities, performing membrane separation (20) to remove nitrogen or/and oxygen in the pre-treated gaseous mixture, adsorbing (30) at least one impurity chosen from carbon dioxide, nitrogen dioxide, water vapor and organic hydrocarbon in the resultant gaseous mixture, compressing and cooling the mixture, and separating and recovering the gaseous mixture by distillation (40).
   USE - Recovery and purification of nitrous oxide in nitrous oxide-containing gaseous mixture or exhaust gas discharged during adipic acid production process, nitric acid production process, caprolactam production process and nitrous oxide production process (all claimed) by pyrolysis of ammonium nitrate, used in preparation of semiconductor, LCD and organic LED.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient recovery and purification of nitrous oxide with recovery rate of 90% or more, and purity of 99.999 vol.% or more.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovery and purification of nitrous oxide involves pre-treating gaseous mixture or exhaust gas containing nitrous oxide to remove moisture in fine droplet state, oil in fine droplet state, and/or fine particle impurities, performing membrane separation to remove nitrogen or/and oxygen contained in the resultant pre-treated gaseous mixture, to concentrate nitrous oxide, adsorbing at least one impurity chosen from carbon dioxide, nitrogen dioxide, water vapor and organic hydrocarbon in the resultant gaseous mixture, compressing and cooling the mixture, and separating and recovering the gaseous mixture by distillation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the recovery and purification of nitrous oxide.
   Pretreatment process (10)
   Membrane separation process (20)
   Adsorption process (30)
   Distillation process (40)
   Recovery and purification of nitrous oxide (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The recovery and purification method further involves decomposing the nitrous oxide contained in the gas mixture discharged into air during the membrane separation step and distillation step through thermal decomposition or catalytic decomposition. The adsorption step is carried out using an adsorbent chosen from sodium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, adsorbent coated with silica or zeolite, silica, zeolite, aluminosilicate and activated carbon. The distillation step is carried out in a packed tower filled with a filler made of a metal or ceramic material, or a multi-stage tray tower.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The separation membrane comprises polysulfone resin and/or polyimide resin.

PN CN107535595-A
TI Shelf-life prolonging plastic packaging method by cleaning picked or refrigerated chestnut, removing peel and endothelium, adding vitamin C or citric acid browning agent to chitosan solution, soaking, drying and packaging in box or bag.
AU HE Y
AE TIANJIN ENBOHUA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018041368
AB    NOVELTY - Shelf-life prolonging plastic packaging method comprises cleaning picked or refrigerated chestnut, removing chestnut peel and endothelium, adding 0.05-0.08% vitamin C or 0.1-0.2% citric acid browning agent to 1.5% chitosan solution to obtain coating agent, soaking chestnut kernel in coating agent for 60 seconds, drying, packaging in plastic packaging box or packaging bag with coating film embedded with silicon window at silicon window area to kernel mass ratio of 6-10 cm2/kg, keeping at 20 degrees C, heat sealing polypropylene film, and keeping at room temperature or low temperature.
   USE - Method for shelf-life prolonging plastic packaging (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is more convenient, low equipment investment, simply operation and low cost, saves time and labor, greatly prolongs freshness of product, and improves chestnut quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Shelf-life prolonging plastic packaging method comprises cleaning picked or refrigerated chestnut, removing chestnut peel and endothelium to obtain chestnut kernel, adding 0.05-0.08% vitamin C or 0.1-0.2% citric acid browning agent to 1.5% chitosan solution to obtain coating agent, soaking chestnut kernel in coating agent for 60 seconds, drying, packaging in plastic packaging box or packaging bag with coating film embedded with silicon window at silicon window area to kernel mass ratio of 6-10 cm2/kg, keeping at 20 degrees C, relative humidity of 90%, silicone rubber film oxygen transmittance of 4.58x 109 mol-second1m2Pa1, carbon dioxide transmittance of 24.24x 109 mol-second1m2Pa1, initial gas oxygen volume of 5-7% and carbon dioxide volume of 3-5%, heat sealing polypropylene film, and keeping at room temperature or low temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The plastic packaging box or packaging bag is made of polystyrene or polyethylene.

TI Separating cracking gas comprises e.g. boosting            the cracked gas by using a compressor, cooling the            compressed gas, transferring to the absorption tower            and providing the absorbent enters from the top of the            absorption tower.
AU ZHANG J
   LUO S
   LIAO L
   LI D
   GUO L
   LIU Z
   SHAO H
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP BEIJING CHEM (SNPC-C)
GA 201957273T
AB    NOVELTY - Separating cracking gas comprises e.g.                boosting the cracked gas by using a compressor,                cooling the compressed gas, and transferring to the                absorption tower, providing the absorbent enters                from the top of the absorption tower, absorbs the                C2 fraction and more recombination in the gas, the                top stream of the absorption tower enters the                re-absorption unit or cooling, and transferring the                stream of the tower to the desorption tower,                providing the tower tank stream from the absorption                tower enters the desorption column, the top of the                desorber tower to obtain the carbon dioxide                concentrated gas, and the tower tank obtains a lean                solvent, cooling the lean solvent, and returning to                the top of the absorption tower, providing the                carbon dioxide concentrated gas obtained from the                top of the desorption tower enters the purification                unit for purification treatment, providing the                purified carbon dioxide concentrated gas enters the                demethanizer, returning the top material of the                demethanizer.
   USE - The method is useful for separating cracking                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, high recovery rate and                low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating cracking gas comprises (i) boosting                the cracked gas by using a compressor, (ii) cooling                the compressed gas, and transferring to the                absorption tower, (iii) providing the absorbent                enters from the top of the absorption tower,                absorbs the C2 fraction and more recombination in                the gas, the top stream of the absorption tower                enters the re-absorption unit or cooling, and                transferring the stream of the tower to the                desorption tower, (iv) providing the tower tank                stream from the absorption tower enters the                desorption column, the top of the desorber tower to                obtain the carbon dioxide concentrated gas, and the                tower tank obtains a lean solvent, cooling the lean                solvent, and returning to the top of the absorption                tower, (v) providing the carbon dioxide                concentrated gas obtained from the top of the                desorption tower enters the purification unit for                purification treatment, (vi) providing the purified                carbon dioxide concentrated gas enters the                demethanizer, returning the top material of the                demethanizer to the compressor section or sent to                the boundary zone, and transferring the tower                kettle material to the deethanizer tower, (vii)                using the tower material from the demethanizer                enters the deethanizer, the top of the deethanizer                to obtain crude ethylene gas, transferring to the                ethylene rectification tower, and using the                de-ethanation tower as the liquefied gas product or                sending to a depropanizer, and (viii) providing the                top material from the deethanizer enters the                ethylene rectification column, and the top/side                line of the ethylene rectification column produces                a polymerization grade ethylene product, and the                tower kettle is an ethane-rich product, the method                includes drying, carrying out between step (ii) and                step (iii), or between step (v) and step (vi). An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for pyrolysis                gas separating device, comprising a compressor, a                cooler, an absorbing tower, a desorption tower, a                purifying unit connected in turn, a demethanizer,                deethanizer tower and ethylene rectifying tower,                where the pyrolysis gas separating device comprises                a drying unit, the drying unit is set between the                cooler and the absorption tower, or set between the                purification unit and the demethanizer. The tower                kettle is connected with the desorption, optionally                resorbable unit connected on the tower top, the                resorption unit preferably comprises re-absorption                tower and gasoline stabilization tower, the ethane                removing tower connected ethylene rectifying tower,                a tower kettle optionally connected depropanization                tower and the propylene rectification tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The de-ethanol column                  material is sent to the depropanizer, the method                  further comprises: depropanation: the tower                  material from the deethanizer enters the                  depropanizer column, and the crude propylene                  stream is obtained at the top of the column,                  transferring to the propylene rectification                  tower, and the tower kettle material is produced                  as a liquefied gas product, propylene                  purification: the crude propylene stream enters                  the propylene rectification column, and the                  polymerization grade propylene product is                  produced at the top/side line, and the column                  reactor is a propane-rich product. In the step                  (i), the cracking gas repressurization to 3-5                  MPag. In the step (i), the compressed gas after                  cooling to 5-15&#176; C. The step of the                  absorption tower overhead stream entering the                  reabsorption unit comprises: reabsorption: the                  absorption tower overhead stream enters the                  reabsorption tower, the resorbent enters from the                  top of the reabsorption tower, and the absorption                  tower top stream is sent to the fuel gas system,                  the tower kettle stream is taken as a product or                  enters the gasoline stabilization tower to                  recover the resorbent, when the tower tank stream                  of the reabsorption tower enters the gasoline                  stabilization tower, the method further                  comprises: (3-2) recovering the reabsorbent: the                  gasoline stabilized tower tower tank recovers the                  lean resorbent, and after cooling and cooling,                  returns to the top of the reabsorption tower, the                  gasoline stabilization tower overhead stream                  returns to the top of the absorption tower. The                  method further comprises stripping the                  hydrocarbons cooled between the compressor                  sections and returning the stripped light                  components to the compressor inlet. The                  theoretical number of the absorption tower is                  40-50, the operating pressure is 3-5 MPa, and the                  temperature at the top of the tower is                  5-20&#176; C, the theoretical number of                  plates of the desorption column is 20-60, the                  operating pressure is 1-3 MPa, and the                  temperature at the top of the column is                  10-40&#176; C, the theoretical number of the                  demethanizer is 15-30, the operating pressure is                  1- 3 MPa, and the temperature at the top of the                  column is -20 to -40&#176; C, the theoretical                  number of the deethanizer is 25-80, the operating                  pressure is 1.5-6 MPa, and the temperature at the                  top of the column is -10 to -40&#176; C, the                  theoretical number of plates of the ethylene                  rectification column is 80-120, the operating                  pressure is 1-3 MPa, and the temperature at the                  top of the column is -10 to -40&#176; C, the                  theoretical number of plates of the reabsorption                  column is 10-40, the operating pressure is 2-4                  MPa, and the temperature at the top of the column                  is 20-50&#176; C. The theoretical number of                  plates of the depropanizer is 25-80, and the                  operating pressure is 0.1-4 MPa. The theoretical                  number of plates of the propylene rectification                  column is 80-280, and the operating pressure is                  0.1-4 MPa. Preferred Components: In the step                  (iii), the absorbent is carbon three fractions                  with propane, butane, isobutane, butene fraction,                  or carbon five fraction containing pentane, and                  isopentane. The liquid absorption tower overhead                  stream obtained after the cooling process is to                  return to the absorption tower, the gas phase is                  sent to fuel gas system or a hydrogen recovery                  system. The antiresorptive agent is gasoline,                  heavy naphtha or aromatic hydrocarbon raffinate                  oil. In the step (iv), the desorbing tower is                  introduced with an absorbent as a                  supplement.

PT P
PN CN109593580-A; CN109593580-B
TI Semi-coke furnace exhaust gas fine desulfurization and adiabatic methanation by carrying out gas-liquid separation of methanation product gas, carrying out methanation reaction, removing carbon dioxide, and removing nitrogen gas.
AU YAN B
   HOU N
   GONG W
   LV J
AE WISON ENG CHINA CO LTD (WISO-Non-standard)
GA 201935211Q
AB    NOVELTY - Semi-coke furnace exhaust gas fine desulfurization and adiabatic methanation comprises carrying out gas-liquid separation of a methanation product gas; preheating a top gas (21) in a final methanation feed preheater (22), sending to a final adiabatic methanation reactor (203) to carry out methanation reaction to obtain a final methanation product gas (24), cooling in the final methanation feed preheater and a cooler (25), sending to a third separation tank (26) for gas-liquid separation, removing carbon dioxide from crude product gas (28) in a carbon dioxide removal unit (31), and removing nitrogen gas in a nitrogen removal unit (32).
   USE - Method for semi-coke furnace exhaust gas fine desulfurization and adiabatic methanation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids setting a hydrogenation desulfurization unit separately before the methanation reaction, thus shortening the process flow, and reducing equipment investment and energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Semi-coke furnace exhaust gas fine desulfurization and adiabatic methanation comprises:
   (A) pre-purifying a raw material gas (1) in a raw material gas preheater (2), mixing with a water vapor (3), using as a sulfur-tolerant shift reactor feed (4) to a sulfur-tolerant shift reactor unit to perform water vapor shift reaction to obtain a sulfur-tolerant shift reactor product gas (5), cooling in a first cooler (6), sending to a first separation tank (7) for gas-liquid separation, preheating top gas in a fine desulfurization reactor feed preheater (9), using as a fine desulfurization reactor feed (10) in a fine desulfurization reactor unit to perform fine desulfurization reaction, and removing inorganic sulfur and organic sulfur from the fine desulfurization reactor feed in an inorganic sulfur removing catalyst bed layer and an organic sulfur removing catalyst bed layer to obtain a fine desulfurization reactor product gas (11);
   (B) mixing the fine desulfurization reactor product gas with a first-stage adiabatic methanation reactor temperature-controlled steam (12), and using as a first-stage adiabatic methanation reactor feed (13) to an N-stage adiabatic methanation reactor unit to perform methanation reaction to form a methanation product gas (17);
   (C) sending the methanation product gas to a second separation tank (19) for gas-liquid separation; and
   (D) preheating a second separation tank top gas (21) in a final methanation feed preheater (22), sending to a final adiabatic methanation reactor (203) to carry out methanation reaction to obtain a final methanation product gas (24), cooling in the final methanation feed preheater and a second cooler (25), sending to a third separation tank (26) for gas-liquid separation, removing carbon dioxide from crude product gas (28) in a carbon dioxide removal unit (31), and removing nitrogen gas in a nitrogen removal unit (32).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a process flow diagram of the method for semi-coke furnace exhaust gas fine desulfurization and adiabatic methanation.
   Raw material gas (1)
   Raw material gas preheater (2)
   Water vapor (3)
   Sulfur-tolerant shift reactor feed (4)
   Sulfur-tolerant shift reactor product gas (5)
   First cooler (6)
   First separation tank (7)
   Fine desulfurization reactor feed preheater (9)
   Fine desulfurization reactor feed (10)
   Fine desulfurization reactor product gas (11)
   First-stage adiabatic methanation reactor temperature-controlled steam (12)
   First-stage adiabatic methanation reactor feed (13)
   Methanation product gas (17)
   Second separation tank (19)
   Second separation tank top gas (21)
   Final methanation feed preheater (22)
   Final methanation product gas (24)
   Second cooler (25)
   Third separation tank (26)
   Crude product gas (28)
   Carbon dioxide removal unit (31)
   Nitrogen removal unit (32)
   Final adiabatic methanation reactor (203)

PT P
PN CN109006672-A
TI Internet-based method for hatching chickens, involves fumigating crack-free fertilized eggs with mixed solution of formalin and potassium permanganate, washing with incubation solution, transferring to incubator and monitoring in real time.
AU WAN X
AE ZHAOQING HI-TECH ZONE TIANMU NEW ENERGY (ZHAO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4576E
AB    NOVELTY - Internet-based method for hatching chickens, involves selecting crack-free fertilized eggs with smooth eggshell surface, immediately placing in a low temperature environment of 3-5 degrees C for 2-3 hours, maintaining at 34-35 degrees C and 60-65% humidity for 20-30 minutes, fumigating with 25 ml/m3 mixed solution of formalin and potassium permanganate in weight ratio of 3:2 for 3 hours, washing with 0.7-0.9 %mass incubation solution, air-drying, transferring the eggs to incubator for incubation, controlling temperature in incubator to 38.3 degrees C for 1-3 days, 38 degrees C for 4-7 days, 37.8 degrees C for 8-12 days, 37.6 degrees C for 13-16 days and 37.4 degrees C for 17-19 days, providing temperature sensor, humidity sensor and carbon dioxide concentration sensor in incubator, connecting to controller, connecting controller and mobile phone by Wireless-fidelity (Wi-Fi) or fourth generation (4G), and monitoring incubation in real time through mobile phone.
   USE - The internet-based method is useful for hatching chickens.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves hatching rate and survival rate, ensures feeding scale of chicken, and increases income of farmers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Internet-based method for hatching chickens, involves selecting crack-free fertilized eggs with smooth eggshell surface, immediately placing in a low temperature environment of 3-5 degrees C for 2-3 hours, maintaining at 34-35 degrees C and 60-65% humidity for 20-30 minutes, fumigating with 25 ml/m3 mixed solution of formalin and potassium permanganate in weight ratio of 3:2 for 3 hours, washing with 0.7-0.9 %mass incubation solution, air-drying, transferring the eggs to incubator for incubation, controlling temperature in incubator to 38.3 degrees C for 1-3 days, 38 degrees C for 4-7 days, 37.8 degrees C for 8-12 days, 37.6 degrees C for 13-16 days and 37.4 degrees C for 17-19 days, controlling humidity to 60% for 1-5 days, 70% for 6-10 days, 75% for 11-15 days, and 65% for 16-19 days, during 1-7 days after hatching, controlling concentration of carbon dioxide in incubator to 1000 ppm and continuously irradiating with violet light for 1 hour at 100 Lux, during 8-15 days after hatching, controlling concentration of carbon dioxide in incubator to 500 ppm and continuously irradiating with violet light for 0.8 hour at 80 Lux, controlling temperature to 38.7 degrees C for 1-3 days after hatching, 38.2 degrees C for 4-11 days and 37.5 degrees C for 12-20 days and humidity to 65% for 1-8 days after hatching, and 73% for 7-20 days, controlling concentration of carbon dioxide in incubator to 1500 ppm for 1-13 days after hatching and continuously irradiating with violet light for 1.2 hours at 150 Lux, controlling concentration of carbon dioxide in incubator to 100 ppm for 14-20 days after hatching and continuously irradiating with violet light for 0.5 hour at 100 Lux, providing temperature sensor, humidity sensor and carbon dioxide concentration sensor in incubator, connecting to controller, connecting controller and mobile phone by Wireless-fidelity (Wi-Fi) or fourth generation (4G), and monitoring incubation in real time through mobile phone.
DC P14 (Animal management and care (A01K, L, M).); W01 (Telephone and Data Transmission Systems)

PT P
PN CN108217700-A
TI System useful for preparing battery grade lithium carbonate, comprises a pulping device, carbonization equipment and pyrolysis equipment, filtration equipment, acidification equipment, concentration equipment, and impurity removal equipment.
AU LI P
   SHAO R
   BI D
   LIU Y
   WANG B
   PAN F
AE ANHUI KEDA JIENENG CO LTD (KEDA-C)
GA 201853395V
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a pulping device, carbonization equipment and pyrolysis equipment. The system further comprises filtration equipment, acidification equipment, concentration equipment, impurity removal equipment, lithium sink equipment, and electrolysis equipment. The pulping device, the carbonization device, the pyrolysis device and the filtering device are sequentially connected to perform pulping, carbonization, pyrolysis and filtration, respectively, and the pulping device is provided with a raw material inlet. The carbonization device is respectively subjected to pyrolysis and acidification by being connected to the pyrolysis device and the acidification device through a first solid-liquid separation device, and the carbonization device performs solid-liquid separation by a first solid-liquid separation device. The filtering device is connected to the acidizing device.
   USE - The system is useful for preparing battery grade lithium carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes waste resources generated in battery level lithium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - System comprises a pulping device, carbonization equipment and pyrolysis equipment. The system further comprises filtration equipment, acidification equipment, concentration equipment, impurity removal equipment, lithium sink equipment, and electrolysis equipment. The pulping device, the carbonization device, the pyrolysis device and the filtering device are sequentially connected to perform pulping, carbonization, pyrolysis and filtration, respectively, and the pulping device is provided with a raw material inlet. The carbonization device is respectively subjected to pyrolysis and acidification by being connected to the pyrolysis device and the acidification device through a first solid-liquid separation device, and the carbonization device performs solid-liquid separation by a first solid-liquid separation device. The filtering device is connected to the acidizing device. The acidizing device, the concentrating device, the impurity removing device and the lithium sinking device are sequentially connected to acidify, concentrate, remove impurities and sink lithium respectively. The water outlet of the concentrating device is connected to the raw material inlet of the pulping device. The carbon dioxide outlet of the pyrolysis device is respectively connected to a carbon dioxide inlet of the carbonization device and a carbon dioxide inlet of the lithium sink device. The lithium sinking device is respectively connected to the pulping device and the electrolysis device through a second solid-liquid separation device for slurrying and electrolysis. The lithium sink device performs solid-liquid separation by a second solid-liquid separation device, and the sulfuric acid outlet of the electrolysis device is connected to the sulfuric acid inlet of the acidification device. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the battery grade lithium carbonate, comprising (i) mixing industrial grade lithium carbonate and water, and then slurrying to obtain a slurry, and introducing carbon dioxide into the slurry for carbonization to obtain a lithium hydrogencarbonate solution; (ii) subjecting the lithium bicarbonate solution to solid-liquid separation, and after solid-liquid separation, producing filter residue and filtrate, pyrolyzing the filtrate, collecting heat to interpret the emitted carbon dioxide, filtering the filtrate after pyrolysis to obtain a lithium carbonate wet material and a separation mother liquid I, drying lithium carbonate wet material and pulverizing to obtain finished battery grade lithium carbonate; (iii) mixing the filter residue and the separation mother liquid I and acidifying, acidifying, concentrating and performing impurity removal to obtain a concentrated liquid, adding carbon dioxide to the decontaminated concentrate to sink lithium, subsequently solid-liquid separation, producing crude lithium carbonate wet material and separating mother liquor II, returning the crude lithium carbonate wet material to the step A as a pulping raw material, and mixing with industrial grade lithium carbonate and water; and (iv) separating the mother liquid II for acidification, acidifying and performing electrolysis to obtain sodium hydroxide and sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid, returning to the acidification.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system for preparing battery grade lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The pulping device is a slurry tank, the carbonization equipment is a carbonization tower, and a slurry tank is arranged between the slurry tank and the carbonization tower. The first solid-liquid separation device is a centrifugal separator. The pyrolysis device is a decomposition tower, and a supernatant liquid tank is disposed between the first solid-liquid separation device and the pyrolysis device. The filtering device is an ultrafiltration membrane machine or a reverse osmosis membrane machine, and the filtration device is connected to the pulverizing device through a drying device. The drying device is a tray dryer or a belt dryer, and the pulverizing device is a jet mill or a hammer mill. The filtering device and the first solid-liquid separation device are connected to the acidizing device through a mother liquid tank. The acidification device is an acidification reactor. The concentration device is a double membrane method concentration device or an evaporation concentration device, the lithium sink device is a lithium sink reactor, and the second solid liquid separation device is a centrifugal separator. The electrolysis device is a bipolar membrane electrolysis cell. The impurity removing device is an ion exchanger, and a weakly acidic cation exchange resin is disposed in the ion exchanger. The weakly acidic cation exchange resin is a Li-type diacetic acid imino chelating resin or a weakly acidic acrylic cation exchange resin. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the reaction temperature of carbonization is less than 25 degrees C, the reaction pressure of carbonization is controlled in the range of 0.1-0.3 MPa, and the reaction time of carbonization is controlled in the range of 1.5-2.5 hours. In the step (ii), the reaction temperature of the pyrolysis is controlled in the range of 90-100 degrees C, and the reaction time of the decomposition is controlled in the range of 30-40 minutes. In the step (ii), the reaction temperature of the drying is controlled to be in the range of 120-150 degrees C, the reaction pressure of the pulverization is controlled within the range of 0.7-0.85 MPa, the acidification process to adjust the pH to 2-6 by adding sulfuric acid having a mass concentration of 50-70%, and the reaction time of acidification is controlled in the range of 10-40 minutes. In the step (iii), the reaction temperature of the precipitated lithium is less than 25 degrees C, the reaction pressure of the precipitated lithium is controlled within the range of 0.1-0.3 MPa, and the reaction time of the precipitated lithium is controlled within the range of 1.5-2.5 hours. In the step (iv), the current density of the electrolysis is controlled in the range of 500-800 A/m2.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded.
