PT P
PN EP4029924-A1
TI Integrating pyrolysis and gas or liquid cracking            by supplying hydrocarbon feed to pyrolysis unit,            pyrolyzing hydrocarbon feed, generating first product            stream having hydrogen and untreated hydrocarbons and            collecting second product stream.
AU 
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
GA 2022936830
AB    NOVELTY - Integrating (P1) pyrolysis and gas or liquid                cracking comprises: (a) supplying a hydrocarbon                feed to a pyrolysis unit; (b) pyrolyzing the                hydrocarbon feed, thus generating a first product                stream comprising hydrogen and untreated                hydrocarbons; (c) using the first product stream as                a fuel for at least one cracker furnace included in                a cracker unit; (d) collecting a second product                stream comprising hydrogen and hydrocarbons from                the cracker unit; and (e) supplying the second                product stream to the pyrolysis unit.
   USE - The process is useful for integrating                pyrolysis and gas or liquid cracking.
   ADVANTAGE - The process : reduces carbon dioxide emissions                from crackers for ethylene production, while                ensuring the utilization of by-products without                further carbon dioxide emissions and ensuring full                trackability of each molecule for carbon intensity                assessment; utilizes natural gas pyrolysis unit                produce converts mix of natural gas and other                hydrocarbons into hydrogen and solid carbon;                produces by-products from gas or liquid cracking                are blended with natural gas and used as feedstock                for pyrolysis unit to produce hydrogen; and                utilizes combination of pyrolysis unit and cracker                produces ethylene without direct carbon dioxide                emissions and allows full utilization of by                products without carbon dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) integrating (P2) pyrolysis and gas or                liquid cracking comprising (a2) supplying a first                hydrocarbon feed to a cracking unit, (b2) using the                first hydrocarbon feed to generate a product stream                comprising hydrogen and untreated hydrocarbons from                the cracker unit, (c2) supplying the product stream                to a pyrolysis unit, (d2) supplying a second                hydrocarbon feed to the pyrolysis unit, and (e2)                pyrolyzing the product stream and the second                hydrocarbon feed in the pyrolysis unit thus                generating a stream of hydrogen and solid carbon;                and
   (2) integrating (P3) pyrolysis and gas or                liquid cracking comprising (a3) supplying                electricity to provide power to many furnaces in a                cracking unit, (b3) generating a stream of hydrogen                and hydrocarbons from the cracking unit, (c)                supplying the stream of hydrogen and hydrocarbons                to a pyrolysis unit and (d) pyrolyzing the stream                of hydrogen and hydrocarbons in the pyrolysis unit,                thus generating a stream of hydrogen and solid                carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The process (P1)                  additionally comprises: (1a) collecting a second                  hydrocarbon stream comprising C3 and C4                  hydrocarbons from the cracker unit; and (1b)                  supplying the second hydrocarbon stream to the                  pyrolysis unit. The second product stream is                  collected from a cold train demethanizer section                  of the cracker unit. The second hydrocarbon                  stream is collected from a deethanizer section of                  the cracker unit. The stream of hydrogen and                  hydrocarbons is blended with a supplemental                  hydrocarbon stream prior to supplying the stream                  of hydrogen and hydrocarbons to the pyrolysis                  unit. Preferred Components: The cracker furnaces                  are gas crackers.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN114660573-A
TI Laser radar system for measuring concentration of carbon dioxide and methane column in atmosphere, has double-wavelength double-pulse laser whose output port is connected with input port of laser frequency stabilizing module.
AU CHEN C
   LI R
   ZANG H
   WANG M
   MA X
   ZHU X
   HOU X
   LIU J
   WU Z
   CHEN W
AE CAS SHANGHAI INST OPTICS & FINE MECHANIC (CAOF-C)
GA 202286930C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a photoelectric detection module (8) whose an output end is connected with an input end of a data collection and treated module (9). A light beam of a collimating mirror (7) enters the photoelectric detection module. A spectroscope reflects light to a narrow-band filter. An echo signal is reflected by a hard target through an optical receiving telescope (13). A relay optical module (12) enters the photoelectric detection module (8), where the spectroscope transmits the light in the echo signal into the narrow-band filter and the spectroscope is reflected to a narrow band filter and a detection optical unit and incident to a detector.
   USE - Laser radar system for measuring a concentration of carbon dioxide and methane column in an atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The laser radar system uses the active detection method, the laser with double-wavelength double-pulse can simultaneously 1572 nm and 1645 nm of the laser and the laser without relying on the sun reflection light, it can detect in full day, and the detection module before the two wavelengths has the corresponding narrow-band filter, can filter the influence of solar background noise and parasitic light, improve the detection precision. The system is capable of being used for foundation and vehicle, airborne and satellite-borne platform, it achieves for the synchronous measuring of atmospheric carbon dioxide and methane concentration in different coverage ranges, and it has a large range and high precision of the capability of measuring day.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the laser radar system measuring the concentration of carbon dioxide and methane column.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a laser radar system.
   Beam splitter four (1)
   Narrow-band filter one (2)
   Nm laser frequency stabilizing module (3)
   Nm laser frequency stabilizing module (4)
   Narrow band filter two (5)
   Integrating sphere (6)
   Collimating lens (7)
   Photoelectric detection module (8)
   Data collecting and treated module (9)

PN CN114538481-A
TI Preparation of industrial-grade lithium fluoride            for use in e.g. nuclear industry, involves mixing            industrial-grade lithium carbonate and water, reacting,            filtering, pyrolyzing filtrate returning to obtain            lithium carbonate, mixing with water, stirring with            hydrofluoric acid and filtering.
AU YUAN C
   WANG Q
   HUANG Z
   PAN L
   ZHAN D
   LIAO X
   WEI D
   REN H
   YAO L
AE JIANGXI FEIYU NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202275602A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of industrial-grade lithium                fluoride, involves (s1) mixing industrial-grade                lithium carbonate and water, introducing carbon                dioxide to react to obtain lithium bicarbonate                solution, (s2) filtering lithium bicarbonate                solution to obtain filtrate and returning lithium                carbonate that is not completely carbonized to step                (s1), (s3) passing the filtrate into resin to                remove impurity, (s4) pyrolyzing the filtrate to                obtain lithium carbonate and pyrolysis solution,                returning pyrolysis solution to step (s1), (s5)                stirring and washing the lithium carbonate and                water to obtain high-purity lithium carbonate, (s6)                mixing with water to obtain slurry, introducing                carbon dioxide, reacting to obtain refined lithium                bicarbonate solution, (s7) stirring, dripping 10-40                %mass hydrofluoric acid to form solid-liquid                mixture, and (s8) filtering, returning the filtered                water to step (s1), washing the filtrate, and                vacuum drying to obtain battery-grade lithium                fluoride.
   USE - Preparation of industrial-grade lithium                fluoride for use in nuclear industry, enamel                industry, and optical glass manufacturing                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy, and produces                qualified lithium fluoride product with yield of up                to 90% and stable quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of industrial-grade lithium                fluoride, involves (s1) mixing industrial-grade                lithium carbonate and water, introducing carbon                dioxide to react to obtain lithium bicarbonate                solution with pH of 8-14, (s2) filtering lithium                bicarbonate solution at least twice to obtain                filtrate and returning lithium carbonate that is                not completely carbonized to step (s1), (s3)                passing the filtrate into resin to remove impurity,                (s4) pyrolyzing the filtrate to obtain lithium                carbonate and pyrolysis solution, returning                pyrolysis solution to step (s1), (s5) stirring and                washing the lithium carbonate and water to obtain                high-purity lithium carbonate, (s6) mixing                high-purity lithium carbonate and water to obtain                slurry, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting under                pressure environment to obtain refined lithium                bicarbonate solution, (s7) continuously stirring                the refined lithium bicarbonate solution, dripping                10-40 %mass hydrofluoric acid to form solid-liquid                mixture, and (s8) filtering, returning the filtered                water to step (s1), washing the filtrate, and                vacuum drying to obtain battery-grade lithium                fluoride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of                  industrial-grade lithium carbonate and water is                  1:8-1:20. The liquid-solid ratio of lithium                  carbonate and water in step (s5) is 1.5:1-3:1.                  The ratio of high-purity lithium carbonate and                  water in step (s6) is 1:8-1:20.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The resin is weak                  base-type anion exchange resin.

AU SUI Z
   CHEN D
   LOU T
   WANG M
   SHEN F
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 202191289W
AB    NOVELTY - Smelting vanadium-titanium magnetite in blast furnace involves using hot air in blast furnace smelting vanadium-titanium magnetite which is combination of carbon dioxide (g) and oxygen (g) to form new type of hot air that is nitrogen-free, oxygen-rich, and carbon-rich, using oxygen as oxidant to participate in combustion reaction of carbon, converting carbon dioxide-rich furnace gas into high-concentration carbon monoxide to strengthen indirect reduction of iron oxides, carrying out blast furnace all-vanadium-titanium smelting under new hot air conditions to product high-titanium blast furnace slag and low-silicon-titanium high-vanadium hot metal, making into titanium concentrate, titanium-rich material, and higher-grade vanadium slag through steelmaking converter, and emitting carbon monoxide-rich and nitrogen-free blast furnace gas, which can be used as high-calorific value fuel mainly for heating blast furnace hot blast stove.
   USE - The method is used for smelting vanadium-titanium magnetite in blast furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short smelting cycle, high vanadium-titanium ore proportion, high iron-vanadium yield, titanium element resource utilization, byproduct efficient energy, and green advanced new technique of furnace gas recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Smelting vanadium-titanium magnetite in blast furnace comprises using hot air in blast furnace smelting vanadium-titanium magnetite which is combination of carbon dioxide (g) and oxygen (g) to form new type of hot air that is nitrogen-free, oxygen-rich, and carbon-rich, using oxygen as oxidant to participate in combustion reaction of carbon, converting carbon dioxide-rich furnace gas into high-concentration carbon monoxide to strengthen indirect reduction of iron oxides, carrying out blast furnace all-vanadium-titanium smelting under new hot air conditions to product high-titanium blast furnace slag and low-silicon-titanium high-vanadium hot metal, making into titanium concentrate, titanium-rich material, and higher-grade vanadium slag through steelmaking converter, emitting carbon monoxide-rich and nitrogen-free blast furnace gas, which can be used as high-calorific value fuel mainly for heating blast furnace hot blast stove, returning tail-gas to blast furnace hot blast stove, recombining with oxygen, and using remaining blast furnace gas for power generation or billet heating processes in enterprise.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content (volume) of carbon dioxide (g) is 0-78%, and the content of oxygen (g) is 22-100%, and the rest is carbon monoxide (g). The titanium dioxide content in high-titanium blast furnace slag is 10-40 wt.%. The vanadium content in vanadium-containing iron water is 0.20-0.60 wt.%. The titanium dioxide grade of recovered in new perovskite-type titanium concentrate from high-titanium blast furnace slag is 40-60 wt.%. The carrying amount of pulverized coal is 50-150 Kg/t iron. Preferred Method: The temperature of tuyere area is set at 1600-1800 degrees C. The new hot air temperature is controlled at 800-1200 degrees C, and pressure is 2-3 Kg. The hot air intake is controlled to 1400-2000 m3/t iron, and pulverized coal carrying capacity is 0-200 Kg/t iron. The vanadium-containing molten iron is blown in steelmaking process to obtain high-quality vanadium slag with upper limit of vanadium pentoxide content of 30%. The carbon monoxide-rich blast furnace gas discharging from top of blast furnace, has carbon monoxide (g) content of 30-70%, where temperature is 200-300 degrees C, pressure of 1-2 Kg, and rest is carbon dioxide (g). The third step adopts high-vanadium titanium magnetite blast furnace smelting, and the proportion of vanadium-titanium magnetite in charge ratio is 10-100%.

PT P
PN CN112760184-A
TI Preparing fermented olive wine by sequentially centrifuging three fermentation broths containing olive pulp enzymatic hydrolyzate and white granulated sugar, homogenizing it, filtering it, and sterilizing it.
AU ZENG L
   HE S
AE SICHUAN TIANYUAN OLIVE CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202154002B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing fermented olive wine involves (a) picking fresh olives, cleaning it and adding water to crush it to obtain olive fruit pulp, (b) adding pectinase and cellulase to olive fruit pulp for enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain enzymatic hydrolyzate and sterilizing it, (c) adding white granulated sugar and yeast fermentation broth for first fermentation to obtain a first fermentation broth, (d) adding brown sugar and yellow wine for secondary fermentation to obtain a secondary fermentation broth, (e) adding glutamic acid and sodium glutamate, shading, filling with carbon dioxide for sealing treatment, treating it in an aerobic environment, filling it with carbon dioxide and sealing it, (f) inoculating ethanol-resistant Pediococcus ethanolidurans to secondary fermentation broth processed to perform third fermentation to obtain third fermentation broth, and (g) sequentially centrifuging three fermentation broths, homogenizing it, filtering it, and sterilizing it.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fermented olive wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares fermented olive wine which has good quality, high active ingredient content, unique flavor and good market promotion prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing fermented olive wine involves (a) picking fresh olives, cleaning it and adding water to crush it to obtain olive fruit pulp, (b) adding pectinase and cellulase to olive fruit pulp for enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain enzymatic hydrolyzate and sterilizing it, (c) adding white granulated sugar and yeast fermentation broth for first fermentation to obtain a first fermentation broth, (d) adding brown sugar and yellow wine to the primary fermentation broth for secondary fermentation to obtain a secondary fermentation broth, (e) adding glutamic acid and sodium glutamate to the secondary fermentation broth, shading for 7-10 hours, filling with carbon dioxide at room temperature for sealing treatment for 2-6 hours, treating it in an aerobic environment for 3-5 hours, filling it with carbon dioxide and sealing it for 2-6 hours, (f) inoculating ethanol-resistant Pediococcus ethanolidurans to secondary fermentation broth processed to perform third fermentation to obtain third fermentation broth, and (g) sequentially centrifuging three fermentation broths, homogenizing it, filtering it, and sterilizing it to obtain a fermented olive wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves adding olive fruit to water in a weight ratio of 1-2:1-2. The step (b) involves adding 15-25 mg/l pectinase and 180-325 mg/l cellulase to olive fruit pulp, and performing enzymatic hydrolysis at 5-10 degrees C for 10-30 hours. The step (c) involves adding white granulated sugar in an amount of 0.3-0.6 times the weight of the olive fruit, adding yeast fermentation broth in an amount of 0.3-0.6 times the weight of the olive fruit, and performing first fermentation at 15-20 degrees C for 12-16 hours, and then at 25-28 degrees C for 8-12 hours. The step (d) involves adding brown sugar in an amount of 0.3-0.6 times the weight of the olive fruit, adding yellow wine in an amount of 1-2 times the weight of the olive fruit, and performing second fermentation at 28-30 degrees C for 12-16 hours, and then at 30-33 degrees C for 8-12 hours. The step (e) involves adding 3-6 g/l glutamic acid and 0.1-0.3 g/l sodium glutamate to the secondary fermentation broth. The step (f) involves inoculating ethanol-resistant P.ethanolidurans at a rate of 2-5 volume/volume%, and performing third fermentation at 18-25 degrees C for 12-16 hours, fermenting at 25-30 degrees C for 8-12 hours, fermenting at 30-35 degrees C for 8-12 hours, and fermenting at 25-30 degrees C for 8-12 hours.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

   USE - Illumination anaerobic treatment method of sulfate organic wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively increases removal rate of sulfate and chemical oxygen demand in sulfate organic wastewater compared with existing anaerobic treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Illumination anaerobic treatment method of sulfate organic wastewater involves treating anaerobic sludge under illumination and anaerobic conditions to obtain domesticated anaerobic sludge with desulfurization ability containing photosynthetic bacteria and sulfate-reducing bacteria capable of non-oxygen-producing photosynthesis, using domesticated anaerobic sludge with desulfurization ability to treat sulfate organic wastewater to be treated having chemical oxygen demand concentration of more than 1000 mg/L, so that microorganisms contained in the domesticated anaerobic sludge to effectively degrade chemical oxygen demand in sulfate organic wastewater into gaseous products e.g. carbon dioxide and methane gas and to reduce sulfate in wastewater to thiol by sulfate reducing bacteria and treating wastewater, where some of photosynthetic bacteria use generated carbon dioxide and thiol to obtain energy and synthesize nutrients needed for their own growth under light conditions and other portion of photosynthetic bacteria provides electrons for the system to further promote the reduction of sulfate, and the photosynthetic bacteria consume thiol and reduce the content of thiol in wastewater and its toxic effect on microorganisms, and photosynthetic bacteria also promote the reduction of sulfate by sulfate-reducing bacteria and improve the removal rate of sulfate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for treating sulfate organic wastewater.

TI Removing hard seed dormancy involves soaking seeds in hot water containing penetrant, transferring and freezing, repeating hot water soaking, freezing, and soaking seed in oxygen-releasing agent soaking liquid containing penetrating agent.
AU LIU Z
AE LIU Z (LIUZ-Individual)
   HEILONGJIANG CANGHAO AGRIC DEV CO LTD (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2133D
AB    NOVELTY - Removing hard seed dormancy involves soaking seeds in hot water at more than 50 degrees C containing penetrant for 10-30 minutes, then transferring to less than 0 degrees C and freezing for 3-30 minutes, repeating hot water soaking and freezing for 2-8 times, then alternately soaking the seed in oxygen-releasing agent soaking liquid containing penetrating agent and carbon dioxide absorbing agent soaking liquid containing penetrating agent, changing a soaking liquid every 12-24 hours, until the seed expanded. The oxygen releasing agent is hydrogen peroxide, and the concentration in seed soaking solution is 0.05-0.5%. The carbon dioxide absorbent is an alcoholamine solution, which is mixed in equal volume with ethanolamine or ethanolamine and N-methyldiethanolamine, and the concentration of the alcoholamine in seed soaking solution is 0.5-5%.
   USE - Method for removing hard seed dormancy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is low in cost, easy to operate, and suitable for popularization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing hard seed dormancy involves soaking seeds in hot water at more than 50 degrees C containing penetrant for 10-30 minutes, then transferring to less than 0 degrees C and freezing for 3-30 minutes, repeating hot water soaking and freezing for 2-8 times, then alternately soaking the seed in oxygen-releasing agent soaking liquid containing penetrating agent and carbon dioxide absorbing agent soaking liquid containing penetrating agent, changing a soaking liquid every 12-24 hours, until the seed expanded. The oxygen releasing agent is hydrogen peroxide, and the concentration in seed soaking solution is 0.05-0.5%. The carbon dioxide absorbent is an alcoholamine solution, which is mixed in equal volume with ethanolamine or ethanolamine and N-methyldiethanolamine, and the concentration of the alcoholamine in seed soaking solution is 0.5-5%. The penetrant is stable in hard water, acid and alkali resistance, and one of sodium diisobutyl naphthalene sulfonate and polyoxyethylene laureth ether or sodium diisobutyl naphthalene sulfonate and polyoxyethylene laureth ether is selected, mixed in volume ratio of 2-8:1. The concentration of penetrant in the seed soaking solution is 0.03-0.3%.

PN US2020290935-A1
TI Treating concrete before using within a mold involves mixing a nanobubble-infused liquid into a dry concrete mix to form an infused wet concrete and delivering the infused wet concrete to the mold to form a molded product.
AU GOODE R T
   CLARK J
   GILDERSLEEVE S
AE COLUMBIA MACHINE INC (COLU-Non-standard)
GA 202090988S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing a nanobubble-infused liquid into a dry concrete mix to form an infused wet concrete, where the nanobubble-infused liquid includes a concentration of nanobubbles of a gas at least 25% more than a natural concentration of nanobubbles of the gas within a natural state of the liquid; and delivering the infused wet concrete to the mold to form a molded product.
   USE - Method for treating concrete before using within a mold.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce the greenhouse gas emissions associated with making concrete products and for sequestering carbon dioxide. The addition of carbon-dioxide bubbles into the concrete helps to cure faster and stronger, improve flowability of the mixture, and help make a more carbon-neutral material because of the absorbed carbon-dioxide within the molded product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method of retrofitting an existing apparatus for forming concrete products, which involves adapting the existing component to treat fresh concrete to be delivered to the product mold by a new component with treated water infused with a concentration of carbon dioxide nanobubbles, where adapting involves adding to the existing component a water delivery system that is configured to direct the treated water into the fresh concrete, and the water delivery system is provided with one or more liquid manifolds configured to dispense the treated water into the fresh concrete to form treated concrete; and
   (2) an apparatus for delivering a wet concrete mix to a product mold, which comprises a hopper configured to retain a fresh concrete mix, a source of treated water having a concentration of nanobubbles of a gas 25% more than a natural concentration of nanobubbles of the gas within a natural state of the water, a water transport coupling the source of treated water with the hopper, a valve interposed within the water transport for selectively releasing the treated water into the hopper and a mixer in communication with the hopper for mixing the treated water with the fresh concrete mix to yield an infused wet concrete.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method for forming infused wet concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The liquid is water and the gas is carbon-dioxide. Preferred Conditions: The concentration of nanobubbles of gas within the liquid reduces a density of the liquid to below 0.9 g/cm3.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU PARK S
   PARK S Y
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
   LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
   LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 2020711899
AB    NOVELTY - Beverage maker comprises fermentation tank forming a space in which a beverage is made, a gas discharge (7) channel connected to an inside of the fermentation tank, a gas discharge valve set in the gas discharge channel, a flow sensor set in the gas discharge channel, and a controller configured to control a primary fermentation operation of the beverage to be made, where controller is configured to further perform the primary fermentation operation by opening the gas discharge valve, when completion of the primary fermentation operation's confirmed, acquires information on an amount of carbon dioxide discharged by the gas discharge channel using the flow sensor while the primary fermentation operation is further performed, and performs a bottling inducing operation for bottling of the beverage. The controller performs the bottling inducing operation when the amount of discharged carbon dioxide reaches or exceeds a reference discharge amount.
   USE - Beverage maker.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage maker enhances taste of beer, such as fruit, syrup, and vanilla beans and maintains optimum temperature during the fermentation operation is important for homemade beer, and easier the beer is to make, and capable of easily making a beer-like beverage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for controlling a beverage maker including a fermentation tank forming a space in which a beverage is made, which involves:
   (A) confirming completion of a primary fermentation operation while the beverage is made;
   (B) performing the primary fermentation operation by opening a gas discharge valve set in the gas discharge channel, when completion of the primary fermentation operation is confirmed;
   (C) acquiring information on an amount of carbon dioxide discharged by the gas discharge channel using a flow sensor set in the gas discharge channel, while the primary fermentation operation is further performed; and
   (D) performing a bottling inducing operation for bottling of the beverage, which is being made into an external container based on the amount of discharged carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of beverage maker.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle system, has oil and gas separator whose inlet is connected to first and second constant pressure chambers and outlet includes oil return port connected with oil supply unit and air return port.
AU ZHAO Z

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor (1), a heat source (2), a turbine (3), and a cooler (4) that are connected in sequence. A motor (5) is provided between the compressor and the turbine that are coaxially connected. A rotating shaft connecting unit of the machine and the motor is provided with a first static seal case for forming a first constant pressure cavity (8). The rotating shaft connecting unit of the turbine and the motor is provided with a second static sealing case for forming a second constant pressure cavity. An oil and gas separator (6) separates lubricating oil and circulating working fluid. An inlet of the oil and gas separator is connected to the first and second constant pressure chambers. An outlet of the oil and gas separator includes an oil return port connected with an oil supply unit and an air return port.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle system.
   ADVANTAGE - The rotating shaft connecting unit of the compressor and the motor is provided with a first labyrinth seal for reducing the gas leakage at the shaft end at one end close to the compressor. The rotating shaft connecting unit of the compressor and the motor is close to an end of the motor is provided with a first magnetic fluid sealing device for preventing leakage gas from entering the interior of the motor. The end of the motor is provided with a second magnetic fluid sealing device for preventing leakage gas from entering the interior of the motor. The separated lubricating oil enters the oil supply unit to re-enter the lubricating oil circulation and the separated carbon dioxide enters the circulation system again from the cooler, thus, realizing oil and gas separation and working fluid recovery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle system.
   Compressor (1)
   Heat source (2)

   Motor (5)

   First constant pressure cavity (8)

PT P
PN WO2020046657-A1; CA3108276-A1; AU2019332779-A1; EP3844211-A1; CN113056509-A; KR2021084439-A; US2021292510-A1; JP2021535237-W
TI Making expanded poly(lactide) bead foam used for            producing molded article, by pressurizing unfoamed            beads of poly(lactide) resin blend, heating,            depressurizing carbon dioxide-saturated partially            crystallized unfoamed beads, and cooling.
AU HOSSIENY N
   NATAL M A W
AE NATUREWORKS LLC (CRGI-C)
   NATUREWORKS LLC (CRGI-C)
   NATUREWORKS LLC (CRGI-C)
   NATUREWORKS LLC (CRGI-C)
GA 202019434V
AB    NOVELTY - Making expanded poly(lactide) (PLA) bead foam                involves (a) pressurizing unfoamed beads of a PLA                resin blend having a crystallinity of not greater                than 10 J/g as measured by differential scanning                calorimetry with carbon dioxide to a                superatmospheric pressure while maintaining the                temperature at or below the 40 degrees C, (b)                heating the pressurized unfoamed beads to 90-160                degrees C to at least partially crystallize the PLA                resin blend to form carbon dioxide- saturated,                partially crystallized, unfoamed beads, (c)                depressurizing the unfoamed beads such that the                carbon dioxide at least partially vaporizes to                expand the beads, and (d) simultaneously and/or                after step (c), cooling the expanded beads to below                40 degrees C. The PLA resin blend includes (i) a                first PLA polymer containing at least 88% L-lactic                units, and (ii) a second PLA polymer containing at                least 88% D-lactic units. The first and second PLA                copolymers are present in the PLA resin blend at a                weight ratio of 75:25-98:2 or 25:75-2:98.
   USE - The process is used for making expanded PLA                bead foam, which is used for producing a molded                article (all claimed), such as cups, plates,                coolers and other thermal insulation, buoys and                other flotation devices and shock absorbing devices                such as automotive bumper components.
   ADVANTAGE - The process produces expanded PLA bead foam                that easily obtains very large expansions and has                excellent mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an expanded PLA bead obtained by the                process, which comprises a cellular blend PLA resin                blend, where the expanded PLA bead has a density of                0.025-0.35 g/cm3 and a volume of 0.5-1500 mm3, and                the PLA resin blend contains PLA stereocomplex                crystals having a crystalline peak melting                temperature of 200-240 degrees C and PLA                homocrystals having a peak melting temperature of                140-180 degrees C; and
   (2) a method for producing a molded article,                comprising introducing expanded PLA beads into a                mold and sintering and optionally further expanding                the expanded PLA beads in the mold to form the                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The superatmospheric                  pressure in step (a) and the superatmospheric                  pressure in step (b) each is 3-10 MPa gauge. In                  step (b), the pressurized unfoamed beads are                  maintained at a temperature of 110-140 degrees C                  for a period of 5-30 minutes. The PLA                  stereocomplex crystals and PLA homocrystals form                  during step (b). Preferred Composition: The first                  PLA polymer contains at least 92%, preferably at                  least 95% L-lactic units and the second PLA                  polymer contains at least 92%, preferably at                  least 95% D-lactic units. The PLA stereocomplex                  complex comprises 10-50% combined weight of the                  PLA stereocomplex crystals and PLA homocrystals.                  The first and second PLA copolymers are present                  in the PLA resin blend at a weight ratio of                  85:15-98:2 or 15:85-2:98. Preferred Components:                  The carbon dioxide-saturated, partially                  crystallized, unfoamed beads formed in step (b)                  have an extent of crystallization of 10-35% based                  on the total weight of the PLA resin blend in the                  beads, as measured by differential scanning                  calorimetry.

PT P
PN CN108854898-A; CN108854898-B
TI Photocatalytic reactor comprises transparent columnar insulating shell, graphene/silicon composite film horizontally arranged in columnar insulating shell, and electrode plate mounted on top surface and bottom surface.
AU GAO C
   PENG L
   LIU Y
   GUO Y
AE HANGZHOU GAOXI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201895698Y
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic reactor comprises a transparent columnar insulating shell (1), a graphene/silicon composite film (2) horizontally arranged in the columnar insulating shell, and an electrode plate mounted on the top surface and bottom surface of the columnar insulating casing, where the two electrode plates are respectively connected to the positive electrode and negative electrode of the power source. The side wall of the columnar insulating casing is provided with a gas injection hole (5), and the gas injection hole is sealed by an insulating rubber stopper. The graphene/silicon composite film comprises a graphene film and a silicon nano film on the front side of the graphene film, and the silicon nano film is composed of silicon nanoparticles. The graphene film layer is cross-linked, the graphene film has a thickness of 10-100 nm, and a defect density ID/IG less than or equal to 0.01.
   USE - Photocatalytic reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic reactor catalyzes the formation of methane, carbon monoxide and oxygen by carbon dioxide and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The graphene/silicon composite film is parallel to the upper and lower electrode plates, and the silicon nanolayer is on the low potential side. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for catalytically reducing carbon dioxide in a photocatalytic reactor, which involves:
   (A) injecting water vapor and carbon dioxide into the reactor through a gas injection hole, and then sealing;
   (B) connecting the two electrode plates to the positive and negative electrodes of the power supply, and illuminating the graphene surface;
   (C) seperating electrons and holes by the upper and lower electrode plates, and locating the separated electrons and holes on two surfaces of the composite film, where one side of the silicon nanoparticles is coated with electrons to form an electron layer, and the other side is formed with holes to form a hole layer; and
   (D) forming a potential difference between the electron layer and hole layer to reduce carbon dioxide and water to form methane, carbon monoxide and oxygen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of photocatalytic reactor.
   Transparent columnar insulating shell (1)
   Graphene/silicon composite film (2)
   Anode plate (3)
   Cathode plate (4)
   Gas injection hole (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The graphene/silicon composite film is prepared by preparing graphene oxide to a concentration of 0.5-10 microgram/mL of graphene oxide aqueous solution, and filtering to form a film, placing the graphene oxide film attached to the suction filtration substrate in a closed container, fumigation from the bottom upwards at a temperature of 80-100 degrees C HI for 0.1-1 hour, uniformly coating the melted solid transfer agent on the surface of the reduced graphene oxide film, and cooling at room temperature until the film and seperating the substrate, subjecting the reduced graphene oxide film to heat treatment, so that the solid transfer agent is sublimated or volatilized, heating the reduced graphene oxide film to 300 degrees C at 1 degrees C/minute (slow heating, increasing the surface of the graphene film wrinkles to expand the area of the graphene film in the unit space), keeping warm for 6-12 hours to remove most of the atomic defects inside the graphene, but not restore the internal stacking structure of graphene, spraying a layer of metal nanoparticles on the surface of the graphene film by magnetron sputtering, selecting the metal nanoparticles from titanium, tungsten, iron, magnesium, and molybdenum, such that the molar amount of the sputtered metal nanoparticles is not more than 30% of the molar amount of carbon atoms in the graphene film, and chlorinating the graphene film sputtered with the metal nanoparticles at 800-1200 degrees C, and dispersing the metal nanoparticles in the form of chloride, treating the chlorinated graphene film at a high temperature of 2000 degrees C to obtain an interlayer crosslinked graphene film, and spraying the surface of the cross-linked graphene film with a layer of silicon nanoparticles having a thickness of 5-40 nm. Preferred Conditions: The solid transfer agent is selected from paraffin, naphthalene, arsenic trioxide, camphor, sulfur, norbornene, rosin, etc., which are submerged or volatilized under certain conditions, and are insoluble in water. The solid transfer agent has a sublimation temperature of not more than 320 degrees. The graphene film sputtered with the metal nanoparticles is subjected to heat treatment in an environment having a chlorine gas content of 0.5-10% for 0.1-4 hours. The temperature rise process of the 2000 degree high temperature process is below 1500 degrees C, 5-20 degrees C per minute, or above 1500 degrees C, 2-5 degrees C per minute.

PN CN108659163-A; CN108659163-B
TI Preparing double-stimulated swelling gel particle comprises e.g. dissolving an acrylamide monomer in water to form solution, adding crosslinking agent, and carbon dioxide responsive monomer to resulting solution and mixing.
AU FENG Y
   TIAN Q
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
   UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 201884834Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing double-stimulated swelling gel particle comprises e.g. (i) dissolving acrylamide monomer in water to form solution, adding crosslinking agent, temperature-responsive monomer N,N-dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate monomer, and carbon dioxide responsive monomer to the resulting solution 2-(Methylacryloyloxy)ethyl dimethyl-(3-sulfonic acid propyl)ammonium hydroxide, stirring uniformly to form mixed monomer solution polymerization system, stirring, passing through nitrogen polymerization system to remove system, dissolved oxygen, acrylamide, N,N-dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate, 2-(methacryloyloxy)ethyl dimethyl-(3-sulfonylpropyl)ammonium hydroxide (ii) mixing mixed monomer with initiator solution, and reacting in 39-41 degrees C water bath, and continuing heating to 59-61 degrees C for 8-10 hours and completing reaction, and (iii) granulating, drying and crushing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing double-stimulated swelling gel particle.
   ADVANTAGE - The gel particles: can continuously expand under the action of carbon dioxide and thermal stimulation, and can be expanded twice in an acidic environment, exerts the sealing function for long time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing double-stimulated swelling gel particle comprises (i) dissolving acrylamide monomer in water to form solution, adding crosslinking agent, temperature-responsive monomer N,N-dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate monomer, and carbon dioxide responsive monomer to the resulting solution 2-(Methylacryloyloxy)ethyl dimethyl-(3-sulfonic acid propyl)ammonium hydroxide, stirring uniformly to form mixed monomer solution polymerization system, stirring, passing through nitrogen polymerization system to remove system, dissolved oxygen, acrylamide, N,N-dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate, 2-(methacryloyloxy)ethyl dimethyl-(3-sulfonylpropyl)ammonium hydroxide and the mass of the crosslinking agent is 22.65-22.84%, 0.84-1.66%, 1.44-1.45%, and 0.01-0.02% of the total mass of mixed monomer solution, (ii) mixing mixed monomer with initiator solution, and reacting in 39-41 degrees C water bath for 8-10 hours, and continuing heating to 59-61 degrees C for 8-10 hours and completing reaction, and (iii) granulating, drying and crushing. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for double-stimulated swelling gel particle is prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent is N,N-methylenebisacrylamide. The initiator is ammonium persulfate, and the initiator is added in a mixed form. The total mass of the bulk solution is 0.06-0.08%. The double-stimulated swelling gel particle continuously swells under a carbon dioxide stimulation under a pH environment of 4.3 plus minus 0.1. Preferred Method: In the step (iii) pulverizes or pulverizes the copolymer gel to be sieved to below 140 meshes. pulverizing the copolymer gel obtained by the obtained reaction is carried out by smashing pulverizer for 2 seconds and allowing to stand for 8 seconds, repeating cycle for 10 times and supplemented by a fan to dissipate heat. The stimulating factor is temperature and carbon dioxide under the action of carbon dioxide and thermal stimulation, and the temperature range of the thermal stimulation expansion is from 25-65 degrees C.

GA 2018533747
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling higher alkynes from partial oxidation of natural gas to acetylene comprises cooling solvent, absorption tower absorbing soluble components of higher alkyne gas; extracting higher alkyne gas by acetylene concentration device, diluting the gas phase mixture containing acetylene, propyne, vinyl acetylene, diacetylene, benzene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide or/and methane with acetylene tail gas; where the acetylene tail gas is mixed gas rich in hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide or/and methane, and obtained by separating cracking gas; where the absorption refers to process in which cooled solvent is countercurrently contacted with higher alkyne gas in absorption column, and soluble component in higher alkyne gas is transferred from gas phase to liquid phase; where the soluble component comprises acetylene, methyl alkyne, propadiene, vinyl acetylene, diacetylene and/or benzene.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling higher alkynes from partial oxidation of natural gas to acetylene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the operational risk in the gas phase higher alkyne transportation process, and realizes efficient use of by-products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the recycling higher acetylene in partial oxidation of natural gas to acetylene comprising cooling solvent, absorption tower absorbing soluble components of higher alkyne gas; extracting higher alkyne gas by acetylene concentration device, incorporating acetylene tail gas to dilute gas phase mixture containing acetylene, propyne, vinyl acetylene, diacetylene, benzene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and/or methane; where the acetylene tail gas is mixed gas rich in hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide or/and methane, and obtained by separating cracking gas; where the solvent is cooled to use ethylene glycol as refrigerant, and cooling the NMP solvent to 0-10 degrees C; where the absorption refers to process in which cooled solvent is countercurrently contacted with higher alkyne gas in absorption column, and soluble component in higher alkyne gas is transferred from gas phase to liquid phase; where the absorption condition is temperature of 0-10 degrees C under normal pressure; the volume ratio of the liquid phase to the gas phase in the absorption process is 0.025-0.040; the soluble component contains a mixture of acetylene, methyl alkyne, propadiene, vinyl acetylene, diacetylene or/and benzene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is N-Methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP) or dimethylformamide, the solvent-cooled refrigerant is ethylene glycol, and the solvent cooling is cooling the solvent to -2 to 15 degrees C. The solvent is NMP, the solvent is cooled to use ethylene glycol as refrigerant, the NMP solvent is cooled to 0-10 degrees C. The volume ratio of the liquid phase to the gas phase during the absorption process (abbreviated as the liquid to gas ratio (vol.%/vol.%)) is from 0.010-0.050. The liquid to gas ratio during the absorption process is 0.025-0.040.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The absorption tower is packed absorption tower or sieve plate absorption tower; the absorption conditions includes pressure is 0.1 MPa and the temperature is -2 to 15 degrees C, The absorption temperature is 0-10 degrees C.

PT P
PN FR3056597-A1; WO2018059871-A1
TI Hydrotreating hydrocarbon feedstock comprises contacting feedstock with catalyst containing active phase group VIB and VIIIB metals deposited on support, contacting support with carbon dioxide, and subjecting support to reducing treatment.
AU UZIO D
   AYMONIER C
   QUILFEN C
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 201824728X
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrotreating a hydrocarbon feedstock comprises contacting the feedstock with at least one catalyst containing an active phase having at least one group VIB metal and at least one group VIIIB metal deposited on a support comprising at least one oxide, at 0.5-30 MPa and 180-450 degrees C with an hourly space velocity of 0.1-20 per hour, (b) contacting the impregnated support with carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions, and (c) subjecting the impregnated support to a reducing treatment.
   USE - The process is useful for hydrotreating a hydrocarbon feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: improves the characteristics of the feeds by reducing the presence of sulfur or other heteroatoms e.g. nitrogen and reducing the content in aromatic hydrocarbon compounds by hydrogenation to increase the cetane number; provides the hydrotreatment catalysts having a higher specific activity by bringing an impregnated support into contact with carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions; and provides the catalyst in a relatively shorter time by limiting the number of unit steps compared to conventional industrial processes which generally comprise an impregnation, maturation phase, drying and calcination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrotreating a hydrocarbon feedstock comprises contacting the feedstock with at least one catalyst containing an active phase having at least one group VIB metal and at least one group VIIIB metal deposited on a support comprising at least one oxide, at 0.5-30 MPa and 180-450 degrees C with an hourly space velocity of 0.1-20 per hour, where a hydrogen/charge ratio expressed as a volume of hydrogen, measured under normal conditions of temperature and pressure, per volume of liquid charge is 50-5000 l/l, and the catalyst being prepared by a process comprising at least the following steps i.e. impregnating the support with at least one aqueous solution and/or an organic solution, preferably a solution of alcohol(s) and/or polyol(s), with at least one compound containing at least one group VIIIB metal, and at least one compound containing at least one group VIB metal, (b) contacting the impregnated support with carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions, and (c) subjecting the impregnated support to a reducing treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (a) is carried out with a solution of alcohol(s) and/or polyol(s). The step (b) is carried out: at 30-500 degrees C and 7-30 MPa and for 1 second and 24 hours, preferably 300 seconds and 120 minutes; and in the presence of a solution of alcohol(s) and/or polyol(s), where the molar ratio alcohol(s) and/or polyol(s)/carbon dioxide is 0.1/99.9-90/10.5, where a step of maturation under a saturated solvent atmosphere carried out between steps (a) and (b) for 1 minutes and 24 hours. The solvent is identical to the solution of alcohol(s) and/or polyol(s) used in step (a), where after step (b) and before step (c) drying under a supercritical carbon dioxide flow is performed. Preferred Components: The group VIB metal is molybdenum or tungsten and the group VIIIB metal is cobalt or nickel. The group VIB metal is molybdenum and the group VIIIB metal is cobalt. The group VIB metal content is 1-40 wt.% based on the total weight of the catalyst. The molar ratio of metal group VIB/metal group VIIIB is 0.01-1. The group VIB metal precursor is heteropolyanions, chlorides or any other soluble inorganic derivative in alcoholic and/or polyalcoholic solution. The precursor of the group VIIIB metal is nitrates, carbonates, acetates, chlorides, alkoxides, hydroxides, oxalates complexes formed by a polyacid or an alcoholic acid and its salts, complexes formed with acetylacetonates, or any other soluble inorganic derivative in alcoholic and/or polyalcoholic solution.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN114249344-A
TI Preparing spherical aragonite used in micro-capsule, food additive, medicine and catalyst carrier, comprises putting steel slag in ammonium chloride solution and pouring carbon dioxide into obtained leaching solution under ultrasonic waves.
AU CHEN J
   CHEN X
   ZHANG N
   SUN J
   TAN Z
   WANG J
   TONG Z
AE UNIV YANGTZE NORMAL (UYYN-C)
GA 202250660H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing spherical aragonite comprises putting steel slag in ammonium chloride solution to carry out ammonium leaching reaction to obtain leaching solution of the calcium chloride-ammonium chloride-ammonia-water system for later use. The carbon dioxide is poured into the obtained leaching solution and turned on ultrasonic waves at the same time. The pH of the leaching solution reaches 9.00 for 1.0 hours. The solution aging continues to introduce carbon dioxide until the pH value of the solution is 8.20 and then aged again for 1.0 hour. The solution is aged, suction filtered and then dried for 2.0 hours. The ammonia water is added to the filtrate to continue the reaction. The solution is reacted to a pH value of 7.10. The solution is subjected to suction filtration and then dried for 2.0 hours to obtain the vaterite calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing spherical aragonite used in micro-capsule, food additive, medicine and catalyst carrier.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the spherical aragonite in a simple manner with high purity, small granularity and reduced agglomeration between the particles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical representation depicting the change of pH and calcium content with reaction time in the one-stage secondary carbonization reaction process.. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   DING Z
AE SHANGHAI TIANCI INT PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202168132F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chiral 5-fluoro-3-methylisobenzofuran-1(3H)-one (IV), comprises subjecting 1-(2-bromo-5-fluorophenyl)ethanone (I) to reduction reaction in presence of biological enzyme-catalyzed reduction system to form chiral 1-(2-bromo-5-fluorophenyl)ethanol compound (II), (ii) reacting (II) in first inert solvent in the presence of n-butyl lithium and carbon dioxide to obtain chiral 4-fluoro-2-(1-hydroxyethyl)benzoic acid (III), and (iii) subjecting (III) to esterification reaction in second inert solvent to obtain (IV).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing chiral 5-fluoro-3-methylisobenzofuran-1(3H)-one.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has synthetic route segment, low cost and simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing chiral 5-fluoro-3-methylisobenzofuran-1(3H)-one of formula (IV), comprises subjecting 1-(2-bromo-5-fluorophenyl)ethanone of formula (I) to reduction reaction in presence of biological enzyme-catalyzed reduction system to form chiral 1-(2-bromo-5-fluorophenyl)ethanol compound of formula (II), (ii) reacting (II) in first inert solvent in the presence of n-butyl lithium and carbon dioxide to obtain chiral 4-fluoro-2-(1-hydroxyethyl)benzoic acid of formula (III), and (iii) subjecting (III) to esterification reaction in second inert solvent to obtain (IV). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Lorlatinib key intermediate i.e. chiral 5-fluoro-3-methylisobenzofuran-1(3H)-one (I) prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the reaction temperature is -80 to -30 degrees C, preferably -40 to -30 degrees C; and the molar ratio between n-butyllithium and (II) is 0.8-1.2:1, preferably, 1 plus minus 0.1:1. In step (i), the pH of the bioenzyme catalytic system is 7 plus minus 1, preferably 7 plus minus 0.1; the concentration of (I) is 0.1-2 mM, preferably 0.5-0.7 mM based on the total volume of the biological enzyme catalytic system; the biological enzyme catalytic system further comprises glucose and glucose dehydrogenase; the concentration of glucose is 0.1-10 mmol/l, preferably 1 plus minus 0.1 mmol/l based on the total volume of the bioenzyme catalytic system; the concentration of glucose dehydrogenase is 0.38-1 mg/ml, preferably 0.69-0.77 mg/ml based on the total volume of the biological enzyme catalytic system; and the solvent of the bioenzyme catalytic system is water. In step (iii), the esterification reaction is carried out in the presence of an acid, and the acid is sulfuric acid, acetic acid, oxalic acid and/or phosphoric acid. Preferred Components: In step (i), the biological enzyme is combination of perakine reductase and coenzyme NADPH; the concentration of the perakine reductase is 0.38-1 mg/ml, preferably 0.69-0.77 mg/ml; the concentration of coenzyme NADPH is 0.002-0.15 mmol/l, preferably 0.01-0.02 mmol/l based on the total volume of the bioenzyme catalytic system. In the biological enzyme catalytic system, the mass ratio between glucose dehydrogenase and perakine reductase is 0.5-2:1, preferably 1 plus minus 0.1:1. In step (iii), the second inert solvent is alcohol solvents (preferably methanol and/or ethanol) and/or water; the volume ratio of alcohol solvent and water in the second inert solvent is 5-20:1, preferably 9 plus minus 1:1; the volume-to-mass ratio (ml:g) between second inert solvent and (III) is 5-20:1, preferably 10 plus minus 1:1; the molar ratio between acid and (III) is 5-20:1, preferably 10/|1:1; the reaction temperature of the esterification reaction is 0-50 degrees C, preferably 20-30 degrees C; the reaction time of the esterification reaction is 1-10 hours, preferably 2-3 hours.

   AHMED Z T

   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO LAW DEPT (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202098160F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen substantially without                greenhouse gas emissions involves producing a                product gas comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide                from a hydrocarbon fuel source, separating hydrogen                from the product gas to create a hydrogen product                stream and a byproduct stream, injecting the                byproduct stream into a reservoir containing mafic                rock, and allowing components of the byproduct                stream to react in situ with components of the                mafic rock to precipitate and store components of                the byproduct stream in the reservoir.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen substantially                without greenhouse gas emissions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce hydrogen                substantially without greenhouse gas emissions is                advantageously high tolerance to acid gas                impurities, does not require deep wells, such as                those 850 m deep or deeper, and does not require                long-term reservoir monitoring.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for producing hydrogen substantially without                greenhouse gas emissions, which comprises a                hydrogen production unit with a hydrocarbon fuel                inlet operable to produce a product gas comprising                hydrogen and carbon dioxide from hydrocarbon fuel,                a hydrogen separation unit operable to separate                hydrogen from the product gas to create a hydrogen                product stream and a byproduct stream, and an                injection well operable to inject the byproduct                stream into a reservoir containing mafic rock to                allow components of the byproduct stream to react                in situ with components of the mafic rock to                precipitate and store components of the byproduct                stream in the reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mafic rock                  comprises basaltic rock. Preferred Process: The                  method further involves injecting the byproduct                  stream into the reservoir, the byproduct stream                  is further treated to separate and purify carbon                  di-oxide from other components to increase carbon                  di-oxide concentration of the byproduct stream                  for injection into the reservoir, liquefying                  carbon di-oxide in the byproduct stream for                  injection into the reservoir, mixing the                  byproduct stream with water, the byproduct stream                  comprising H2S, reacting the separated hydrogen                  with nitrogen to form compressed liquid ammonia,                  transporting the compressed liquid ammonia and                  returning the compressed liquid ammonia to                  hydrogen and nitrogen via electrolysis for use of                  hydrogen as a hydrogen fuel source, producing a                  product gas includes steam reforming or partial                  oxidation, allowing components of the byproduct                  stream to react in situ with components of the                  mafic rock to precipitate produces precipitates                  selected from the group consisting of calcium                  carbonates, magnesium carbonates, iron                  carbonates, and combinations.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Porous spherical photocatalytic material useful as            photocatalyst for catalytic reduction of carbon            dioxide, is zinc hydroxystannate photocatalytic            material with porous spherical structure formed by            stacking nanoparticles.

   WANG H
   WANG C
   ZHAO H
   TU J
   YAN S

AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202074593Y
AB    NOVELTY - A porous spherical photocatalytic material is                a zinc hydroxystannate photocatalytic material with                a porous spherical structure, which is formed by                stacking nanoparticles and exhibits typical                adsorption characteristics of porous                structure.
   USE - The porous spherical photocatalytic material                is useful as photocatalyst for catalytic reduction                of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of the                porous spherical photocatalytic material with low                energy consumption, realizes the preparation of                porous spherical structures without templates, and                constructs the metal defect sites without high                temperature and reducing atmosphere, so that the                material can be recycled and regenerated, and the                consumed crystal lattice hydroxy group can be                repaired by adsorbing water molecules.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the porous                spherical photocatalytic material, which involves                (1) using distilled water as a solvent to fully                dissolve zinc chloride to obtain a uniform                solution, and adjusting the pH of the zinc chloride                solution to 8-11 with ammonia, (2) dispersing tin                oxide in distilled water, adding a zinc chloride                solution, uniformly mixing, and controlling the                molar ratio of tin ion to zinc ion to 1:1, (3)                transferring the mixed solution to a hydrothermal                reaction kettle, and putting into an oven at                120-160 degrees C for 2-12 hours, and (4) taking                out, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging,                washing and drying; and
   (2) use of the porous spherical photocatalytic                material as photocatalyst for catalytic reduction                of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The porous spherical                  photocatalytic material has a spherical diameter                  of 1-2 mu m, and a specific surface area of 19.8                  m2/g. Preferred Components: The tin oxide is                  sodium stannate or tin oxide.

   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2020534216
AB    NOVELTY - Installation comprises: compressor A making it possible to compress the feed gas stream; first separation unit by membrane able to receive the gas flow coming from the compressor and to provide first permeate and first retentate; second membrane separation unit able to receive the first retentate and to provide second permeate and second retentate; third membrane separation unit capable of receiving the first permeate and of providing third permeate and third retentate; at least one means for measuring the suction pressure of the second permeate of the second membrane unit; and at least one compressor B allowing the suction of the second permeate and adjustment of the suction pressure of the second permeate as a function of the suction pressure measured before recycling the second permeate in the feed gas stream downstream of the compressor A, with each separation unit per membrane comprising at least one membrane more permeable to carbon dioxide than to methane.
   USE - The installation is useful for treating by membrane permeation of feed gas stream having methane and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for controlling an installation comprising measuring the suction pressure of the second permeate, comparing this measurement with a set value, and adjusting the suction pressure of the second permeate by the compressor B to keep the pressure value equal to the set value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The adjustment step the compressor B undergoes acceleration or a deceleration. The steps of measurement, comparison and adjustment are carried out automatically by means of data transmission. The method comprises measuring the concentration of methane in the second retentate, comparing this measurement with a set value, and determining the deviation from this set value, and adjusting the pressure of the supply gas flow so as to reduce the determined deviation. The pressure of the feed gas flow is adjusted using the compressor or using a shut-off valve and progressive pressurization. The step of adjusting the supply gas flow comprises an increase or a decrease in the pressure. The comparison step and the adjustment step are performed automatically by means of data transmission and data processing. Preferred Components: The installation comprises at least one means for measuring the concentration of methane in the second retentate, and at least one means for adjusting the pressure of the gas flow of feeding according to the measured methane concentration. The means for adjusting the pressure of the supply gas flow is a compressor or a shut-off valve and progressive pressurization. The third retentate is recycled to the compressor making it possible to compress the feed gas flow. The membranes used in the membrane separation units have the same selectivity. The feed gas flow is biogas.

PN CN110270315-A; CN110270315-B
TI Preparing metal organic frame polymer composite            for adsorbent, involves mixing precursor, polymer, and            first organic solvent to form raw solution which is            subjected to solvent evaporation to obtain metal            organic frame polymer.
AU ZHU S
   ZHU H
   CHEN C
AE UNIV HONG KONG CHINESE SHENZHEN RES INST (UYCE-C)
GA 201982596C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal organic frame (MOF)-polymer                composite, involves mixing the MOF precursor, the                polymer, and the first organic solvent to form a                raw solution. The raw solution is subjected to                solvent evaporation to obtain a preliminary                material of the MOF-polymer composite material,                preferably, the MOF precursor is selected from a                precursor material corresponding to Hong Kong                University of Science and Technology (HKUST)-1,                copper benzenedicarboxylate (CuBDC), Zeolitic                imidazolate frameworks (ZIFs)-8 or Universitetet i                Oslo (UiO)-66, and the precursor material comprises                a salt and a ligand, more preferably, the MOF                precursor is a precursor material corresponding to                HKUST-1.
   USE - The method enables to prepare metal organic                frame polymer composite that is used as an                adsorbent, preferably, the adsorbent is used to                adsorb carbon dioxide, more preferably, the                adsorbent adsorbs carbon dioxide in an amount of                from 0.8-1.5 mmol/g.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare metal organic                frame polymer composite that has high specific                surface area of the metal organic framework                material and good mechanical properties of the                polymer, and the specific surface area of the                composite material is 100-600 m2/g, which is a A                multi-stage pore structure containing micropores,                mesopores and macropores, which has good adsorption                performance for carbon dioxide gas, large                adsorption capacity and fast adsorption rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Polymer is selected                  from one or more of polyvinylidene fluoride,                  polyether sulfone, polyacrylonitrile, cellulose                  acetate, and polyimide, preferably polyvinylidene                  fluoride. First organic solvent is selected from                  one or more of dimethyl sulfoxide, N,                  N-dimethylformamide, and dimethyl group                  acetamide. It is preferably dimethyl sulfoxide.                  Preferred Process: solvent evaporation is carried                  out at 70-110 degrees C for 5-12 hours,                  preferably, the evaporation temperature is 85-95                  degrees C and the evaporation time is 8-10 hours.                  Preparation process of the original solution is                  to dissolve the polymer and the MOF precursor                  separately In the same solvent to obtain a                  polymer solution and a MOF precursor solution,                  and then mixing the polymer solution and the MOF                  precursor solution, preferably, a MOF crystal                  synthesis adjusting additive is added to the MOF                  precursor solution, more preferably, the crystal                  synthesis adjusting additive corresponding to the                  HKUST-1, CuBDC, and ZIF-8 precursor solution is                  triethylamine, and the crystal synthesis                  adjusting additive corresponding to the UiO-66                  precursor solution is hydrochloric acid or acetic                  acid. Preparation method further involves drying                  the MOF-polymer composite material in a second                  organic solvent, preferably, the soaking time is                  1-4 hours, more preferably, it is 1.5-3 hours,                  more preferably, the second organic solvent is                  selected from anhydrous methanol and/or absolute                  ethanol. Preferred Compositions: In the original                  solution, the total mass fraction of the MOF                  precursor is 0.5-15 wt.%, the mass fraction of                  the polymer is 0.5-15 wt.%, preferably, the total                  mass fraction of the MOF precursor is 4-10 wt.%,                  and the mass fraction of the polymer is 4-10                  wt.%, more preferably, the precursor salt and                  ligand mass ratio of HKUST-1 is 1.8-2.3:1.                  Composite material has macropores, mesopores and                  micropores, preferably, the macropores of the                  HKUST-1/PVDF composite have a pore size ranging                  from 50-3 mu m, the mesopores have an average                  pore diameter of 3.8 nm, and the pore diameter of                  the micropores is 0.9 nm, preferably, the                  composite has a specific surface area of from 100                  to 600 m 2 /g. Preferred Conditions: Drying                  process is vacuum drying at 80-120 degrees C for                  12-24 hours.

TI Ignition control system for pre-chamber-ignited            four-stroke gaseous-fueled engine, has electronic            control unit structured to output fuel delivery signal            to adjust air-fuel ratio in combustion prechamber            towards target air-fuel ratio.
AU WANG P
   GINTER D
   SINGH J
   KIM A
AE CATERPILLAR INC (CATE-C)
   CATERPILLAR INC (CATE-C)
GA 201907758B
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a sensor (102) structured                to measure carbon dioxide or oxygen in a combustion                prechamber of a prechamber ignition device (44). An                electronic control unit (106) receives data                indicative of an amount of the carbon dioxide or                oxygen, determines an air-fuel ratio in the                combustion prechamber associated with production of                the amount of the carbon dioxide or oxygen and                outputs a fuel delivery signal to adjust air-fuel                ratio in the combustion prechamber towards a target                air-fuel ratio.
   USE - Ignition control system for an internal                combustion engine (claimed) e.g.                pre-chamber-ignited four-stroke gaseous-fueled                engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides precise and accurate                knowledge of and control over a prechamber air-fuel                ratio so as to assist in achieving precise and                accurate control over ignition timing in a                prechamber and ignition timing in a main combustion                chamber, while optimizing contribution of the                prechamber to an engine's emissions profile.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a method for adjusting stoichiometry of a                combustion prechamber in an internal combustion                engine
   (2) an internal combustion engine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional side view of an                engine having an ignition control system.
   Air (32)
   Prechamber ignition device (44)
   Ignition control system (100)
   Sensor (102)
