PN WO2018219995-A1; AU2018275637-A1; KR2020012956-A; CA3072134-A1; SG11201910553-A1; EP3630940-A1; CN111133097-A; US2020181629-A1; BR112019025285-A2; JP2020521476-W; HK40018448-A0; IN201927046150-A
TI Yeast for producing protein of interest, comprises            nucleotide sequence expression system expressing            synthetic Calvin cycle having heterologous genes of            Calvin cycle and include gene encoding enzyme from            ribulose-bisphosphate carboxylase.
AU MATTANOVICH D
   SAUER M
   STEIGER M
   GASSLER T
   GASSER B
AE UNIV WIEN BODENKULTUR (UYWI-Non-standard)
   ACIB GMBH (ACIB-Non-standard)
   UNIV BODENKULTUR WIEN (UYBO-Non-standard)
   UNIV BODENKULTUR WIEN (UYBO-Non-standard)
   UNIV BODENKULTUR WIEN (UYBO-Non-standard)
   UNIV BODENKULTUR WIEN (UYBO-Non-standard)
   ACIB GMBH (ACIB-Non-standard)
   UNIV WIEN SOIL CULTURE (UYWI-Non-standard)
GA 201896870D
AB    NOVELTY - Yeast comprises a nucleotide sequence                expression system expressing a synthetic Calvin                cycle having heterologous genes of the synthetic                Calvin cycle, which include a gene encoding an                enzyme from class of ribulose-bisphosphate                carboxylases (EC number: 4.1.1.39) (RuBisCO gene),                and a gene encoding an enzyme from class of                ribulose phosphate kinases (EC number: 2.7.1.19)                (PRK gene), where each of RuBisCO gene and PRK gene                is fused with a nucleotide sequence encoding a                peroxisomal targeting signal (PTS), and the yeast                further comprises a heterologous expression                construct expressing a gene of interest                (GOI).
   USE - Yeast for producing a protein of interest                and/or metabolite by using a carbon source                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The yeast supports biomass assimilation from                carbon dioxide and is suitable for industrial                production scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for culturing yeast in a cell                culture, which involves culturing the yeast in                growing phase by using gaseous carbon dioxide                and/or dissolved carbonate and/or bicarbonate                compounds as a carbon source to obtain an                accumulated yeast biomass in the cell culture;                and
   (2) a method for producing an organic product                in a yeast, which involves using yeast having                synthetic Calvin cycle, where 20% of product's                total organic carbon is from a carbon source which                is gaseous carbon dioxide and/or dissolved                carbonate and/or bicarbonate compounds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The synthetic Calvin                  cycle comprises heterologous genes. The each of                  RuBisCO gene and PRK gene is fused with a                  nucleotide sequence encoding a peroxisomal                  targeting signal (PTS) to express a synthetic                  Calvin cycle in yeast peroxisomes. The yeast                  comprises one or more endogenous genes to                  complete synthetic Calvin cycle. The synthetic                  Calvin cycle comprises one or more further                  heterologous genes, which are gene encoding an                  enzyme from a class of phosphoglycerate kinases                  (EC number: 2.7.2.3) (PGK1 gene), and/or a gene                  encoding an enzyme from class of                  glyceraldehyde-3- phosphate dehydrogenases (EC                  number: 1.2.1.12) (TDH3 gene), and/or a gene                  encoding an enzyme from class of triose-phosphate                  isomerases (EC number: 5.3.1.1) (TP11 gene),                  and/or a gene encoding an enzyme from class of                  transketolases (EC number: 2.2.1.1) (TKL1 gene),                  where one or more, or each of PGK1, TDH3, TPI1,                  and TKL1 genes is/are fused with a nucleotide                  sequence encoding a PTS. The RuBisCO gene is of                  Thiobacillus denitrificans origin having                  nucleobases (SEQ ID NO: 37), given in the                  specification, or its functionally active variant                  with 90% sequence identity expressing a                  ribulose-bisphosphate carboxylase, and/or PRK                  gene is of Spinacia oleracea origin having                  nucleobases (SEQ ID NO: 38), given in the                  specification, or its functionally active variant                  with 90% sequence identity expressing a ribulose                  phosphate kinase, and/or PGK1 gene is of Ogataea                  polymorpha origin having nucleobases (SEQ ID NO:                  39), given in the specification, or its                  functionally active variant with 90% sequence                  identity expressing a phosphoglycerate kinase,                  and/or TDH3 gene is of Ogataea polymorpha origin                  having nucleobases (SEQ ID NO: 40), given in the                  specification, or its functionally active variant                  with 90% sequence identity expressing a                  glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase, and/or                  TP11 gene is of Ogataea parapolymorpha origin                  having nucleobases (SEQ ID NO: 41), given in the                  specification, or its functionally active variant                  with 90% sequence identity expressing a                  triose-phosphate isomerase, and/or TKL1 gene is                  of Ogataea parapolymorpha origin having                  nucleobases (SEQ ID NO: 42), given in the                  specification, or its functionally active variant                  with 90% sequence identity expressing a                  transketolase. The yeast further comprises a                  heterologous genes expressing one or more                  molecular chaperones in cytosol of yeast, where                  chaperones assist the covalent folding and/or                  assembly of enzymes. The chaperones are GroEL of                  Escherichia coli origin encoded by nucleobases                  (SEQ ID NO: 43), given in the specification, or                  its functionally active variant with 90% sequence                  identity expressing a molecular chaperone, and                  GroES of Escherichia coli origin, which is                  encoded by a nucleobases (SEQ ID NO: 8), given in                  the specification, or its functionally active                  variant with 90% sequence identity expressing a                  molecular chaperone. The one or more of                  heterologous genes of synthetic Calvin cycle are                  codon-optimized for expression in yeast. The                  yeast is selected from a group consist of Pichia,                  Komagataella, Hansenula, Ogataea, Candida, and                  Torulopsis, preferably selected from a group                  consist of Pichia pastoris, Komagataella                  pastoris, Komagataella phaffii, and Komagataella                  pseudopastoris. The yeast incorporates                  heterologous genes operably linked to a promoter,                  which is inducible by methanol, and the growing                  phase starts upon adding methanol to culture                  medium. The method further involves culturing                  accumulated yeast biomass in a production phase                  by using a carbon source to produce protein of                  interest (POI) and metabolite,                  respectively.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN207885332-U
TI Mushroom house has main water pipe that is provided with booster pump, and temperature sensor, humidity sensor and carbon dioxide concentration sensor that are electrically connected to input end of programmable logic controller.
AU PAN J
AE XINPING YUANJIAN AGRIC DEV CO LTD (XINP-Non-standard)
GA 201876898K
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model belongs to edible fungi cultivation field, specifically relates to a mushroom room, comprising four walls and a transparent top plate; the transparent top plate is provided with a retractable sun-shading layer; outer side of the wall body is provided with a solar cell module; inner side of the wall body is provided with a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, a carbon dioxide concentration sensor, a heating pipe and a PLC controller; the wall body is provided with an exhaust fan and a door; the wall body is provided with a first branch water tube and the second branch, the first branch water pipe is provided with a nozzle and a first electromagnetic valve; the branch water pipe is provided with a spray head and the second electromagnetic valve, the first branch pipe and the second branch water pipe connected with the main water pipe, the main water pipe is equipped with a pressurizing pump, a temperature sensor, humidity sensor, carbon dioxide concentration sensor and an exhaust fan, a first electromagnetic valve, a second electromagnetic valve and booster pump electrically connected with the PLC controller; electrically connecting the solar cell module and the exhaust fan, a booster pump and a PLC controller. The utility model reduces the labour intensity and improves the production efficiency.

PT P
PN JP6312230-B1; JP2019053660-A
TI Method for calculating prediction of green house effect gas emission for an air conditioning installation, involves calculating carbon dioxide emission reduction amount which shows reduction of annual emission amount.
AU SUZUKI T
AE PERSH KK (PERS-Non-standard)
   PERSH KK (PERS-Non-standard)
GA 201830981T
AB    NOVELTY - The prediction calculation method involves calculating (S2) the carbon dioxide (CO2) emission reduction amount which shows the reduction of the annual CO2 emission amount which is estimated about an air conditioning installation. The credit of the quantity of the CO2 emission reduction amount of the air conditioning installation of a provider terminal is requested by the control unit. The credit claim check document is received for the CO2 emission reduction transmitted from the provider terminal.
   USE - Method for calculating the prediction of green house effect gas emission for an air conditioning installation.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat exchange effect in air conditioning installation is improved, and the electric power consumption is reduced, which reduces the CO2 emissions of air conditioning installation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for calculating the prediction of green house effect gas emission.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method for calculating the prediction of green house effect gas emission.
   Subtracting a portion for the effect by energy saving element from the annual CO2 emission amount by air conditioning installation (S1)
   Calculating the carbon dioxide emission reduction amount which shows the reduction of the annual CO2 emission amount which is estimated about an air conditioning installation (S2)
   Calculating the reduction essential quantity (S3)
   Determining the availability of credit-izing from the quantity and reduction essential quantity for the effect by energy saving element (S4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - The ceramics contain silicon dioxide, ferric oxide, aluminum oxide, magnesium oxide, and calcium oxide.

TI Disposable carbon dioxide cracking device has heating device which comprises wire having one end arranged with two cavities and other end connected to external initiator power source through terminal and gland.

   WANG Z
AE ZHANGJIAKOU FANGYU MACHINERY MFG CO LTD (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018213366
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide technology field, especially a carbon dioxide-induced cracking device, comprising a cracking pipe, heating device and the gland bush, bursting pipe and pressing cover cavity, a heating device is set in one end of the cavity. bursting the pipe bottom by the cylinder, the cylinder and the cylinder head, the cylinder bottom is provided with an exhaust valve, the cylinder head is provided with a liquid filling hole and the outer liquid filling port, a liquid charging hole connected with external liquid filling port, a liquid charging hole is provided with a filling valve device, an outer liquid filling opening is provided with a safety valve device, the heating device comprises paper cylinder, heat reactive material, electric ignition head, head, a lead wire with high strength low temperature glue, paper and the tip cavity, is provided in the cylinder head, the thermal conversion material arranged in the cavity, one end of the lead is installed in the cavity, and the other end passes through the end head and the gland are connected to external starting explosion device power supply. PT P
PN WO2021231650-A2; WO2021231650-A3
TI Storage of blood product by adding additive solution to blood product to prepare storable blood product and storing in di-2-ethylhexyl phthalate free blood compatible carbon dioxide permeable bag comprising specific gas permeability for carbon dioxide.
AU SOWEMIMO-COKER S O
AE HEMANEXT INC (HEMA-Non-standard)
GA 2021D07157
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for storage of a blood product involves obtaining a blood product having a %SO2 of greater than 30%, adding an additive solution to the blood product to prepare a storable blood product and storing in di-2-ethylhexyl phthalate free (DEHP-free) blood compatible (BC) carbon dioxide permeable bag comprising a gas permeability for carbon dioxide of at least 0.62 cm3/cm2 at about 1 atm at 25 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for storage of a blood product, treating a blood product and maintaining level of 2,3-DPG and ATP in a blood product (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides for whole blood and blood components having reduced levels of carbon dioxide and elimination of plasticizer DEHP, provides for maintenance of low oxygen content in blood and blood components during storage and provide for preparation of carbon dioxide depleted blood and blood components for storage that improves overall quality of transfused blood and improves health outcomes in patients and reduces risks associated with DEHP.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a container for storing blood comprising a DEHP-free carbon dioxide permeable and oxygen impermeable material, where the material comprises a gas permeability for oxygen of less than 0.05 cm3/cm2 at 1 atm at 25 degrees C and gas permeability for carbon dioxide of at least 0.62 cm3/cm2 at 1 atm at 25 degrees C;
   (2) method (M2) for treating a blood product involves adding an additive solution to the blood product and storing in a DEHP-free BC carbon dioxide permeable bag comprising a gas permeability for carbon dioxide of at least 0.62 cm3/cm2 at about 1 atm at 25 degrees C, where the storage is at least 7 days and the blood product comprising an oxygen level at the 7 days of storage that is decreased or about the same as an oxygen level in the blood product at day 1 of storage;
   (3) method (M3) for storing a storable blood involves placing a blood product in a storage container comprising a DEHP-free BC material having a permeability to carbon dioxide of at least 0.62 cm3/cm2 at about 1 atm at 25 degrees C and a permeability to oxygen of no more than 0.3 cm3/cm2 at about 1 atm and a carbon dioxide sorbent and storing the container comprising the storable blood for a period to prepare stored blood;
   (4) method (M4) for storing red blood cells involves placing the red blood cells in a storage container comprising an outer oxygen and carbon dioxide impermeable container enclosing a DEHP-free BC permeable inner collapsible container consisting of a material having a permeability to carbon dioxide of at least 0.62 cm3/cm2 at about 1 atm and a permeability to oxygen of no more than 0.3 cm3/cm2 at about 1 atm at 25 degrees C and enclosing a carbon dioxide sorbent, an oxygen sorbent, or an oxygen and a carbon dioxide sorbent between the inner and outer bag and storing the container comprising the red blood cells for at least 7 days to prepare a stored blood product;
   (5) method (M5) for maintaining level of 2,3-DPG and ATP in a blood product involves placing a blood product involving an oxygen saturation of at least 10% in a storage container comprising an outer oxygen and carbon dioxide impermeable container enclosing a BC material having a permeability to carbon dioxide of at least 0.62 cm3/cm2 at about 1 atm at 25 degrees C and a permeability to oxygen of no more than 0.3 cm3/cm2 at about 1 atm and enclosing a carbon dioxide sorbent between the inner and outer bag, and storing the container comprising the blood product, where the level of 2,3-DPG is increased for up to 14 days of storage compared to a level of 2,3-DPG of a blood product conventionally stored and the level of ATP is increased after 42 days of storage compared to a level of ATP of a blood product conventionally stored;
   (6) composition comprising a blood product chosen from whole blood, platelets and leukocytes and an additive solution comprising sodium bicarbonate, dibasic sodium phosphate, adenine, guanosine, glucose, mannitol, N-acetylcysteine, 6-hydroxy-2,5,7,8-tetramethylchroman-2-carboxylic acid (Trolox) and l-ascorbic acid (vitamin C);
   (7) an additive composition comprising N-acetylcysteine, 6-hydroxy-2,5,7,8-tetramethylchroman-2-carboxylic acid (Trolox) and l-ascorbic acid, where the additive composition comprises a pH 8-9;
   (8) composition comprising a blood product chosen from whole blood, platelets, and leukocytes and an additive solution comprising a concentration of dibasic sodium phosphate, sodium citrate, adenine, guanosine comprising a concentration of 1-2 mM, glucose, and mannitol; and
   (9) a device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The storable blood product is not deoxygenated prior and during the storing. The method involves depleting oxygen from the storable blood product during storage. The BC carbon dioxide permeable bag comprises an oxygen permeability of less than 0.3 cm3/cm2. Preferred Components: The BC carbon dioxide permeable bag does not comprise DEHT and the bag comprises 1,2-cyclohexane dicarboxylic acid diisononyl ester (DINCH) or butyryltrihexylcitrate (BTHC) as a plasticizer, where the bag is enclosed in an outer bag impermeable to oxygen and carbon dioxide. The material is chosen from polyvinyl chloride (PVC), polyolefin, silicone, polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF), polysulfone (PS), polypropylene (PP) or polyurethane. Preferred Composition: The BC carbon dioxide permeable bag comprises 20-70 weight/weight% in PVC of DINCH or BTHC as a plasticizer.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details about preparation and storage of red blood cells for sampling that do not support the claims.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); P33 (Medical aids, oral administration (A61G, H, J).); 
MC B04-B03A; B04-B04D; B04-F04A; B05-A01B; B05-B02A3; B06-A01; B06-D09; B10-A07A; B10-A07B; B10-C02; B10-C04E1; B11-B05; B15-C00; D05-H08B; A04-E02E; A04-E10B; A05-G01E; A06-A00E3; A08-P04; A11-C07; A12-V03B; A12-V03D; A12-W; P33-B; P34-A02

PN CN112717727-A; CN112717727-B
TI Glassy metal organic framework film material is material obtained by pressing obtained metal organic frameworks powder material into sheet-melting-cooling or hot pressing on substrate.
AU WANG B
   FENG X
   LI J
AE BEIJING INST TECHNOLOGY (BEIT-C)
GA 202147405V
AB    NOVELTY - Glassy metal organic framework (MOF) film material is a material obtained by pressing an obtained MOFs powder material into a sheet-melting-cooling or hot pressing on a substrate.
   USE - Glassy MOF film material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has hydrogen separation properties, universality, maximum hydrogen/carbon dioxide separation ratio of 92.7 and hydrogen flux of 230.4 Barrer, and is prepared by simple method, cost-effective and suitable for large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the film material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The MOFs powder material is synthesized from azole, phosphoric acid and metal salts. The MOFs powder material is prepared by mixing the above-mentioned raw materials, adding ethanol to grind, washing and drying the solid to obtain the product. The glassy MOF film is prepared by pressing-melting-cooling process of the MOFs powder material by pressing the MOFs material with glass transition properties, calcining at 180-210 degrees C and a pressure of 1-5 MPa for 0.5-1 hour, preferably 0.5 hour in a tube furnace for 0.5-1 hours, hot-pressing at 180-210 degrees C and a pressure of 0.6 MPa for 5 minutes, and cooling to obtain the material. Preferred Components: The azole comprises 5,6-dimethylimidazole or 2-ethylimidazole. The phosphoric acid comprises phosphoric acid or methylphosphonic acid. The metal salt comprises zinc, cadmium, copper, manganese, and iron.

TI Resin composition used for forming easy-tear film, comprises biomass polyolefin resin, and cyclic olefin resin.
AU ITO K
AE NIPPON POLYETHYLENE KK (NPPP-C)
GA 2021171549
AB    NOVELTY - A resin composition comprises biomass polyolefin resin (a1), and a cyclic olefin resin (b).
   USE - Resin composition used for forming easy-tear film used for packaging material (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to packaging bags, packaging containers for foods, chemicals, medical instruments, industrial component, miscellaneous articles and magazines.
   ADVANTAGE - The resin composition produces easy-tear film having excellent tearability in the film flow direction and vertical direction, saves petroleum resources, reduces carbon dioxide emissions, and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for easy-tear film, which comprises at least one resin layer (L1) comprising the resin composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The resin composition comprises 10-80 wt.% cyclic olefin resin (b). Preferred Components: The cyclic olefin resin (b) is an ethylene/cyclic olefin copolymer. The resin composition further comprises polyolefin resin (a2) other than the biomass polyolefin resin (a1). The easy-tear film further comprises resin layer (L2) comprising polyolefin resin (c), and resin layer (L3) comprising polyolefin resin (d). The easy-tear film comprises multilayer structure of at least three layers comprising resin layer (L1) comprising resin composition comprising at least a polyolefin-based resin (a) and cyclic olefin-based resin (b), resin layer (L2) comprising polyolefin resin (c) and resin layer (L3) comprising polyolefin resin (d). At least one or more of the polyolefin resin (a), polyolefin resin (c), or polyolefin resin (d) comprises biomass polyolefin resin (a1). The resin layer (L2) is outer layer. The resin layer (L3) is inner layer. The film comprises three-layer structure, in which the resin layer (L2), resin layer (L1) and resin layer (L3) is laminated. The polyolefin resin (c) and polyolefin resin (d) are polyethylene resins. The film comprises base material layer (L4). Preferred Properties: The cyclic olefin resin (b) has a glass transition temperature of 60 degrees C or more. The ratio of melt flow rate (M2) of the polyolefin resin (a2) at 190 degrees C and 2.16 kg to melt flow rate (M1) of the biomass polyolefin resin (a1) at 190 degrees C and 2.16 kg is 30 or less. The film has a Elmendorf tear strength of 30 N/mm or less measured in accordance with JIS K7128-2 in the film flow direction (MD) and vertical direction (TD).

PN CN110790284-A
TI Preparing medium silicon 13X molecular sieve            powder comprises taking seed crystal with high            silicon-aluminum ratio as guide agent, then mixing with            synthetic liquid, and aging at a low temperature, and            crystallizing.
AU CHEN W
   CHEN C
   WANG Y
   WANG P
   WEN X
   ZHANG J
   LI C
   ZHU L
   ZHANG F
AE SHANGHAI LUQIANG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SHNR-C)
   SHANGHAI CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (SHNR-C)
GA 202015429P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing medium silicon 13X molecular sieve                powder comprises taking seed crystal with high                silicon-aluminum ratio as guide agent, then mixing                with synthetic liquid, and aging at a low                temperature to obtain crystallization liquid, and                crystallizing to obtain final product.
   USE - The molecular sieve powder is useful for                removing carbon dioxide impurities in air at low                partial pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple preparation process, low                input cost, stable product performance and                excellent repeatability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i)                  taking silicon dioxide and aluminum oxide in a                  molar ratio of 10-18, sodium oxide/aluminum oxide                  in a molar ratio of 10-20, and water and aluminum                  oxide in a molar ratio of 250-450, and mixing                  aluminum source, silicon source, sodium hydroxide                  and water, then stirring at 20-70 degrees C for                  0-10 hours, and allowing to stand at room                  temperature for 12-72 hours to obtain seed                  crystals, (ii) taking silicon dioxide and                  aluminum oxide in a molar ratio of 2.2-3.5,                  sodium oxide and aluminum oxide in a molar ratio                  of 2.6-4.8, and water and aluminum oxide in a                  molar ratio of 100-250, mixing aluminum source,                  silicon source, sodium hydroxide and water,                  stirring at 10-60 degrees C for 0.1-6 hours to                  obtain synthetic solution, (iii) adding the seed                  crystal prepared in step (i) (0.05-10 wt.% of the                  total mass of the synthetic solution in step                  (ii), then stirring at 0-50 degrees C for 0.5-6                  hours, and aging at 5-70 degrees C for 2-10 hours                  to obtain crystallization solution, and (iv)                  placing the crystallizing solution obtained in                  step (iii) in a reaction kettle, and then close                  the crystallization at 90-120 degrees C for 3-12                  hours, separating, washing and drying to obtain                  medium silicon 13X molecular sieve powder. The                  mixing method of aluminum source, silicon source,                  sodium hydroxide and water is: dissolving                  aluminum source and silicon source in sodium                  hydroxide solution respectively, and removing                  impurities by filtering to obtain silicon                  solution and aluminum solution, and slowly adding                  the silicon solution into the aluminum solution                  dropwise. in step (ii), the temperature of the                  stirring is room temperature, and the stirring                  time is 0.5-4 hours. In step (iv), the                  crystallization temperature is 95-110 degrees C,                  and the crystallization time is 4-8 hours. The                  silicon-aluminum ratio of the medium silicon 13X                  molecular sieve powder is 2.25-2.30, carbon                  dioxide adsorption performance: carbon dioxide                  adsorption capacity is greater than or equal to                  7.5 wt.% under 2-3 mmHg pressure, the saturated                  adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide is greater                  than or equal to 24 wt.% under 250 mmHg pressure,                  which is used for the removal of carbon dioxide                  impurities in the air at a low partial pressure.                  Preferred Components: The aluminum oxide                  concentration in the aluminum solution is 50-300                  g/l, the sodium oxide concentration is 30-350                  g/l, the silicon dioxide concentration in the                  silicon solution is 100-400 g/l, and the sodium                  oxide concentration is 50-250 g/l. The molar                  concentration ratio of aluminum oxide and sodium                  oxide in the aluminum solution is 1.5-2.5, and                  the molar concentration ratio of silicon dioxide                  and sodium oxide in the silicon solution is 2-4.                  The molar ratio of the seed crystals in step (i)                  is silicon dioxide and aluminum oxide in a ratio                  of 12-16, sodium oxide and aluminum oxide in a                  ratio of 15-20, and water and aluminum oxide in a                  ratio of 280-350. In step (iii), the addition                  amount of the seed crystals is 1-8% of the total                  mass of the synthetic liquid, stirring is carried                  out at room temperature for 0.5-3 hours, aging                  temperature is from room temperature to 40                  degrees C, and the aging time is 4-8 hours.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN US2019224618-A1; US10632417-B2
TI Treating hydrocarbon gas stream, comprises sending hydrocarbon gas stream to membrane unit to be separated into residue stream and permeate gas stream, sending permeate gas stream to solvent absorption unit and recovering treated gas.
AU TANNA N P
   LECHNICK W J
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 201964359X
AB    NOVELTY - Treating a hydrocarbon gas stream, comprises: sending the hydrocarbon gas stream to a membrane unit to be separated into a residue stream and a permeate gas stream; sending the permeate gas stream to a solvent absorption unit to remove carbon dioxide and other impurities; and recovering a treated gas.
   USE - The process is useful for treating a hydrocarbon gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: offers nearly 100% hydrocarbon recovery; and is environmentally friendly and cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process: the permeate gas stream is not compressed before being sent to the solvent absorption unit; the permeate gas stream is compressed before being sent to the solvent absorption unit; greater than 99.5 wt.% of hydrocarbons in the hydrocarbon gas stream are recovered; greater than 99.9 wt.% of hydrocarbons in the hydrocarbon gas stream are recovered; the hydrocarbon gas stream is treated to remove water and other contaminants before being sent to the membrane unit; the permeate gas stream is cooled and liquid is then removed from the permeate gas stream before passing to the solvent absorption unit; during the regenerating of the solvent, acid gas is removed from a solvent in the absorption unit; the feed is at a temperature of 16-60 degrees C, the residue is at a temperature of 27-82 degrees C, and the permeate is at a temperature of 4-88 degrees C; the feed is at a pressure of 2068-7584 kPa and the permeate is at a pressure of 69-2068 kPa; and the treated gas is cooled and then sent through a water wash before exiting. The process further comprises: regenerating the solvent in the solvent absorption unit; and washing the treated gas, where the water in the acid gas is condensed and then a portion of the acid gas is sent to be flared, incinerated or routed to a sulfur recovery unit, and the portion of the acid gas is recycled to the solvent absorption unit. Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon gas stream is natural gas, synthesis gas, flue gas or biogas. The treated gas comprises 2-30% impurities of the feed gas. The solvent absorption unit contains a solvent including methyldiethanolamine, piperazine, diethanolamine, monoethanolamine, triethanolamine, diglycolamine, diisopropanolamine, triethylene glycol and/or dimethyl ethers of polyethylene glycol. The impurities are carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide or carbonyl sulfide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Sewage treatment using Fenton reaction comprises            mixing hydrogen peroxide with sewage, continuously            adding ferrous solution along flow direction of            hydrogen peroxide containing sewage, aerating,            neutralizing, flocculating and precipitating.
AU HU X
   ZHANG B
AE JIAOZUO YUQIAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAO-Non-standard)
   HU X (HUXX-Individual)
GA 201963774U
AB    NOVELTY - Sewage treatment using Fenton reaction                comprises mixing hydrogen peroxide with sewage,                continuously adding ferrous solution along the flow                direction of the hydrogen peroxide containing                sewage in the catalytic reaction tank at multiple                points to carry out the catalytic reaction and                aerating, neutralizing, flocculating and                precipitating.
   USE - The method is useful in sewage treatment using                Fenton reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method decomposes the refractory                substances in the water carbon dioxide, thus                reducing the Chemical oxygen demand in the                sewage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The ferrous solution                  dosing point is set along the flow direction of                  the material and number of dosing points is                  greater than or equal to 3. The pH of the                  neutralized material is 7.5-8.5. The flocculating                  agent is anionic polyacrylamide. Preferred                  Method: The ferrous solution is adding                  continuously at the same time at each dosing                  point.

PT P
PN IN201721042669-A; IN406816-B
TI Producing hydrogen rich syngas involves providing            biomass feedstock, feeding biomass feedstock into            reactor, where reactor is loaded with metal catalyst,            providing gasifying agent into reactor, and exposing            biomass feedstock in reactor.
AU GHOLKAR P V
   TANKSALE A
   SHASTRI Y
AE IITB-MONASH RES ACAD (IITB-Non-standard)
   IITB-MONASH RES ACAD (IITB-Non-standard)
GA 2019634842
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen rich syngas involves                providing biomass feedstock, feeding biomass                feedstock into reactor, where reactor is loaded                with metal catalyst, providing gasifying agent into                reactor, exposing biomass feedstock in reactor with                metal catalyst in presence of gasifying agent for                Reactive Flash Volatilization (RFV) reaction, and                extracting hydrogen rich syngas from reactor, where                hydrogen rich syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide and methane.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen rich                syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce hydrogen rich                syngas that has high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal catalyst                  comprises multiple of transition metal elements                  which is supported on post transition metal                  element which is maintained at 400-650 degrees C.                  The transition metal elements are metals selected                  from Nickel (Ni), Copper (Cu), Palladium (Pd),                  Platinum (Pt), Rhodium (Rh), Rhenium (Re),                  Ruthenium (Ru), Iron (Fe). The post transition                  metal element comprises Aluminum (Al) or gamma                  Alumina. The each transition metal element                  comprises nanoparticles of metal or metal alloy                  with particle size of 20-100 nanometer. The                  biomass feedstock comprises microalgae in dried                  powdered or slurry form, fed at room temperature                  and pressure. The biomass feedstock is selected                  from Scenedesmus species. The slag contains                  transition elements, non-metals, post transition                  elements, metalloids, alkali and alkaline earth                  metals. The alkali and alkaline metals comprise                  sodium, potassium, magnesium and Calcium. The                  non-metals comprise phosphorus. The metalloids                  comprise silicon. The metals comprise zinc, iron.                  The post transition elements comprise aluminum.                  Preferred Composition: The biomass feedstock                  comprises 11 % ash, 35-42% Carbon, 4-8% Hydrogen,                  4-10% Nitrogen, 0.1-2% sulfur and 40-60% of                  oxygen. Preferred Process: The biomass feedstock                  is dropped onto the metal catalyst by a screw                  feeder and allowed to react in the reactor for                  0.5-100 seconds. The metal catalyst is preheated                  to 400 degrees C and reduced in presence of                  hydrogen. The gasifying agent comprises steam                  having temperature of 150-180 degrees C, provided                  into the reactor at an atmospheric pressure. The                  biomass feedstock is converted into gaseous phase                  before contacting the catalyst surface or the                  biomass feedstock gets pyrolyzed by contacting a                  bed formed due to transformation of elements into                  tar and gas and reacts in the metal catalyst bed                  formed due to the element transformation.                  Preferred Condition: The reaction temperature is                  maintained at 550-650 degrees C. The                  concentration of steam in the reactor is adjusted                  to maintain ratio of carbon-to-steam in reactor                  in the range of 0.1-0.8. The volume percentage                  (v/v) of molecular hydrogen in the extracted                  hydrogen rich syngas is 25-70%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC E31-A02C; N01-A; N01-B; N01-C03; N02-A01; N02-B01; N02-C01; N02-D01; N02-E; N02-F02; N03; N06-F; N07-J

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for increasing production of a product comprising culturing the microorganism in the presence of a gaseous substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The first exogenous CoA transferase replaces a coding region of an acetolactate decarboxylase gene. At least one additional exogenous CoA transferase replaces a coding region of an aldehyde-alcohol dehydrogenase gene. The microorganism further comprises an exogenous thiolase and an exogenous decarboxylase selected from acetoacetate decarboxylase or alpha ketoisovalerate decarboxylase, or their combination, function with the first exogenous CoA transferase or function with the least one additional exogenous CoA transferase. The exogenous CoA transferase are Clostridium acetobutylicum CtfA and CtfB, or Clostridium beijerinckii CtfA and CtfB. The exogenous CoA transferase are native and non-native to the microorganism. The exogenous CoA transferase are the same and different. The exogenous CoA transferases are under the control of both an acetolactate decarboxylase promoter and at least one other promoter. The microorganism does not produce 2,3-butanediol. The microorganism produces one or more of acetone and isopropanol. The microorganism further comprises a disruptive mutation in a primary secondary alcohol dehydrogenase gene, a thioesterase gene, a 3-hydroxybutyryl coA dehydrogenase gene, or their combination. The microorganism is a 1C fixing microorganism. The microorganism is a bacterium. The microorganism is a member of a genus selected from Acetobacterium, Alkalibaculum, Blautia, Butyribacterium, Clostridium, Eubacterium, Moorella, Oxobacter, Sporomusa or Thermoanaerobacter. The gaseous substrate comprises a 1C-carbon source comprising one or more of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The gaseous substrate comprises syngas or industrial waste gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The exogenous CoA transferases enable production of 1-butanol, butyrate, butene, butadiene, methyl ethyl ketone, ethylene, acetone, isopropanol, lipids, 3-hydroxypropionate, terpenes, isoprene, fatty acids, 2-butanol, 1,2-propanediol, 1-propanol, 1-hexanol, 1-octanol, chorismate derived products, 3-hydroxybutyrate, 1,3-butanediol, 2-hydroxyisobutyrate or 2-hydroxyisobutyric acid, isobutylene, adipic acid, keto-adipic acid, 1,3-hexanediol, 3-methyl-2-butanol, 2-buten-l-ol, isovalerate, isoamyl alcohol or monoethylene glycol.

PT P
PN CN113968933-A; CN113968933-B
TI Preparation of cationic polyacrylamide particles            used as flocculants, involves forming cationic            polyacrylamide, adding bicarbonate into the            polymerization reaction system, supplying carbon            dioxide gas at preset pressure, and mixing cationic            polyacrylamide with organic acid.
AU ZHENG S
   GUO Y
   XU Z
   XU J
   YUAN L
   ZOU H
   LI S
   DUAN L
AE JIANGSU FEYMER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202222627M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cationic polyacrylamide                particles involves:
1) polymerizing acrylamide and cationic                monomers to form cationic polyacrylamide;
2) adding bicarbonate into the polymerization                reaction system comprising cationic polyacrylamide                particles, supplying carbon dioxide gas during                polymerization reaction, maintaining a pressure                higher than atmospheric pressure and lower than 1.1                MPa, followed by mixing the cationic polyacrylamide                particles with organic acid.
   USE - The prepared cationic polyacrylamide particles                are used as flocculants.
   ADVANTAGE - The cationic polyacrylamide particles prepared                have the property of dissolving quickly at low                temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                cationic polyacrylamide particles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the SEM image of cationic                polyacrylamide particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: During preparation of                  cationic polyacrylamide particles, carbon dioxide                  gas is passed into the reaction system,                  preferably at pressure 0.15-1 MPa. Before                  introducing carbon dioxide gas, the                  polymerization system is deoxidized by using                  inert gas. The polymerization reaction is                  performed for 2-5 hours. The cationic                  polyacrylamide particles are porous particles                  mixed with organic acid, at room                  temperature.Preferred Components: The bicarbonate used                  is selected from sodium bicarbonate, potassium                  bicarbonate and/or calcium bicarbonate. The                  organic acid used is selected from adipic acid,                  tartaric acid, gluconic acid, L-malic acid and/or                  citric acid. The cationic monomer is selected                  from acryloyloxyethyltrimethyl ammonium chloride,                  methacryloyloxyethyltrimethylammonium chloride                  and/or methacryloylpropyltrimethylammonium                  chloride.The mass ratio of cationic monomer and                  acrylamide used is 20-100: 0-80. The amount of                  bicarbonate used is 0.3-3 percent by mass. The                  amount of organic acid used is 0.5-3.5 percent by                  mass.The polymerization system also comprises an                  initiator, metal chelating agent, chain transfer                  agent and cosolvent. The amount of metal                  chelating agent in the reaction system, is                  0.005-0.020 percent by mass. The amount of chain                  transfer agent is 0.001-0.010 percent by mass and                  the amount of cosolvent is 0.05-1.0 percent by                  mass. The metal chelating agent is selected from                  ethylenediamine tetraacetic acid, disodium                  ethylenediamine tetraacetic acid,                  diethylenetriamine and/or pentasodium                  pentaacetate. The chain transfer agent is                  selected from isopropanol, ethylene glycol,                  ethanol, sodium hypophosphite and/or sodium                  formate. The cosolvent is selected from urea,                  polyoxyethylene alkyl ether and/or polyethylene                  glycol.The initiator comprises a composite oxidant                  initiator, reducing agent and azo initiator. The                  amount of oxidant present in the reaction system,                  is 0.001-0.005 percent by mass. The amount of                  reducing agent present, is 0.001-0.005 percent by                  mass. The oxidant is selected from potassium                  persulfate, sodium persulfate, ammonium                  persulfate and/or hydrogen peroxide. The reducing                  agent is selected ferrous sulfate, sodium sulfite                  and/or sodium bisulfite. The azo initiator is a                  combination of azobisisobutylimidazoline                  hydrochloride, azobisisobutylamidine                  hydrochloride and azobisimidazolinyl propane. The                  amount of azobisisobutylimidazoline hydrochloride                  in azo initiator, is 0.001-0.005 percent by mass                  and the amount of azobisisobutylamidine                  hydrochloride is 0.001-0.005 percent by mass. The                  amount of azobisimidazolinyl propane in the azo                  initiator, is 0.001-0.005 percent by mass. The                  cationic polyacrylamide particles have particle                  size distribution 20-80 meshes and bulk density                  0.4-0.5 g/cm3.

PN CN113877362-A
TI Selective adsorption and separation of nitrogen and proximity gas by using 5- aminotetrazole-zinc energetic complex as adsorbent, useful for separation of nitrogen and methane gas components in later flue gas (composed of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and nitrogen).
AU ZHANG P
   XU M
   MO H
   LIU N
   TAN B
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 202211122P
AB    NOVELTY - Selective adsorption and separation of nitrogen and proximity gas by using 5- aminotetrazole-zinc energetic complex as adsorbent, is claimed.
   USE - The method is useful for separation of nitrogen and methane gas components in the later flue gas (the flue gas flow generated by fuel combustion, which is mainly composed of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and nitrogen).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has selective and excellent adsorption effect on nitrogen.
The method provides new idea for the adsorption and separation of nitrogen and methane molecules with very similar physical and chemical properties, especially for solving the key common technical problems in the purification/recovery process of unconventional methane gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The proximity gas is methane or carbon dioxide. The 5- aminotetrazole-zinc energetic complex is tetrakis(5-aminotetrazole)zinc energetic complex of formula (I).Preferred Method: (I) is prepared by: either stirring N,N-dimethylformamide and water, adding 5-aminotetrazole and zinc nitrate hexahydrate, and heating at 40 degrees Celsius to dissolve completely to obtain colorless clear liquid, then cooling to room temperature, filtering out insoluble substance, placing the filtrate in a clean beaker, and slowly volatilizing for 7 days at room temperature to obtain colorless crystal, and then filtering off the reaction liquid to obtain (I) as white solid; or adding water into N,N-dimethylformamide, adding 5-aminotetrazole and zinc nitrate hexahydrate while stirring, stirring to dissolve completely, heating and reacting at 90 degrees Celsius for 24 hours, then cooling to room temperature, filtering, and drying to obtain (I). The mol ratio of 5-aminotetrazole to zinc nitrate hexahydrate is 1:2. The amount of N,N-dimethylformamide is 80 ml/1 mmol 5-aminotetrazole. The amount of water is 20 ml/1 mmol 5-aminotetrazole.

TI Preparation of aerogel felt by mixing sol with the gel catalyst, mixing with fiber felt, transporting into microwave cavity to carry out microwave heating, curing, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide drying.
AU DENG L
   SONG D
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 2021E7773D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aerogel felt comprises mixing sol with the gel catalyst to obtain mixed solution, mixing with fiber felt to obtain wet gel felt, transporting into microwave cavity to carry out microwave heating at 55-60degrees Celsius, transporting to insulation section, curing at room temperature, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide drying.
   USE - The method is for preparation of aerogel felt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the problem that the wet gel felt has fast external gel and slow internal gel caused by the traditional water tank heating process, ensures the perfect gel inside the wet gel felt by reducing the belt running speed or lengthening the transportation distance, and has greatly improved production efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INDUSTRIAL STANDARDS - Preferred Components: The ratio of sol and gel catalyst is 10:0.1-1.Preferred Method: The gel catalyst is prepared by mixing basic catalyst and organic solvent. The basic catalyst is 25-28% concentrated ammonia water, 1-90% ammonium fluoride aqueous solution, silane coupling agent KH-502 and/or silane coupling agent KH-550. The sol is prepared by mixing and hydrolyzing silicon source precursor, solvent, and hydrolysis catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent comprises refined methanol and/or 95% ethanol.

PN CN113325108-A; ZA202202828-A
TI Method for detecting tacrolimus ointment related            substance, involves pre-treating sample and detecting            tacrolimus related substance, where pre-treatment of            sample uses supercritical fluid to extract related            substance of tacrolimus.
AU ZHU Z
   CAO H
   WANG S
   LV M
   DONG S
   LIU Z
   DONG X
AE SHANDONG GUANXIU BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG TEKING PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A7847B
AB    NOVELTY - Detection of substance related to tacrolimus                ointment comprises pre-treating sample using                n-hexane-n-butyl chloride-acetonitrile solvent and                detecting tacrolimus related substance. The                pre-treatment of sample uses supercritical fluid to                extract the related substance of tacrolimus. The                supercritical fluid is carbon dioxide. The                extraction temperature is 60-65 degrees C. The                extracting pressure is 20-35 MPa. The extracting                time is 45-60 minutes. The flow speed of carbon                dioxide is 0.1-0.8 l/minute. The method utilizes                the matrix which is propylene carbonate, white                petrolatum, mineral oil, white wax, and                paraffin.
   USE - Method for detection of tacrolimus ointment                related substance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables detecting the ointment                tacrolimus related substance in a simple                manner.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PT P
PN JP2021115508-A
TI The modification catalyst in which catalyst performance does not deteriorate due to the sulfur component contained in fuel gas and its manufacturing method.
AU OKAMOTO Y
   HIDAKA S
AE DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
GA 202191873S
AB    NOVELTY - Reforming catalyst comprises a barium oxide compound (I) as a composite powder.
   USE - Reforming catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalyst performance and shape retention capability, is not easily deteriorated by sulfur component contained in fuel gas, ensures modification activity, and exhibits long time catalytic function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reforming catalyst comprises a barium oxide compound of formula: (BaO1-a(CO3)a)YM14-cM2cO6-b (I) as a composite powder.
   a = 0-1;
   b = 0-4.5;
   c = 0-4;and
   M1 and M2 = transition metals chosen from Co, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Ni, Cu, and Zn.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing the reforming catalyst, involving preparing base particle powder in a reducing gas comprising carbon dioxide or in reducing gas not containing carbon dioxide by heat-treating the base particle powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite powder comprises a barium oxide compound of formula: (BaO1-a(CO3)a)YCo4-cM2cO6-b (II), a barium oxide compound of formula: BaO1-a(CO3)aYCo4O6-b (III), a barium compound of formula: BaYM14-cM2cO7+~s (IV), a barium compound of formula: BaYCo4-cM2cO7+~s (V), and a barium compound of formula: BaYCo4O7+~s (VI). The reforming catalyst comprises a (a) base particle, (b) barium carbonate fine particles adhered to the surface of the base particle, and (c) transition metal fine particles comprising (1c) at least one of fine particles (M1) and fine particles (M2) adhered to the surface of the base particle, where the transition metals (M1) and (M2) are chosen from Co, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Ni, Cu, and Zn, (2c) cobalt fine particles or fine particles (M2) adhered to the surface of the base particle, where fine particles (M2) is a transition metal chosen from V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Ni, Cu and Zn, or (3c) cobalt fine particles adhered to the surface of the base particle. The reforming catalyst comprises catalyst metal and carrier supporting the catalyst metal. Preferred Properties: The average particle size of barium carbonate fine particles is 10-50 nm. The average particle size of the transition metal fine particles is 0.5-30 nm. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the composite powder is 20-80%.a = 0-1;b = 0-4.5;c = 0-4;M2 = transition metal chosen from group consisting of V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Ni, Cu, and Zn;andigma = less than or equal to 1.5 or 0-1.5.
   EXAMPLE - Example contains tables 1-5 described properties, so do not support the claims.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN113149056-A; CN113149056-B
TI Preparing calcium carbonate by passing carbon            dioxide gas into calcium acetate solution in form of            microbubbles, performing carbonization to obtain            reacted slurry, and subjecting it to solid-liquid            separation to obtain calcium carbonate and separation            liquid.
AU LIU S
   DU H
   WANG S
   WANG Y
   LIU B
   TIAN W
AE SHANXI SHIQI SHIDAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOL (SHAN-Non-standard)
   INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2021895119
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing calcium carbonate by                enhanced carbonization of microbubbles, involves                (a) passing carbon dioxide gas into the calcium                acetate solution in the form of microbubbles, and                obtaining the reacted slurry through carbonization                reaction, and (b) after reacting, subjecting the                slurry to solid-liquid separation to obtain calcium                carbonate and separation liquid.
   USE - Method for preparing calcium carbonate by                enhanced carbonization of microbubbles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high carbonization reaction                efficiency and mild preparation conditions, and                while obtaining the nano calcium carbonate,                simultaneously realizes the direct regeneration                cycle of the separated liquid, which saves the                preparation cost, and avoids the generation of                three wastes at the source, which has high economic                and environmental benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method includes (a)                  introducing carbon dioxide gas into a calcium                  acetate solution with a pH of 5-14 and a                  concentration of 0.1-2 mol/l in the form of                  microbubbles with a size of 20 nm to 1000 mu m,                  and performing carbonization reaction for 0.2-7                  hours under normal pressure and 5-120 degrees C                  conditions to obtain the reaction slurry, and (b)                  after reacting, subjecting the slurry to                  solid-liquid separation to obtain calcium                  carbonate and separation liquid, where the                  separation liquid contains calcium acetate and                  acetic acid, and reacting the separation liquid                  with the calcium-containing raw material to                  obtain a circulating calcium acetate solution,                  which is returned to step (a) for recycling. The                  separation liquid in step (b) reacts with                  calcium-containing raw materials to obtain a                  circulating calcium acetate solution, which is                  returned to step (a) for recycling. Preferred                  Conditions: The microbubbles in step (a) include                  microbubbles and/or nanobubbles. The                  carbonization reaction in step (a) is performed                  at 5-120 degrees C, preferably 40-95 degrees C                  for 0.2-7 hours, preferably 0.5-3 hours, and the                  pressure of the carbonization reaction is normal                  pressure. Preferred Composition: The                  concentration of the calcium acetate solution in                  step (a) is 0.1-2 mol/l, preferably 0.1-1.8                  mol/l. In step (b), the separation liquid                  contains calcium acetate, and further contains                  acetic acid. Preferred Components: The                  calcium-containing raw material includes any one                  or a combination of at least two of limestone,                  calcium carbonate, calcium oxide or calcium                  hydroxide. Preferred Properties: The pH of the                  calcium acetate solution in step (a) is 5-14,                  preferably 7-12. The size of the microbubbles in                  step (a) is 20 nm to 1000 mu m, preferably 20 nm                  to 100 mu m.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN CN113149070-A
TI Preparing composite silica useful as adsorbent in adsorption-degradation of formaldehyde, and preparing anti-ultraviolet reinforcing agent, comprises e.g. using titanium tetrachloride, reacting, self-organizing and coating.
AU ZHI X
   WANG Y
   LIN D
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 202191305E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite silica comprises (i) using titanium tetrachloride for reacting with sodium metasilicate and self-organizing to obtain co-precipitated core, and(ii) using the precipitation method for preparing high specific surface area silica and coating the co-precipitated core to obtain composite white carbon black.
   USE - The composite silica is useful as adsorbent in adsorption-degradation of formaldehyde, and preparing anti-ultraviolet reinforcing agent (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: protects the inner co-precipitation core so that the material does not shell; ensures solidity of the material, and the carbon dioxide is less harmful and economical than the sulfuric acid used in the traditional precipitation method to make white carbon black; absorbs carbon dioxide; and environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) specifically comprises (a) preparing specific concentration of sodium silicate solution, adding specific amount of surfactant, and starting mechanical stirring, and(b) maintaining the sodium silicate solution in a water bath at a constant temperature of 40-100 degrees C, continuously mechanically stirring at high speed, adding specific concentration of titanium(IV) chloride solution, and controlling the adding speed. The concentration of the sodium silicate is 0.1-30 g/l. The concentration of the titanium(IV) chloride solution is 0.1-30/l. The volume ratio of sodium silicate solution and titanium(IV) chloride solution is 1-9:1-9. In the step (a), the mass ratio of sodium silicate and surfactant is 1:0.0001-0.1. The concentration of surfactant is 0.01-30 g/l. In the step (b), the dropping rate of the titanium(IV) chloride solution is 0.1-1000 ml/minutes. The step (ii) specifically (a) adding titanium(IV) chloride, specific concentration of hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid, controlling the adding speed, and stopping adding when the pH reaches 9-10, turning off the stirring, and allowing to stand for 30 minutes, (b) filtering the solution, placing the filter cake into a constant temperature solution, controlling specific concentration, adding acid for adjusting the pH value of 3-5, mechanically vigorously stirring for half an hour, and allowing to stand for 0.5-2 hours, and(c) filtering and washing for more than three times, spraying-drying at 450 degrees C to obtain composite powder product. The constant temperature is 40-100 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (a), the surfactant includes stearic acid, polyethylene glycol, sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, lauric acid and/or ethanol.

PN CN113087019-A; CN113087019-B
TI Preparation of ferromagnetic vanadium dioxide used            in magneto-optical and magneto-electric applications,            involves dispersing nonmagnetic vanadium dioxide in            mixed solvent, injecting carbon dioxide, reacting under            supercritical conditions, cooling, separating, and            drying separated liquid.
AU ZHOU Y
   XU Q
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 2021825774
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ferromagnetic vanadium dioxide,                involves dispersing nonmagnetic vanadium dioxide in                a mixed solvent of 10-90 vol.% N-methylpyrrolidone                and water to obtain a dispersion, transferring the                dispersion to a supercritical device, injecting                carbon dioxide into the supercritical device,                stirring and reacting for 3-6 hours under                supercritical conditions, cooling naturally to room                temperature, releasing pressure, separating the                system after supercritical treatment, collecting                the separating liquid, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of ferromagnetic vanadium dioxide                used in magneto-optical and magneto-electric                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and                provides ferromagnetic vanadium dioxide, which can                generate stronger and more stable magnetic                response.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The supercritical                  conditions include a temperature of 40-200                  degrees C and a pressure of 8-30 MPa. Preferred                  Composition: The ratio of nonmagnetic vanadium                  dioxide and mixed solvent is 0.1-10 mg/ml.                  Preferred Component: The crystal phase of the                  nonmagnetic vanadium dioxide is A, B, M or R                  phase.

PT P
PN WO2021075043-A1; JP2021512832-X
TI Ultra-fine bubble-containing solution used in drink and pharmaceutical for preventing and treating disease caused by active oxygen, comprises carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas inside ultra-fine bubble.
AU FUJITA T
   ZHANG L
   DODBIBA G
   MATSUI H
   KUROKAWA H
   SUZUKI C
AE UNIV TOKYO (UYTY-C)
   UNIV TSUKUBA (UTSK-C)
   BELLPACK CO LTD (BELL-Non-standard)
   BELL TECHNO SERVICE CO LTD (BELL-Non-standard)
GA 2021420640
AB    NOVELTY - An ultra-fine bubble-containing solution comprises carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas inside ultra-fine bubbles.
   USE - Ultra-fine bubble-containing solution is useful in ultra-fine bubble-containing drink and pharmaceutical for preventing and treating disease caused by active oxygen. The disease is senility disease and cancer (all claimed). The medicine is also useful for providing antioxidant effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The ultra-fine bubble-containing solution having excellent active oxygen scavenging ability, cytotoxic effect on cancer cells, and antitumor effect on cancer-bearing mice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an ultra-fine bubble-containing drink, which comprises the ultra-fine bubble-containing solution; and
   (2) an ultra-fine bubble-containing pharmaceutical, which comprises the ultra-fine bubble containing solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The ultra-fine bubble containing carbon dioxide has 50% average particle size of 50-300 nm. The ultra-fine bubble containing hydrogen has 50% average particle size of 10-500 nm. Preferred Composition: The ultra-fine bubble comprises 100 million/mL to 10 billion/mL carbon dioxide, and 10 million/mL to 50 billion/mL hydrogen. Preferred Components: The gas inside the ultra-fine bubble is generated by at least one chosen from porous ceramics pass, porous plastic film pass, double bottle hydrogen generator, pressurized dissolution system, and spiral flow system. The carbon dioxide inside the ultra-fine bubble is generated by passing carbon dioxide through the porous ceramics. The hydrogen inside the ultra-fine bubble is hydrogen generated by electrolysis using a double bottle hydrogen generator. Both carbon dioxide and hydrogen produced by at least one chosen from pressurized dissolution system and a spiral flow system.
   ACTIVITY - Nootropic; Cytostatic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

GA 202103111E
AB    NOVELTY - Non-aqueous amine solution comprises 5-30 %mass active component, 50-90 %mass non-aqueous solvent, 1-20 %mass absorption rate promoter, 1-15 %mass absorption capacity enhancer, 1-10 %mass regeneration rate promoter, 0.1-5 %mass regeneration lean enhancer, 0.01-1 %mass antioxidant, 0.01-1 %mass corrosion inhibitor, and 0.01-1 %mass additives, where the amine solution does not contain water.

   ADVANTAGE - The amine solution has strong solvation effect, low regeneration energy consumption, high absorption capacity, fast speed, excellent oxidation resistance and low corrosion.

The additive is monoethanolamine phosphate, tributyl phosphate and/or methyldiethanolamine phosphate.

   KHABEEV R R (KHAB-Individual)
   OLISKEVICH V V (OLIS-Individual)
   KNYAZEV M YU (KNYA-Individual)
GA 202009468E
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to production of inorganic materials, namely chemically deposited chalk, intended for food, medical, perfume-cosmetic, chemical, petrochemical, paint and other industries. Method of producing chemically precipitated chalk includes annealing of natural chalk to lime with extraction of carbon dioxide, suppression of obtained lime with water to lime milk, filtration on vibrating sieves, carbonization of purified lime milk with production of chalk milk with carbon dioxide. Further, the obtained chemically precipitated chalk is dried. Prior to drying, lime milk is washed in the medium of complete ozonation of air. Calcination, quenching and carbonization are carried out in the medium "air: ozonized air". Duration of annealing of natural chalk makes 110 hours from the moment of reaching of 850900C in natural chalk loading zone.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention simplifies the technology of producing chemically precipitated PCC chalk while improving the quality of the finished product.

PN CN110372757-A
TI Metal complex used as photocatalyst for reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide; metal complex is prepared by ligand 4,4'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine with iron nitrate nonahydrate or manganese pentacarbonyl bromide.
AU XU Q
   LIU R
   KOU J
AE UNIV YUNNAN NORMAL (UNYN-C)
GA 201990265B
AB    NOVELTY - Metal complex, is claimed. The metal complex is prepared by ligand 4,4'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine with iron nitrate nonahydrate or manganese pentacarbonyl bromide to obtain metal complex.
   USE - The metal complex is used as photocatalyst for the reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal complex: uses dimethylbipyridine as ligand: is simple to prepare; and has high purity and high yield during the preparation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal complex, is claimed. The metal complex is prepared by ligand 4,4'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine with iron nitrate nonahydrate or manganese pentacarbonyl bromide to obtain metal complex i.e. carbon-hydrogen-iron-nitrogen-oxygen complex of formula (C24H25Fe2N8O13.5) (I) or carbon-hydrogen-bromine-manganese-nitrogen complex of formula (C24H24Br2MnN4) (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The form of the metal complex is a solid crystal; the space group of the metal complex (I) is P2(1/c), the cell parameters includes a=16.927(3)A degrees , b=14.216(3), c=13.546(3)A degrees , alpha = gamma =90 degrees , beta =93.74, gamma =3252.7(11)A degrees 3; the space group of the metal complex (II) is Pbcn, the unit cell parameters includes a=15.460(3)A degrees , b = 10.297 (2), c=15.579(3)A degrees , alpha = beta = gamma =90 degrees , gamma =2479.9A degrees 3. Preferred Method: The preparation of the metal complex carbon-hydrogen-iron-nitrogen-oxygen complex of formula (C24H25Fe2N8O13.5) (I) comprises (a) weighing iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate (Fe(NO3)3.9H2O) in round bottom flask (according to the Fe(NO3)3.9H2O to 4,4'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine molar ratio 1:2); first dissolving 4,4'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine in methanol, and dropwise adding to the round bottom flask; (b) condensing and refluxing the mixed solution obtained in the step (a) for 3-5 hours, completing reaction, removing the solvent by rotary evaporation to obtain sample; and (c) washing the sample obtained in step (b), dissolving was dissolved in methanol, and obtaining single crystal sample of metal complex (I) by diethyl ether diffusion method. The preparation of the metal complex carbon-hydrogen-bromine-manganese-nitrogen complex of formula (C24H24Br2MnN4) (II) comprises (i) weighing manganese pentacarbonyl bromide (Mn(CO)5Br) in round bottom flask (according to the Mn(CO)5Br to 4,4'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine molar ratio of 1:1.5-2), dissolving 4,4'-dimethyl-2,2'-bipyridine in diethyl ether, and dropwise adding to the round bottom flask; (ii) condensing and refluxing the mixed solution obtained in step (i) for 3-5 hours, finishing reaction, steaming to remove the solvent, obtaining the sample; and (iii) washing the sample obtained in (ii), dissolving in acetonitrile, obtaining single crystal sample of metal complex (II) by normal-hexane diffusion method.

AU VOR D E L
   HOFMANN J
   BRAUN S
   TRAVING M
   HOFFMANN J
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 201971769C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyether carbonate polyol                involves adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide                to an H-functional starter substance in a reactor                comprising a subset of H-functional starter                substance together with a double metal cyanide                catalyst, and adding the H-functional starter                substance and a suspending agent which does not                contain H-functional groups continuously during the                reaction in the reactor.
   USE - Preparation of polyether carbonate polyol.                Uses include but are not limited to                washing/cleaning agent formulation, drilling fluid,                fuel additive, ionic/nonionic surfactant,                lubricant, process chemical for paper/textile                production, cosmetic formulation, and flexible                polyurethane foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of polyether                carbonate polyol with high selectivity and reduced                generation of byproducts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The suspending agent is                  chosen from 4-methyl-2-oxo-1,3-dioxolane,                  1,3-dioxolan-2-one, acetone, methyl ethyl ketone,                  acetonitrile, nitromethane, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  sulfolane, dimethylformamide, dimethylacetamide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone, dioxane, diethyl ether,                  methyl t-butyl ether, tetrahydrofuran, ethyl                  acetate, butyl acetate, pentane, n-hexane,                  benzene, toluene, xylene, ethylbenzene,                  chloroform, chlorobenzene, dichlorobenzene, and                  carbon tetrachloride, preferably                  4-methyl-2-oxo-1,3-dioxolane and/or                  1,3-dioxolan-2-one.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: Preparation of polyether                  carbonate polyol further involves transferring                  the reaction mixture to a reactor, in which the                  content of free alkylene oxide in the reaction                  mixture is reduced after the reaction, removing                  the reaction mixture continuously from the                  reactor, performing addition process in the                  presence of a metal complex catalyst based on                  metallic zinc and/or cobalt or the double metal                  cyanide catalyst, and adding the double metal                  cyanide catalyst suspended in the H-functional                  starter substance continuously into the reactor.                  Preferred Composition: The addition amount of the                  suspending agent is 2-20 wt.% with respect to the                  total content of H-functional starter substance                  and suspending agent. The H-functional starter                  substance is chosen from ethylene glycol,                  propylene glycol, 1,3-propanediol,                  1,3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol,                  2-methylpropane-1,3-diol, neopentyl glycol,                  1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, diethylene                  glycol, dipropylene glycol, glycerol,                  trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol, sorbitol,                  polyether carbonate polyol having a molecular                  weight of 150-8000 g/mol and a functionality of                  2-3, and polyether polyol having a molecular                  weight of 150-8000 g/mol and a functionality of                  2-3.

PN DE102018210105-A1; US2019241437-A1; CN110127691-A; KR2019093960-A; US10815127-B2
TI Production of activated carbon used as gasoline-vapor-trapping agent for vehicle, involves carbonizing carbon material by heat treating under inert gas atmosphere and activating by heat treating under carbon dioxide atmosphere.
AU CHOI S W
   MOON D N
   KIM B J
   LEE H M
   CHOI S
   MOON D
   KIM B
   LEE H
   JOO K B
   HYEMINLEE
AE HYUNDAI MOTOR CO LTD (HYMR-C)
   KIA MOTORS CORP (KIAK-C)
   KOREA INST CARBON CONVERGENCE TECHNOLOGY (KOCA-Non-standard)
   KIA MOTORS CORP (KIAK-C)
   KOREA INST CARBON CONVERGENCE TECHNOLOGY (KOCA-Non-standard)
GA 201968223V
AB    NOVELTY - Production of activated carbon involves carbonizing a carbon material in a reactor by heat treating (T1) under an inert gas atmosphere, activating the carbonized carbon material by heat treating (T2) under an oxidizing gas atmosphere, and activating the activated carbon material by heat treating (T3) under a carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   USE - Production of activated carbon used for vehicle (claimed) preferably used as gasoline-vapor-trapping agent for vehicle. Uses include but are not limited to passenger car, sports utility vehicle, bus, truck, boats and ships, aircraft, plug-in hybrid vehicle, hydrogen-powered vehicle, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces activated carbon having improved pore property and gasoline vapor adsorption/desorption performance characteristics.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Production of activated carbon further involves increasing the temperature under an inert gas atmosphere after carbonization process, and cooling under an inert gas atmosphere and reducing by heat treating (T4) at 200-500 degrees C for 10-50 minutes under an atmosphere of 1-20 vol.% reducing gas including hydrogen and/or ammonia, after heat treatment (T3). Preferred Conditions: Heat treatment (T1) is performed at 600-1000 degrees C for 1-2 hours at a rate of 0.5-10 degrees C/minute. Heat treatment (T2) is performed at 600-1100 degrees C for 10-60 minutes. The oxidizing gas is steam and/or oxygen. Heat treatment (T3) is performed at 900-1200 degrees C for 10-60 minutes. Heat treatment (T3) is performed at a temperature higher by 50-300 degrees C than the temperature of heat treatment (T2). Preferred Composition: The activated carbon has an oxygen content of 3.5 wt.% or less after reduction process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon material is at least one chosen from coal, starch, coconut shells, tangerine shells, organ shell, coffee ground, bamboo stem, wood chip, petroleum-based pitch, coal-based pitch, polyvinylidene-based polymer, phenol-based polymer, urethane-based polymer, and polyacrylonitrile polymer.
