PN CN109894111-A; CN109894111-B
TI Preparing highly dispersed metal molybdenum-based catalyst useful in electrocatalysis, comprises e.g. impregnating treated carbon cloth with metal molybdenum precursor solution, drying, reducing carbon dioxide loaded with molybdenum dioxide.
AU WANG Y
   CHEN J
   WANG Z
   WANG H
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201956365F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly dispersed metal molybdenum-based catalyst comprises impregnating a treated carbon cloth with a metal molybdenum precursor solution and drying, and calcining the dried carbon cloth under an inert atmosphere to obtain a supported dioxide carbon cloth of molybdenum dioxide, and reducing the carbon dioxide loaded with molybdenum dioxide and calcining under a reducing atmosphere.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in electro catalysis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and possible for high dispersion of inexpensive metals which can be obtained by high-temperature reduction, is suitable for high-temperature reaction, and for large-scale production. The catalyst has a highly dispersed characteristic, a very catalytic effect, useful electrocatalysis, e.g. electrocatalytic reduction of nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal molybdenum precursor solution is metal molybdenum precursor water, ethanol and water and ethanol mixture solution having a concentration of 0.1-1 mol/l. The metal molybdenum precursor is molybdate. The carbon cloth is coated tannic acid, polydopamine, or polypyrrole. The carbon cloth is coated with a layer of polymer before using the carbon cloth. The loading amount of the metal molybdenum on the carbon cloth is 0.1-5 wt.%. Preferred Method: The immersion times are 1-5 times, the immersion time is 5-60 second/time. The drying time is 5-20 minutes. The reaction conditions of the carbon cloth calcined and dried under an inert atmosphere are: a heating rate of 1-15 degrees C/minute. The calcination temperature of 200-1000 degrees C. The calcination time 0.5-5 hours. The inert gas flow rate is 200-450 ml/minute. The heating rate is 1-10 degrees C/minute. The inert gas flow rate is 100-400 ml/minute. The calcination temperature is 500-1000 degrees C. The calcination interval is 0.5-3 hours. The reaction conditions of the reduction calcination under a reducing atmosphere are: a heating rate of 1-20 degrees C/minute. The reduction temperature of 200-800 degrees C. The reduction time is 0.5-10 hours. The reducing gas flow rate is 30-80 ml/minute. The reduction temperature is 500-600 degrees C. The reduction time is 5-10 hours. The reducing gas flow rate is 50-70 ml/minute.

PN NL1042656-B1
TI Preparation of an elastic epoxy material useful in e.g. windmills, roofs or walls, aircraft and ships comprises reacting epichlorohydrin with a hardener comprising biobased cardanol and at least one component obtained from carbon dioxide.
AU WOUDSMA J
AE ECOFLEX BV (ECOF-Non-standard)
GA 201951898H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of an elastic epoxy material comprises reacting epichlorohydrin with a hardener, where the hardener comprises biobased cardanol and at least one component obtained from carbon dioxide.
   USE - The process is useful for preparation of elastic epoxy material (claimed) which is useful in windmills, ships, aircraft, roofs or walls, the road surface of streets, floors and laminate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides the elastic epoxy material which exhibits both an external and an internal self-healing capacity, and is able to repair itself in the event of damage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the elastic epoxy material obtained by applying the process and products obtained from the elastic epoxy material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: In the process, a hybrid forms on the basis of a polyamine and/or propylene ether amine and/or propylene carbonates and/or carbohydrates based on non-isocyanate polyurethane molecules. The elastic epoxy material prepared by the process comprises a hybrid based on propylene carbonate and polymercapane. The process comprises joining at least two of the elastic epoxy material without prior complete curing. The process comprises producing composites by adding at least one of the fillers comprising glass fiber, glass wool, carbon fiber or nanosols to the elastic epoxy material. Preferred Components: The components obtained from carbon dioxide comprise propylene carbonate. The propylene carbonate is obtained from a reaction between reactants comprising carbon dioxide and monopropylene glycol. The molecular weight of the prepared elastic epoxy material is at most 700. The elastic epoxy material has the embodiment of an adhesive, primer, coating or foil. The hardness of the elastic epoxy material to be prepared can be changed by changing the ratio of the polymers present in the hybrid. The hybrid comprises at least one of the harder components comprising air, UV, moisture, polyols, polyether amine, aliphatic, polyether amines, modified cycloaliphatic polyamine, polymer captan, polysulfide, polyaminoamide, polyamino imidazoline, cyanoacrylates, methacrylate, aliphatic polyurethane, pentadecatrienyl (cardanol) or biobased cashew nutshell liquid. The elastic epoxy material comprises at least one of: plastic glue comprising PVC, polysulfide, polystyrene, acrylonitrile butadiene styrene or a tribrid epoxy glue; elastic glue comprising polyurethane, silicone, ethylene propylene diene monomer or nitrile butadiene rubber, epoxy or hot melt; non-elastic glue comprising polyester, epoxy, polyurethane or cyano; and semi-elastic glue comprising acrylate, methyl 2-cyanoacrylate or polyamide, including aramid and nylon. The material comprises elastic composites. The material comprises at least one component which in their initial state have a dual pot-life.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109704901-A; CN109704901-B
TI Production of low-carbon olefin from synthesis            gas, involves reacting synthesis gas stream to obtain            product stream, separating, decarburizing, separating            stream into stream containing carbon monoxide and            hydrogen and recycling.
AU HU S
   YANG W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP SINOPEC (SNPC-C)
GA 201942309C
AB    NOVELTY - The production of low-carbon olefin from                synthesis gas, involves reacting synthesis gas                stream through reaction zone to obtain product                stream, separating product stream into main carbon                dioxide-containing stream (I), decarburizing stream                through decarburization zone, separating                decarbonization stream into stream (II) mainly                containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen and mixed                hydrocarbon stream through dehydrogenation zone,                partially recycling above carbon dioxide-containing                stream (I) to the reaction zone, and partially                recycling back above stream (II) containing carbon                monoxide and hydrogen to the reaction zone.
   USE - Production of low-carbon olefin from synthesis                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables low-carbon olefin with slow                catalyst coking, long regeneration period, and high                olefin yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 10 wt.%, 20 wt.%                  and 30 wt.% stream (I) is recycled back to the                  reaction zone. The 60 wt.% and 80 wt.% stream                  (II) is recycled back to the reaction zone. The                  decarburization zone separates carbon dioxide by                  absorption analysis. The bicarbonate ions are                  formed in decarburization zone. The pressure                  swing adsorption separation process and membrane                  separation process (all preferred) and/or                  cryogenic separation process are used in                  dehydrogenation zone to separate carbon monoxide                  or/and hydrogen.

PT P
PN CN109575292-A; CN109575292-B
TI New ion exchange resin useful in catalyzing the addition reaction of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide.
AU YU F
   XIE T
   HE W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 2019334834
AB    NOVELTY - Ion exchange resin (I) are new.
   USE - (I) are useful in catalyzing the addition reaction of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ion exchange resin of formula (I) are new.
   ring p = macroporous nano composite resin matrix;
   M- = anion;
   A = cage silsesquioxane unit having the formula (-SiO(1.5))m (POSS);
   m = 6, 8, 10 or 12; and
   R = alkylene group (methylene, ethylene or propylene) or arylene group (phenylene, naphthylene or phenylene) (linking group between the POSS unit and the imidazole cation unit).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of (I) in addition reaction comprising reacting (I) and alkylene oxide at a pressure of 0.1-10.0 MPa at 60-180 degrees C for 1-8 hours, where molar ratio between (I) and alkylene oxide is 0.001-1:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The content of the POSS unit in the ion exchange resin catalyst is 5-15 wt.%. The gel-type nanocomposite resin matrix is styrene monomer, co-monomer and nano material obtained by in-situ polymerization of nano gel-type copolymer. The styrenic monomer is styrene, alpha -methylstyrene or 4-butylstyrene; the co-monomer is ethylene glycol dimethacrylate, dipropylene benzene, divinyl phenyl methane and/or divinyl benzene and nanomaterial is single layer graphene, multilayer graphene, graphene oxide or graphene. The styrenic monomer is styrene; and the co-monomer is divinylbenzene; and the porogen is polystyrene. The amount of the styrene monomer is 85-95 pts. wt., the amount of the co-monomer is 2-5 pts. wt., the amount of the nano material is 0.1-3 pts. wt., and the amount of the porogen is 10-100 pts. wt.. The alkylene oxide is of formula (II).R1-R4 = H or 1-6C alkyl group.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M- = fluoro, chloro, bromo, iodido, acetate, formate, hydrogen oxalate, bicarbonate, hydroxide, hydrogen sulfite, carboxylate, citrate, sulfite, phosphite, tetrafluoroborate, triflate, phosphotungstic acid, phosphomolybdate, silicotungstic acid, silicon molybdate, arsenate tungstate, arsenate molybdate, strontium tungstate, bismuth molybdate, phosphotungstic vanadate, phosphomolybdate, triflate, p-toluenesulfonate, benzenesulfonate, methanesulfonate, tetrafluoroborate or hexafluorophosphate.

TI Preparation of graphene/calcium-magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite composite material used for carbon dioxide adsorption and capture, involves adding calcium nitrate, magnesium nitrate and aluminum nitrate into distilled water, and stirring.
AU FENG Y
   LI Y
   YANG W
   HUANG H
   LIANG J
AE UNIV GUILIN TECHNOLOGY (UYGI-C)
GA 201904954P
AB    NOVELTY - A graphene/calcium-magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite composite material is prepared by adding 0.63 g calcium nitrate, 0.68 g magnesium nitrate and 1.0 g aluminum nitrate into 70 mL distilled water, stirring, adding 4.32 g urea, stirring, adding 0.06 g graphite oxide, ultrasonicating for 0.5 hours, reacting at 120 degrees C for 12 hours, cooling, decompressing and filtering, washing 5-6 times with distilled water and 2-3 times with absolute ethanol, and drying at 80 degrees C for 6 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing graphene/calcium-magnesium aluminum hydrotalcite composite material used for carbon dioxide adsorption and capture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has increased effective specific surface area and provides more adsorption active sites.

PN JP2018197278-A
TI Flexible polyurethane foam for e.g. pillow, comprises raw material obtained by reacting polyol component containing polyol having preset hydroxyl value, polyisocyanate component, foaming agent containing carbon dioxide, and catalyst.
AU TAKAHASHI I
   SUZUKI H
AE ACHILLES CORP KK (KOKC-C)
GA 201899063M
AB    NOVELTY - A flexible polyurethane foam comprises a polyurethane foam raw material obtained by reacting a polyol component, a polyisocyanate component, a foaming agent, and a catalyst. The flexible polyurethane foam has an impact resilience of less than 15%. The polyol component contains a polyol (A) having a hydroxyl value of 50-60 mg potassium hydroxide/g and a polyol (B) having a hydroxyl value of 225-500 mg potassium hydroxide/g. The foaming agent contains liquefied carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - Flexible polyurethane foam. Uses include but are not limited to pillow, mattress, mattress bedding, sofa, and chair.
   ADVANTAGE - The flexible polyurethane foam has excellent physical properties and discoloration-proof property, and low density and repulsivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst is an amine catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The number average molecular weight of polyol (A) and polyol (B) is 1000-5000 and 500-1000, respectively. The flexible polyurethane has an isocyanate index of 90-113. Preferred Composition: The polyol component contains 30-100 parts mass polyol (B) with respect to 100 parts mass polyol (A). The polyurethane foam raw material contains 1.5-2.5 parts mass liquefied carbon dioxide, 1.3-2 parts mass water, and 1-2 parts mass catalyst, with respect to 100 parts mass polyol component.

AU GE J
   DENG F
   WANG F
   ZHANG Z
   LIANG S
AE GUANGZHOU LIZHONG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018718247
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of defatted flaxseed powder comprises mixing linseed with water, microwave treating and detoxifying to obtain detoxification linseed; degreasing by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; mixing defatted flaxseed meal with water, vacuum concentrating up to solid content of 13-16 mass%, homogenizing, and spray drying using pressure-type spray dryer to obtain defatted flaxseed powder.
   USE - Preparation method of defatted flaxseed powder used for preparing medicine for preventing and treating tumor and for preparing diet products (all claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mass ratio of linseed and water is 1:5-20. The microwave treatment conditions are 500-800 W for 1-3 minutes. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at 50-70 degrees C and 10-15 MPa for 40-60 minutes, and carbon dioxide concentration of 0.7-0.9 g/mL, CO2 flow speed of 4 mL/minute. The mass ratio of defatted flaxseed meal and water is 1: 4-6. The homogenizing is carried out at 40-50 MPa. The spray drying is carried out at 165-175 degrees C spray drying inlet temperature and spray dryer outlet temperature of 70-80 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Anorectic.

TI Preparing cyclic carbonate by combined catalytic reaction of succinimide and halide involves using carbon dioxide and epoxy compound as raw materials to synthesize ring under synergistic action of succinimide and halide.
AU CHANG H
   LI Q
   YANG H
   LI R
   QIAO C
   ZHOU D
AE UNIV HENAN (UYEH-C)
GA 2018590678
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate by combined catalytic reaction of succinimide and halide involves using carbon dioxide and an epoxy compound as raw materials to synthesize a ring under the synergistic action of succinimide and a halide. The carbonate carbonate has a reaction pressure of 0.1-1.0 MPa, a reaction temperature of 25-90 degrees C, and a reaction time of 1-10 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing cyclic carbonate by combined catalytic reaction of succinimide and halide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare cyclic carbonate by combined catalytic reaction of succinimide and halide in simple manner under mild conditions, which has strong adaptability, and has better industrial application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is represented by formula (I). The halide is potassium iodide, sodium iodide, lithium iodide or tetrabutyl iodide, ammonium, tetrabutylammonium bromide, cetyltrimethyliodideamine, and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of the succinimide to the epoxy compound is 0.25-10:100. The molar ratio of the succinimide to the halide is 1:4-4:1. The reaction pressure is 0.2-0.5 MPa. The reaction temperature is 50-80 degrees C.R=CH3, CH2Cl, CH2OCH3, CH2OCH2CH3, CH2CHCH2OCH2, C6H5, C6H5OCH2 or CH(OCH2CH3)2..

PT P
PN CN108265081-A
TI Preparing biomethane comprises e.g. adding biogas            slurry in anaerobic bioreactor, filling nitrogen to            replace air, adding activating material for activating,            completing activation of methane bacteria, and            enriching methane bacteria.
AU JIANG H
   HAO W
   DING J
   LI Y
   XU Q
   ZHOU H
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 2018575247
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomethane comprises (i) adding                biogas slurry in a anaerobic bioreactor, filling                nitrogen to replace the air, ensuring strict                anaerobic environment, replacing air, adding                activating material for activating, not generating                biogas in the anaerobic bioreactor, and completing                activation of methane bacteria, (ii) enriching                hydrogen nutritional type methane bacteria in a                anaerobic bioreactor by hydrogen/carbon dioxide,                and completing enrichment of hydrogen nutrient                methane bacteria when the methane content in biogas                is risen to 60%, and (iii) adjusting the volume                ratio of hydrogen/carbon dioxide in the anaerobic                bioreactor to 2.5-4.5 for stable biological methane                production.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                biomethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not require any other                nutrients or nutrient solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  temperature of the anaerobic bioreactor is 35-40                  degrees C, and the rotating speed is controlled                  to 100-300 revolutions/minute (rpm). In the step                  (ii), the enrichment time is 12-20 days, the                  temperature of the anaerobic bioreactor is 35-40                  degrees C, the rotating speed is 100-600 rpm, and                  the total gas flow rate of hydrogen and carbon                  dioxide is 0.2-1.5 ml/minute per liter of biogas                  slurry. In the step (ii), the hydrogen                  nutritional methane bacteria enriching process                  specifically comprises detecting gas components,                  monoglyceride content volume, and pH value, where                  the monoglyceride comprises acetic acid,                  propionic acid, butyric acid, isobutyric acid,                  valeric acid and isovaleric acid, and using gas                  chromatography to measures gas component, and                  recording volume and pH value each day, measuring                  monoglyceride content twice a week using gas                  chromatography. In the step (iii), the pH value                  is rapidly increased, rapidly increases acetic                  acid content, increasing carbon dioxide injection                  rate in a short time, if hydrogen content in                  biogas increases, there is a gradual upward                  trend, and reducing hydrogen injection rate to                  produce gas within anaerobic bioreactor.                  Preferred Components: The activating substance is                  glucose and/or microcrystalline cellulose, the                  content of activating substance is 0.30-1.50 g/l                  (methane), and the number of days for activating                  is 2-3 days. In the step (i), the anaerobic                  reactor is a continuous stirred tank reactor. In                  the step (iii), the temperature of the anaerobic                  bioreactor is 35-40 degrees C, the rotating speed                  is 100-600 rpm, and total gas flow rate of                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas is 0.2-2                  ml/minute per liter of biogas liquid. The step                  (iii) specifically comprises adjusting volume                  ratio of hydrogen/carbon dioxide in the anaerobic                  bioreactor to 3.5-4.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Improving zinc-containing zeolite framework base material for absorbing carbon dioxide, involves dissolving zinc-containing zeolite base framework in anhydrous hexane under ultrasonic, adding piperazine and subjecting to reflux reaction.
AU LIU S
   LIU L
   ZHOU Y
   HU C
   WANG T
AE UNIV SHANGQIU (UYSH-Non-standard)
GA 201826479N
AB    NOVELTY - Improving zinc-containing zeolite framework base material involves dissolving the zinc-containing zeolite base framework in the anhydrous hexane under ultrasonic in the protection of nitrogen, adding piperazine and subjecting to the reflux reaction under magnetic stirring and filtering the reaction liquid. The obtained product is washed with anhydrous n-hexane, filtered and dried under vacuum at normal temperature to obtain modified zinc-containing zeolite framework base material (ZIF-8).
   USE - Method for improving zinc-containing zeolite framework base material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve zinc-containing zeolite framework base material in simple manner, and increases the adsorption capacity for carbon dioxide by 30-35%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The specific surface area of ZIF-8 is 1000-1200 square meter per gram. The ultrasonic dispersion is carried out. The molar ratio of piperazine and substrate framework material containing zinc zeolite is 5-10:1.

PN CN107630268-A; CN107630268-B
TI Aromatic cyclization of polyacrylonitrile carbon fiber precursor comprises e.g. winding the polyacrylonitrile carbon fiber precursor on the tensioner with tension, adding into reaction kettle and reacting.
AU MUHUO Y
   MENGMENG Q
   HAIJUAN K
   XIAOMA D
   WU Y
   XUDUO Y
   JIANGBIN L
   YU M
   QIAO M
   KONG H
   DING X
   YU X
   LIN J
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
   SHANGHAI FISHERMAN.NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLO (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 201810134E
AB    NOVELTY - Aromatic cyclization of polyacrylonitrile (PAN) carbon fiber precursor comprises (1) winding the PAN carbon fiber precursor on the tensioner with tension, (2) adding into reaction kettle and reacting at 140-220 degrees C, at pressure of 8-18 Mpa, the reaction process includes removing the air, filling carbon dioxide inside the reaction space using supercritical carbon dioxide state and slowly releasing to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for aromatic cyclization of polyacrylonitrile carbon fiber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and environmentally friendly, has low reaction temperature, short reaction time, no need to add other solvent and catalyst and has large industrial application, can prepare the large intensity fiber, provides a new technology for preparing high-performance carbon fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1) the (PAN) precursor is carbon fiber T1000, T800 or T300 corresponding to the PAN precursor. The amount of tension in step (1) is 10-150N. The reaction time in step (2) is 1-40 minutes. In the step (2), slowly releasing is slowly opening the gas outlet valve to make the pressure in the container is reduced to normal pressure with very small speed. The slow release time is 2-4 minutes.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)

PT P
PN CN107603650-A
TI Preparing biomass carbon involves crushing straw and mixing with urea solution, boiling, filtering, drying, using carbon dioxide to discharge air in carbonization furnace at high temperature and mixing with straw in carbonization furnace.
AU WANG Z
AE ANHUI ZONGZHENG AGRIC TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201808582V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass carbon involves crushing straw and mixing with urea solution, boiling, filtering, drying, using carbon dioxide to discharge air in carbonization furnace, carrying out reaction at high temperature and mixing with straw in carbonization furnace. The reaction mixture is air cooled to -10 degrees C for 2 hours. The reaction mixture is recovered to room temperature naturally, ground and mixed with ethanol, filtered, sieved with 70-mesh sieve and dried obtain desired product.
   USE - Method for preparing biomass carbon in soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare biomass carbon which has promoted the crop to fertilize nutrient absorption and growth of crops, and improved the yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The straw is corn straw and fruticose strip which are present in mass ratio of 10:1.5-3.0. The straw is crushed to 1.5 centimeter. Preferred Process: The straw is crushed and mixed with urea solution and boiled at 100 degrees C, fumigated and steamed for 40 minutes to reach 5 wt.% urea solution, where straw and urea solution are present in mass ratio of 250g:800mL. The mixture is boiled for 30 minutes, naturally cooled to room temperature and filtered. Preferred Condition: The drying temperature is 120 degrees C to reach water content to less than 10%. The high temperature process is heating process which is divided into two times, firstly heating is carried out at 300 degrees C for 1 hours, and treated at 600 degrees C for 30 minutes. The mixed solvent is acetone and ethanol. The mixture is ground for 2 hours, where ethanol is absolute ethyl alcohol.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC H09-F03
IP C10B-053/02; C10B-057/00
PD CN107603650-A   19 Jan 2018   C10B-053/02   201814   Pages: 5   Chinese
AD CN107603650-A    CN11088458    08 Nov 2017
PI CN11088458    08 Nov 2017

      CN102515914-A   UNIV BEIJING NORMAL (UYBN)   SHAN Y, CHEN S, LIN C, HAO F, OUYANG W, ZHAO X, LAI X
      CN107151016-A   SUZHOU FENGYUAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)   JIE A

PT P
PN CN113663650-A
TI Use of eutectic ionic liquid/molecular sieve composite material in capturing carbon dioxide comprises capturing by using eutectic ionic liquid DES as gas capture and binding material, molecular sieve as supporting material and gas adsorption material, eutectic ionic liquid and molecular sieve.
AU JIANG H
   XIA G
   LIN S
   SUN S
   WANG Y
   WEN X
   WU L
   ZHANG X
   ZHENG W
   XU G
   FANG D
AE UNIV YANCHENG TEACHERS (UAET-C)
GA 2021D7258J
AB    NOVELTY - Use of eutectic ionic liquid/molecular sieve composite material in capturing carbon dioxide comprises using eutectic ionic liquid DES as gas capture and binding material, molecular sieve as supporting material and gas adsorption material, eutectic ionic liquid and molecular sieve, where YCTC-3 composite material obtained by the combination of materials used for the induction, capturing and storing carbon dioxide in the field of carbon peaking and carbon neutralization, the eutectic ionic liquid DES is a mixture of hydrogen bond acceptors and hydrogen bond donors molecular sieve is 4A (sodium A type), 5A (calcium A type), 10Z (calcium Z type), 13Z (sodium Z type), Y (sodium Y type) with a pore size greater than 0.33 nm and pH less than or equal to 11 in water, and/or sodium mordenite type.
   USE - The low-eutectic ion liquid molecular sieve composite material is useful in two-carbon economy and other fields
   ADVANTAGE - The method requires raw materials are easy to obtain, the operation process is simple, and the equipment requirements are not high; (2) the YCTC3 composite material prepared is significantly improved compared with other publicly reported carbon dioxide gas adsorption materials. It can be used in two-carbon economy and other fields. The composite material is easy to recover, reusable, environmentally friendly and easy to industrially scale up.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of eutectic ionic liquid/molecular sieve composite material in capturing carbon dioxide comprises using eutectic ionic liquid DES as gas capture and binding material, molecular sieve as supporting material and gas adsorption material, eutectic ionic liquid and molecular sieve, where YCTC-3 composite material obtained by the combination of materials is used for the induction, capturing and storing carbon dioxide in the field of carbon peaking and carbon neutralization; the eutectic ionic liquid DES is a mixture of hydrogen bond acceptors and hydrogen bond donors molecular sieve is 4A (sodium A type), 5A (calcium A type), 10Z (calcium Z type), 13Z (sodium Z type), Y (sodium Y type) with a pore size greater than 0.33 nm and pH less than or equal to 11 in water, and/or sodium mordenite type. The specific steps for the construction of the composite YCTC comprises taking molecular sieves, hydrogen bond acceptors or donors required to form DES, and deionized water, introducing to the reaction kettle in turn, stirring for 1-2 hours under normal temperature and pressure, carrying out temperature program, where heating rate is 1degreesC/ minutes, heating to 40-70degreesC, warming and stirring for 8 hours, and cooling naturally under stirring conditions to room temperature, introducing hydrogen bond donor or acceptor, and carrying out temperature program control, the heating rate is 0.5 degreesC/minutes, heating up to 40~70 degreesC, stirring at a constant temperature for 8-15 hours, stirring, and naturally cooling to room temperature, standing overnight, the obtained mixture was simply filtered or suction filtered, and vacuum-drying solid product at 100 degreesC for 2 hours to obtain YCTC-3, a composite material of DES and molecular sieve; where, the loading of DES was 10-20 wt.% of the mass of molecular sieve, (ii) molecular sieves, substances used as hydrogen bond acceptors or hydrogen bond donors, sequentially deionized water to the reaction kettle, and stirring for 1-24 hours under normal temperature and pressure conditions, carrying out temperature program, heating rate is 0.5 degrees Celsius/min, heating to 40-70 degrees Celsius, maintaining and stirring for 8 -15 hours, stopping and cooling natural temperature to room temperature and placing overnight, simply filtering or suction filtering mixture, and vacuum drying solid product at 100 degreesC for 2 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The eutectic ionic liquid/molecular sieve composite material YCTC-3 into the gas absorption tank, and at a certain temperature and under constant stirring conditions, introducing carbon dioxide, and passing pressure in the absorption tank after the absorption reaches equilibrium, changing to calculate the absorption of carbon dioxide; where the partial pressure Pe of the balanced carbon dioxide is 0.001-3 bar, wherein the preferred Pe is 0.1-3 bar, the absorption temperature is 20~100 degrees Celsius, where the preferred absorption temperature is 20-40 degreesC. The application method for absorbing carbon dioxide comprises regeneration process of the absorbent after the absorption, placing composite material YCTC-3 that has absorbed carbon dioxide in a sealed tank with a pressure of 0.005 bar, and the residual amount of desorption is 0.12-0.15 mmol/g; where the desorption temperature is 60-120degreesC, and the preferred desorption temperature is 70-80degreesC; the regeneration time is 0.1-3 hours, and the preferred regeneration time is 1 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN113749994-A
TI Essential oil composition as essential oil nano-emulsion composition for preparing anti-allergy acne-removing cosmetics, anti-sensitive acne-removing moisturizer water, and anti-allergy acne-removing cream, comprises Humulus scandens essential oil and citron peel essential oil.
AU JIN H
   FENG X
   JIANG Y
   AN Z
   GONG Y
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 202202432B
AB    NOVELTY - Essential oil composition comprises Humulus scandens essential oil, citron peel essential oil and Litsea essential oil in 3:6:4 ratio. The Humulus scandens essential oil preparation method involves taking Humulus scandens whole plant, drying, crushing, sieving with 80-mesh sieve, putting into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting under 20 MPa pressure, 50?oC temperature and 2.0L/minute carbon dioxide flow for 1.5 hours, collecting the volatile oil in the separating kettle, drying for 24 hours by anhydrous sodium sulfate, centrifuging for 15 minutes at 3000 rotation/minute speed, absorbing the upper oily transparent liquid to obtain the essential oil product.
   USE - Essential oil composition as essential oil nano-emulsion composition for preparing anti-allergy acne-removing cosmetics, anti-sensitive acne-removing moisturizer water, and anti-allergy acne-removing cream (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The essential oil compositions are all extracted from natural plants, can play a synergistic effect, have activities such as inhibiting hyaluronidase, inhibiting Staphylococcus aureus and Propionibacterium acnes, and inhibiting melanosis. The prepared nanoemulsion can reduce the volatilization of essential oils and maintain the stability of active ingredients of essential oils. When added to cosmetics, long-term use can achieve the effect of anti-allergic and acne-removing. It is safe and effective, has a mild effect, and has a lasting effect without rebound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Essential oil composition comprises Humulus scandens essential oil, citron peel essential oil and Litsea essential oil in 3:6:4 ratio. The Humulus scandens essential oil preparation method involves taking Humulus scandens whole plant, drying, crushing, sieving with 80-mesh sieve, putting into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting under 20 MPa pressure, 50?oC temperature and 2.0L/minute carbon dioxide flow for 1.5 hours, collecting the volatile oil in the separating kettle, drying for 24 hours by anhydrous sodium sulfate, centrifuging for 15 minutes at 3000 rotation/minute speed, absorbing the upper oily transparent liquid to obtain the essential oil product. The preparation method of citron peel essential oil involves mixing the cellulase and pectinase in ratio of 2:1 to prepare the complex enzyme, taking citron peel, drying, crushing, sieving with 80-mesh sieve, placing in round bottom flask, adding 18 times amount of distilled water, adjusting pH to 4.8 with hydrochloric acid, adding 0.5 wt.% complex enzyme in the flask, putting in water bath at 50?oC constant temperature enzymolysis for 60 minute, raising the temperature to 80?oC, then transferring the round bottom flask into microwave extractor, setting the microwave power to 750 W, microwave radiation for 60 minutes, connecting the microwave extractor with volatile oil separator, and collecting the distilled essential oil to obtain the citron peel essential oil. The preparation method of Litsea oil essential oil involves mixing the cellulase with pectinase in 1:1 ratio to prepare complex enzyme, taking the fruit, crushing, sieving with 40-mesh sieve, placing in the flask, adding 12 times amount of distilled water, using hydrochloric acid to adjust the pH value to 5.0, adding 1 wt.% complex enzyme in the flask, putting in water bath at 45?oC constant temperature for enzymolysis for 30 minutes, raising the temperature to 80?oC, adding glass beads to the round bottom flask, stirring and mixing, connecting reflux condenser pipe and volatile oil separator, keeping boiling for 6 hours, and collecting the distilled essential oil to obtain the oil. The composition is obtained by selecting Humulus scandens essential oil, citron peel essential oil and Litsea essential oil, and mixing uniformly to obtain the essential oil composition.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: a method for preparing the essential oil nano-emulsion composition, which involves using 1 vol.% glacial acetic acid as solvent to prepare 3.2 mg/mL chitosan solution, ultrasonic water bath for 1 hour, filtering through 1 micron microporous filter film, using sodium hydroxide to adjust the pH value of filtrate to 4.0, adding Tween-80 as surfactant to the chitosan filtrate, stirring for 30 minute at 60?oC to obtain uniform mixture, taking the essential oil composition dissolved in absolute ethyl alcohol, adding the essential oil composition to the cooled chitosan solution in 1:1 ratio, stirring at normal temperature for 30 minutes, stirring for 20 minutes under 1200 rotations/minutes condition, adding the mixture into sodium tripolyphosphate solution, continuously stirring for 50 minutes at 1000 rotations/minutes to obtain the nano-emulsion; and#a method for applying the essential oil nano-emulsion composition, which involves the adding 10-15 pts. wt. nano-emulsion to anti-anaphylactic anti-acne cosmetic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The essential oil nano-emulsion composition adopts ionic gel combined with ultrasonic method, by adding essential oil composition and chitosan in 1:1 ratio.
   ACTIVITY - Antiseborrheic; Dermatological; Antiallergic; Antibacterial.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Hyaluronidase inhibitor.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)
MC A10-E07B; A10-E08A; A10-E08B; A08-S02; A10-E09; A12-V01; A12-V04C; B04-A08G2; B04-A10; B04-A98; B04-B01C1; B14-A01; B14-G02A; B14-N17D; B05-C05; B05-A01B; B04-C02E3; B10-C04E6; B04-C03C; B05-B02A3; B14-S18; B04-L01; D08-B09A1A; D08-B03B; D05-A02

PT P
PN WO2022159408-A1; US2022234975-A1
TI Making mixed alcohols comprise e.g. purifying            biogas feedstock, introducing renewable natural gas            stream of methane into methane-to-syngas unit operated            at efficient conditions to convert methane to first            syngas stream having e.g. hydrogen.
AU RIDLEY R
   PARDUN M
   PARDUN M E
   RIDLEY R D
AE STANDARD ALCOHOL CO AMERICA INC (STAL-Non-standard)
   STANDARD ALCOHOL CO AMERICA INC (STAL-Non-standard)
GA 202295638W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing mixed alcohols comprise: (a)                obtaining a biogas feedstock; (b) purifying the                biogas feedstock to generate a renewable natural                gas stream comprising methane; (c) introducing the                renewable natural gas stream into a                methane-to-syngas unit operated at efficient                conditions to convert the methane to a first syngas                stream containing at least hydrogen, carbon                monoxide, or carbon dioxide; (d) purifying the                first syngas stream including separating the carbon                dioxide from the first syngas stream, and                optionally separating some of the hydrogen from the                first syngas stream, thus generating a clean syngas                stream; (e) introducing the clean syngas stream                into a mixed-alcohol reactor operated at effective                alcohol synthesis conditions and in the presence of                an alcohol-synthesis catalyst, thus generating                mixed alcohols and a reactor off-gas; and (f)                optionally purifying the mixed alcohols to generate                a mixed-alcohol product.
   USE - The process is useful for: producing mixed                alcohols (claimed); and producing fuels with low                carbon intensity.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides improved hydrocarbon                fuel compositions; and is environmentally                friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing mixed alcohols comprising the steps (a)                and (b) as per se, (c1) introducing the renewable                natural gas stream to a pipeline, where the                renewable natural gas stream is co-mingled with                other natural gas contained within the pipeline,                (d1) withdrawing a stream of natural gas from the                pipeline, (e) introducing the stream of natural gas                into a methane-to-syngas unit operated at effective                conditions to convert the methane to a first syngas                stream containing at least hydrogen, carbon                monoxide, and carbon dioxide, (f) purifying the                first syngas stream including separating the carbon                dioxide from the first syngas stream, and                optionally separating some of the hydrogen from the                first syngas stream thus generating a clean syngas                stream, (g1) introducing the clean syngas stream                into a mixed-alcohol reactor operated at effective                alcohol synthesis conditions and in the presence of                an alcohol-synthesis catalyst thus generating mixed                alcohols and a reactor off-gas, and (h1) optionally                purifying the mixed alcohols to generate a                mixed-alcohol product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (e) and (g1),                  the alcohol-synthesis catalyst is a metal sulfide                  catalyst. The step (f) and (h1) are conducted and                  includes at least one of sulfur removal,                  dehydration, and distillation. The reactor                  off-gas is recycled to the step (d) and (f1),                  where the reactor off-gas is optionally treated                  in a hydrogen sulfide removal unit prior to                  recycling to the step (d) and (f1).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (b) utilizes:                  water scrubbing, and/or chemical scrubbing to                  remove at least some carbon dioxide from the                  biogas feedstock; and a membrane to remove at                  least some carbon dioxide from the biogas                  feedstock. The step (b) utilizes drying to remove                  at least some water from the biogas feedstock. In                  the step (c), a pipeline natural gas stream is                  co-fed, along with the renewable natural gas                  stream to the methane-to-syngas unit. In the step                  (d), at least some of the hydrogen is separated                  and recovered from the first syngas stream. At                  least some of the hydrogen is: used as fuel                  within the process; recovered as a motor fuel;                  and recovered and utilized in an oil and gas                  refinery. The carbon dioxide separated in the                  step (d) and (f1) is: recovered and sequestered                  in a geological formation; and recovered and                  utilized in enhanced oil recovery. In the step                  (d) and (f1), nitrogen is separated from the                  first syngas stream. In the step (d) and (f1), a                  methane-rich stream is separated from the first                  syngas stream. The methane-rich stream is                  recycled to the methane-to-syngas unit; and                  combusted to provide heat to the                  methane-to-syngas unit. The step (f) is conducted                  to produce a mixed-alcohol product. The process                  further comprises blending the mixed-alcohol                  product with a hydrocarbon fuel comprising                  gasoline, diesel fuel, or jet fuel to produce a                  blended fuel. In the process (p2) the step (c1)                  generates a renewable natural gas credit that                  applies to the mixed alcohols produced in the                  step (g1). The step (h1) is conducted, where the                  step (c) generates a renewable natural gas credit                  that applies to the mixed-alcohol product                  produced in the step (h1). In the step (f), at                  least some of the hydrogen is separated and                  recovered from the first syngas stream. In the                  step (g1) is conducted to produce a mixed-alcohol                  product. Preferred Components: The biogas                  feedstock is obtained from a source comprising a                  landfill, an industrial compost facility, and/or                  an anaerobic digestor. The anaerobic digestor is                  configured for anaerobic digestion of animal                  waste. The biogas feedstock contains hydrogen                  sulfide, where the step (b) utilizes absorption                  of the hydrogen sulfide into an absorption media                  to remove at least some of the hydrogen sulfide                  from the biogas feedstock. The methane-to-syngas                  unit comprises a steam reforming reactor, an                  autothermal reforming reactor, and/or a                  partial-oxidation reactor. The step (d) utilizes                  an amine-based system, a cryogenic system, a                  membrane-separation system, and/or a                  pressure-swing adsorption unit. The blended fuel                  has at least 10%, preferably at least 30% lower                  carbon intensity than the hydrocarbon fuel.
   EXAMPLE - Mixed alcohols were synthesized by passing the                cleaned syngas over a potassium-promoted carbonyl                sulfide-molybdenum disulfide catalyst at 300                degrees C and 100 bar. In the mixed-alcohol                reactor, hydrogen and carbon monoxide were heated                and passed over an alcohol synthesis catalyst to                generate methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol,                pentanol, and small amounts of higher alcohols. The                syngas conversion to mixed alcohols in the                mixed-alcohol reactor was 30%. The consumed syngas                was 35.2 million standard cubic feet per day                (MMSCFD) (17.5 MMSCFD CO and 17.8 MMSCFD hydrogen).                Co-products produced in the mixed-alcohol reactor                included small quantities of water, carbon dioxide,                and methane. Following mixed-alcohol synthesis, a                mixed-alcohol stream was generated, and a reactor                off-gas.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PT P
PN CN114609338-A
TI Detection of activity of red mud e.g. strong            alkaline waste produced in alumina production, involves            mixing red mud powder with water, adding dispersing            agent, stirring, measuring initial pH value,            dealkalizing red mud slurry, measuring pH value, and            determining red mud activity based on pH values.
AU DONG H
   HUANG D
   XUE Y
   DENG T
   ZHOU D
   SUN H
AE ZIBO HIGH-TECH IND DEV ZONE ADVANCED CER (ZIBO-Non-standard)
   LUOYANG JUN JIANG JIANCAI TECHNOLOGY CO (LUOY-Non-standard)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 202282663T
AB    NOVELTY - Detection of activity of red mud involves                drying red mud in a muffle furnace, ball-milling                dried red mud, sieving to obtain red mud powder,                mixing red mud powder with water, adding obtained                mixture to a container, adding a dispersing agent,                stirring at constant temperature to obtain red mud                slurry, measuring initial pH value (C1) of the red                mud slurry using a pH meter, controlling carbon                dioxide gas flow through a gas flow meter,                introducing carbon dioxide gas into the red mud                slurry, dealkalizing, measuring pH value (C2) of                the dealkalized red mud slurry using the pH meter,                and determining red mud activity based on the pH                value (C1), pH value (C2) and red mud                dealkalization time. The change rate of pH value of                red mud slurry before and after dealkalization                reflects the change of free alkali content in red                mud slurry.
   USE - Detection of activity of red mud e.g. strong                alkaline waste produced in alumina production                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of effectively, fast and                accurately detecting red mud activity, and is                simple in operation, low in cost and suitable for                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detection of activity of red mud involves                drying red mud in a muffle furnace, ball-milling                dried red mud, sieving to obtain red mud powder,                mixing red mud powder with water, adding obtained                mixture to a container, adding a dispersing agent,                stirring at constant temperature to obtain red mud                slurry, measuring initial pH value (C1) of the red                mud slurry using a pH meter, controlling carbon                dioxide gas flow through a gas flow meter,                introducing carbon dioxide gas into the red mud                slurry, dealkalizing, measuring pH value (C2) of                the dealkalized red mud slurry using the pH meter,                and determining red mud activity by the formula:                (C1-C2)/t, where t is red mud dealkalization time.                The change rate of pH value of red mud slurry                before and after dealkalization reflects the change                of free alkali content in red mud slurry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The red mud powder is                  sieved using a screen with size of 100 mesh, 250                  mesh, 500 mesh or 1000 mesh.Preferred Composition: The liquid-solid mass                  ratio of water and red mud powder is 3-10:1. The                  concentration of carbon dioxide gas is more than                  90%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The dispersing agent                  is sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate and/or fatty alcohol                  polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfate. The                  addition amount of dispersing agent is 0-1 wt.%                  with respect to the total mass of red mud                  slurry.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC A10-E21; A10-E24; A12-L04B; S03-F10; S03-E13D

TI In-mold laser cutting device useful for hand-paste            glass fiber reinforced plastic product, comprises hand            paste glass fiber reinforced plastic mold, lifting            working table, two-dimensional code scanning system,            pneumatic ejecting device and carbon dioxide laser            cutting machine.
AU SONG H

GA 2022708950
AB    NOVELTY - In-mold laser cutting device comprises a hand                paste glass fiber reinforced plastic mold, a                lifting working table, a two-dimensional code                scanning system, a pneumatic ejecting device and a                carbon dioxide laser cutting machine, the glass                fiber reinforced plastic mold is composed of glass                steel mold main body and a frame fixed on the upper                surface, the cutting position of the mold is                pre-embedded with copper plate, pre-embedded copper                plate requirement and mold surface for modified                mold, the cutting position of the mold is thin                copper foil adhesive tape, the copper foil and                copper foil adhesive tape is used with high                reflectivity, reducing damage of carbon dioxide                laser to the mold, the upper surface of the glass                fiber reinforced plastic mold is added with a                pneumatic ejection device, lifting working table                comprises a lifting mechanism, a motor, a guide                rail, a locating pin, a guide wheel, the motor                provides power to the screw rod to drive the                working table to move left and right.

   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes the effect of the copper                and copper foil adhesive tape using the high                reflectivity, reduces the damage of the carbon                dioxide laser to the mold, the upper surface of the                glass fiber reinforced plastic mold is added with                pneumatic ejection device, ensures the cut product                can be easily and quickly remolded, solves the                problem that is difficult to solve the cost and                locating precision of the machine automated cutting                the glass fiber reinforced plastic product with                multiple kinds and small batches, greatly reduces                the cost, solves the problem that the hand paste                product is difficult locate on the tool due to                deformation after demolding, and realizes the                multi-variety small batch product automated be cut                into possible.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of the glass steel mold of the in-mold                laser cutting device.

PN CN114291835-A
TI Preparation of large and small cubic dispersed precipitated calcium carbonate by preparing lime slurry, processing into rod-shaped precipitated calcium carbonate suspension and cubic precipitated calcium carbonate suspension, and mixing.
AU YANG A
   ZHAN Q
   HUANG Y
   WEN Q
   ZHU Y
   LI R
AE GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202255146B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of large and small cubic dispersed precipitated calcium carbonate includes subjecting quicklime to digestion reaction, sieving, refining and adjusting into lime slurry, aging, adding crystal form control agent, passing through carbon dioxide mixed gas, stop ventilating when pH of reaction system is 8, and stopping the carbonation reaction to obtain rod-shaped precipitated calcium carbonate suspension; adding lime slurry with crystal form control agent, passing through carbon dioxide mixed gas, stop ventilating when pH of reaction system is 7.5, and stopping the carbonation reaction to obtain cubic precipitated calcium carbonate suspension; and mixing rod-shaped precipitated calcium carbonate suspension and cubic precipitated calcium carbonate suspension, adding behenic acid, mixing, heating, subjecting to surface treatment through stearic acid saponification solution, pressure filtering, oven drying, and sieving.
   USE - The method is for preparation of large and small cubic dispersed precipitated calcium carbonate
   ADVANTAGE - The precipitated calcium carbonate prepared by the method has monodisperse feature, large particle diameter surface smooth, low oil absorption value, and good compatibility. The sealing rubber product filler has good compatibility, ensuring the small vertical diameter nano-calcium carbonate reinforcing performance, and greatly improving the flow performance of the sealing glue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of large and small cubic dispersed precipitated calcium carbonate comprises subjecting quicklime to digestion reaction, sieving, refining and adjusting into lime slurry with specific gravity of 1.040-1.050, aging for 12-24 hours, adding crystal form control agent comprising sodium hexametaphosphate or sodium pyrophosphate and soluble barium salt, passing through carbon dioxide mixed gas, stop ventilating when pH of reaction system is 8, and stopping the carbonation reaction to obtain rod-shaped precipitated calcium carbonate suspension; adding lime slurry with crystal form control agent comprising complexes in sodium lauryl sulfate, lauryl trimethyl betaine and/or sodium polystyrene sulfonate, passing through carbon dioxide mixed gas, stop ventilating when pH of reaction system is 7.5, and stopping the carbonation reaction to obtain cubic precipitated calcium carbonate suspension; and mixing rod-shaped precipitated calcium carbonate suspension and cubic precipitated calcium carbonate suspension, adding behenic acid, mixing, heating to 90-95degrees Celsius for 1 hour, subjecting to surface treatment through stearic acid saponification solution, pressure filtering, oven drying, and sieving.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PN CN113999543-A; CN113999543-B
TI Preparing nanometer calcium carbonate with            calcium-silicon core-shell structure involves            carbonating nano-calcium slurry to pH, then the            silicate solution for primary mixing, then high speed            dispersing and homogenizing.
AU LU C
   ZHAN Q
   ZHU Y
   RONG Q
   NONG H
AE GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202225944U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nanometer calcium carbonate with                calcium-silicon core-shell structure involves                carbonating nano-calcium slurry to pH is 6.5-7.5,                then the silicate solution according to dry basis                mass ratio of 50:1-10:1 for primary mixing, then                high speed dispersing and homogenizing to obtain                mixed slurry, mixing the mixed slurry with kiln gas                containing carbon dioxide concentration of 30-35%                according to the volume ratio of 20:1-5:1, making                the silicate in the mixed solution react with                carbon dioxide to obtain calcium-silicon core-shell                structure nano calcium carbonate crude product                solution, mixing the crude solution with the                concentration of 3-7% modifying agent solution                according to the dry basis mass ratio of 40:1-10:1                at 80-95degrees Celsius.
   USE - Method for preparing nanometer calcium                carbonate with calcium-silicon core-shell                structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the acid resistance of the                nano calcium carbonate, dispersibility of the                product in the polymer, breaks the use limitation                and expands the use range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nanometer calcium carbonate with                calcium-silicon core-shell structure involves                carbonating nano-calcium slurry to pH is 6.5-7.5,                then the silicate solution according to dry basis                mass ratio of 50:1-10:1 for primary mixing, then                high speed dispersing and homogenizing to obtain                mixed slurry, mixing the mixed slurry with kiln gas                containing carbon dioxide concentration of 30-35%                according to the volume ratio of 20:1-5:1, making                the silicate in the mixed solution react with                carbon dioxide to obtain calcium-silicon core-shell                structure nano calcium carbonate crude product                solution, mixing the crude solution with the                concentration of 3-7% modifying agent solution                according to the dry basis mass ratio of 40:1-10:1                at 80-95degrees Celsius, stirring for surface                modification reaction at a rotating speed of 5-10                m/s after finishing the surface modification                reaction, homogenizing and stirring, and then                cooling and stirring at 40-55degrees Celsius,                finally dehydrating, drying, packaging to obtain                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicate is water                  glass, sodium metasilicate, potassium silicate.                  The modifier is lauric acid and sodium salt,                  myristic acid and sodium salt, palmitic acid and                  sodium salt, stearic acid and sodium salt.                  Preferred Conditions: The calcium-silicon                  core-shell structure nano-calcium carbonate is                  prepared by carrying out primary mixing in the                  liquid-liquid venturi tube. The high speed                  dispersing and homogenizing is carried out in the                  emulsifying pump, the reaction of the mixed                  slurry and the carbon dioxide is carried out in                  the gas-liquid venturi tube, the surface                  modification reaction is carried out in the                  modified reaction kettle the homogenizing                  stirring is carried out in the homogenizing                  stirring tank, the cooling and stirring is                  carried out in the cooling stirring tank, the                  liquid-liquid venturi tube, emulsifying pump,                  gas-liquid venturi tube, modified reaction                  kettle, homogenizing stirring tank, cooling                  stirring tank are connected in turn, the modified                  reaction kettle, the homogenizing stirring tank                  and the cooling stirring tank form a self-flowing                  conveying, the heat conducting system is set on                  the modified reaction kettle capable of heating                  the modified reaction kettle, the cooling system                  is set on the cooling stirring tank and capable                  of cooling the cooling stirring tank. The                  liquid-liquid venturi tube is horizontally                  arranged, the front end respectively provided                  with a calcium carbonate slurry inlet and a                  silicate inlet, and the rear end is connected                  with the emulsifying pump, the gas-liquid venturi                  tube is vertically arranged, the upper end is                  respectively with a mixed liquid inlet, a carbon                  dioxide inlet, the lower end is connected with                  the top of the modified reaction kettle, the                  mixing liquid inlet is connected with the                  emulsification pump through pipeline.

PT P
PN EP3895695-A1; EP3895695-A4
TI Incorporating bioactive compounds in lipid phase used in e.g. therapy comprises loading high-pressure vessel with lipid phase, pumping carbon dioxide to reach supercritical conditions followed by bioactive solute dissolution and collecting.
AU FORNARI REALI T
   MARTIN GARCIA D
   QUINTANA MARTINEZ S E
   REGLERO RADA G
   RODRIGUEZ GARCIA-RISCO M
   VILLANUEVA-BERMEJO D
   FORNARI R T
   MARTIN G D
   QUINTANA M S E
   REGLERO R G
   RODRIGUEZ G M
AE UNIV AUTONOMA MADRID (UMAD-C)
   UNIV MADRID AUTONOMA (UMAD-C)
GA 2021C0383J
AB    NOVELTY - Incorporating bioactive compounds in a lipid phase using supercritical carbon dioxide (SCCO2) technology comprises: (a) loading a high-pressure vessel with a lipid phase, optionally including a surfactant; (b) pumping carbon dioxide into the pressure vessel until supercritical conditions are reached; (c) a dissolution comprising a solvent and a bioactive solute or a mixture of bioactive solutes, is pumped into the high-pressure vessel through a nozzle together with the SCCO2 flow set; and (d) collecting the lipid dispersion or emulsion formed in the lipid phase.
   USE - The method is useful for incorporating bioactive compounds in a lipid phase using supercritical carbon dioxide technology, which is useful in solid-liquid dispersion, water-lipid emulsion, lipid substance with solid bioactive particles, and oil-water emulsion, which are used in therapy, nutraceutical field and in the cosmetic field (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides incorporated bioactive compounds, which exhibits low toxicity and are not mutagenic; and utilizes supercritical fluids, that enables the control of the size and morphology of the bioactive particles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a solid-liquid dispersion or a water-lipid emulsion in a lipophilic liquid obtainable by the method;
   (2) a lipid substance with solid bioactive particles homogeneously dispersed in a lipid phase with the solid bioactive particles homogeneously dispersed in the lipid phase by a normal particle size distribution with mean size of 0.4-1.3 mm, and a mass concentration of bioactive of 0.2-3% (2-30 mg/g);
   (3) an oil-water emulsion in a weight ratio of 10:90-30:70, preferably 20:80, in which the water phase comprises the bioactive solutes have a drop size of 45-110 mm, and a mass concentration of bioactive of 0.2-3.0% (2-30 mg/g) and the lipid phase comprises olive oil, flaxseed oil, sunflower oil, fish oils, argan oil and/or triglycerides; and
   (4) a composition comprising the solid-liquid dispersion or the water-lipid emulsion, the lipid substance with solid bioactive particles, or the oil-water emulsion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid of step (c) is a dissolution comprising a solvent, where the solvent is an organic solvent which can be dissolved in SCCO2 in a concentration of at least 2 wt./wt.% and a bioactive solute or its mixture. The liquid of step (c) is a dissolution comprising water or water-solvent mixtures, and a bioactive solute or its mixture. The liquid of step comprises olive oil, flaxseed oil, sunflower oil, fish oils, essential oils, argan oil and/or triglycerides. The solvent comprises 1-4C alcohols, preferably methanol and ethanol, isopropanol, methyl acetate, ethyl acetate, ethyl lactate, dimethyl sulfoxide, acetone and/or water or water-ethanol mixtures. The bioactive solutes comprise quercetin, rosemary bioactive compounds (rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid and carnosol), licorice bioactive compounds (liquiritin, liquiritigenin, glycyrrhizic acid and isoliquiritigenin, glabridin) and/or saponins. If the solvent of the solution is soluble in supercritical carbon dioxide: the liquid of step (c) comprises the bioactive molecule or a mixture of bioactive substances and the solvent in a weight ratio of 1:15-1:300, preferably 1:50-1:110; and the liquid phase and the supercritical carbon dioxide are mixed in a weight ratio of 1:4-1:120, preferably 1:20-1:40. The collected liquid phase: has solid bioactive particles encapsulated of size of 0.1-5 mm, preferably 0.4-1.3 mm; and has droplets encapsulated with a volume weighted average size of 5-200 mm, preferably 45-110 mm.
   EXAMPLE - The preparation present in the example not the scope of our invention, so the examples do not support the claims.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); ; D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)

TI Engraving images, and patterns with carbon dioxide laser on fruit epidermis or plant using three dimensional laser marking equipment, involves preparing illustration file, and arranging stems and branches in pot planted with good plant.
AU BAEK J H
AE BAEK J H (BAEK-Individual)
GA 202199011Y
AB    NOVELTY - Engraving images, drawings, and patterns with carbon dioxide (CO2) laser on fruit epidermis or plant, involves preparing an illustration file, and arranging stems and branches on pot in which a good plant is planted. The fruit or potted plant is moved under CO2 laser lens by a moving unit, operating the CO2 laser, engraving an image, picture, or pattern or completing the side portion of the fruit, covering the fruit or plant stem with a cover after the CO2 laser has completed the operation, after the predetermined time has elapsed, in the state in which the skin of a fruit or tree bark is supplied with nutrients from the roots. A clear mooni is formed as a wound, and a color is painted on a sticker. A computer is provided to irradiate laser light to a portion, and images, drawings, and sentences are engraved on two ends of the fruit. A frame is placed with a human hand and an engraving knife is utilized to finish or engrave the side portions.
   USE - Method for engraving images, drawings, and patterns with carbon dioxide laser on fruit epidermis or plant using three dimensional laser marking equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables engraving the image, drawing, or pattern on the epidermis of the fruit by using the CO2 laser while the plant is growing so as to reduce large amount of energy in short time, so that the skin of the fruits can be prevented from being damaged. The method enables performing simple patterning process in 10-20 seconds with high speed and high working efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a three dimensional (3D) laser marking equipment for engraving images, drawings, and patterns with carbon dioxide (CO2) laser on fruit epidermis or plant, comprises short rail with the sharp portion on the furrow or ridge of the field is driven into the ground and fixed, and after one short rail is driven into the ground and fixed, and two short rails are connected with a joint, fixed with a pin, and a wheel is placed on the rail.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The laser marking is difficult on the side of fruits and stems, one point is connected to multiple and becomes one line, when creating an illustration file that displays the pattern, by placing more points or lines on the images, drawings, and patterns corresponding to both side portions than the central portion, by instructing the software of the computer to irradiate more laser light to this portion. The body, head, and lens irradiated with light are separated so that the lens move freely, when drawing an image, drawing, or engraving a pattern using a CO2 laser, if the line is blurred because the lens is out of focus on the side of the fruit, place the frame with a human hand and use a engraving knife to finish or engraving How to complete both side portions only with a knife.

PN CN113198423-A; CN113198423-B
TI Covalent organic framework material used in adsorption-separation of acetylene/carbon dioxide and acetylene/methane, is obtained by ultrasonically dispersing aldehyde precursor and amine precursor in solvent, adding acid catalyst, cooling, freezing and degassing, reacting, and post-processing.
AU PENG Y
   CHEN L
   GONG C
   HUANG M
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202191958K
AB    NOVELTY - A covalent organic framework material is obtained by ultrasonically mixing and dispersing an aldehyde precursor (I) and an amine precursor in an organic solvent, adding an acid catalyst, cooling using liquid nitrogen, freezing and degassing, sealing and reacting for 3-9 days at 80-150 degrees C, and post-processing. The acid catalyst is formic acid, acetic acid, scandium trifluoromethylsulfonate, or ferric trichloride. The organic solvent is trimethylbenzene, o-dichlorobenzene, n-butanol, dioxane, N,N-dimethylacetamide, ethanol, and/or benzyl alcohol. The amine precursor is one or more chosen from compounds (II)-(V).
   USE - Covalent organic framework material is used in adsorption-separation of acetylene/carbon dioxide and acetylene/methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The covalent organic framework material has a specific surface area of 3475 m2/g, an acetylene adsorption capacity of 77.8 cm3/g, a carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of 39.4 cm3/g, and a methane adsorption capacity of 9.6 cm3/g at 298 K, and a separation selection coefficient of 306.2 with respect to acetylene/methane and 4.8 with respect to acetylene/carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A covalent organic framework material is obtained by ultrasonically mixing and dispersing an aldehyde precursor and an amine precursor in an organic solvent, adding an acid catalyst, cooling using liquid nitrogen, freezing and degassing, sealing and reacting for 3-9 days at 80-150 degrees C, and post-processing. The acid catalyst is formic acid, acetic acid, scandium trifluoromethylsulfonate, or ferric trichloride. The organic solvent is trimethylbenzene, o-dichlorobenzene, n-butanol, dioxane, N,N-dimethylacetamide, ethanol, and/or benzyl alcohol. The aldehyde precursor is compound(s) of formula (I). The amine precursor is one or more chosen from compounds of formulae (II)-(V).
   R1,R2 = H, halo, hydroxy, 1-4C alkoxy, or aryl optionally substituted by halo, hydroxy, formyl, 1-4C alkyl, and/or 1-4C alkoxy;and
   R3 = H, 1-4C alkyl, or aryl optionally substituted by halo, hydroxy, formyl, and/or 1-4C alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of aldehyde precursor, amine precursor, and acid catalyst is 1:1:30-60. The acid catalyst is acetic acid fed a 6 mol/L acetic acid aqueous solution. The organic solvent is a mixed solvent of o-dichlorobenzene and n-butanol in a volume ratio of 1:1. Preferred Method: Post-processing involves filtering the reaction liquid, washing the crude product with acetone, centrifuging and filtering 3-5 times, and vacuum-drying at 30-80 degrees C.
