   ADVANTAGE - The supported gold atom cluster has reduced gold content, and can be mass-produced efficiently and economically with high productivity, without affecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of supported gold cluster, which involves preparing 1-5 %mass aqueous solution of chloroauric acid, preparing 1-5 %mass toluene solution of tetraoctylammonium bromide, preparing 0.5-1 %mass aqueous solution of sodium borohydride, mixing aqueous solution of chloroauric acid and toluene solution of tetraoctylammonium bromide in the volume ratio of 1:2, vigorously stirring for 10-30 minutes, removing layered aqueous layer, adding organic phosphine ligand to the toluene phase under vigorous stirring, quickly adding sodium borohydride-ethanol solution to the obtained toluene solution, stirring continuously for 3.5 hours, evaporating the toluene to dryness, extracting with an organic solvent, adding 4 vol.% sulfur-containing organic ligand and sodium borohydride-ethanol solution to the obtained extract, heating to 40 degrees C, reacting for 6 hours, adding triphenylphosphine whose content is 10-15 times the amount of chloroauric acid, continuously reacting at 40 degrees C for 24 hours, evaporating the solvent, washing using hexane, extracting using toluene, obtaining gold cluster of formula: Aun(PPh)10(SC2H4Ph)5X2 (I), adding obtained gold cluster to preset amount of oxide (MO) carrier, ultrasonically processing for 15 minutes, after stirring at room temperature for 12 hours, vacuum drying at 60 degrees C for 24 hours and obtaining gold cluster-supported catalyst (Aun/MO) having atomic number of 24; and
   (2) method of using the supported gold cluster, which involves catalyzing hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide in presence of supported gold cluster. The catalytic reaction is carried out at 100-150 degrees C under pressure of 1-4 MPa in presence of reaction gases chosen from carbon dioxide, hydrogen and nitrogen.
   n,X = not defined.
   N.B: n and X are not defined.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carrier is transition metal oxide. The oxide carrier is silica, alumina, magnesia, zirconia, titania, molybdenum oxide, zinc oxide or cobalt oxide. During carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction, nitrogen is used as internal standard.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solvent of sodium borohydride solution is water, methanol, ethanol, isopropanol or butanol. The organophosphonate ligand is tetraphenylphosphonium bromide or triphenylphosphine. The organic solvent is dichloromethane, toluene, acetone, acetonitrile, diethyl ether, dimethylformamide or tetrahydrofuran. The sulfur-containing organic ligand is phenethyl mercaptan, cyclohexyl mercaptan, t-butyl mercaptan, glutathione, 4-t-butylthiophenol or mercaptobenzoxazole.

PN KR1957473-B1; WO2019093726-A1
TI Producing calcium carbonate coated fine particles            using waste concrete sludge, comprises e.g. separating            only water in sludge and fine particles which not            larger than reference particle size in sludge            separately.
AU YOUNG S H
   SANGWOO J
   CHAN Y J
   SHIN H Y
   JI S
   YOO J
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 2019254934
AB    NOVELTY - Producing calcium carbonate coated fine                particles using waste concrete sludge, comprises                (a) separating only water in sludge and fine                particles which not larger than reference particle                size in the sludge separately through solid-liquid                separation and particle separation in sludge                containing waste concrete and water, (b) forming a                suspension by mixing the fine particles separated                from the sludge with water to form a suspension,                (c) adding carbon dioxide into the suspension,                reacting the calcium ions dissolved in water with                carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate, and                generating fine particles in the suspension to                deposit on surface, and (d) separating the liquid                and the solid from the suspension. The separation                process is carried out using a multistage cyclone                device continuously arranged with multiple cyclone                units to perform the particle size separation in                underflow product containing particles of                relatively large size.
   USE - The method is useful for producing calcium                carbonate coated fine particles using waste                concrete sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing calcium carbonate coated fine                particles using waste concrete sludge, comprises                (a) separating only water in sludge and fine                particles which not larger than reference particle                size in the sludge separately through solid-liquid                separation and particle separation in sludge                containing waste concrete and water, (b) forming a                suspension by mixing the fine particles separated                from the sludge with water to form a suspension,                (c) adding carbon dioxide into the suspension,                reacting the calcium ions dissolved in water with                carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate, and                generating fine particles in the suspension to                deposit on surface, and (d) separating the liquid                and the solid from the suspension. The separation                process is carried out using a multistage cyclone                device continuously arranged with multiple cyclone                units to perform the particle size separation in                underflow product containing particles of                relatively large size. The device continuously                performs particle size separation using multiple                cyclone units on an overflow product. The sludge is                generated from process of making waste concrete                into recycled aggregate. The microparticles                included in the overflow product of the cyclone                unit last disposed in the multistage cyclone device                are classified into a microparticle having particle                size passing through 200 sieve.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); P41 (Crushing: centrifuging, separating solids (B02, B03, B04).); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

TI Home plant factory system, has show case-type plant re-distributor placed in indoor, and auto-controller for providing maintenance of correcting value and deviation correction through temperature value deviation correction.
AU HYUN Y S
   CHOI C K
AE XTREME FARM CO LTD (XTRE-Non-standard)
GA 201859484T
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a show case-type plant re-distributor placed in an indoor to provide an artificial environment for cultivating a plant. An environment control part (130) controls temperature of an unite sensor module (140) for sensing temperature of an inner side. A green LED lamp controls illumination of the inner side. A display unit (111) receives data of illumination and carbon dioxide concentration. A daytime temperature setting part (120) sets up temperature for multiple daytime time regions of an inner side of the plant re-distributor. An indicating lamp (113) indicates operation of a night time temperature setting part (125). The environment control part controls the temperature of the inner side of the plant re-distributor. A blower controls humidity of the inner side of the plant re-distributor. An auto-controller (110) provides maintenance of correcting value and deviation correction through temperature value deviation correction.
   USE - Home plant factory system.
   ADVANTAGE - The auto-controller controls the temperature sensed in the unite sensor module so as to control carbon dioxide concentration and an environment of the inner side of the plant re-distributor according to set value for time slot data of illumination, so that error of sensor value can be reduced and the deviation correction and the correcting value can be maintained at a temperature controller module. The system controls the brightness of the green LED lamp being installed at the inner side of the plant re-distributor so as to control a ventilation valve, so that carbon dioxide of the inner side can be ejected and the carbon dioxide concentration of the inner side of the plant re-distributor can be automatically controlled. The system ensures effective plant growth and supplies AC power source in which a direct current (DC) power supply is supplied into DC, so that better energy saving effect can be ensured.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a home plant factory system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

   Indicating lamp (113)

   Unite sensor module (140)

TI Treating oxygen-containing gases, preferably steam and/or carbon dioxide, comprises contacting the feed gases with molten ferrous oxide supplied and thermal decompositing formed molten magnetic oxide iron.
AU GOVOROV G V
   GOVOROV V G
   GOVOROVA E G
AE GOVOROV G V (GOVO-Individual)
   GOVOROV V G (GOVO-Individual)
   GOVOROVA E G (GOVO-Individual)
GA 2018089699
AB    NOVELTY - Treating oxygen-containing gases, preferably steam and/or carbon dioxide, comprises contacting the feed gases with molten ferrous oxide supplied to from above, at a residual pressure of 760-300 mm mercury, and thermal decompositing the formed molten magnetic oxide iron at melt temperature of 1600-2200 degrees C and at a residual pressure of at least 100 mm mercury to molten ferrous oxide for re-use in the contacting step, where both stages are carried out alternately in one reaction volume and continuously turbulizing the melts, and 100-300 mm thick melts are contacted and thermal decomposed.
   USE - The method is useful for treating oxygen-containing gases, preferably steam and/or carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating oxygen-containing gases, preferably steam and/or carbon dioxide, comprises contacting the feed gases with molten ferrous oxide supplied to from above, at a residual pressure of 760-300 mm mercury, and thermal decompositing the formed molten magnetic oxide iron at melt temperature of 1600-2200 degrees C and at a residual pressure of at least 100 mm mercury to molten ferrous oxide for re-use in the contacting step, where both stages are carried out alternately in one reaction volume and continuously turbulizing the melts, and 100-300 mm thick melts are contacted and thermal decomposed, and each step is carried out in within 1-6 minutes. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for oxygen-containing gas treatment plant comprising two reactive lined apparatus provided with heating sources and devices for turbulizing the melts, where in each apparatus, nozzles and fittings for the removal of gaseous reaction products are connected, connected by pipelines to heat exchangers and sources of dilution, in each apparatus, distribution pipes with the nozzles fixed at the bottom to the axis of each tube, each of which through manifolds and valves for alternate supply of oxygen the holding gas in each of the apparatuses is connected to a supply pipeline, valves are installed on each of the discharge pipelines for alternately connecting to heat exchangers and vacuum sources, characterized in that both units are square or rectangular in shape.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The thermal decomposition is carried out by solar, electron or laser beams. Both units are connected together by a common or with their walls.

TI Aqueous alkaline binder composition useful as a binder in molding material mixture, comprises e.g. phenol group-containing, negatively charged or uncharged phenol-aldehyde resin including resoles and mixture comprising resol and novolac.

AE HUETTENES ALBERTUS CHEM WERKE GMBH (ALBV-C)

GA 201801094L
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous alkaline binder composition for curing with carbon dioxide gas, comprises a phenol group-containing, negatively charged or uncharged phenol-aldehyde resin including resoles and mixture comprising at least one resol and at least one novolac, an oxyanion including borate ions, aluminate ions, stannate ions, zirconate ions, titanate ions and their mixture to form a stable complex with the resol-phenolaldehyde resin, 2.5-10 wt.% at least one silane and 2.5-10 wt.% at least one epoxysilane including 3-glycidyloxypropyltriethoxysilane and 3-glycidyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane.
   USE - The binder composition is useful as a binder in molding material mixture to produce foundry molded body in the phenol-resol-carbon dioxide process (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The binder composition produces stable sized foundry molded body with high strength and improved surface finish and/or edge hardness, and has increased flowability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Aqueous alkaline binder composition for curing with carbon dioxide gas, comprises a phenol group-containing, negatively charged or uncharged phenol-aldehyde resin including resoles and mixture comprising at least one resol and at least one novolac, an oxyanion including borate ions, aluminate ions, stannate ions, zirconate ions, titanate ions and their mixture to form a stable complex with the resol-phenolaldehyde resin, 2.5-10 wt.% at least one silane or 2.5-10 wt.% at least one epoxysilane including 3-glycidyloxypropyltriethoxysilane and 3-glycidyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane, based on the total weight of the binder composition. The molar total amount of the phenol groups of the phenolaldehyde resin in the aqueous alkaline binder composition is 1-3 mol/kg, based on the total mass of the aqueous alkaline binder composition. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) molding material mixture for producing a foundry molded body, comprising the aqueous alkaline binder composition and a foundry mold material;
   (2) producing a foundry mold body comprising preparing or providing a molding material mixture, molding the prepared or provided molding material mixture, and curing the molded molding mixture by gassing with carbon dioxide gas; and
   (3) foundry mold body, produced by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises 1-40 wt.%, preferably 1-15 wt.% at least one compound including polyalkylene glycols, phenylalkylene glycol ethers, propylene glycol alkyl ethers, substituted or unsubstituted pyrrolidones, monoethylene glycol and polyethylene glycol, based on the total weight of the binder composition, and/or reaction products of the above mentioned compounds, 0.1-5 wt.%, preferably 0.8-1.5 wt.% at least one compound including 4-20C saturated or unsaturated aliphatic carboxylic acid and alkali metal salts of the above mentioned acids, preferably isononanoic acid and alkali metal salts of isononanoic acid, 3-10 wt.%, preferably 3-6 wt.% phenoxyethanol, butyldiglycol and/or monoethylene glycol, and 0.1-5 wt.%, preferably 0.5-1.5 wt.% 1,3,5-trioxacyclohexane. Preferred Components: The binder composition comprises 3-10 wt.%, preferably 3.5-6 wt.% at least one silane. At least one or all of the silanes used are 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, N-(2-aminoethyl)-3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane and phenyltrimethoxysilane and/or 2.5-7 wt.%, preferably 4-6 wt.% epoxy silane, preferably 3-(glycidyloxy)propyl-triethoxysilane or 3-(glycidyloxy)propyl-trimethoxysilane. The binder composition has pH at 20 degrees C of 12-14, preferably 13-14. The molar amount of the phenolic groups in the aqueous alkaline binder composition is 1.5-2.5 mol/kg, preferably 1.8-2 mol/kg, based on the total mass of the binder composition, and/or the viscosity of the alkaline binder composition at 20 degrees C of 100-1000 mPas, preferably 150-500 mPas, determined according to DIN EN ISO 3219: 1994. The molar ratio of the total amount of alkali metals to phenol groups is 1:1-2.5:1, preferably 1.7:1-1.9:1 and/or the molar amount of the alkali metals in the aqueous alkaline binder composition is 1-7.5 mol/kg, preferably 3-4 mol/kg, based on the total mass of the binder composition. The molar ratio of the total amount of potassium cations to the total amount of sodium cations is 47:1-59:1, preferably 52:1-55:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The negatively charged or uncharged phenol-aldehyde resin is a negatively charged or uncharged resole-phenol-aldehyde resin for curing with carbon dioxide gas in the phenol-resol-carbon dioxide process. The phenol-aldehyde resin has an average molecular weight of 750-1200 g/mol, preferably 850-980 g/mol, determined by gel permeation chromatography.

AU DONG Y
AE LOFON CONTROL EQUIP BEIJING CO LTD (LOFO-Non-standard)
GA 2022D8919U
AB    NOVELTY - The device has servo motors (4) whose output                ends are provided with a first bevel gear (5). An                inner side of an installation groove (8) is                provided with a screw rod (9). A bottom end of a                fixed arm (1) is provided with a movable adjustment                assembly. A top end of the fixed arm is provided                with a brush cleaning assembly. A front end of the                fixed arm is provided with a control panel that is                electrically connected with the servo motor by a                wire. A bottom end of a movable adjusting component                is provided with a hinge seat.
   USE - Device for automatically controlling and                removing carbon dioxide from blast furnace                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes stronger meshing torque                between the first bevel gear, the second bevel gear                and the third bevel gear, so that the adjustment of                the distance is stable, which improves clamping                force and positioning effect of the semi-arc                clamping seat on the pipeline and increases working                efficiency of disassembly and assembly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partial sectional view of                a device for automatically controlling and removing                carbon dioxide from blast furnace gas.

AU XIE W
   DAI X
AE BEIJER REF WUXI CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022D1151Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting pressure of an                air suction port and an exhaust port of a                compressor (1). An outlet temperature of a gas                cooler (2) is detected. A current working condition                exhaust pressure value is calculated according to                the pressure and the outlet temperature. An opening                of a high pressure throttle adjusting valve (3) is                adjusted on an outlet of the gas cooler. The                pressure value reaches the exhaust pressure values                obtained in the step.
   USE - Method for controlling exhaust pressure of a                trans-critical carbon dioxide refrigeration system.                Uses include but are not limited to a refrigeration                exhaust pressure control method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calculating the exhaust                pressure optimization value under the current                working condition according to the optimized                exhaust pressure algorithm, and adjusting the valve                opening to obtain the high pressure throttle                adjusting valve of the gas cooler outlet target                pressure value, so that the method is simple and                reliable, thus effectively improving the                performance coefficient of the refrigeration                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                new-type trans-critical carbon dioxide exhaust                pressure control system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of the new trans-critical carbon dioxide                exhaust pressure control system.

PN CN115084358-A
TI Method for preparing multi-iron heterojunction            barium titanate-cobalt ferrite without substrate            support useful in ferroelectric random access memory            device, comprises e.g. adding sintered powder to            ethanol, transferring suspension to supercritical            device, and injecting carbon dioxide.
AU GAO B
   XU Q
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 2022C4287A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing multi-iron heterojunction                barium titanate-cobalt ferrite without substrate                support comprises (a) mixing barium titanate and                cobalt ferrite according to a molar ratio of                1:0.1-1, adding to water, stirring to obtain a                suspension, and drying to obtain powder, (b)                sintering powder at 500-700&#8451; for 1-2                hours, cooling with a furnace, (c) adding sintered                powder to 40-60 vol.% ethanol, stirring,                transferring suspension to a supercritical device,                injecting carbon dioxide into ultracritical device                to make it reach the supercritical state, where the                stirring reaction time is 3-5 hours, and (d)                cooling to in air, slowly releasing carbon dioxide,                separating suspension after supercritical                treatment, taking supernatant, and drying to obtain                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing multi-iron                heterojunction barium titanate-cobalt ferrite                without substrate support, which is useful in                ferroelectric random access memory (FeRAM)                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides multi-iron heterojunction                with ferromagnetic and ferroelectric properties,                successfully embedding the cobalt ferrite into the                barium titanate substrate under the state of no                substrate support, solves problem of                multireflective substrate clamping effect, has                strong adaptability, controllable, easy operation                and other technical advantages. The thickness of                the hetero-junction is easily controlled by                adjusting the content of cobalt ferrite.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); U14 (Memories, Film and Hybrid Circuits)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Process: The process (i) involves contacting the polymer particles with gas comprising 0.2-0.4 vol.% oxygen, less than 0.2 vol.% nitrogen and carbon dioxide and water, and combusting to flue gas comprising carbon dioxide and water. Water in the flue gas is separated by condensing water in water separator, and the condensed water (13) is separated from the residual gaseous flue gas to yield a dried flue gas (14). Carbon dioxide as contained in the dried flue gas is condensed. The condensed carbon dioxide (16) is stored in a subterranean reservoir.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The powdered polymer feed is powdered polymer waste obtained from cryogenic milling of polymer waste products (1). The powdered polymer waste is a mixture of different polymer waste products comprising at least two polymers including low-density polyethylene, high-density polyethylene, polypropylene, polystyrene, polyvinyl chloride, polyethylene terephthalate, polyurethane and polyphthalamide fibers, preferably polystyrene. Preferred Process: The cryogenic milling process uses liquid nitrogen (4) obtained in the air separation process. The air separation process is cryogenic air distillation process.

   STRUEWING S
   UCHIYAMA M
   NAGASAWA H
AE KAO CORP (KAOS-C)
GA 2022267283
AB    NOVELTY - Production of film on the skin involves (i) applying a composition (B) on the skin, and (ii) coating a film (X) comprising a fiber-containing deposit on the skin in order or reverse order. The composition (B) comprises carbon dioxide.
   USE - Production of film on skin for improving skin wrinkles e.g. large wrinkles (coarse lines) and small wrinkles (fine lines) (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to face, neck, limb, eyes, forehead, eyelid and eyebrow.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides film which reduces wrinkles on the skin by 10 30%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) film (X), which contains the fiber-containing deposit and carbon dioxide;
   (2) film forming kit, which comprises the composition (B) and the film (X) or a film-forming composition for forming the film (X);
   (3) use of the kit for improving skin wrinkles;
   (4) improving skin wrinkles, which involves applying one or two compositions (X) selected from compositions (A) and (B) on the skin, and coating the film (X). The composition (A) contains an amphipathic molecule and has a lamellar structure; and
   (5) use of mixture of one or two compositions (X) and the film (X) or film-forming composition for improving skin wrinkles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of electrostatic spray apparatus.
   Electrostatic spray apparatus (10)
   Low voltage power supply (11)
   High voltage power supply (12)
   Electric circuit (13)
   Pressure-resistant container (15)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The amphipathic molecule is ceramides, polyhydric alcohol fatty acid esters containing a hydroxy group in the molecule, surfactant and/or higher alcohols. The composition (B) further comprises oil, polyhydric alcohol, surfactant, water-soluble thickener and water. The composition (B) is an aerosol composition in which a stock solution and a propellant are filled in a pressure-resistant container (15), and contains carbon dioxide gas as a propellant. Preferred Process: The method further involves applying the composition (A) on the skin. The film (X) is formed by electrostatically spraying the composition containing one or more volatile substances (c1) selected from water, alcohol and ketone, and polymer (c2) having film forming ability. The step (ii) preferably involves attaching the film (X) to the skin, or forming the film (X) directly on the skin by electrostatic spraying. The wrinkles improving method involves holding the film (X) on the skin for 4-20 hours per day and peeling off the film (X), and is continuously used for 3-24 weeks.

PN CN113976178-A
TI Preparation of chloromethylated polystyrene resin-loaded heteropolyacid ion liquid catalyst used for synthesis of cyclic carbonate from carbon dioxide by heating and reacting sodium tungstate, central atom salt and coordination atom salt.
AU YANG X
   MA Z
   ZHENG R
   MA T
   WANG L
   SONG X
   WANG H
   SHAN L
   ZHANG S
   GUO L
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 202224652S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of chloromethylated polystyrene resin (CPS)-loaded heteropolyacid ion liquid catalyst comprises dissolving sodium tungstate, central atom salt and coordination atom salt in distilled water at mol ratio of 1: 1-3: 1-3, heating, adjusting pH to 4-5 using pH regulator, reacting at 60-85degrees Celsius for 2.0-6.0 hours, washing, concentrating and drying to obtain heteropolyacid; adding CPS, N-methylimidazole, bromo-n-butane and acetonitrile at mol ratio of 1: 2-6: 0.8-1.2: 10-25 into reactor, reacting at 60-80degrees Celsius for 24-48 hours, filtering by suction, washing and drying to obtain CPS-MIM-Br; and dissolving heteropolyacid in solvent, adding cation exchange resin and CPS-MIM-Br, slowly stirring at 25-30degrees Celsius, reacting for 8-12 hours, stopping reaction, filtering by suction, washing and drying.
   USE - The method is for preparing CPS-loaded heteropolyacid ion liquid catalyst used for synthesis of cyclic carbonate from carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst solves the problem that the ion liquid for catalyzing esterification reaction of carbon dioxide has high viscosity when preparing and the problem of difficult separation and recovery, has high catalytic effect and good service life, anhd has propylene carbonate yield reaching to 80.08% and selectivity reaching to 93.81% after circulating for 6 times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The mol ratio of sodium tungstate and distilled water is 1: 80-100. The mol ratio of heteropolyacid, cation exchange resin, CPS-MIM-Br and solvent is 1: 0.005-0.04: 0.02-0.05: 10-25.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pH regulator is acetic acid or hydrochloric acid. The solvent is ethanol, methanol and/or dichloromethane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The central atom salt is manganese acetate or zirconium oxychloride. The coordination atom salt is cobalt acetate or zinc chloride. The heteropolyacid is cobalt tungsten manganese compound (CoW11Mn), zinc tungsten manganese compound (ZnW11Mn), cobalt tungsten zirconium compound (CoW11Zr) or zinc tungsten zirconium compound (ZnW11Zr), preferbly Keggin type.

PN CN215403294-U
TI pH adjusting device for direct drinking water system, has water tank whose left side of inner wall is horizontally and fixedly installed with heating pipe.
AU WANG S
AE WANG S (WANG-Individual)
GA 202213175A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a pH adjusting device for direct drinking water system, comprising a water tank, the bottom of the left side of the water tank is fixedly connected with a circulating pump, the left side of the circulating pump is fixedly connected with a circulating pipe, the other end of the circulating pipe is fixedly connected with the top of the left side of the water tank, the middle part of the upper surface of the water tank is fixedly connected with a carbon dioxide tube. The utility model detects the pH in the water tank through the pH detecting head in the water tank, the detected data is transmitted to the single chip, the single chip analyzes the data, when the pH value in the water tank is higher than 9, the single chip controls the electromagnetic valve B to close the water inlet pipe and the water outlet pipe, the electromagnetic valve A is opened, the carbon dioxide is introduced into the water tank, the circulating pump pumps the water in the water tank into the water tank again through the circulating pipe, increasing the flow of the water in the water tank, uniformly adjusting the pH value of the water in the water tank; when the pH value in the water tank is lower than 7, the single chip controls the electromagnetic valve A to close, stopping adding carbon dioxide.

PN WO2021260258-A1; FI202005673-A
TI Producing active carbon by admixing alkali metal carbonate and/or alkali metal bicarbonate, alkali metal chloride and carbonaceous material, pyrolyzing admixture, separating active carbon, inorganic portion, and pyrolytic gases, and combusting pyrolytic gases to yield carbon dioxide.
AU PONOMAREV N
AE LAPPEENRANNAN-LAHDEN TEKNILLINEN YLIOPIS (LAPP-Non-standard)
   UNIV LAPPEENRANTA-LAHTI TECHNOLOGY LUT (UYLA-Non-standard)
GA 2021F0756H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing active carbon, involves (a) admixing alkali metal carbonate and/or alkali metal bicarbonate, alkali metal chloride and carbonaceous material to form an admixture, (b) pyrolyzing the admixture to yield pyrolytic gases, active carbon, and an inorganic portion comprising alkali metal and alkali metal chloride, (c) separating the active carbon, inorganic portion, and pyrolytic gases, and optionally (d) combusting the pyrolytic gases to yield carbon dioxide characterized in that the alkali metal is potassium.
   USE - The method is useful for producing active carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recycles alkali metal carbonate and alkali metal chloride.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for recycling potassium carbonate and potassium chloride in the above-mentioned, involving (i) treating the inorganic portion of step (c) with water to produce an admixture comprising potassium hydroxide and potassium chloride, (ii) treating at least portion of the admixture with carbon dioxide to produce potassium carbonate, and (iii) recycling the admixture comprising potassium carbonate and potassium chloride to step (a);
   (2) use of a mixture of alkali metal carbonate and/or alkali metal bicarbonate, and alkali metal chloride as an activation agent in the method for producing active carbon characterized in that the alkali metal is potassium; and
   (3) use of at least portion of the potassium hydroxide of step (i) in the method for producing active carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of alkali metal carbonate and/or alkali metal bicarbonate to the alkali metal chloride is 5/1-1/2, preferably 1/1. Preferred Conditions: The pyrolyzing is performed at 600-1000 degrees C, preferably 850-900 degrees C. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is the carbon dioxide of step (d). The mixture comprises potassium carbonate and potassium chloride, or the mixture comprises potassium bicarbonate and potassium chloride, where the weight ratio of potassium carbonate to potassium chloride is 5/1-1/2, preferably 1/1, and the weight ratio of potassium bicarbonate and potassium chloride is 5/1-1/2, preferably 1/1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbonaceous material is lignin comprising hydrolytic lignin (preferred) and kraft lignin.

Preferred Properties: In step (i), the mixed powder of corn stalk and wheat stalk is sieved to 100-150 mesh, and the whole corn flour is sieved to 150 mesh. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the baking is carried out at 100-120 degrees C for 10-20 minutes. The step (iii) involves adding and mixing glycerol, dioctyl phthalate and oil repellent for 20-30 minutes.

PN CN112393938-A; CN112393938-B
TI Method for evaluating temperature resistance of air conditioning unit, involves comparing theoretically tolerable maximum ambient temperature of air conditioning unit with maximum tolerable ambient temperature of air conditioning unit.
AU SUN L
   YANG Y
AE SHIJIAZHUANG KING TRANSPORTATION EQUIP (CRRC-C)
GA 202121244M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves continuously filling an air conditioning unit with nitrogen in stages. A weight of the nitrogen cylinder after each level of filling and corresponding high-pressure side pressure value and low-pressure side pressure value of the air conditioning unit is recorded, and volume of refrigeration system of the air conditioning unit is calculated. A density of carbon dioxide in the system is calculated under an optimal charge of carbon dioxide refrigerant. A carbon dioxide physical property database is queried to obtain relationship between pressure, density, temperature, and system discharge pressure to obtain corresponding temperature in the system pressure reaching a predetermined discharge pressure. A theoretically tolerable maximum ambient temperature of the air conditioning unit is obtained. The theoretically tolerable maximum ambient temperature of the air conditioning unit is compared with a maximum tolerable ambient temperature of the air conditioning unit.
   USE - Method for evaluating temperature resistance of air conditioning unit of rail vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The system for evaluating temperature resistance of air conditioning unit ensures that the delivered product does not have a refrigerant discharge failure due to standing storage under the environment of the highest operating temperature of the vehicle by verifying the air conditioning unit before leaving the factory.

TI Production of formic acid used as raw material for            fuel cell, involves preparing ammonium bicarbonate by            reacting carbon dioxide, reacting ammonium bicarbonate            with hydrogen, and replacing amine of formic acid-amine            addition compound.

   HO P K
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
   UNIV KOOKMIN IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYKO-C)
GA 202043949M
AB    NOVELTY - Production of formic acid involves preparing                ammonium bicarbonate by reacting carbon dioxide,                amine and water, reacting the ammonium bicarbonate                with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst to form                a formic acid-amine adduct, replacing the amine of                the formic acid-amine addition compound with                another additive base to form a formic                acid-additive base addition compound, and                separating the formic acid-additive base addition                compound by distillation.
   USE - Production of formic acid using apparatus                (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to raw                material for fuel cell, additive for acidification                in manufacture of animal feed, preservative,                textile or leather industry, reactant for                ice-making in aircraft and runways.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical                production of formic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                apparatus, which consists of a carbonation reactor                for producing ammonium bicarbonate by reacting                carbon dioxide, amine and water, a hydrogenation                reactor for reacting the ammonium bicarbonate and                hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst to produce a                formic acid-amine product, a base exchange reactor                for preparing a formic acid-additive base product                compound in which an amine of a formic acid-amine                adduct compound is substituted with the additive                base by reacting the formic acid-amine adduct                compound and an auxiliary base, and a distiller for                distilling the formic acid-additive base addition                compound to obtain formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The hydrogenation                  reactor is a trickle bed reactor in which a                  heterogeneous catalyst is fixed. The apparatus                  further consists of amine storage container for                  forming an aqueous amine solution by mixing                  water, and amine in the front end of the                  carbonation reactor, and separator for separating                  the amine discharged from the carbonation reactor                  between the carbonation reactor and the                  hydrogenation reactor and recycling to the                  carbonation reactor, and separating the unreacted                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen discharged from the                  hydrogenation reactor and recycling the                  hydrogenation reactor between the hydrogenation                  reactor and the base exchange reactor. The                  carbonated reactor is an absorption tower for                  supplying the exhaust gas containing carbon                  dioxide from the bottom, and supplying the amine                  aqueous solution containing water and amine from                  the top. The carbonation reactor is preferably a                  homogenizer for forming ammonium bicarbonate by                  reacting a high-purity carbon dioxide with an                  aqueous amine solution. The amine separated from                  the top of the distiller is moved to an amine                  storage vessel, and the base separated from the                  bottom of the distiller is moved back to the base                  exchange reactor to be recycled.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is a                  heterogeneous catalyst in which an active metal                  or an active metal salt is supported in a porous                  carrier. The active metal is chosen from iridium                  (Ir), ruthenium (Ru), and/or mixture. The porous                  carrier is a nitrogen-doped porous carbon                  component, a nitrogen-doped metal oxide, a                  nitrogen-doped metal nitride or a nitrogen-doped                  frame ligand comprising a nitrogen or covalent                  organic framework (COF). The additive base is a                  hydrophobic base. Preferred Properties: The                  additive base has lower pKa than the amine and                  high boiling point.R1-R2 = 1-6C or less than 12C alkyl.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine is a                  tertiary amine of formula: NR1R2R3 (I). The                  tertiary amine is one or more chosen from                  triethylamine, tripropylamine, tributylamine,                  N,N-dimethylbutylamine, N-methylpiperidine,                  1-ethylpiperidine, NN-dimethylpiperazine,                  dimethylcyclohexylamine, dimethylhexylamine,                  N-methylpyrrolidine, N-ethylpyrrolidine                  dimethylphenethylamine, and dimethyloctylamine.                  The additive base is one or more chosen from                  N-methylimidazole, N-ethylimidazole,                  N-methylbenzimidazole, N-ethylbenzimidazole,                  n-butylimidazole, N-methylpyrrolidone,                  N-ethylpyrrolidone, N-butylpyrrolidone,                  N-formylacetamide, N-methylacetamide,                  N-ethylacetamide, N-methylmorpholine, and                  N-formylmorpholine. Preferred Process: The method                  further involves supplying carbon dioxide in the                  flue gas, and amine selectively absorbing the                  carbon dioxide in the flue gas, discharging the                  unreacted carbon dioxide after the hydrogenation                  step and discharging the amine or base after the                  carbonation reaction, and recycling the base                  exchange reaction and distillation step.

GA 2019692238
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing thermally viscous water-in-water polymerization emulsion involves adding a monomer and 7-19 pts. wt. constant salt dispersant into reactor, dissolving in 100-108.8 pts. wt. deionized water and stirring at a rotation speed of 190-210 radations/minute, where the monomer contains 15-40 pts. wt. acrylamide and 0-6 pts. wt. acrylic acid. The reaction solution is heated at 28-32 degrees C temperature, and 15-150 pts. wt. small molecular amine is added to the reactor and carbon dioxide is passed to bring the solution to a saturated state, then 0.002-0.06 pts. wt. initiator is added. When the viscosity of the emulsion system is 10-700 mPa/asterisks, 10-40 wt.% dispersing agent is added, and dispersing agent contains 30-100% variable salt dispersion, follwed by continuously holding in a carbon dioxide atmosphere for 11-13 hours at 30-70 degrees C temperature to obtain the product. The variable salt dispersant is an anion as hydrogencarbonate, and a cation is a soluble salt of ammonium, amine or ammonia.
   USE - Method for preparing thermally viscous water-in-water polymerization emulsion (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method offers product having low viscosity at room temperature and is convenient for transportation. When the subsequent temperature rises, the viscosity will increase with or without a carbon dioxide atmosphere, which is convenient for post-treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Preparing thermally viscous water-in-water polymerization emulsion involves using invariable salt type dispersing agent containing 7-9 pts. wt. anhydrous sodium sulfate and 0-10 pts. wt. ammonium sulfate, preferably 0-5 pts. wt. ammonium sulfate. Further, 5-40 wt.% stabilizer is added as anion stabilizer comprising a polymer obtained by polymerizing acrylic acid, sodium acrylate, and 2-acrylamide-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid. The stabilizer is a cationic stabilizer, and the cationic stabilizer comprises a product obtained by polymerizing acryloyloxyethyltrimethylammonium chloride. It further adds 0-0.02 pts. wt. chelating agent ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid. The initiator contains first initiator and second initiator, where first initiator is sodium hydrogen sulfite, and second initiator is ammonium persulfate, and sodium hydrogen sulfite and ammonium persulfate are added to the reactor. Preferred Processes: The variable salt dispersant involves reacting small molecule amine with carbon dioxide hydrate to obtain the variable salt dispersant, where R1, R2 and R3 are hydrogen group, a saturated alkyl group and an unsaturated alkyl group.. Preferred Compositions: When the polymerization is required, the other one is added, and 0.001-0.02 pts. wt. sodium hydrogen sulfite is added, and 0.001-0.04 pts. wt. ammonium persulfate is added.

PN CN109363033-A
TI Mineral carbonated beverage comprises mineral water, sodium bicarbonate and carbon dioxide.
AU WAN D
AE GUANGDONG GANTEN IND CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201920621G
AB    NOVELTY - Mineral carbonated beverage comprises 1000 kg mineral water, 25-30 kg sodium bicarbonate and 0.01-0.02 g carbon dioxide, where the pH of the carbonated beverage is 5-6.
   USE - As mineral carbonated beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The mineral carbonated beverage is a safe and healthy beverage with special taste of natural minerals and prolonged quality guarantee period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the mineral carbonated beverage, which involves performing water extraction, water source ultrafiltration, raw material ratio, tank sterilization, automatic filling, online testing, labeling and packaging product, where the raw material processing step is performed by blending step and a mixing step, the tank sterilization step is performed by bottle sterilization step and a cap sterilization step, the online detecting step involves detecting step and a rejecting step, the labeling and packaging process involves a labeling step, a coding step, and a wrapping step, and the method involves sequentially subjecting natural mineral water to water source extraction through the water source mining process, filtering by the water source ultrafiltration, blending the filtered water source with sodium bicarbonate through the blending step to obtain a formulated liquid, then mixed with carbon dioxide gas to obtain a soda water, sterilizing the bottle and bottle cap, filling the soda water through the automatic filling process, after filling, sorting the defective portions by the detecting step and the eliminating step, transferring the qualified product to the warehouse through the automatic labeling process of the labeling step, and then packaging based on package code.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The mineral carbonated beverage comprises 1000 kg mineral water, 29 kg sodium bicarbonate and 0.012 g carbon dioxide, where the pH of the carbonated beverage is 5.8. Preferred Method: The water source mining process involves collecting water source at a depth of 160 m or more, taking water from a rock formation fracture, through a water-cooled water pump, pumping pipe and facilities which are stainless steel sealed pipe fittings. The water source ultrafiltration process involves performing the natural mineral water filtration by an ultrafiltration system comprising an ultrafiltration membrane filament and a separation device on both sides of the ultrafiltration membrane filament. The raw material batching process involves blending using a blending system comprising a food grade stainless steel aseptic tank, and then mixing sodium bicarbonate solid with the filtered natural mineral water to obtain blending liquid, and mixing using a mixing system of a two-stage mixing ratio machine for mixing the mixing liquid and the carbon dioxide gas. The tank sterilization involves (a) taking bottle disinfection system comprising a bottle washing machine and chlorine dioxide to perform high temperature sterilization on the bottle cap sterilization operation and (b) bottle cap sterilization system comprising an online UV lamp sterilizer. The automatic filling process involves performing washing and filling step using filling system comprising a bottle washing machine, a filling machine and a capping machine for filling and capping the carbonated beverage. The online detecting process involves using an X-ray transmitter, two high-frequency cameras, and a remover to automatically detect and sort the system for reserve detection and capping monitoring, and to sort out unqualified items. The labeling and packaging process includes a labeling step, a coding step and a wrapping step. The labeling step is performed using labeling machine, two high-frequency cameras, and a rejector to form an automatic labeling system for automatic labeling operation and sorting out defective products with poor labeling operations. The coding step is performed using a laser coding device, a high-frequency camera and a rejector to form a laser coding system for automatic laser coding and sorting out defective products with poor coding. The wrapping code step is performed using a wrapping and stacking system comprising a wrapping machine and a palletizing package for quality product packaging and stacking operation.

   MATSUBARA W

   OKINO S
   NAKATANI H
   YUSHIMA M
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND CO LTD (MITO-C)
GA 201876727Q
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a pressure adjuster (131) for adjusting pressure of natural gas (1) after carbon-dioxide separation by carbon-dioxide separators (121-123). A high-boiling-point-hydrocarbon-component separator (132) liquefies and separates a high-boiling-point hydrocarbon component or natural-gas liquid (3) by cooling the natural gas after the pressure adjustment by the pressure adjuster. A heater (133) heats the natural gas after the separation of the high-boiling-point hydrocarbon component by the high-boiling-point-hydrocarbon-component separator.
   USE - Mobile apparatus for purifying natural gas from ground in oil and gas fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus can be moved to natural-gas source that discharges a natural gas differing in composition from the natural gas, where the natural gas can be efficiently purified by simply adjusting and controlling opening degrees of on-off valves according to composition of the natural gas. TI Respiration gas monitor (RGM) device for monitoring of target gas e.g. carbon dioxide, has control device that is operative to switch RGM device between monitoring state and calibration state.
AU GERETY E P
AE KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
   KONINK PHILIPS NV (PHIG-C)
GA 201875979S
AB    NOVELTY - The RGM device (10) has a respired air flow path (36) for carrying respired air. An infrared light source (30) is arranged to launch infrared light (34) through respired air flow path. An optical detector (40) is arranged to detect infrared light after passing through respired air flow path. An absorption line bandpass filter (42) having a passband encompasses absorption line of target gas. A control device is operative to switch RGM device between monitoring state in which absorption line bandpass filter is in path of the infrared light and reference line bandpass filter is not in path of infrared light and calibration state in which reference line bandpass filter is in path of infrared light and absorption line bandpass filter is not in path of infrared light.
   USE - Respiration gas monitor (RGM) device for monitoring of target gas e.g. carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The monitoring of carbon dioxide or another target gas in respired air is more accurate. The RGM device does not require connection to nitrogen or calibration gas. The respired gas flow through the RGM device is not interrupted to perform calibration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of operating a respiration gas monitor (RGM) device.

   Infrared light (34)
   Respired air flow path (36)
   Optical detector (40)
   Absorption line bandpass filter (42)

PT P
PN CN108523845-A; CN108523845-B
TI Portable metabolic measuring instrument, has fixed system which consists of front end holder, data line, and rear end holder to ensure that measuring device does not fall off during normal movement.
AU DING L
   XU Y
   LUO B
   LIU D
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 201874296T
AB    NOVELTY - The instrument has a flow rate sensing module (1), a pressure sensing module (2), a temperature sensing module (3), and a carbon dioxide concentration sensing module (4) that obtain data such as flow rate, pressure, temperature, and carbon dioxide concentration directly from the gas exhaled from the human nostrils. The fixed system consists of a front end holder (6), a data line (7), and a rear end holder (8) to ensure that the measuring device does not fall off during normal movement or a displacement that affects the measurement. The final result is transmitted to the mobile terminal through the wireless transmission system. The final metabolic rate-time curve is made in combination with the scanned nostril area. The total amount of metabolism is determined for any period of time to allow the user to intuitively get the own metabolic status.
   USE - Portable metabolic measuring instrument.
   ADVANTAGE - The real-time metabolic status of the human body is monitored. The total amount of metabolism in any period of time is found.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the portable metabolic measuring instrument. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Flow rate sensing module (1)
   Pressure sensing module (2)
   Temperature sensing module (3)
   Carbon dioxide concentration sensing module (4)
   Front end holder (6)
   Data line (7)
   Rear end holder (8)

PT P
PN US2018221787-A1; WO2018144740-A1; WO2018144740-A8; IN201947031274-A; CN110291061-A; KR2019115008-A; SG11201906611-A1; EP3577097-A1; JP2020507454-W; BR112019015922-A2; MX2019009164-A1; US10710004-B2; IN347874-B; BR112019015922-B1; KR2234883-B1; RU2019125892-A; SG11201906611-B; JP6896085-B2; MX378115-B; EP3577097-B1; RU2756585-C2; ES2897519-T3; CN110291061-B
TI Treating heavy residual streams formed in            coproduction of propylene oxide and styrene monomer,            comprises contacting organic stream with e.g. water in            which sodium is extracted from the stream, and            separating aqueous phase from organic phase.
AU NAGY S
   NGUYEN H H
   LINDSEY D D
   KIMMICH B
   BRUSCHI N
   MONTANO-LAWRENCE J D
   TURNER J E
   JACKSON D L
   DEARTH A S
   MONTANO L YA D
   TURNER YU E
AE LYONDELL CHEM TECHNOLOGY LP (LYOD-C)
   LYONDELL CHEM TECHNOLOGY LP (LYOD-C)
   LYONDELL CHEM TECH LP (LYOD-C)
GA 201866262J
AB    NOVELTY - Treating heavy residual process streams formed                in the coproduction of propylene oxide and styrene                monomer via contact of the residual process streams                with carbon dioxide, comprises: (a) contacting an                organic stream with water and carbon dioxide, where                a sodium is extracted from the organic stream; and                (b) separating an aqueous sodium salt-containing                slurry phase from an organic phase comprising a                reduced sodium content relative to the organic                stream.
   USE - The method is useful for treating heavy                residual process streams formed in the coproduction                of propylene oxide and styrene monomer via contact                of the residual process streams with carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: upgrades low value heavy                residue(s) produced in the co-production of                propylene oxide and styrene monomer (POSM) process;                removes de-ashing agent of at least a portion of                the sodium content to increase the fuel quality of                waste streams; is efficient in reducing the sodium                contamination in the waste stream that generates a                large volume of acidic waste stream and results in                a fuel product that is acidic, potentially                resulting in corrosion and susceptibility to                undesired decomposition/fouling; and provides                enhanced removal of sodium from the organic phase                as compared to conventional methods and sodium                removal without or with a reduced usage of the                conventional utilization of acid and/or added                solvent to control the properties of the organic                stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The organic stream is a                  heavy residue formed in the co-production of                  propylene oxide and styrene. The contacting in                  (a) comprises combining the carbon dioxide with                  the water to form a carbon dioxide-saturated                  water stream, and contacting the carbon                  dioxide-saturated water stream with the organic                  stream. The method further comprises: controlling                  the concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon                  dioxide-saturated water stream by controlling the                  temperature and/or the pressure; and repeating                  (a) and (b) at least one time on the organic                  phase and/or subjecting the organic phase to ion                  exchange to obtain an organic phase having a                  further reduced sodium content. The pressure is                  atmospheric pressure to 500 psi. The temperature                  is 5-90 degrees C. The contacting in (a)                  comprises combining the organic stream and the                  water to form a mixture, and injecting the carbon                  dioxide as a gas. The carbon dioxide gas is                  injected at a flow rate of 1-90 wt.% of the                  combined mass of the organic and aqueous phases.                  The contacting in (a) is performed at a pressure                  of greater than or equal to atmospheric pressure.                  The separating in (b) further comprises                  separating a gas comprising carbon dioxide and/or                  a carrier gas and/or recycling at least a portion                  of the separated gas to the contacting of (a).                  (a) and (b) are performed in a single apparatus.                  The further reduced sodium content comprises the                  sodium content of less than 100 ppm sodium. The                  reduced sodium content comprises the sodium                  content of less than 250 ppm sodium. The organic                  stream and the water are present at a volumetric                  ratio of 1:1-10:1. The contacting of (a) and/or                  the separating of (b) are performed continuously.                  The separating of (b) is performed at a                  temperature of 15-85 degrees C. The carbon                  dioxide is provided by a gas comprising pure                  carbon dioxide and/or other gases comprising                  greater than 50 vol.% of carbon dioxide. At least                  a portion of the contacting of (a) occurs in a                  liquid-liquid contactor.
   EXAMPLE - 2 l Basic propylene oxide/styrene process                waste stream comprised of 4000 ppm of sodium was                diluted with 2 l of ethyl benzene (resulting in                2000 ppm sodium in the organic phase), and combined                with 4 l of water. The process waste stream                comprised a mixture of methyl benzyl alcohol,                2-phenyl ethanol and ethers and included higher                molecular weight components. The diluted mixture                was combined in a 5 gallon vessel equipped with a                recirculation loop and a dip-tube for carbon                dioxide purging. The vessel was then purged with                carbon dioxide, and pressurized up to 30 psi of                carbon dioxide pressure. The mixture was                periodically (3 times, at an interval of 1 hour)                mixed by circulating the liquid through the                recirculation loop for 1 minute. After 4 hours, the                mixture was allowed to separate for 4 hours, and                the aqueous layer was drained from the vessel,                providing a neutral, organic phase containing 250                ppm of sodium. The resulting organic phase                comprised of 250 ppm of sodium, was again treated,                by mixing with fresh water and carbon dioxide. The                treatment resulted in an organic phase containing                less than 10 ppm sodium.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.)

PT P
PN CN108101923-A; CN108101923-B
TI Separating and purifying glabridin monomer involves putting licorice into extracting kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, heating the extracting device, increasing to extraction condition, starting circular extraction.
AU DUAN M
   FANG T
   GE F
   LI H
   SHI Q
   MA J
   WANG X
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
   UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201844507C
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and purifying glabridin monomer involves putting licorice into extracting kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, heating the extracting device, increasing to extraction condition, starting circular extraction at constant temperature and constant pressure to extraction time in the extracting process to extract as extracting agent, adding ethanol as entrainer to supercritical carbon dioxide, extracting from the separating kettle I and separating kettle II, discharging and weighing to obtain licorice extract. The licorice extract is added organic solvent to dissolve and certain concentration of sample liquid is prepared. The sample liquid is fitered by 0.45 mm filter film.
   USE - Method for separating and purifying glabridin monomer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate and purify glabridin monomer that is suitable for industrial separation and purification, and has high performance liquid chromatography, high purity i.e. more than 90%, long production period, low preparation monomer purity, difficult industrial popularization, high preparing efficiency, short production period and controllable product quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The prepared sample solution of certain sample is injected to dynamic axial compression in industrial chromatography, organic solvent is eluted. The eluting flow speed is controlled, and ultraviolet online sample separation situation is used. The detector detects light, the height of peak and peak time. The eluent is collected at ending time, decompressed and eluent is concentrated, and dried in vacuum to obtain glabridin monomer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: The temperature of the extraction kettle is 40-65 degrees C. The extracting pressure is 30-65 megapascal. The extracting time is 0.5-3.0 hours. The separating kettle I temperature is 40-65 degrees C. The pressure is 6-18 mapascal. The separating kettle II temperature is 45-55 degrees C. The pressure is 4.5-8 megapascal. The detection wavelength of ultraviolet detector is 283 nanometer. The elution flow rate is 2-10 mL/minute. The ratio of eluting in water and organic solvent is 0:100-80:20. Preferred Components: The organic solvent is methanol, acetonitrile or 50-100 % ethanol. The dynamic axial compression chromatograph filler of industrial chromatogram is C18-100AA, UniQ-50, UniSi10-300, NM100, UniPSA30-300. Preferred Composition: The amount of the sample liquid is 1-10 mL.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); B02 (Fused ring heterocyclics.)
MC A01-D02; A01-E13; B06-A03; B11-B03C

PN CN107866234-A; CN107866234-B
TI Use of zinc thioindate/titanium dioxide Z-scheme            system composite photocatalyst for reducing carbon            dioxide for generating methane.
AU YANG G
   CHEN D
   DING H
AE UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES BEIJING (UYGJ-C)
GA 201829785L
AB    NOVELTY - A zinc thioindate/titanium dioxide Z-scheme                system composite photocatalyst is used for reducing                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Zinc thioindate/titanium dioxide Z-scheme                system composite photocatalyst used for reducing                carbon dioxide for generating methane                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst has excellent performance,                uses cheap raw material, has simple process,                effectively reduces cost of product, expands light                absorption range, and improves utilization rate of                sunlight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the composite photocatalyst                comprising:
   (A) weighing 0.6 g P25 nanocrystalline                titanium dioxide mixed crystal type, ultrasonic                dispersing in 120 mL 10 mol/L sodium hydroxide                solution for 30 minutes, stirring at room                temperature for 30 minutes, transferring to                PTFE-lined reaction kettle, hydrothermal treating                at 180 degrees C for 48 hours, centrifuging,                washing product, separating, and oven drying for 10                hours;
   (B) dispersing in 200 mL 0.1 mol/L dilute                hydrochloric acid, soaking for 48 hours, and ion                exchanging;
   (C) dispersing in 80 mL 0.02 mol/L sulfuric                acid solution, hydrothermal treating at 100 degrees                C for 12 hours, centrifuging, washing product,                separating, oven drying for 10 hours , and                calcining at 600 degrees C for 2 hours in                air;
   (D) weighing zinc chloride, indium chloride,                and thioacetamide at molar ratio of 1:2:4,                dissolved in ethylene glycol, adding to product in                third step, and continuously stirring for 30                minutes;
   (E) heating for 2 hours at 120 degrees C with                solvent; and
   (F) precipitating, centrifuging, washing,                separating, and oven drying for 10 hours.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN107814395-A
TI Preparing cyanamide comprises e.g. adding organic polar solvent or water into reaction vessel, adding liquid carbon dioxide and lime nitrogen into reactor, adding inhibitor and then stirring reaction.
AU MA X
   SHI S
AE NINGXIA JIAFENG CHEM CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2018240643
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyanamide comprises e.g. adding organic polar solvent or water into reaction vessel, adding liquid carbon dioxide and lime nitrogen into reactor, then adding inhibitor, stirring reaction to obtain dicyandiamide slurry mixed solution, adjusting pH of dicyandiamide slurry mixture solution to 7.5, filtering dicyandiamide slurry mixture solution to obtain dicyandiamide filtrate, filtering residue and washing to obtain mother liquor and black residue and adding mother liquor and dicyandiamide filtrate into reaction vessel to obtain predetermined concentration of dicyandiamide solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyanamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyanamide has no explosion phenomenon of high reaction temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cyanamide comprises (i) adding organic polar solvent or water into reaction vessel, (ii) adding liquid carbon dioxide and lime nitrogen into reactor, then adding inhibitor, (iii) stirring reaction to obtain dicyandiamide slurry mixed solution, adjusting pH of dicyandiamide slurry mixture solution to 7.5, (iv) filtering dicyandiamide slurry mixture solution to obtain dicyandiamide filtrate, filtering residue and washing to obtain mother liquor and black residue, (v) adding mother liquor and dicyandiamide filtrate into reaction vessel, and repeating step (ii) to obtain predetermined concentration of dicyandiamide solution, (vi) adjusting pH of predetermined concentration of dicyandiamide solution after filtering, (vii) adjusting pH of finely-digested dicyandiamide solution to second predetermined pH, and (viii) settling the dicyandiamide solution obtained in step (vii) to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (v), preset concentration of dicyandiamide solution is 50%, repeating step (ii) for 4 times, when step (ii) is performing for the first three times, reaction temperature in the reactor is 15 degrees C, when step (ii) is performing for the fourth time, reaction temperature in the reactor is 10 degrees C. In step (ii), passing air into reactor. The method comprises adding liquid carbon dioxide and lime nitrogen for 7 hours. In step (iii), stirring rate is 125 revolutions per minute. In step (iv), filtration time is 40 minutes. The storage temperature of the finished dicyandiamide is 15-18 degrees C. Preferred Components: The weight ratio between lime nitrogen and liquid carbon dioxide is 1:1.5. The nitrogen content in lime nitrogen is greater than 20.5%, and fineness is 200 mesh. The nitrogen content in black residue is less than 1.9%.

TI Sound-proofing structure for air blower, which supplies carbon dioxide gas to leaf surface vicinity of crops in house, has main body for installing sound absorption material to inner surface of indoor air contact part of casing.
AU FURUTA S
   ISHIGAKI Y
AE FURUTA DENKI KK (FUTA-C)
GA 201805398T
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a sound absorption material (10) installed to an interior wall surface (1e) of a casing (1) while excluding an opening part of a suction-inlet (2) and an exhaust port. The sound absorption material is arranged to an inner surface of a guard plate (11), which is provided in the suction-inlet. A structure main body installs the sound absorption material to the inner surface of an indoor air contact part of the casing and the guard plate of the suction-inlet. The exhaust port is provided in an upper-lower surface of the casing.
   USE - Sound-proofing structure for an air blower, which supplies carbon dioxide gas to a leaf surface vicinity of crops in a house.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure main body installs the sound absorption material to the inner surface of the indoor air contact part of the casing and the guard plate of the suction-inlet, so that growth of crops can be promoted, utility property of the structure can be improved and supply of the carbon dioxide gas to a leaf surface vicinity of the crops in a house can be ensured.

PT P
PN CN110002473-A
TI Recovering carbon dioxide granulated baking soda in flue gas comprises dedusting industrial tail gas containing carbon dioxide, desulfurizing, cooling, washing, and separating into fine crystals by slurry pumping hydrocyclone classifier.
AU DU W
AE DALIAN RUNLIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 201963531R
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering carbon dioxide granulated baking soda in flue gas comprises (i) dedusting industrial tail gas containing carbon dioxide, desulfurizing, pressurizing and sending to a carbon dioxide purification, (ii) taking out crystal slurry out from lower part of crystallizer enters hydrocyclone classifier through slurry pump, sending to crystallizer circulation pump outlet pipe through fine crystal pump to return to crystallizer or alkali tank, providing upper part of crystallizer with mother liquid discharge pipe, (iii) cooling exhaust of alkali tank, washing by water, combining with carbonized tail gas discharged from crystallizer and pressurizing by fan and sending to upper part of small diameter end of crystallizer to further recycle the carbon dioxide in system, and (iv) taking out granular baking soda crystal slurry from lower part of carbonization crystallizer and separating into fine crystals by slurry pumping hydrocyclone classifier.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering carbon dioxide granulated baking soda in flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is conducive to energy saving, consumption reduction, environmentally friendly and has stable operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering carbon dioxide granulated baking soda in flue gas comprises (i) dedusting industrial tail gas containing carbon dioxide, desulfurizing, pressurizing, sending to a carbon dioxide purification and concentration device for concentration, sending to the circulation pipe of the baking soda crystallizer circulation pump, producing soda ash and baking soda fine powder in the production process is quantitatively fed into the alkali tank, dissolving with filtrate and make-up water, and sending lye to the circulation tube before the circulation pump of the crystallizer, (ii) taking out crystal slurry out from the lower part of the crystallizer enters the hydrocyclone classifier through the slurry pump, where the large-sized particles enter the centrifuge to separate the solid and liquid, sending solid to be dried and sieving to obtain products of different particle sizes, discharging fine crystals from the top of the hydrocyclone classifier enter the fine crystal tank, sending to the crystallizer circulation pump outlet pipe through the fine crystal pump to return to the crystallizer or alkali tank, providing upper part of the crystallizer with a mother liquid discharge pipe, where the two coordinately control the crystal disk storage amount and particle distribution in the crystallizer, (iii) cooling exhaust of the alkali tank, washing by water, combining with the carbonized tail gas discharged from the crystallizer and pressurizing by the fan and sending to the upper part of the small diameter end of the crystallizer to further recycle the carbon dioxide in the system, and (iv) taking out granular baking soda crystal slurry from the lower part of the carbonization crystallizer and separating into fine crystals by a slurry pumping hydrocyclone classifier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chemical exhaust gas, the adsorption method or the membrane separation method is used to purify the industrial exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide to more than 90%, and is used as a raw material for the production of granular baking soda. The hydrocyclone classifier controls the ratio of the top flow to the underflow of 0.5-1.5:1, so that the ratio of the particle baking soda size to the centrifugal separation and drying system is more than 80%. The hydrocyclone classifier controls the ratio of the top stream to the underflow of 0.5-1.5:1, so that the proportion of particles entering the centrifugal separation and drying system is less than 80%. Preferred Method: The method comprises providing upper part of the carbonization crystallizer is small and small and upper part with a central tube, adding circulating exhaust gas to the outside of the bottom end of the central tube, forming natural circulation by the density difference, connecting bottom of the central tube with the forced circulation tube, passing circulation pump and mother liquid and returning to the bottom of the crystallizer after the cooler. The method comprises recovering carbonized tail gas and alkali tail gas 20-40% of carbon dioxide content, and returning to the carbonization crystallization system through the pressurized portion. The method comprises taking out granular baking soda from the lower part of the carbonization crystallizer and controlling to further separate fine crystals by a slurry-pumping liquid classifier of 30-40%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
