PN CN110746691-A
TI Modified polypropylene composite material used for            pipeline, is obtained by extruding and granulating e.g.            polypropylene, ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer,            unsaturated carboxylic acid graft copolymer,            phosphogypsum, and montmorillonite.
AU XU D
   GUO J
   QIN S
   ZHOU Y
   HE W
   WU H
   GAO C
AE GUIZHOU MATERIALS IND TECHNOLOGY RES INS (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202012769R
AB    NOVELTY - A modified polypropylene composite material is                obtained by extruding and granulating 100 pts. wt.                polypropylene, 1-6 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate                copolymer, 1-6 pts. wt. ethylene propylene diene                monomer rubber, 5-20 pts. wt. unsaturated                carboxylic acid graft copolymer of polyolefin                elastomer, phosphogypsum treated with supercritical                carbon dioxide, 6-9 pts. wt.                decabromodiphenylethane treated with supercritical                carbon dioxide, nano-antimony trioxide treated with                supercritical carbon dioxide, silane-coupling                agent, dicumyl peroxide, micro-nano organic                modified montmorillonite treated with supercritical                carbon dioxide, micro-nano flake graphite treated                with supercritical carbon dioxide,                1,1,3-tris(2-methyl-4-hydroxy-5-tert-butylphenyl)butane,                2-mercaptobenzimidazole, light stabilizer, and                calcium stearate. The unsaturated carboxylic acid                graft copolymer of a polyolefin elastomer is                obtained by melt-extruding butynedioic acid, a                polyolefin elastomer, and dicumyl peroxide.
   USE - Modified polypropylene composite material is                used for producing outer layer pipe of power                double-walled pipe (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The modified polypropylene composite material                produces power double-walled pip with high flame                retardance, antistatic performance, and notch                impact strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A modified polypropylene composite material is                obtained by extruding and granulating 100 pts. wt.                polypropylene, 1-6 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate                copolymer, 1-6 pts. wt. ethylene propylene diene                monomer rubber, 5-20 pts. wt. unsaturated                carboxylic acid graft copolymer of polyolefin                elastomer, 3-10 pts. wt. phosphogypsum treated with                supercritical carbon dioxide, 6-9 pts. wt.                decabromodiphenylethane treated with supercritical                carbon dioxide, 2-3 pts. wt. nano-antimony trioxide                treated with supercritical carbon dioxide, 0.2-1                pt. wt. silane-coupling agent, 1-6 pts. wt. dicumyl                peroxide, 1-5 pts. wt. micro-nano organic modified                montmorillonite treated with supercritical carbon                dioxide, 1-6 pts. wt. micro-nano flake graphite                treated with supercritical carbon dioxide, 0.4-0.6                pt. wt.                1,1,3-tris(2-methyl-4-hydroxy-5-tert-butylphenyl)butane,                0.4-0.6 pt. wt. 2-mercaptobenzimidazole, 0.4-0.6                pt. wt. light stabilizer GW-944                (poly((6-((1,1,3,3-tetramethylbutyl)amino)-1,3,5-triazine-2,4-diyl)((2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-4-piperidinyl)imino)-1,6-hexanediyl((2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-4-piperidinyl)imine))),                and 0.4-0.6 pt. wt. calcium stearate, through a                twin-screw extruder with a micro-nano lamination                stretching device. The supercritical carbon                dioxide-treated components are made by mixing                phosphogypsum, decabromodiphenylethane, antimony                trioxide, montmorillonite, and flake graphite with                an ethanol solution of methacryloxy functional                silane. The unsaturated carboxylic acid graft                copolymer of a polyolefin elastomer is obtained by                melt-extruding butynedioic acid, a polyolefin                elastomer, and dicumyl peroxide. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for preparation of modified                polypropylene composite material, which involves                uniformly mixing the components, extruding and                granulating, cooling, palletizing, drying, and                packing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The flake graphite or                  montmorillonite is crushed by supersonic jets to                  accelerate the collision to obtain material                  particles with a size of 1-3 mu m, and then                  subjected to supercritical carbon dioxide                  treatment.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silane-coupling                  agent includes a methacryloxy functional silane.                  The mass ratio of phosphogypsum,                  decabromodiphenylethane, antimony trioxide or                  flake graphite to methacryloxy functional silane                  is 100-200:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polypropylene is a                  copolymerized polypropylene having a melt index                  of 0.29-0.3 g/10 minutes. The ethylene-vinyl                  acetate copolymer is an ethylene-vinyl acetate                  copolymer having a vinyl acetate content of 8-10%                  and a melting index of 0.6-0.9 g/10 minutes. The                  ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber is                  ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber having a                  Mooney viscosity of 38-52. The mass ratio of                  butynedioic acid, a polyolefin elastomer, and                  dicumyl peroxide is 1-3:100:0.1-0.3. Preferred                  Property: The polyolefin elastomer is a                  polyolefin elastomer having a melt index of 0.5                  g/10 minutes.

PT P
PN WO2019142220-A1; WO2019142220-A4
TI Preserving wine, involves sealing neck, and phase of bottle, injecting phase of carbon dioxide within bottle, and instaurating phase of essential equilibrium condition between injected carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide.
AU FABBRO A
AE FABBRO A (FABB-Individual)
GA 201964695V
AB    NOVELTY - Method of preserving wine containing within bottle, involves applying phase of a cap suitable for sealing a neck of the bottle, sealing phase of the bottle with respect to an external environment with respect to the same bottle such that the cap hermetically closes the neck of the bottle, injecting phase of a quantity of carbon dioxide within the bottle such that the injected carbon dioxide reaches a free space comprising the remaining volume of the overall volume of the bottle with respect to the volume occupied by the wine remaining in the bottle following the preliminary phase of only partial drinking of the overall wine initially contained in the bottle, and instaurating phase of an essential equilibrium condition between the quantity of injected carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide which is present in a dissolved form inside the wine.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving wine containing within bottle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method preserves wine in a simple and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of preserving wine containing within bottle following a preliminary phase of only partial drinking of the overall wine initially contained in the bottle leaving a remaining quantity of wine in the bottle, involves applying phase of a cap suitable for sealing a neck of the bottle, sealing phase of the bottle with respect to an external environment with respect to the same bottle such that the cap hermetically closes the neck of the bottle, injecting phase of a quantity of carbon dioxide within the bottle such that the injected carbon dioxide reaches a free space comprising the remaining volume of the overall volume of the bottle with respect to the volume occupied by the wine remaining in the bottle following the preliminary phase of only partial drinking of the overall wine initially contained in the bottle, and instaurating phase of an essential equilibrium condition between the quantity of injected carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide which is present in a dissolved form inside the wine, such that the carbon dioxide contained in the wine remaining in the bottle remains essentially unchanged with respect to the initial content in the wine, where the wine essentially not is subject to losses or increasing except negligible ones with reference to the carbon dioxide which is present in a dissolved form, where the method further understands: an evaluation or calculation phase of a value of the carbon dioxide which is present in a dissolved form inside the wine, the quantity of injected carbon dioxide is calculated depending on the value of the carbon dioxide which is present in a dissolved form inside the wine such that the essential equilibrium condition is reached between the quantity of injected carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide which is present in a dissolved form inside the wine, and an activation phase of an indicator which is pushed outside with respect to the cap following the injection of carbon dioxide, the indicator is protruding with respect to the cap following the pushing action applied by the carbon dioxide injected in the free space within the bottle above the level of the wine remaining in the same bottle. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cap for bottle, which comprises a flange suitable for upper support on a neck of the bottle, a lower protrusion with respect to the flange and with respect to the direction of the force of gravity in which the protrusion is suitable for insertion within the neck of the bottle, a case is mounted on the structure, where the case is provided with a shaft which is inserted in a slidable way within the protrusion of the structure, where the shaft is slidable between at least one first position completely protruding with respect to the protrusion and at least one second position in which the shaft is at least partially retracted within the protrusion, the shaft has a length to protrude outside from the protrusion by a distance such that an elastic sleeve applied on the shaft is in a rest position when the shaft is in the first position in which the shaft is completely inserted within the protrusion of the structure, the shaft is further provided with a base against which the elastic sleeve is in abutment condition so that the sleeve is thus placed and vertically delimited between the lower limit of the protrusion of the structure and the base, the elastic sleeve is in an expanded deformation condition radially outwards for engagement and application of a sealing pressure against an internal surface of the wall of the neck of the bottle in the second position, in which the case of the cap comprises at least one chamber which on the one side is in flow communication with a connecting duct to the base for connection with the inside the bottle and on the other side is in flow communication with a plug for injection of a pressurized gaseous fluid at a determined injection pressure, where the case of the cap comprises at least one compartment which is in flow communication at least with a bottom of the base, the compartment is provided with an indicator which includes a piston, which is maintained in an inwardly-directed pushing condition with respect to the compartment itself by counter-pushing element which opposes to the pushing action applied by the pressure present in the compartment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The injection phase of quantity of carbon dioxide provides the injection to obtain an excess pressure within the free space with respect to the external environment with respect to the same bottle in which the excess pressure is 0.1-0.6 bar. The injection phase of quantity of carbon dioxide provides the injection of a quantity of carbon dioxide to obtain an excess pressure within the free space with respect to the external environment with respect to the same bottle in which the excess pressure is 0.20-0.40 bar for a bottle having a capacity of 750 ml, 0.30-0.50 bar for a bottle having a capacity of 500 ml, 0.15-0.35 bar for a bottle having a capacity of 1500 ml, or 0.10-0.30 bar for a bottle having a capacity of 3000 ml. The injection phase of quantity of carbon dioxide provides the injection of a total quantity of carbon dioxide to obtain an excess pressure within the free space with respect to the external environment with respect to the same bottle in which the excess pressure is 0.20-0.30 bar in the case of a bottle having a capacity of 750 ml and wine having an initial content of dissolved carbon dioxide of 200-400 mg/l or 0.25-0.35 bar in the case of a bottle having a capacity of 750 ml and a wine having an initial content of dissolved carbon dioxide of 400-500 mg/l, or 0.30-0.40 bar in the case of a bottle having a capacity of 750 ml and a wine having an initial content of dissolved carbon dioxide of 500-800 mg/l. The injection of carbon dioxide occurs by duct of the cap and the air expulsion contained in the free space occurring by an exit connection for expulsion of the air from the free space provided with a one-way valve set correspondingly to the provided injection pressure in an expulsion phase of the air contained in the free space under the action of the injection of carbon dioxide.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); Q31 (Packaging processes and equipment (B65B, C)); Q32 (Container/closure types, special packaging features and transit packaging (B65D))

TI Electrolysis cell has catalyst portion containing            catalyst which accelerates reaction of hydrogen            generated in hydrogen electrode with carbon dioxide            supplied from inside of gas flow path.
AU NAKAMURA T
   OMORI M
AE NGK INSULATORS LTD (NIGA-C)
GA 2022A92631
AB    NOVELTY - An electrolysis cell comprises a gas flow path                (43) extending in a direction (A), a porous support                substrate (4) having recesses (49) spaced apart                from each other in the direction (A), a crosspiece                arranged between adjacent recesses, a catalyst                portion (50) arranged within the crosspiece and a                hydrogen electrode (6) arranged in the recess. The                catalyst portion is a portion containing a catalyst                which promotes a reaction between hydrogen                generated in the hydrogen electrode (6) and carbon                dioxide supplied from the inside of gas flow path                (43).
   USE - Electrolysis cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolysis cell has excellent                heat-absorption property.

PN KR2022095364-A
TI Producing soybean ice cream, comprises e.g. treating soybean powder with supercritical carbon dioxide, superheating, adding water into superheated defatted soybeans, heating to prepare soymilk, homogenizing, sterilizing, and aging.
AU CHOI S G
   LEE K
   KIM A
   KIM B
AE UNIV GYEONGSANG IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYGY-C)
GA 202289138X
AB    NOVELTY - Soybean ice cream preparation involves treating soybean powder with supercritical carbon dioxide to prepare defatted soybean. The prepared soybean is subjected to supercritical steam treatment. Water is added to the superheated steam-treated soybean and heated to prepare soymilk. An ice cream mixture is homogenized and sterilized with fresh cream, skim milk powder, sugar and guar gum. The sterilized ice cream is aged, stirred and frozen to prepare the ice cream.
   USE - The method is useful for producing soybean ice cream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare soybean ice cream with excellent overrun and heat resistance while maintaining the appearance of the ice cream for a long time, soft and improved taste.

TI Preparing polycrystalline interface copper oxide            for electrocatalysis carbon dioxide reduction comprises            weighing copper oxide powder and sodium bicarbonate            powder for ball milling, dissolving in ethyl alcohol,            and annealing.
AU LI W
   WANG J
   LIU B
   WANG C
   LI X
   ZHANG W
   YAN C
   YANG H
AE UNIV XIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYXT-C)
GA 2022662339
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (i) weighing copper oxide                (CuO) powder and sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3) powder                for ball milling, (ii) dissolving the powder after                ball milling in absolute ethyl alcohol, then                carrying out solid-liquid separation, and (iii)                annealing the obtained product to obtain the                catalyst.
   USE - Preparation method of polycrystalline                interface CuO for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction used for converting greenhouse gas into                low carbon fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The simple method of ball milling increases                the number of CuO surface the amount of the CuO, in                the electro-catalysis CO2 reduction process,                enhances the adsorption of the CO2 intermediate,                promotes C-C coupling and subsequent hydrogenation                reaction, so as to improve the selectivity of C2                product, and improve the yield of 2C product.

PN KR2022035580-A
TI Multi-step process in-situ precipitated calcium carbonate treatment method for preparing hand sheets comprises calcining lime mud, hydrating, homogenizing hydrated calcium hydroxide with pulp slurry and supplying carbon dioxide.
AU AHN J
   LEE S
   KIM C S
   NAM S Y
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
   HANIL CEMENT MFG CO LTD (HANI-Non-standard)
GA 202242525Y
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-step process in-situ precipitated calcium carbonate treatment method comprises calcining lime mud to convert it to calcium oxide, hydrating the calcined calcium oxide to convert it into calcium hydroxide, adding the hydrated calcium hydroxide into a pulp slurry and homogenizing, supplying carbon dioxide to the homogenized mixture to convert the calcium hydroxide into calcium carbonate, manufacturing a hand sheet from a mixture containing the precipitated calcium carbonate and the pulp slurry.
   USE - The multi-step process in-situ precipitated calcium carbonate treatment method is useful for preparing hand sheets.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the whiteness of lime mud and improves the paper brightness for manufacturing the hand sheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The lime mud is calcined at 800-1200 degrees C for 1-4 hours. The calcium oxide is hydrated at 10-100 degrees C for 1-24 hours. The homogenization is carried out using grinding equipment until the particle size is less than 100 mu m. The impurities are reduced and the whiteness of the lime mud powder is improved as the temperature of the hydration process increases. The whiteness of the powder at high temperature increases when the reaction time of the hydration process is increased. The carbon dioxide is supplied through a porous glass bubbler.The brightness of the hand sheet increases as the calcium hydroxide increases. Preferred Components: The pulp slurry is kraft pulp slurry. The high temperature hydration of the calcined calcium oxide produces more white powder when compared to low temperature hydration.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

GA 2021139092
AB    NOVELTY - Fluorosilicate porous hybrid material (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in separating acetylene/ethylene mixed gas and acetylene/carbon dioxide mixed gas fields (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low production cost, and simple operation; utilizes cheap and easily available raw materials; is easy to control the conditions and suitable for industrial production. (I): has suitable pore size and fluorinated functional sites, high separation selectivity, and large adsorption capacity; can enhance the identification of acetylene by hydrogen bonding; and has separation performance of greater than the many existing solid adsorption materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fluorosilicate porous hybrid material of formula ((Cu(dpds)2(SiF6))n) (I) is new, where the dpds is 4,4'-dipyridyl disulfide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises slowly dropping copper hexafluorosilicate solution into 4,4'-dipyridyl disulfide methanol solution under stirring condition, stirring and reacting under the heating condition, filtering and washing to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The concentration of 4,4'-dipyridyl disulfide methanol solution is 15-25 mg/ml. The molar ratio between 4,4'-dipyridyl disulfide and copper hexafluorosilicate is 1-2:1. The concentration of copper hexafluorosilicate solution is 0.1-0.2 mol/l. Preferred Method: The dripping speed of the copper hexafluorosilicate solution is 0.05-0.2 ml/second. The stirring reaction temperature is 25-50 degrees C and the stirring reaction time is 24-72 hours. In the preparation method of (I), the washing process is carried out by using anhydrous methanol. In the preparation of 4,4'-dipyridyl disulfide methanol solution, ultrasonic vibration is carried out at ultrasonic power of 80-120 W for 20-30 minutes.

TI Culturing anaerobic bacteria, e.g. Actinomyces,            Bacteroides, Clostridium, Fusobacterium, or Prevotella            genus, by culturing anaerobic bacteria in bioreactor            under anaerobic atmosphere comprising carbon dioxide            and nitrogen.
AU JAHIC M
   EMERSON D
   MCKENNA C
   RAZIUDDIN R
   MACKENNA C
AE EVELO BIOSCIENCES INC (EVEL-Non-standard)
   EVELO BIOSCIENCES INC (EVEL-Non-standard)
   EVELO BIOSCIENCES INC (EVEL-Non-standard)
   EVELO BIOSCIENCES INC (EVEL-Non-standard)
GA 2020B6343G
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing anaerobic bacteria involves                culturing the anaerobic bacteria in a bioreactor                under an anaerobic atmosphere comprising greater                than or equal to 1% carbon dioxide (CO2); or                culturing the anaerobic bacteria in a bioreactor                into which a gas mixture comprising greater than or                equal to 1% CO2 is added.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing anaerobic                bacteria (claimed); or for fermentation of                anaerobic bacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - By providing CO2 throughout the culture (e.g.                beyond providing CO2 in the headspace), bacterial                growth is improved. The methods and compositions                result in increased bacterial yield compared to                conventional culture conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                bioreactor comprising anaerobic bacteria under an                anaerobic atmosphere comprising greater than or                equal to 1% CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Alternatively, culturing                  anaerobic bacteria, involves: (a) purging a                  bioreactor with an anaerobic gas mixture                  comprising greater than or equal to 1% CO2; and                  (b) culturing the anaerobic bacteria in the                  bioreactor purged in step (a). The method further                  involves inoculating a growth media with                  anaerobic bacteria, where the inoculation step                  precedes step (b); or inoculating about 5 vol.%                  of the cultured bacteria in a growth media.                  Culturing the anaerobic bacteria involves                  agitating at 50-300 revolutions per minute (RPM),                  preferably 150 RPM. The anaerobic gas mixture is                  continuously added during culturing. CO2 is                  continuously added during culturing. The method                  further involves harvesting the cultured bacteria                  when a stationary phase is reached; centrifuging                  the cultured bacteria after harvesting to produce                  a cell paste; diluting the cell paste with a                  stabilizer solution to produce a cell slurry;                  lyophilizing the cell slurry to produce a powder;                  and irradiating the powder with gamma radiation.                  Preferred Composition: The anaerobic atmosphere                  or anaerobic gas mixture comprises greater than                  or equal to 8%, greater than or equal to 20%,                  8-40%, 20-30%, or 25% CO2. It comprises about 25%                  CO2 and about 75% N2. The anaerobic gas mixture                  comprises about 100% CO2. The growth media                  comprises 5-15 g/L yeast extract 19512; 10 g/L                  yeast extract 19512; 10-15 g/L or 12.5 g/L soy                  peptone A2SC 19649; 10-15 g/L or 12.5 g/L soy                  peptone E110 19885; 1-2 g/L or 1.59 g/L                  dipotassium phosphate; 0.5-1.5 g/L or 0.91 g/L                  monopotassium phosphate; 0.1-1.0 g/L or 0.5 g/L                  L-cysteine-HCl; 0.1-1.0 g/L or 0.5 g/L ammonium                  chloride; 20-30 g/L or 25 g/L maltodextrin; 5-15                  g/L or 10 g/L glucose; 0.01-0.05 g/L or 0.02 g/L                  hemoglobin; and 20-30 g/L or 25 g/L glucidex 21                  D. Preferred Components: The anaerobic atmosphere                  or the anaerobic gas mixture consists essentially                  of CO2 and nitrogen (N2). The growth media                  comprises yeast extract, soy peptone A2SC 19649,                  Soy peptone E110 19885, dipotassium phosphate,                  monopotassium phosphate, L-cysteine-HCl, ammonium                  chloride, maltodextrin, glucose, and hemoglobin.                  The anaerobic bacteria are selected from bacteria                  of the genus Actinomyces, Bacteroides,                  Clostridium, Fusobacterium, Peptostreptococcus,                  Porphyromonas, Prevotella, Propionibacterium, or                  Veillonella. The anaerobic bacteria are from a                  strain of Prevotella bacteria comprising one or                  more proteins given in the specification (Table                  1). Further, the anaerobic bacteria are from a                  strain of Prevotella substantially free of a                  protein given in the specification (Table 2). The                  anaerobic bacteria are Prevotella albensis,                  Prevotella amnii, Prevotella bergensis,                  Prevotella bivia, Prevotella brevis, Prevotella                  bryantii, Prevotella buccae, Prevotella buccalis,                  Prevotella copri, Prevotella dentalis, Prevotella                  denticola, Prevotella disiens, Prevotella                  histicola, Prevotella intermedia, Prevotella                  maculosa, Prevotella marshii, Prevotella                  melaninogenica, Prevotella micans, Prevotella                  multiformis, Prevotella nigrescens, Prevotella                  oralis, Prevotella oris, Prevotella oulorum,                  Prevotella pallens, Prevotella salivae,                  Prevotella stercorea, Prevotella tannerae,                  Prevotella timonensis, Prevotella jejuni,                  Prevotella aurantiaca, Prevotella baroniae,                  Prevotella colorans, Prevotella corporis,                  Prevotella dentasini, Prevotella enoeca,                  Prevotella falsenii, Prevotella fusca, Prevotella                  heparinolytica, Prevotella loescheii, Prevotella                  multisaccharivorax, Prevotella nanceiensis,                  Prevotella oryzae, Prevotella paludivivens,                  Prevotella pleuritidis, Prevotella ruminicola,                  Prevotella saccharolytica, Prevotella scopos,                  Prevotella shahii, Prevotella zoogleoformans, or                  Prevotella veroralis. The bioreactor further                  comprises a growth media. The growth media                  comprises yeast extract, soy peptone A2SC 19649,                  Soy peptone E110 19885, dipotassium phosphate,                  monopotassium phosphate, L-cysteine-HCl, ammonium                  chloride, glucidex 21 D, glucose, and hemoglobin.                  Preferred Parameters: The volume of anaerobic                  bacteria is about 0.1 vol.% of the growth media.                  It is about 1 L in volume, or about 1 ml. The                  growth media is about 20 L in volume or or about                  3500 L in volume. The anaerobic gas mixture is                  added at a rate of 0.002-0.02 vvm. The CO2 is                  added at a rate of 0.002-0.1 vvm, 0.007 vvm, or                  0.1 vvm. Bioreactor is 1-500000 L. Preferred                  Conditions: The anaerobic bacteria are cultured                  for 10-24 hours or for 14-16 hours, or 12-14                  hours. The anaerobic bacteria are cultured at                  35-42 degrees C, preferably 37 degrees C and at                  pH of 5.5-7.5, preferably 6.5. The bioreactor is                  at 35-42 degrees C, preferably 37 degrees C. The                  growth media is at a pH of 5.5-7.5, preferably                  6.5.

TI Regenerating deactivated carbonyl sulfide hydrolyzing agent by heating deactivated carbonyl sulfide hydrolyzing agent, then passing carbon dioxide gas containing saturated water vapor into reducing gas atmosphere, and heat preserving.
AU ZHENG Y
   CAO Y
   LIANG S
   XIAO Y
   LIU S
   JIANG L
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
   FUJIAN SINOGASHOLDER EQUIP INSTALLATION (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2020A00306
AB    NOVELTY - Regeneration of deactivated carbonyl sulfide (COS) hydrolyzing agent involves: (1) heating deactivated COS hydrolyzing agent at a heating temperature (A) under reducing gas atmosphere to obtain a regenerated COS hydrolyzing agent (A); and (2) maintaining the reducing gas atmosphere in step (1), heating the regenerated COS hydrolyzing agent (A) for the second time, then passing carbon dioxide gas containing saturated water vapor into the reducing gas atmosphere, and heat preserving at a heating temperature (B).
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating deactivated COS hydrolyzing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The regeneration method is green regeneration method that does not require solution washing and does not use active component additives, and can effectively save the regeneration period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (1) further involves introducing an inert gas into the reducing gas atmosphere, where the volumetric space velocity of the inert gas is 1000-20000 hour-1. The reducing gas atmosphere in step (1) is obtained by introducing reducing gas, where the ratio of the volumetric space velocity of the reducing gas to the volumetric space velocity of the inert gas is 1: (1-10), and the ratio of the volumetric space velocity of the carbon dioxide gas containing saturated water vapor to the volumetric space velocity of the inert gas is 1:(1-10). The regeneration method uses a device, which is a quartz tube. Preferred Conditions: The temperature for heating the deactivated COS hydrolyzing agent under the inert gas atmosphere is 150-200 degrees C, and the heat preserving time is 30-120 minutes. In step (1), the heating temperature (A) is 200-300 degrees C, and the heat preserving time is 30-240 minutes. In step (2), the heating temperature (B) is 300-400 degrees C, and the heat preserving time is 30-240 minutes. Preferred Components: The volume ratio of saturated water vapor and carbon dioxide gas in the carbon dioxide gas containing saturated water vapor is 1:(0.1-10). The reducing gas is hydrogen, carbon monoxide and/or methane. The inert gas is nitrogen. The carrier of the COS hydrolyzing agent is carbon nitride, aluminum, oxide, silicon oxide, magnesium oxide, titanium oxide, zirconium oxide and/or cerium oxide. The active ingredient is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium oxalate, potassium oxalate, sodium sulfate, and/or potassium sulfate.

GA 202093734J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified molecular sieve catalyst,                comprises (i) modifying molecular sieve using water                vapor passivation, acid treatment, alkali                treatment, alkali metal modification, alkaline                earth metal modification and rare earth metal                modification, and (ii) baking the product obtained                in step (i) at 350-500 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours in                plasma atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified                molecular sieve catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process and is                environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing modified molecular sieve                catalyst; and
   (2) preparing acetic acid by converting                methane and carbon dioxide to obtain acetic acid,                comprises activating methane and carbon dioxide                using synergistic effect of low-temperature plasma                and the modified molecular sieve catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molecular sieve is                  ZSM-5, ZSM-11, ZSM-12, TS-1, SAPO-34, S-1 and/or                  MOR. The plasma atmosphere is hydrogen, argon,                  helium, nitrogen and/or oxygen. The molar ratio                  between methane and carbon dioxide is 0.5-2:1.                  Preferred Method: The modified molecular sieve                  catalyst filling in dielectric barrier discharge                  plasma zone. The reaction temperature is 25-200                  degrees C. The residence time of methane and                  carbon dioxide in the dielectric barrier                  discharge plasma zone is 4-8 seconds.

   JIANG J
AE ZHEJIANG XINHUANKE ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOL (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202081820V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves monitoring real-time emissions of carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, hydrofluorocarbons, perfluorocarbons, and sulfur hexafluoride by a greenhouse gas monitor. Monitored values are transmitted to a central computer for storage. Determination is made to check whether the monitored values are less than a set threshold value. A real-time greenhouse gas emission required value is calculated. Calibration emissions at corresponding time are determined in a corresponding calibration curve. An alarm is sent if the monitored values are less than the set threshold value. Corresponding real-time emission information is obtained by the alarm.
   USE - Greenhouse gas emissions monitoring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively reducing greenhouse gas emissions and effectively monitoring greenhouse gasses produced by industrial production and people lives.

PT P
PN CN111348666-A
TI Preparation of composite anion intercalation            hydrotalcite corrosion inhibitor involves dissolving            metal soluble salts in deionized water, mixing with            anionic soluble salt and sodium hydroxide in water,            hydrothermal reacting, and drying.
AU LIU X
   TONG H
   LI X
   SUI Y
   SUN Z
AE NO 725 RES INST CHINA SHIPBUILDING IND C (CSHI-C)
GA 202063037F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of composite anion intercalation                hydrotalcite corrosion inhibitor comprises                dissolving metal(II) ion soluble salt and                metal(III) soluble salt in deionized water, boiling                to remove carbon dioxide to prepare first solution,                dissolving anionic soluble salts and sodium                hydroxide in deionized water, and boiling to remove                carbon dioxide to prepare second solution; and                mixing prepared solutions, transferring to                PTFE-lined hydrothermal reactor, adding sodium                hydroxide solution to adjust pH to 9-10, reacting                for 24 hours, filtering, washing with deionized                water, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of                composite anion intercalation hydrotalcite                corrosion inhibitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The corrosion inhibitor achieves exchange of                the corrosion anion in the corrosive environment                and interlayer corrosion inhibitor anion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal(II) ion is                  magnesium(II) ion, zinc(II) ion, nickel(II) ion,                  or copper(II) ion. The metal(III) ion is                  aluminum(III) ion, iron(III) ion,, or                  chromium(III) ion. The soluble salt is nitrate,                  chloride, or sulfate. The anion is phosphate,                  vanadate, and/or molybdate. The anionic soluble                  salt is soluble sodium salt. The molar ratio of                  metal(II) ion soluble salt and metal(III) soluble                  salt is 2-4:1, The concentration of metal(II) ion                  in the first solution is 0.10-0.60 mol/L. The                  molar ratio of anion to second solution is                  0.5-2:1. Preferred Method: The hydrothermal                  reaction is done at 80-120 degrees C. The drying                  is done by vacuum drying at 60 degrees C for                  12-24 hours.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN CN111185236-A
TI Use of aryloxy rare earth metal complexes and            borane in catalytic reduction reaction of carbon            dioxide hydrosilylation.
AU XU X
   CHANG K
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202046603J
AB    NOVELTY - Use of aryloxy rare earth metal complexes and                borane, is claimed, where the aryloxy rare earth                metal complex is (REX3)(n) (I).
   USE - The aryloxy rare earth metal complexes and                borane is useful in catalytic reduction reaction of                carbon dioxide hydrosilylation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aryloxy rare earth metal complex: has high                activity and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of aryloxy rare earth metal complexes and                borane, is claimed, where the aryloxy rare earth                metal complex is (REX3)(n) of formula (I).
   n = 1-12;
   RE = rare earth metal element (preferably                scandium, yttrium, lanthanum, cerium, praseodymium,                neodymium, promethium, samarium, europium,                gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, holmium, erbium,                thulium, ytterbium or lutetium);
   X = 6-18C aryloxy (preferably phenoxy) or                boron containing moiety of formula (II); and
   R1-R3 = H, 1-4C alkyl or 6-9C aryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  aryloxy rare earth metal complex comprises                  reacting lithium phenol compound and rare earth                  trichloride in organic solvent at a molar ratio                  of 3:1 at 0-110 degrees C, complexing triaryloxy                  clay with the solvent, sublimating triaryloxy                  rare earth of the complex solvent to obtain                  aryloxy rare earth metal complex without solvent                  coordination. The method for catalyzing the                  hydrosilylation reduction reaction of carbon                  dioxide of aryloxy rare earth metal complex and                  borane comprises dissolving aryloxy rare earth                  metal complex, norane and silane in the                  deuterated reagent in the reactor, introducing                  carbon dioxide gas into the reactor, reacting at                  0-80 degrees C to obtain bis-silyl acetal                  compound of formula (IV) or methane and silicon                  ether. Preferred Components: The molar ratio                  between (I) and borane is 1:1-2. In the                  hydrogenation reduction reaction of carbon                  dioxide silicon, the reducing agent is silane                  compound of formula (III). The molar ratio                  between (I), borane and silane is                  1:1-2:200-5000.R4-R6 = H, 1-4C alkyl, 6-24C aryl or 1-12C                  alkoxy.

PN US2020122904-A1
TI Vent for coffee bean package, comprises laminated film comprising vent having laser-formed channel whose length and cross-sectional area configure to exhaust carbon dioxide at rate more than counter-flow diffusion of oxygen and water vapor.
AU KLANN K
AE LASERSHARP FLEXPAK SERVICES LLC (LASE-Non-standard)
GA 202031806M
AB    NOVELTY - A vent comprises a multilayer laminated film comprising a vent having a breach (A) and a breach (B), and a laser-formed channel (225). The length and cross-sectional area of the laser-formed channel are configured to exhaust carbon dioxide at rate more than counter flow diffusion rate for oxygen and water vapor.
   USE - Vent used for package e.g. bag for packaging roasted coffee beans.
   ADVANTAGE - The vent reduces permeability of oxygen and water vapor, thereby improving the shelf life of coffee beans.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the vent.
   Interior layer (215)
   Channel (225)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The vent has oxygen transmission rate of 0.01-1.5 mL/day, preferably less than 0.01 ml/day, and exhaust 500 mL carbon dioxide at 1Psi for 24 hours. The laser-formed channel has diameter of 50-200 microns. Preferred Components: The multilayer laminate film comprises layer (A) which is laser transmission layer, and layer (B) is laser absorptive layer and layer (C) is laser reflective layer. The layer (B) comprises laser formed channel. The laser-formed channel is inflated and expanded beyond an original thickness of the layer (B). The breach (A) or the breach (B) is laser formed breach, preferably mechanically cut breach. The breach (A) is in contact with interior of a package comprising the package film. The breach (B) is in communication with an exterior of the package. The multilayer laminate film is a patch comprising an adhesive layer for adhesion to a package film exterior surface. The vent further comprises a wetted sealing film over the breach (B) and the multilayer laminate film patches are provided on a roll stock to crush the vent to inflate in size.

   (1) a sensor for detecting carbon dioxide,                which comprises substrate and amidine-functional                polymer which is associated with carbon conductive                material on substrate;

   (3) a method for preparing sensor for                detecting analyte, which involves associating                polymer includes amino side chain and an alkylazide                side chain with conductive material, linking                polymer associated with conductive material to                substrate, quaternizing amino side chain and                functionalizing alkylazide side chain.

The amidine-functional                  polymer is copolymer of amine-containing monomer                  and amidine-functional monomer. The                  amidine-functional polymer is immobilized on                  surface of the substrate. The amidine-functional                  polymer is a copolymer of amine-containing                  monomer and an amidine-functional monomer. The                  amine-containing monomer is vinyl pyridine.                  Preferred Process: The method further involves                  functionalizing alkylazide side chain includes                  introducing amidine-functional group to the side                  chain.

AB    NOVELTY - Methane is dehydroaromatized by introducing feed stream of methane and carbon dioxide to aromatization reactor; converting a portion of methane to aromatic hydrocarbons in the presence of dehydroaromatization catalyst, preferably Mo-HZSM-5 catalyst; regenerating dehydroaromatization catalyst with hydrogen at a regeneration temperature of 700-800 degrees C; repeating introducing, converting, and regenerating steps; and periodically increasing the regeneration temperature by 5-15 degrees C.
   USE - Dehydroaromatization of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - Increasing the reaction temperature has been found to increase the catalyst conversion efficiency by maintaining the percent conversion for a longer period of time. The Mo-oxide/ZSM-5 catalyst provides good catalytic performance and high aromatic compound selectivity and productivity (e.g., greater than or equal to 90% selectivity).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Catalyst is regenerated only with hydrogen. Reaction temperature is increased after a decrease in conversion percentage of 5-20. Reaction temperature is increased after every 2-8 cycles of regenerating. Dehydroaromatization catalyst is carburized before being used in the aromatization reactor, where reaction temperature is increased after a decrease in conversion percentage of greater than or equal to 5%; converting is initially at a temperature of greater than or equal to 745 degrees C; and feed stream comprises 0.05-3 vol.% carbon dioxide, based on 100 vol.% of the feed stream. Reaction temperature is increased after a decrease in conversion percentage of 8-15%. Reaction temperature is increase after every 3-5 cycles. Preferred Conditions: Converting is initially at a temperature of 700-780 degrees C, preferably 730-770 degrees C. Preferred Compositions: Feed stream comprises greater than or equal to 90 vol.% methane and 0.05-3 vol.% carbon dioxide, based on 100 vol.% feed stream. Feed stream comprises 0.05-2 vol.% carbon dioxide based on 100 vol.% feed stream. Preferred Reactor: Aromatization reactor is a fixed bed reactor.

TI Production of methionine used as feed additive for animals, involves performing crystallization by introducing carbon dioxide into reaction liquid containing alkali salt of methionine obtained by hydrolyzing (methylthio)imidazolidine-dione.
AU SATOH Y
   OMOTO N
   KOIZUMI Y
   YAMASHIRO N
   KATAGAMI R
AE SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
GA 201885657J
AB    NOVELTY - Production of methionine involves performing crystallization (2) by introducing carbon dioxide into a reaction liquid containing an alkali salt of methionine obtained by hydrolyzing (1) 5-(2-(methylthio)ethyl) imidazolidine-2,4-dione in presence of an alkali compound, to precipitate methionine, and adding rejected methionine to the reaction liquid.
   USE - Production of methionine used as feed additive for animals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides methionine having improved yield. The methionine rejected in the manufacturing process is efficiently recovered.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of recovery of rejected methionine. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Hydrolysis reaction (1)
   Crystallization process (2)
   Solid-liquid separation process (3)
   Washing process (4)
   Drying process (5)
   Sieving process (6)
   Product-packing process (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rejected methionine is methionine in a mother liquor separated at solid-liquid separation step of separating precipitated methionine in the crystallization step and the mother liquor. Preferred Process: The addition step of the rejected methionine to the reaction solution is carried out by dissolving the rejected methionine in water and adding the methionine to the reaction solution. The method further involves separating methionine precipitated in the crystallization step by solid-liquid separation (3), washing (4) methionine obtained by the solid-liquid separation, and adding the washing liquid separated in the washing step to the reaction liquid. The method further involves subjecting the reaction liquid containing methionine precipitated in the crystallization step to solid-liquid separation to obtain methionine and the mother liquor, after the crystallization step, filtering methionine in the mother liquor to obtain recovered methionine, drying (5) the methionine separated in the solid-liquid separation step to obtain dried methionine, sieving (6) the dried methionine to obtain sieved methionine, packing (7) the sieved methionine powder into a product to obtain a packaged methionine product, and transporting methionine between filtering and packing steps. The rejected methionine further comprises at least one recovered methionine chosen from methionine discharged to outside of the system accompanied by the exhaust in the drying step, micronized methionine which passed through the screen mesh in the sieving step, methionine when filling the container with methionine in the product packaging process, and methionine discharged in each transporting steps.

TI Platinum1.MIL nanocatalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction comprises metal organic framework MIL-101.
AU CHEN Y
   LI H
   WANG M
   YAN X
   ZHANG W
   ZENG J
AE UNIV SCI & TECHNOLOGY CHINA ADVANCED TEC (UYSC-Non-standard)
GA 2018861762
AB    NOVELTY - Platinum1.MIL (Pt1.MIL) nanocatalyst comprises substrate. The substrate is metal organic frame MIL-101. The metal organic framework MIL-101 is uniformly loaded with mutually independent platinum (Pt) atoms. The mass ratio of platinum (Pt) atom and metal organic framework MIL-101 is 0.18-0.25:95-105.
   USE - Platinum1.MIL nanocatalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanocatalyst has simple procedure, low cost, high catalytic activity, high methanol selectivity, catalytic stability, hydrogen gas dissociation path and good stability and is environment friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the Pt1.MIL nanocatalyst comprising dispersing metal organic framework MIL-101 nanoparticles in deionized water to obtain mixture A, adding potassium tetrachloroplatinate solution and sodium borohydride solution to mixture A to obtain mixture B, mixing mixture B at room temperature, washing and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass-to-volume ratio of metal organic framework MIL-101 and deionized water is 95-105:50-70. The concentration of potassium tetrachloroplatinate solution is 0.03-0.05 mg/mL. The concentration of sodium borohydride solution is 0.003-0.005 mg/mL. The volume ratio of potassium tetrachloroplatinate solution to deionized water is 9-11:50-70. The volume ratio of sodium borohydride solution and deionized water is 9-11:50-70.

PN CN108435169-A
TI Preparation of gold-titanium dioxide nanotube catalyst for catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to formic acid, involves reacting aqueous extract of arborvitae leaves, chloroauric acid aqueous solution and titanium dioxide nanotubes.
AU LIANG Z
   HUANG Y
   ZHU Z
   NA Y
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 201868319Q
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of gold-titanium dioxide nanotube catalyst, involves (1) mixing aqueous extract of arborvitae leaves with aqueous solution of chloroauric acid and reacting for 1-10 minutes to obtain gold sol, (2) adding titanium dioxide nanotubes to gold sol obtained in step (1), stirring, filtering, drying and grinding filtered solid to obtain gold-titanium dioxide nanotube precursor, (3) heating gold-titanium dioxide nanotube precursor is at 300-400 degrees C and calcining in oxidizing atmosphere.
   USE - Preparation of gold-titanium dioxide nanotube catalyst used for catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to formic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of gold-titanium dioxide nanotube catalyst ensuring less toxicity, excellent thermal and chemical stability and enhanced catalytic properties by simple, economical and environmentally-friendly process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The preparation of aqueous extract of arborvitae leaves, involves mixing 1-1.2 g arborvitae leaf powder with 100-120 mL water, stirring for 2 hours and filtering the stirred mixture to obtain filtrate. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of aqueous solution of chloroauric acid is controlled at 60-90 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The step (1) involves mixing 50 mL aqueous extract of arborvitae leaves with 30-60 mL of 0.5-5 g/L aqueous solution of chloroauric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation method of titanium dioxide nanotubes, involves adding 8-14 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution to titanium dioxide, heating, adding hydrochloric acid solution, ultrasonically dispersing, suction filtering, washing obtained solid, drying and grinding. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of gold and titanium dioxide is (1-10):100. Preferred Conditions: The step (3) involves calcining for 2-3 hours and using air atmosphere as oxidizing atmosphere. The partial pressure of carbon dioxide in reactor is controlled at 4-6 MPa, and the partial pressure of hydrogen is controlled at 8-12 MPa. The reaction temperature is controlled at 140-160 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN108220847-A
TI Producing flexible shaft, comprises e.g. heating iron-based alloy, sodium thiosulfate and nickel-based alloy to melt, performing homogenization treatment, performing aging heat treatment and introducing carbon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide.
AU LIU J
AE SHENYANG LITAI AUTOMATION TECHNOLOGY CO (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201853324T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing flexible shaft, comprises heating 10-24 pts. wt. iron-based alloy, 8-12 pts. wt. sodium thiosulfate and 30-98 pts. wt. nickel-based alloy to melt at 1000-2111 degrees C, performing homogenization treatment, rapidly cooling to 20 degrees C for 3-9 minutes, performing aging heat treatment at 211-356 degrees C for 12-67 minutes, simultaneously introducing a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide, then forging, continuing aging heat treatment at 205-378 degrees C for 12-52 minutes and cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for producing flexible shaft.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product: which does not break easily, and having long lasting effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises heating 21 pts. wt. iron-based alloy, 9 pts. wt. sodium thiosulfate and 45 pts. wt. nickel-based alloy to melt at 2000 degrees C, performing homogenization treatment, rapidly cooling to 20 degrees C for 5 minutes, performing aging heat treatment at 267 degrees C for 34 minutes, simultaneously introducing a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide, then forging, continuing aging heat treatment at 200 degrees C for 41 minutes and cooling.
DC M29 (Changing the physical structure of nonferrous metals and alloys - including tempering, annealing, work-hardening and recrystallising (C22F).); M26 (Non-ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).); M27 (Ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).)

PN CN108192735-A
TI Extracting plant extract oil useful in edible oil,            comprises e.g. pulverizing a raw material, mixing the            pulverized raw material, water and vegetable oil and            subjecting the mixed solution to supercritical carbon            dioxide extraction.
AU LIU Y
AE LIU Y (LIUY-Individual)
GA 2018514193
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting plant extract oil comprises                pulverizing a raw material, mixing the pulverized                raw material, water and vegetable oil to obtain a                mixed liquid, where the raw material is a spice                plant, subjecting the mixed solution to                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain                an extract and separating the extract from oil and                water by a high speed centrifuge to obtain the                plant extract oil.
   USE - The extracted oil is useful in edible oil                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the quality and production                rate and has high separating rate. The oil is                environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                extracting the plant extract oil by the above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The material is                  honeysuckle, Houttuynia cordate, Chrysanthemum,                  cogongrass rhizome, Gardenia, pagodatree flower,                  Chinese olive, Ageratum, cassia seed, fructus                  cannabis and/or liquorice, preferably, the raw                  material comprises 1-30 pts. wt. honeysuckle,                  1-30 pts. wt. cordate houttuynia, 1-30 pts. wt.                  chrysanthemum and 1-30 pts. wt. cogongrass                  rhizome, preferably, the raw material is a                  mixture comprises 1-15 pts. wt. honeysuckle, 1-20                  pts. wt. Chrysanthemum, 1-25 pts. wt. Chinese                  scholartree, 1-30 pts. wt. white canary tree                  fruit, 1-30 pts. wt. cassia seed, 1-30 pts. wt.                  liquorice and 1-30 pts. wt. fructus momordicae.                  The ratio of the raw material, water and                  vegetable oil is 1:2-10:2-10, preferably 1:5:5.                  The vegetable oil is sesame oil, peanut oil,                  rapeseed oil, soybean oil and/or cottonseed oil,                  the vegetable oil is mixed oil includes sesame                  oil and peanut oil in a mass ratio of 1:0.5-5 or                  the mixed oil comprises sesame oil and soybean                  oil in a mass ratio of 1:0.5-5. Preferred Method:                  In the raw material pulverization step, the raw                  material is pulverized to 20-100 mesh. In the                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction step, the                  extraction temperature is 25-48 degrees C, the                  extraction pressure is 15 to 35 Mpa and the                  extraction time is 1-4 hours; the supercritical                  carbon dioxide is used as a solvent and an                  aqueous solution of 80-90% by volume of an                  aqueous solution is used as an cosolvent for                  supercritical extraction, the mixed liquid is                  preheated prior to the supercritical carbon                  dioxide extraction step and the preheating                  temperature is 30-100 degrees C for 2-5 hours.                  The method comprises stirring the extract,                  allowing to stand, precipitating, filtering,                  collecting the filtrate, placing the filtrate at                  room temperature under vacuum for 10-60 minutes                  and carrying out high speed centrifugation before                  the extract is separated by a high-speed                  centrifuge, where the rotation speed of the high                  speed centrifuge is 2000-4000 revolutions/minute                  and the centrifugation time is 10-30 minutes. The                  filtering of the dregs comprises subjecting the                  collected dregs to water reflux extraction                  treatment to obtain an extract, centrifuging the                  extract through a high speed centrifuge to obtain                  a concentrate, filtering the concentrate and                  concentrating.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A

TI Extraction of oil from Suaeda salsa seed involves washing Suaeda salsa seed and crushing, adding deionized water and composite enzyme and enzymolyzing, extracting using carbon dioxide, adding protease and fermenting and separating.
AU WANG S
AE WEIFANG YOURONG IND CO LTD (WFYR-C)
GA 201837494D
AB    NOVELTY - The extraction process of oil from Suaeda salsa seed involves washing S.salsa seed and then crushing into powder, adding deionized water to the crushed powder and then sterilizing, adding 0.1-0.5 wt.% composite enzyme to the mixture and then enzymolyzing, extracting using carbon dioxide and then crushing, adding protease to the mixture and then enzymolyzing, centrifugally separating the mixture and then inoculating into 2-8 vol.% culture medium, fermenting the mixture and then reacting and centrifugally separating.
   USE - The process is useful for extraction of oil from S.salsa seed.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables extraction of oil with high yield. The oil is rich in linoleic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite enzyme is pectinase, cellulase or ligninase. The protease is papain or bromelin.

PN WO2018065070-A1
TI New aminosiloxanes useful in textile finishing, cosmetic formulation for treating surfaces, producing block copolymers, and binding and removing carbon dioxide from exhaust gases.
AU POPP A
   SCHAFER O
AE WACKER CHEM AG (WACK-C)
GA 201828720L
AB    NOVELTY - Aminosiloxanes (I), are new.
   USE - (I) are useful in textile finishing, cosmetic formulation for treating surfaces, producing block copolymers, and binding and removing carbon dioxide from exhaust gases (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) have low viscosity and high thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Aminosiloxanes of formula (R1-NH-(CH2)n-R22Si-O-(SiR32-O)m-SiR22-(CH2)n-NH-R4) (I), are new.
   R1, R4 = partially saturated aliphatic or aromatic hydrocarbon having 1-20C in which methylene units does not contain heteroatom including N, O, S or P, and remaining groups containing N, O, S or P (optionally substituted);
   R2, R3 = partially saturated aliphatic or aromatic hydrocarbon having 1-20C (optionally substituted);
   n = 1 or 2; and
   m = greater than or equal to 0.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (a) reacting amine compound of formula (H2NR1) (IV) with (halo-organyl)silicon compound of formula (R21-SiR22-(CH2)n-x) (V) to obtain amine based compound of formula (R21-SiR22-(CH2)n-NHR1) (VI), and performing hydrolytic condensation in presence of silane reactive compounds of formula (R212SiR32) (VII) to obtain (I); (b) performing hydrolytic condensation of formula (V) in presence of (VII) to obtain silane compound of formula (R22-SiR22-(CH2)n-x) (VIII), and reacting with (IV) to obtain (I); (c) reacting (VI) with linear siloxane of formula (HO(SiR32O)mH) (II) to obtain (I); or (d) reacting (aminoorganyl)silicon compounds of formula (R1-NH-(CH2)n-R22Si-O-(SiR32-O)q-SiR22-(CH2)n-NH-R4) (IX) with (II) or cyclic siloxane of formula ((SiR32O)p) (III) to obtain (I).R21 = acyloxy and alkoxy having 1-10C;R22 = O-(SiR22-O)m-R22Si-(CH2)n-Y1;X, Y1 = Cl, Br or I;p = greater than or equal to 3;q = m; andM = not defined.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R2, R3 = methyl, ethyl, iso-propyl, n-propyl, iso-butyl, n-butyl, phenyl or vinyl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI New strontium-yttrium-titanium-nickel oxide-based catalyst, useful for dry reforming reaction of biogas containing carbon dioxide.
AU HAM H C
   KWON B W
   KIM G S
   OH J H
   JANG S C
   CHOI S H
   PARK H S
   YOON C W
   YOON S P
   HAN J
   NAM S W
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 201825051X
AB    NOVELTY - Strontium-yttrium-titanium-nickel oxide-based catalyst (I), is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for dry reforming reaction of biogas containing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has excellent resistance to carbon deposition, excellent resistance to poisoning by sulfur contained in biogas, high activity and performance, easily produce gas having high hydrogen ratio with high activity for long time, is very economical and efficient, and can be produced as single phase homogeneous catalyst substantially free of impurities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Strontium-yttrium-titanium-nickel oxide-based catalyst of formula (Sr1-yYyTi1-xNixO3-z) (I), is new.
   x = more than 0 and less than 1, preferably 0-0.5;
   y = more than 0 and less than 1, preferably 0-0.08; and
   z = 0-1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises doping part of strontium with yttrium and part of titanium with nickel in perovskite material i.e. strontium titanate by substitution. Preferred Components: (I) is single phase homogeneous catalyst, is activated by using inert gas or hydrogen, exhibits higher activity when treated with inert gas than the catalyst treated with hydrogen, is used for dry reforming of biogas containing carbon dioxide, and has methane conversion rate of 72% when treated with dry reforming of biogas containing carbon dioxide at 750 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere. The inert gas includes nitrogen, helium and argon. Preferred Method: The preparation is carried out by dissolving compound containing yttrium, strontium, titanium and nickel in solvent including distilled water or deionized water via Pechini method. The method further comprises activating (I) by reacting with inert gas or hydrogen at 750 degrees C, preferably under nitrogen as inert gas.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Phosphine-ionic liquid organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst used in coupling reaction of epoxy compound and carbon dioxide, is obtained by reacting phosphine-ionic liquid organic copolymer, with or without Lewis acid salt.
AU DING Y
   WANG W
   LI C
   YAN L
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201803586G
AB    NOVELTY - A phosphine-ionic liquid organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst is obtained by adding phosphine-ionic liquid organic porous copolymer formed by copolymerization of olefin-functionalized organic phosphine ligand and olefin-functionalized ionic liquid, with or without Lewis acid metal salt, and processing. When Lewis acid metal salts are added, metal ions in metal salt form coordination bonds with phosphine in ionic liquid organic phosphorus-containing copolymer.
   USE - Phosphine-ionic liquid organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst is used in coupling reaction of epoxy compound and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The phosphine-ionic liquid organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst has high activity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of phosphine-ionic liquid organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The Lewis acidic metal salt is halides of zinc, aluminum, silver, gold, copper, calcium, rhodium, iridium, cobalt, magnesium and rare-earth metal. The halide is of formula: MXn. The loading amount of metal element is 0.01-10 wt.%. The terminal alkylene group-containing organophosphine ligand is vinyl-containing bidentate phosphite organophosphine ligand or vinyl-containing triphenylphosphine ligand. The terminal olefin-containing ionic liquid is imidazole ionic liquid.M=metal element;X=Cl, Br or I;andn=1-3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The phosphorus-ionic liquid organic porous copolymer is obtained by solvent thermal polymerization method. The phosphine-ionic liquid organic porous copolymer has multistage pore structure, specific surface area of 100-3000 m2/g, pore volume of 0.1-5 cm3/g and pore size distribution of 0.2-50 nm.

Preferred Components: The zinc ion is in the form of soluble zinc salt comprising zinc nitrate hexahydrate, zinc nitrate tetrahydrate, zinc chloride, or zinc acetate dehydrate. The low carbon alcohol is methanol, ethanol, propanol or butanol, and the volume in the mixed solvent is 20-60%.

PN CN113582214-A; CN113582214-B; CN216303899-U
TI Green low-carbon closed-loop metallurgy and carbon dioxide capture and utilization method involves sending steel slag and carbon dioxide regenerated by purification and regeneration device to steel slag resource utilization device for resource utilization.
AU WU W
   LIU Q
   ZHANG X
   ZHU H
   ZHOU X
   WANG F
   ZHANG Y
AE GREENORE CLEANTECH SHANGHAI CO LTD (GREE-Non-standard)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   SHANGHAI YUANHAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D4471U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using a synthesis gas production device (1) to produce crude synthesis gas rich in hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The synthesis gas conversion device treats crude synthesis gas in raw synthesis gas. The absorbent in a syngas purification device (4) absorbs carbon dioxide and moisture in the crude syngas, so that content of hydrogen (H2) + carbon monoxide (CO) in purified syngas reaches 90%v/v or more. The purified synthesis gas is sent to an ironmaking furnace (5), where it undergoes a reduction reaction with iron ore to produce sponge iron. The purified tail gas cycle is sent to the ironmaking furnace together with the purified synthesis gas. The regenerated absorbent is sent to the synthesis gas purification device and a tail gas purification device (8) for recycling. The steel slag and carbon dioxide regenerated by the purification and regeneration device, are sent to a steel slag resource utilization device (10) for resource utilization.
   USE - Green low-carbon closed-loop metallurgy and carbon dioxide capture and utilization method.
   ADVANTAGE - The green low carbon closed-loop metallurgy and carbon dioxide trapping and utilization process provides high concentration carbon dioxide product with low cost. The carbon dioxide with high concentration can be used as the sealing gas of the iron-making system, and there is no need to increase the inert gas. The iron-rich tailings after steel slag resource treatment is used as iron making raw material, which reduces the iron making cost, reduces the problem that coking coal resource is scarce gradually, improves the clean steel quality and steel structure, optimizes the steel structure has great significance, and reduces the use amount of iron making coke increasingly scarcity, with green, environmentally-friendly, energy saving important significance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a green low-carbon closed-loop metallurgy and carbon dioxide capture and utilization device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Syngas purification device (4)

PN CN107930181-A; CN107930181-B
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction includes selecting whole farmed or wild ant body, washing, drying, cooling, performing cell wall breaking treatment, reducing pressure in carbon dioxide extractor, standing, extracting and separating.
AU LU Z
   HUANG Y
AE GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201832904Q
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises selecting whole farmed or wild ant body, washing ant body with clean water, placing treated ant body in drying device, drying with hot air at 90-100 degrees C for 3-4 hours, cooling with air at room temperature, placing ant body in carbon dioxide extractor, performing cell wall breaking treatment under 10-12 MPa at 25-30 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, reducing pressure in carbon dioxide extractor to 5 MPa by pressure reducer, standing for 20-30 minutes, performing cell wall breaking treatment, extracting and separating by separator.
   USE - Method of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the yield of effective component.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises selecting whole farmed or wild ant body, washing ant body with clean water, placing treated ant body in drying device, drying with hot air at 90-100 degrees C for 3-4 hours, cooling with air at room temperature, placing ant body in carbon dioxide extractor, performing cell wall breaking treatment under 10-12 MPa at 25-30 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, reducing pressure in carbon dioxide extractor to 5 MPa by pressure reducer, standing for 20-30 minutes, performing cell wall breaking treatment under 35-40 MPa at 45-55 degrees C for 60-80 minutes, extracting under 38-40 MPa at 50-55 degrees C for 30-40 minutes for first stage, 1-2 hours for second stage, for 1-2 hours for third stage and for 3-4 hours for fourth stage and separating by separator under 20-25 MPa at 50-55 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, under 10-15 MPa at 50-55 degrees C for 5-10 minutes and under 5-8 MPa at 50-55 degrees C for 5-10 minutes. The first transition is between the first phase and second phase. The second transition is between the second stage and third stage. The third transition is between the third stage and fourth stage. The duration of first transition is 5 minutes. The duration of second transition is 5 minutes. The duration of third transition is 10 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The ant is red forest ant, red ant pest or brown forest ant.

GA 2022A4078G
AB    NOVELTY - A ship carbon emission reduction system                comprises an engine (1) which provides power to                ship, a pipeline (2) for processing tail gas                produced by engine, carbon dioxide separator (3)                fixed on the tail gas processing pipeline. The                system also contains a hydrogen storage container                (6) and a catalyst storage container (7) for                storing catalytic carbon dioxide and hydrogen for                producing fuel, a carbon oil reaction device (5)                comprising a reaction chamber, connected to a                carbon dioxide separator (3) provided for                separating carbon dioxide in the reaction chamber.                The carbon oil reaction device and hydrogen storage                container are connected to the reaction chamber.                The carbon oil reaction device is further connected                to the catalyst storage container, to enable the                catalyst to enter the reaction cavity, so that                hydrogen reacts with carbon dioxide and generates                fuel in the reaction cavity. The reaction chamber                has a fuel outlet which discharges the generated                fuel.
   USE - Carbon emission reduction system is used in                fuel gas transport ship namely liquefied natural                gas (LNG) transport ship or liquefied petroleum gas                (LPG) transport ship (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon emission reduction system uses the                fuel oil for storage and reuse, without the need                for storing and reusing the recovered carbon                dioxide, to enable reduction in cost of carbon                recovery.
The carbon-to-oil reaction device present in                the marine carbon emission system, enables at least                part of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas                discharged from the engine, to react with hydrogen,                to form fuel oil so that carbon emissions are                reduced and the generated fuel oil is reused.

The gas making system comprises a fuel reactor (101), a steam reactor (102), a regeneration reactor (103). The fuel reactor, steam reactor and the regeneration reactor are connected end to end to form a closed cycle loop. The carbon dioxide separation system is connected with the fuel reactor for separating carbon dioxide and water. The hydrogen purifying system comprises a first cooler, a first gas-liquid separating tank, a drier and a purifier. The steam reactor is connected with first gas-liquid separating tank. The drier and the purifier are connected in turn. The first cooler is set on the pipeline connected with the steam reactor and the first gas-liquid separating tank.
   USE - Used as hydrogen production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can realize hydrogen production and hydrogenation in the hydrogenation station.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing hydrogen comprising introducing the gas containing alkane, synthesis gas or naphtha into the combustion reactor, gas containing alkane, synthesis gas or naphtha reacts with the metal oxide of high valence state in the combustion reactor, generating metal oxide with low valence state, carbon dioxide and water, separating the carbon dioxide and water with a carbon dioxide separation system, sending the lowvalence metal oxide into the steam reactor, reacting with water steam and generating the product containing hydrogen and metal oxide in middle valence state, sending the product containing hydrogen into the hydrogen purifying system, orderly cooling, gas-liquid separation, drying and purifying and removing impurity to obtain hydrogen product, sending the metal oxide of the intermediate valence state into the regeneration reactor, reacting with oxygen, generating the metal oxide with high valence state and returning to the combustion reactor.
