PN JP2018150315-A; JP6538921-B2
TI Manufacturing method of ethylene oxide, involves raising raw material gas containing ethylene by using centrifugal compressor having dry gas seal as shaft seal device, and supplying pressurized raw material gas to ethylene oxidation reactor.
AU WATANABE A
   SUZUKI H
   KAWAGUCHI Y
AE NIPPON SHOKUBAI CO LTD (JAPC-C)
GA 2018751668
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying a portion of the carbon dioxide gas-containing gas discharged from the top portion of the ethylene oxide absorption tower to the carbon dioxide gas absorption tower. A carbonic acid gas rich absorbent obtained by bringing the carbon dioxide gas-containing gas into contact with an absorption liquid is withdrawn as a bottom liquid of the carbon dioxide absorption tower and supplied to the upper portion of the carbon dioxide gas dissipation tower. The carbon dioxide gas is discharged from the carbonic acid gas rich absorbent liquid and discharging it as exhaust gas from the top of the carbon dioxide gas dissipation tower. The raw material gas containing ethylene is raised by using a centrifugal compressor having a dry gas seal as a shaft seal device. The pressurized raw material gas is supplied to the ethylene oxidation reactor.
   USE - Manufacturing method of ethylene oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is capable of further reducing the argon purge amount in the ethylene oxide production process. The contamination of nitrogen from the shaft sealing device of the centrifugal compressor as the pressure raising blower into the process is suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A second nitrogen supply unit comprises a liquid nitrogen tank and a vaporizer for vaporizing the liquid nitrogen stored in the liquid nitrogen tank. The nitrogen is supplied from the first nitrogen supply facility to the seal portion of the centrifugal compressor during steady operation of the ethylene oxide production process. The nitrogen is supplied to the seal portion of the centrifugal compressor using the second nitrogen supply facility, when the supply of nitrogen from the first nitrogen supply facility to the seal portion of the centrifugal compressor is stopped.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram showing a configuration example of a process for implementing a method for producing ethylene oxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Ethylene oxidation reactor (1)
   Ethylene oxide absorption tower (2)
   Pressure rise blower (4)
   Carbon-dioxide-gas absorption tower (7)
   Carbon-dioxide-gas diffusion tower (8)

PN WO2022201366-A1
TI New cellulose derivative in which some or all of            hydroxyl groups of cellulose are esterified with            carboxylic acid derived from biomass resources used for            production of molded articles.
AU MATSUO Y
   TANIGUCHI H
   HIGUCHI A
AE DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
GA 2022C4371F
AB    NOVELTY - A cellulose derivative in which some or all of                the hydroxyl groups of cellulose are esterified                with carboxylic acid derived from biomass                resources, is new.
   USE - The cellulose derivative is used for the                production of molded articles for various                fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The cellulose derivative can be applied to                various fields by changing the type of substituent,                the degree of substitution, and the molecular                weight depending on the application. The cellulose                derivative does not increase the amount of carbon                dioxide generated as a whole even if the cellulose                derivative is incinerated after being used as a                product, thus it can contribute to the achievement                of zero emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carboxylic acid is                  a fatty acid having 1-4C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation: No preparation method is given.                  Preferred Composition: The cellulose derivative                  has an iron content of 0.7 ppm or more. The total                  degree of substitution with the carboxylic acid                  is 1.8 or more. Preferred Properties: The                  cellulose derivative has a biomass degree of 100%                  calculated according to .
   EXAMPLE - Ion-exchanged water (500 ml) was added to 10 g                wood pulp and soaked for 1 hour, followed by                suction filtration to obtain a filter cake. After                adding 500 ml dimethylformamide to the filter cake                and stirring, the procedure of collecting the                filter cake by suction filtration was repeated a                total of three times. Then, the entire amount of                the obtained filter cake was placed into a                separable flask equipped with a three-one motor, a                reflux condenser, a thermometer and a dropping                funnel, and 300 ml dimethylformamide and 71 g                p-toluenesulfonyl chloride was added. While                stirring at room temperature, 22 g acetic acid was                dripped. Subsequently, the mixture was stirred at                70&#176; C for 4 hours, and then allowed to                cool to room temperature. By adding the obtained                reaction solution to 3 l methanol, the reaction                product was precipitated, and a white solid was                separated by suction filtration. After washing the                resulting white solid three times with                ion-exchanged water, it was dried by heating at                80&#176; C for 12 hours to obtain a 11 g                cellulose derivative.

PN CN111905794-A
TI New high-density single-atom electrocatalyst complex prepared by e.g. dissolving sucrose in water, then adding iron metal salts, and mixing, adding melamine and pore former, and mixing ultrasonically, reacting, cooling, and grinding.
AU YANG H
   WANG S
   LI X
   ZHANG Y
   YAN C
   YI X
AE UNIV XIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYXT-C)
GA 2020B39230
AB    NOVELTY - High-density single-atom electrocatalyst complex (I) is new.
   USE - Used as high-density single-atom electrocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has excellent oxygen reduction reaction performance and excellent carbon dioxide electro-reduction reaction (CO2RR) activity; regulates precursor and carbonization temperature; adopts coordination control strategy to achieve anchoring of metal atoms and form steric hindrance; and prevents migration and agglomeration at high temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-density single-atom electrocatalyst complex of formula (Fe-N4) (I) is new. The metal content of the electrocatalyst (I) is 5-12 wt.%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises (i) dissolving sucrose in water, then adding iron metal salts of different molar ratios, and mixing uniformly, adding melamine and pore former, and mixing ultrasonically to obtain mixed suspension, (ii) reacting the step (i) mixed suspension at 100-160 degrees C, and maintaining temperature for 4-6 hours, then cooling to room temperature, taking out and grinding into powder, (iii) reacting the step (ii) powder at 280-400 degrees C, and maintaining temperature for 1-3 hours, then reacting 750-950 degrees C, and maintaining temperature for 1-3 hours under nitrogen atmosphere, and cooling to room temperature, and (iv) immersing the step (iii) powder immersed in 6M sodium hydroxide solution to remove the pore former, washing with water, centrifuging, and drying to obtain final product. Preferred Components: In step (i): iron metal salt is iron(III) chloride, Iron(III) nitrate, ferric thiocyanate, iron(II) chloride, iron acetylacetonate or ferrocene; molar ratio between sucrose and iron metal salt is 1:1, 2:1, 3:1, 4:1, 5:1 or 6:1; and mass ratio between sucrose and melamine is 1:1; and pore former is Santa Barbara Amorphous-15 (SBA-15), and mass ratio between pore former and sucrose is 1:4.

TI Producing absorbent for preserving freshness of agricultural products comprises preparing activated carbon by heating palm tree and applying silica to uneven part of charcoal, precipitating in platinum catalyst solution, drying, and packing.
AU PYO H Y
   PARK M
   CHANG M S
AE RURAL DEV ADMINISTRATION (RURA-C)
GA 202055857U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing absorbent comprises (a) preparing activated carbon, (b) precipitating the activated carbon in platinum catalyst solution, (c) drying the activated carbon, and (d) packing the activated carbon.
   USE - The method is useful in absorbent for preserving freshness of agricultural products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides absorbent having excellent adsorbing power of ethylene and carbon dioxide by coating platinum catalyst solution on activated carbon, can easily adjust amount and size of the adsorbent according to respiratory rate and ethylene production of agricultural products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for adsorbent produced by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises heating palm tree to produce charcoal, and applying silica to the uneven part of the charcoal. The step (a) further comprises pulverizing the activated carbon. The step (d) is carried out by charging the activated carbon in a non-woven fabric or absorbent paper.
   EXAMPLE - Activated carbon and platinum were mixed in weight ratio of 90:1 to obtain absorbent.

PN CN111153801-A; CN111153801-B
TI Preparation of aromatic carboxylic acid ester compound involves reacting aromatic hydrocarbon compound with carbonate compound in presence of catalyst comprising one or more of acylation reaction catalysts.
AU LI C
   LIU H
   LI Z
   WANG J
   LU S
   WANG M
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202044231H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aromatic carboxylic acid ester compound involves reacting aromatic hydrocarbon compound with carbonate compound in presence of catalyst. The catalyst is chosen from one or more of acylation reaction catalysts.
   USE - Preparation of aromatic carboxylic acid ester compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses catalyst having high catalytic efficiency which improves product yield. The reaction has Delta G value of less than -20 kJ/mol which can realize aromatic carboxylation under high thermodynamic equilibrium conversion rate and improve the reaction equilibrium yield. The aromatic carboxylic acid ester products are easy to separate and purify, which can realize efficient indirect resource utilization of carbon dioxide to prepare high value-added chemicals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The metal oxide catalyst is prepared by using precipitation method, precipitating the solution containing the metal salt and/or mixed metal salt and precipitating agent in the aqueous solution, drying and roasting. Preferred Conditions: The roasting is static roasting and/or flowing atmosphere roasting. The roasting atmosphere is chosen from one or more of air, oxygen and nitrogen. The roasting temperature is room temperature to 1000 degrees C. Preferred Components: The catalyst is homogeneous catalyst and/or heterogeneous catalyst. The homogeneous catalyst is organic acid and/or Lewis acid. The heterogeneous catalyst is metal oxide catalyst. The metal oxide catalyst is single metal oxide and/or its composite metal oxide. The metal element in the metal oxide catalyst is chosen from one or more of group IIA, group IIIA, group IVA, group VA, group VB, group VIB, group VIIB, and group VIIIB. The composite metal oxide is chosen from two or more of the single metal oxides. The metal element in the metal oxide catalyst is chosen from one or more of magnesium, aluminum, lanthanum, zinc, cerium, titanium, zirconium, indium, gallium, yttrium, tin, antimony, tungsten, molybdenum, chromium, manganese, iron, and niobium. The single metal oxide is chosen from one of magnesium oxide, aluminum oxide, lanthanum oxide, zinc oxide, cerium oxide, titanium dioxide, zirconium dioxide, indium(III) oxide, gallium(III) oxide, yttrium oxide, tin(IV) oxide, antimony pentoxide, tungsten trioxide, molybdenum trioxide, chromium trioxide, manganese dioxide, ferric oxide, and niobium pentoxide. The metal salt is chosen from one or more of metal element nitrate, acetate, halide, sulfate, oxalate, ammonium salt, and organic salt. When the metal element is titanium, the salt is chosen from one or more of titanyl sulfate, titanium isopropoxide, and butyl titanate. When the metal element is zirconium, the salt is chosen from one or more of zirconium oxychloride, zirconyl nitrate, and zirconium isopropoxide. The precipitating agent is chosen from one or more of aqueous ammonia, ammonium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, and potassium hydroxide. Preferred Composition: The ratio of each metal element of the composite metal oxide is 1:10-10:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction is carried out at temperature of 0-600 degrees C, preferably 100-500 degrees C and initial pressure of 0-20 MPa, preferably 1-5 MPa. The reaction atmosphere is chosen from one or more of nitrogen, carbon dioxide, helium, argon, and hydrogen, preferably nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and argon. The aromatic carboxylic acid ester compound is prepared by carboxylation coupling reaction which is carried out in an autoclave batch reactor. Preferred Composition: The molar concentration ratio of the reactants in the reaction solution of n(carbonate compounds) and n(aromatic hydrocarbon compounds) is 1:9-9:1, preferably 1:9-1:1. Preferred Components: The aromatic hydrocarbon compound is of formula (Ia) or (Ib), preferably is chosen from one or more of benzene, toluene, ethylbenzene, and naphthalene. The carbonate compound is of formula (IIa) or (IIb), preferably is chosen from one or more of dimethyl carbonate, propylene carbonate, styrene carbonate, and 2-oxazolidinone.R11, R12, R211, R212 = H or and 1-5C alkyl;n = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 or 5;m = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 or 7;R221, R222 = H, alkyl and aryl;andX, Y' = C, O or N.

PN EP3640224-A1
TI Cement free binder composition useful as e.g. adhesive and reinforcing plasters, finishing plasters, mortar, grout, natural stone adhesive, and concrete repair compounds, comprises latent hydraulic binder, hydrated lime and water repellent.
AU HOLZER M
   MUELLER M
AE STO SE & CO KGAA (STOS-Non-standard)
GA 2020315139
AB    NOVELTY - Cement free binder composition comprises (a) at least one latent hydraulic binder, (b) hydrated lime and (c) at least one water repellent.
   USE - The composition is useful as adhesive and reinforcing plasters, finishing plasters, mortar, grout, tile adhesive, natural stone adhesive, screed, and concrete repair compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is free from water-soluble sulfate phases, preferably gypsum and hemihydrates, reduces efflorescence in plasters, mortars, tile and natural stone adhesives, enables production of plasters, mortars and tile or natural stone adhesives with a favorable carbon dioxide balance, avoids undesirable efflorescence on the surface.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The binder composition further comprises at least one accelerator, and additives and/or fillers. Preferred Components: The accelerator is calcium formate. The latent hydraulic binder is slag sand flour, trass flour, brick dust, fly ash, microsilica, silicate dust, oil shale or metakaolin, preferably slag sand flour. The weight ratio of latent hydraulic binder, preferably blastfurnace slag, to hydrated lime is not greater than 50:1, preferably 15:1. The water repellent is calcium laurate. The additives are at least one of dispersants, flow aids, retarders, biocides e.g. algicides, fungicides, wetting agents, pore formers, defoamers, dedusting agents, water retention agents, fibers, fillers, dispersion powders, flame retardants and thixotropic agents. The fillers are at least one of organic and/or inorganic fillers, preferably flours and/or grains and/or light fillers. The fillers are carbonate and/or silicate rock or mineral grains and/or rock or mineral flours, optionally in a mixture with light fillers. The light fillers are mineral or organic light fillers e.g. expanded perlites, pumice, expanded clay, expanded slate, expanded glass granulate, light filler made from tobermorite, hollow glass spheres and/or organic light fillers e.g. beads and/or granules made of expanded polystyrene or beads and/or granules made of bioplastics. The binder composition comprises 20-40 wt.%, preferably 25-35 wt.% latent hydraulic binder, preferably blastfurnace slag, 0.1-10 wt.%, preferably 1-5 wt.% hydrated lime, 0.2-2 wt.%, preferably 0.3-1 wt.% accelerator, preferably calcium formate,m0.05-1 wt.%, preferably 0.1-0.8 wt.% hydrophobicizing agent, preferably calcium laurate, 0.2-8 wt.%, preferably 0.3-5 wt.% additives and 20-80 wt.%, preferably 30-70 wt.% fillers.
   EXAMPLE - Typical cement free binder composition comprised of: 30 wt.% cottage sand flour; 2 wt.% lime hydrate; 0.3 wt.% calcium formate; 0.5 wt.% calcium laurate; 5 wt.% other additives, preferably water retention agents and dispersion powder; 2 wt.% light fillers; 47 wt.% aggregate; and 13.2 wt.% rock powder.

PN CN110824101-A; CN110824101-B
TI Carbon fiber surface functional group measuring method involves washing to-be-tested carbon fiber sample, adding alkaline solution, titrating to-be-tested carbon fiber sample with blank sample and calculating titrating result.
AU WANG F
   ZHAO H
   ZHANG C
AE LANZHOU LANXING FIBER CO LTD (LANZ-Non-standard)
GA 2020180176
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining weight of a to-be-tested carbon fiber sample. The to-be-tested carbon fiber sampleis added with excessive acid. The to-be-tested carbon fiber sample is washed. Alkaline solution is added and replaced by non-Carbon dioxide(CO2) water. The to-be-tested carbon fiber sample is titrated with a blank sample. Titrating result is calculated, where weight of the to-be-tested carbon fiber sample is 5 to 15 grams.
   USE - Carbon fiber surface functional group measuring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing measuring time and cost, improving operation efficiency and satisfying factory production requirements.

PN CN110639047-A
TI Nano phytoncide composition, comprises nano phytoncide, plant essential oil and water in specific weight parts.
AU WANG C
AE SHENZHEN FDJ NANO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202004664U
AB    NOVELTY - A nano phytoncide composition comprises 40-55 pts. wt. nano phytoncide, 15-25 pts. wt. plant essential oil and 60-80 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Nano phytoncide composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano phytoncide composition has better deodorizing effect, strong practicability, stability and can be prepared in convenient and secure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing nano phytoncide composition, which involves crushing branches and leaves of Cinnamomum camphora, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the pulverized Cinnamomum camphora tree leaves in the presence of a carrier, collecting an extract, and concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain phytoncide, dissolving the linalum oil and the phytoncide extract in diethyl ether, and dissolving a surfactant in deionized water while sonicating to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The plant essential oil comprises thyme essential oil, clove essential oil, tea tree essential oil and Osmanthus essential oil.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier is absolute ethanol, water, isopropanol, methanol, acetone and ethyl acetate.

PT P
PN CN110240541-A
TI Extraction of curcumin involves screening fresh turmeric root to remove decaying and mildewed raw materials, washing, slicing, drying, pulverizing, sieving, performing vacuum coupled ultrasonic assisted extraction and purifying.
AU WANG Z
   WANG W
   CHENG C
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201981825P
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of curcumin comprises screening fresh turmeric root to remove decaying and mildewed raw materials, washing, slicing, drying, pulverizing, sieving, performing vacuum coupled ultrasonic assisted extraction, extracting liquid and filter residue after suction filtration, drying filter residue, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of filter residue, placing extracts in vacuum rotary evaporator for concentration to obtain curcumin extract concentrate, separating curcumin and purifying.
   USE - The method is used for extracting curcumin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate; has improved extraction efficiency and yield and no toxic and harmful substances; improves purity of curcumin; and has simple production process and reduced production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The cleaning, slicing, drying, pulverizing and sieving methods are carried out by cleaning until no impurities remain, slicing to thickness of 0.2-0.4 cm, oven-drying at 50-60 degrees C for 6-8 hours to water content of 2-3% and pulverizing and sieving into 20-80 mesh. The vacuum coupled ultrasonic assisted extraction is carried out at 50-70 degrees C for 30-60 minutes with ultrasonic power of 350-500 W using 70-90% ethanol where solid-liquid ratio is 1:32-1:64 and vacuum pressure is 0.08-0.1 MPa. The suction filtration is done at vacuum pressure of 0.05-0.09 MPa using filter cloth size of 200 mesh. The drying step is done at 50-60 degrees C. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of filter residue is carried out by using 80-100% ethanol as entrainer where temperature of extraction kettle is 40-60 degrees C, temperature of separation kettle 1 is 40-50 degrees C, temperature of separation kettle 2 is 40-55 degrees C, extraction time is 30 minutes-2 hours, extraction pressure is 30-40 MPa, carbon dioxide flowrate is 20-30 L/hour and the amount of entrainer is 0.8-1.2 mL/g. The vacuum rotary evaporation is done at 45-60 degrees C for 2-3 hours with vacuum of 0.08-0.1 MPa and speed of 50-70 revolutions/minute. The separation and purification steps are carried out by purifying curcumin concentrate using ADS-17 macroporous resin and dextran gel LH-20.

The virus is selected from influenza virus, herpes virus, enterovirus, and pseudorabies virus. The influenza virus is selected from H1N1, H3N2, H5N1, H7N1, H7N2, H7N3, H7N7, H7N9, H9N2, and H10N8; the herpesvirus is selected from herpes simplex virus, and varicella-zoster virus; and the enterovirus is selected from poliovirus, coxsackie virus, and echovirus. The enterovirus is selected enterovirus 71 and the coxsackie virus is coxsackievirus type 16. The condition caused by the enterovirus is hand, foot and mouth disease.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is selected from water, absolute ethanol, and aqueous ethanol. Preferred Compositions: The solvent is 60-80% aqueous ethanol solution. It is used in an amount of 2-30 times the amount of the original medicinal material. The entraining agent is 0-100 vol.% aqueous ethanol solution, methanol, ethyl acetate, or acetone. The entrainer is 75-95 vol.% aqueous ethanol solution. The amount of the entrainer is 0.10-2.5 L/kg per kg of the medicine. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the solvent extraction step is a temperature from room temperature to reflux of the solvent. The filtrate is concentrated at 55-90 degrees C. The condition when the extract is subjected to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is selected from: (1) extraction temperature is 30-70 degrees C; (2) the extraction pressure is 10-40 MPa; (3) the separation temperature is 25-70 degrees C; and (4) the separation pressure is 2-17 MPa. Conditions for the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction further comprise: (1) the flow rate of the extracting agent carbon dioxide is 20-150 liters per kilogram of medicinal material per hour; and (2) the extraction time is 30-400 minutes. The condition at the time of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction further comprises the use of an entrainer. The temperature of the separation kettle I is 30-70 degrees C, and the pressure of the separation kettle I is 4-17 MPa; and the temperature of the separation vessel II is 25-50 degrees C, and the pressure of the separation kettle II was 2-8 MPa. The conditions for the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction further comprise: (1) supercritical extraction of the original medicinal material without entraining agent, the original medicinal material is added to the extraction kettle, (2) the temperature of the extraction kettle is 40-50 degrees C, and the pressure is 15-40 MPa, (3) the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas through the extraction kettle is 23-125 L/hour.kg crude drug, and the extraction time is 90-300 minutes, (4) the temperature of the separation kettle I is 50-60 degrees C, and the pressure is 7.5-9 MPa, (5) the temperature of the separation kettle II is 35-45 degrees C, and the pressure is 4.8-6 MPa, and (6) the obtained extract in the separation vessel is the extract above. Conditions for the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction further comprise: (1) supercritical extraction of the original medicinal material and entrainer, and the original medicinal material is added to the extraction kettle; (2) the temperature of the extraction kettle is 40-50 degrees C, the pressure is 15-40 MPa, and the entrainer is added; (3) the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas through the extraction kettle is 23-125 L/hour.kg crude drug, and the extraction time is 90-300 minutes, (4) the temperature of the separation kettle I is 50-60 degrees C, and the pressure is 7.5-9 MPa, (5) the temperature of the separation kettle II is 35-45 degrees C, and the pressure is 4.8-6 MPa; and (6) the obtained extract in the separation vessel is the extract above. Conditions for the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction further comprise: (1) extracting the original medicinal material without adding an entrainer and then adding the entrainer; (2) the original medicinal material is added to the extraction kettle, the temperature of the extraction kettle is 40-50 degrees C, and the pressure is 15-40 MPa; (3) the temperature of the separation kettle I is 50-60 degrees C, the pressure is 7.5-9 MPa; (4) the temperature of the separation kettle II is 35-45 degrees C, the pressure is 4.8-6 MPa; (5) The flow rate of carbon dioxide gas through the extraction kettle is 23-125 L/hour.kg crude drug, and the extraction time is 90-300 minutes; (6) releasing the extract from the separation vessel; (7) adding an entrainer, the carbon dioxide gas is passed through the extraction vessel at a flow rate of 23-125 L/hour.kg of crude drug, and then extracted for 90-300 minutes; and (8) the extract obtained again in the separation vessel is the extract above. Preferred Parameters: The number of extractions of the solvent extraction step is 1-5 times. The extraction time of the solvent extraction step is 0.5-5 hours each time. The filtrate has a relative density of 1.1-1.5 after concentration.
   ACTIVITY - Antimicrobial; Antibacterial; Virucide.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A10; B04-A98; B11-B03C; B14-A01; B14-A02; B14-A04; J01-F

   SUN J

AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 201884251W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alkali recovered white mud refined paper calcium carbonate filler comprises (i) taking alkali, recovering the white mud and sieving; (ii) grinding the sieved white mud; (iii) adding organic matter into the ground white mud, heating at 60-100 degrees C, pressing carbon dioxide to pressurize, controlling the white mud system, carrying out carbonization reaction at 0.1-0.6 MPa and adjusting the pH value of the white mud system to 8-10.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing alkali recovered white mud refined paper calcium carbonate filler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is low-cost and improves the filling performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the alkali recovery white mud temperature is 70-90 degrees C, the mass concentration is 20-25%, and the sieve mesh is 100-400 mesh. In the step (iii), the carbonization reaction is stirred, and the stirring speed is 100-700 revolutions/minute and the carbonization reaction time is 0.2-2 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the particle size of the white mud is 2-10 mu m. In the step (iii), the mass concentration of the white mud after grinding is first adjusted to 10-40%, and then the organic matter is added, and the organic matter is added in a mass of 1-5 of the dry mass of the white mud, the organic matter is a mixture of polyvinyl alcohol, sodium polyacrylate and/or polyethylene oxide.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of blend, which involves pre-drying polyethylene terephthalate and polyglycolic acid for 2-3 hours at 90-100 degrees C, vacuum-drying at 100-120 degrees C for 2-6 hours, adding compatilizer, antioxidant and anti-hydrolytic agent, extruding and granulating at 240-250 degrees C, and drying.

Preferred Components: The polyglycolic acid is coal-based and has an average molecular weight of 50000-120000. PT P
PN KR1823952-B1
TI Producing lithium carbonate, comprises e.g.            leaching waste cathode active material powder into            acid, filtering, hydrolyzing filtrate using sodium            carbonate, separating aqueous solution and carbonate            cake, and adding alkali compound.
AU JI Y P
   SANG Y L
   SEONG G L
   YOUNG S L
   YOUNG S P
AE TOWN MINING RESOURCE CO LTD (TOWN-Non-standard)
GA 2018113138
AB    NOVELTY - Producing lithium carbonate, comprises (a)                leaching waste cathode active material powder into                acid, and filtering, (b) hydrolyzing the filtrate                obtained in the step (a) using sodium carbonate,                and separating aqueous solution and carbonate cake                to obtain lithium hydroxide solution, (c) adding                alkali compound into the lithium aqueous solution                to remove impurities at 25-50&#176; C and pH of                7-13, and (d) adding sodium carbonate or carbon                dioxide gas into the lithium aqueous solution to                synthesize lithium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing lithium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and high-efficiency                process, and produces high purity lithium                carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried                  out by adding hydrogen peroxide as reducing agent                  into a reaction vessel in presence of                  hydrochloric acid, and leaching at                  75-85&#176; C for 180-300 minutes. The step                  (a) comprises adjusting pH of the obtained                  product to 0.8-1.8 using hydrochloric acid and                  hydrogen peroxide, where the concentration of                  hydrogen peroxide is adjusted to 10-35 wt.% and                  0.1-5 LPM (liter per minute). The step (b) is                  carried out by adding aqueous sodium carbonate                  solution having sodium carbonate content of 10-25                  wt.% based on total weight of the aqueous sodium                  carbonate solution into the reaction vessel. The                  temperature of the reactor is 45-75&#176; C,                  pH is 6.5-8.5, and stirring speed is 250-350                  revolutions per minute. The step (c) is carried                  out by adding 5-30 wt.% alkaline compound aqueous                  solution into the lithium aqueous solution to                  remove impurities. The method further comprises                  concentrating 30-70 vol.% impurity-removed from                  lithium aqueous solution between the step (c) and                  the step (d). The step (d) is carried out by                  adding 10-25 wt.% aqueous sodium carbonate                  solution into the reaction vessel while                  maintaining the temperature of the reaction                  vessel at 40-80&#176; C and stirring for                  30-180 minutes. Preferred Components: The alkali                  compound and the sodium carbonate are used in                  form of powder or aqueous solution.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC E11-N; E33-G; L03-A02B; L03-E01B3; L03-E01B5B; L03-E08B; L03-H05; X16-B01; X16-E01C; X16-M

PN JP2020019751-A
TI Manufacture of methane from sulfur oxide-containing carbon dioxide generated from e.g. steelworks, involves mixing carbon dioxide containing sulfur oxides, and hydrogen, and reducing mixed gas using hydrogen reduction catalyst.
AU ABE T
   TAZUKE T
   SHINDO M
AE UNIV TOYAMA (UTOY-C)
   TOHOKU ELECTRIC POWER CO (TOEL-C)
GA 202010937K
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of methane involves generating mixed gas by mixing carbon dioxide containing 20 ppm or less sulfur oxides and hydrogen as a reducing agent, and reducing the mixed gas using a catalyst for hydrogen reduction in which nanoparticles are dispersed and supported on the surface of a powdery carrier.
   USE - Manufacture of methane from sulfur oxide-containing carbon dioxide generated from coal-fired power generation, oil-fired power generation, steelworks, and chemical plants (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of methane having excellent yield even at low temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves measuring whether the sulfur oxide concentration is 20 ppm or less or not prior to mixing. If the concentration is 20 ppm or more, then the carbon dioxide is desulfurized to reduce the sulfur oxide concentration to 200 ppm or less. The method further involves regenerating the catalyst by spraying argon gas or a mixed gas of argon gas and hydrogen gas on the hydrogen reduction catalyst to remove sulfur oxides covering the surface of the hydrogen reduction catalyst. Preferred Components: The powdery carrier comprises material comprising at least one oxide chosen from alumina, zirconia, magnesia, titania, zeolite, silica, niobia, silica-alumina, and calcium phosphate. The nanoparticles comprises material particles including alloy particles composed of at least two metals chosen from iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, silver, iridium, platinum, and gold. The hydrogen reduction catalyst comprises ruthenium nanoparticles supported powdery titania carrier, or nickel nanoparticles supported powdery zirconia carrier. Preferred Properties: The powdery carrier has a diameter of 0.1-30 mu m. About 90% or more of the nanoparticles are having a particle size of 10 nm or less. Preferred Condition: The low-temperature reduction is carried out at a temperature of 300 degrees C or less.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Preparation of carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst involves mixing palladium chloride with synthesized tetramethyl guanidine lactate, processing mixture in oil bath to obtain catalyst precursor and preparing catalyst.
AU NING W
   LIANG M
   MIAO S
   ZHANG P
   HUO M
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
   UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 2018210217
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst involves synthesizing tetramethyl guanidine lactate, mixing palladium chloride with the tetramethyl guanidine lactate, processing the mixture in an oil bath to obtain a catalyst precursor and preparing pyramid-shaped palladium nanoparticle catalyst using the precursor.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared catalyst has improved specific surface area and electroconductivity, increased catalytic activity with respect to electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, inhibits hydrogen evolution reaction, and enhances the selectivity of the product formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of the catalyst involves dissolving 60 mg ascorbic acid and 105 mg polyvinylpyrrolidone in 3 ml tetramethyl guanidine lactate, stirring, adding mixed solution to the catalyst precursor solution in drops, adjusting the reaction temperature, magnetically stirring for 3 hours, centrifuging and washing 3-5 times. The reaction is carried out at 80-130 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The synthesis of the tetramethyl guanidine lactate involves placing 5% 1,1,3,3-tetramethyl guanidine ethanol solution in an ice water bath, magnetically stirring for 3 hours, adding 5% ethanol solution of lactic acid in drops into 5% 1,1,3,3-tetramethyl guanidine ethanol solution, stirring for 5-8 hours in an ice-water bath, stirring for 24-48 hours, and carrying out rotary evaporation at 60 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: The oil bath treatment is carried out at 80-100 degrees C. In tetramethyl guanidine lactate synthesis, the ice water bath is stirred for 5-8 hours and the stirring is carried out at room temperature for 24-48 hours. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of 34 mg palladium chloride to 7-9 ml tetramethyl guanidine is 3.2-3.4:1.

TI High efficiency extraction of yolk lecithin            includes treating yolk liquid with subcritical carbon            dioxide fluid, separating egg butter, vacuum            freeze-drying degreased yolk solution and purifying            yolk powder with supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU ZHU H
   LOU H
   ZHANG X
AE QINGDAO BAIFUDE SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202027543J
AB    NOVELTY - High efficiency extraction of yolk lecithin                comprises using liquid yolk as raw material,                treating yolk liquid with subcritical carbon                dioxide fluid, separating egg butter, degreasing,                vacuum freeze-drying degreased yolk solution to                obtain yolk powder, extracting to obtain yolk                lecithin and purifying yolk powder with                supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is for high efficiency extraction                of yolk lecithin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe and environmental friendly.                The yolk lecithin has high activity, good quality                and no solvent residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The treatment of yolk                  liquid with subcritical carbon dioxide fluid                  comprises feeding yolk liquid into extraction                  device, introducing carbon dioxide at 25-30                  degrees C and extracting for 2-4 hours under 6-8                  Megapascal (MPa) to obtain yolk oil and degreased                  yolk liquid. The vacuum freeze-drying comprises                  placing degreased yolk in vacuum freeze dryer,                  cooling to - 40 to - 30 degrees C for 2-4 hours,                  turning on vacuum pump, adjusting vacuum to 30-60                  Pa, drying for 6-8 hours, heating at 30-45                  degrees c, cooling to - 40 to - 35 degrees C for                  1-2 hours to obtain egg yolk powder. The                  extraction and purification of yolk powder with                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid comprises                  placing yolk powder in extraction kettle,                  extracting at 25-30 degrees C, introducing liquid                  carbon dioxide for supercritical extraction,                  extracting for 2-3 hours under the pressure of                  15-18 Megapascal (MPa), decompressing to 5-6 MPa                  and separating at 25 degrees c to obtain egg yolk                  lecithin.

PN CN110747160-A; JP6759497-B1; CN110747160-B; JP2021083437-A
TI Culturing fertilized eggs of sheep for in vitro            culture of embryos involves performing oocyte culture,            treating semen, performing in vitro fertilization and            culturing fertilized egg.
AU LIANG J
   LIU L
   WANG S
   DONG X
   WANG Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2020116825
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing fertilized eggs of sheep                involves (a) performing oocyte culture by                transferring collected oocyte complexes into mature                droplets, transferring 10-20 oocytes into each                droplet of mature fluid, and incubating at                38-39&#176; C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide                and saturated humidity for 22-26 hours, (b)                treating semen by taking fresh or thawed semen,                diluting into a sperm dilution, transferring the                sperm dilution into each fertilized droplet, and                placing the fertilized droplets in a carbon dioxide                incubator together with a Petri dish or plate to                equilibrate for 2-3 hours, (c) performing in vitro                fertilization by transferring the mature eggs in                the mature fluid into the fertilized droplets at a                ratio of 30-50 mature eggs in each fertilized                droplet, and then placing in an incubator at                38-39&#176; C, 5% carbon dioxide and saturated                humidity for in vitro fertilization and culture for                18-24 hours, and (d) culturing fertilized                egg.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing fertilized                eggs of sheep for in vitro culture of                embryos.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves survival rate of                fertilized eggs, and maintains the nutrition                environment in the incubator during the process of                continuously viewing the fertilized eggs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing fertilized eggs of sheep                involves (a) performing oocyte culture by                transferring collected oocyte complexes into mature                droplets, transferring 10-20 oocytes into each                droplet of mature fluid, and incubating at                38-39&#176; C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide                and saturated humidity for 22-26 hours, (b)                treating semen by taking fresh or thawed semen,                diluting into a sperm dilution, transferring the                sperm dilution into each fertilized droplet, and                placing the fertilized droplets in a carbon dioxide                incubator together with a Petri dish or plate to                equilibrate for 2-3 hours, (c) performing in vitro                fertilization by transferring the mature eggs in                the mature fluid into the fertilized droplets at a                ratio of 30-50 mature eggs in each fertilized                droplet, and then placing in an incubator at                38-39&#176; C, 5% carbon dioxide and saturated                humidity for in vitro fertilization and culture for                18-24 hours, and (d) culturing fertilized egg by                after fertilizing the sperm and eggs for 18-24                hours, removing the granular cells and attached                sperm around the fertilized eggs, placing the                embryo development droplets at a density of 15-40                portions/drop, and conducting in vitro culture in a                saturated humidity incubator for 6-8 days. The                incubator has a box main portion, a several culture                dishes placed in the box main portion, a lifting                device for individually raising and lowering a                several culture dishes, control device for                controlling the temperature inside the main portion                and carbon dioxide and oxygen, and isolation                mechanism isolated from the main portion during the                process of taking or storing the culture dish. The                lifting device comprises a several hollow tubes                fixedly arranged on the box main portion in an                equidistant annular distribution, a hollow disk                provided in communication with several hollow tube                inner cavities, piston rod in hollow tube, fixed                seat fixed on the upper end of the piston rod for                placing a Petri dish, air inlet pipe located at the                axis of the hollow disk and inserted into the                hollow disk, suction tube provided on the hollow                disk, seal provided in the hollow disc for sealing                the hollow pipe, cavity provided in the seal,                connecting pipe for communicating the cavity on the                seal with the intake pipe, large gear fixed on the                intake pipe, small gear provided in mesh with the                large gear, and first motor for driving the                rotation of the small gear. The suction tube sucks                out the air in the hollow disk and the hollow tube,                and the piston rod located in the hollow tube                descends at the bottom. The Petri dish on the fixed                seat is located in the incubator. The first motor                drives the pinion to rotate, which drives the gear                to rotate. The intake pipe and the seal are                connected through a connecting pipe, the seal is                rotated around the intake pipe as the axis, and the                seal is displaced directly below the culture dish.                The hollow tube communicates with the air inlet                tube through the cavity and the connecting tube on                the seal, and the air inlet tube is injected with                gas, which drives the piston rod, the fixed seat                and the culture dish as a whole to complete the                single take of the culture dish.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mature fluid is                  added to the M199 medium stock solution                  comprising 8-12 g/ml follicle stimulating                  hormone, 8-12 g/ml luteinizing hormone, 0.5-1.5                  g/ml estrogen, 0.05-0.15 mmol/l &#946;                  -mercaptoethanol and 10-20% estrous ewe serum.                  The culture solution is made into mature droplets                  of 40-60 &#956; l/drop before use. The                  fertilization fluid in step (c) is added to                  fallopian tube synthesis fluid comprising 0.1-0.2                  &#956; mol/l penicillamine, 0.1-0.2                  &#956; mol/l taurine, 5-10 &#956; g/ml                  heparin and 2-10% estrous ewe serum.

The step (ii) is carried out at 60-150 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN109234758-A; CN109234758-B
TI Preparing long carbon chain oxygenate using carbon dioxide and water involves adding aqueous solution of potassium bicarbonate or sodium bicarbonate in transparent closed container, connecting photoanode with photocathode.
AU JING H
   WANG J
AE UNIV LANZHOU (ULAN-C)
GA 201913237S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing long carbon chain oxygenate using carbon dioxide and water involves adding an aqueous solution of potassium bicarbonate or sodium bicarbonate at a concentration of 0.05-0.3 mol/L in a transparent closed container, and connecting a photoanode with a photocathode loaded with metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) on the metal foam material and a bismuth vanadate or platinum or palladium or silver or gold electrode to the photocathode and photoanode respectively. The voltage between the electrodes is 0.4-1.8 volt, and the container is placed in the sunlight to replace the gas in the container with carbon dioxide gas, and then the container is sealed or reacted in a continuous carbon dioxide atmosphere. The carbon dioxide gas is replenished in the reaction to form a long carbon chain containing oxygen in the solution. The supported MOFs are any of ZIF-8 or ZIF-67 or UiO-66 or MIL-101, and the metal foam is either iron or nickel or copper or titanium or aluminum.
   USE - Method for preparing long carbon chain oxygenate using carbon dioxide and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare long carbon chain oxygenate using carbon dioxide and water, which has a carbon content of about 50-300C, and the high-carbon organics can be converted into a variety of high-value-added chemicals such as alkanes, alkenes, gasoline and diesel through a cracking process to produce a range of downstream products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The organic dye is added to an aqueous solution of potassium bicarbonate or sodium bicarbonate, and the reaction is carried out. The solution used is an aqueous potassium bicarbonate solution having a concentration of 0.1 mol/L and an interelectrode voltage of 1.4 Volt. Preferred Process: The method for preparing a metal-supported MOFs photocathode material, which involves dissolving 3 mmol of 10 mmol of zinc nitrate hexahydrate in 30 mL of anhydrous ethanol solution and stirred uniformly, adding 10 mL of 12 mmol of an aqueous solution of dimethylimidazole dropwise with vigorous stirring, and placing a clean metal mesh in the solution. The reaction is continued for 24 hours at room temperature with slow agitation, and then the metal mesh loaded with ZIF-8 particles is taken out of the solution. The solution is placed in a vacuum oven at 60 degrees C overnight after rinsing with absolute ethanol, and then heated to 400 degrees C at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute under argon protection. The air is introduced after 30 minutes and the calcination is continued at this temperature for 30 minutes, and cooled to obtain foamed metal-loaded ZIF-8 photocathode material. The method for preparing a metal foam-supported MOFs photocathode material, which involves dissolving 3 mmol cobalt nitrate hexahydrate in 30 mL of anhydrous ethanol solution and stirred uniformly, adding 10 mL of 12 mmol of an aqueous solution of dimethylimidazole (9.84 g) dropwise with vigorous stirring, and placing a clean copper mesh in the solution. The reaction is continued for 24 hours at room temperature with slow agitation, and then the metal mesh loaded with ZIF-67 particles is taken out of the solution. The solution is placed in a vacuum oven at 60 degrees C overnight after rinsing with absolute ethanol, and then heated to 400 degrees C at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute under argon protection. The air is introduced after 30 minutes and the calcination is continued at this temperature for 30 minutes, and cooled to obtain foamed metal-loaded ZIF-67 photocathode material. The method for preparing a metal foam-supported MOFs photocathode material, which involves dissolving 0.45 g of 1.923 mmol of zirconium chloride and 0.318 g of 1.923 mmol of terephthalic acid in 30 mL dimethylformamide (DMF), transferring to a 50mL hydrothermal kettle, putting into a clean metal mesh, controlling the reaction temperature at 120 degrees C for 48 hours, taking out the metal mesh of the UiO-66 particles after the reaction is completed, washing with a DMF solution, dried under vacuum at room temperature, and then rinsing to remove residual DMF solvent molecules. The temperature is raised to 450 degrees C at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute under the argon protection condition, and the condition is maintained for 30 minutes. The air is introduced and calcination is continued at this temperature for 30 minutes, and cooling to obtain a titanium oxide-loaded UiO-66 photocathode material. The method for preparing a metal foam-supported MOFs photocathode material, which involves dissolving 1.003 g of 2.5 mmol of cadmium nitrate nonahydrate and 0.412 g of terephthalic acid in 25 mL of deionized water, adding 2.5mmol of 48 wt.% HF, stirring at room temperature for 20 minutes, transferring to a 50mL hydrothermal kettle, and putting into a clean metal mesh. The reaction temperature is controlled at 220 degrees C for 10 hours. The metal mesh sheets loaded with MIL-101 particles are taken out after the reaction is completed, rinsed with deionized water, vacuum dried at room temperature, and the temperature is raised to 380 degrees C at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute under argon protection conditions, and kept for 30 minutes. The air is introduced, and calcination is continued at this temperature for 30 minuytes, and cooling to obtain a foamed metal mesh loaded MIL-101 photocathode material.

PN US2018311596-A1; WO2018203231-A1
TI Method for developing carbon dioxide based chromatography separation, involves determining if chromatography system exhibits laminar flow from chart showing relationship between laminar flow and set of chromatography conditions.
AU TARAFDER A
   HILL J F
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 2018854398
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a chromatography system including a carbon dioxide pump (10b). A set of chromatography conditions, including a first percent carbon dioxide and a first percent modifier in the mobile phase, a first system temperature, a first system pressure, and a first mobile phase flow rate, is selected. Determining if the chromatography system at the first set of chromatography conditions exhibits laminar flow is derived from a chart showing the relationship between laminar flow and the first set of chromatography conditions for the system.
   USE - Method for developing carbon dioxide based chromatography separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a chart that easily estimates the demarcation between laminar and non-laminar flow conditions, as the chart can easily and visually demarcate flow rates as a function of the volumetric percent of mobile phase components, temperature, etc. The laminar flow condition chart that can be generated for a carbon dioxide based chromatography system can be used during method development to quickly determine, i.e., determine without experimental testing, if a flow rate may result in non-laminar flow in the connecting tubes through which the mobile phase is flowing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method of developing a laminar flow condition chart for a carbon dioxide based chromatography system; and
   (2) a chromatographic system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of a chromatography system.
   Carbon dioxide pump (10b)
   Column (12)
   Detector (14)
   Back pressure regulator (16)
   Jacket (18)

TI Acid gas removal/capture system used for            converting carbon dioxide to chemical compound,            comprises absorption section for adding solvent to acid            gas stream to form mixture, where solvent capable of            absorbing carbon dioxide, and regeneration section for            separating chemical compound and solvent.
AU CHAN Z P
   QUEK V C
   ZAIN M M
AE PETRONAS PETROLIAM NASIONAL BERHAD (PETR-Non-standard)
GA 2022A8591C
AB    NOVELTY - Acid gas removal/capture system comprises an                absorption section (100) for adding a solvent to an                acid gas stream to form a mixture, where the                solvent capable of absorbing carbon dioxide, and a                regeneration section (200) for separating the                chemical compound and solvent from the mixture,                where the regeneration section comprises a                photoreactor (24) comprising at least one catalyst                for initiating a photoreaction to convert the                carbon dioxide in the mixture to the chemical                compound. The regeneration section comprises a                liquid-gas phase separator (34) for separating the                chemical compound from the mixture, a pervaporation                membrane (32) for separating the chemical compound                from the mixture, a distillation apparatus for                separating the chemical compound from the mixture,                and a flash drum for recovery of light or volatile                hydrocarbons from the mixture before it is received                by the photoreactor. The photoreactor irradiates                the mixture with a light source at a wavelengths of                200-700 nm.
   USE - The acid gas removal/capture system is useful                for converting carbon dioxide to at least one                chemical compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is environmentally-friendly of                removing acid gases from natural gas streams, and                reduces the amount of carbon dioxide emitted into                the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process                for converting carbon dioxide to one or more                chemical compounds in an acid gas removal/capture                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an                acid gas removal/capture system.
   24Photoreactor
   32Pervaporation membrane
   34Liquid-gas phase separator

TI Method for controlling vehicle-inside pressure            fluctuation iterative learning system under carbon            dioxide constraint in high speed motor train, involves            calculating difference between expected in-vehicle            pressure comfort index and target in-vehicle pressure            comfort index.
AU DENG J
   ZHANG M
   QU G
   CHEN C
   YANG L
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST JIAOTONG (UYSJ-C)
GA 202287329Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a carbon                dioxide volume concentration limit value, an                in-vehicle pressure 1s change rate limit value and                an expected in-vehicle pressure comfort index. A                concentration of carbon dioxide in a vehicle is                obtained in real time. A judgment is made to check                whether the concentration of the carbon dioxide in                the vehicle is greater than a limit value. A                ventilation air duct is opened to enable a pressure                protection valve to-be in a full-open state.                Pressure control operation is performed in the                vehicle. Amplitude variation processing is carried                out to calculate control error. A difference                between the expected in-vehicle pressure comfort                index and a target in-vehicle pressure comfort                index is calculated. An initial control quantity is                determined. An iterative learning law is designed                for determining a performance index.
   USE - Method for controlling a vehicle-inside                pressure fluctuation iterative learning system                under carbon dioxide constraint in a high speed                motor train.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can simultaneously ensure comfort                of the pressure in the vehicle and satisfy                requirement of the fresh air quantity in the                vehicle, thus ensuring wide range of                applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for controlling a vehicle-inside pressure                fluctuation iterative learning system under carbon                dioxide constraint in a high speed motor                train.

TI Preparing metal-doped bismuth layer oxide useful in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises soaking and drying conductive substrate in bismuth precursor solution, soaking conductive substrate modified in heteroatom-containing bismuth precursor solution and synthesizing product.

   GUO X
   ZHOU H
   LI Z
   DUAN H
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
   UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 202207879V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-doped bismuth layer oxide comprises using bismuth salt to prepare bismuth precursor solution, soaking and drying the conductive substrate in the bismuth precursor solution to obtain the conductive substrate modified with bismuth oxide seed layer, using bismuth salt and compound containing heteroatoms to prepare heteroatom-containing bismuth precursor solution, soaking the conductive substrate modified withbismuth oxide seed layer in the heteroatom-containing bismuth precursor solution and using hydrothermal method to synthesize metal-doped bismuth layer oxide with nano-sheet array structure on the conductive substrate, where the heteroatom comprises silver, gold, copper, platinum, palladium, rhodium, tin, iridium, ruthenium, cobalt, nickel, copper, manganese and/or iron.
   USE - The layer oxide is useful in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The layer oxide: is more stable in structure and performance; has a honeycomb nanosheet array structure; can provide a large electrochemical active area; is used as a cathode catalyst in the reaction of electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide; can promote the enrichment and reduction of carbon dioxide on the electrode surface and increases the current density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use method of the metal-doped bismuth layer oxide in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprising supporting the metal-doped bismuth layer oxide on a conductive substrate as a cathode, assembling an electrolytic cell with an anode and an electrolyte, feeding carbon dioxide, and electro catalytically reducing carbon dioxide on the cathode to generate formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bismuth salt comprises bismuth nitrate, bismuth chloride and/or bismuth sulfate; the heteroatom-containing compound is nitrate, sulfate and chloride salt containing heteroatoms. The conductive substrate comprises carbon cloth, carbon paper, foam nickel, nickel sheet, copper net, copper sheet, foam copper, titanium net, stainless steel, iron sheet and FTO conductive glass. The electrolyte is a solid electrolyte or a liquid electrolyte. The solid electrolyte is a polymer solid electrolyte or an oxide solid electrolyte. The liquid electrolyte comprises lithium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate and/or potassium sulfate. The oxygen evolution catalyst used in the anode comprises metal iridium/carbon catalyst, metal ruthenium/carbon catalyst, iridium dioxide, ruthenium dioxide, nickel iron hydrotalcite and/or nickel hydroxide.Preferred Method: The step of preparing the bismuth precursor solution comprises dissolving 0.01-0.05 mg/ml bismuth salt in 1-10 ml ethylene glycol and/or 1-10 ml ethanol, stirring until to obtain the bismuth precursor solution, soaking the conductive substrate in the bismuth precursor solution for 1-5 hours, drying for 5-10 minutes and repeatedly soaking and drying process for 3-6 times. The preparation of the bismuth precursor solution containing hetero atoms comprises dissolving the heteroatom-containing compound and bismuth salt in 10-50% glycol and/or 10-50% ethanol and stirring to obtain the heteroatom-containing bismuth precursor solution. The hydrothermal method comprises reacting for 5-10 hours under the constant temperature of 120-200degrees Celsius, taking out and washing and drying at 60-degrees.

TI Self-driving tire has carbon dioxide power drive system that changes physical form of carbon dioxide, and turbine drive device that is pushed through pressure difference, to rotate around turbine drive fixed shaft by driving wheel hub.
AU LI L
AE SHENZHEN PUBAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020902765
AB    NOVELTY - The tire has a tire case (1) that is coaxially sleeved on an outer ring of a circular wheel hub (3). A turbine drive device (5) is arranged on the inner ring side of the hub is arranged coaxially and connected to the hub. A turbine drive fixed shaft (55) is coaxially arranged at the center of the turbine drive device, and inside the turbine drive device. A carbon dioxide power drive system is provided, and in the turbine drive device. The carbon dioxide flowing in the flow channel of the carbon dioxide power drive system cyclically changes the physical form of carbon dioxide by absorbing external heat. A generated pressure difference pushes the turbine drive device to rotate around the turbine drive fixed shaft by driving the wheel hub and the tire case to operate synchronously.
   USE - Self-driving tire.
   ADVANTAGE - The physical form of carbon dioxide is changed by absorbing the heat generated when the tire is running. The pressure difference generated drives the turbine to rotate by driving the tire to run, avoiding the tire from being driven by the engine, the driving efficiency is improved. The energy and environmental protection are ensured.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the self-driving tire.
   Tire case (1)
   Circular wheel hub (3)
   Turbine drive device (5)

The metal complex has substituted metal complexes of formula (Lp-M-(L1)m) (II). The initiating ligand (L1) is any anion. The initiating ligand (L1) comprises azide, halides, alkyl sulfonates, carboxylates, alkoxides or phenolates. The ligand is a polymerization initiator and the chain transfer agent are the same compound or ionic forms of the same molecule. The chain transfer agent is present in a molar ratio of at least 10:1, preferably 1:100-1:250 relative to the metal complex. The polymerization system further comprises a co-catalyst. The co-catalyst comprises amines, guanidines, amidines, phosphines, nitrogen-containing heterocycles, ammonium salts, phosphonium salts, arsonium salts and/or bisphosphine ammonium salts. The co-catalyst comprises an onium salt. The onium salt includes a counterion i.e. polyfunctional polymerization initiator. The counterion is polyfunctional polymerization initiator and the chain transfer agent are the same compound or ionic forms of the same compound. The counterion i.e. a polyfunctional polymerization initiator and the chain transfer agent and the ligand i.e. polymerization initiator are the same compound, or ionic forms of the same compound.Lp = permanent ligand set;M = metal atom, preferably Cr, Mn, V, Fe, Co, Mo, W, Ru, Al or Ni;L1 = ligand i.e. a polymerization initiator;m = 0-2, inclusive representing the number of initiating ligands present;Y1 = functional group capable of initiating chain growth of epoxide carbon dioxide copolymers and each Y1 group may be the same or different, preferably -OH or -C(O)OH;-A- = covalent bond or multivalent moiety, preferably polymer chain or oligomer comprising polyolefins, polyethers, polyesters, polycarbonates, polycarbonate polyethers or polyoxymethylene, or a scaffold derived from a compound comprising sugar alcohols, carbohydrates, saccharides, polysaccharides, starch, starch derivatives, lignins, lignans, partially hydrolyzed triglycerides or their derivatives; andn = 1-10, preferably 1-4.Preferred Method: The method (M1) further comprises: calculating a molar ratio of chain transfer agent relative to metal complex to be used in the polymerization, where the calculation comprises (i) selecting a desired length of time for which the polymerization reaction is to be run, (ii) multiplying the selected length of time for which the polymerization reaction is to run by the turnover frequency of the metal complex under the reaction conditions iii) multiplying this result by the desired mol % conversion of epoxide, and iv) using the inverse of this result as the ratio of metal complex to epoxide used for the reaction; calculating the ratio of chain transfer agent to polymerization metal complex to used in the polymerization, calculation comprising the substeps of (v) taking the value from step (iii) and multiplying this result by the molecular weight of the repeating unit of the polycarbonate, (vi) selecting a desired molecular weight for the polyol and dividing the result from step (v) by this number, and (vii) subtracting the number of chains produced per metal complex molecule from the result of step (vi) and taking the result as the ratio of chain transfer agent to metal complex used in step (a); measuring the quantity of water present in the reaction mixture and reducing the quantity of chain transfer agent added to the reaction mixture by an amount determined by the result of the water measurement; drying the reaction mixture prior to step (b); and measuring the molecular weight of the polymer formed in the reaction mixture at least two time points, extrapolating the time at which a desired molecular weight of polymer will have formed, and terminating the polymerization reaction at the extrapolated time. In the method (M1): at least 50% of epoxide in the reaction mixture is converted to aliphatic polycarbonate polyol; the molar ratio of chain transfer agent to metal complex is 10:1-1000:1, preferably 40:1-400:1; at least 90%, preferably at least 99% of the end groups of the polycarbonate polyol formed are hydroxy groups; the PDI of the polycarbonate polyol formed is less than 1.6, preferably less than 1.2; more than 80%, preferably more than 95% of the linkages formed by the metal complex are carbonate linkages; the polycarbonate polyol has an average molecular weight number of 300-20000 g/mol; the epoxide comprises propylene oxide; the head-to-tail ratio of the polycarbonate polyol is greater than 80%, preferably greater than 95%; less than 5% cyclic carbonate is formed; and the molecular weight of the polymer is determined by gel permeation chromatography. Preferred Composition: The composition: comprises greater than 92%, preferably greater than 99% carbonate linkages; has number average molecular weight of 500-5000 g/mol, preferably 8000 g/mol; comprises polymer chains denoted having substituted polymers of formula (T-Y1-A-(Y-T1)n) (P1); polymer chains denoted having substituted polymers of formula (X-E-O-(C(=O)-O-E-O)p-H) or (X-(C(=O)-O-E-O)p-H) (P2), where the ratio of P1 polymer chains to P2 polymer chains is greater than 9:1, preferably greater than 100:1; and further comprises polymer chains having substituted polymers of formula (H-(O-E-O-C(=O)p-O-E-O-(C(=O)-O-E-O)p-H) (P1a), where the ratio of P1 polymer chains to P1a polymer chains is greater than 20:1.T = polycarbonate chain having HO-(E-O-C(=O)-O)p-E-) or H-(O-E-O-C(=O)p-);E = optionally substituted 2C unit derived from an epoxide, preferably e.g. -CH2-CH2-, -CH(CH3)-CH2-, -CH(CH2-CH3)-CH2-, -CH((CH2)2)-CH2- and -CH((CH2)3)-CH2-, where E may represent a monomer unit derived from one type of epoxide or a mixture of at least two types of epoxide and the -E- comprises -CH2CH(CH3)- groups derived from propylene oxide, or units derived from propylene oxide in combination -E- groups derived ethylene oxide, or units derived from propylene oxide in combination with lesser quantities of more complex -E- groups derived from other epoxides, units derived from propylene oxide in combination with units derived from a glycidyl ester, units derived from propylene oxide in combination with units derived from a glycidyl ether, or units derived from propylene oxide in combination with units derived from an epoxide of 6-30C alpha olefin;p = 5-10000; andX = bound form of an anion capable of initiating only one polymer chain.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN US2019170348-A1; US10995948-B2
TI Gas-assisted liquid fuel oxygen reactor system, has vessel comprising inlet for heated air and fuel stream, where vessel is defined by structure that surrounds reaction zone, such that heat is transferred from heated air to reaction zone.
AU BEN-MANSOUR R
   HABIB M A
   JAMAL A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 201950355T
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide-assisted atomizer (115) comprising an inlet (110) adapted to receive liquid fuel and an outlet adapted to spray atomized fuel and carbon dioxide. An evaporation zone (105) comprises an inlet (120) adapted to receive the atomized liquid fuel and carbon dioxide. A reaction zone (145) is co-axially aligned, and in flow communication with the evaporation zone, such that the reaction zone receives flow of the vaporized fuel and carbon dioxide from the evaporation zone. A heating vessel comprises an inlet for heated air and gaseous fuel stream, where the heating vessel is defined by a structure that surrounds the reaction zone, such that heat is transferred from the heated air and gaseous fuel stream to the reaction zone.
   USE - Gas-assisted liquid fuel oxygen reactor system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system maintains constant temperature at an ion transport unit to avoid thermal stresses in the ion transport unit, thus improving film stability and thermal performance. The system determines cross-flow of the vaporized fuel and carbon dioxide to enhance oxygen permeation from the air stream through tubes, and efficiency of combustion reaction in the reaction zone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for facilitating low-carbon dioxide emission combustion of a liquid fuel in a gas-assisted liquid fuel oxygen reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a gas-assisted liquid fuel oxygen reactor in a cylindrical configuration.
   Evaporation zone (105)
   Inlet (110)
   Carbon dioxide-assisted atomizer (115)
   Inlet (120)
   Reaction zone (145)

AU YU X

   FANG Y
AE HUADAO SHANGHAI CAR-TCELL MEDICINE CO (HUAD-Non-standard)
GA 2019509432
AB    NOVELTY - Fully enclosed cell culture gas control system comprises intake passage, separated air passage (1), carbon dioxide passage (2), and oxygen passage (3) and incubator (4) is connecting with the air passage. The carbon dioxide passage and the oxygen passage for buffering and mixing the gas from the intake passage to form a mixed gas and culture tank (9) for performing cell culture and being placed in an incubator. The mixed gas suction passage is connecting with the incubator and the culture tank for feeding the mixed gas in the incubator into the culture tank. The exhaust gas discharge passage is connecting with the culture tank for discharging exhaust gas generated during cell culturing. The gas concentration sensing module comprising an oxygen gas concentration sensor and a carbon dioxide gas concentration sensor used to determine the real-time oxygen gas concentration value and the real-time carbon dioxide gas concentration value in the incubator.
   USE - Used as fully enclosed cell culture gas control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas control system: ensures changes in external gases during cell culture which do not directly affect cell culture and content of each component of gas is constant; reduces manual operations and risk of operational errors; is economical; and improves culturing effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for controlling a fully enclosed cell culture gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of fully enclosed cell culture gas control system.

   Carbon dioxide passage (2)
   Oxygen passage (3)
