PN CN111153381-A; CN111153381-B
TI Carbon dioxide capture system comprises iron-based            oxygen carrier modified with sodium aluminate, and            carbon dioxide capture device containing gas            distribution system, steam generator, high temperature            coal feed hopper, and condenser.
AU MA J
   LI J
   GUO Q
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
   UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 202044240Y
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide capture system comprises                iron-based oxygen carrier modified with sodium                aluminate, and carbon dioxide capture device. The                oxygen carrier comprises iron-aluminum crystals                with aluminum iron garnet structure, and 3-10 wt.%                sodium iron oxide. Water gas is used as reducing                agent. Oxygen carrier reduction reaction is carried                out in fluidized bed reactor at 800-900&#176;                C. Oxygen carrier oxidation reaction is carried out                with water vapor as oxidant to obtain mixture of                iron-aluminum crystals containing iron(II, III)                oxide, aluminum iron garnet structures, alumina,                ferric oxide, and sodium iron oxide. The carbon                dioxide capture device captures carbon dioxide and                increase hydrogen-to-carbon ratio of chemical chain                hydrogen production. The device comprises gas                distribution system, steam generator, high                temperature coal feed hopper, reactor, automatic                temperature control device, cyclone separator,                condenser, and gas collection and test                system.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide capture system makes the                fuel pyrolysis components sufficient, can reduce                the energy demand of solid fuel gasification, and                reduce energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide capture system comprises                iron-based oxygen carrier modified with sodium                aluminate, and carbon dioxide capture device. The                oxygen carrier comprises iron-aluminum crystals                with aluminum iron garnet structure, and 3-10 wt.%                sodium iron oxide of formula: NaFeO2. Water gas is                used as reducing agent. Oxygen carrier reduction                reaction is carried out in fluidized bed reactor at                800-900&#176; C. The product after reduction is                ferrous oxide. Oxygen carrier oxidation reaction is                carried out with water vapor as oxidant to obtain                mixture of iron-aluminum crystals containing                iron(II, III) oxide, aluminum iron garnet                structures, alumina, ferric oxide, and sodium iron                oxide. The particle size of oxygen carrier is 0-4                mm, and particle size of solid fuel is 0.08-0.18                mm. The carbon dioxide capture device captures                carbon dioxide and increase hydrogen-to-carbon                ratio of chemical chain hydrogen production. The                device comprises gas distribution system, steam                generator, high temperature coal feed hopper,                reactor, automatic temperature control device,                cyclone separator, condenser, and gas collection                and test system. The gas distribution system                provides argon, air and water vapor for reactor.                Air is provided by air compressor. Water vapor is                generated by steam generator. The flow rate is                controlled by volume pump. Argon gas is used as                purge gas, and water vapor and air are fluidized                media of system. The reactor is fluidized bed. Air                and water vapor are introduced into bottom of                reactor, argon is introduced into top of reactor                connected to high temperature coal feed hopper, and                middle of reactor is filled with oxygen carrier.                The outlet at top of reactor is connected to                cyclone separator, inlet to condenser is connected                to separator, and outlet is connected to gas                collection and test system. The reactor is equipped                with automatic temperature control system. The                operating temperatures of reactor and steam                generator are 750-1050&#176; C and                650-850&#176; C, respectively, and the                operating pressure is 0.1-4 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The oxygen carrier is                  prepared using sodium aluminate, ferric oxide and                  &#947; -alumina in mass ratio of                  (0.5-1):4:(6-16). The mass ratio of solid fuel,                  oxygen carrier and water vapor is                  1:(10-15):(3-5).

The catalytic system                  comprises an inorganic oxide preferably chosen                  from titanium dioxide, alumina or sepiolite, and                  comprising at least 10 %mass preferably 1 %mass                  metal (M). Preferred Property: The molar ratio of                  inorganic oxide to the reducing agent is 0.1-1.0.                  Preferred Process: The preparation of inorganic                  oxide comprising oxygen vacancies involves (1.1)                  mixing a solid inorganic oxide with a reducing                  agent, (1.2) heat-treating the mixture at 300-400                  degrees C, under an inert atmosphere, (1.3)                  cooling the mixture to room temperature, to                  obtain a cooled mixture, (1.4) removing the                  reducing agent by washing the cooled mixture, to                  obtain the inorganic oxide with oxygen vacancies                  and free of reducing agent, and (1.5) drying the                  inorganic oxide under an inert atmosphere. The                  drying step (1.5) of inorganic oxide is carried                  out under a flow of argon, for at least 12 hours,                  and at 110-150 degrees C. The deposition of                  metal(s) (M) on the inorganic oxide comprising                  oxygen vacancies involves (2.1) impregnating the                  inorganic oxide with a solution, in a solvent, of                  at least one salt of metal (M) chosen from                  nickel, platinum, palladium, cobalt, ruthenium,                  iron and copper, preferably nickel, platinum,                  palladium and their mixtures, and at least one                  anion (A), to obtain an inorganic oxide                  impregnated with the salt of metal (M), (2.2)                  removing the solvent, by vacuum evaporation, or                  centrifugation, then filtration, (2.3) drying the                  inorganic oxide impregnated with the salt of                  metal (M) under an inert atmosphere, and (2.4)                  calcining the inorganic oxide under an inert                  atmosphere, to obtain an inorganic oxide                  comprising at least one metal (M) anchored in the                  oxygen vacancies of the inorganic support. The                  calcination is carried out at 400-600 degrees C,                  for a period of 4-8 hours. The reduction                  reactions are chosen from the methanation                  reaction and the reaction for reducing carbon                  dioxide to carbon monoxide or to mixture of                  carbon monoxide and methane. The reaction for                  reducing carbon dioxide is a methanation reaction                  and in that the catalytic system comprises metal                  (M) in the form of particles, the quantity of                  metal (M) present in the catalytic system is 2                  %mass with respect to the mass of the inorganic                  oxide. The carbon dioxide reduction reaction                  leads to the formation of carbon monoxide or a                  mixture of carbon monoxide and methane, and the                  catalytic system comprises metal (M) in the form                  of isolated atoms and/or particles, and the                  quantity of metal (M) present in catalytic system                  is less than 2 %mass, with respect to the mass of                  the inorganic oxide. The method further involves                  (i) depositing the catalytic system in the heart                  of the reactor, and the catalytic system which is                  arranged between two layers of an inert material,                  and (ii) reducing the catalytic system under a                  flow of hydrogen and in the presence of an inert                  gas, before carrying out the catalytic reduction                  of carbon dioxide. Preferred Composition: The                  quantity of metal (M) during (2.1) impregnation                  is 0.1-40 %mass with respect to the mass of the                  inorganic oxide resulting from step (1).                  Preferred Condition: The carbon dioxide reduction                  reaction is carried out at 250-500 degrees C. The                  duration of the carbon dioxide reduction reaction                  is 5-24 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN JP2021114968-A
TI Method for estimating internal environment of greenhouse, involves estimating amount of carbon-dioxide production in greenhouse and ventilation rate by using tracer gas as carbon dioxide in greenhouse.
AU SHIMODA K
   JINZAI Y
AE SEIWA KK (SEIW-Non-standard)
GA 2021919098
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves applying a Kalman filter as a state-space model. A state estimation value of an amount of carbon dioxide production obtained by a characteristic equation is considered. A Karman gain is applied to an observation estimated value of the amount of carbon dioxide production obtained from an observation equation, which is seasoned with observation noise of an environment measuring apparatus attached to a greenhouse. A last estimated value regarding the amount in the greenhouse is calculated. The respiration amount of a plant is estimated from the estimated value.
   USE - Greenhouse environment estimation method for estimating the amount of carbon dioxide production in a greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The amount of carbon-dioxide production in a greenhouse and the ventilation rate can be estimated precisely. The physiological state of the plant represented by respiration amount can be calculated at a high precision, without giving a stress to a plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following :
   (1) a greenhouse internal environment estimation apparatus has the last estimated value regarding the ventilation rate in the greenhouse to be calculated; and
   (2) a computer program for making a computer perform the method.

PN AU2018101151-A4; KR2019031926-A; KR2017658-B1
TI Method for improving injectivity in underground gas storage layer of e.g. aquifer, involves filing nanofluid and water in pores of storage layer such that irreversible water saturation is lowered, and injecting and storing carbon dioxide.
AU PARK Y
   SEO Y
   LEE S
   PARK Y C
   YOUNGJIN S
   SEUNGWOO L
   SEO Y J
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
   KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 2018748852
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving injectivity in underground gas storage layer involves injecting a nanofluid including nanoparticles such that irreversible water saturation of a storage layer is lowered through an injection well connected to an underground gas storage layer, and injecting and storing carbon dioxide into the storage layer. The nanofluid and water are filled in pores the storage layer, and which are discharged together and the irreversible water saturation is lowered around the injection well, when the carbon dioxide is injected and thus the carbon dioxide injectivity of the storage layer is improved.
   USE - Method for improving injectivity in underground gas storage layer of oil field, gas field, and aquifer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the carbon dioxide injectivity into the entire storage layer by reducing the irreversible water saturation of the storage layer around an injection well into which carbon dioxide is injected, and ensures large-capacity storage layer and smooth injectivity. The nanoparticles have high adsorbability which reduces the interfacial tension between fluids in the storage layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the method for improving injectivity in underground gas storage layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nanofluid is a mixture of nanoparticles and water, or a mixture polymer and surfactant. The nanoparticles include metal oxides of titanium oxide, zinc oxide, iron oxide, aluminum oxide, and/or calcium oxide, or particles comprising magnetite or silica combined with an organic material e.g. dendrimers, micelles, and vesicles. The nanoparticles are formed of ore-based nanoclay including coal ash, and silica. The nanoparticles are in an emulsion form. Preferred Property: The nanoparticles have particle size of 100 nm or less.

   R1=a 1-16C alkyl group, a 3-16C cycloalkyl group, a substituted alkyl group or a hydrogen atom; and
   R2=an acyl group.

The reducing agent comprises phenylsilane, diphenylsilane, triethylsilane or polymethylhydrogensiloxane. The organic solvent comprises acetonitrile, benzene, carbon tetrachloride, petroleum ether, tetrahydrofuran, N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethylacetamide, diethyl ether, dichloromethane, chloroform, toluene, xylene, cyclohexane, n-hexane, n-heptane or dioxane. PN KR2454438-B1
TI Producing decaffeinated coffee beans, involves            e.g. stirring green coffee beans in chamber having            outlet at lower portion and closed path connected to            carbon dioxide nanobubbles generator, and filtering            water with activated carbon filter.
AU PARK K W
AE PARK K W (PARK-Individual)
GA 2022C9445N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing decaffeinated coffee                beans involves (a) pressurizing and stirring green                coffee beans in a chamber having an outlet provided                at the lower portion and having a closed path                connected to the carbon dioxide nanobubbles                generator at the lower portion in 0.5-0.8 times the                volume of the green beans at 60-80&#176; C for                5-8 hours, (b) filtering the water with an                activated carbon filter provided in the path                between the water outlet and the carbon dioxide                nanobubbles generator, and transferring the                filtered water to the carbon dioxide nanobubbles                generator, (c) injecting the filtered water and                carbon dioxide nanobubbles into the chamber and                stirring at a high pressure of 3-5 bar and a                temperature of 50-70&#176; C for 30 minutes-2                hours, (d) repeating steps (b) and (c) 3-7 times,                and (e) recovering carbon dioxide at a low pressure                of 0.5-1 bar, and aging and drying the green coffee                beans.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                decaffeinated coffee beans.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare decaffeinated                coffee beans in a simple and cost-effective manner                with good taste and aroma.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  using 50-70% green beans, sealing the chamber and                  injecting water vapor. The method involves                  injecting the filtered water with carbonated                  water in a carbon dioxide nanobubbles generator                  before being injected into the chamber. The                  method involves repeating steps (b) and (c) for a                  total of 5-10 hours. The step (e) involves adding                  5-10 pts. wt. catechin concentrate, and the step                  (e) is performed at 40-50&#176; C.

PN KR2438061-B1
TI Method for storing and mineralizing carbon dioxide            in papermaking process, involves filling carbon capture            unit with soda lime prepared by mixing aqueous sodium            hydroxide solution and slaked lime, and transferring            flue gas of boiler.
AU PARK H
   KWON I T
   HOON S J
AE ENKI E & C CO LTD (ENKI-Non-standard)
GA 2022B22914
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves filling the carbon capture                unit with soda lime prepared by mixing an aqueous                sodium hydroxide solution and slaked lime. The flue                gas of the boiler, lime kiln, drying furnace, and                incinerator of the papermaking process is                transferred to the carbon capture unit, and the                soda lime is reacted with carbon dioxide contained                in the flue gas, precipitating it in the form of                calcium carbonate and separating it. The separated                calcium carbonate is washed to obtain a high-purity                mineral resource. A portion of the soda lime is                prepared by heating the calcium carbonate salt,                which is the product of the step, after putting it                in an aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, and the                carbon dioxide produced by thermal decomposition                during heating is dissolved in a mixture of calcium                carbonate and water and is collected in the form of                an easy-to-transport calcium hydrogen carbonate                aqueous solution.
   USE - Method for storing and mineralizing carbon                dioxide in papermaking process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method minimizes carbon dioxide emission                and changes in conventional process in papermaking                process that generates excessive amount of carbon                dioxide, and simply obtains calcium mineral of high                purity.

TI Production of carbon material by decomposition of            carbon dioxide discharged from e.g. steel plant,            involves reacting carbon dioxide and oxygen-deficient            iron oxide compound as reducing agent formed by            reducing magnetite while maintaining crystal structure,            or using oxygen-completely deficient iron.
AU WENBIN D
   NOBUTAKE H
   KAZUHISA I
   TATSUYA F
   MIKI I
   YAYOI T
   YUKO S
   DAI W B
   HORIUCHI N
   INADA K
   FUKUDA T
   ITO M
   TANEICHI Y
   SAKAMOTO Y
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 202298252U
AB    NOVELTY - Production of carbon material involves (s1)                reacting carbon dioxide and a reducing agent. The                reducing agent is an oxygen-deficient iron oxide                compound (I), and obtained by (s2) reducing                magnetite while maintaining a crystal structure, or                using an oxygen-completely deficient iron obtained                by completely reducing magnetite.
   USE - Production of carbon material by decomposition                of carbon dioxide which is exhaust gas discharged                from steel plants (all claimed), thermal power                plants, cement manufacturing plants and garbage                incineration facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is effective and economical,                enables production of carbon material with improved                carbon recovery rate and high reaction                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of carbon material involves (s1)                reacting carbon dioxide and a reducing agent. The                reducing agent is an oxygen-deficient iron oxide                compound of formula: Fe3O4-~d (I), where delta is                1-4 (excluding 4) and obtained by (s2) reducing                magnetite while maintaining a crystal structure, or                using an oxygen-completely deficient iron obtained                by completely reducing magnetite. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) the carbon material;
   (2) decomposition of carbon dioxide; and
   (3) the reducing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method further                  involves reacting the magnetite to be reduced                  with hydrogen to deoxygenate the magnetite to                  generate the oxygen-deficient iron oxide or the                  oxygen-completely oxygen-deficient iron, using at                  least portion of the magnetite made from iron                  sand contained in an iron ore in a steel plant,                  supplying at least portion of the oxidized                  reducing agent to the steel plant as a raw                  material, supplying at least portion of carbon to                  an iron plant as a substitute for coke consumed                  in the iron plant, post-processing the carbon,                  and dissolving the oxidized reducing agent to                  separate the carbon, recovering the carbon. The                  reducing agent is prepared by setting a reaction                  temperature to 300-450 degrees C, using 5-100                  vol.% hydrogen, dissolving the oxidized reducing                  agent, and separating the carbon. The step (s1)                  is performed under a temperature of 300-450                  degrees C and reaction pressure of 0.01-5 MPa,                  and heating using exhaust heat generated in the                  steel plant. The step (s2) is performed under a                  reaction temperature of 300-450 degrees C, and                  heating using the waste heat generated in the                  iron manufacturing plant. Preferred Properties:                  The carbon is nanocarbon with a particle size of                  1 mu m or less. The reduced magnetite has a                  specific surface area of 0.1-10 m2/g by the BET                  method, and an average particle size of 1-1000 mu                  m, a bulk density of 0.3-3 g/cm3. The magnetite                  has the specific surface area of 0.1-10 m2/g by                  the BET method, and the average particle size of                  1-1000 mu m, and the bulk density of 0.3-3                  g/cm3.

AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to automatic control of the process of producing biodiesel fuel. Method of controlling the technology of producing biodiesel fuel, involving re-esterification of vegetable oil with supercritical alcohol includes condensation of carbon dioxide vapour in an evaporator of a refrigerating machine operating in heat pump mode; heating and reducing the viscosity of the obtained pure biodiesel fuel; measurement of volumetric consumption of rapeseed oil and alcohol; steam into the reactor coil; unreacted alcohol vapours; cold water into a refrigerator for condensation of unreacted alcohol vapours; carbon dioxide in front of the evaporator of the refrigerating machine; temperature of carbon dioxide before and after evaporator of refrigerating machine and boiling point of refrigerant in evaporator of refrigerating machine; stabilization of the ratio of volumetric consumption of vegetable oil and supercritical; stabilization of carbon dioxide condensation temperature in the refrigerating machine evaporator; affecting the flow rate of cold water supplied for condensation of spent working steam after the coil of the re-esterification reactor and after the recuperative heat exchanger for heating the carbon dioxide to supercritical temperature; as well as monitoring the level of liquefied carbon dioxide in the tank.
   USE - Control; technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - High accuracy and reliability of controlling the technology of producing biodiesel fuel from vegetable oil in supercritical conditions, which increases efficiency with rational use of energy costs.

PN KR2313653-B1
TI Acid-activated montmorillonite catalyst used for regenerating solvent used for capturing carbon dioxide, comprises element(s) comprising silicon, aluminum, iron and magnesium, and total acid site.
AU NAMSUNGCHAN
   BAEK I H
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2021C06896
AB    NOVELTY - An acid-activated montmorillonite catalyst comprises element(s) comprising 0-23% silicon, 0-7% aluminum, 0-1.2% iron and 0-1% magnesium, and 0.3 mmol/g or more total acid site.
   USE - Acid-activated montmorillonite catalyst is used for regenerating solvent e.g. amine solvent used for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The acid-activated montmorillonite catalyst maintains constant catalytic activity even with repeated use, and can be manufactured using clay mineral as raw material by economical method. Since the catalyst has excellent BET surface area, mesoporosity and material property of surface acidity, it efficiently optimizes carbon dioxide desorption rate at low temperature in carbon dioxide capture and amine solvent regeneration process, thereby reducing energy requirement for carbon dioxide capture and amine solvent regeneration process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of the acid-activated montmorillonite catalyst, which involves (i) reacting montmorillonite with an aqueous acid solution to obtain a product (a), and (ii) filtering the product (a) and washing the filtrate until the pH of the filtrate is 5-9; and
   (2) method for regenerating solvent for capturing carbon dioxide, which involves using the acid-activated montmorillonite catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The catalyst has a surface area of 50-500 m2/g, a pore volume of 0.15-0.50 cm3/g, an average pore diameter of 1-15 nm, and a surface acidity of 2 mmol/g or more. Preferred Process: The process involves reacting montmorillonite with the aqueous acid solution in a ratio (weight:volume) of 1:1 to 1:100 at 90-110 degrees C for 1-20 hours to obtain product (a), cooling the product (a) to room temperature, and filtering the product (a). Preferred Composition: The acid is hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, sulfuric acid, benzoic acid, oxalic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, chloric acid, oxonium ion, iodic acid, sulfurous acid, chlorous acid, phosphoric acid, arsenic acid, chloroacetic acid, hydrofluoric acid, nitrous acid, formic acid, carbonic acid, hydrosulfuric acid, hypochlorous acid, hydroiodic acid, hydrobromic acid and/or perchloric acid. The aqueous acid solution has a concentration of 1-10 mol/L.

TI Photo-electrochemical system for reducing carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide, comprises electrochemical cell, photocatalytic electrode, layer comprising e.g. cadmium sulfide, protecting layer, and final molecular layer.
AU ROBERT M
   BOUTIN E
   PATI P B
   ODOBEL F
   BARREAU N
AE UNIV PARIS (UYPA-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV NANTES (UNNT-C)
   UNIV NANTES (UNNT-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV PARIS (UYPA-Non-standard)
GA 2021988996
AB    NOVELTY - A photo-electrochemical system to reduce carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide, comprises an electrochemical cell with an anodic compartment having an anode with a current collector, an electrolyte solution comprising a solvent, and an anodically supporting electrolyte; and a cathodic compartment with cathodic electrolyte solution comprising solvent which is water and cathodic supporting electrolyte, reagent carbon dioxide and photocatalytic electrode comprising a chalcogenide film surmounted by a multilayer of layer of indium or gallium dope copper selenide (Cu(Inx, Gay)Se2, CIGS) or alloy of indium or gallium dope copper selenium sulfide (Cu(Inx, Gay)SewSz), where x is 0-1, y is 1-x, w is 0-2 and z is 2-w, layer having material selected from cadmium sulfide or zinc sulfide, protecting layer, final molecular layer which is molecular catalyst grafted onto surface of protecting layer to electrochemically reduce carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
   USE - A photo-electrochemical system to reduce carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The photo-electrochemical system reduces carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide without formation of formic acid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an explanatory view of the mesoporous titanium oxide film coated with Co-qPyH.
   Fluorine-doped tin oxide conductive glass substrate (1)
   Successive layers of mesoporous titanium oxide (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The protective layer is selected from titanium oxide (TiO2), strontium titanium oxide (SrTiO3), tin oxide (SnO2), and metal doped TiO2. The system comprises a layer selected from zinc oxide (ZnO) or aluminum-doped zinc oxide (AZO), indium tin oxide (ITO), between the layer having material selected from cadmium sulfide (CdS) and zinc sulfide (ZnS), and the protecting layer. The molecular catalyst is grafted on mesoporous TiO2 or tin oxide (SnO2) or SrTiO3 to form a mesoTiO2(SnO2)|Co-qPyH electrode. The chalcogenides photovoltaic cell is coupled with the mesoTiO2|Co-qPyH electrode. The system presents a gas diffusion porous cathode which comprises, on a current collector, electrochemically inert, the molecular catalyst incorporated in the porous cathode with a geometric surface S, to electrochemically reduce the carbon dioxide gas into gaseous carbon monoxide, with an eventual by-production of gaseous hydrogen, the cathode is a CIGS photovoltaic cell or a photocathode; anodic compartment comprising an anode with a current collector, and on the current collector, catalyst to electrochemically oxidize water to oxygen gas, an anodic electrolyte solution, at a controlled flow rate, comprising a solvent, and an anodic electrolyte, cathodic compartment comprising a cathodic electrolyte solution, at a controlled flow rate, comprising a solvent, and a cathodic electrolyte, a gas diffusion porous cathode which comprises, on a gas diffusion porous current cathode collector, the molecular catalyst incorporated in the porous cathode with a geometric surface, to electrochemically reduce the gas comprising carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon monoxide, with a by-production of gaseous hydrogen, channel for flowing the reagent gas carbon dioxide, at a controlled flow rate, onto or through the surface of the gas diffusion porous current cathode collector; cathodic electrolyte solution inlet and a cathodic electrolyte solution outlet connected to the cathodic compartment, to circulate the cathodic electrolyte solution, and the remaining reagent gas carbon dioxide and the product gas carbon monoxide by the outlet. The photovoltaic cell is lit from the side where the molecular catalyst is, or laterally from the photovoltaic cell. Preferred Parameters: The pH of anodic electrolyte solution and the pH of cathodic electrolyte solution is 4-14.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molecular catalyst is chosen from metal porphyrin with one or some +N(1-4C alkyl)3 groups, with the metal chosen from iron and cobalt; metal phthalocyanine, with the metal chosen from iron and cobalt; metal phthalocyanine with the metal chosen from iron or cobalt, with one of +N(1-4C alkyl)3, fluorine, C(CH3)3, or cobalt quater pyridine. The molecular catalyst is a cobalt complex with the planar tetradentate ligand 2,2':6',2'':6'',2'''-quaterpyridine (Co-qPyH). The molecular catalyst presents anchoring groups which are phosphonic acid, alkoxysilane, or hydroxamic acid, to be grafted on the surface. The structure of cobalt complex with phosphonic acid is formula (Co-qPyH).

PN IN201941009604-A
TI Gas metal arc welding process involves obtaining shielding gas mixture comprises argon, carbon dioxide, forming an electric arc between a consumable weld electrode and a work piece to form a weld deposit on work piece from weld electrode.
AU S M
   K G
   K M
AE BEML LTD (BEML-Non-standard)
GA 2021713490
AB    NOVELTY - Gas metal arc welding process involves obtaining shielding gas mixture comprises 80 wt.% argon, 20 wt.% carbon dioxide, forming an electric arc between a consumable weld electrode and a work piece to form a weld deposit on the work piece from the consumable weld electrode and completely shielding the electric arc and the weld depositing with the shielding gas mixture.
   USE - Gas metal arc welding process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas metal arc welding system, which comprises an argon tank comprises argon, carbon dioxide tank comprising carbon dioxide, gas mixture coupled to the argon tank and the carbon dioxide tank to generate a shielding gas mixture comprises 80% argon and 20% carbon dioxide, wire feeder including a consumable electrode and gas metal arc welding torch coupled to the gas mixture and the wire feeder to form an electric arc between a consumable weld electrode and a work piece to form a weld deposit on the work piece from the consumable weld electrode and completely shield the electric arc and the weld deposit with the shielding gas mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The work piece is an abrasion resistant extra high strength steel plate. The consumable electrode comprises approximately 0.06-0.15 wt.% carbon by weight, 1.40-1.85 wt.% manganese by weight, 0.80-1.15 wt.% silicon, 0.025 wt.% phosphoros, 0.035 wt.% sulfur and 0.50 wt.% copper. Preferred Conditions: The consumable electrode is a copper coated solid wire having a diameter of 1.2 millimeter and having a finer grain structure. The welding wire feed is approximately 7-l1 meters/minute. The method involves controlling a flow of the argon from an argon tank and a flow of the carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide tank to maintain a composition of the shielding gas mixture at about 80% argon and 20% carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 202147230F
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing soda ash and calcium sulfate from                ammonium bicarbonate and calcium mirabilite                tailings comprises (a) using ammonium bicarbonate                and bittern as raw materials, conducting metathesis                reaction to obtain solid sodium bicarbonate and                mother liquor of heavy alkali and small amount of                sodium sulfate, (b) calcining solid sodium                bicarbonate to obtain soda ash product and carbon                dioxide, (c) preheating heavy alkali mother liquor                to remove ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium                carbonate to obtain ammonia, carbon dioxide and                deamination mother liquor, (d) reacting mother                liquor for deamination with lime and calcium                mirabilite tailings to obtain ammonia, carbon                dioxide and bittern for removing ammonium, (e)                allowing ammonium-removing bittern reacts with lime                and soda ash, (f) reacting ammonium-removing                refined bittern with carbon dioxide, ammonia and                carbon dioxide in and ammonia and carbon dioxide to                obtain bittern and (g) recycling bittern of double                decomposition reaction process.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing soda ash                and calcium sulfate from ammonium bicarbonate and                calcium mirabilite tailings.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for co-producing soda ash and calcium                sulfate from ammonium bicarbonate and calcium                mirabilite tailings.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of bittern                  is 200-330 g/l sodium chloride, 20-300 g/l                  ammonium bicarbonate and 1-8 g/l sodium sulfate.                  The content of heavy alkali mother liquor is                  50-200 g/l sodium chloride, 1-8 g/l sodium                  sulfate and 100-250 g/l ammonium chloride. The                  content of mother liquor for deamination is                  100-250 g/l ammonium chloride , 1-10 g/l sodium                  sulfate and 50-200 g/l sodium chloride. The                  content of calcium mirabilite tailings is 41-99                  wt.% sodium sulfate, 1-99 wt.% calcium sulfate,                  31-99 wt.% calcium carbonate and 0.01-10 wt.%                  magnesium sulfate. The content of ammonium                  removal bittern is 200-330 g/l sodium chloride,                  1-7 g/l calcium sulfate and 0.02-10 g/l magnesium                  sulfate. The content of in refined bittern for                  removing ammonium is 200-330 g/l sodium chloride                  and 1-8 g/l sodium sulfate. Preferred Method: The                  method comprises taking nitrate halogen, nitrate,                  sodium sulfate decahydrate, anhydrous sodium                  sulfate and mixed salt of nitrate salt replace                  the calcium mirabilite tailings and using ammonia                  and carbon dioxide as raw materials to replace                  ammonium bicarbonate.

AU PENG H
   ZHANG B
   BAI H
AE UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 2020A47876
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst precursor halogen-doped basic copper                chloride compound, is claimed. The catalyst                precursor halogen-doped basic copper chloride                compound is prepared by sol-gel method, where                halogen is one of bromine or iodine, and content is                1-20 wt.%.
   USE - The compound is useful for electrocatalytic                carbon dioxide reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound: is simple; can realizes highly                selective electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction                to generate methane or ethylene; and has good use                prospects in field of electrocatalytic carbon                dioxide reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the catalyst precursor halogen-doped                basic copper chloride compound comprising using                propylene oxide auxiliary hydrolysis process; the                specific steps comprises firstly, mixing ethanol                and isopropanol in container, adding copper(II)                chloride, and potassium bromide or potassium                iodide, and stirring until dissolved; adding                deionized water to the solution, slowly dropwise                adding propylene oxide; allowing to stand for 12-36                hours, adding acetone to the container,                transferring to centrifuge tube, continuously                allowing to stand for 80-120 hours; finally, using                high speed centrifuge, taking acetone as solvent,                centrifuging for 5-8 times, drying the product,                grinding and collecting to obtain catalyst                precursor bromine or iodine doped basic copper                chloride; and #halogen-doped basic copper chloride                compound used as electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction reaction catalyst precursor for preparing                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction                catalyst electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of                  electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction                  reaction catalyst electrode by using                  halogen-doped basic copper chloride compound,                  comprises (I) dispersing 5-10 mg catalyst in                  0.8-1.2 ml deionized water, isopropanol and 5                  wt.% Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene                  based fluoropolymer-copolymer) mixed solution,                  and ultrasonically dispersing for 25-35 minutes                  to obtain slurry; and (II) (a) preparing glassy                  carbon working electrode with catalyst film by                  drip coating method by: dropping 4-6 microliters                  of slurry on glassy carbon electrode with surface                  area of 0.07 cm2, naturally drying it to obtain                  working electrode; using electrochemical                  workstation to apply constant potential to                  activate; the obtained catalyst has high                  selectivity to methane, and the activated                  catalyst is named as methane-copper catalyst; and                  (b) dispersing halogen-doped basic copper                  chloride and binder in mixed solvent of ethanol                  and water, carrying ultrasonic dispersion,                  dropping the catalyst slurry on the catalyst                  carrier; drying, obtaining the catalyst                  electrode. In the method, further adding                  conductive agent e.g. carbon black, carbon                  nanotubes and graphene with a specific gravity of                  10-30%. Preferred Components: The binder is 5%                  perfluorosulfonic acid type polymer solution, and                  the proportion of the binder in the catalyst                  slurry is 5-10 wt.%. The catalyst carrier is                  carbon paper, carbon cloth, carbon felt, metal                  foam or metal foil, the loading amount of                  catalyst is 2-20 mg/cm2.

TI Nanomaterial comprises carbon sphere and carbon            nitride, where carbon sphere is loaded on surface of            carbon nitride to form complex two-dimensional layered            structure.
AU XU Y
   GUO Q
   FENG H
   SHEN D
   HUANG J
   CHEN S
   TANG C
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG GONGSHANG (UYZG-C)
GA 2018289179
AB    NOVELTY - A nanomaterial comprises carbon sphere and                carbon nitride in a molar ratio of 0.025-0.1. The                carbon sphere is loaded on a surface of carbon                nitride to form a complex two-dimensional layered                structure.
   USE - Nanomaterial is useful for the photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the method for preparing the nanomaterial,                which involves (a) dissolving carbon nitride in an                organic solvent under uniform mixing condition to                obtain a solution (i) and dissolving citric acid or                glucose in same organic solvent under uniform                mixing condition to obtain a solution (ii), (b)                mixing the solution (i) and the solution (ii), (c)                carrying out hydrothermal reaction, (d)                centrifuging to obtain precipitate, and (e)                freeze-drying to obtain the nanomaterial; and
   (2) the method for photocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide using the nanomaterial, which                involves laying the nanomaterial on a                photocatalytic reactor, purging the reactor with                carbon dioxide gas to discharge the impurity gas in                the reactor to ensure a pure carbon dioxide                environment, introducing carbon dioxide into a                water washing bottle to carry out water washing,                introducing carbon dioxide gas with water vapor                into the reactor, and illuminating the reactor                using 500 W xenon lamp as a light source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The carbon nitride has                  a particle size of 2-5 nm. The carbon sphere has                  a particle size of 0.02-0.04 nm. Preferred                  Method: The carbon nitride is prepared by                  calcining the precursor under an air atmosphere                  at a rate of 4-5 degrees C/minute and a                  temperature of 500-600 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours,                  naturally cooling to room temperature, grinding,                  dissolving the cooled sample in water, carrying                  out ultrasonic dispersion, centrifuging to obtain                  precipitates, and freeze-drying. Preferred                  Composition: The concentration of carbon nitride                  in the solution (i) is 100-300 mg/ml. The mixed                  volume ratio of the solution (i) to the solution                  (ii) is 4-6:1. Preferred Conditions: The                  hydrothermal reaction step (c) is carried out at                  195-205 degrees C for 20-30 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon nitride is                  prepared from precursor urea, melamine or                  cyanamide. The organic solvent is                  ethylenediamine, ethylene glycol or acetone.                  Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  citric acid or glucose in the solution (ii) is                  20-30 mg/ml.

PN CN111036275-A; CN111036275-B
TI Preparing oxygen-doped graphite phase carbon            dioxide nitrogen photolysis hydrogen production            catalyst involves adding melamine to amide organic            solvent and stirring at room temperature to obtain            sample A.
AU LI W
   XU H
   GUAN Q
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
   UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 2020347113
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oxygen-doped graphite phase carbon                dioxide nitrogen photolysis hydrogen production                catalyst involves adding melamine to the amide                organic solvent and stirring at room temperature                for 15 minutes to obtain sample A, where mass ratio                of melamine and amide organic solvent is 1:3-8. The                sample A is transferred to a hydrothermal reaction                kettle, reacted at a constant temperature at                120-200 degrees C for 2-24 hours, cooled to room                temperature and dried at 80 degrees C to obtain                sample B. The sample B is dispersed in distilled                water, polysaccharide is added under continuous                stirring, stirred at room temperature for 4 hours,                and dried at 80 degrees C to obtain sample C, where                mass ratio of sample B to polysaccharide is                1:0.005-0.1. The sample C is heated to 500-550                degrees C in a tube furnace at a temperature                increase rate of 5 degrees C/minute under a flowing                inert atmosphere, and maintained for 1-5 hours,                cooled, sample D is obtained by grinding. The                volumetric space velocity of the gas is 600-1200                per hour.
   USE - Method for preparing oxygen-doped graphite                phase carbon dioxide nitrogen photolysis hydrogen                production catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare oxygen-doped                graphite phase carbon dioxide nitrogen photolysis                hydrogen catalyst, which increases light absorption                range of the catalyst and improves the separation                of charge carriers, has synergistic effect,                realizes formation of a large delocalized bond with                the adjacent heptazine ring, improves light                absorption and charge separation of the catalyst,                greatly improves photocatalytic hydrogen production                activity, has low risk, is environment friendly,                and has good practical application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing oxygen-doped graphite phase carbon                dioxide nitrogen photolysis hydrogen production                catalyst involves adding melamine to the amide                organic solvent and stirring at room temperature                for 15 minutes to obtain sample A, where mass ratio                of melamine and amide organic solvent is 1:3-8. The                sample A is transferred to a hydrothermal reaction                kettle, reacted at a constant temperature at                120-200 degrees C for 2-24 hours, cooled to room                temperature and dried at 80 degrees C to obtain                sample B. The sample B is dispersed in distilled                water, polysaccharide is added under continuous                stirring, stirred at room temperature for 4 hours,                and dried at 80 degrees C to obtain sample C, where                mass ratio of sample B to polysaccharide is                1:0.005-0.1. The sample C is heated to 500-550                degrees C in a tube furnace at a temperature                increase rate of 5 degrees C/minute under a flowing                inert atmosphere, and maintained for 1-5 hours,                cooled, sample D is obtained by grinding. The                volumetric space velocity of the gas is 600-1200                per hour. The sample D is heated to 450-500 degrees                C at a temperature increase rate of 3 degrees                C/minutes under a flowing air atmosphere and                maintained for 1 hour to obtain an oxygen-doped                graphite-phase nitrogen carbide photolysis hydrogen                production catalyst. The volume space velocity of                air is 600-1200 per hour. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are                included for the following:
   (1) an oxygen-doped graphite phase carbon                dioxide nitrogen photolysis water production                hydrogen catalyst; and
   (2) a method for using oxygen-doped                graphite-phase nitrogen carbide photohydrolysis                hydrogen production catalyst, which involves using                oxygen-doped graphite phase carbonitride carbon                dioxide photolysis hydrogen production catalyst as                active material, dispersing in water, adding                sacrificial agent and chloroplatinic acid                (H2PtCl6), where mass ratio of catalyst to water is                1: 1000-2000, the mass ratio of catalyst to                sacrificial agent is 1: 100-500, mass ratio of                catalyst to H2PtCl6 is 1:0.01-0.1, the sacrificial                agent is one of triethanolamine and methanol,                vacuuming the photolysis water hydrogen production                system, using constant temperature circulating                water to maintain the temperature of the reaction                system, and under the irradiation of a xenon lamp                with a filter, decomposing the water into hydrogen,                where temperature is 5-8 degrees C, power of the                xenon lamp is 150-500 Watt, the cut-off wavelength                of the filter is 420 nanometer, testing generated                gas using online TCD gas chromatography with a                sampling interval of 1 hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amide organic                  solvent is N, N-dimethylformamide, N,                  N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylformamide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone, N, N'-methylenediacrylamide.                  The inert atmosphere is one of nitrogen, argon                  and helium. The polysaccharide is one or more of                  dextran, chitosan, poria cocos, and konjac                  mannan.

PN WO2018115596-A1; FI201606030-A
TI Manufacturing methanol from organic waste, involves producing biogas from organic waste in a biogas reactor via anaerobic digestion, enriching biogas by concentration equipment, and producing syngas in a syngas reactor.
AU RAUTIANEN M
   LEHTOMAKI A
   LEHTOMAEKI A
   RAUTIAINEN M
AE AVEFORI OY (AVEF-Non-standard)
GA 201851594T
AB    NOVELTY - Methanol is manufactured from organic waste by producing biogas from organic waste in a biogas reactor (14) via anaerobic digestion; enriching biogas by concentration equipment (16); producing syngas containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen in a syngas reactor (18) applying a high temperature and pressure required by the manufacture of syngas; producing methanol in a methanol synthesis reactor (26) utilizing a methanol synthesis catalyst; decreasing the temperature of manufactured methanol with a methanol cooler (30) for recovering heat and controlling the temperature of the methanol synthesis reactor; decreasing the temperature of syngas by a syngas cooler (22) for recovering heat; condensing syngas for separating excess water from syngas with a 1st condenser (24); and after decreasing the temperature of methanol in the methanol cooler, condensing mixed gas in a 2nd condenser (32) for separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen of methanol from methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing methanol from organic waste (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Provided is a method with a higher overall economic advantage compared to methods for manufacturing methanol from organic waste, since a methanol synthesis reactor is advantageous as a spiral reactor in terms of manufacture regarding its investment costs. When manufactured from a pipe with a sufficiently small diameter, a spiral reactor can be used without expensive tests related to the pressure vessel regulation, the pipe automatically meeting the regulations. In addition, a pipe bent in a spiral form has a large specific surface area enabling efficient heat transfer. On the other hand, the cross-sectional flow area of a spiral reactor is notably small compared, for instance, to large fixed bed reactors enabling syngas to efficiently collide with the catalyst in the reaction volume thus increasing the yield of the method. In this way, a method with low investment costs and a high yield is achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Methanol is manufactured from organic waste by producing biogas from organic waste in a biogas reactor (14) via anaerobic digestion; enriching biogas by concentration equipment (16); producing syngas containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen in a syngas reactor (18) applying a high temperature and pressure required by the manufacture of syngas; producing methanol in a methanol synthesis reactor (26) utilizing a methanol synthesis catalyst; decreasing the temperature of manufactured methanol with a methanol cooler (30) for recovering heat and controlling the temperature of the methanol synthesis reactor; where a spiral reactor is used as the methanol synthesis reactor, the spiral reactor having a reaction volume arranged from a pipe in a spiral form, the reaction volume having a catalyst arranged in it in the methanol synthesis reactor, and after the syngas reactor and before the methanol synthesis reactor; decreasing the temperature of syngas by a syngas cooler (22) for recovering heat; condensing syngas for separating excess water from syngas with a 1st condenser (24); and after decreasing the temperature of methanol in the methanol cooler, condensing mixed gas in a 2nd condenser (32) for separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen of methanol from methanol. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system (10) for manufacturing methanol from organic waste, including a biogas reactor for producing biogas from organic waste via anaerobic digestion; concentration equipment arranged after the biogas reactor for enriching biogas produced in the biogas reactor; a syngas reactor arranged after the concentration equipment for manufacturing syngas containing carbonmonoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen from the enriched biogas; a methanol synthesis reactor arranged after the 1st condenser for manufacturing methanol from carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen by a methanol synthesis catalyst, where the methanol synthesis reactor is a spiral reactor, the spiral reactor having a reaction volume arranged from a pipe in a spiral form, the reaction volume having a catalyst arranged in it, and where the system further includes, after the syngas reactor and before the methanol synthesis reactor, a syngas cooler for decreasing temperature of syngas; a 1st condenser arranged after the syngas cooler for condensing syngas for separating excess water from carbon monoxide; and after the methanol synthesis reactor, a methanol cooler arranged after the methanol synthesis reactor for decreasing temperature of methanol, and a 2nd condenser arranged after the methanol cooler for separating carbon dioxide and water contained in methanol of the mixed gas from methanol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of system for manufacturing methanol from organic waste.
   Biogas reactor (14)
   Concentration equipment (16)
   Syngas reactor (18)
   Syngas cooler (22)
   1st condenser (24)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: A temperature of 750-950 degrees C, preferably 800-900 degrees C, and a pressure of 15-30 bar, preferably 18-22 bar, are applied in the syngas reactor, when manufacturing syngas using steam reforming. Further, a temperature of 400-1200 degrees C, preferably 700-1000 degrees C, and a pressure of 10-100 bar, preferably 15-40 bar, are applied in the syngas reactor, when manufacturing syngas using the partial oxidation method (POX). A temperature of 250-450 degrees C, preferably 300-400 degrees C, and a pressure of 40-120 bar, preferably 50-100 bar, are applied in the methanol synthesis reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: One of the following is used in its solid state as the catalyst in the methanol synthesis reactor: copper (Cu), zinc oxide (ZnO), and aluminum oxide (Al2O3). The catalyst is in a solid state in the following forms: granules, pellets and/or rings.

PN CN113998720-A
TI Preparing high-white and high-purity aluminium hydroxide in cement building material production raw material, involves crushing to process control fineness of raw material as pre-powder and pre-powder and preparing raw material.
AU LI Z
   ZENG F
AE INNER MONGOLIA HUIYUAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (INNE-Non-standard)
   INNER MONGOLIA MAOSHEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2022306791
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-white and high-purity aluminium hydroxide involves crushing to process control fineness of raw material as pre-powder, pre-powder and preparing raw material, blank forming to obtain blank, blank material sintering to obtain clinker, then clinker to obtain slurry, slurry and slurry, carbon reaction, hydrogen-aluminium separating and drying to prepare high-white high-purity aluminium hydroxide, the raw material comprises fly ash, coal gangue, calcium-containing solid waste, coal powder and soda ash, the blank sintering is the industrial tunnel kiln program heating roasting blank, the roasting temperature range is 800-1100degrees Celsius. The flue gas generated by sintering the blank material is subjected to carbon dioxide recovery and purification treatment, and then performed carbon separation reaction with the fine desic silicon liquid.
   USE - Method for preparing high-white and high-purity aluminium hydroxide used in cement building material production raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high-white and high-purity aluminum hydroxide with reduced carbon emission, saving energy, protecting, and recycling the aluminum element resource in high-silicon low-grade bauxite. The method can realize industrial scale recycling fly ash, coal gangue and other solid waste in the aluminium element resource, realizing fly ash and carbide slag. The production process recycles carbon dioxide and waste heat in the flue gas generated by roasting and is used for preparing aluminium hydroxides. The process is matched with the industrial tunnel kiln energy-saving effect is good, heat can be high-efficient utilization, and the process is easy to construct and install, it is more convenient for industrial scale production and the blank material is in the self-heating roasting process, the generated self-heat
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a high-white high-purity aluminium hydroxide clean production system, comprises a raw material preparing module, blank forming unit, blank sintering unit, clinker dissolving unit, a slurry silicon removing unit, a carbon separation reaction unit, a hydrogen-aluminium separating unit and a drying unit, and carbon dioxide recycling unit connected with the blank sintering unit and the carbon separation reaction unit through the gas conveying pipeline, where the raw material preparation module receives the crushing process control fineness of the raw material of the pre-powder and mixing to obtain the homogenized raw material, the raw material comprises fly ash, coal gangue, calcium-containing solid waste, coal powder and calcined soda, homogenizing the raw material into the blank forming unit for molding to obtain the blank, the blank sintering unit comprises an industrial tunnel kiln, heating and roasting the blank program in the industrial tunnel kiln, the roasting temperature range is 800-1100degrees Celsius, the carbon dioxide recovery unit receives the flue gas generated by the blank sintering unit and performs purification treatment to form carbon dioxide enriched gas and fine desic silicon liquid generated by the slurry desiliconation unit to perform carbon division reaction in the carbon separation reaction unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material further comprises high-silicon bauxite with aluminium-silicon ratio of 0.5-2.5. Preferred Conditions: The blank is sintered from the self-heating blank, and the self-heat is from the coal gangue and the coal powder. The raw material respectively and processed to the fineness of the same material. The method involves heating and drying the blank before sintering the blank, where the drying temperature is in the range of 50-800degrees Celsius after sintering the blank, further comprising cooling the blank after finishing roasting according to the program; the cooling temperature is in the range of 10-40degrees Celsius. The heating and drying speed of the programme is 50-150degrees Celsius per hour, the speed of the programme heating and roasting is 50-150degrees Celsius per hour, and the cooling speed of the programme cooling is 50-200 per hour. The drying time is 2-8 hours, the roasting time is 1-3 hours, and the cooling time is 1-6 hours. The blank is an eight-edge column with through holes in the centre of the blank. The pre-powder materials are mixed and homogenized to obtain the homogenized raw material after being mixed with raw material, where the raw material is prepared into the raw material, the raw material comprises the pre-powder into the raw material middle bin, by metering dosing, the ingredient homogenizing comprises dry homogenizing, dry homogenizing temperature is 30-50degrees Celsius.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN CN111659681-A
TI Butadiene storage tank inner wall cleaning involves manipulating drone to control and spraying chemical agents on inner wall of storage tank.
AU ZOU J
AE DALIAN TIANFAN ANTISEPSIS ENG CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 202092234H
AB    NOVELTY - Butadiene storage tank inner wall cleaning involves manipulating a drone to control and spraying chemical agents on the inner wall of the storage tank. The chemical agent is sprayed to the surface of the metal substrate of the storage tank, which can realize the addition reaction with the residual butadiene and the compound agent to generate acetic acid, carbon dioxide and water with good solubility and stability. The chemical reaction equation of butadiene and potassium permanganate is performed under acidic conditions. The double bond of butadiene is broken to generate formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, and propionaldehyde under acidic conditions in potassium permanganate oxidant to generate carbon dioxide, acetaldehyde oxidizes to generate acetic acid, and a small amount of propionaldehyde generates propionic acid.
   USE - Method for rapidly cleaning inner wall of a butadiene storage tank with a thickening catalytic method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method rapidly cleans the inner wall of butadiene storage tank in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner by eliminating climbing work of professional construction personnel, breaking traditional construction work of hanging baskets, ropes, etc. on the inner walls of storage tanks and spherical tanks for wiping by traditional construction personnel, and replacing traditional tank cleaning and erecting scaffolding along the tank body, and greatly improving safety of the physical and chemical cleaning of the inner walls of oil tanks and spherical tanks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Butadiene storage tank inner wall cleaning involves manipulating a drone to control and spraying chemical agents on the inner wall of the storage tank. The chemical agent is sprayed to the surface of the metal substrate of the storage tank, which can realize the addition reaction with the residual butadiene and the compound agent to generate acetic acid, carbon dioxide and water with good solubility and stability. The chemical reaction equation of butadiene and potassium permanganate is performed under acidic conditions. The double bond of butadiene is broken to generate formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, and propionaldehyde under acidic conditions in potassium permanganate oxidant to generate carbon dioxide, acetaldehyde oxidizes to generate acetic acid, and a small amount of propionaldehyde generates propionic acid. The residence time of the chemical cleaning agent is generally within 5 hours, where the addition reaction product is mainly acetic acid and carbon dioxide. The compound chemical agent comprises descaling and rust removing agent, catalytic reactant, surfactant, defoaming agent, thickener, and passivation agent composition. The chemical composition is sodium polysulfide, benzyltriazole, cocamide acrylic betaine, sodium perborate and potassium permanganate composite powder salt, citric acid, alkyl poly glucoside and fatty acid methyl ester ethoxy compound sulfonate, glucoside and fatty acid methyl ester ethoxy compound sulfonate, thiourea. The composition is a compound chemical mixture liquid that forms a misty mixed droplet after spraying. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a cleaning system, which comprises a tapered roller rotary spray gun and an unmanned aerial vehicle spraying equipment, where the tapered roller rotary spray gun comprises a tapered roller nozzle and a cone connected in sequence.

PN JP2019189870-A
TI Manufacture of hydrated fuel involves mixing raw water modified by stirring and ionizing with ultrasonic wave, fuel base oil modified by applying ultrasonic wave, and additive, injecting air or carbon dioxide, purifying and precipitating.
AU SHIDA H
AE SHIDA H (SHID-Individual)
GA 2019921035
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of hydrated fuel involves modifying soft raw water (40) having hardness of 20 mg/L or less as raw water by stirring, applying ultrasonic wave to raw water, and stimulating ionization by contacting resultant water with a catalyst material which stimulates ionization of water, modifying a fuel base oil (20) by applying ultrasonic wave to the fuel base oil and stirring, introducing the modified raw water, the modified fuel oil base and an additive (50) to a purification stirring vessel (5) to precipitate oxygen, stirring, generating a mixed fluid, injecting a nanobubble of air or carbon dioxide gas through a nanobubble generator (56) into the mixed fluid, purifying the mixed fluid by generating a purified fluid rich in hydrocarbon by reaction of hydrogen ions in water and carbon ions in ionized carbon dioxide, heating and pressurizing, transferring resultant to a sedimentation tank (7), leaving the mixed fluid still, and removing a precipitate of unnecessary substance.
   USE - Manufacture of hydrated fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and rapid continuous industrial manufacture of highly transparent hydrated fuel having excellent long-term storage stability and combustion performance, reduced emission of harmful exhaust gas during combustion, and suppressed cloudiness and change in properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of hydrated fuel involves modifying soft raw water having hardness of 20 mg/L or less as raw water by stirring using a stirring blade (21) at high rotation speed in a water modification tank (3), applying ultrasonic wave to raw water, and stimulating ionization by contacting resultant water with a catalyst material which stimulates ionization of water, modifying a fuel base oil by subdividing molecules of the fuel base oil by applying ultrasonic wave to the fuel base oil and stirring using a stirring blade (30) at high rotation speed in a fuel oil modification tank (2), introducing the modified raw water, the modified fuel oil base and an additive to a purification stirring vessel to precipitate oxygen, stirring with a stirring blade at high rotation speed, generating a mixed fluid, injecting a nanobubble of air or carbon dioxide gas through a nanobubble generator into the mixed fluid, purifying the mixed fluid by generating a purified fluid rich in hydrocarbon by reaction of hydrogen ions in water and carbon ions in ionized carbon dioxide, heating and pressurizing the purified mixed fluid in a heating and pressurization tank (6), transferring resultant to a sedimentation tank, leaving the mixed fluid still, and removing a portion of product comprising a precipitate of unnecessary substance. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for hydrated fuel manufacturing apparatus (1).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the hydrated fuel manufacturing apparatus.
   Hydrated fuel manufacturing apparatus (1)
   Oil modification tank (2)
   Water modification tank (3)
   Purification stirring vessel (5)
   Heating and pressurization tank (6)
   Sedimentation tank (7)
   Fuel base oil (20)
   Stirring blades (21,30)
   Raw water (40)
   Additive (50)
   Nanobubble generator (56)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst material is one or more minerals chosen from tourmaline, obsidian, nacre, rosinite and granite, or one or more metals chosen from aluminum, magnesium, manganese, sodium, zinc, copper, iron, nickel, titanium, calcium and silicon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ionization of the fuel base oil is performed in presence of a catalyst material. Preferred Conditions: The stirring is carried out at 100-800 rpm. The heating and pressurization of the purified mixed fluid is performed at 25-90 degrees C and 2-15 atm. Preferred Component: The additive is one or more chosen from aqueous citric acid, aqueous fumaric acid and aqueous sodium bicarbonate.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN CN109234731-A
TI Laser surface treating tooth surface of roller cone drill bit comprises e.g. degreasing, and dusting tooth surface of the roller cone drill, inspecting, selecting high-power carbon dioxide laser and nickel base alloy powder, and adjusting.
AU ZENG H
AE ZENG H (ZENG-Individual)
GA 2019097870
AB    NOVELTY - Laser surface treating tooth surface of roller cone drill bit comprises e.g. (i) degreasing, dusting and de-rusting the tooth surface of the roller cone drill with a cleaning agent, and cleaning with hydrogen peroxide; (ii) inspecting the tooth surface of roller cone drill bit for ensuring that there are no defects; (iii) selecting high-power carbon dioxide laser, using numerical control machine tool as workbench, selecting the best laser mode by plexiglass burnt method, and using the high-power carbon dioxide laser complete equipment for melting the base alloy; (iv) selecting nickel base alloy powder as the bottom layer of the tooth surface of the roller cone drill bit, regulating powder feeding device, and adjusting the amount of powder; and (v) selecting spherical WC and Ni-Cr-B-Si self-fluxing mixed alloy powder as the working layer of the tooth surface of the roller cone drill bit, regulating powder feeding device, and adjusting the amount of powder.
   USE - The method is useful for laser surface treating tooth surface of roller cone drill bit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical; and strengthens the tooth surface of the roller cone drill bit, increases the service life of the roller cone drill bit, solves the problems of premature abrading and spalling of the roller cone drill bit, and improves the efficiency of the roller cone drill bit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Laser surface treating tooth surface of roller cone drill bit comprises (i) degreasing, dusting and de-rusting the tooth surface of the roller cone bit with a cleaning agent at normal temperature, and cleaning with hydrogen peroxide; (ii) inspecting the tooth surface of roller cone drill bit for ensuring that there are no defects i.e. cracks, pores and inclusions in the part to be processed; (iii) selecting high-power carbon dioxide laser, using numerical control machine tool as workbench, selecting the best laser mode by plexiglass burnt method, and using the high-power carbon dioxide laser complete equipment for melting the base alloy; (iv) selecting nickel base alloy powder as the bottom layer of the tooth surface of the roller cone drill bit, regulating powder feeding device, where the alloy powder from the automatic powder feeding head falls into the laser molten pool, and adjusting the amount of powder to make the thickness of the alloy powder coating reach 2-2.5 mm; and (v) selecting spherical WC and Ni-Cr-B-Si self-fluxing mixed alloy powder as the working layer of the tooth surface of the roller cone drill bit, regulating powder feeding device, where the alloy powder from the automatic powder feeding head falls into the laser molten pool, and adjusting the amount of powder to make the thickness of the alloy powder coating reach 1.6-2.2 mm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel base alloy powder comprises 0.03 wt.% carbon, 21.5 wt.% cobalt, 1.4 wt.% iron, 9 wt.% molybdenum, 0.4 wt.% silicon, 0.3 wt.% manganese, 3.8 wt.% niobium and nickel (remaining amount).
