PN WO2018196832-A1; TW201839130-A; TW690592-B1
TI Preparing Japanese encephalitis vaccine by inoculating and culturing cells for vaccine preparation, inoculating and culturing Japanese encephalitis virus, harvesting influenza virus, purifying and inactivating.
AU KUO T
   SUN C
   WU C C
   WU C
AE MEDIGEN VACCINE BIOLOGICS CO (MEDI-Non-standard)
   MEDIGEN VACCINE BIOLOGICS CORP (MEDI-Non-standard)
GA 2018862733
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a Japanese encephalitis vaccine involves (a) inoculating a cell for producing a vaccine on a cell culture carrier, and culturing in a serum-free medium at 34-37 degrees C and 5-10% carbon dioxide in a tidal culture system, (b) continuously culturing the cells for 3-10 days at 34-37 degrees C and 0-10% carbon dioxide, (c) inoculating Japanese encephalitis virus onto the cells at 34-37 degrees C and 5-10% carbon dioxide, where the multiplicity of infection (M.O.I) is 1x 10-2-1x 10-6, and the virus is inoculated for 1-12 hours, (d) continuously culturing the Japanese encephalitis virus and cells after inoculation in a culture environment having 34-37 degrees C and 0-10% carbon dioxide, and (e) harvesting the virus solution once every 5-8 days, and purifying and inactivating to obtain a Japanese encephalitis deactivated virus vaccine, where the Japanese encephalitis deactivated virus vaccine is stored at 2-8 degrees C for 4 months, and the viral protein cleavage rate is 5% or less.
   USE - The method is useful for producing Japanese encephalitis vaccine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of increasing the stability of the Japanese encephalitis vaccine antigen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing a Japanese encephalitis vaccine involves (a) inoculating a cell for producing a vaccine on a cell culture carrier, and culturing in a serum-free medium at 34-37 degrees C and 5-10% carbon dioxide (CO2) in a tidal culture system, (b) continuously culturing the cells for 3-10 days at 34-37 degrees C and 0-10% CO2, (c) inoculating Japanese encephalitis virus onto the cells at 34-37 degrees C and 5-10% CO2, where the multiplicity of infection (M.O.I) is 1x 10-2-1x 10-6, and the virus is inoculated for 1-12 hours, (d) continuously culturing the Japanese encephalitis virus and cells after inoculation in a culture environment having 34-37 degrees C and 0-10% CO2, and (e) harvesting the virus solution once every 5-8 days, and purifying and inactivating to obtain a Japanese encephalitis deactivated virus vaccine, where the Japanese encephalitis deactivated virus vaccine is stored at 2-8 degrees C for 4 months, and the viral protein cleavage rate is 5% or less. The volume ratio of the cell culture carrier to the serum-free medium is 1:5-1:20, the liquid-free lifting rate of the serum-free medium is 1000-2000 ml/minute, the residence time of the serum-free medium after the liquid level rises completely is 0-60 seconds, the residence time of the serum-free medium after the liquid level is completely lowered is 0-60 seconds, the adjusted CO2 concentration adjusts the pH to 7-7.4, the glucose content is maintained above 1 g/l, and cell inoculation is performed for 1-12 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Cells: The cell for producing a vaccine is African green monkey kidney cells (VERO cells). Preferred Medium: The serum free medium is VP-SFM (RTM: Serum-free media expressly designed for virus production). The serum-free medium is raised to just cover all of the cell culture carriers, and the whole cell culture carrier is completely immersed in the serum-free medium, where the carriers are not suspended in the serum-free medium. The serum-free medium in step (b) comprises 50% or more dissolved oxygen of fresh unused serum-free medium. The serum-free medium in step (d) comprises 40% or more dissolved oxygen of fresh unused serum-free medium. Preferred Method: The residence time of the serum-free medium in the step (a) is 20 seconds after the liquid level rises completely, and the residence time is 5 seconds after the liquid level of the serum-free medium is completely decreased. The residence time of the serum-free medium in the step (b) is 10 seconds after the liquid level rises completely, and the residence time is 30 seconds after the liquid level of the serum-free medium is completely decreased. The residence time of the serum-free medium in the step (c) is 20 seconds after the liquid level rises completely, and the residence time is 5 seconds after the liquid level of the serum-free medium is completely decreased. The residence time of the serum-free medium in the step (d) is 10 seconds after the liquid level rises completely, and the residence time is 30 seconds after the liquid level of the serum-free medium is completely decreased. The serum-free medium in the step (b) is replaced once every 3-5 days in the cell culture before the virus attack. The inactivation treatment is one of an inactivating agent treatment and a heat treatment, where the inactivating agent comprises formaldehyde, paraformaldehyde, beta -propiolactone, and binary ethyleneimine. Preferred Product: The vaccine further comprises a carrier. The vaccine further comprises one or more pathogenic antigens chosen from coxsackievirus, echovirus, poliovirus, enterovirus, hepatitis B virus, Mycobacterium bovis, Corynebacterium diphtheriae, Clostridium tetani, Bordetella pertussis, Haemophilus influenzae, varicella-zoster virus, measles virus, mumps virus, rubella virus, rotavirus, and influenza virus.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Vaccine.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B03 (Other heterocyclics.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Application of adenosine derivatives in human acutemyeloid leukemia cell by suspending human acute leukemia cell strain U937 in sterile DMED culture liquid of inactivated fetal calf serum, putting carbon dioxide, culturing and growing cell.
AU CHENG J
   WANG L
   CUI C
   LIU S
   PAN H
   LIN F
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201885144Q
AB    NOVELTY - Application of adenosine derivatives in human acutemyeloid leukemia cell involves suspending human acute leukemia cell strain U937 in sterile DMED culture liquid of inactivated fetal calf serum, putting carbon dioxide, culturing into culture box, and growing cell; dissolving ortho-topolin riboside (oTR), kinetin riboside (KR), N6-benzyladenosine (BAR) and N6-isopentenyladenosine (iPR) by dimethyl sulfoxide, continuously dissolving with sterile Dulbecco's modified eagle's medium to oTR, KR, BAR and iPR, filtering, and storing; inoculating in logarithmic growth phase into 24-well plate containing Dulbecco's modified eagle's medium culture liquid, setting 8 groups of experiment, putting carbon dioxide, culturing into culture box, and collecting cell; collecting, washing with for fetal bovine serum (FBS)-containing phosphate buffered saline (PBS), incubating cells with blocking antibody, dark culturing with human CD11b-PE antibody, and analyzing by flow cytometry.
   USE - Method for applying adenosine derivatives in human acutemyeloid leukemia cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has strongest drug sensitivity to leukemia cell lines, and significant proliferative inhibition activity in vitro in 60 tumor cell lines of human 9 large tumor lines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Application of adenosine derivatives in human acutemyeloid leukemia cell involves:
   (A) suspending human acute leukemia cell strain U937 in sterile DMED culture liquid of inactivated fetal calf serum, putting 5% carbon dioxide, culturing into culture box at 37 degrees C at relative humidity of 90%, and growing cell to culture bottle bottom area of 70-80%;
   (B) dissolving ortho-topolin riboside (oTR), kinetin riboside (KR), N6-benzyladenosine (BAR) and N6-isopentenyladenosine (iPR) by 0.1% dimethyl sulfoxide, continuously dissolving with sterile Dulbecco's modified eagle's medium to 0.1-30 mu M oTR, KR, BAR and iPR, filtering, and storing at room temperature;
   (C) inoculating 5asterisk104-1asterisk105 cells in logarithmic growth phase into 24-well plate containing 1 ml of Dulbecco's modified eagle's medium culture liquid, setting 8 groups of experiment including group 1 blank control group, group 2 10 mu M oTR group, group 3 of 10 mu M KR, group 4 of 10 mu M BAR and group 5 of 10 mu M iPR, putting 5% carbon dioxide, culturing into culture box at 37 degrees C for 36 hours at relative humidity of 90%, and collecting cell;
   (D) collecting, washing with for 0.2% fetal bovine serum (FBS)-containing phosphate buffered saline (PBS) 3 times, incubating cells with blocking antibody at room temperature for 15 minutes, dark culturing with human CD11b-PE antibody at 4 degrees C for 30 minutes, and analyzing by flow cytometry; and
   (E) collecting, washing with for 0.2% FBS-containing PBS 3 times, fixing glass slide with methanol, dyeing with Wright-Giemsa stain dyeing solution for 20 minutes, rinsing with distilled water, drying, and observing using microscope.

PN CN114955992-A
TI Producing hydrogen from blast furnace gas,            involves compressing blast furnace gas raw material gas            by the compressor, and then washing with water/alkali            washing to obtain the pretreated blast furnace gas, and            compressing pretreated blast furnace gas again.
AU LI F
AE SHANGHAI FUXI YOUHAO ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B65082
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen from blast furnace gas,                involves (a) compressing blast furnace gas raw                material gas by the compressor, and then washing                with water/alkali washing to obtain the pretreated                blast furnace gas, (b) compressing the pretreated                blast furnace gas again, and then undergo                detoxification treatment to obtain purified blast                furnace gas, (c) mixing the purified blast furnace                gas and water vapor, sending it to the adiabatic                shift furnace, and change portion of the carbon                monoxide (CO) and water vapor to produce carbon                dioxide and hydrogen, after the cooler and the                liquid separator and removed most of the water                vapor, and (d) passing blast furnace gas after                liquid separation and washing into the absorption                tower, and separating carbon dioxide with a carbon                dioxide absorbent, processing by a cooler and a                liquid separator to obtain relatively dry carbon                dioxide gas, and finally passed into the                desulfurization device After desulfurization to                capture carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen from blast                furnace gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method catalyzes the reaction of lower                concentrations of carbon monoxide and water in                blast furnace gas, also has a longer life and low                manufacturing cost, realizes carbon capture and                blue hydrogen co-production before blast furnace                gas combustion, which improves the added value of                blast furnace gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen from blast furnace gas,                involves (a) compressing blast furnace gas raw                material gas by the compressor, and then washing                with water/alkali washing to obtain the pretreated                blast furnace gas, (b) compressing the pretreated                blast furnace gas again, and then undergo                detoxification treatment to obtain purified blast                furnace gas, (c) mixing the purified blast furnace                gas and water vapor, sending it to the adiabatic                shift furnace, and change portion of the carbon                monoxide (CO) and water vapor to produce carbon                dioxide and hydrogen, after the cooler and the                liquid separator and removed most of the water                vapor, (d) passing blast furnace gas after liquid                separation and washing into the absorption tower,                and separating carbon dioxide with a carbon dioxide                absorbent, processing by a cooler and a liquid                separator to obtain relatively dry carbon dioxide                gas, and finally passed into the desulfurization                device, after desulfurization to capture carbon                dioxide, and (e) introducing the remaining gas into                the pressure swing adsorption device, extracting                portion of the hydrogen and pressurize it, storing                it in the high-purity hydrogen collection tank as                high-purity hydrogen, and outputting the remaining                gas as sulfur-free fuel gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The blast furnace                  gas raw material gas comprises 15-30 wt.% carbon                  dioxide, 15-30 wt.% carbon monoxide, 0.1-5 wt.%                  hydrogen, 30.39-60 wt.% nitrogen, 0-1.5 wt.%                  oxygen, 0.002-0.012 wt.% hydrogen sulfide,                  0.002-0.016 wt.% carbonyl sulfide, and the                  balance is water vapor. Preferred Components: The                  tungstate is sodium tungstate and ammonium                  tungstate. The precious metal cation salt is                  platinum nitrate, nickel nitrate, and silver                  nitrate. The washing device is filled with a                  washing agent, and the washing agent is water and                  alkali liquor. The detoxification device contains                  a desulfurizing/dechlorinating agent, and the                  desulfurizing/dechlorinating agent is iron oxide,                  aluminum oxide, sodium carbonate, sodium                  metavanadate, tannin extract and water. The                  carbon dioxide absorbent is an aqueous solution                  of sodium hydroxide, an aqueous solution of                  calcium bicarbonate and water. Preferred                  Conditions: The volume ratio of the blast furnace                  gas to the steam is 1: (0.2-1.5), the volume                  space velocity is 1000-3/(m3.h). The first-stage                  adiabatic shift converter and the second-stage                  adiabatic shift converter are filled with                  catalysts. The reaction conditions of the                  first-stage adiabatic shift converter has inlet                  temperature of 200-380&#8451;, and the                  pressure is 0.3-5.0 MPa. The reaction conditions                  of the second-stage adiabatic shift converter has                  reaction temperature of 200-320&#8451;, and                  the pressure is 0.3-5.0 MPa. Preferred Process:                  The catalyst is prepared by adding tungstate and                  molybdenum hexacarbonyl into a nitric acid                  aqueous solution at room temperature according to                  parts by mass, adding a noble metal cation salt                  into the nitric acid aqueous solution for                  reaction, and centrifugally separating out a                  precipitate, washing and drying to obtain light                  yellow solid powder, heating the solid powder,                  cooling to room temperature to obtain precious                  metal-supported WMoOx catalyst. The                  metal-supported WMoOx catalyst and polyethylene                  glycol are mixed to prepare emulsion slurry by                  wet ball milling method, and spray the emulsion                  slurry onto cordierite honeycomb ceramic carrier                  to form a noble metal-supported WMoOx catalyst                  transition layer, followed by carrying out room                  temperature channel blowing, drying and high                  temperature roasting on the cordierite honeycomb                  ceramic carrier sprayed with the milky slurry to                  obtain the catalyst.

AB    NOVELTY - Method of magnesium sulfate crystallization,                which involves: i. pre-cooling configured magnesium                sulfate solution in the heat exchanger, pumping                pre-cooled magnesium sulfate solution into the                crystallizer, and introducing carbon dioxide gas                into the crystallizer at the same time, and the                crystallizer is carried out by a refrigerant,                maintaining a pressure and temperature in the                crystallizer, after a certain period of time, a                mixture of magnesium sulfate and carbon dioxide                clathrate is formed; ii. discharging magnesium                sulfate mixture through the discharge port at the                bottom of the crystallizer, and the magnesium                sulfate mixture is obtained by solid-liquid                separation to obtain magnesium sulfate solid and                magnesium sulfate mother liquor; iii. magnesium                sulfate mother liquor is returned to the                crystallizer for secondary circulation                crystallization, and recrystallizing magnesium                sulfate solid; iv. discharging carbon dioxide                clathrate is through the upper liquid port of the                crystallizer.
   USE - Method of magnesium sulfate                crystallization
   ADVANTAGE - Method for crystallizing the magnesium sulfate                with the structure improves the eutectic point                temperature of the magnesium sulfate by                pressurizing the system, overcomes the technical                defect of low eutectic freeze crystallization                eutectic point temperature in the prior art, and                has the advantage of low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of magnesium sulfate crystallization,                which involves: i. pre-cooling configured magnesium                sulfate solution in the heat exchanger, pumping                pre-cooled magnesium sulfate solution into the                crystallizer, and introducing carbon dioxide gas                into the crystallizer at the same time, and the                crystallizer is carried out by a refrigerant,                cooling, maintaining a certain pressure and                temperature in the crystallizer, after a certain                period of time, a mixture of magnesium sulfate and                carbon dioxide clathrate is formed; ii. discharging                magnesium sulfate mixture through the discharge                port at the bottom of the crystallizer, and the                magnesium sulfate mixture is obtained by                solid-liquid separation to obtain magnesium sulfate                solid and magnesium sulfate mother liquor; iii.                magnesium sulfate mother liquor is returned to the                crystallizer for secondary circulation                crystallization, and recrystallizing magnesium                sulfate solid; iv. discharging carbon dioxide                clathrate is through the upper liquid port of the                crystallizer, and washing carbon dioxide clathrate                and decomposing to obtain carbon dioxide gas and                pure water; v. obtained carbon dioxide gas is                returned to the crystallizer for reuse, and pure                water is used as a cooling medium for pre-cooling                the heat exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method                for crystallizing magnesium sulfate. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Magnesium sulfate                  content in the magnesium sulfate solution is                  1.0-17.3%. Preferred Method: Method has heat                  exchanger is a tubular heat exchanger, the                  refrigerant of the heat exchanger is ice water,                  and the pre-cooling temperature is 5-10 degreesC.                  Crystallizer is disk tower crystallizer (CDCC) or                  scraped wall cooling crystallizer (SCWC), etc.                  Refrigerant of the crystallizer is ethylene                  glycol, and the CO2 gas flows from the                  crystallizer bottom access. In step (1), the                  temperature is 0-6.5 degreesC, the pressure is                  1.0-4.0 megapascal, magnesium sulfate mixture is                  meridianiite, and the carbon dioxide clathrate                  floats upward. The magnesium sulfate mother                  liquor is added from the upper feeding port of                  the crystallizer, the magnesium sulfate solid is                  magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, and the                  recrystallization conditions are a temperature of                  2-10 degreesC and normal pressure. The carbon                  dioxide clathrate decomposes the dry ice by                  reducing the pressure. The washing equipment is a                  washing tower. Solid-liquid separator is a                  hydrocyclone. The carbon dioxide clathrate is a                  mixture of dry ice and ice, and the molecular                  formula is carbon dioxide hptahydrate                  (CO2.7.3H2O).

PN CN110714321-A
TI Performing supercritical treatment-microwave radiation on flax staple fiber, by adding peroxyacetic acid to staple fiber, adding sodium periodate, flushing with carbon dioxide, modifying by acrylic grafting and subjecting to microwave.
AU TIAN L
AE TIAN L (TIAN-Individual)
GA 202009938A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing supercritical treatment-microwave radiation on modified flax staple fiber, involves (a) adding peroxyacetic acid to flax staple fiber, adjusting pH using sodium hydroxide and acetic acid, washing the peracetic acid-treated flax staple fiber with water, adding oxidant comprising sodium periodate solution to the treated flax staple fiber, oxidizing, soaking in glycerol solution, washing with water and drying, (b) adding the oxidized pre-treated flax staple fiber to an autoclave, flushing with carbon dioxide gas, removing air in the kettle, filling with carbon dioxide, and rapidly opening the gas release valve to release the carbon dioxide gas in the foaming kettle, and (c) performing acrylic grafting flame-retardant modification treatment on obtained supercritical carbon dioxide treated flax staple fiber, packaging and sealing using a polyethylene film bag, adding to a microwave device, processing, removing the flax staple fiber and cooling.
   USE - Method for performing supercritical treatment-microwave radiation on modified flax staple fiber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for performing supercritical treatment-microwave radiation on modified flax staple fiber, involves (a) adding peroxyacetic acid to the flax staple fiber in a ratio of 1:10, adjusting the pH value to 5 using sodium hydroxide and acetic acid, treating for 1-2 hours, washing the peracetic acid-treated flax staple fiber with water, adding oxidant sodium periodate solution to the treated flax staple fiber, oxidizing at 45-50 degrees C for 50-60 minutes under light-avoiding condition, soaking in glycerol solution for 40-50 minutes, uniformly washing with water, and drying to obtain oxidized pre-treated flax staple fiber, (b) adding the oxidized pre-treated flax staple fiber to an autoclave, flushing with carbon dioxide gas 3-5 times, removing the air in the kettle, filling with carbon dioxide under 20 MPa, maintaining the pressure constant, increasing the temperature of the kettle to 25-30 degrees C, maintaining the temperature for 20-30 minutes, and rapidly opening the gas release valve to release the carbon dioxide gas in the foaming kettle to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide treated flax staple fiber, and (c) performing acrylic grafting flame-retardant modification treatment on obtained supercritical carbon dioxide treated flax staple fiber, packaging and sealing using a polyethylene film bag, adding to a microwave device, processing for 60-70 seconds at a power of 600-700 W, slowly removing the flax staple fiber from microwave device, and cooling to obtain the modified flax staple fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of peroxyacetic acid in step (a) is 10-20%. The addition amount of sodium periodate solution in step (a) is 5-7 g/l. The concentration of glycerol solution is 0.1-0.2 mol/l. Preferred Conditions: The average pressure reduction rate in step (b) is 5-10 MPa. Preferred Method: The step (c) involves performing acrylic grafting flame-retardant modification treatment by adding 4-8 wt.% acrylic acid to 0.2-0.3 mol/l nitric acid solution, continuously stirring, adding supercritical carbon dioxide treated flax staple fiber in a ratio of 1:50, performing grafting reaction at 60-65 degrees C for 1-2 hours, washing using distilled water, adding to 0.1-0.2 mol/l sodium hydroxide alcohol solution, performing alkalization reaction for 30-60 minutes, washing with water to neutral, adding ethanol and aluminum sulfate aqueous solution in a volume ratio of 2:1, stirring for 1-2 hours for salinization reaction, washing using aqueous solution of ethanol to obtain acrylic grafted flame-retardant modified flax staple fiber, where the concentration of aluminum sulfate aqueous solution is 5-10%.

TI Partitioned fireproof battery system for new            energy vehicle, has high pressure cavity that is opened            inside pressure accumulator block, and battery column            whose upper end is communicated with inside of high            pressure cavity.
AU NIE H
AE NIE H (NIEH-Individual)
GA 2022B5605N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a protective case (1) whose                bottom is provided with an isolation plate. The                heat exchange fixing assembly includes a heat                exchange plate. A shock absorption and heat                dissipation core is arranged inside the battery                box. Several battery columns are embedded in the                shock-absorbing and heat-dissipating core at equal                distances. An isolation cover is provided at the                upper end of the battery box, and several                temperature-sensitive crushing rods extending                through the isolation cover and extending to the                outside are provided inside the shock-absorbing and                heat-dissipating core. The temperature-sensitive                breaking rod includes a tempered glass tube, and a                sealing expansion block is arranged inside the                tempered glass tube. A high pressure cavity is                opened inside the pressure accumulator block. The                inside of the high pressure cavity is filled with                carbon dioxide gas. The upper end of the battery                column is communicated with the inside of the high                pressure cavity.
   USE - Partitioned fireproof battery system for new                energy vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively improves the fixing                effect on battery columns by arranging the battery                columns in a battery box in groups and matching                with a damping and heat dissipation core to wrap                the battery columns and reduces the vibration on                the battery columns, when the temperature in the                battery box is rapidly increased due to the short                circuit and fire of the battery columns. The                sealing expansion block is heated and expands in                volume to extrude a toughened glass tube to break                the toughened glass tube, carbon dioxide gas and                fire fighting powder in a high-pressure cavity are                released to effectively extinguish the fire in the                battery box, the fire fighting powder is diffused                more uniformly by matching with a porous damping                and heat dissipation core, an aerosol area is                formed in the battery box to effectively block the                fire, the possibility of spreading the fire is                reduced, a large amount of heat is absorbed by                matching with the high-pressure gas injection to                cool the interior of the battery box and the safety                of the battery is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic external view                of a main structure of the partitioned fireproof                battery system for new energy vehicle.
   1Protection case
   7Inflation protection cover

PN CN114588128-A
TI Preparing cinnamon oil nanoparticles involves            adding chitosan to acetic acid solution, stirring to            prepare chitosan solution, dispersing mesoporous            silicon nanoparticles in absolute ethanol,            ultrasonicating to disperse, and adjusting pH of            solution, and adding silane coupling agent.
AU LEI H
AE UNIV XIANGNAN (UYXI-Non-standard)
GA 2022806719
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cinnamon oil nanoparticles involves                adding chitosan to acetic acid solution and                stirring for 24 hours to prepare chitosan solution,                dispersing the mesoporous silicon nanoparticles                MCM-41 in absolute ethanol, ultrasonicating for 15                minutes to disperse, and adjusting the pH of                solution to 3.0-3.2 with acetic acid, adding silane                coupling agent and stirring at 500 rotations/minute                for 4 hours, adding the chitosan solution, continue                stirring for 24 hours, and then centrifuging,                washing the free chitosan solution on surface with                acetic acid solution, then washing with water and                absolute ethanol until neutral, and drying in an                oven at 57 &#176;C for 6 hours to obtain dry                chitosan-mesoporous silicon nanoparticles CS-MSNs,                putting the nanoparticles into a supercritical                autoclave at 71.5-72 &#176;C, and compressing                the carbon dioxide by the high pressure pump,
   USE - Method used for preparing cinnamon oil                nanoparticles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides nanoparticles, which can                effectively improve the drug loading amount.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cinnamon oil nanoparticles involves                adding chitosan to acetic acid solution and                stirring for 24 hours to prepare chitosan solution,                dispersing the mesoporous silicon nanoparticles                MCM-41 in absolute ethanol, ultrasonicating for 15                minutes to disperse, and adjusting the pH of                solution to 3.0-3.2 with acetic acid, adding silane                coupling agent and stirring at 500 rotations/minute                for 4 hours, adding the chitosan solution, continue                stirring for 24 hours, and then centrifuging,                washing the free chitosan solution on surface with                acetic acid solution, then washing with water and                absolute ethanol until neutral, and drying in an                oven at 57 &#176;C for 6 hours to obtain dry                chitosan-mesoporous silicon nanoparticles CS-MSNs,                putting the nanoparticles into a supercritical                autoclave at 71.5-72 &#176;C, compressing the                carbon dioxide by the high pressure pump and                preheating by the preheater and then feeding into                the autoclave having 7.2-7.5 MPa pressure, and                starting magnetic stirring device to stir for 10-15                minutes, inputting the cinnamon oil into autoclave                from the high-pressure infusion pump and keeping it                for 10-30 minutes, and evacuating the gas in kettle                slowly for 1-30 minutes to obtain the                nanoparticles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: The pH is 3.0,                  temperature in the autoclave is 72 &#176;C                  and pressure is 7.3 MPa. The concentration of                  acetic acid solution is 5 wt.%, chitosan solution                  is 1 wt.%, and acetic acid solution is 5 wt.%.                  The gas in kettle is slowly emptied in 20                  minutes. Preferred Components: The silane                  coupling agent is                  3-glycine-polyoxypropyltrimethoxysilane.

AU FU Y
   FAN Q
   BAGENNA
   LI B
AE INNER MONGOLIA YILI IND GROUP CO LTD (IMYL-C)
GA 202286250F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonated aerated fermented milk beverage                comprises raw milk, stabilizer, and dry ice.
   USE - The beverage is useful for improving                intestinal health of a consumer.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage: has high nutritional value, high                fluidity and refreshing and dense taste due to high                protein content (2.3-3.1%), low viscosity (1560cp)                and high aeration volume multiple (1-3 GV).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                the carbonated aerated fermented milk beverage (1)                preparing fermented milk by: heating the raw milk                to 50-60&#176;C, adding it into chemical tank,                adding the weighed white granulated sugar and                polydimethylsiloxane, stirring with high shear for                15-20 minutes, cooling the material liquid to below                15&#176;C, and adding the flavoring agent;                sterilizing the milk after the powder is dissolved                by pasteurizer at 90-100&#176;C for 200-400                seconds; after sterilization, cooling the milk to                fermentation temperature of 42-44&#176;C,                adding in 50-150U Streptococcus thermophilus and                50-150 U Lactobacillus bulgaricus, stirring for                10-15 minutes to start fermentation, the                fermentation time is 6-8 hours, the end point                acidity is 80-90&#176;T, and the end point pH                value is 4.2-4.4; (2) heating the water for glue                solution to 60-80&#8451;, adding it into glue                solution tank, adding soybean polysaccharide,                pectin and nisin, stirring with high shear for                15-20 minutes, sterilizing the glue solution at                110-120&#8451; for 30-60 seconds and then                cooling to 42-44&#8451;; (3) start stirring to                break the demulsification of the fermented liquid                for 2-3 minutes, mixing the fermented milk and glue                liquid online in a ratio of 5:1-9:1 through a                static mixer, at same time, homogenizing the mixed                liquid again, homogenization condition is                20-40/140-160 bar, and cooling to below                15&#176;C before entering the tank to be                filled; (4) sterilizing at 70-80&#8451; for                20-30 seconds; and (5) cooling the filling sample                to 0-4&#176;C, adding dry ice to the packaging                bottle according to the target volume of carbon                dioxide, mixing the inside of bottle evenly to                fully dissolve the carbon dioxide in the fermented                milk, and allowing to stand to balance the air                pressure in the bottle until it is stable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonated aerated                  fermented milk beverage further comprises white                  sugar, antifoaming agent, edible essence, nisin                  and water. The stabilizer consists 0.2-0.5 wt.%                  pectin and 0.2-0.6 wt.% soluble soybean                  polysaccharide. The antifoaming agent is                  polydimethylsiloxane. The content of the dry ice                  is 0.5-0.7 wt.%. The content of raw milk is 70-90                  wt.%, content of white granulated sugar is 7-9                  wt.%, content of polydimethylsiloxane is                  0.001-0.1 wt.%, content of edible essence is                  0.1-0.3 wt.%, content of nisin is 0.1-0.001 wt.%,                  and water is balance.

PN CN114539060-A; CN114539060-B
TI Preparation of methyl formate involves            dehydrogenating methanol raw material and methanol from            methanol synthesis system after being vaporized and            superheated, cooling and condensing methyl formate and            unreacted methanol, separating and purifying crude            product of methyl formate.
AU ZHAO J
   QUAN Y
   LI Y
   REN J
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202274845P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of methyl formate involves: (1)                dehydrogenating methanol raw material and methanol                from methanol synthesis system after being                vaporized and superheated, cooling and condensing                methyl formate, unreacted methanol and                dehydrogenation tail gas generated by the reaction,                introducing into methyl formate absorption tower,                separating and purifying the crude product of                methyl formate and methanol in the tower to obtain                methyl formate product; (2) directly subjecting the                dehydrogenation tail gas, which is not cooled to                reverse water gas shift reaction; and (3)                compressing the mixed gas after reverse water gas                shift reaction with fresh gas and circulating gas                after purification and dewatering, preheating,                introducing into a methanol synthesis tower,                cooling the synthesis gas discharged from the                tower, purifying the separated crude                methanol.
   USE - Preparation of methyl formate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively utilizes a mixed gas of                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen                produced by methanol dehydrogenation and methanol                cracking, obtains single target product methyl                formate, effectively improves the yield of the                target product methyl formate, and does not                discharge methanol. The production process is                clean.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of methyl formate involves: (1)                dehydrogenating methanol raw material and methanol                from methanol synthesis system after being                vaporized and superheated, cooling and condensing                methyl formate, unreacted methanol and                dehydrogenation tail gas generated by the reaction,                introducing into methyl formate absorption tower,                separating and purifying the crude product of                methyl formate and methanol in the tower to obtain                methyl formate product, refluxing purified methanol                to participate in dehydrogenation, and introducing                the dehydrogenation tail gas mixture in the methyl                formate absorption tower into the methanol                synthesis system; (2) directly subjecting the                dehydrogenation tail gas, which is not cooled to                reverse water gas shift reaction using carbon                dioxide to supplement carbon under the action of a                catalyst; and (3) compressing the mixed gas after                reverse water gas shift reaction with fresh gas and                circulating gas after purification and dewatering,                preheating, introducing into a methanol synthesis                tower, cooling the synthesis gas discharged from                the tower, introducing into a separator, purifying                the separated crude methanol participate in                dehydrogenation, mixing the separated circulating                gas and the fresh gas, and introducing the mixture                to the tower for continuous synthesis                reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (3), 3-6 vol.%                  carbon dioxide is used for carbon supplementation                  in the reverse water gas shift reaction.Preferred Conditions: The temperature of                  vaporizing and superheating is                  200&#177;50&#176;C. Dehydrogenation is                  carried out at 250-300&#176;C and 0.3-0.5                  MPa. Reverse water gas shift reaction is carried                  out at 500-600&#176;C and 0.5-1 MPa. The                  compressed pressure of fresh gas and circulating                  gas is 5-6 MPa. Preheating temperature is                  220-240&#176;C. In step (3), the reaction                  temperature in the methanol synthesis tower is                  250-275&#176;C, and the reaction pressure is                  4.5-5.5 MPa.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  the circulating gas and fresh gas is 2:8.

PN CN114477650-A
TI Waste water treatment process for high concentration, comprises filtering waste water through grid, filtering and introducing into sedimentation tank to settle, filling settled waste water into water distribution pool, introducing wastewater into anerobic active sludge tank for anerobic treatment.
AU QIU S
AE FUJIAN QINGWO ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202276540X
AB    NOVELTY - Treating wastewater comprises filtering wastewater through a grid and 30-60 mesh filter screen, filtering and introducing into a sedimentation tank to settle, filling the settled wastewater into a water distribution pool filled with diluted clean water to fully mix, introducing the wastewater into an aerobic active sludge tank for aerobic treatment for at least 6 hours, and performing aeration treatment, introducing wastewater into the anaerobic active wastewater tank for anaerobic treatment for 8 hours, feeding and filling gas generating particles for generating carbon dioxide at a fixed point, discharging the wastewater after anaerobic treatment by the inclined plate sedimentation tank. The gas generating particle comprises an outer metal shell and an effervescent tablet filled in an inner portion. The metal shell comprises a left hemisphere and a right hemisphere with multiple vent holes, the left hemisphere is hinged with one end of the right hemisphere.
   USE - Waste water treatment process for high concentration, such as sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The process improves the activity of the anaerobic bacteria and improves the waste water processing efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating wastewater comprises filtering wastewater through a grid and 30-60 mesh filter screen, filtering and introducing into a sedimentation tank to settle, filling the settled wastewater into a water distribution pool filled with diluted clean water to fully mix, introducing the wastewater into an aerobic active sludge tank for aerobic treatment for at least 6 hours, and performing aeration treatment, introducing wastewater into the anaerobic active wastewater tank for anaerobic treatment for 8 hours, feeding and filling gas generating particles for generating carbon dioxide at a fixed point, discharging the wastewater after anaerobic treatment by the inclined plate sedimentation tank. The gas generating particle comprises an outer metal shell and an effervescent tablet filled in an inner portion. The metal shell comprises a left hemisphere and a right hemisphere with multiple vent holes, the left hemisphere is hinged with one end of the right hemisphere, the other end is movably connected. The one end of the left hemisphere and the right hemisphere movably set is provided with a locking device for locking the movable end of the two. The gas generating particle is connected with a pull rope. The other end of the pull rope extends out of the anaerobic active sludge tank to be fixed.

PN CN114436296-A
TI Continuous production method for joint preparation            of sodium carbonate and ammonium chloride by sodium            chloride, comprises carrying out co-production of            high-quality sodium carbonate and ammonium chloride            products, obtaining crude product of sodium            bicarbonate, collecting ammonia and recycling.
AU LIU J
   ZUO H
   CHEN X
   ZHANG Y
   PAN M
   HU D
   ZHENG X
   LIU A
   ZHANG L
   WENG J
   XIE Y
   WU H
   WANG W
   YUAN Z
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 202266422K
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous production method for the joint                preparation of sodium carbonate and ammonium                chloride by sodium chloride, comprises taking                saturated sodium chloride aqueous solution as raw                material, ammoniacal liquor as ammonia source, and                carbon dioxide as carbon source, carrying out                co-production of high-quality sodium carbonate and                ammonium chloride products, dissolving sodium                chloride in high-purity water ultrasonically to                prepare a supersaturated sodium chloride aqueous                solution, pumping ammonia and carbon dioxide into                the gas-liquid reactor by the metering pump,                entering the reaction zone for reaction after                preheating and mixing, controlling the reaction                temperature by an external circulating heat                exchange system, controlling the amount of material                feeding by adjusting the flow rate, and controlling                the residence time of the material mixing reaction                by changing the inner diameter of the reactor pipe                to 0.5-10 mm and the volume to 8-15 ml.
   USE - Continuous production method for the joint                preparation of sodium carbonate and ammonium                chloride by sodium chloride
   ADVANTAGE - The process has high continuous degree, high                atom utilization rate, high separation efficiency,                high product purity, no generation of three wastes,                realizing efficient synergistic value-added                conversion path of waste salt quality purification                to ammonium alkali, changing the waste salt into                valuable industrial grade product, reducing the                pollution to the environment, which is good for                sustainable development of ecological environment.                The gas-liquid reactor has a small reaction space                and large specific surface area of the structure                characteristic, can strengthen mass transfer and                heat transfer, accurately control the reaction                temperature and reaction time, preventing the                phenomenon of flying temperature and generation of                by-product, improving conversion rate and yield. At                the same time, the micro-channel reactor has large                liquid holding amount, short reaction residence                time, strong mass transfer, heat transfer effect,                no dead volume, high safety, waste no waste liquid                is generated in the processing process, waste                residue, the waste salt can be completely converted                into useful components, the alkali salt separation                effect is high, the product purity is high, the                purpose of resource maximization recycling and                utilization is reached.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating                the continuous production method for the joint                preparation of sodium carbonate and ammonium                chloride by sodium chloride (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises obtaining the crude product of sodium                  bicarbonate through crystallization, filtration,                  washing, carrying out high temperature                  calcination to obtain sodium carbonate product,                  collecting calcined carbon dioxide gas and                  recycling into gas-liquid reactor, where the                  mother liquor after filtration is the aqueous                  solution containing ammonium chloride and sodium                  chloride, precipitating ammonium chloride crude                  product through low temperature crystallization,                  chemical sublimation, refining into ammonium                  chloride product through recrystallization, so                  that the sodium chloride impurity content in it                  is reduced to below 2%, and collecting the                  ammonia released after crystallization of                  ammonium chloride and chemical sublimation and                  recycling to the above-mentioned gas-liquid                  reactor, where the residual liquid after the                  low-temperature crystallization and                  recrystallization is an aqueous solution in which                  sodium chloride and ammonium chloride are                  dissolved, which is applied to the                  above-mentioned gas-liquid reactor                  cyclically.

GA 202227725J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a metal-semiconductor hybrid                structure involves introducing, under first                conditions, a first precursor comprises a metal                from group 11-group 14 to an amine and an anion                precursor to form a semiconductor nanoparticle that                comprises group 11-group 14 metal, introducing,                under second conditions, a second precursor                comprising a metal from group 7-group 11 to the                semiconductor nanoparticle to form a                metal-semiconductor mixture, the metal of the first                precursor is the same or different from the metal                of the second precursor and introducing the                metal-semiconductor mixture to separation                conditions to produce the metal-semiconductor                hybrid structure.
   USE - Method for producing a metal-semiconductor                hybrid structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve processes for                producing metal-semiconductor hybrid structures                that has the ability to control the size of the                metal nanoclusters, is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a metal-semiconductor hybrid structure                which comprises a first component comprising a                metal from group 11-group 14 and an element from                group 15-group 16; and a second component                comprising a metal from group 7-group 11, where the                group 11-group 14 metal of the first component is                the same or different from the group 7-group 11                metal of the second component, an average size of                the first component is from 3-20 nm as determined                by transmission electron microscopy, and an average                size of the second component is from 0.5-3 nm as                determined by transmission electron microscopy;                and
   (2) a method for converting carbon dioxide to                a conversion product which involves:
   a. introducing carbon dioxide to a                metal-semiconductor hybrid structure, the                metal-semiconductor hybrid structure comprising a                first component comprising a metal from group                11-group 14 and an element from group 15-group 16;                and a second component comprising a metal from                group 7-group 11, where the group 11-group 14 metal                of the first component is the same or different                from the group 7-group 11 metal of the second                component, an average size of the first component                is from 3-20 nm as determined by transmission                electron microscopy, and an average size of the                second component is from 0.5-3 nm as determined by                transmission electron microscopy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anion precursor                  comprises an element from group 15-group 16. The                  metal-semiconductor hybrid structure further                  comprises the group 15-group 16 elements. The                  group 15-group 16 elements comprises Sulfur (S),                  Selenium (Se), Tellurium (Te), Phosphorus (P), or                  its combination. The metal of the first precursor                  is different from the metal of the second                  precursor. The group 11-group 14 metal of the                  first precursor comprises Cadmium (Cd), Zinc                  (Zn), copper (Cu), lead (Pb), Silver (Ag),                  mercury (Hg), Indium (In), or its combination.                  The group 7-group 11 metal of the second                  precursor comprises ruthenium (Ru), rhodium (Rh),                  palladium (Pd), silver (Ag), osmium (Os), iridium                  (Ir), platinum (Pt), gold (Au), or its                  combination. The group 7-group 11 metal of the                  second precursor comprises cobalt (Co), nickel                  (Ni), iron (Fe), or its combination. The group                  11-group 14 metal of the first precursor                  comprises cadmium (Cd), the group 7-group 11                  metal of the second precursor comprises gold                  (Au), palladium (Pd), or platinum (Pt) or its                  combination. The amine is an alkylamine. The                  alkylamine comprises tetradecylamine (TDA),                  oleylamine (OLA), octadecylamine (ODA),                  hexadecylamine (HDA), dodecylamine (DDA), and its                  combination. The metal-semiconductor hybrid                  structure comprises a first component comprising                  the group 11-group 14 metal, a second component                  comprising the group 7-group 11 metal, and the                  second component is on a surface of the first                  component. The average size of the first                  component is from 3-20 nm, as determined by                  transmission electron microscopy; an average size                  of the second component is from 0.5-3 nm, as                  determined by transmission electron microscopy;                  or its combination. The first component has an                  average surface area from 28-1300 nm2; the second                  component has an average surface area from 0.7-29                  nm2, or its combination.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical                synthesis or preparations that do not support the                claims.

AE SHANGHAI BIOHEART BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202231762D
AB    (CN215839741-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a degradable blood                vessel bracket system, comprising a bracket, the                bracket comprises a bracket base body, a medicine                coating set on the bracket base body, the material                of the bracket base body is biodegradable material;                sacculus transportation system, the sacculus                conveying system transports the bracket to the                lesion; the sacculus transportation system                comprises a sacculus, the state of the sacculus and                the bracket base body can be connected or separated                by changing the state of the sacculus and the                bracket base body. The bracket in the vascular                stent system in the technical solution is made of                degradable material and carrying drug layer, which                not only can realize the radial expansion and                support effect of the blood vessel, but permanently                can release the drug, reaches the effect of                long-term treatment of the injury part, and                gradually repairing and improving the blood vessel,                the bracket is gradually degraded, generating the                carbon dioxide and water which can be absorbed by                human body, avoiding the bracket remaining in the                body for a long time, avoiding secondary damage                caused by vascular restenosis or complications and                so on.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                preparation method of vascular stent system, which                involves: (a) preparing bracket; (b) preparing                balloon transportation system; (c) pressing bracket                on sacculus of sacculus transportation                system.
EA    NOVELTY - Vascular stent system comprises stent matrix,                where stent comprises drug coating arranged on                stent matrix, and material of matrix is                biodegradable material; sacculus transportation                system transports bracket to lesion. The sacculus                transport system includes sacculus. The state of                sacculus and bracket base component is connected or                separated by changing status of sacculus and base                component of bracket.
   USE - Vascular stent system used for knee                treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The vascular stent system is made of                degradable material and carrying drug layer, which                realize radial expansion and support effect of                blood vessel; permanently release drug, reaches                effect of long-term treatment of injury portion,                and gradually repairing and improving blood                vessels, where bracket is gradually degraded,                generate carbon dioxide and water which is absorbed                by human body, avoids bracket remaining in body for                long time, avoids secondary damage caused by                vascular restenosis or complications.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: The internal                  degradation time of stent matrix is 24-48 months.                  The time for drug release of 95 wt.% in drug                  coating is &#8805; 90 days. The beam width of                  supporting beams is 0.1-0.3 millimeter (mm), the                  height difference between adjacent peaks and                  valleys is 0.3-0.8 mm, the vertical distance                  between two support beams adjacent to each other                  is 0.5-0.9 mm, the radian corresponding to peak                  is &#960;/4-5 &#960;/6, and radian                  corresponding to concave valley is &#960;/4-5                  &#960;/6. The Shore hardness of raw material                  for section A is 70-75 D, Shore hardness of raw                  material for section B is 66-72 D, Shore hardness                  of raw material for section C is 50-55 D, and                  length ratio of section A, section B and section                  C is (10-15): (1-3): (0.8-2). The hydrophilic                  coating has coefficient of friction of                  0.01-0.02.Preferred Components: The drug in drug                  coating is selected from at least one of                  rapamycin, sirolimus, tacrolimus, everolimus,                  leflunomide, M-dehydrocortisol, dexamethasone,                  cyclosporine, mycophenolic acid, mizoribine,                  tranilast, zotarolimus, actinomycin,                  methotrexate, angiostatin, vincristine,                  mitomycin, statins, and probucol. TI Manufacturing purifying liquefied natural gas by using methane involves selecting fermentation strain for cultivation, preparing cultivated fermentation strain into fermentation liquor, adding raw material for preparing biogas.
AU ZHANG P
AE ZHANG P (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2022068047
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing purifying liquefied natural gas by using methane involves selecting fermentation strain for cultivation, preparing cultivated fermentation strain into fermentation liquor, adding raw material for preparing biogas into closed anaerobic fermentation tank, adding the fermentation liquor into the anaerobic fermentation tank, extruding repeatedly the raw materials by using extruding equipment during fermentation of the raw material to ensure that liquid in the raw materials can be fully exuded from the raw material, waiting for fermentation of the raw material, generating biogas after fermentation of the raw material, and introducing the biogas into a desulfurizing tower.
   USE - Method for manufacturing purifying liquefied natural gas by using methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has effectively uses the raw materials, greatly improve the output of biogas, and thus improve the output of liquefied natural gas, and when the raw materials are stacked to ferment biogas, the raw materials can be fully stacked, which greatly improves the efficiency of biogas production. The space utilization rate is improved, and the output of biogas and liquefied natural gas is further improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing purifying liquefied natural gas by using methane involves selecting fermentation strain for cultivation, preparing cultivated fermentation strain into fermentation liquor, adding raw material for preparing biogas into closed anaerobic fermentation tank, adding the fermentation liquor into the anaerobic fermentation tank, extruding repeatedly the raw materials by using extruding equipment during fermentation of the raw material to ensure that liquid in the raw materials can be fully exuded from the raw material, waiting for fermentation of the raw material, generating biogas after fermentation of the raw material, introducing the biogas into a desulfurizing tower, desulfurizing the biogas through the desulfurizing tower, introducing the desulfurized biogas into a compression device for compression, introducing the compressed gas into a water washing tower for removing carbon dioxide in the biogas, introducing the biogas without the carbon dioxide into drying equipment for drying, releasing the pressure of the compressed biogas to produce biogas, introducing the biogas into heat exchange equipment for cooling, compressing, and liquefying the cooled biogas through compression equipment to obtain a liquefied natural gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a manufacturing purifying liquefied natural gas by using methane. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The packing layer of the desulfurization tower is iron oxide desulfurizer. The drying equipment is an adsorption tower filled with hydrated aluminosilicate. Preferred Process: The method for selection of fermented strains is cultivated, and the fermented strains after the cultivation are prepared into fermentation broth further involves adjusting the concentration of the fermentation broth, so that the concentration of the fermentation broth is adjusted to between 10-28%. The method for adding the raw materials for making biogas into the closed anaerobic fermentation tank, and adding the fermentation liquid to the anaerobic fermentation tank, further involves adjusting the internal temperature of the anaerobic fermenter to between 20-50degrees Celsius through a heating device, adjust the pH value inside the anaerobic fermentation tank to 6.5-7.5. The method for raw material is fermented, the raw material is repeatedly squeezed by the extrusion equipment, so that the liquid in the raw material can be fully exuded from the raw material, further involves smoothing the extruded raw materials with a scraper, so that the extruded raw materials are flat and stacked. The method for biological natural gas is introduced into the heat exchange equipment for cooling, and the cooled biological natural gas is compressed and liquefied by the compression equipment to form liquefied natural gas further involves passing liquefied natural gas into gas storage equipment for storage.

PN CN113801672-A
TI Method to improve production process of internal            hot-rolled coke furnace, involves filling combustion            chamber with carbon dioxide gas, introducing fuel gas            as semi-coke tail gas and pure oxygen as            combustion-supporting gas, and heating semi-coke            furnace using combustion flame.
AU NING P
   YANG P
   ZHAO Q
   YANG H
   YANG F
   TIAN S
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2022040974
AB    NOVELTY - The method to improve the production process                of an internal hot-rolled coke furnace, involves:                filling a combustion chamber with carbon                dioxide(CO_2) gas; introducing fuel gas as                semi-coke tail gas and pure oxygen as                combustion-supporting gas, heating semi-coke                furnace using combustion flame, mixing the                combustion flue gas with CO_2; cooling one part of                semi-coke tail gas, and separating the other part                to obtain CO_2, introducing CO_2 to a red carbon                collecting tank at the lower part of the semi-coke                furnace, pre-heating to 150-350 degrees Celsius via                red carbon and automatically rising to the                combustion chamber, so that the reaction degree of                pure oxygen and high-calorific-value gas is                controlled, and cooling the red carbon to 80-150                degrees Celsius; and post-treating the low CO_2                semi-coke tail gas after separating CO_2 to obtain                semi-coke tail gas with high calorific value and                low nitrogen, where the excess CO_2 gas can be                directly discharged or liquefied for use.
   USE - Method for improving the production process of                an internal hot-rolled coke furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls the length of flame of the                four-nozzle opposite jet combustion in the                combustion chamber, prevents the inner wall of the                semi-coke furnace from being damaged by overlong                flame, reduces the reaction intensity of fuel gas                and combustion-supporting gas and protects the                blanking grid from high-temperature melting. The                CO_2 gas rises to the combustion chamber, and has                preheating and heat preservation effects on the                combustion chamber and a combustion layer, so that                the use amount of fuel gas is reduced, and the                problem that high-temperature sensible heat of the                semi coke cannot be utilized is solved. The CO_2                gas is separated from the tail gas generated in the                semi-coke production, and is cheap and easy to                obtain. The CO_2 gas is beneficial to the                low-temperature carbonization process of coal in                the carbonization chamber, and higher yield of                semi-coke can be obtained.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow diagram of                the method to improve the production process of an                internal hot-rolled coke furnace. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

PN CN112403466-A
TI Preparing core-shell catalyst used for dry            reforming of methane carbon dioxide, by dispersing            cobalt hydroxide and ferrous hydroxide nanosheets and            metal in deionized water, centrifuging, washing by            deionized water, drying and roasting.
AU SUN Y
   WANG H
   ZHANG L
   MA C
   DU Y
AE SHANGHAI CURUI LOW-CARBON ENERGY TECHNOL (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202123707S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing core-shell catalyst,                involves (a) dissolving calcium oxide and cobalt                source in deionized water, stirring and reacting,                filtering and separating out product cobalt(II)                hydroxide nanosheet, washing and drying, and                dissolving calcium oxide and iron source in                deionized water, stirring and reacting, and                filtering and separating out product ferrous                hydroxide nanosheet, washing the product, and                drying, (b) dispersing cobalt(II) hydroxide                nanosheet, ferrous hydroxide nanosheet and metal                source M in deionized water, uniformly stirring,                transferring the solution to hydrothermal kettle                for reaction, centrifuging, washing the product                with deionized water, and drying to obtain                cobalt-iron-metal mixed hydroxide, (c) roasting the                obtained cobalt-iron-metal mixed hydroxide in                muffle furnace to obtain cobalt-iron-metal mixed                hydroxide mixed oxide, and (d) reducing temperature                of cobalt-iron-metal mixed hydroxide mixed oxide                under mixed gas atmosphere of hydrogen and                nitrogen.
   USE - The catalyst is used for dry reforming of                methane carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has strong carbon deposition                resistance, effectively inhibits the growth of                active metal particles in high-temperature                reforming reaction, and ensures excellent catalytic                performance, catalytic stability and carbon                deposition resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing core-shell catalyst,                involves (a) dissolving calcium oxide and cobalt                source in deionized water, stirring and reacting in                water bath at room temperature, filtering and                separating out product cobalt(II) hydroxide                nanosheet, washing separated product cobalt(II)                hydroxide nanosheet with deionized water to                neutral, and drying, and dissolving calcium oxide                and iron source in deionized water, stirring and                reacting in water bath at room temperature, and                filtering and separating out product ferrous                hydroxide nanosheet, washing the product ferrous                hydroxide nanosheet with deionized water to                neutral, and drying, (b) dispersing cobalt(II)                hydroxide nanosheet, ferrous hydroxide nanosheet                and metal source M in deionized water, uniformly                stirring, transferring the solution to hydrothermal                kettle for reaction, centrifuging, washing the                product with deionized water, and drying to obtain                cobalt-iron-metal mixed hydroxide, (c) roasting the                obtained cobalt-iron-metal mixed hydroxide in                muffle furnace to obtain cobalt-iron-metal mixed                hydroxide mixed oxide, and (d) reducing temperature                of cobalt-iron-metal mixed hydroxide mixed oxide                under mixed gas atmosphere of hydrogen and nitrogen                to obtain cobalt-iron doped metal-oxide                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (a) involves                  reacting for 12-60 hours, and drying at 80                  degrees C for 12 hours. The step (b) involves                  reacting at 60-180 degrees C for 6-18 hours. The                  step (c) involves roasting at 600 degrees C for 4                  hours. Preferred Components: In step (a), the                  cobalt source is cobalt nitrate, the iron source                  is ferrous nitrate, and the cobalt source and                  iron source are added in same molar amount, where                  the molar ratio of calcium oxide and cobalt or                  iron is (0.4-1.2):1, and the ratio of cobalt or                  iron and deionized aqueous solution is 1                  mmol:(1-4) ml. Preferred Composition: In step                  (b), the molar ratio of cobalt to iron in                  cobalt(II) hydroxide nanosheet and ferrous                  hydroxide nanosheets is 7:3, where the metal                  source M comprises titanium nitrate, aluminum                  nitrate and chromium nitrate, and the molar ratio                  of metal source M and the sum of moles of cobalt                  and iron is (0.5-3):1, and the ratio of molar sum                  of cobalt and iron to deionized water is 1                  mmol:(1-6) ml. In step (d), the concentration of                  hydrogen and nitrogen in mixed gas atmosphere are                  each 50%, and performing reduction at 400-700                  degrees C for 1-8 hours.

TI System for culturing biological cells comprises            culturing vessel comprising top cap and bottom support            unit defining culturing chamber, base assembly, culture            medium source, and control system comprising            sensors.
AU WEGENER C J
   SCHLINKER A
AE FENWAL INC (FENW-C)
   FENWAL INC (FENW-C)
GA 202097062G
AB    NOVELTY - System (10) for culturing biological cells                comprises: (a) a culturing vessel (12) comprising a                top cap (16) and a bottom support unit defining a                culturing chamber (19), the bottom support unit                carrying a gas permeable membrane (18); (b) a base                assembly configured to receive the bottom support                unit in a gas-tight, mating manner, the base                assembly further comprising one or more channels                for delivering to and/or removing from the base one                or more gases; (c) a culture medium source; and (d)                a control system comprising one or more sensors                (48,52,53) for sensing at least one of temperature                or pressure during cell culturing.
   USE - The system is useful for culturing biological                cells; and for automatically controlling conditions                of a biological cell culturing environment (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system may be pre-programmed to deliver                cells, media and supplements to vessel; and control                the conditions of the cell culture including pH,                temperature, % carbon dioxide (CO2) and monitor                cell growth (enumeration) without significant                operator intervention. Alternatively, the system                may be used in a more temporary fashion in                conjunction with more traditional cell culturing                systems. The more automated system and method of                cell culturing do not need to be performed in its                entirety or at all, in a traditional humidity-,                temperature-, and carbon dioxide (CO2)-controlled                incubator; and provide a stand-alone cell culturing                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) an automated method for culturing                biological cells which involves: (a) monitoring                certain conditions within a culturing vessel having                a gas permeable membrane at the base of the vessel;                and (b) automatically adjusting the conditions                based on the monitoring; and
   (2) automatically controlling the conditions                of a biological cell culturing environment which                involves: (a) sensing the conditions of a                biological cell culturing with one or more sensors                at or near the interface (80) of a culturing vessel                and base unit for receiving the vessel; (b)                communicating selected conditions detected by the                sensors to a controller (56) coupled to the base                unit; and (c) adjusting, as necessary, one or more                conditions of the culturing environment based on                the detected selected conditions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                cell culture system.
