PT P
PN CN111574388-A
TI Tranexamic acid prepared by preparing            p-methylbenzoic acid from p-bromotoluene, reacting with            N-bromosuccinimide, carbon tetrachloride and dibenzoyl            peroxide, and reacting with tetraethylammonium bromide            and sodium carbonate.
AU SUN X
   YANG H
   MAO J
AE ANHUI DINGWANG PHARM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202084780S
AB    NOVELTY - A tranexamic acid is prepared by preparing                p-methylbenzoic acid by reacting p-bromotoluene                with magnesium and iodine in tetrahydrofuran, and                reacting with carbon dioxide and hydrochloric acid                solution; reflux reacting p-methylbenzoic acid,                N-bromosuccinimide and carbon tetrachloride in the                presence of dibenzoyl peroxide to prepare first                intermediate; reflux reacting first intermediate                with tetraethylammonium bromide in the presence of                sodium carbonate and deionized (DI) water to obtain                second intermediate; reflux reacting second                intermediate and saturated toluene solution of                ammonia in the presence of activated alumina to                obtain third intermediate; and dissolving third                intermediate in DI water and reacting with                supported nickel catalyst.
   USE - Tranexamic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - Raw materials have low cost, which reduces                tranexamic acid production cost. Preparation method                has high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A tranexamic acid is prepared by:
   (A) adding magnesium powder, iodine powder and                tetrahydrofuran to reaction kettle, dropping                p-bromotoluene under nitrogen protection at 100-200                revolutions/minute (rpm) and 155-160 degrees C,                reflux reacting for 1-2 hours, introducing carbon                dioxide, reacting at 3-5 degrees C for 30-40                minutes, adding hydrochloric acid solution, and                reacting for 1-1.5 hours to obtain p-methylbenzoic                acid;
   (B) adding p-methylbenzoic acid,                N-bromosuccinimide and carbon tetrachloride to                reaction kettle, stirring at 200-300 rpm until                p-methylbenzoic acid and N-bromosuccinimide are                completely dissolved, adding dibenzoyl peroxide,                and reflux reacting at 80-90 degrees C for 2-4                hours to prepare first intermediate;
   (C) adding sodium carbonate and DI water to                reaction kettle, stirring at 200-300 rpm until                sodium carbonate is completely dissolved, adding                tetraethylammonium bromide and first intermediate,                and reflux reacting at 110-120 degrees C for 1.5-2                hours to obtain second intermediate;
   (D) adding second intermediate and saturated                toluene solution of ammonia to reaction kettle,                stirring until second intermediate is completely                dissolved, adding activated alumina, and reflux                reacting at 5-7 MPa and 200-220 degrees C for 15-20                hours to obtain third intermediate; and
   (E) adding third intermediate and DI water to                stirring kettle, stirring at 60-70 degrees C until                third intermediate is completely dissolved, and                reacting with supported nickel catalyst at 0.3-1                MPa, 320-350 degrees C and solution space velocity                of 5-8/hour.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of tranexamic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight-volume                  ratio of magnesium powder, iodine powder,                  tetrahydrofuran, p-bromotoluene and hydrochloric                  acid solution in step (A) is 3:0.1:5:3:2. The                  concentration of hydrochloric acid solution is                  5-10 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight-volume                  ratio of p-methylbenzoic acid,                  N-bromosuccinimide, benzoyl peroxide and carbon                  tetrachloride in step (B) is 1:2:0.5:5. The                  weight-volume ratio of sodium carbonate, first                  intermediate, DI water and tetraethylammonium                  bromide in step (C) is 1:1:5:0.2. The                  weight-volume ratio of second intermediate,                  activated alumina and saturated toluene solution                  of ammonia gas in step (D) is 2:1:5. The                  weight-volume ratio of third intermediate and DI                  water in step (E) is 1:3.

PT P
PN CN111229237-A
TI Preparation of metal-biochar-based hydrogenation            catalyst used for hydrorefining biomass pyrolysis oil,            involves adding template agent to water, adding coffee            grounds, carbonizing, adding obtained product to salt            solution, and reducing.
AU LI X
   LIU T
   SHAO S
   ZHANG H
   YAN J
   GE S
   LV Z
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202051997E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of metal-biochar-based                hydrogenation catalyst involves (i) adding template                agent to deionized water to obtain a template agent                suspension, (ii) adding coffee grounds to deionized                water, stirring to form a flowing slurry, adding                the template agent suspension, stirring, drying,                grinding the obtained product into a powder and                placing in a fixed bed two-stage reactor under                carbon dioxide atmosphere for carbonization to                obtain biochar, (iii) washing the obtained biochar                with dilute hydrochloric acid to remove impurities                and drying to obtain activated carbon, (iv) adding                nickel nitrate and cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate                into deionized water and mixing to obtain a mixed                salt solution, and (v) adding the activated carbon                obtained in step (iii) to the mixed salt solution,                stirring to obtain a gel-like catalyst, aging the                catalyst, drying, calcining the obtained dried                product in a muffle, and reducing the obtained                product using a hydrogen stream at a preset flow                rate.
   USE - Preparation of metal-biochar-based                hydrogenation catalyst used for hydrorefining                biomass pyrolysis oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical, safe and                environmentally-friendly preparation of                metal-biochar-based hydrogenation catalyst. The                capable is capable of improving yield of aromatic                hydrocarbons after deoxygenation and hydrogenation                of biomass pyrolysis products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of metal-biochar-based                hydrogenation catalyst involves (i) adding template                agent to deionized water to obtain a template agent                suspension, (ii) adding coffee grounds to deionized                water, stirring to form a flowing slurry, adding                the template agent suspension, stirring, drying,                grinding the obtained product into a powder and                placing in a fixed bed two-stage reactor under                carbon dioxide atmosphere for carbonization to                obtain biochar, (iii) washing the obtained biochar                with dilute hydrochloric acid to remove impurities                and drying to obtain activated carbon, (iv) adding                nickel nitrate and cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate                into deionized water and mixing to obtain a mixed                salt solution, and (v) adding the activated carbon                obtained in step (iii) to the mixed salt solution,                stirring to obtain a gel-like catalyst, aging the                catalyst, drying, calcining the obtained dried                product in a muffle, and reducing the obtained                product using a hydrogen stream at a preset flow                rate. The template agent is at least one chosen                from sodium alginate, cellulose nanofibers,                cellulose nanocrystals, carbon nanotubes and                hydrotalcite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The usage amount of                  nickel nitrate, cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate                  and deionized water used in step (iv) is 3-9                  g:1-2 g:100 mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The template agent is                  preferably a mixture of sodium alginate and                  cellulose nanofibers. The template agent                  suspension comprises less than 2% template agent.                  The coffee grounds are 30% defatted coffee                  grounds, 60% defatted coffee grounds or defatted                  coffee grounds, preferably defatted coffee                  grounds. The dosage ratio of coffee grounds,                  deionized water and template agent suspension in                  step (ii) is 8-10 g:40-50 mL:30-50 mL. The mass                  ratio of nickel nitrate and cerium(III) nitrate                  hexahydrate in the activated carbon with respect                  to mixed salt solution in step (v) is                  100:12-36:4-8. Preferred Process: The drying                  process in step (ii) is carried out at 110-120                  degrees C for 18-24 hours. The carbonization                  process in step (ii) is carried out at 600                  degrees C for 30-90 minutes. The rate of                  temperature increase of carbonization is 50                  degrees C/minute. The drying process in step                  (iii) is carried out at 110-120 degrees C for                  10-12 hours. The aging process in step (v) is                  carried out for 10-12 hours. The drying process                  in step (v) is carried out at 110-120 degrees C                  for 10-12 hours. The calcination process in step                  (v) is carried out at 550 degrees C for 3-5                  hours. The reduction process in step (v) is                  carried out at a temperature of 350 degrees C for                  3-5 hours at a flow rate of hydrogen stream of                  100 mL/minute.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Device useful for e.g. hydrogen oxidation, hydrogen pumping and formic acid dehydrogenation, comprises porous support substrate and film comprising first oxide layer and an ionic conductor layer, in contact with porous support substrate.
AU SURENDRANATH Y
   YAN B
   SCHREIER M
AE MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
GA 202017405Y
AB    NOVELTY - Device (D1) comprises a porous support substrate, and a film in contact with the porous support substrate, where the film comprises a first oxide layer, and an ionic conductor layer, the oxide comprises tungsten(VI) oxide, tungsten dioxide, molybdenum trioxide, molybdenum dioxide, titanium dioxide, titanium oxide, zinc oxide, zirconium dioxide, zirconium oxide, cerium dioxide, cerium oxide, titanium cerium oxide, yttrium cerium oxide, carbon, vanadium pentoxide, molybdenum disulfide, tungsten disulfide, nickel(II) hydroxide, manganese dioxide, tin dioxide, ferric oxide or chromium oxide (i.e. CrOx, where x is 0.1-3), and the ionic conductor comprises an electrolyte, a polymer membrane or an inorganic compound.
   USE - The device is useful for: hydrogen oxidation (claimed); hydrogen pumping; formic acid dehydrogenation; methanol dehydrogenation; alkene hydrogenation; forming a carbon-carbon bond; silane coupling; producing a hydrocarbon; reducing diatomic nitrogen; producing hydrogen and carbon monoxide; carbon dioxide hydrogenation; producing methanol; isomerizing an alkane; oxygen reduction; cracking methane; producing carbon dioxide and hydrogen; hydrodesulfurization; hydrodenitrogenation; carbon monoxide oxidation; ketone hydrogenation; and dehydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The device catalyzes industrially important chemical transformations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) device (D2) comprising the porous support substrate and the film in contact with the porous support substrate, where the film comprises a first catalyst layer, the first oxide layer and the ionic conductor layer, and the catalyst comprises a metal, a metal nitride, a metal oxide, a metal sulfide, a metal carbide, platinum, palladium, ruthenium, rhenium, cobalt, copper, rhodium, cobalt-molybdenum, nickel-molybdenum, nickel tungsten sulfide, nickel, iron or gold;
   (2) device (D3) comprising the porous support substrate, and the film in contact with the porous support substrate, where the film comprises the first catalyst layer, the first oxide layer, the ionic conductor layer, a second oxide layer and a second catalyst layer and each catalyst comprises a metal, a metal nitride, a metal oxide, a metal sulfide, a metal carbide, platinum, palladium, ruthenium, rhenium, cobalt, copper, rhodium, platinum sulfide, palladium sulfide, rhodium sulfide, cobalt-molybdenum sulfide, nickel-molybdenum sulfide, nickel tungsten sulfide, nickel, iron or gold; and
   (3) hydrogen oxidation, comprising contacting the device (D2) with a source of hydrogen, thus forming a reaction mixture, and applying an electrical potential to the reaction mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ceramic is aluminum trioxide, titanium dioxide, zirconium dioxide or yttria-stabilized zirconia. The metal salt is calcium phosphate. The metal is stainless steel, titanium or silicon. The non-metal is carbon. The first catalyst layer is platinum or palladium, and the first oxide layer is tungsten(VI) oxide. The electrolyte is an aqueous acidic solution, an aqueous neutral solution or an aqueous basic solution. The aqueous electrolyte comprises sodium perchlorate or perchloric acid.    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolyte is a non-aqueous electrolyte comprising an alcohol, an ether, an acetate, a carboxylic acid, a nitrile, a formamide, an acetamide, a sulfoxide, a halogenated hydrocarbon, a ketone or a non-aqueous acid. The non-aqueous electrolyte comprises N-methylpyrrolidone, dimethylcarbonate, ethyl methyl carbonate, propylene carbonate, phosphoric acid, ethylene carbonate, acetonitrile, methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol, isopropanol, acetic acid, trifluoroactic acid, butanoic acid, propanoic acid, dimethyl formamide, dimethylacetatemide, dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran, methyltetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane, trichloroethane, tetrachloromethane, dichloroethane, ethyl acetate, methyl acetate, propyl acetate or acetone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous support substrate has a thickness of 10-50 mu m. The pores of the porous support substrate have an average diameter of 50-1000 nm. The porous support substrate is a polymer, a ceramic, a metal salt, a metal or a non-metal. The polymer is a polycarbonate, polybenzimidazole, a polystyrene, a polyurethane, cellulose, a polyvinyl chloride or latex. The first catalyst layer has a thickness of 1-500 nm. The polymer is tetrafluoroethylene-perfluoro-3,6-dioxa-4-methyl-7-octenesulfonic acid copolymer. The first oxide layer is electrocatalytically inert.

PT P
PN CN110624610-A; CN110624610-B
TI Visible light photocatalyst for synthesizing water gas, comprises low-temperature liquid phase synthesis method, which is used to introduce cobalt into covalent organic framework polymer to synthesize covalent organic framework polymer.
AU BI J
   TAN Z
   NIU Q
   LI L
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202002642G
AB    NOVELTY - Visible light photocatalyst comprises low-temperature liquid phase synthesis method, which is used to introduce cobalt into the covalent organic framework polymer to synthesize a covalent organic framework polymer containing cobalt quantum dots.
   USE - Visible light photocatalyst used used for simultaneous visible light photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and photolysis of water to produce hydrogen, and water gas is synthesized (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The visible light photocatalyst has a good visible light response, and can simultaneously realize visible light photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and photolytic water to produce hydrogen, that is, water gas with a final composition of carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and environmental pollution and energy crisis provide a potential solution, and the synthetic method is simple and convenient, with a wide range of applications, and has great application potential.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a visible light photocatalyst, which involves:
   (A) mixing 30-50 mg of 2,3,6,7,10,11-hexahydroxytriphenyl, 24-50 mg of 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoropyridine-4-carbonitrile, and then adding 0.5-0.9 mL 1,4 dioxane, 0.5-0.9 mL mesitylene, 78-100 microliter of triethylamine to the glass tube in order and then ultrasonicating, and after three times of liquid nitrogen freezing and evacuation, the tube was flame-sealed, heating at 120-140 degrees C for 1-5 days, and then centrifuging and washed with dimethylformamide (DMF);
   (B) adding DMF to soak and wash at room temperature for 1-3 days, and then soaking and washing with distilled water for 1-3 days;
   (C) collecting the solids for Soxhlet extraction, and using tetrahydrofuran and acetone in reflux for 24 hours in order to collect the samples, and after freeze-drying, vacuum drying at room temperature first, and heating to 100-160 degrees C for 15 hours to obtain covalent organic framework polymer;
   (D) weighing 50-60 mg of covalent organic frame polymer and 10-70 mg of cobalt nitrate hexahydrate in a beaker, adding 10-30mL of DMF, heating and stirring at 100-150 degrees C for two days, then washing with acetone, suction filter and wash, and then soaking and washing for 5-6 days; and
   (E) collecting solid and drying under vacuum to obtain a covalent organic framework polymer containing cobalt quantum dots.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The particle size of the cobalt quantum dots contained in the visible light photocatalyst using the covalent organic framework polymer as a precursor is about 5 nanometer. The vacuum drying is performed at 120-140 degrees C for 2 days.

TI Full heat cycle based zero carbon emission cogenerating device, has first stop valve is connected with solution pump, and condenser connected to right port of second stop valve, where second stop valve is connected with fan coil.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a liquefied natural gas pump (8) whose right interface is connected with a first heat exchanger (6). A right interface of the first heat exchanger is connected with a three-way valve (24). An upper interface of the three-way valve is connected with a turbine expansion machine (5) that is connected to a left interface of a second heat exchanger (9). A left interface of the first heat exchanger is connected with a compressor (7) that is connected with a re-heater (3). A right interface of a condenser (19) is connected to a right port of a first stop valve (30). A left interface of the first stop valve is connected with a solution pump (12). The right interface of the condenser is connected to a right port of a second stop valve (29). A left interface of the second stop valve is connected with a fan coil (42).
   USE - Full heat cycle based zero carbon emission cogenerating device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device combines liquefied natural gas vaporization, carbon capture, waste heat recovery and heat pump technology to realize zero carbon emission and cogeneration, and ensures that liquefied natural gas cold energy enters a Brayton cycle system to generate electricity, a turbine to generate electricity by direct expansion process, and a carbon capture system to liquefy carbon dioxide so as to achieve cascade utilization of liquefied natural gas cold energy. The device ensures that vaporized natural gas is driven into the gas turbine to drive a generator and a heat pump to work, so that waste heat generated by the gas turbine is recovered to supply heat, generate electricity or prepare domestic hot water. The device ensures that liquefied natural gas cold energy is utilized to liquefy carbon dioxide to achieve low-cost carbon capture after exhaust gas is separated.

GA 201960480B
AB    NOVELTY - An exhaust gas purifying catalyst (1) has a base material (10) and a catalyst coat layer (11) formed on the surface of the base material. The catalyst coating layer includes upper layer (13) and lower layer (12) with the layer closer to base surface as lower layer and relatively farther layer as upper layer. Upper layer of catalyst coat layer contains rhodium and palladium, and a carrier. Upper layer of catalyst coat layer includes palladium outermost surface layer (15) having high palladium concentration than other portions of the upper layer. Lower layer of catalyst coat layer contains noble metal(s) chosen from palladium, platinum and rhodium, and a support. Mass ratio of palladium with respect to rhodium in upper layer is 0.5-7. In upper layer, the carrier contains 9-23 %mass cerium oxide.
   USE - Catalyst is used for oxidizing hydrocarbon and carbon dioxide contained in exhaust gas discharged from engine of motor vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The exhaust gas purifying catalyst has excellent hydrocarbon decontamination capacity and oxygen storage capacity.

The catalyst coating layer includes upper and lower layers with the layer closer to base surface as lower layer and relatively farther layer as upper layer. Upper layer of catalyst coat layer contains rhodium and palladium, and a carrier. Upper layer of catalyst coat layer includes palladium outermost surface layer having high palladium concentration than other portions of the upper layer. Lower layer of catalyst coat layer contains noble metal(s) chosen from palladium, platinum and rhodium, and a support. In upper layer, 65 %mass or more palladium contained in palladium outermost layer is present in the layer from the surface of the palladium outermost layer relatively far to the substrate surface to the thickness of 50% of the upper layer. Mass ratio of palladium with respect to rhodium in upper layer is 0.5-7. In upper layer, the carrier contains 9-23 %mass cerium oxide.

PN CN109644991-A
TI Processing frozen sample in combination with laser technology without osmotic protective agent comprises e.g. cell sample freezing by removing the cell sample from the cell buffer, immersing in the freezing solution, and transferring.
AU JIN B
   LIU X
AE LIMENG HYPOTHERMAL MEDICINE SHENZHEN CO LTD (LIME-Non-standard)
GA 201940684Q
AB    NOVELTY - Processing frozen sample in combination with laser technology without osmotic protective agent comprises e.g. (i) cell sample freezing by removing the cell sample from the cell buffer, immersing in the freezing solution, maintaining for at least 2.0 minutes, transferring the droplets of the frozen liquid containing cell sample to the vitrification freezing carrier and the droplets, (ii) taking out from the liquid nitrogen, heating the droplets by using laser pulse, allowing droplets to first thaw, absorbing the heat of laser and simultaneously transferring the heat to the cell sample within the droplet to ultimately achieve thawing of the cell sample, (iii) placing the thawed cell sample in thawing solution for washing to remove the components of the frozen liquid on the cell sample, and (iv) transferring the thawed cell sample to carbon dioxide incubator for culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for processing frozen sample in combination with laser technology without osmotic protective agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes low concentration, improves survival rate and development potential of biological samples.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing frozen sample in combination with laser technology without osmotic protective agent comprises (i) cell sample freezing by removing the cell sample from the cell buffer, immersing in the freezing solution, maintaining for at least 2.0 minutes, transferring the droplets of the frozen liquid containing cell sample to the vitrification freezing carrier and the droplets, controlling the size is within 3 mu l, immersing the vitrified freezing rod directly in liquid nitrogen for cryopreservation, where the frozen liquid is 1.0 mol/kg sucrose solution, 0.15 mol/kg salt solution and 0.0062 mol/ by mass molar concentration, where the solution is prepared by adding 1.0 mol/kg sucrose solution, 0.15 mol/kg salt solution and 0.0062 mol/kg polysucrose solution to the base solution by adding 0.4 mu g/ml carbon nanoparticle, (ii) taking out from the liquid nitrogen, heating the droplets by using laser pulse, allowing droplets to first thaw, absorbing the heat of laser and simultaneously transferring the heat to the cell sample within the droplet to ultimately achieve thawing of the cell sample, (iii) placing the thawed cell sample in thawing solution for washing to remove the components of the frozen liquid on the cell sample, and (iv) transferring the thawed cell sample to carbon dioxide incubator for culturing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), after the droplets of the frozen liquid containing the cell sample are transferred to the vitrification freezing carrier, and it is ensured that the vitrified freezing carrier is brought into liquid nitrogen for cryopreservation within 2 minutes. In step (iii), the thawed cell sample is immersed in lyophilic solution of sucrose at molar concentration of 0.5 mol/kg for at least 5 minutes to remove the components of the frozen liquid on the cell sample.

PN CN109574044-A; CN109574044-B
TI Producing sulfate comprises metering the waste alkylation acid and waste alkali, controlling temperature, decomposing the organic matter, desulfurizing, filtering to remove impurities, adding filtrate and drying sulfate in a drying cylinder.
AU LIU B
   LV S
   GUAN S
   ZHANG C
AE PANJIN HONGSHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (PANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019335144
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sulfate comprises (i) metering the waste alkylation acid and waste alkali or light-burned magnesia through metering tank and a scale and adding into the reactor for neutralization reaction, controlling the temperature of the reaction by circulating water cooling and adjusting the pH to neutral with sodium hydroxide or concentrated sulfuric acid; (ii) adding the product into a high temperature reactor for high temperature reaction, decomposing the organic matter into carbon dioxide and water, desulfurizing excessive waste acid in the high temperature reaction and sulfur dioxide decomposed in part of the sulfate; (iii) adding the high temperature reversed sulfate into a dissolution tank for dissolution and filtering through a filter press to remove impurities; (iv) adding the filtrate into a crystallization tank for cooling and crystallization; and (v) adding the sulfate crystals into the centrifuge for drying and drying the dried sulfate in a drying cylinder.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly; has no waste discharge, avoids contamination of alkylation waste acid and waste alkali and can produce other sulfates e.g. magnesium sulfate heptahydrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing sulfate comprises (i) metering the waste alkylation acid and waste alkali or light-burned magnesia through metering tank and a scale and adding into the reactor for neutralization reaction, controlling the temperature of the reaction by circulating water cooling and adjusting the pH to neutral with sodium hydroxide or concentrated sulfuric acid; (ii) adding the product into a high temperature reactor for high temperature reaction at 800 degrees C, decomposing the organic matter into carbon dioxide and water, desulfurizing excessive waste acid in the high temperature reaction and sulfur dioxide decomposed in part of the sulfate by a two-stage desulfurization tower and subjecting the high temperature reactor to a high pressure reaction at a high temperature; (iii) adding the high temperature reversed sulfate into a dissolution tank for dissolution and filtering through a filter press to remove impurities; (iv) adding the filtrate into a crystallization tank for cooling and crystallization when the density of the filtered filtrate reaches 1.3-1.4 g/cm3 and the pH index reaches 6-7; and (v) adding the sulfate crystals into the centrifuge for drying and drying the dried sulfate in a drying cylinder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the temperature of the circulating water cooling control reaction is 90-95 degrees C. In the step (ii), the pressure of the high temperature reactor in the high temperature reaction is -0.005 to -0.01 MP. In the step (v), the end point of the drying operation is the product moisture content reaches 10-12% and the end point of the drying operation is the product contains free water of less than or equal to 5%. The desulfurizing agent is caustic soda or lightly burned magnesia.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The high temperature reaction is carried out using a high temperature reactor having a breathing function. The high temperature reactor comprises a fixed reaction chamber sealing cover. The front end of the fixed reaction chamber sealing cover is provided with a high temperature reactor rotating cylinder. The high temperature reactor rotating cylinder is a high temperature reaction chamber. The high temperature reactor rotating cylinder is rotated and matched with the fixed reaction chamber sealing cover. The side wall of the high-temperature reactor rotating cylinder is provided with a slide support. The slide sealing cover is provided with a breathing valve slide. The breathing valve is arranged outside the breathing valve slide. The breathing valve is annular. The annular breathing valve is sleeved on the outer side of the junction of the high temperature reactor rotating cylinder and the fixed reaction chamber sealing cover. The annular lap joint of an adjustable breathing valve is further disposed in the axial direction of the annular breathing valve. The annular overlapping closed mouth is provided with a retractable elastic member. The annular breathing valve adjusts the size of the suction valve by a retractable elastic member in the annular overlapping closure. The two sides of the annular breathing valve are respectively overlapped on the fixed reaction chamber sealing cover and the breathing valve slide. The breathing valve comprises a negative pressure working position on both sides of the fixed reaction chamber sealing cover and the breathing valve slide, and a closed end of the breathing valve away from the fixed reaction chamber sealing cover and pressure relief position separated from the slide of the breathing valve. The outside of the breathing valve is further provided with a breathing valve cover for protecting the breathing valve. The one side of the breathing valve cover is mounted on the fixed reaction chamber sealing cover and the other side is slid over the slide. The chute is arranged on the breathing valve slide. The one end of the breathing valve cover is slidably engaged with the sliding groove. The breathing valve cover is made of a thin material. The multiple of circular holes for controlling the amount of intake air of the reaction chamber are provided in the middle of the breathing valve.

PT P
PN CN109422284-A
TI Processing flue gas desulfurization and recycling            gas deamination pollutants to waste treatment comprises            e. g. passing sulfur dioxide-containing flue gas and            oxidizing air from bottom of desulfurization oxidation            tower.
AU CHENG X
AE CHENG X (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201923369L
AB    NOVELTY - Processing flue gas desulfurization and                recycling gas deamination pollutants to waste                treatment comprises e. g. (i) passing the sulfur                dioxide-containing flue gas and oxidizing air from                the bottom of the desulfurization oxidation tower                and moving up at low speed, reacting with the                magnesium hydroxide emulsion sprayed on the top of                the tower to form magnesium sulfite, oxidizing to                magnesium sulfate, and obtaining the magnesium                hydroxide emulsion by hydrolyzing and emulsifying                of magnesium oxide as raw material; (ii) subjecting                the reaction product obtained in the step (i) to                solid-liquid separation to obtain magnesium sulfate                solution and small amount of gypsum impurities;                (iii) sending the magnesium sulfate solution                obtained in the step (ii) to the top of the                deamination tower and spraying through the shower,                reacting with the ammonia in the bottom to remove                the carbon dioxide gas to form ammonium sulfate and                magnesium hydroxide precipitate.
   USE - The method is useful for processing flue gas                desulfurization and recycling gas de-amination                pollutants to waste treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces gas consumption and                prolongs the life of the equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing flue gas desulfurization and                recycling gas deamination pollutants to waste                treatment comprises (i) passing the sulfur                dioxide-containing flue gas and oxidizing air from                the bottom of the desulfurization oxidation tower                and moving up at low speed, reacting with the                magnesium hydroxide emulsion sprayed on the top of                the tower to form magnesium sulfite, oxidizing to                magnesium sulfate, and obtaining the magnesium                hydroxide emulsion by hydrolyzing and emulsifying                of magnesium oxide as raw material; (ii) subjecting                the reaction product obtained in the step (i) to                solid-liquid separation to obtain magnesium sulfate                solution and small amount of gypsum impurities;                (iii) sending the magnesium sulfate solution                obtained in the step (ii) to the top of the                deamination tower and spraying through the shower,                reacting with the ammonia in the bottom to remove                the carbon dioxide gas to form ammonium sulfate and                magnesium hydroxide precipitate; and (iv)                performing solid-liquid separation of the reaction                product obtained in the step (iii), obtaining                magnesium ammonium sulfate solution and                regenerating magnesium hydroxide respectively,                separating the magnesium ammonium sulfate solution                by crystallization to obtain ammonium magnesium                sulfate product, and recycling the magnesium                hydroxide as raw material of the step (i).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises separating and drying the magnesium                  hydroxide, and obtaining powder magnesium                  hydroxide product. In the step (ii), after                  emulsification of the calcium sulfate obtained                  and the semi-burned dolomite powder, the flue gas                  containing carbon dioxide is fully reacted at                  65-80 degrees C to obtain magnesium sulfate and                  powder calcium carbonate products after                  separation and the magnesium sulfate is used in                  the subsequent reaction together with the                  magnesium sulfate solution, where the                  semi-burning calcination temperature is 700-800                  degrees C. The magnesium sulfate solution is                  sprayed in a multi-stage air-spray absorption                  tower to absorb ammonia in the upwardly rising                  gas. The magnesium hydroxide is isolated in                  multiple clarification separator. The                  ammonia-containing gas is subjected to                  decarburization treatment before passed to the                  deamination tower. The raw material of the                  desulfurizing agent for removing sulfur dioxide                  in the flue gas, except brucite, magnesite or                  dolomite, after treatment with other                  magnesium-containing minerals of serpentine,                  forsterite and talc, this method can be used to                  remove sulfur dioxide from flue gas and purify                  the magnesium hydroxide product, when the                  concentration of the ammonium sulfate solution                  reaches 45-50%, then pumped to the vacuum                  evaporator, and keep the density of the solution                  less than or equal to 1.25 g/cm3 to ensure the                  tar floats on the surface of the multi-separator                  solution and removed in time. Preferred                  Components: The gas is coke oven gas and other                  ammonia-containing gases.

PN CN109200963-A; CN109200963-B
TI High-efficiency coupling, poly-generation and low-nitrogen coal gangue activation process comprises e.g. wide-preheating the coal gangue raw material and subjecting first coal gangue to field effect low nitrogen roasting and re-circulating.
AU WANG L
   LIU S
   XU H
   ZHU F
AE LIAONING SHENGDA ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOG (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 2019076133
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency coupling, poly-generation and low-nitrogen coal gangue activation process, comprises (ii) wide-preheating the coal gangue raw material to obtain first coal gangue having a water content of less than 0.8 wt.%, (ii) subjecting first coal gangue to field effect low nitrogen roasting to obtain first activated powder product, and high temperature flue gas, and (iii) subjecting high temperature flue gas to residual heat coupling adjustment to obtain second activated powder product, then re-circulating low temperature flue gas to (i) wide heating, and circulating to participate of (ii) in field effect low nitrogen roasting air.
   USE - The method is useful for high-efficiency coupling, poly-generation and low-nitrogen coal gangue activation process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the mechanical and high temperature; and has no longer a single roasting process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for processing coal gangue activation method, comprising a crusher for primary crushing the gangue raw material to obtain primary granules, rotary preheater for obtaining first-stage granule from the pulverizer for wide preheating to obtain first coal gangue, a pulverizer for obtaining a first coal gangue from a rotary preheater for secondary crushing to obtain secondary granules, roasting furnace for obtaining secondary particles for high temperature activation from the pulverizer, adding activator for chemical activating to obtain first activated powder product and the high temperature flue gas, dust collector for obtaining high temperature flue gas from the roaster for high temperature dust removal to obtain second activated powder product and dust-free high temperature flue gas, waste heat recovery system for obtaining dust-free and high-temperature flue gas from the dust collector for heat exchanging to obtain low temperature flue gas and high temperature air, coupled fan for blowing air or low temperature air into the preheating recovery system, desulfurization tower for obtaining the low-temperature flue gas involved in the wide preheating from the rotary preheater for desulfurizing and purifying to obtain mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide compressor for obtaining mixed gas from the desulfurization tower for compressing and supplying the carbon workshops.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises (ia) firstly crushing coal gangue raw material to obtain primary granules having a particle size of 30- 50 mm, and (ib) preheating the first stage granules to obtain a first coal gangue having a water content of less than 0.8%. The step (ii) comprises (iia) performing secondary crushing of the first coal gangue to obtain secondary crushed particles, and (iib) performing secondary crushing of the first coal gangue to obtain secondary crushed particles, and (iiic) activating secondary crushed particles at a high temperature, and then adding activator for chemical activation to obtain first activated powder product and high temperature flue gas. The step (iii) comprises (iiia) subjecting high temperature flue gas to high temperature dust removal to obtain second activated product, and dust free high temperature flue gas, (iiib) blowing in air to recover waste heat from dust-free high-temperature flue gas to obtain low temperature flue gas and high temperature air, (iiic) (a) circulating low temperature flue gas to the location of step (i) to participating in wide preheating, (b) supplying high temperature air to the outside of the heat at the same time as (a) to obtain low temperature air, and (iiid) circulating low temperature air to site of step (ii) to participating in field effect low nitrogen roasting. The step (iii) further comprises circulating the low temperature air to the place of C2 to participate in the waste heat recovery while performing (iiid). The method further comprises performing desulfurization and compression of the low-temperature flue gas after participating in the wide preheating, and supplying to the carbon workshop. Preferred Components: The size of the secondary granules is 5-8 mm, 1-3 mm, or 80-325 mesh.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The roasting furnace adopts an activated roasting furnace of wind distribution structure in a regional block furnace. The high temperature activation module is included uniformly obtain stable temperature field to avoid an increase in the concentration of nitrogen oxides in the flue gas. The roasting furnace further includes a chemical activation module for adding an activator to the secondary granules. The activator is calcium carbonate, slaked lime, quicklime, manganese dioxide, sodium chloride, alcohol and/or amine.

PN CN109180172-A
TI Preparing nano far infrared ceramics, comprises mixing pulverized dried purple seaweed with ethyl ammonium nitrate and lecithin in ultrasonic oscillator, performing ultrasonic oscillation reaction and performing hydrothermal carbonization.
AU CHEN Q
   DING C
AE CHEN Q (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201909346F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano far infrared ceramics, comprises (a) pulverizing dried purple seaweed in tissue crusher, mixing pulverized material with ethyl ammonium nitrate and lecithin in ultrasonic oscillator, and performing ultrasonic oscillation reaction to obtain mixed solution, (b) placing in hydrothermal reaction kettle to perform hydrothermal carbonization, filtering to obtain filter residue, washing with anhydrous ethanol and deionized water, drying, transferring to carbonization furnace, continuously carbonizing under protection of carbon dioxide, and discharging to obtain mesoporous carbon alginate, (c) mixing magnesium chloride, aluminum chloride, silicon tetrachloride, silicon tetrachloride, zirconium oxychloride, barium chloride and deionized water to obtain mixture, (d) adding polyethylene glycol, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose and mesoporous carbon alginate, uniformly stirring, adding ammonia water to adjust pH of 8-9, reacting and filtering, and (e) calcining in calcining furnace.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano far infrared ceramics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces nano far infrared ceramics having uniform grain diameter, and high far infrared radiation emitting rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano far infrared ceramics, comprises (a) pulverizing dried purple seaweed in a tissue crusher for 20 to 30 minutes, mixing pulverized material with ethyl ammonium nitrate and lecithin having mass fraction of 30% in an ultrasonic oscillator, and performing ultrasonic oscillation reaction to obtain mixed solution, (b) placing the mixed solution in a hydrothermal reaction kettle, performing hydrothermal carbonization, filtering and separating to obtain filter residue, washing with anhydrous ethanol and deionized water for 3-5 times, drying, transferring to carbonization furnace, continuously carbonizing under protection of carbon dioxide, and discharging to obtain mesoporous carbon alginate, (c) mixing 12-15 pts. wt. magnesium chloride, 50-60 pts. wt. aluminum chloride, 35-45 pts. wt. silicon tetrachloride, 50-60 pts. wt. silicon tetrachloride, 25-30 pts. wt. zirconium oxychloride, 1-2 pts. wt. barium chloride and 500-600 pts. wt. deionized water in reaction kettle to obtain mixture, (d) adding polyethylene glycol, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose and mesoporous carbon alginate to the reaction kettle, uniformly stirring, adding 30% ammonia water in dropwise manner with stirring to adjust pH of 8-9, reacting and filtering residue, and (e) introducing the filter residue in a calcining furnace to perform calcination, and discharging to obtain nano far infrared ceramics.

TI Constructing transplanted tumor model of normal-immune mouse liver cancer derived based on organoids method useful for developing liver cancer medicine, comprises e.g. culturing liver cancer cells and carcinoma tissue and incubating.
AU HONG F
   BI Y
   PAN H
   WANG Q
   ZHANG K
   CHEN T
   ZHANG F
AE SHANGHAI MEIFENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019101923
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing transplanted tumor model of normal-immune mouse liver cancer derived from a based on organoids method comprises e.g. (i) taking liver cancer cells and hepatocellular carcinoma tissue mesenchymal cells, culturing, adjusting the cell concentration, and placing in incubator, (ii) immersing the microcarriers in alcohol, washing with 1XPBS buffer, incubating in DMEM medium, mixing the human primary liver cancer cell suspension with incubated microcarrier, and culturing, and (iii) washing the cell-microcarrier complex with 1XPBS buffer for 4 times, resuspending the cells with 1xPBS buffer, adjusting the cell concentration to 2/asterisk107/ml, and adjusting the microcarrier concentration to 200-300 mu g/ml to obtain cell-microcarrier suspension and placing on ice, injecting the cell-microcarrier suspension into normal immunized mice at 7-8 weeks, and performing in vivo implantation for 20-30 days, and constructing mouse model.
   USE - The model is useful for developing liver cancer medicine and screening anti-hepatocarcinoma medicine (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Constructing transplanted tumor model of normal-immune mouse liver cancer derived based on organoids method comprises (i) taking liver cancer cells and hepatocellular carcinoma tissue mesenchymal cells, completely culturing with DMEM containing 10% fetal bovine serum, 1% penicillin with mass percentage and 1% streptomycin, mixing the cell uniformly, if the trypan blue counts the number of viable cells more than 95%, adjusting the cell concentration to 2/asterisk107/ml, and placing in incubator at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide, (ii) immersing the microcarriers in alcohol with volume of 75% for 24 hours, washing 5 times with 1XPBS buffer, and incubating in DMEM medium for 24-48 hours, mixing the human primary liver cancer cell suspension with the incubated microcarrier, and culturing in a 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24-48 hours to obtain incubated cell-microcarrier complex, and (iii) washing the cell-microcarrier complex with 1XPBS buffer for 4 times, resuspending the cells with 1xPBS buffer, adjusting the cell concentration to 2/asterisk107/ml, and adjusting the microcarrier concentration to 200-300 mu g/ml to obtain cell-microcarrier suspension and placing on ice, injecting the cell-microcarrier suspension into normal immunized mice at 7-8 weeks, and performing in vivo implantation for 20-30 days, and constructing mouse model.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microcarrier is composed of a positively-charged organic composite polymer, and it has a multi-hole-shaped strip rope shape and cross-links to form a labyrinth irregular structure with sufficient space. The volume ratio of the human primary liver cancer cell suspension to the incubated microcarrier is 1:3-5. The mouse is a mouse with normal immune function. The mice are C57BL/6 mice and BALB/c mice. In the step (iii), the site of the cell-microcarrier suspension injected into the mouse is subcutaneous and each injection of 100 mu l. Preferred Method: The liver cancer cells and liver cancer tissue mesenchymal cells are obtained by taking fresh liver cancer tissues by 20-30 g, cells were digested with collagenase with a mass percentage of 0.05% to obtain liver cancer cells and hepatocarcinoma cells.

PN CN108980859-A; CN108980859-B
TI Method for treating electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block waste, involves mixing electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block hazardous waste and calcium-based fluorochemical agent followed by decomposing cyanide.
AU FANG S
   YAO W
   WU Y
   WANG G
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2018A25163
AB    NOVELTY - An electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block waste treating method involves mixing electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block hazardous waste and calcium-based fluorochemical agent according to the proportion through steel ball milling and powder making system to prepare powder, adding reacted electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block hazardous waste and the calcium-based fluorine-fixing agent powder on the boiler burner into the boiler and electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block powder in the combustion process of the boiler and decomposing cyanide into carbon dioxide and nitrogen under high temperature environment of the boiler, during the combustion process of electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block powder, releasing the fluorine element into F- boiler under high temperature environment followed by forming a part of fluorine to hydrogen fluoride.
   USE - Method for treating electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables treating electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block waste without excessive devices and equipment in the power plant, and other hazardous wastes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block waste treating method involves mixing electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block hazardous waste and calcium-based fluorochemical agent according to the proportion through steel ball milling and powder making system to prepare powder, where the total amount of calcium-based fluorochemical agent is not less than 2.1 times the total amount of fluorine in the waste metal of the electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block, followed by adding reacted electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block hazardous waste and the calcium-based fluorine-fixing agent powder on the boiler burner into the boiler and electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block powder in the combustion process of the boiler and decomposing cyanide into carbon dioxide and nitrogen in the high temperature environment of the boiler, during the combustion process of electrolytic aluminum waste cathode carbon block powder, releasing the fluorine element into F- boiler under high temperature environment, reacting F- with calcium-based fluorine-fixing agent and calcium ions in the coal to form insoluble calcium fluoride, distributing with fly ash, forming a part of fluorine to hydrogen fluoride with the flue gas entering into wet desulfurization system, dissolving in water, reacting part of F- with calcium ions in the limestone slurry to form calcium fluoride, and absorbing F- by the desulfurization wastewater.

The fragrant crispy beef comprises main material and auxiliary material. The main material is 100-120 pts. wt. cucumber beef. The auxiliary material comprises salt, sugar, cooking wine, scallion, ginger, and chinese prickly ash.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing the fragrant crispy beef.
   ADVANTAGE - The fragrant crispy beef: has excellent taste and appearance, which fills the gap in the market.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing fragrant crispy beef comprises (i) taking cucumber beef fat and tendon of the large surface, (ii) the processing of beef screwdriver into a uniform size of the meat and cleaning, (iii) placing the cooking auxiliary material in clean water, and cooking the meat piece obtained in step (ii) into water having a temperature of 90-105 degrees C, and cooking for 60 minutes, (iv) cutting the cooked meat into step (iii) into a piece of meat having a thickness of 0.5 mm, (v) heating the salad oil to 175-180 degrees C, frying sliced meat pieces in 95-105S in salad oil, and draining and cooling, and (vi) the controlled atmosphere packaging meat slice after cooling, and filling gas is 30% carbon dioxide and 70% nitrogen. The fragrant crispy beef comprises main material and auxiliary material. The main material is 100-120 pts. wt. cucumber beef. The auxiliary material comprises 1.8-2.2 pts. wt. salt, 1-1.5 pts. wt. sugar, 5-7 pts. wt. cooking wine, 0.5-0.75 pts. wt. scallion, 1.5-3 pts. wt. ginger, and 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. chinese prickly ash.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (ii) further comprises a salting the meat with the curing material stirring uniformly after cleaning, at 2-4 degrees C for 6-8 hours, uniformly mixed with slurry, sizing for 120-180 minutes at 2-10 degrees C, the pickling material comprises 100 pts. wt. cooking wine, 10 pts. wt. soy sauce, 20 parts of coconut, 10 parts of sugar, 3 parts of vinegar, 20 parts red bean curd. The slurry comprises 100 pts. wt. whole egg liquid, 5 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate, and 2 pts. wt. salt. The step (ii) comprises blanching, pickling and the meat with the curing material after stirring evenly, after washing at 2-4 degrees C for 6-8 hours. the pickled meat into cold water to heating start heating after the water is boiling, taking out the meat, using ice water washing and cooling the meat. The salting material comprises 100 parts of cooking wine, 10 parts of soy sauce, 20 parts of coconut, 10 parts of sugar, 3 pts. wt. white vinegar, and 20 parts of red bean curd. The step (iv), further comprises oiling, and the sliced meat pieces are placed in the scallion oil and smeared in a container to be fried. The scallion oil was prepared by placing 20 pts. wt. onion leaves in 100 pts. wt. corn oil for 240 minutes.

PN CN108767354-A; CN108767354-B
TI Recovering valuable metals from spent lithium ion            battery cathode material comprises preparing calcined            material, stirring, separating, adjusting, adding,            stirring, precipitating, adjusting, adding and            performing solid-liquid separation.
AU HE S
   LIU Z
   XIA L
   LI Q
   ZHU Y
   HUANG H
   GAO L
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2018893944
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering valuable metals from spent lithium                ion battery cathode material comprises (i)                disassembling waste nickel-cobalt-manganese                ternary, lithium cobaltate, lithium manganate, and                lithium nickelate lithium ion battery, discharging                treatment, separating anode sheet in the anode                sheet from aluminum foil; (ii) mixing separated                anode material with a calcining agent, performing                low temperature roasting to obtain calcined                material; (iii) subjecting the obtained calcined                material to stirring water immersion, and                solid-liquid separation to obtain carbon and                leachate; (iv) adjusting pH of resulting leachate,                adding precipitant, stirring, precipitating other                metal elements other than lithium, and separating                the solid and liquid; and (v) adjusting the pH                value of the filtrate obtained by solid-liquid                separation, and adding ammonium carbonate or                ammonium hydrogen carbonate or bubbling                carbon-dioxide to sink lithium, and performing                solid-liquid separation.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering valuable                metals from spent lithium ion battery cathode                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly, simple                and economical; has mild technical condition and                short process time, does not need to consume a                large amount of acid and alkali, realizes valuable                metal and recovery of carbon, green and does not                generate large solid rubbish and waste water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering valuable metals from spent lithium                ion battery cathode material comprises (i)                disassembling at least one of the waste                nickel-cobalt-manganese ternary, lithium cobaltate,                lithium manganate, and lithium nickelate lithium                ion battery after the discharge treatment,                separating the anode sheet, and separating the                anode material in the anode sheet from aluminum                foil; (ii) mixing the separated anode material with                a calcining agent, performing low temperature                roasting to obtain a calcined material, where the                calcining agent is ammonium sulfate and/or ammonium                hydrogen sulfate, and the molar ratio satisfies                (n((ammonium sulfate) +n((ammonium hydrogen                sulfate): (n(nickel +cobalt +manganese +2 lithium))                (I) is 0.9-1.5; (iii) subjecting the obtained                calcined material to stirring water immersion, and                solid-liquid separation to obtain carbon and                leachate; (iv) adjusting the pH of the resulting                leachate, adding a precipitant thereto, stirring,                precipitating other metal elements other than                lithium, and separating the solid and liquid; and                (v) adjusting the pH value of the filtrate obtained                by the solid-liquid separation, and adding ammonium                carbonate or ammonium hydrogen carbonate or                bubbling carbon-dioxide to sink lithium after the                completing reaction, and performing solid-liquid                separation to obtain lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (vi),                  adjusting the pH of the filtrate obtained after                  the solid-liquid separation to 5-8, and                  evaporating and crystallizing to obtain ammonium                  sulfate. In the step (vi), calcining the obtained                  ammonium sulfate, when the calcining agent is                  ammonium hydrogen sulfate, and baking. When The                  calcining agent is ammonium sulfate is formula                  (I), when the calcining agent is ammonium                  hydrogen sulfate, formula (I) is 0.9-1.4, when                  the calcining agent is a mixture of ammonium                  sulfate and ammonium hydrogen sulfate. In the                  step (iv), the precipitating agent is ammonium                  oxalate, ammonium hydrogen oxalate, oxalic acid,                  ammonium carbonate, ammonium hydrogen carbonate,                  or carbon-dioxide. The precipitating agent is                  ammonium oxalate, the temperature of the                  precipitation is 25-90 degrees C, and the                  precipitation time is 0.5-3 hours. In the step                  (ii), the calcination temperature of the                  low-temperature calcination is 350-600 degrees C                  and/or the calcination time of the                  low-temperature calcination is 20-120 minutes. In                  the step (iii), the conditions of the stirring                  water immersion are as follows: the liquid-solid                  ratio of the water immersion is 5:1-2:1 ml/g, the                  leaching temperature is 25-70 degrees C, and the                  leaching time is 10-60 minutes. In the step (iv),                  the pH value is adjusted using the                  ammonia-containing flue gas generated by the                  low-temperature calcination and/or adjusting the                  pH using the ammonia-containing flue gas                  generated by the low-temperature calcination. In                  the step (v), the lithium has a pH of 10.5-12.5,                  a temperature of 30-90 degrees C, and a time of                  0.5-3 hours.

PN CN108623822-A
TI Preparing silicon dioxide/cellulose hybrid aerogel comprises e.g. vacuum drying cellulose powder, then soaking in distilled water and stirring, and mixing cellulose solution and treated alkaline solution.
AU HUANG H
   ZHANG M
   ZHOU J
   MIAO L
   WANG X
   LIU C
   LIU X
   SUN Y
   XU H
AE GUANGDONG DIOR APPLIED MATERIALS TECHNOL (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201880216Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon dioxide/cellulose hybrid aerogel comprises e.g. (i) vacuum drying cellulose powder, then soaking in distilled water and stirring to cellulose solution; mixing the cellulose solution and treated alkaline solution and stirring to obtain first solution having a concentration of 1-6 wt.%, (ii) dissolving the alkali silicate in distilled water, adjusting to a concentration of 20-50 wt.%, maintaining temperature at 60-80 degrees C, stirring and pre-freezing to obtain second solution, (iii) mixing first solution and second solution, stirring, pouring into a mold, and sealing to obtain wet gel, (iv) immersing wet gel in an acidic solution for aging for 1-7 days to adjusting mechanical strength and pore size distribution, (v) sequentially washing wet gel after the aging in step (iv) by distilled water and ethanol, and (vi) drying the wet gel after washing in the step (v) by a supercritical carbon dioxide device or a vacuum freeze dryer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silicon dioxide/cellulose hybrid aerogel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing silicon dioxide/cellulose hybrid aerogel comprises (i) vacuum drying the cellulose powder, then soaking in distilled water, stirring at 1-6 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain a cellulose solution; mixing the cellulose solution and treated alkaline solution at a temperature of -10 to -6 degrees C, stirring for 1-3 hours to dissolving the cellulose to obtain first solution having a concentration of 1-6 wt.%, (ii) dissolving the alkali silicate in distilled water, adjusting to a concentration of 20-50 wt.%, maintaining the temperature at 60-80 degrees C, stirring for 1-3 hours, and then pre-freezing at -10 to -6 degrees C to obtain second solution, (iii) mixing first solution and second solution at -10 to -6 degrees C, stirring, pouring into a mold, and sealing at room temperature to obtain wet gel, (iv) immersing the wet gel in an acidic solution for aging for 1-7 days to adjusting mechanical strength and pore size distribution, (v) sequentially washing the wet gel after the aging in step (iv) by distilled water and ethanol for 1-2 days, and (vi) drying the wet gel after the washing in the step (v) by a supercritical carbon dioxide device or a vacuum freeze dryer to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, and/or lithium hydroxide; the cellulose is nanocrystalline cellulose, microcrystalline cellulose, bacterial cellulose, methyl cellulose or hydroxypropyl methylcellulose. In step (ii), the alkali silicate is sodium silicate, potassium-sodium silicate or sodium metasilicate nonahydrate. In step (iv), the acidic solution is hydrochloric acid, acetic acid, and citric acid, and the concentration is 0.1-0.5 mol/l. Preferred Method: In step (i), the specific configuration method of alkaline solution: adding a strong base includes sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, lithium hydroxide to distilled water to a concentration of 5-15 wt.%; adding amphoteric oxides zinc oxide, aluminum oxide, titanium dioxide and zirconium dioxide, then adjusting the concentration of 0.5-3 wt.%, stirring and dissolving and freezing at a temperature of -10 to -6 degrees C for 1-3 hours.

PN CN108439450-A
TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate useful for            construction, comprises e.g. digesting the deionized            water and quicklime in hot water, obtaining a crude            solution of calcium hydroxide, adding a dispersing            agent, aging and sieving.
AU DOU N
AE ANHUI XUECHENG SUPERFINE CALCIUM CARBONA (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201868224M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises (i)                digesting the deionized water and quicklime in hot                water at 80 degrees C, obtaining a crude solution                of calcium hydroxide, adding a dispersing agent,                aging and sieving to obtain a calcium hydroxide                slurry, (ii) dissolving sodium oleate and deionized                water to obtain a 1% sodium oleate solution, (iii)                mixing the calcium hydroxide slurry obtained in                step (i) and the sodium oleate aqueous solution                obtained in the step (ii), adding the additive and                stirring to obtain a mixed slurry, (iv) adding the                mixed slurry obtained in the step (iii) into the                carbonization reactor, setting the temperature to                25-40 degrees C and stirring, introducing the                carbon dioxide gas through the rotameter, stirring                the mixture uniformly and carrying out                carbonization 7 to obtain a nano calcium carbonate                solution and (v) heating the nano-calcium carbonate                solution obtained in step (iv) to 80 degrees C,                adding sodium stearate for 2 hours, filtering,                drying and crushing.
   USE - The nano calcium carbonate is useful for                construction (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises (i)                digesting the deionized water and quicklime into                hot water at 80 degrees C, obtaining a crude                solution of calcium hydroxide, adding a dispersing                agent, aging for 24 hours and sieving with a 200                mesh sieve to obtain a calcium hydroxide slurry,                (ii) dissolving sodium oleate and deionized water                to obtain a 1% sodium oleate solution, (iii) mixing                the calcium hydroxide slurry obtained in step (i)                and the sodium oleate aqueous solution obtained in                the step (ii), adding the additive and stirring at                35 degrees C for 30 minutes to obtain a mixed                slurry, (iv) adding the mixed slurry obtained in                the step (iii) into the carbonization reactor,                setting the temperature to 25-40 degrees C and                stirring for 10 minutes after electric stirring,                introducing the carbon dioxide gas through the                rotameter, stirring the mixture uniformly and                carrying out carbonization for 20 minutes when the                pH of the reaction system reaches to less than 7 to                obtain a nano calcium carbonate solution and (v)                heating the nano-calcium carbonate solution                obtained in step (iv) to 80 degrees C, adding                sodium stearate for 2 hours, filtering, drying and                crushing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  mass ratio of the deionized water and the raw                  stone ash is 6:1. In the step (i), the dispersing                  agent is a polycarboxylate, sodium                  hexametaphosphate and sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate. In the step (iii), the                  additive is glutinous rice syrup and egg white.                  In the step (iv), the carbon dioxide gas                  concentration is 20-35%.

PN RU2663432-C1
TI Synthesis gas production process control method for the low-tonnage methanol production.
AU ZAGASHVILI YU V
   KUZMIN A M
AE ZAGASHVILI YU V (ZAGA-Individual)
GA 2018634585
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the synthesis gas production process control method for the low-tonnage methanol production. Method is implemented by the hydrocarbon gases partial oxidation at a pressure of 6.0-7.5 MPa in the gas generator, equipped with hydrocarbon gas and oxidizer input units, which include the hydrocarbon gas and oxidant mass flow flowmeters-regulators. Synthesis gas from the gas generator outlet is supplied to the waste heat boiler and cooled down with the chemically purified water stream, which mass flow is adjusted by the flowmeter regulator. At that, the waste heat boiler output is connected to the synthesis gas composition correction unit input, which includes: (1) hydrogen and carbon monoxide molar concentrations ratio in the synthesis gas correction unit, containing splitter, which input is connected to the waste heat boiler output, and the mixer, the splitter first branch pipe is connected directly to the mixer, the splitter second branch pipe is connected to the mixer via in-series connected controllable high-temperature choke and converter with the carbon monoxide vapor conversion catalyst; (2) carbon dioxide content correction unit, including connected to the mixer outlet heat exchanger-refrigerator, in which the synthesis gas is cooled down by the chemically purified water stream to 15-70 degrees C, which mass flow rate is adjusted by the flowmeter-regulator, and connected to the heat exchanger-refrigerator outlet separator, in which from the cooled vapor-gas mixture water vapor condensate and the partially dissolved therefrom liquefied carbon dioxide are separated, after which the synthesis gas is supplied to the methanol synthesis unit; (3) gas analyzer connected to the leaving the separator synthesis gas pipeline, wherein using the automated monitoring and control system performing the hydrogen and carbon monoxide molar concentrations ratio control in the range H2/CO=2.2-2.6 and the stoichiometric ratio of its components in the range M=1.95-2.15, which, based on the results of coming from the gas analyzer discrete information on the hydrogen and carbon oxides current concentration in the leaving the separator synthesis gas, automatically calculates the adjusted control signals, which are supplied to the inputs of controlled high-temperature choke and the water supply mass flow rate flowmeter-regulator to the heat exchanger-refrigerator.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result consists in the possibility of automatic control over the synthesis gas production technological process parameters, and in the synthesis gas composition optimization for the subsequent methanol catalytic synthesis.

PT P
PN CN107868190-A
TI Silicon dioxide-polymethylmethacrylate composite thermal insulation material has specified average porosity, uniform pore size distribution, phase interface thermal resistance and thermal conductivity.
AU LI H
   LIU H
   SONG L
   WEI Y
   LI R
   FU Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 2018271335
AB    NOVELTY - A silicon dioxide-polymethylmethacrylate composite thermal insulation material has an average porosity of 93-95%, an uniform pore size distribution of 20-25 nm, a phase interface thermal resistance of 30-34 MWm-2K-1 and a thermal conductivity of 0.02-0.03 Wm-1K-1. The composite thermal insulation material is prepared by adding tetraethyl orthosilicate to aqueous solution of hydrogen chloride, stirring and leaving still for 30-180 minutes, then adding solid sodium hydroxide, stirring to completely dissolve, and leaving the above solution for 2-6 hours to obtain a wet gel.
   USE - Silicon dioxide-polymethylmethacrylate composite thermal insulation material.
   ADVANTAGE - The silicon dioxide-polymethylmethacrylate composite thermal insulation material has excellent thermal resistance and thermal insulation property, and uniform three-dimensional network structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A silicon dioxide-polymethylmethacrylate composite thermal insulation material has an average porosity of 93-95%, an uniform pore size distribution of 20-25 nm, a phase interface thermal resistance of 30-34 MWm-2K-1 and a thermal conductivity of 0.02-0.03 Wm-1K-1. The composite thermal insulation material is prepared by adding 80-150 pts. wt. tetraethyl orthosilicate to 0.1-1 pt. wt. 2 mole/l aqueous solution of hydrogen chloride (i.e. hydrochloric acid), stirring at a room temperature of 20-25 degrees C for 5-30 minutes and leaving still for 30-180 minutes, then adding 0.01-0.2 pt. wt. solid sodium hydroxide, stirring to completely dissolve, leaving the above solution for 2-6 hours to obtain a wet gel, adding 100 pts. wt. methyl methacrylate to the wet gel, soaking for 12-24 hours, removing the supernatant, repeating the soaking process for 2-3 times, continuously adding 0.02-0.2 pt. wt. dibenzoyl peroxide to the wet gel, leaving still for 2-6 hours, placing the product in a carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction device, and using carbon dioxide as a medium to perform supercritical drying at a temperature of 30-50 degrees C under pressure of 7-10 MPa for 10-20 hours to obtain the product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the silicon dioxide-polymethylmethacrylate composite thermal insulation material, which involves the steps as per above se.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The supercritical drying is carried out at a temperature of 35-40 degrees C under pressure of 8-9 MPa for 2-3 hours. Preferred Components: The raw materials used in preparation of composite thermal insulation material comprise 100-120 pts. wt. tetraethyl orthosilicate, 0.3-0.8 pt. wt. 12 mole/l aqueous solution of hydrogen chloride, 0.05-0.1 pt. wt. solid sodium hydroxide and 0.07-0.15 pt. wt. dibenzoyl peroxide.
DC A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.)
