PN US2020360853-A1; WO2020236676-A1; KR2022010550-A; EP3953020-A1; CN114072220-A; US11420153-B2
TI Recovering sulfur comprises providing sour gas to            membrane separation unit, separating sour gas using            carbon dioxide-selective membrane in membrane            separation stage and processing hydrogen            sulfide-enriched gas in sulfur recovery unit.
AU VAIDYA M M
   DUVAL S A
   HAMAD F
   BAKER R
   MERKEL T
   LOKHANDWALA K
   BAHAMDAN A A
   AL-OTAIBI F D
   BAHAMMADAN A A
   FAISALDI A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2020B3357M
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering sulfur from a sour gas, comprises:                providing the sour gas to a membrane separation                unit having a membrane stage that comprises a                carbon dioxide-selective membrane comprising a                perfluoropolymer, where the sour gas comprises                carbon dioxide and at least 1 mol.% hydrogen                sulfide; separating the sour gas using the carbon                dioxide-selective membrane in the membrane                separation stage to obtain hydrogen                sulfide-enriched gas and hydrogen sulfide-stripped                gas, where the hydrogen sulfide-enriched gas has a                hydrogen sulfide concentration of at least 20                mol.%, and the hydrogen sulfide-stripped gas                comprises carbon dioxide; and processing the                hydrogen sulfide-enriched gas in a sulfur recovery                unit to obtain sulfur.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering sulfur                from a sour gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is more cost-effective hydrogen                sulfide enriching method to reliably produce higher                concentrations of hydrogen sulfide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrogen                  sulfide-stripped gas further comprises residual                  hydrogen sulfide, where the process further                  comprises oxidizing the hydrogen sulfide-stripped                  gas in an oxidizer so that residual hydrogen                  sulfide in the hydrogen sulfide-stripped gas is                  reacted with oxygen to produce sulfur                  dioxide-bearing gas. The process further                  comprises: venting the sulfur dioxide-bearing gas                  to the atmosphere; and capturing the sulfur                  dioxide-bearing gas for use in enhanced oil                  recovery operations. Preferred Components: The                  hydrogen sulfide-stripped gas has less than 0.1                  mol.%, preferably less than 0.01 mol.% hydrogen                  sulfide. The hydrogen sulfide-enriched gas has                  greater than 80 mol.%, preferably greater than 95                  mol.% hydrogen sulfide. The carbon                  dioxide-selective membrane has selectivity                  greater than 10, preferably greater than 30                  carbon dioxide-hydrogen sulfide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The membrane                  separation unit has a single-pass membrane stage                  configuration, a two-stage retentate-in-series                  membrane configuration, a two-stage                  permeate-in-series membrane configuration or a                  three-stage membrane configuration. The                  perfluoropolymer comprises a monomer having of                  formula (I) or (II), preferably e.g.                  2-(difluoromethylene)-3a,4,4,6,6,6a-hexafluorotetrahydrofuro(3,4-d)(1,3)dioxole,                  2-(difluoromethylene)-4,4,5-trifluoro-5-(trifluoromethyl)-1,3-dioxolane                  and/or                  2-(difluoromethylene)-4-(difluoro(trifluoromethoxy)methyl)-4,5,5-trifluoro-1,3-dioxolane.m, n = not defined.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); ; E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN111632476-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises e.g.            adding precursor of calcium and nitrate into deionized            water to mix at normal temperature to form solution,            stirring prepared solution to form sol, drying and            calcining gel.
AU WU H
   CHEN B
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2020896291
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises                adding the precursor of calcium and nitrate into                deionized water to mix at normal temperature to                form solution, where the mol ratio of the cation of                calcium ion and nitrate is 1-5:1, stirring the                prepared solution to form sol, and drying the sol                to form gel, calcining the gel to obtain                calciumbased adsorbent particles, the calcining                temperature is 600-900 degrees C, and grinding and                sieving the calcium-based adsorbent particles. The                precursor of calcium is calcium acetate, calcium                citrate, calcium gluconate or calcium lactobionate.                The nitrate is magnesium nitrate or aluminum                nitrate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method good circulating carbon dioxide                absorption ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                carbon dioxide adsorbent prepared according to                above mentioned method.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN WO2020104002-A1; CA3111630-A1; EP3883904-A1; US2022017420-A1; JP2022517495-W
TI Producing gypsum article involves introducing a measured quantity of carbon dioxide into a process water and mixing stucco with the thus treated process water to form a slurry, where addition of carbon dioxide to the process water results.
AU HALBACH M
   LO MORO G
   LO M G
AE KNAUF GIPS KG (KNAF-C)
   KNAUF GIPS KG (KNAF-C)
   KNAUF GIPS KG (KNAF-C)
GA 202045598G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing gypsum article involves introducing a measured quantity of carbon dioxide into a process water and mixing stucco with the thus treated process water to form a slurry, where addition of carbon dioxide to the process water results in a reduction of the amount of process water necessary to achieve a given fluidity of the slurry.
   USE - Method for producing gypsum article.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce gypsum article that increases the efficacy of the dispersant, and allows for a reduction of water in the compositions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a apparatus for preparing a slurry, which comprises mixture of stucco and process water, where units are provided upstream of a mixer in which the stucco is mixed with the process water, and allow for the directed introduction of carbon dioxide into the process water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of carbon dioxide introduced into the process water provides a concentration of dissolved/dispersed carbon dioxide is 1 g/L, preferably at least 1.5 g/L. The process water having the carbon dioxide introduced exhibits a pH of 8.0 or less. The stucco is mixed with the process water having the carbon dioxide introduced to provide a water/stucco ratio in the range of about 0.5-1.2. The prior to the mixing of the stucco with the carbon dioxide containing process-water paper pulp or additives are fed into the carbon dioxide containing water. The foam is mixed with the slurry, and where the addition of carbon dioxide to the process water results in a reduction of the amount of foaming agent necessary to achieve a given density of the slurry. The mixture containing stucco and carbon dioxide containing process water is formed to an article, preferably a building board, especially a plasterboard. The method further involves drying the article or plasterboard.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)
MC L02-A02; L02-C05; L02-D01; L02-J02C

TI Method for generating power using extracted            natural gas, invovles recyclying flow of steam to            oxy-fuel combustor, compressing flow of dioxide to            liquid or supercritical phase, and injecting liquid or            supercritical dioxide into reservoir.
AU HASTINGS A D
   HASTINGS R A
AE TIGRE TECHNOLOGIES LTD (TIGR-Non-standard)
GA 202293457R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves separating carbon dioxide                from exhaust fluids from a heat recuperator and a                steam condenser (8) to provide a flow of the carbon                dioxide. Water is separated from the exhaust fluids                from the heat recuperator and the steam condenser                to provide a flow of water. The flow of water is                passed back through the heat recuperator and the                steam condenser to provide a flow of steam. The                flow of steam is recycled to an oxy-fuel combustor                (6). The flow of the carbon dioxide is compressed                to a liquid or supercritical phase. The liquid or                supercritical carbon dioxide is injected into a                subsurface reservoir to sequester the carbon                dioxide and to re-pressurise the subsurface                reservoir.
   USE - Method for generating power using extracted                natural gas.

PN CN113368861-A
TI Preparing catalyst for synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation, comprises mixing copper source, zinc source and zirconium source, adding citric acid and surfactant, grinding to obtain catalyst precursor, drying, and calcining.
AU MAO D
   HONG C
   GUO X
   YU J
   WU G
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 2021A8093K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst, comprises (1) mixing copper source, zinc source and zirconium source, adding citric acid and surfactant, and grinding to obtain catalyst precursor; and (2) drying and calcining catalyst precursor in sequence to obtain methanol synthesis catalyst by carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing catalyst for synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses grinding method to prepare catalyst precursor, improves the dispersibility of copper, zinc and zirconium in the catalyst precursor by adding citric acid and surfactant, and improves the dispersibility and atom utilization rate of each metal atom in the obtained catalyst. The reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize methanol has high catalytic activity and methanol selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for catalyst for synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation, prepared as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the molar ratio of copper source, zinc source and zirconium source is 4-6:1-3:2-4. In the step (1), the copper source comprises cupric nitrate trihydrate, the zinc source comprises zinc nitrate hexahydrate, and the zirconium source comprises zirconium nitrate pentahydrate. In the step (1), the ratio of the total molar weight of the copper source, zinc source and zirconium source to the molar amount of citric acid is 1-2:1. In the step (1), the surfactant comprises polyethylene glycol or hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide, where the adding amount of the polyethylene glycol 1-9 ml/6.18 g total mass of copper source, zinc source and zirconium source, and the adding amount of the hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide 0.1-5 g/6.18 g total mass of copper source, zinc source and zirconium source. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the grinding is carried out at room temperature for 30 minutes. In the step (2), the drying is carried out at 80-150 degrees C for 4-24 hours, and the calcining is carried out at 300-600 degrees C for 2-14 hours. The reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize methanol comprises placing catalyst in reactor, introducing hydrogen mixed gas, performing reduction treatment on the catalyst at 250-350 degrees C for 2-10 hours, introducing reaction mixed gas, and performing carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesis methanol at 200-280 degrees C and 2-5 MPa, where the hydrogen mixed gas is a mixed gas composed of hydrogen and nitrogen in a volume ratio of 1-3:10, the reaction mixed gas is a mixed gas composed of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in a volume ratio of 1:3-5, and the airspeed of the reaction mixed gas is 1000-10000 ml/gx h.

AB    NOVELTY - Production of acrylonitrile involves utilizing a source of carbon comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, (102) converting carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide to ethylene, dimerizing ethylene to produce 1-butene, isomerizing 1-butene to produce 2-butene, metathesizing 2-butene to produce propylene, and producing acrylonitrile by (105) ammoxidation of propylene.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ethylene which is not generated by the conversion of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide is input from another source. The methane generated during anaerobic digestion is used as fuel source to power the other process of the method. The biomass comprises one or more of crops, agricultural waste, food waste, municipal solid waste, municipal liquid waste, wood waste, wood, wood processing waste, sawdust, paper waste, or compost. The carbon-containing compounds are found in biomass. The byproduct generated by the method is steam.Preferred Process: The conversion, isomerization, metathesis, and ammoxidation steps are performed in a one-pot synthesis. The conversion of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide involves electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide. The conversion process utilizes renewable sources of electricity. The method further involves performing anaerobic digestion of biodegradable materials to produce carbon dioxide and methane, combusting to process methane into additional carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, and incinerating carbon-containing compounds as the source of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide. The method involves performing carbon dioxide and methane by microorganisms in an air-free environment by digestion of biomass. The combustion process is utilized to generate power. The steam is used in one or more of the isomerizing and metathesizing process. The metathesis process of 2-butene further involves utilizing ethylene produced through conversion of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, combusting byproducts generated by each process to generate carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide. The industrial process involves taking place at one or more of power generation processes.

TI Producing a carbonate bonded, compacted article            involves providing a particulate carbonatable material,            compacting the particulate material to form a compact            and carbonating compact for a predetermined period of            time with a gas.
AU VAN MECHELEN D

GA 202100991U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a carbonate bonded, compacted                article involves providing a particulate                carbonatable material, compacting the particulate                material to form a compact and carbonating compact                for a predetermined period of time with a gas which                contains carbon dioxide to produce carbonates thus                transforming the compact into carbonate bonded,                compacted article. The carbonation of s started and                subsequently continued for at least 1 hour with a                low partial carbon dioxide pressure in gas, after                which carbonation of compact is continued for at                least 8 hours with a high partial carbon dioxide                pressure in gas, low partial carbon dioxide                pressure being lower than 0.5 bars and high partial                carbon dioxide pressure being equal to or higher                than 0.5 bars.
   USE - Method for producing a carbonate bonded,                compacted article.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce a carbonate                bonded, compacted article with improved mechanical                strength.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbonation is                  continued for at least 1.5 hours with low partial                  carbon dioxide pressure in gas. The carbonation                  of compact is continued for less than 16 hours,                  preferably for less than 12 hours and more                  preferably for less than 8 hours, with low                  partial carbon dioxide pressure in gas. The low                  partial carbon dioxide pressure is lower than                  0.45 bars. The compact is started with gas having                  a temperature lower than 50 degrees C, preferably                  lower than 45 degrees C and more preferably lower                  than 40 degrees C. Preferred Components: The                  particulate material comprises carbonatable slag                  from a metal production process, slag from the                  production of phosphorus, bottom ash and/or fly                  ash, the particulate material preferably                  comprises steel slag, in particular stainless                  steel slag.

PN CN111151285-A; CN111151285-B
TI Preparation of nitrogen-doped porous carbon-loaded zinc sulfide nanocomposite material used as photocatalyst e.g. for cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide by dissolving and dispersing zinc salt and sulfur-containing protein into water.
AU LIU Y
   FANG Y
   TANG F
   WANG L
   MA L
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202044290L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nitrogen-doped porous carbon-loaded zinc sulfide (ZnS) nanocomposite material comprises dissolving and dispersing zinc salt and sulfur-containing protein into water, freeze-drying and pyrolyzing.
   USE - The method is for preparation of nitrogen-doped porous carbon-loaded ZnS nanocomposite material used as photocatalyst for cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide or methylation of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require the introduction of template or acid-base for post-processing, and provides nanocomposite material, which has a large number of pore channel structures, rich Lewis acid, basic sites, good photothermal conversion performance, excellent selectivity and high catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) nitrogen-doped porous carbon-loaded ZnS nanocomposite material;
   (2) application of the nanocomposite material as photocatalyst for cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide; and
   (3) application of the nanocomposite material as photocatalyst for methylation of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The zinc salt is zinc chloride, zinc acetate, zinc sulfate, and/or zinc nitrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The application method comprises taking the nanocomposite material as catalyst, introducing carbon dioxide (CO2) into a mixed system of tetra-n-butylammonium bromide, dimethylformamide and epoxy compound, and carrying out catalytic cycloaddition reaction under illumination condition. It comprises introducing CO2 into a mixed system of phenylsilane and amine substrate, taking the nanocomposite material as catalyst, and carrying out catalytic methylation reaction under illumination condition.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The sulfur-containing protein is bovine serum albumin (BSA) and/or egg white. The mass ratio of zinc salt and sulfur-containing protein is 1.15:1-3:1. The pyrolysis is conducted under the protection atmosphere at 700-1000 degrees C for 2-4 hours. Preferred Component: The nanocomposite material has mesopores with diameter of 2-10 nm, micropores with diameter of less than 2 nm, micropores specific surface area of 1300-1800 m2/g, mesoporous specific surface area of 700-1200 m2/g, and total pore volume of 0.80-1.00 cm3/g.

TI Preparing copper-based carbon dioxide            electrocatalytic material comprises e.g. mixing the            oxidant solution and organic ligand solution to prepare            mixed solution, placing metallic copper into mixed            solution, and causing organic ligand.
AU XU Z
   LIN P
   CHEN C
   WU T
   CAO Y
   ZENG X
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 2019A5236A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-based carbon dioxide                electrocatalytic material comprises mixing oxidant                solution and organic ligand solution to prepare                mixed solution, placing the metallic copper into                the mixed solution, causing the organic ligand to                be adsorbed on specific crystal plane of metallic                copper, make the crystal surface of the metallic                copper not adsorbed by the organic ligand undergo                an oxidation reaction, performing cleaning                treatment on the metal copper after the oxidation                reaction, removing the organic ligand adsorbed on                the metallic copper crystal plane, and carrying out                electrochemical reduction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                copper-based carbon dioxide electro catalytic                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves catalytic activity to                carbon dioxide and selectivity to multi-carbon                products and realizes the regulation of different                crystal structure; the obtained product has high                surface roughness and grain boundary density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                copper-based carbon dioxide electrocatalytic                material prepared by the above mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidant solution                  is hydrogen peroxide solution, potassium                  persulfate solution, sodium persulfate solution,                  amine persulfate solution, and/or potassium                  persulfate solution. The concentration of the                  oxidant solution is 0.01mM-2M. The organic ligand                  solution is sodium dodecyl sulfate solution,                  sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate solution, and/or                  cetyl ammonium bromide solution. The                  concentration of the organic ligand solution is                  0.5-4 mM. Preferred Method: The oxidation                  reaction time is 5-120 minutes. The metallic                  copper is copper sheet, copper powder, copper                  block, copper mesh and/or copper wire.

PT P
PN CN110394145-A; CN110394145-B
TI Preparing spherical lithium orthosilicate-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, by synthesizing lithium orthosilicate powder, adding lithium orthosilicate powder and agar, dripping slurry to dimethyl silicone oil, solidifying and calcining sample.
AU HU Y
   LI H
   QU M
   QU W
   YANG J
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201992765D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a spherical lithium orthosilicate-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, involves (i) synthesizing the lithium orthosilicate powder using a lithium source and a silicon source as raw materials by different preparation process, (ii) adding the lithium orthosilicate powder and agar to water and uniformly mixing by heating to obtain a slurry, (iii) dripping slurry to dimethyl silicone oil at 0-35 degrees C, leaving still, solidifying the slurry in a spherical solid, washing and drying, (iv) calcining the dried sample in an oxygen atmosphere and burning the agar contained in the sample to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing spherical lithium orthosilicate-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of the spherical lithium orthosilicate-based carbon dioxide adsorbent with excellent sphericity, ability of cyclic adsorption of carbon dioxide, and smooth surface, by simple, convenient and industrial process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (ii), the mass ratio of the water, the lithium orthosilicate powder and the agar is (1-10):1:(0.1-0.4), preferably (3-6):1:(0.15-0.3). Preferred Components: In step (i), the lithium source and the silicon source comprise two groups, respectively, lithium carbonate and silicon dioxide, lithium acetate and silica sol, where the two groups of raw materials for preparing the lithium orthosilicate powder respectively carried out by the solid phase reaction method and the soaking precipitation method. Preferred Method: The step (iii) involves adding the slurry to the dimethyl silicone oil through a burette or syringe, dripping the slurry at 1-16 ml/minute, washing the slurry using petroleum ether and drying at 35-60 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the heating process is carried out at 90-120 degrees C. In step (iii), the temperature of the dimethyl silicone oil is controlled at 15-25 degrees C. In step (iv), the calcination is carried out at 500-1000 degrees C for 30-90 minutes, preferably 800-900 degrees C for 50-70 minutes. Preferred Properties: The particle diameter of the spherical lithium orthosilicate-based carbon dioxide adsorbent is 3-4 mm.

   LI S

GA 201962098F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oxyacetylene carbon material by ball milling calcium carbide and carbon dioxide or carbonate comprises adding calcium carbide, carbon dioxide or carbonate into the ball mill jar, ball milling under the conditions of 300-600 revolutions/minute for 2-8 hours, pickling solid product, washing with water and drying to obtain oxyacetylene carbon material, where molar ratio between calcium carbide and carbon dioxide or carbonate is greater than or equal to 2:1.

PN CN109612000-A; CN109612000-B
TI Method for controlling fresh air system with fire            protection function, involves setting fire threshold on            PM2.5 sensor or carbon dioxide sensor of fresh air            system.
AU SUN S
   ZHAO H
AE TIANJIN DIWUJI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201935726H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting the fire threshold                on the PM2.5 sensor or carbon dioxide sensor of the                fresh air system. The fresh air inlet valve is                closed when the PM2.5 and carbon dioxide                concentration in the air is detected to reach the                fire threshold, and the exhaust power is increased.                The original operating mode is restored, when the                PM2.5 and carbon dioxide concentration in the air                is detected to be less than the fire                threshold.
   USE - Method for controlling fresh air system with                fire protection function.

PN CN109468353-A
TI Preparing chitosan oligosaccharide by enzymatic hydrolysis of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid comprises e.g. adding chitosan into the acetic acid solution, grinding chitosan solution and then spray drying.
AU FANG X
   CHEN X
   YUAN G
   CHI H
   SUN H
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG OCEAN (UYZO-C)
GA 201926511Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chitosan oligosaccharide by enzymatic hydrolysis of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid comprises (i) adding chitosan into the acetic acid solution to obtain chitosan solution, grinding chitosan solution and spray drying to obtain a pretreated chitosan powder, (ii) adding sodium alginate solution into the glutaraldehyde solution, stirring at room temperature, adding chitosan solution, carrying out low temperature stirring cross-linking to obtain a sodium alginate hydrosol of immobilized chitosanase, ultrasonically activating to obtain activated immobilized chitosanase, and (iii) adding pretreated chitosan prepared in step (i) into the ultrahigh pressure reaction vessel, adding activated immobilized chitosanase prepared in step (ii), passing carbon dioxide gas, pressurized heating to bring carbon dioxide to a supercritical state, continuously heating to the enzymatic temperature and performing enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing chitosan oligosaccharide by enzymatic hydrolysis of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple preparation process; and is convenient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), concentration of the chitosan solution is 1-3 w/v%. The ball milling ratio of the grinding process is 5-6:1, speed of the ball mill is 1000-2500 revolutions per minute, and time is 30-90 minutes. In step (ii), mass ratio between sodium alginate, glutaraldehyde and chitosan is 1:0.6-0.9:0.2-0.5. In step (ii), frequency of ultrasonic activation is 12-13 kHz, strength is 20-25 W/cm2, and time is 10-20 minutes. The activated chitosan has an activity of 50-70 U/g. In step (iii), chitosan oligosaccharide has a molecular weight of 1200-1500. Preferred Method: In step (ii), temperature of low temperature stirring cross-linking is 4-10 degrees C, speed is 200-500 revolutions per minute, and time is 2-4 hours. In step (iii), pressure of pressurized heating is 7.4-8 MPa, and temperature is 33-40 degrees C. The temperature of enzymatic hydrolysis is 45-50 degrees C, enzymatic hydrolysis time is 45-90 minutes, and pH is 6-6.5.

PN CN108250061-A
TI Synthesizing 5-chloro salicylic acid comprises e.g. adding para-chlorophenol and strong base into a solvent mixture of non-polar solvent and water-insoluble solvent, adding catalyst, reacting, cooling, removing carbon dioxide and refining.
AU HU L
   HU G
AE UNIV JINGCHU TECHNOLOGY (UYJI-Non-standard)
GA 201854533U
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing 5-chloro salicylic acid comprises (i) adding para-chlorophenol and strong base into a solvent mixture of non-polar solvent and water-insoluble solvent, where molar ratio of para-chlorophenol and a strong base is 1:1 and removing water by oil-water separation to obtain a suspension of sodium para-chlorophenolate; (ii) adding catalyst which is capable of dissolving sodium p-chlorophenolate and carbon dioxide added into a suspension of sodium p-chlorophenolate, placing in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, heating to 100-150 degrees C, reacting until there is no absorption of carbon dioxide, cooling and removing carbon dioxide to obtain a mixed liquid; and (iii) refining mixed liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing 5-chloro salicylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical and convenient to operate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of refining mixed liquid comprises (a) heating mixed liquid to 40-70 degrees C, extracting with 40-70 degrees C hot water and layering to obtain aqueous and an organic layer; (b) adding activated carbon into the aqueous layer, decoloring at 50-70 degrees C for 0.1-1 hours and filtering under heat to obtain a filtrate; (c) adding reducing agent is into the filtrate for carrying out reduction and fading, controlling temperature of at 50-70 degrees C, adjusting pH value to 2-3, carrying out precipitation, fitering precipitate, cooling precipitate completely, filtering, and washing filter cake with water until all chloride ions all removed; and (d) drying filter cake. The method of refining mixed liquid further comprises adjusting pH of aqueous layer in step (a) to 6-7, subjecting to back extraction with a non-polar solvent, and recycling organic layer obtained in organic phase. The pressure of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide atmosphere is 0.2-1 MPa. Preferred Components: The non-polar solvent and water-insoluble solvent are both toluene or xylene. The reagent used to adjust the pH is dilute sulfuric acid. The strong base is sodium hydroxide. The catalyst is formamide, N-methylformamide, N,N-dimethylformamide, acetamide, N-methylacetamide, dimethylacetamide and dimethyl sulfoxide, where weight of catalyst is 2-10 wt.% of para-chlorophenol. The weight of activated carbon is 1-5 wt.% of para-chlorophenol. The reducing agent is sodium thiosulfate, sodium dithionite, sodium bisulfite or sodium sulfite, where weight of reducing agent is 3-6 wt.% of para-chlorophenol.

   HE F
   WANG X
   XIE N
   XIE F
   XIE M
AE FORTY EIGHT RES INST CHINA ELECTRONIC TE (CETC-C)
GA 201841730T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing acid treatment on the aqueous solution to be removed to remove inorganic substances and divide into a detection sample and a control sample. The detection sample and the control sample are separated into gas-liquid separation. The corresponding carbon dioxide content is obtained through optical detection. AB    NOVELTY - A tobacco in a closed baking room baking time shortening method involves manually filling the oxygen during the early yellowing period and maintaining the oxygen concentration in the closed baking room at 0.5-2%.    USE - Method for shortening tobacco baking time in a closed baking room.

PN CN111961373-A
TI Water-based coating of nano calcium carbonate powder comprises acrylic resin, epoxy resin, styrene-acrylic emulsion, modified nano calcium carbonate, kaolin, titanium dioxide, modified hydrotalcite powder.
AU WEI M
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B81015
AB    NOVELTY - Water-based coating of nano calcium carbonate powder comprises 30-40 pts. wt. acrylic resin, 5-10 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 15-25 pts. wt. styrene-acrylic emulsion, 10-15 pts. wt. modified nano calcium carbonate, 5-8 pts. wt. kaolin, 5-8 parts pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 3-5 pts. wt. modified hydrotalcite powder, 0.5-1 pts. wt. film-forming agent, 0.5-1 pts. wt. leveling agent, 1-2 pts. wt. antifreeze agent and 60-80 pts. wt. water. The method for preparing the modified nano calcium carbonate involves stirring and mixing nano calcium hydroxide and deionized water to obtain a slurry. The concentration of nano calcium hydroxide in the slurry is 60-90g/L, then urea is added and stirred for 15-20min under ultrasonic conditions, and then heated to a constant temperature of 50-60 degrees C, then added ammonia water to adjust the pH to 7-8, and then passed in carbon dioxide for carbonization reaction for 30-60 minutes, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 100-150L/h.
   USE - Water-based coating of nano calcium carbonate powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The water-based coating of nano calcium carbonate powder has good rheology and high water resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Water-based coating of nano calcium carbonate powder comprises 30-40 pts. wt. acrylic resin, 5-10 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 15-25 pts. wt. styrene-acrylic emulsion, 10-15 pts. wt. modified nano calcium carbonate, 5-8 pts. wt. kaolin, 5-8 parts pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 3-5 pts. wt. modified hydrotalcite powder, 0.5-1 pts. wt. film-forming agent, 0.5-1 pts. wt. leveling agent, 1-2 pts. wt. antifreeze agent and 60-80 pts. wt. water. The method for preparing the modified nano calcium carbonate involves stirring and mixing nano calcium hydroxide and deionized water to obtain a slurry. The concentration of nano calcium hydroxide in the slurry is 60-90g/L, then urea is added and stirred for 15-20min under ultrasonic conditions, and then heated to a constant temperature of 50-60 degrees C, then added ammonia water to adjust the pH to 7-8, and then passed in carbon dioxide for carbonization reaction for 30-60 minutes, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 100-150L/h, and then added stearic acid and sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate and continued to introduce carbon dioxide for carbonization reaction for 1-2 hours. The slurry is filtered, dried, and crushed to obtain modified nano calcium carbonate. The mass ratio of the slurry, stearic acid and sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate is 100:(2-5):(1-3). The preparation method of the modified hydrotalcite powder includes stirring and mixing starch and water at a mass ratio of 1:(3 to 5), and then ultrasonically dispersing for 10-15 minutes under ultrasonic conditions, and then heating at 70-80 degrees C, after constant temperature, adding double-chain quaternary ammonium salt Gemini amphiphile molecule and continue to stir for 2-3 hours. The added amount of the double-chain quaternary ammonium salt Gemini amphiphile molecule is 1-3 wt.% starch mass, and then added talc powder, then heated to 90 degrees C and continue to stir for 20-30 hours to obtain modified hydrotalcite powder. The mass ratio of described talcum powder and starch is 1:40. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a preparation method of nano calcium carbonate powder water-based paint, which involves:
   (A) stirring and mixing nano-calcium hydroxide and deionized water to obtain a slurry, where concentration of nano-calcium hydroxide in the slurry is 60-90 g/L;
   (B) adding urea and stirring for 15-20 minutes under ultrasonic conditions, and then heating at 50-60 degrees C at a constant temperature;
   (C) adding ammonia water to adjust the pH to 7-8, then pass in carbon dioxide for carbonization reaction for 30-60min, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 100-150L/h;
   (D) adding stearic acid and sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate and continue to introduce carbon dioxide for carbonization reaction for 1 to 2 hours, and then press filter, drying and crushing the slurry to obtain modified nano calcium carbonate, where mass ratio of the slurry: stearic acid: sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate is 100:(2-5):(1-3), stirring and mixing starch and water according to the mass ratio of 1:(3-5), and then ultrasonically disperse for 10-15 minutes under ultrasonic conditions, then heat to 70-80 degrees C;
   (E) adding talc powder, and then heating to 90 degrees C and continue stirring for 20-30 hours to obtain modified hydrotalcite powder, where mass ratio of talc powder and starch is 1:40;
   (F) putting the kaolin and titanium dioxide into a grinder to grind, then sieving and mixing the ground kaolin and titanium dioxide, then adding the modified hydrotalcite and modified nano calcium carbonate, stir and mix uniformly to obtain a first mixture; and
   (G) adding water, acrylic resin, epoxy resin, and styrene-acrylic emulsion to fisrt mixture and stirring for 10-15 minutes, then adding film-forming agent, leveling agent, and antifreeze agent, stirring for 45-60 minutes, and then sonicate under ultrasonic conditions and treating for 20-30 minutes to obtain nano calcium carbonate powder water-based paint.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The film-forming agent is 2,2,4-trimethyl-1,3-pentanediol monoisobutyrate. The leveling agent is a polydimethylsiloxane copolymer. The antifreeze agent is obtained by mixing ethylene glycol and propylene glycol in equal volumes.

PN CN113753935-A
TI Co-producing nano barium sulfate and nano calcium carbonate involves adding hydrochloric acid into barium carbonate to prepare barium chloride solution, collecting carbon dioxide gas, feeding carbon dioxide and calcium hydroxide slurry into Taylor reactor for gas-liquid precipitation reaction.
AU ZHANG X
   XIANG T
   WANG G
   ZOU J
   LONG L
AE KUNSHAN DUPLEXX ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (KUNS-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU RES INST CHEM IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022046623
AB    NOVELTY - The method for co-producing nano barium sulfate and nano calcium carbonate involves: adding hydrochloric acid into barium carbonate used as a barium source to prepare a barium chloride solution, and collecting carbon dioxide gas; feeding the carbon dioxide and the calcium hydroxide slurry into an improved Taylor reactor for gas-liquid precipitation reaction, filtering, washing, drying and crushing to prepare nano calcium carbonate, collecting the filtered filtrate and washing water, and mixing into water for preparing a calcium hydroxide slurry; and feeding the barium chloride solution and sulfuric acid into an improved Taylor reactor for liquid-liquid precipitation reaction, filtering, washing, drying and crushing to prepare nano barium sulfate, collecting the filtered filtrate and the washing solution, mixing to obtain a hydrochloric acid solution, and concentrating, The improved Taylor reactor is conical.
   USE - Method for co-producing nano barium sulfate and nano calcium carbonate used in rubber, ink, coating, paper-makings and composite materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the purpose of the industrial production of nano-barium sulfate, and fully guarantee the quality of nano-barium sulfate. The method effectively improves the particle size, whiteness, purity and other characteristics of nano barium sulfate products, and reduce the production cost of nano barium sulfate. The method uses barium carbonate as a barium source, adopt acid hydrolysis process to convert into barium chloride solution, and then carry out liquid-liquid precipitation reaction with sulfuric acid, and recover and recycle hydrochloric acid, so that the whole process has no waste acid and other waste liquid or salt discharge, thus reduces the pressure on environmental protection. The use of carbon dioxide collection and recycling realizes the preparation of nano-calcium carbonate products, so that the entire process has no waste gas emissions, and produces nano-calcium carbonate products with higher added value, reducing the production cost of the entire process. The Taylor reactor is improved to adopt the cone-shaped Taylor reactor structure to strengthen the uniformity and rapidity of material mixing, so that the material reacts to form explosive nucleation to prepare nano-scale particles, which increases the added value of precipitated products. The process flow and device structure are simple, easy to control, high production efficiency, no exhaust gas or waste liquid discharge, and can meet the needs of green and environmentally friendly chemical process routes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: 1. the nano barium sulfate obtained by the above process; 2. a device for co-producing nano barium sulfate and nano calcium carbonate; and 3. an improved Taylor reactor for the production of nano barium sulfate and/or nano calcium carbonate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow diagram of the method for co-producing nano barium sulfate and nano calcium carbonate. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI New core-shell catalyst i.e. copper-based complex useful for preparing low molecular weight of carbon olefin by performing hydrogenation coupling of carbon dioxide, prepared by e.g. preparing salt solution, stirring and post processing.

AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)

GA 2019167666
AB    NOVELTY - Core-shell catalyst i.e. copper-based complex (I) is new, where (Ax-B1y)Oz-M is copper-based catalyst and (Ax-B1y)Oz is solid solution, M acts as a dual function of structure auxiliary agent and carrier, and M is synergistically catalyzed with (Ax-B1y)Oz, C1 is zinc-modified SAPO-34 molecular sieve, zinc/aluminum ratio is 0.01-0.5, (preferably zinc/aluminum ratio is 0.03-0.08), the mass of (Ax-B1y)Oz-M is 50-80 wt.% of the total mass of the catalyst, (preferably 70-80 wt.%), and C1 (balance amount), (Ax-B1y)Oz-M is a core in catalyst, C1 is a shell. The (Ax-B1y -)Oz-M bonded to C1 is formed by silica sol coupling, the core of the catalyst is catalytically propelling performing hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to alcohol, the shell of the catalyst is catalytically propelled to dehydrating alcohol to olefins, especially low molecular weight of carbon olefin.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing low molecular weight of carbon olefin by performing hydrogenation coupling of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: achieves high selectivity; reduces contact area between interfaces and also reduces methane and C5+ product selectivity; and inhibits secondary reaction initiated by hydrogenation reaction of intermediate product and re-adsorption of primary olefin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Core-shell catalyst i.e. copper-based complex of formula (Ax-B1y) Oz-M-C (I) is new, where (Ax-B1y)Oz-M is copper-based catalyst and (Ax-B1y)Oz is solid solution, M acts as a dual function of structure auxiliary agent and carrier, and M is synergistically catalyzed with (Ax-B1y)Oz, C1 is zinc-modified SAPO-34 molecular sieve, zinc/aluminum ratio is 0.01-0.5, (preferably zinc/aluminum ratio is 0.03-0.08), the mass of (Ax-B1y)Oz-M is 50-80 wt.% of the total mass of the catalyst, (preferably 70-80 wt.%), and C1 (balance amount), (Ax-B1y)Oz-M is a core in catalyst, C1 is a shell. The (Ax-B1y -)Oz-M bonded to C1 is formed by silica sol coupling, the core of the catalyst is catalytically propelling performing hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to alcohol, the shell of the catalyst is catalytically propelled to dehydrating alcohol to olefins, especially low molecular weight of carbon olefin, the core of the catalyst, shell interaction coupling metal and molecular sieve activity, tandem reaction occurs during hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, synergistically preparing low molecular weight of carbon olefin, the amount of copper in the catalyst is 30-60 wt.%.
   A = copper;
   B1 = zinc, manganese or cerium; and
   O = oxygen;
   z = 1-4; and
   M = oxides of aluminum, zirconium, titanium or germanium.
   Provided that: x+y = 2. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprising (i) preparing salt solution containing elements A and B1 in a molar ratio of 1:0.5-5, adding 10-40 wt.% nitrate solution containing M element (preferably salt solution containing aluminum, zirconium elements), mixing, stirring at a speed of 100-300 revolutions/minute for 10-30 minutes, dropwise adding 0.5-2 mol/l sodium carbonate solution in a water bath at 50-80 degrees C, controlling pH to 7-10, continuously stirring for 2-6 hours, allowing to stand for overnight, drying at 120 degrees C for 12 hours, roasting at 250-450 degrees C, grinding, and granulating to 20-40-meshes, (ii) (iia) dissolving phosphoric acid as phosphorus source in water, adding zinc nitrate solution as zinc source and aluminum isopropoxide as aluminum source in a ratio of 0.01-0.5, and stirring, (iib) dropwise adding mixture solution of silica sol and tetraethylammonium hydroxide as template, continuously stirring, performing hydrothermal reaction by heating in water bath at 50-80 degrees C and stirring for 2-8 hours to obtain zinc-modified SAPO-34, and (iiic) allowing to stand, then placing in to a reaction kettle, crystallizing at 160-200 degrees C for 24-72 hours, filtering, centrifuging, washing, drying, baking at 500-600 degrees C for 4-6 hours, and grinding to more than 100 meshes, and (iii) mixing step (i) and step (ii) product in a ratio of 2:10, adding silica sol to bond step (i) and step (ii) product together, then calcining in a muffle furnace at 400-600 degrees C for 1-4 hours to obtain final product, where in step (i), on the basis of obtaining step (i) product, it is also impregnating with magnesium nitrate or cerium nitrate solution to obtain modified step (i) product of magnesium and cerium. Preferred Method: The catalyst is applied to performing hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide to obtain low molecular weight of carbon olefin, and the reaction conditions are: the ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 2-3, temperature is 350-450 degrees C, and pressure is 2-5 MPa, 1000-9000 per hour.

TI Device useful for preparing calcareous sinter preform comprises e.g. a shell is a hollow shell, and comprises combustion cavity, buffer cavity and calcium preform preparation chamber arranged from top to bottom.
AU DAI Q
   DENG Y
   DONG J
   DANG Z
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 201891977J
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises e.g. a shell is a hollow shell. A cavity of the shell comprises combustion cavity, buffer cavity and calcium preform preparation chamber arranged from top to bottom. The combustion chamber is provided with a biomass combustion furnace component which is useful for burning biomass fuel and generating heat and carbon dioxide. Multiple air inlet channels are defined in the side wall of the shell corresponding to the combustion chamber, and each of the air inlet channels communicates with the combustion chamber and the outer portion of the shell. A buffering component is set in the buffer cavity, and comprises an upper layer of high temperature resistant material and a lower layer of high temperature resistant material, where the upper layer of high temperature resistant material is uniformly provided with an upper through hole, and the lower layer of high temperature resistant material is uniformly provided with a lower through hole.
   USE - The device is useful for preparing calcareous sinter preform (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: avoids the problems of the above prior art; utilizes discarded or broken calcareous; improves the stability; and repairs effect of calcareous repair materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises a shell is a hollow shell. A cavity of the shell comprises combustion cavity, buffer cavity and calcium preform preparation chamber arranged from top to bottom. The combustion chamber is provided with a biomass combustion furnace component which is useful for burning biomass fuel and generating heat and carbon dioxide. Multiple air inlet channels are defined in the side wall of the shell corresponding to the combustion chamber, and each of the air inlet channels communicates with the combustion chamber and the outer portion of the shell. A buffering component is set in the buffer cavity, and comprises an upper layer of high temperature resistant material and a lower layer of high temperature resistant material, where the upper layer of high temperature resistant material is uniformly provided with an upper through hole, and the lower layer of high temperature resistant material is uniformly provided with a lower through hole. The upper through hole and the lower through hole are staggered in a vertical direction. The calcareous sinter preform preparation chamber is provided with a calcareous sinter preform preparation assembly which comprises a temperature sensor, a carbon dioxide concentration sensor, a gas permeable panel, and at least one load bearing frame. The load bearing frame is used for placing and supporting the shaping blank. The temperature sensor and the carbon dioxide concentration sensor are respectively used for monitoring temperature and carbon dioxide concentration in the preparation chamber of the calcareous sinter preform. The transparent panel is set on the top of the calcareous sinter preform preparation chamber, and is provided with multiple through holes.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device for preparing calcareous sinter perform.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a)                  adding chlorine-containing porous resin                  microspheres and triphenylphosphine to                  dimethylamide respectively, heating the reaction                  to obtain a chlorine-containing intermediate and                  (b) washing the chlorine-containing intermediate,                  placing in the potassium Iodide solution, keeping                  at a temperature and allowing to stand to obtain                  a catalyst. The step (a) comprises preparing                  porous structure containing chlorine-containing                  porous resin microsphere to the deionized water                  sequentially, adding vinylidene chloride, benzoyl                  peroxide, ethylene glycol dimethacrylate, toluene                  and white oil, reacting for 8 hours at                  70&#8451;, filtering the solid phase and                  washing to obtain the chlorine-containing porous                  resin microsphere, (b) loading nano-iron on the                  porous resin microsphere containing chlorine,                  placing the porous resin microsphere containing                  chlorine and ferric chloride powder in ethanol,                  stirring and dropping sodium borohydride                  solution, stirring and reacting for 10 hours,                  filtering, washing to obtain the                  chlorine-containing porous resin microsphere                  loaded with nano iron and (c) loading nano-iron                  on triphenylphosphine, placing triphenylphosphine                  in a ferric chloride solution, stirring for 30                  minutes, allowing to stand for 4 hours,                  filtering, and drying to obtain nano-iron-loaded                  triphenylphosphine. The step (a) comprises                  preparing porous structure containing                  chlorine-containing porous resin microsphere to                  the deionized water sequentially, adding                  vinylidene chloride, benzoyl peroxide, ethylene                  glycol dimethacrylate, toluene and white oil,                  reacting for 8 hours at 70&#8451;, filtering                  the solid phase and washing to obtain the                  chlorine-containing porous resin microsphere, (b)                  loading nano-iron on the porous resin microsphere                  containing chlorine, placing the porous resin                  microsphere containing chlorine and ferric                  chloride powder in ethanol, stirring and dropping                  sodium borohydride solution, stirring and                  reacting for 10 hours, filtering, washing to                  obtain the chlorine-containing porous resin                  microsphere loaded with nano iron and (c) loading                  nano-iron on triphenylphosphine, placing                  triphenylphosphine in a ferric chloride solution,                  stirring for 30 minutes, allowing to stand for 4                  hours, filtering, and drying to obtain                  nano-iron-loaded triphenylphosphine.Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  chlorine-containing porous resin microspheres:                  triphenylphosphine: dimethylamide is                  1:(0.4-0.6):(6-8). PN CN113150249-A; CN113150249-B
TI Preparing diacetylenic conjugated microporous            polymer used for adsorption and storage of gas or            pollutants, or in field of optoelectronics, involves            using one or more aromatic compounds containing            polyhalogenated alkynyl groups as monomers.
AU TAN B
   LIU Q
   JIN S
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
   WUHAN HUAKE ZHONGYING NANO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
   WUHAN NEW ENERGY INST CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
   BEIJING BIOLOGICAL PROD INST (WUHA-Non-standard)
   WUHAN HUAKE ZHONGYING NANO TECHNOLOGY CO (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2021910130
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing diacetylenic conjugated microporous                polymer involves using one or more aromatic                compounds containing polyhalogenated alkynyl groups                as monomers, and dispersing the monomers in an                organic solvent, adding iodine-containing as a                catalyst to carry out monomer self-polymerization                reaction, and obtaining diacetylenic conjugated                microporous polymer after the reaction is                completed.
   USE - Method for preparing diacetylenic conjugated                microporous polymer used for the adsorption and                storage of gas or pollutants, or in the field of                optoelectronics, where gas includes hydrogen,                carbon dioxide or methane, and the pollutants                include radioactive substances, waste oil or dyes                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high specific surface area and                high adsorption capacity, residual noble metal or                rare metal catalyst does not affect the intrinsic                property of conjugated microporous polymers and the                production cost is inexpensive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aromatic ring                  compound containing a polyhalogenated alkynyl                  group comprises tris(4-bromoethynylphenyl)amine,                  1,3,6,8-tetra(bromoethynyl)pyrene,                  1,3,5-tris(bromoethynyl)benzene,                  1,3,5-tris(p-bromoethynyl)phenylbenzene,                  tetra(bromoethynyl)tetraphenylmethane,                  1,1,2,2-tetra(4-bromoethynyl)tetrastyrene,                  1,2,4,5-tetra(bromoethynyl)benzene,                  2,4,6-(p-bromoethynylphenyl)-1,3,5-triazine or                  2,2',7,7                  '-tetra(bromoethynyl)-9,9'-spirobifluorene. The                  iodine-containing compound is one or more of                  potassium iodide, sodium iodide, lithium iodide,                  magnesium iodide and tetrabutylammonium iodide.                  The organic solvent is toluene, xylene,                  trimethylbenzene, dichlorobenzene, 1,4-dioxane,                  acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N,N,-dimethylacetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone and m-cresol. Preferred                  Process: The iodine-containing compound is added                  as a catalyst and subjected to ultrasonic                  treatment, under anhydrous and oxygen-free                  conditions, the monomer self-condensation                  reaction is carried out at 120-150 degrees C for                  48-72 hours, after the reaction is completed,                  wash with methanol, distilled water,                  dimethylformamide, and acetone successively, then                  successively use methanol, tetrahydrofuran,                  soxhlet extraction and purification, and finally                  dry to obtain the conjugated microporous polymer.                  The container containing the monomer, organic                  solvent and catalyst is rapidly frozen in a                  liquid nitrogen environment, and the inside is                  vacuumed and sealed with a flame. Preferred                  Conditions: The diacetylenic conjugated                  microporous polymer includes a skeleton and a                  pore channel formed between the skeletons, and                  the skeleton is aromatic and uses diacetylene as                  the connecting unit. The polymer has a layered                  structure, and the pore channels include                  micropores with a pore diameter of less than 2                  nm, mesopores with a pore diameter of 2-50 nm,                  and macropores with a pore diameter greater than                  50 nm.

PN IN201941027729-A
TI Synthesis of metal oxide nanostructure and heterostructure used in e.g. photocatalytic water treatment, involves metal salt comprising metal nitrate and organic salt containing glycine, and exposing mixture to concentrated sunlight.
AU BYRAPPA K
   HEZAM A
   NAMRATHA K
AE UNIV MYSORE (UYMY-Non-standard)
GA 2021097318
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of metal oxide nanostructure and heterostructure involves (101) mixing a metal salt and an organic salt to form a homogeneous mixture, and (102) exposing the mixture to a concentrated sunlight for a time period of at least 0.06-4 minutes. The metal salt is a metal nitrate. The organic salt is glycine or sucrose.
   USE - Synthesis of metal oxide nanostructure and heterostructure. Uses include but are not limited to photocatalytic water treatment, photocatalytic hydrogen production, gas sensing, carbon dioxide reduction, photosynthesis, antibacterial agent, and antioxidant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple synthesis of metal oxide nanostructure and heterostructure having excellent photocatalytic activity using sunlight as sustainable energy source.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the synthesis of metal oxide nanostructure and heterostructure.
   Mixing metal salt and organic salt (101)
   Exposing the mixture (102)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The metal oxide nanostructures have a particle size of 3-20 nm. Preferred Components: The metal oxide nanostructures comprise iron (III) oxide, iron (II) oxide, zinc oxide, cerium oxide, bismuth oxide, aluminum oxide, lanthanum oxide, copper oxide, cobalt oxide, cobalt (II, III) oxide, and nickel oxide. The metal oxide heterostructure comprises iron (III) oxide/iron (II) oxide, iron (III) oxide/cerium oxide, aluminum oxide/cerium oxide, bismuth oxide/cerium oxide, and bismuth oxide/cerium oxide/iron (III) oxide. The metal salt comprises metal sulfide or metal alloys. The metal sulfide comprises cadmium sulfide, zinc sulfide, nickel sulfide, nickel disulfide, cobalt disulfide, bismuth sulfide, and indium sulfide. The metal salt acts as an oxidizer. The sunlight is concentrated using a convex lens. The metal oxide heterostructures include porous nature and uniform pores size distribution having higher concentration of oxygen vacancies. The metal salt and organic salt are dissolved in water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The metal alloys comprises 50% iron-nickel alloy.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic salt acts as an organic fuel.

PT P
PN CN112111465-A
TI New recombinant adenovirus expressing porcine delta coronavirus useful in preparing porcine delta coronavirus vaccine.
AU ZHAO P
   LV Y
   ZHANG X
   JING H
   WU Y
AE UNIV HENAN ANIMAL HUSBANDRY & ECONOMY (UAHE-C)
GA 2020C9994Y
AB    NOVELTY - Recombinant adenovirus expressing porcine delta coronavirus comprising N gene of the porcine Delta coronavirus, and can stably express the N protein of the porcine Delta coronavirus, is new.
   USE - The recombinant adenovirus is useful in preparing a porcine delta coronavirus vaccine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The recombinant adenovirus has high titer, large amount of expressed protein, eukaryotic expression, good immunogenicity, better function of activating the immune system, and broad application prospects for making diagnostic reagents and vaccines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for constructing the recombinant adenovirus expressing porcine Delta coronavirus comprising (i) amplification of the N gene of porcine Delta coronavirus, (ii) Double enzyme digestion of N gene and pDC315, (iii) N gene and pDC315 connection, (iv) transformation of competent cells, (v) extraction of pDC315/N recombinant plasmid, (vi) placing the AD293 cell line DMEM culture medium containing 10% calf serum is cultured in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and (vii) packaging of recombinant adenovirus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The N gene of the porcine Delta coronavirus comprises fully defined 1029 nucleotide sequence (SEQ ID NO:1) as given in the specification. The N protein of the porcine Delta coronavirus comprises fully defined 342 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO:2) as given in the specification. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the amplification of the N gene of porcine Delta coronavirus includes (I) Design primer PDCoV-N2-F comprises fully defined 36 nucleotide sequence (SEQ ID NO:5) as given in the specification, PDCoV-N2-R fully defined 66 nucleotide sequence (SEQ ID NO:6) as given in the specification, (II) PCR amplification comprises amplification system comprising 25 mu l 2xPhatan Master Mix 2xPhatan CoV, 2 mu l PD2-N-PD-R, 2 mu l pMd18T-PDCoV-N plasmid, 19 mu l double distilled water. The reaction program comprises first pre-denaturation at 94 degrees C for 5 minutes, denaturation at 94 degrees C for 30 seconds, annealing at 56 degrees C for 30 seconds, extension at 72 degrees C for 1.5 minutes, a total of 35 cycles, and finally extension at 72 degrees C for 10 minutes, and (III) purification and recovery of genes. The synthetic steps of the pMd18T-PDCoV-N plasmid comprises (1) treating the intestinal tissue of pig infected with PDCoV, adding 1 ml of sterile phosphate buffered saline (PBS), fully grinding and adding in a 1.5ml EP tube, repeatedly freezing and thawing for 3 times in -80 degrees C, 4 degrees C, 8, 000xg centrifuging for 10 minutes, taking the supernatant, (2) extracting RNA by using virus RNA extraction reagent box method, (3) Synthesis of the first strand cDNA Mix 3 mu l of total RNA, 1 mu l of Random hexamers, 4 mu l of ddH2O and separate them, adding them in a constant temperature water bath at 65? for 5 minutes, then quickly place them on ice to quench, and then bath them for 2 minutes, then mixing the mixed solution and the 2Xrt Mix 10L, HiScript Enzyme Mix 2 mu l for the first chain cDNA synthesis, 25 degrees C for 5 minutes, 50 degrees C for 45 minutes, 85 degrees C for 2 minutes, (4) amplification system of the PCR amplification of the N gene, 25 mu l Taq enzyme, 2 mu l PDCoV-N1-F, 2 mu l PDCoV-N1-R, 6 mu l cDNA, 15 mu l double distilled water, PDCoV-N1-F comprises 5'-cgtggttcttgctaatgcta-3' nucleotide sequence, and PDCoV-N1-R 5'-ctctggctatacactacgact-3' nucleotide sequence, firstly pre-denaturing for 5 minutes at 94 degrees C, denaturing for 30 seconds at 94 degrees C, annealing for 30 seconds at 56.2 degrees C, extending for 2 minutes at 72 degrees C, circulating for 35 times in total, at last extending for 10 minutes, (5) purifying and recycling the N gene and connecting the N gene with the T carrier, (7) transferring competent cell DH5 alpha, adding 100 mu DH5 alpha competent cell into the 1.5ml EP tube, thawing on the ice, adding connecting liquid, lightly mixing uniformly, ice water bath for 30 minutes, water bath at 42 degrees C, heat shock for 90 seconds, placing for 5 minutes, adding 900 mu l of Lysogeny broth (LB) liquid culture medium without adding ampicillin, reversing mixing uniformly, culturing shaking table at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, d. 3000xg centrifuging for 3 minutes, removing 850 mu l supernatant, uniformly mixing the bacterial suspension and coating the plate, culturing at constant temperature of 37 degrees C, taking white single colony by inoculating loop under super-clean bench, inoculating in LB culture solution containing Amp + resistance, shaking and culturing at 37 degrees C for 12 hours. In the step (iii), the N gene is connected to pDC315, and the connection system comprises 2 mu l 10xBuffer, 1 mu l T4-DNA-Ligase, 15 mu l N gene, 2 mu l pDC315, and 16 degrees C for 12 hours. In the step (iv), the transformation of competent cells includes (a) adding 100 mu l of DH5 alpha competent cells to a 1.5ml EP tube, thaw on ice, adding ligation solution, mix gently, and ice-water bath for 30 minutes, (b) 42 degrees C water bath, heat shock for 90 seconds, placing on ice for 5 minutes, (c) adding 900 mu l of LB liquid medium without ampicillin, mixing upside down, incubating at 37 degrees C on a shaker for 1 hours, centrifuging at 3000xg for 3 minutes, discarding 850 mu l of supernatant, and suspending the bacteria After the solution is mixed, all the plates are coated and incubated at 37 degrees C, and (e) picking a single white colony with an inoculating loop under the ultra-clean table, and inoculating it in Amp+ resistant LB broth, shaking culture at 37 degrees C for 12 hours. In the step (v), the extraction of pDC315/N recombinant plasmid includes (1) taking 5 ml the step (iv), dividing the bacterial solution cultured on a shaker into five 1.5ml EP tubes, centrifuging at 8,000xg for 2 minutes to collect the bacterial cells, and discarding the medium, (2) adding 250 mu l of Buffer P1 to an EP tube, suspending the bacteria thoroughly, transferring the liquid to the next EP tube, and suspending all the precipitate, (3) adding 250 mu l Buffer P2, and immediately mixing by gently inverting the centrifuge tube 5-10 times, and allowing to stand at room temperature for 2-4 minutes, (4) adding 350 mu l Buffer P3, and immediately mix by gently inverting the centrifuge tube 5-10 times, (5) centrifuging at 12,000xg for 5-10 minutes, transfer the supernatant to the adsorption tube, centrifuging at 8,000xg for 30 seconds, and pouring out the liquid in the collection tube, (6) adding 500 mu l Buffer DW1, centrifuging at 9,000xg for 30 seconds, discarding the liquid in the collection tube, (7) adding 500 mu l wash solution, centrifuging at 9,000xg for 30 seconds, pouring out the liquid in the collection tube, (8) repeating the step (7) once, (9) centrifuging the empty adsorption column at 9,000xg for 1 minute, and (10) adding the adsorption column into a clean 1.5ml centrifuge tube, adding 50 mu l ElutionBuffer to the center of the adsorption membrane, allowing to stand for 1 minute at room temperature, centrifuging for 1 minute, and saving the DNA solution in the tube. In the step (vii), the packaging of the recombinant adenovirus includes (a) spreading the AD293 cells obtained in step (6) into a six-well plate, add 3ml of 10% DMEM culture medium to each well, culturing in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and waiting until the cells grow to 80%, (b) preparing DNA system and transfection reagent system in two 1.5 ml EP tubes, where the DNA system includes 10 mu l of backbone plasmid pBHGlox-E1.3Cre, 2 mu l of pDC315/N plasmid, and 125 mu l of DMEM, the transfection reagent system includes 125 mu l of DMEM and 7.5 mu l of transfection reagent, gently mixing the above two systems and let stand at room temperature for 5 minutes, adding the DNA system to the transfection reagent system and mix upside down to obtain a mixed solution, allowing to stand for 10 minutes, and (c) aspirating the culture solution in the six-well plate of the step (a), washing twice with PBS, adding the mixed solution of step b to the six-well plate, continuing adding 3ml of 5% DMEM culture solution to the six-well plate at 37 degrees C, cultivating in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and collecting the virus solution after obvious lesions appear.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-E05; B04-E99; B04-F0100E; B04-F11B2E; B04-L01; B11-C01E; B14-S11A; B14-S11D3; B14-S12; C04-E05; C04-E99; C04-F0100E; C04-F11B2E; C04-L01; C11-C01E; C14-S11A; C14-S11D3; C14-S12; D05-A02; D05-H07; D05-H12F; D05-H18; D05-H99

TI Preparing para-electric phase lithium tantalite comprises e.g. mixing stoichiometric lithium carbonate and tantalum oxide, and sealing pressure calibration material and pressure transmission medium in diamond counter-anvil device.
AU HAN Y
   ZHANG C
   LIU X
   ZHANG X
   HAN T
   GAO C
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 2020C42834
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing para-electric phase lithium tantalite comprises mixing stoichiometric lithium carbonate and tantalum oxide, heating in a muffle furnace at 600 degrees C for 48 hours to discharge carbon dioxide, and heating at 800 degrees C for 24 hours to cooling sample to obtain lithium tantalite crystals, after grinding the lithium tantalite crystal for one hour, sealing pressure calibration material and the pressure transmission medium in diamond counter-anvil device, the pressure in the counter-anvil device is not less than 38.1 GPa, and maintaining pressure for 3 minutes to obtain the para-electric phase lithium tantalite material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing para-electric phase lithium tantalite.
   ADVANTAGE - The lithium tantalite: has simple operation, short preparation time, high purity and good repeatability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the diamond anvil device, using T301 steel sheet as gasket material, silicone oil as pressure transmission medium, and ruby fluorescence peak as pressure calibration object, after punching gasket, placing sample in a sealed sample cavity composed of diamond counter anvil surface and gasket. The pressure of diamond to anvil device is set at 38.1-50 GPa.

Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the extraction parameters are: the extraction temperature is 36-38 degrees C, preferably 37 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 21-22.5, preferably 22 Mpa, and the extraction time is 2-3, preferably 3 hours. In the step (i), the drying comprises placing durian shell extract into oven, drying at 50-60 degrees C until the moisture content is 10-15%, placing into crusher, and crushing into powder with particle size more than 60 mesh.

PN CN111790378-A
TI Manganese dioxide supported platinum-palladium            bimetallic nanosphere useful in degrading toluene and            in preparing toluene degradation materials.
AU LU J
   CHEN D
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 2020A42323
AB    NOVELTY - Manganese dioxide supported platinum-palladium                bimetallic nanosphere is claimed. The bimetallic                nanosphere is prepared by (i) placing                silica-polyaniline in potassium permanganate                aqueous solution, stirring, washing, heat treating,                alkali soaking, and allowing to stand to obtain                manganese dioxide nanospheres, and (ii) mixing the                manganese dioxide nanospheres with solution                containing platinum salt and palladium salt,                drying, calcining, and reducing to obtain final                product.
   USE - The bimetallic nanosphere is useful in                degrading toluene, and in preparing toluene                degradation materials (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bimetallic nanosphere: achieves                degradation of toluene at low temperature, and                ensures the product is harmless water and carbon                dioxide; greatly reduces the processing cost and                energy consumption; and is environmentally                friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) processing toluene in gas, comprising                introducing gas containing toluene into the                manganese dioxide supported platinum-palladium                bimetallic nanosphere to complete the treatment of                toluene in the gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  concentration of the potassium permanganate                  aqueous solution is 0.2 M, the alkali is 5 M                  sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, and the heat                  treatment is performed at 200 degrees C for 4                  hours. In step (ii), the platinum salt is                  chloroplatinic acid, and the palladium salt is                  chloropalladic acid. In step (ii): the dosage                  ratio between manganese dioxide nanospheres and                  solution containing platinum salt and palladium                  salt is 1 g :0.8-1.2 ml. Preferred Method: The                  silica-polyaniline in step (i) is prepared by                  adding aniline into solution containing                  hydrochloric acid and silica under ice bath, and                  adding mixed solution of hydrochloric acid and                  ammonium persulfate to react. In step (ii), the                  aqueous solution of chloroplatinic acid is mixed                  with aqueous solution of chloroplatinic acid to                  obtain solution containing platinum salt and                  palladium salt. In the solution containing                  platinum salt and palladium salt, the molar ratio                  between platinum and palladium is 0.4-2.5:1. The                  reduction in step (ii) is carried out in hydrogen                  environment at 150-200 degrees C for 1-2 hours.                  The calcination is performed at 200-300 degrees C                  for 1-2 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of iron-based catalyst comprises performing coprecipitation reaction of aqueous solution containing iron salt, copper salt and rare earth metal salt with precipitant to obtain precipitation slurry, filtering to obtain first filter cake, mixing first filter cake, silicon source, inorganic acid and water, aging to obtain aged slurry, adding water, filtering to obtain second filter cake, mixing second filter cake, boron source, water and optional potassium salt to obtain catalyst slurry, spray drying and heating.
