The rare earth metal salt is lanthanum nitrate, cerium nitrate, praseodymium nitrate, neodymium nitrate, samarium nitrate and/or erbium nitrate. The iron salt and copper salt is nitrate, chloride, carbonate, bicarbonate, ammonium salt, sulfate, organic salt and/or hydroxide of iron and copper, preferably nitrate of iron and copper. The inorganic acid is nitric acid, carbonic acid and/or sulfuric acid, preferably nitric acid. The potassium salt is potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate and/or potassium nitrate, preferably potassium nitrate. The silicon source is potassium silicate and/or silica sol. The boron source is boric acid, boron oxide, boron carbide and/or metal boride, preferably boric acid. The precipitant is sodium carbonate, ammonia water, potassium carbonate, ammonium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, potassium silicate and/or silica sol, preferably ammonia, sodium carbonate, potassium silicate and/or silica sol.

PN CN111422854-A
TI Preparing carbon aerogel involves dissolving            phenolic compound, aldehyde compound and catalyst in            reaction solvent to form precursor solution, which is            mixed with the modifier to form mixed solution.
AU XING Y
   CHEN J
   YUAN J
   LI J
   GONG W
   ZHAO K
AE TIANJIN CHENXIANG FENGKAI NEW MATERIAL (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202069430A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon aerogel involves dissolving                phenolic compound, aldehyde compound and catalyst                in the reaction solvent to form a precursor                solution. The precursor solution is mixed with the                modifier to form a mixed solution. The mixed                solution is heated to cause the phenolic compound                and the aldehyde compound to polymerize to form a                polymerized product. The high temperature                calcination is performed under the protection of                inert gas to remove modifiers to obtain organic                carbon materials. The organic carbon material is                activated in a carbon dioxide environment to obtain                a carbon aerogel material, where modifier is a                mixture containing microspheres.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon aerogel                which overcomes the defect of carbon aerogel high                temperature framework collapse, so as to obtain                carbon aerogel with large pore size.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The phenolic compound                  comprises at least one selected from the group                  consisting of phenol and its homologues,                  benzenediol allotrope and its homologues,                  benzenetriol allotrope and its homologues. The                  aldehyde compound includes at least one selected                  from formaldehyde, paraformaldehyde, furfural,                  acetaldehyde and its homologues and derivatives.                  The catalyst is any one selected from acidic                  catalysts and basic catalysts. The acid catalyst                  includes one selected from boric acid,                  hydrochloric acid, citric acid, and ascorbic                  acid. The basic catalyst includes one selected                  from sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide,                  potassium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, and                  ammonia. The modifier includes selected from the                  group consisting of octyl phenyl polyoxyethylene                  ether, tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide, dodecyl                  trimethyl ammonium bromide, polyethylene                  oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide                  triblock copolymer, polyethylene glycol, and                  sodium lauryl sulfate. The microspheres comprises                  one selected from polystyrene microspheres,                  polymethylmethacrylate microspheres, and silica                  microspheres.

PN CN111393801-A
TI Preparation of composite material for e.g. adhesive, involves adding graphene oxide and reducing agent to water, dispersing, heating, removing excess water, adding hydrogel block to aqueous epoxy resin and curing agent and curing.
AU CHEN Z
AE CHEN Z (CHEN-Individual)
GA 202068839X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of composite material involves adding 1 pt. wt. graphene oxide and 1-5 pts. wt. reducing agent to 100-1000 pts. wt. deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing for 20-30 minutes, sealing, heating to 90-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain graphene hydrogel, replacing graphene hydrogel with deionized water for 3-8 times, removing excess water to obtain graphene hydrogel block, adding graphene hydrogel block to aqueous epoxy resin, stirring, adding curing agent, uniformly stirring and curing.
   USE - Preparation of composite material for aqueous adhesive and floor paint (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple, short, and economical and avoids the use of expensive freeze-drying equipment or supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The graphene oxide is Hummers method graphene oxide or modified Hummers method graphene oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The reducing agent is one or more of sodium iodide, sodium sulfide, hydroquinone, ascorbic acid, dopamine, melamine, sodium citrate and hydroiodic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of graphene hydrogel block and aqueous epoxy resin is 10:10-100. The weight ratio of aqueous epoxy resin and curing agent is 100:10-300. Preferred Components: The aqueous epoxy resin is epoxy resin prepared by mechanical method, self-emulsification method, or reverse rotation method. The curing agent is one or more of polyamine, amidated polyamine prepared by the reaction of polyamine and monofatty acid, polyamide formed by condensation of dimer acid and polyamine and polyamine-epoxy adduct formed by addition reaction of polyamine and epoxy resin. Preferred Property: The aqueous epoxy resin has solid content of 30-80 wt.%.

AU LIU S
   GUAN X
   WANG Y
   ZHANG H
   ZHU J
   GOU M
   SHI C
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
   UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 202066714J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ferric belite cement comprises (i) taking 50-60 wt.% Bayer process red mud and 40-50 wt.% limestone are mixing uniformly, grinding into raw meal powder and compressing to obtain raw meal flakes, (ii) calcining the resultant and then cooling to obtain cement clinker, and (iii) uniformly mixing the cement clinker and gypsum and grinding.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ferric belite cement.
   ADVANTAGE - The cement: is economical, improves the utilization rate of Bayer process red mud solid waste; and reduces the carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, nitric oxide and other pollutant emissions during production; and the obtained product has high strength and excellent mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for ferric belite cement prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw meal powder includes 50-55 wt.% Bayer process red mud is and 45-50 wt.% limestone. The Bayer process red mud and limestone includes silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, ferric oxide, calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, potassium oxide, sodium oxide and titanium dioxide, preferably, the content of silicon dioxide in the Bayer process red mud is 15-30 wt.%. The content of aluminum oxide in the Bayer process red mud is 15-25 wt.%. The content of ferric oxide in the Bayer process red mud is 15-20 wt.%. The content of calcium oxide in the Bayer process red mud is 5-20 wt.%. The content of calcium oxide in the limestone is 45-55 wt.%. The fineness of the pulverized raw meal powder is less than or equal to 75 mu m. The mass ratio of gypsum and high-speed belite cement clinker is 0-20 wt.%. The specific surface area of the high-speed belite cement prepared by grinding is 350-450 m2/kg. The cement clinker comprises 45-60 wt.% C2S, 25-40 wt.% C4AF and 5-20 Wt.% C12A7, preferably 50-60 wt.% C2S, 30-40 wt.% C4AF and 5-10 wt.% C12A7. Preferred Method: In the step (ii) the calcination temperature is 1100-1250 degrees C and the calcination time is 1-2 hours.

   x = 0.78-0.82;
   y = 0.18-0.22; and
   delta ( delta ) = 0-1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing material, comprising (a) generating a mixed mixture or solid material comprising compounds containing lanthanum, a compound containing strontium, compounds containing cobalt and compounds containing iron, and (b) calcining the mixture or solid material to produce lanthanum strontium cobalt iron composite oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activation energy of the cathode made of the lanthanum strontium cobalt iron oxide compositeelectrode material is 1.04-1.12 eV. The electrical conductivity of the cathode made of the electrode material is 500-1200 Omega -1-cm-1 at 550-750 degrees C. The first salt is lanthanum nitrate. The second salt is strontium nitrate. The third salt is cobalt nitrate. The fourth salt is iron nitrate. Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises mixing the lanthanum oxide, strontium oxide, cobalt oxide and iron oxide powders, and ball milling for 20-24 hours. The method comprises preparing a metal salt aqueous solution by dissolving salts of compounds containing lanthanum, a compound containing strontium, compounds containing cobalt and compounds containing iron in distilled water at a ratio based on a molar ratio of the cations, forming a mixture by mixing a chelating agent in the aqueous metal salt solution, heating the mixture of the aqueous metal salt solution and the chelating agent to a first temperature to evaporate distilled water to produce a mixture gel, and heating the mixture gel to a second temperature to obtain a mixture solid, where the first temperature is 90-100 degrees C, and the second temperature is 230-260 degrees C. The method comprises calcining the mixture or solid at 1000-1100 degrees C for 4-6 hours.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN WO2019224326-A1; CA3100233-A1; AU2019274775-A1; EP3797084-A1
TI Generating hydrogen used e.g. in fuel cells, by introducing catalyst into in subterranean hydrocarbon reservoir containing hydrocarbon, carrying out catalyzed conversion of hydrocarbon to hydrogen and recovering hydrogen by membrane filter.
AU SURGUCHEV L
   SURGUCHEV M
   BERENBLYUM R
AE HYDROGEN SOURCE AS (HYDR-Non-standard)
   HYDROGEN SOURCE AS (HYDR-Non-standard)
   HYDROGEN SOURCE AS (HYDR-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0600L
AB    NOVELTY - Process for generating hydrogen involves (a) introducing a catalyst or precursor of it into a hydrocarbon containing zone, preferably a hydrocarbon gas-containing zone in a subterranean gas reservoir, (b) increasing temperature in the reservoir to a temperature at which catalyzed conversion of hydrocarbon to hydrogen occurs and (c) recovering a hydrogen stream via a membrane filter installed in a production well, where the production well is vertical or deviated vertical.
   USE - The process is useful for producing hydrogen, which is useful in energy production applications, fuel cells, heavy oil hydrogenation or ammonia production, and fertilizers.
   ADVANTAGE - The process segregates greenhouse gases comprising carbon and nitrogen oxides downwards within the reservoir under influence of gravity, which are denser than hydrogen, and produces products without producing carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide as by-products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is in a water soluble form. The catalyst precursor is a metal compound which is thermally decomposable to a catalytically active form or a solution of it. The metal compound comprises metal salt, metal carbonyl, metal alkyl, metal nitrate, metal sulfate, metal carbonate, metal carboxylate compound or a humic acid salt. Preferred Method: The process involves introducing the catalyst into a porous or fractured medium hydrocarbon oil or gas-containing zone in the reservoir using a first injection well. The process further involves optionally achieving commercial purity of hydrogen in the production stream from the well in the hydrogen generation from hydrocarbons subterrain (HGHS) process with segregation of generated in situ hydrogen from the heavier gas components and fluids present in gas and liquid phases, and/or carrying out gas separation by inert membrane filters installed downhole in the production well or at the well head to obtain a required commercial purity of hydrogen in the production stream, where if gravity segregation of hydrogen inside the reservoir is not complete within the field project time frame and the other gas components are still present in the production stream. The process further involves capturing produced carbon dioxide in situ. The process is performed in a natural gas field onshore or offshore. The catalyst is dissolved in an aqueous solution. The process involves increasing temperature by introducing an agent to the reservoir. The catalyzed conversion of hydrocarbon to hydrogen occurs by reactions chosen from a steam reforming (SR), water gas shift reaction (WGSR) and methane catalytic cracking (MCC) reactions. The process involves carrying out separation of hydrogen by gravity segregation prior to contact with the membrane filter. The hydrogen stream is recovered from said reservoir by using tubing connected from surface to the membrane filter. The process further involves recovering heat from the reservoir by circulating water between the surface and said reservoir using the first injection well or a second injection well connected to the first injection well, and injecting calcium and/or magnesium-containing materials into the reservoir using an injection well. The reservoir is a coal field. The reservoir comprises a carbonate reservoir, a sandstone reservoir, a shale reservoir, a tight low permeable reservoir or a natural gas or oil reservoir, optionally with carbon dioxide content in the gas. The step of increasing temperature is carried out at 400-1000 degrees C, preferably 700-1000 degrees C by using a downhole in the well flameless reactor, heat pump, electric heater, exothermic reactants, plasma and plasma pyrolysis or microwave reactors reactions in situ, a non-flameless reactor or exothermic reaction(s) in the down-hole of an injection well. The process involves introducing air, oxygen, carbon dioxide, water, steam or their combination into the reservoir during the HGHS process. The process involves using a downhole microwave reactor for operation to yield hydrogen and solid phase carbon, where the hydrogen and carbon are produced by plasma driven hydrocarbon phase thermal decomposition. The microwave reactor is operated by heating at 1000-2000 degrees C in plasma pyrolysis regime at micro-wave frequencies of 300 MHz to 3 GHz.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. GA 201994309Q
AB    NOVELTY - A modified alumina is prepared by dissolving dispersant and rare earth modifier in water, obtaining modifier solution, adding expanding agent wetted with absolute ethanol to 75-120 g/L sodium aluminate solution, obtaining expanding agent sodium aluminate solution, pumping carbon dioxide gas, adding modifier solution to prepare slurry via co-precipitation method, aging at 50-90 degrees C for 24-240 hours, filtering, washing filter cake, adding 5-50 g/L alkaline aqueous solution, performing hydrothermal reaction, filtering, washing, drying, and calcining at 730 degrees C for 2 hours.
   USE - A method for preparing modified alumina.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is short, simple and low-cost. The product has excellent product performance, and improves high-temperature thermal stability and catalyst service life of automobile exhaust catalytic purification agents.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Methods: The co-precipitation method is carried out at 25-80 degrees C and industrial carbon dioxide flow rate of 1-100 L/minute at pH value endpoint of 99.5 and modifier solution titration rate of 0.1-50 L/hour. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 100-160 degrees C for 1-10 hours. Preferred Components: The rare earth modifier is lanthanum nitrate, cerium nitrate and zirconium nitrate at dosage of 5-40 wt.%. The expanding agent is acetylene black, activated carbon powder and graphite powder at dosage of 5-40 wt.%. The alkaline aqueous solution is alkaline ammonium bicarbonate or ammonia aqueous solution. The weight ratio of filter cake to alkaline aqueous solution is 1:1-20:1.

PT P
PN CN110229330-A; CN110229330-B; WO2020258383-A1
TI Performing synthetic synthesis of polypyrrole            useful as gas adsorbent, involves mixing acidic            solution with pyrrole monomer, stirring, adding            oxidizing agent and catalyst, stirring, maintaining,            filtering, washing and drying product.
AU LIU L
   LI G
   WANG Z
   DU T
   XIE L
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
   UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
GA 201980524H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing synthetic synthesis of                polypyrrole, involves mixing acidic solution with                pyrrole monomer, stirring uniformly to obtain mixed                solution, adding an oxidizing agent and a catalyst                to the mixed solution, stirring uniformly, then                maintaining under static condition, filtering,                washing and drying to obtain a black solid                polypyrrole.
   USE - The method is useful for performing synthetic                synthesis of polypyrrole, which is used as a gas                adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method synthesizes polypyrrole simply in a                natural and environmentally-friendly manner, and is                suitable for industrial application. The                polypyrrole directly captures carbon dioxide in the                humid flue gas, without pretreatment to remove                moisture in the flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                the prepared polypyrrole, as a gas adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic solution is                  hydrochloric acid. The pH is controlled at 0-3.                  The oxidizing agent is hydrogen peroxide. The                  catalyst is iron ion. The molar ratio of the                  oxidizing agent with respect to pyrrole monomer                  is 0.5:1. The molar ratio of the iron ion to the                  hydrogen peroxide is 0.001-0.27. The iron ion is                  ferric ion (Fe3+) or divalent iron ion (Fe2+).                  Preferred Method: The method involves stirring                  for 0.5-3 hours, maintaining under static                  condition for 2-48 hours, filtering by vacuum                  pumping and centrifugation, then washing using                  deionized water until the filtrate is clarified,                  and then drying at 40-95 degrees C for 4-24                  hours. The method further involves collecting the                  filtrate, providing acidic solution and catalyst                  for the synthesis reaction of the next                  polypyrrole, where the amount x of the                  supplemental hydrochloric acid and the amount y                  of the oxidizing agent are determined by the                  relationship between the residual acid m and the                  amount n of the iron ions in the filtrate, both                  expressed in moles, the sum of m and n values is                  1, and the sum of y and n values is 7.4x 10-5.                  The polypyrrole is used as a gas adsorbent to                  adsorb and separate carbon dioxide and nitrogen                  gases under a pressure swing condition at 0-90                  degrees C, where the pressure-variable condition                  is 0.01-1 bar.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC A02-A06; A05-J12; A10-D06; A10-E01; A12-W11D; J01-D; J01-E02B; J01-F; J01-H01

PN CN110075901-A; CN110075901-B
TI Preparation of porous sulfur-doped graphite-phase carbon nitride-reduced graphene oxide nanosheet used e.g. for photocatalytic degradation of pollutant, by calcining melamine-cyanuric acid supra-molecular precursor and thermal treating.
AU GUO X
   ZHENG Y
   CHEN Z
   LIU Y
   ZHANG W
   TANG X
   WANG Y
   PENG Z
   ZHANG M
   LI R
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2019692895
AB    NOVELTY - A porous sulfur-doped graphite-phase carbon nitride-reduced graphene oxide nanosheet is prepared by calcining melamine-cyanuric acid supra-molecular precursor in muffle furnace to obtain graphite-phase carbon nitride nanosheet, dissolving graphite-phase carbon nitride nanosheet and graphene oxide in dimethyl sulfoxide, ultrasonically treating to obtain uniform and stable graphite-phase carbon nitride-graphene oxide dispersed solution, transferring to stainless steel high pressure kettle with PTFE liner, and solvent thermal treating.
   USE - The method is used for porous sulfur-doped graphite-phase carbon nitride-reduced graphene oxide nanosheet preparation used for photocatalytic hydrogen production, carbon dioxide reduction and photocatalytic degradation of pollutant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method optimizes experimental condition. The product has stable sample structure and good electrochemical performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Calcining is done at 450-550 degrees C for 1-4 hours at rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute. Solvent thermal treatment is done at 120-200 degrees C for 8-24 hours.

PN CN110051681-A
TI Preparation of low-embryo toxicity nanocomposite anti-tumor medicine by vigorously stirring sodium hydroxide solution and anti-tumor medicine powder under nitrogen atmosphere, adding metal ion salt solution, heating, and vacuum-drying.
AU WANG S
   ZHU R
   SUN X
   WANG Z
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 201967033B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of low-embryo toxicity nanocomposite anti-tumor medicine comprises preparing metal ion salt solution containing divalent metal ion salt and trivalent metal ion salt at mol ratio of 1: 1-3: 1 as solute, preparing sodium hydroxide solution using double-distilled water of carbon dioxide as solvent, vigorously stirring sodium hydroxide solution and anti-tumor medicine powder under nitrogen atmosphere, adding metal ion salt solution to obtain suspension, transferring suspension into hydrothermal synthesis kettle, heating at 80-120 degrees C for 14-18 hours, and vacuum-drying in vacuum-drying box.
   USE - The method is for preparing low-embryo toxicity nanocomposite anti-tumor medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The medicine can reduce embryo toxicity and embryonic developmental disorder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The divalent metal ion salt is magnesium nitrate, zinc nitrate, calcium nitrate, magnesium chloride, zinc chloride or calcium chloride. The trivalent metal ion salt is aluminum nitrate, ferric nitrate, ferric chloride or aluminum chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The anti-tumor medicine powder is cell cycle-based anti-tumor drug, preferably etoposide or podophyllotoxin.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B02 (Fused ring heterocyclics.)

PT P
PN CN109984318-A
TI Freshly cutting and preservation of fresh taste of burdock involves selecting burdock root, washing, frying, chilling, peeling, soaking in color protection agent, dicing, soaking in water, adding potassium sorbate, bagging, and sealing.
AU WANG F
AE JIANGSU ZHENBANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201962122K
AB    NOVELTY - Freshly cutting and preservation of fresh taste of burdock comprises selecting pest-free, non-corrosive, and tender burdock root; washing roots to remove sludge and impurities; frying and chilling with nitrogen at 0-8 degrees C for 6-10 hours; peeling skin to remove the mildewed and rotted tissue; soaking in color protection agent; dicing to 7-9 g/block, soaking in water for 1-2 hours, and draining; adding potassium sorbate and stirring; and putting into a bag with gas barrier properties, charging mixed gas of oxygen, carbon dioxide, or nitrogen, sealing, and packing.
   USE - The methods are used for freshly cutting and preserving fresh taste of burdock.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not influence the fresh burdock taste during storage.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN109876642-A; CN109876642-B
TI Treating nitrogen oxide containing flue gas involves introducing flue gas into photobioreactor for microalgae culture, harvesting microalgae cells, adding denitrifying bacteria to filtrate, and performing denitrification treatment.
AU ZHAO L
   SHI W
   LIU Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 201956587V
AB    NOVELTY - Treating nitrogen oxide containing flue gas involves introducing flue gas into the photobioreactor for microalgae culture having pH of 9 or more, preferably 10-12, collecting the microalgae that is resistant to high pH and discharging through vent gas, subjecting to solid-liquid separation, harvesting the microalgae cells and the filtrate, adding denitrifying bacteria to the filtrate, and performing denitrification treatment.
   USE - Treating nitrogen oxide-containing flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is economical, and uses biological method to remove carbon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide in the flue gas, does not use any catalyst and large amount of chemical reagents, and has excellent removal effect, low treatment cost, and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for treating a nitrogen-containing oxide flue gas, which comprises photobioreaction system, exhaust gas collection system, solid-liquid separation system, and denitrification system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: The microalgae culture medium uses BG11 (blue-green medium containing ferric ammonium citrate, citric acid, sodium nitrate, magnesium heptahydrate), SE (serum-free culture medium), BBM (Bold's Basal Medium) to culture the micro algae liquid medium. The microalagae resistant to high pH is preferably Chlorella vulgaris FSH-Y3 or/and Scenedesmus obliquus. Preferred Composition: The inoculum comprises 1-50 vol.% microalgae seed liquid of the volume of the microalgae medium. Preferred Process: Preparation of seed liquid involves using C.vulgaris FSH-Y3 and the S.obliquus in the ratio of 5:1-1:5, adjusting the pH of the medium to 10-12, preferably 6-9 at 20-30 degrees C,preferably 10-30 degrees C for a photoperiod of 24 hours in the light to dark ratio of 10:14-14:10 having light intensity of 2000-10000 Lux, preferably 2000-20000 Lux, shaking the culture based on logarithmic growth phase, and adding Chlorella SF-B1 for 1-5 days based on the preservative number CGMCC No.11005 in the ratio of 1:1-1:50 by volume ratio with the seed liquid. The denitrification treatment uses at least one of denitrifying Denitococcus DN-3 and methyl Bacillus SDN-3, and the total nitrogen removal rate is 95% or more. Preferred Property: The sludge concentration after inoculation is 2000-10000 mg/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The flue gas comprises nitric oxide and/or nitrogen dioxide, sulfur dioxide, and carbon dioxide. The flue gas is obtained from at least one of sulfur recovery unit incineration tail gas, catalytic cracking regeneration tail gas, an S-zorb regeneration tail gas, coal combustion flue gas, and sintering flue gas. The filtrate is nitrate and nitrite. Preferred Composition: The multi-stage photobioreactor controls carbon dioxide content in each stage of the photobioreactor to less than 45 vol.%, preferably 5-35 vol.%, nitrogen oxide content is less than 0.1 vol.%, preferably less than 0.05 vol.%, and ventilation is 0.1-1.0 vvm.

PT P
PN CN109437832-A
TI Preparing self-hydrophobic silica aerogel            composite material comprises e.g. taking silicon source            precursor, hydrophobic modifier, solvent, water, and            hydrolysis catalyst to prepare sol and preparing gel            with sol and substrate composite gel.
AU CAO Y
   ZHU C
   SONG D
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 201924521L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing self-hydrophobic silica aerogel                composite material comprises (i) taking silicon                source precursor, hydrophobic modifier, solvent,                water, and hydrolysis catalyst as raw material to                prepare sol, (ii) preparing gel with sol and                substrate composite gel, (iii) aging of the alcohol                gel, (iv) drying the ultra-critical carbon dioxide                of the alcohol gel to obtain a silica aerogel                composite material, and (v) detecting relevant                properties of aerogel composites.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                self-hydrophobic silica aerogel composite                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has excellent heat preservation                performance, waterproof performance, fireproof and                mechanical properties; and is convenient for                construction. The method: avoids the separate                hydrophobic modification step; simplifies the                process production steps; is simple, controllable,                and continuous method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon source                  precursor comprises tetraethyl orthosilicate,                  methyl orthosilicate, trimethylchlorosilane,                  methyltriethoxysilane and/or                  methyltrimethoxysilane. The hydrophobic modifier                  comprises methyltriethoxysilane and/or                  methyltrimethoxysilane. The solvent comprises                  methanol, ethanol, butanol and/or linear alcohol                  having a mass fraction of 96% or more. The water                  is pure water or ultrapure water. The hydrolysis                  catalyst comprises hydrochloric acid, oxalic                  acid, nitric acid, sulfuric acid, acetic acid,                  ammonia water, ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium                  hydrogencarbonate. The sol is prepared by using                  ethyl orthosilicate, methyltriethoxysilane, 99%                  industrial methanol and water in a weight ratio                  of 1:0.57:39.55:0.77, where the volume of the                  hydrolysis catalyst added is 0.33 Torr of the                  total volume of the above mixture. The basic                  catalyst comprises aqueous ammonia, aqueous                  ammonium fluoride solution and/or sodium                  hydroxide. The base material is a uniform plate                  or felt made of fiber filaments having a diameter                  of nanometers, and has a density of not more than                  200 kg/m3, and comprises glass fiber needle felt,                  glass fiber centrifugal cotton, glass fiber                  plywood, quartz fiber needle felt, carbon fiber                  felt, polyester fiber needle felt and ceramic                  fiber felt. Preferred Method: In the step (ii),                  the alcohol gel is prepared by uniformly mixing                  the sol with a basic catalyst by in-line mixing,                  then casting onto the base material, saturating                  the substrate to absorb the sol, starting the                  conveyor belt, and bringing the substrate of the                  saturated adsorbed sol out of the shallow groove                  to obtain glass fiber felt aerogel wet material.                  In the step (iii), the aging is carried out by                  placing the alcohol gel in an ethanol solution at                  50-55&#176; C for 28-35 hours. In the step                  (iv), the temperature required for the                  supercritical is 45-55&#176; C, and the                  pressure is 10-16 MPa, the flow rate of carbon                  dioxide through the supercritical drying kettle                  is 2000-2500 kg/hour, and the drying time is 6-12                  hours. In the step (v), the test is to randomly                  select three points of the prepared silica                  aerogel composite material, and take two pieces                  of 300x 300 mm materials to measure density,                  thermal conductivity, hydrophobicity and/or                  mechanical strength.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

TI Processing solid carbon fixation of a concrete            preform comprises e.g. immersing a concrete preform in            a calcium-based alkaline solution, removing the            concrete pre-formed product after alkali leaching in            the carbonization curing kiln.
AU LIAO H
   REN G
   FAN B
   CHENG F
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 201917285X
AB    NOVELTY - Processing solid carbon fixation of a concrete                preform comprises (i) immersing a concrete preform                in a calcium-based alkaline solution, removing the                concrete pre-formed product after alkali leaching                in the carbonization curing kiln, introducing the                waste hot flue gas containing (carbon dioxide) CO2                into carbonization curing kiln, where the concrete                pre-product stays in the atmospheric carbonization                curing kiln, removing the concrete preform from the                carbonized curing kiln.
   USE - The method is useful for processing solid                carbon fixation of a concrete perform.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is suitable for the reinforcement                of inorganic silicate pre-products and the fixed                emission reduction of flue gas CO2 mineralization,                and the process is simple and easy, is                environmentally-friendly and economical and is easy                to implement in industrial utilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concrete                  pre-product comprises a cement concrete                  pre-product, a foamed concrete pre-product, and a                  solid waste-based active cement concrete                  pre-preparation, solid waste-based reactive                  cementitious foamed concrete pre-products. The                  calcium-based alkaline solution is a saturated                  solution containing calcium ions, including lime                  water, calcium carbide slag aqueous solution and                  steel slag aqueous solution. The immersion time                  of the concrete preform in a calcium-based                  alkaline solution is 0.5-24 hours. The source of                  CO2 for carbonization curing comprises waste heat                  flue gas of coal-fired power plant, waste heat                  flue gas of lime kiln, waste heat flue gas of                  steel factory is at least one of the waste heat                  fumes of chemical plants and waste heat fumes of                  cement plants. The carbonization curing time is                  adjusted according to the quality of the concrete                  preform, and the concrete preform is required to                  be immersed in the calcium hydroxide. Preferred                  Method: The CO2 carbonization reaction in the                  flue gas has a carbonation curing time of 24-48                  hours. The carbonization curing temperature is                  50-80 degrees C, and the volume concentration of                  CO2 in the waste heat flue gas introduced into                  the carbonization curing kiln is 10-20%.

The                core layer two sides are placed with first                anti-radiation layer and second anti-radiation                layer.    USE - Anti-high temperature and anti-radiation heat                insulating member used for heat insulation in                anoxic environment including carbon dioxide                atmosphere and/or nitrogen atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The member has excellent high temperature                anti-radiation performance, ensures heat insulating                property, and prevent residues generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the anti-high temperature and                anti-radiation heat insulating member.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fiber-reinforced                  matrix is made of fibers selected from quartz                  fiber, aluminum oxide fiber and mullite fiber,                  preferably mullite fiber and aluminum oxide                  fiber. The density of matrix is 0.1-0.14 g/cm3.                  The aerogel is prepared by silicon-aluminum sol                  precursor. The density of aerogel is 0.3-0.4                  g/cm3. The anti-radiation layer is graphite                  paper, and has thickness of 0.05-2.00 mm. The                  fiber cloth is alumina, quartz cloth and                  tourmaline. The thickness of fiber cloth is                  0.1-0.3 mm. The heat insulating member can be                  used in carbon dioxide atmosphere at 25-1200                  degrees C, is able to withstand high temperatures                  above 1200 degrees C, thermal conductivity at 300                  C in carbon dioxide ambient atmosphere of 0.026 W                  (m.K), density of less than 0.4 g/cm3, insulation                  performance of 12 mm thickness is 300 seconds,                  vacuum loss of less than 0.1%, condensable                  volatile matter of less than 0.01%, and                  emissivity of 0.60-0.85 at room temperature to                  1200 degrees C.

AU SHEN F
   HE J
   WANG L
   LIU Y
AE SHEN F (SHEN-Individual)
GA 2018A5438V
AB    NOVELTY - An Aloe tablet comprises 0.1-0.5 g Aloe freeze dried powder, 0.1-0.5 g xylooligosaccharide, 0.2-0.5 g radix astragali extract and 0.1-0.2 g additive.
   USE - Aloe tablet.
   ADVANTAGE - The product is fast acting, and quickly and effectively reduces constipation symptoms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of Aloe tablet comprising taking Aloe leaves, washing and peeling, freeze drying, performing ultramicro crushing, sieving into 80 mesh, obtaining Aloe freeze dried powder, taking radix astragali, cleaning and drying, ultramicro crushing, sieving into 20 mesh, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, sieving into 80 mesh, obtaining radix astragali extract, mixing prepared raw materials, adding xylooligosaccharide, sieving into 80 mesh, adding 80 mesh of dextrin and silica, mixing, adding 85 vol.% ethanol to prepare soft material, granulating into 20 mesh, drying for 55-60 minutes, adding 80 mesh magnesium stearate, mixing for 30 minutes, tableting, bottling, and coating and packing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The Aloe tablet comprises 0.25 g Aloe freeze dried powder, 0.25 g xylooligosaccharide, 0.375 g radix astragali extract and 0.125 g additive. The radix astragali extract is crude polysaccharide; and additive is dextrin, silica or magnesium stearate. Preferred Method: The Aloe tablet is prepared by taking Aloe leaves, washing and peeling, freeze drying, performing ultramicro crushing under 150-180 MPa at 20-25 degrees C, sieving into 20 mesh, sieving into 80 mesh, obtaining Aloe freeze dried powder, taking radix astragali, cleaning and drying, ultramicro crushing under 178 MPa at 25 degrees C, sieving into 20 mesh, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction using 8.5-10.2 wt.% carbon dioxide under 32-38 MPa at 40-50 degrees C for 1.2-2 hours, and using 12.5-14.6 wt.% carbon dioxide under 47-68 MPa at 47-68 degrees C for 2.5-3 hours sieving into 80 mesh, obtaining radix astragali extract, mixing prepared raw materials, adding xylooligosaccharide, sieving into 80 mesh, adding 80 mesh of dextrin and silica, mixing, adding 2 wt.% of 85 vol.% ethanol to prepare soft material, granulating into 20 mesh, drying for 55-60 minutes, adding 80 mesh magnesium stearate, mixing for 30 minutes, tableting, bottling, and coating and packing.
   ACTIVITY - Laxative.

   SUN W
   HUANG L
   DUN W
AE CCCC FHEC HAIWEI ENG CONSTR CO LTD (CCCC-C)
GA 2018A0447W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing coal powder of a first component to a gasification reactor. Determination is made to check whether atmosphere in the gasification reactor is oxidizing atmosphere. The gasification reactor is provided with pulverized coal. Amount of the pulverized coal and oxygen are adjusted. Gas is discharged from the gasification reactor into the combustor for heating asphalt material.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving heating reliability and applicability and mixture quality, increasing service life of a cloth bag and effectively reducing environment pollution and discharge amount of carbon dioxide gas.

PN CN108926539-A
TI Preparing snake gall freeze-dried powder by breeding live snake, starving, removing the snake gall, washing the snake gall, refrigerating, centrifuging, freeze-drying precipitate, adding additive solution, granulating and drying into powder.
AU CHENG L
AE ANHUI WUJIU LIQUOR IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018995511
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing snake gall freeze-dried powder involves (a) breeding live snake at 15-22 degrees C, starving for 5-7 days, removing the snake gall, washing the snake gall with water until no blood stains, ultrasonically processing the snake gall for 5-10 minutes, refrigerating for 3-4 hours and puncturing the snake gall and (b) centrifuging the snake gall for 10-15 minutes, removing the supernatant, freeze-drying the precipitate, adding additive solution, evenly stirring and uniformly mixing, granulating the mixed solution and drying by supercritical carbon dioxide technology to obtain freeze-dried powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing snake gall freeze-dried powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method lowers energy consumption, ensures uniform particle preparation, is environmentally-friendly and retains the medicinal properties of snake gall.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves ultrasonically processing the snake gall at 150-200 W and 20-30 Hz and refrigerating at 4-8 degrees C. The step (b) involves centrifuging the snake gall at 8000-9000 revolutions per minute (rpm), freezing temperature at -15 degrees C to -5 degrees C and drying by supercritical carbon dioxide technology at reactor pressure of 15-25 MPa and temperature of 35-45 degrees C and separation vessel pressure of 5-8 MPa, where the nozzle diameter is 150-240 mu m. Preferred Components: The additive solution is a mixture of a stabilizer and a protective agent in a concentration ratio of 0.45-0.6:1. The stabilizer is nonionic surfactant. The protective agent is mannitol or sucrose. The solid-liquid ratio of bile powder and additive solution is 1:5-9.

PN CN108485517-A
TI Production of heat-resistant powder coating from red mud and phosphogypsum involves mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additives and modifiers, calcining, dissolving in water, separating liquid, precipitating, and mixing solid silicone resin.
AU CHEN X
   CHEN Q
   WANG Q
   ZHAO P
   WANG L
   TANG D
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201871281G
AB    NOVELTY - Production of heat-resistant powder coating from red mud and phosphogypsum comprises mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additives and modifiers, grinding mixture, calcining in kiln, subjecting to water milling, dissolving in water, subjecting to solid-liquid separation, subjecting obtained precipitate to flotation to separate sulfide, and roasting separated sulfide; catalyzing flue gas generated by calcination using vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing in concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid; and adding carbon dioxide to liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until white precipitate is no longer produced, filtering white precipitate off, washing, drying, calcining, grinding, re-calcining to obtain alumina powder; mixing alumina powder with solid silicone resin and leveling agent, subjecting to melt extrusion, cooling extrudate, pulverizing, and sieving.
   USE - Method for producing heat-resistant powder coating from red mud and phosphogypsum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The red mud is red mud produced by Bayer process for producing alumina. The additive is sodium carbonate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon, or coal gangue. The mass ratio of phosphogypsum and Bayer red mud is 1:0.7-1.5. The ratio of additive and total amount of sodium oxide, aluminum oxide and ferric oxide in the raw meal is 1:1. The modifier is added in an amount of 8-20 wt.% of raw meal. The mass ratio of alumina powder, solid silicone resin and leveling agent is 10-25:70-85:1-5. Preferred Method: The mixture of phosphogypsum, red mud, additives and modifiers is calcined at 1100-1340 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours. The sulfide is roasted at 800-1200 degrees C. The white precipitate is calcined at 800-1200 degrees C for 1-5 hours, ground, and re-calcined at 700-900 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. The melt extrusion is carried out at 140-160 degrees C to obtain grain diameter of 20-40 mu m.

PN CN108283221-A
TI Docosahexaenoic acid-rich tea Camellia seed oil nourishing seasoning oil comprises microalgae oil, tea seed oil, pepper oil, ginger oil, garlic oil, sesame oil and peanut oil.
AU LIN S
   LONG T
   XIE J
   LIN X
   YU J
AE GUANGZHOU GINNIBABY EDIBLE OIL CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201857081B
AB    NOVELTY - A docosahexaenoic acid (DHA)-rich tea Camellia seed oil nourishing seasoning oil comprises 3-8 wt.% microalgae oil, 70-80 wt.% tea seed oil, 1-3 wt.% pepper oil, 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. ginger oil, 0.2-0.8 wt.% garlic oil, 5-8 wt.% sesame oil and 5-10 wt.% peanut oil.
   USE - DHA-rich tea Camellia seed oil nourishing seasoning oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The oil is not easy to be oxidized, has high stability and excellent taste and flavor, and meets market demand.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the DHA-rich tea Camellia seed oil nourishing seasoning oil comprising taking tea seed oil, pepper, ginger, garlic, sesame and peanut as raw material, adding into separated supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device to obtain oil, and mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The microalgae oil is prepared by grinding algae fermentation liquid, homogenizing at high pressure, breaking wall, mixing with water according to weight ratio of 2-4:1, heating, stirring and extracting at 50-70 degrees C for 30-90 minutes, centrifugally separating extraction liquid to obtain emulsified oil and sediment, adding demulsifier into emulsified oil, washing with hot water, centrifugally separating, and vacuum dehydrating. The tea seed oil is prepared by drying tea, removing stone, shelling and crushing, adding into extracting kettle of multi-stage supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction device, extracting at 30-60 degrees C for 1.0-3.0 hours at 20-30 MPa, adding separation kettle for reducing carbon dioxide and oil separating, adjusting separation kettle I temperature to 30-50 degrees C and pressure to 10-25 MPa to obtain tea seed crude oil, refining, adding into molecular distilling sample tank, adjusting molecular distillation parameters at 100-150 degrees C at vacuum degree of 2-9 Pa, rotating speed of 100-300 revolutions/minute (rpm), feeding speed of 20-60 mL/minute and condensing temperature of 30-50 degrees C, refining, collecting and refining. The pepper oil is prepared by drying and crushing pepper, adding into extracting kettle of multi-stage supercritical CO2 extraction device, extracting at 35-45 degrees C for 2.0-30 hours at 25-30 MPa, adding mixture into separation kettle, vacuum separating, adjusting separating kettle I temperature at 30-50 degrees C and I pressure to 10-25 MPa to obtain pepper oil crude oil, refining, adding into molecular distillation sample tank, adjusting molecular distillation technology parameter at distillation temperature of 100-150 degrees C at vacuum degree of 2-9 Pa, rotating speed of 100-300 rpm, feeding speed of 20-60 mL/minute and condensing temperature of 30-50 degrees C, and collecting. The sesame oil is prepared by drying and crushing sesame, adding into extracting kettle of multi-stage supercritical CO2 extraction device, extracting at 35-50 degrees C for 1.5-3.5 hours at 25-35 MPa, adding mixture into separation kettle, vacuum separating, adjusting separation kettle I temperature at 30-50 degrees C and pressure at 10-25 MPa to obtain sesame oil crude oil, refining, adding into molecular distillation sample tank, adjusting molecular distillation technology parameter at 100-150 degrees C at vacuum degree of 2-9 Pa, rotating speed of 100-300 rpm, feeding speed of 20-60 mL/minute and condensing temperature of 30-50 degrees C, refining process, and collecting. The peanut oil is prepared by shelling and crushing peanut, adding into extracting kettle of multi-stage supercritical CO2 extraction device, extracting at 30-50 degrees C for 2.0-4.0 hours at 25-35 MPa, adding into separation kettle, reducing carbon dioxide, oil separating, adjusting separation kettle I temperature at 30-60 degrees C and pressure at 10-25 MPa to obtain peanut oil crude oil, refining, adding into molecular distillation sample tank, adjusting molecular distillation technology parameter at 100-150 degrees C at vacuum degree of 2-9 Pa, rotating speed of 100-300 rpm, feeding speed of 20-60 mL/minute and condensing temperature of 30-50 degrees C, refining process, and collecting.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN WO2018088436-A1; CN109789393-A; JP2020506789-W
TI Ammoximation catalyst useful to produce oxime from ketone by ammoximation reaction using hydrogen, oxygen and ammonia, comprises titanosilicate, and metal supported on titanosilicate and/or another support different from titanosilicate.
AU UEURA K
   FUKUTA Y
   YAMAMOTO Y
   LEWIS R
   FREAKLEY S
   EDWARDS J
   HUTCHINGS G
   HUTCHINS G
AE UBE IND LTD (UBEI-C)
   UNIV CARDIFF COLLEGE (UYCF-C)
   UNIV CARDIFF COLLEGE (UYCF-C)
   UBE IND LTD (UBEI-C)
GA 201838694S
AB    NOVELTY - Ammoximation catalyst for producing an oxime from a ketone by ammoximation reaction using hydrogen, oxygen and ammonia, comprises: a titanosilicate; and a metal supported on the titanosilicate and/or another support that is different from the titanosilicate, where the metal comprises palladium and gold.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for producing an oxime (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: produces oximes from ketones with high yield and high selectivity; produces oximes without separating and purifying the hydrogen peroxide,.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing an oxime from a ketone by ammoximation reaction using hydrogen, oxygen and ammonia in a presence of an ammoximation catalyst, comprising performing the ammoximation reaction by supplying carbon dioxide in an amount of more than 0 and less than 10 times by mole based on an amount of ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: A supporting amount of the metal is 0.01-20 wt.%. A weight ratio of gold/palladium is 0.02:50, preferably 0.05:5. The metal further comprises at least one metal comprising platinum, ruthenium, rhodium, osmium, iridium, silver, rhenium, tin, cobalt, nickel, copper or manganese. The titanosilicate comprises TS-1 having an atomic ratio of silicon/titanium of 30. The support for the metal is titanium oxide, activated carbon, silica, alumina or iron oxide. A form of ammonia supplied to the reaction is aqueous ammonia solution, gaseous ammonia, and/or ammonium salts. At least a part of ammonia supplied to the reaction is in a form of ammonium salt, and the ammonium salt is at least one of ammonium carbonate and ammonium hydrogen carbonate. A form of carbon dioxide supplied to the reaction is one of carbon dioxide gas and/or carbonate salts. At least a part of carbon dioxide supplied to the reaction is in a form of carbonate salt, and the carbonate salt is at least one of ammonium carbonate and ammonium hydrogen carbonate. The metal comprises palladium or a combination of palladium and at least one metal comprising gold, platinum, nickel, tin, rhenium, osmium, copper, iridium, ruthenium, rhodium, silver, cobalt and manganese. Preferred Process: In the process: carbon dioxide is supplied in an amount of greater than or equal to 0.1 times by mole and less than 10 times by mole, based on an amount of ammonia; and carbon dioxide is supplied in an amount of 0.5-5 times by mole, based on an amount of ammonia.
   EXAMPLE - Typical ammoximation catalyst comprised of: 2.5% palladium; and 2.5% gold/titania and titanosilicate.

PT P
PN CN107805624-A
TI Inducing formation of islet-like structures involves subjecting islet endocrine cells prepared by directly induced differentiation of adult stem cells or induced pluripotent stem cells to suspension culture at specified temperature.
AU LOU J
   ZHANG W
AE CHINA-JAPAN FRIENDSHIP HOSPITAL (CHNI-Non-standard)
GA 201825059E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for inducing the formation of islet-like structures in vitro, involves (a) subjecting islet endocrine cells prepared by directly induced differentiation of adult stem cells or induced pluripotent stem cells (iPS cells), or islet-derived islet endocrine cell lines to suspension culture to form the islet-like structures, where the suspension culture is performed at 37 degrees C for 4-24 hours, where the carbon dioxide concentration is 5% and the nitrogen concentration is 95%.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing the formation of islet-like structures in vitro.
   ADVANTAGE - The method rapidly produces more than 90% islet-like structures within 4-12 hours, and the produced islet-like structures are uniform in size and similar to natural islets, and have increased insulin secretion function as compared to non-islet-like structures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The suspension culture is performed at 37 degrees C for 6-12 hours, where the carbon dioxide concentration is 5% and the nitrogen concentration is 50%. Preferred Components: The culture medium used in the suspension culture is a cell culture medium added with 20% fetal bovine serum, 4.5 g/l glucose, 1-2 mmol/l calcium ions, and 0.01-2 mmol/l ATP. Preferred Apparatus: The culture container used for the suspension culture is a container that is not conducive to cell adhesion and/or adherence, and the container comprises a culture container with untreated surface or a culture container that prevents the cells from adhering to the surface after treatment.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-B04D4; B04-F02; B11-A04A; D05-H08B
IP C12N-005/071; C12N-005/02

PN CN206985841-U
TI Integrated resource type domestic sewage treatment biological fluidized bed comprises fluidized bed body, grating pond, anaerobic fermentation tank, wind energy aeration fishpond, and ceramic filtering purifying pool.
AU YUAN X
   HAN H
AE HAINAN HUADA BINGSHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 201814101H
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an integrated resource type domestic sewage processing biological fluidized bed, it comprises inner body of fluidized bed, the fluidized bed body is provided with a grid rearing pond, an anaerobic fermenting tank, a wind energy aeration aquarium and ceramic filtering purifying pool. The utility model has simple operation, simple maintenance, using low oxygen impurity, eating, appetite pond fish to consume organic matter in the sewage, reduces the pressure of the anaerobic fermentation bed, plant and absorbs the released fermentation decomposition of carbon dioxide and ammonia nitrogen, purifies the air and water quality is ensured, effectively avoids pollution transfer of activated sludge process increases the carbon discharge, which reduces the investment cost, low energy consumption, small floor area.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E35
